| `\prod`                   | product notation, symbole de produit, 连乘符号                 | A symbol denoting multiplication of a sequence of terms. | EN: `\prod_{k=1}^{4}k=1\cdot2\cdot3\cdot4=24`.<br>FR: `\prod_{k=1}^{4}k=1\cdot2\cdot3\cdot4=24`.<br>中：`\prod_{k=1}^{4}k=1\cdot2\cdot3\cdot4=24`。                |

## 7.4 Common finite sums

| Symbol (LaTeX)                                      | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                                           | Definition                                                               | Examples                                                                                                                                                               |
| --------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `1+2+\cdots+n`                                      | sum of first n positive integers, somme des n premiers entiers positifs, 前 n 个正整数和          | The finite sum of positive integers from `1` to `n`.                     | EN: `1+2+\cdots+100=5050`.<br>FR: `1+2+\cdots+100=5050`.<br>中：`1+2+\cdots+100=5050`。                                                                                   |
| `\sum_{k=1}^{n}k=\frac{n(n+1)}{2}`                  | triangular number formula, formule des nombres triangulaires, 三角数公式                         | A closed form for the sum of the first `n` positive integers.            | EN: `\sum_{k=1}^{10}k=55`.<br>FR: `\sum_{k=1}^{10}k=55`.<br>中：`\sum_{k=1}^{10}k=55`。                                                                                   |
| `\sum_{k=1}^{n}k^2=\frac{n(n+1)(2n+1)}{6}`          | sum of squares, somme des carrés, 平方和                                                       | A closed form for the sum of the first `n` squares.                      | EN: `1^2+2^2+3^2=14`.<br>FR: `1^2+2^2+3^2=14`.<br>中：`1^2+2^2+3^2=14`。                                                                                                  |
| `\sum_{k=1}^{n}k^3=\left(\frac{n(n+1)}{2}\right)^2` | sum of cubes, somme des cubes, 立方和                                                          | A closed form for the sum of the first `n` cubes.                        | EN: `1^3+2^3+3^3=36`.<br>FR: `1^3+2^3+3^3=36`.<br>中：`1^3+2^3+3^3=36`。                                                                                                  |
| `S_n=\frac{n(a_1+a_n)}{2}`                          | arithmetic series formula, formule d’une somme arithmétique, 等差数列求和公式                       | The sum of the first `n` terms of an arithmetic sequence.                | EN: The sum of `2,4,6,\ldots,20` is `110`.<br>FR: La somme de `2,4,6,\ldots,20` est `110`.<br>中：`2,4,6,\ldots,20` 的和是 `110`。                                           |
| `S_n=\frac{n}{2}[2a_1+(n-1)d]`                      | arithmetic sum formula with common difference, formule arithmétique avec raison, 含公差的等差求和公式 | An equivalent form of the arithmetic series formula using `a_1` and `d`. | EN: If `a_1=3`, `d=4`, and `n=5`, then `S_5=55`.<br>FR: Si `a_1=3`, `d=4` et `n=5`, alors `S_5=55`.<br>中：若 `a_1=3`、`d=4`、`n=5`，则 `S_5=55`。                             |
| `S_n=a_1\frac{1-r^n}{1-r}`                          | finite geometric series formula, formule d’une somme géométrique finie, 有限等比求和公式            | The sum of the first `n` terms of a geometric sequence when `r\neq 1`.   | EN: `1+2+4+8=15`.<br>FR: `1+2+4+8=15`.<br>中：`1+2+4+8=15`。                                                                                                              |
| `S_n=a_1n`                                          | constant geometric case, cas géométrique constant, 公比为一的等比求和                                | The geometric sum formula when the common ratio is `1`.                  | EN: If every term is `3`, then the sum of `5` terms is `15`.<br>FR: Si chaque terme vaut `3`, alors la somme de `5` termes vaut `15`.<br>中：若每一项都是 `3`，则 `5` 项之和为 `15`。 |
| `1-r^n`                                             | telescoping pattern, motif télescopique, 望远镜式结构                                             | A cancellation pattern where many intermediate terms disappear.          | EN: `(1-2)+(2-3)+(3-4)=1-4`.<br>FR: `(1-2)+(2-3)+(3-4)=1-4`.<br>中：`(1-2)+(2-3)+(3-4)=1-4`，中间项相互抵消。                                                                     |
| `\sum_{k=1}^{n}(b_k-b_{k+1})`                       | telescoping sum, somme télescopique, 望远镜求和                                                  | A sum whose consecutive terms cancel, leaving only boundary terms.       | EN: `\sum_{k=1}^{n}(1/k-1/(k+1))=1-\frac{1}{n+1}`.<br>FR: `\sum_{k=1}^{n}(1/k-1/(k+1))=1-\frac{1}{n+1}`.<br>中：`\sum_{k=1}^{n}(1/k-1/(k+1))=1-\frac{1}{n+1}`。           |

## 7.5 Infinite series and convergence

| Symbol (LaTeX)                             | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                | Definition                                                                    | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                              |
| ------------------------------------------ | ---------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `\sum_{n=1}^{\infty}a_n`                   | infinite series, série infinie, 无穷级数                             | A formal sum of infinitely many terms of a sequence.                          | EN: `\sum_{n=1}^{\infty}\frac{1}{2^n}` is an infinite series.<br>FR: `\sum_{n=1}^{\infty}\frac{1}{2^n}` est une série infinie.<br>中：`\sum_{n=1}^{\infty}\frac{1}{2^n}` 是无穷级数。                         |
| `S_N=\sum_{n=1}^{N}a_n`                    | partial sum, somme partielle, 部分和                                | The sum of the first `N` terms of a series.                                   | EN: For `\sum 1/2^n`, the third partial sum is `1/2+1/4+1/8`.<br>FR: Pour `\sum 1/2^n`, la troisième somme partielle est `1/2+1/4+1/8`.<br>中：对于 `\sum 1/2^n`，第三个部分和是 `1/2+1/4+1/8`。                   |
| `S_N\to S`                                 | convergent series, série convergente, 收敛级数                       | A series whose partial sums approach a finite limit.                          | EN: `\sum_{n=1}^{\infty}\frac{1}{2^n}=1`.<br>FR: `\sum_{n=1}^{\infty}\frac{1}{2^n}=1`.<br>中：`\sum_{n=1}^{\infty}\frac{1}{2^n}=1`。                                                                     |
| `S_N` diverges                             | divergent series, série divergente, 发散级数                         | A series whose partial sums do not approach a finite limit.                   | EN: `\sum_{n=1}^{\infty}1` diverges.<br>FR: `\sum_{n=1}^{\infty}1` diverge.<br>中：`\sum_{n=1}^{\infty}1` 发散。                                                                                           |
| `\sum_{n=0}^{\infty}ar^n`                  | infinite geometric series, série géométrique infinie, 无穷等比级数     | An infinite series with terms forming a geometric sequence.                   | EN: `1+\frac{1}{2}+\frac{1}{4}+\cdots` is an infinite geometric series.<br>FR: `1+\frac{1}{2}+\frac{1}{4}+\cdots` est une série géométrique infinie.<br>中：`1+\frac{1}{2}+\frac{1}{4}+\cdots` 是无穷等比级数。 |
| `\sum_{n=0}^{\infty}ar^n=\frac{a}{1-r}`    | geometric series sum, somme d’une série géométrique, 无穷等比级数和     | The sum of an infinite geometric series when `\lvert r\rvert<1`.              | EN: `1+\frac{1}{2}+\frac{1}{4}+\cdots=2`.<br>FR: `1+\frac{1}{2}+\frac{1}{4}+\cdots=2`.<br>中：`1+\frac{1}{2}+\frac{1}{4}+\cdots=2`。                                                                     |
| `\sum_{n=1}^{\infty}\frac{1}{n}`           | harmonic series, série harmonique, 调和级数                          | The infinite series formed by reciprocals of positive integers.               | EN: The harmonic series diverges.<br>FR: La série harmonique diverge.<br>中：调和级数发散。                                                                                                                    |
| `\sum_{n=1}^{\infty}\frac{1}{n^p}`         | p-series, série de Riemann, p 级数                                 | A series of reciprocal powers of positive integers.                           | EN: `\sum 1/n^2` converges, but `\sum 1/n` diverges.<br>FR: `\sum 1/n^2` converge, mais `\sum 1/n` diverge.<br>中：`\sum 1/n^2` 收敛，但 `\sum 1/n` 发散。                                                     |
| `a_n\to 0`                                 | term test condition, condition nécessaire de convergence, 通项趋零条件 | A necessary condition for convergence of a series.                            | EN: If `\sum a_n` converges, then `a_n\to 0`.<br>FR: Si `\sum a_n` converge, alors `a_n\to 0`.<br>中：若 `\sum a_n` 收敛，则 `a_n\to 0`。                                                                     |
| `\sum \lvert a_n\rvert`                    | absolute convergence, convergence absolue, 绝对收敛                  | A series is absolutely convergent if the series of absolute values converges. | EN: If `\sum \lvert a_n\rvert` converges, then `\sum a_n` converges.<br>FR: Si `\sum \lvert a_n\rvert` converge, alors `\sum a_n` converge.<br>中：若 `\sum \lvert a_n\rvert` 收敛，则 `\sum a_n` 收敛。        |
| `\sum a_n` but not `\sum \lvert a_n\rvert` | conditional convergence, convergence conditionnelle, 条件收敛        | Convergence of a series without absolute convergence.                         | EN: The alternating harmonic series converges conditionally.<br>FR: La série harmonique alternée converge conditionnellement.<br>中：交错调和级数条件收敛。                                                        |

## 7.6 Recurrence relations and discrete dynamics

| Symbol (LaTeX)        | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                           | Definition                                                                          | Examples                                                                                                                                                                            |
| --------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `a_{n+1}=F(a_n)`      | recurrence relation, relation de récurrence, 递推关系                           | An equation defining each term from previous terms.                                 | EN: `a_{n+1}=2a_n` defines a geometric sequence.<br>FR: `a_{n+1}=2a_n` définit une suite géométrique.<br>中：`a_{n+1}=2a_n` 定义一个等比数列。                                                 |
| `a_0`                 | initial condition, condition initiale, 初始条件                                 | A starting value needed to determine a recursively defined sequence.                | EN: `a_{n+1}=2a_n` needs an initial condition such as `a_0=1`.<br>FR: `a_{n+1}=2a_n` nécessite une condition initiale comme `a_0=1`.<br>中：`a_{n+1}=2a_n` 需要初始条件，例如 `a_0=1`。         |
| `a_{n+1}=a_n+d`       | first-order linear recurrence, récurrence linéaire du premier ordre, 一阶线性递推 | A recurrence where the next term depends linearly on the previous term.             | EN: `a_{n+1}=a_n+3` produces an arithmetic sequence.<br>FR: `a_{n+1}=a_n+3` produit une suite arithmétique.<br>中：`a_{n+1}=a_n+3` 生成等差数列。                                            |
| `a_{n+1}=ra_n`        | multiplicative recurrence, récurrence multiplicative, 乘法递推                  | A recurrence multiplying the current term by a fixed factor.                        | EN: `a_{n+1}=2a_n` doubles each term.<br>FR: `a_{n+1}=2a_n` double chaque terme.<br>中：`a_{n+1}=2a_n` 使每一项翻倍。                                                                        |
| `a_{n+2}=a_{n+1}+a_n` | second-order recurrence, récurrence d’ordre deux, 二阶递推                      | A recurrence where a term depends on the two previous terms.                        | EN: The Fibonacci recurrence is second-order.<br>FR: La récurrence de Fibonacci est d’ordre deux.<br>中：斐波那契递推是二阶递推。                                                                 |
| `F_{n+2}=F_{n+1}+F_n` | Fibonacci sequence, suite de Fibonacci, 斐波那契数列                              | A sequence where each term is the sum of the two preceding terms.                   | EN: With `F_0=0` and `F_1=1`, the sequence begins `0,1,1,2,3,5`.<br>FR: Avec `F_0=0` et `F_1=1`, la suite commence par `0,1,1,2,3,5`.<br>中：若 `F_0=0` 且 `F_1=1`，数列开头为 `0,1,1,2,3,5`。 |
| `a_{n+1}=a_n(1+r)`    | discrete growth model, modèle de croissance discrète, 离散增长模型                | A model where a quantity grows by a fixed percentage each step.                     | EN: A `5%` growth per period gives `a_{n+1}=1.05a_n`.<br>FR: Une croissance de `5%` par période donne `a_{n+1}=1,05a_n`.<br>中：每期增长 `5%` 可写为 `a_{n+1}=1.05a_n`。                      |
| `a_{n+1}=a_n(1-r)`    | discrete decay model, modèle de décroissance discrète, 离散衰减模型               | A model where a quantity decreases by a fixed percentage each step.                 | EN: A `10%` decrease per period gives `a_{n+1}=0.9a_n`.<br>FR: Une baisse de `10%` par période donne `a_{n+1}=0,9a_n`.<br>中：每期减少 `10%` 可写为 `a_{n+1}=0.9a_n`。                        |
| `a=F(a)`              | fixed point, point fixe, 不动点                                                | A value that remains unchanged under a recurrence rule.                             | EN: If `F(x)=x^2`, then `0` and `1` are fixed points.<br>FR: Si `F(x)=x^2`, alors `0` et `1` sont des points fixes.<br>中：若 `F(x)=x^2`，则 `0` 和 `1` 是不动点。                             |
| —                     | stability, stabilité, 稳定性                                                   | A property describing whether nearby values move toward or away from a fixed point. | EN: A stable fixed point attracts nearby iterates.<br>FR: Un point fixe stable attire les itérés voisins.<br>中：稳定不动点会吸引附近的迭代值。                                                      |

## 7.7 Sequences in finance and applications

| Symbol (LaTeX)                       | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                         | Definition                                                                | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| ------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `P`                                  | principal, capital initial, 本金                                            | The initial amount of money invested or borrowed.                         | EN: If the principal is `1000`, interest is calculated from `1000`.<br>FR: Si le capital initial est `1000`, les intérêts sont calculés à partir de `1000`.<br>中：若本金为 `1000`，利息以 `1000` 为基础计算。           |
| `r`                                  | interest rate, taux d’intérêt, 利率                                         | The proportion by which an amount grows over a period.                    | EN: A `5%` annual interest rate means `r=0.05`.<br>FR: Un taux d’intérêt annuel de `5%` signifie `r=0,05`.<br>中：年利率 `5%` 表示 `r=0.05`。                                                                    |
| `A=P(1+rt)`                          | simple interest, intérêt simple, 单利                                       | Interest calculated only on the original principal.                       | EN: With `P=1000`, `r=0.05`, and `t=2`, simple interest gives `A=1100`.<br>FR: Avec `P=1000`, `r=0,05` et `t=2`, l’intérêt simple donne `A=1100`.<br>中：若 `P=1000`、`r=0.05`、`t=2`，单利得到 `A=1100`。          |
| `A=P(1+r)^n`                         | compound interest, intérêt composé, 复利                                    | Interest calculated on both principal and accumulated interest.           | EN: With `P=1000`, `r=0.05`, and `n=2`, compound interest gives `A=1102.5`.<br>FR: Avec `P=1000`, `r=0,05` et `n=2`, l’intérêt composé donne `A=1102,5`.<br>中：若 `P=1000`、`r=0.05`、`n=2`，复利得到 `A=1102.5`。 |
| `A=P\left(1+\frac{r}{m}\right)^{mt}` | compound interest with m periods, intérêt composé m fois par an, 每年 m 次复利 | A formula for interest compounded `m` times per year.                     | EN: Monthly compounding uses `m=12`.<br>FR: La capitalisation mensuelle utilise `m=12`.<br>中：按月复利时 `m=12`。                                                                                               |
| `A=Pe^{rt}`                          | continuous compounding, capitalisation continue, 连续复利                     | The limiting form of compound interest as compounding becomes continuous. | EN: Continuous compounding with rate `r` gives `A=Pe^{rt}`.<br>FR: La capitalisation continue au taux `r` donne `A=Pe^{rt}`.<br>中：利率为 `r` 的连续复利公式是 `A=Pe^{rt}`。                                          |
| `A_n=PMT\frac{(1+r)^n-1}{r}`         | future value of an annuity, valeur acquise d’une annuité, 年金终值            | The accumulated value of equal payments made regularly with interest.     | EN: Regular monthly savings can be modeled by an annuity formula.<br>FR: Une épargne mensuelle régulière peut être modélisée par une formule d’annuité.<br>中：定期每月储蓄可用年金公式建模。                             |
| `PV=PMT\frac{1-(1+r)^{-n}}{r}`       | present value of an annuity, valeur actuelle d’une annuité, 年金现值          | The current value of a stream of equal future payments.                   | EN: Loan payments are often analyzed by present value formulas.<br>FR: Les remboursements d’un prêt sont souvent analysés par des formules de valeur actuelle.<br>中：贷款还款常用年金现值公式分析。                      |

## 7.8 Asymptotic growth of sequences

| Symbol (LaTeX)    | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                                    | Definition                                                                      | Examples                                                                                                                                                     |
| ----------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `a_n=O(b_n)`      | big-O notation, notation grand O, 大 O 记号                                             | Means `a_n` is bounded in magnitude by a constant multiple of `b_n` eventually. | EN: `3n^2+2n+1=O(n^2)`.<br>FR: `3n^2+2n+1=O(n^2)`.<br>中：`3n^2+2n+1=O(n^2)`。                                                                                  |
| `a_n=o(b_n)`      | little-o notation, notation petit o, 小 o 记号                                          | Means `a_n/b_n\to 0`.                                                           | EN: `n=o(n^2)`.<br>FR: `n=o(n^2)`.<br>中：`n=o(n^2)`。                                                                                                          |
| `a_n\sim b_n`     | asymptotic equivalence, équivalence asymptotique, 渐近等价                               | Means `a_n/b_n\to 1`.                                                           | EN: `n+1\sim n` as `n\to\infty`.<br>FR: `n+1\sim n` quand `n\to\infty`.<br>中：当 `n\to\infty` 时，`n+1\sim n`。                                                   |
| `a_n=\Theta(b_n)` | theta notation, notation thêta, Θ 记号                                                 | Means `a_n` and `b_n` have the same order of growth up to constant factors.     | EN: `5n^2+n=\Theta(n^2)`.<br>FR: `5n^2+n=\Theta(n^2)`.<br>中：`5n^2+n=\Theta(n^2)`。                                                                            |
| `a_n=\Omega(b_n)` | omega notation, notation oméga, Ω 记号                                                 | Means `a_n` is eventually bounded below by a constant multiple of `b_n`.        | EN: `n^2+n=\Omega(n^2)`.<br>FR: `n^2+n=\Omega(n^2)`.<br>中：`n^2+n=\Omega(n^2)`。                                                                               |
| `\limsup a_n`     | limit superior, limite supérieure, 上极限                                               | The largest limiting value approached by subsequences.                          | EN: For `a_n=(-1)^n`, `\limsup a_n=1`.<br>FR: Pour `a_n=(-1)^n`, `\limsup a_n=1`.<br>中：对于 `a_n=(-1)^n`，`\limsup a_n=1`。                                      |
| `\liminf a_n`     | limit inferior, limite inférieure, 下极限                                               | The smallest limiting value approached by subsequences.                         | EN: For `a_n=(-1)^n`, `\liminf a_n=-1`.<br>FR: Pour `a_n=(-1)^n`, `\liminf a_n=-1`.<br>中：对于 `a_n=(-1)^n`，`\liminf a_n=-1`。                                   |
| —                 | bounded sequence, suite bornée, 有界数列                                                 | A sequence whose terms stay within a fixed finite range.                        | EN: `a_n=\sin n` is bounded between `-1` and `1`.<br>FR: `a_n=\sin n` est bornée entre `-1` et `1`.<br>中：`a_n=\sin n` 被限制在 `-1` 和 `1` 之间。                    |
| —                 | monotone sequence, suite monotone, 单调数列                                              | A sequence that is always nondecreasing or always nonincreasing.                | EN: `a_n=1-\frac{1}{n}` is increasing for `n\geq 1`.<br>FR: `a_n=1-\frac{1}{n}` est croissante pour `n\geq 1`.<br>中：当 `n\geq 1` 时，`a_n=1-\frac{1}{n}` 是递增数列。 |
| —                 | monotone convergence theorem for sequences, théorème de convergence monotone, 单调收敛定理 | A bounded monotone real sequence converges.                                     | EN: Every increasing sequence bounded above has a limit.<br>FR: Toute suite croissante majorée converge.<br>中：每个有上界的递增实数列都收敛。                                |

# 8. Limits, Continuity, and Differential Calculus

## 8.1 Limits and limiting language

| Symbol (LaTeX)                            | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                | Definition                                                                                       | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| ----------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `\lim_{x\to a}f(x)`                       | limit, limite, 极限                                | The value that `f(x)` approaches as `x` approaches `a`, whether or not `f(a)` itself is defined. | EN: `\lim_{x\to 2}(x+1)=3`.<br>FR: `\lim_{x\to 2}(x+1)=3`.<br>中：`\lim_{x\to 2}(x+1)=3`。                                                                                                                                    |
| `x\to a`                                  | x approaches a, x tend vers a, x 趋于 a            | Indicates that the input variable gets arbitrarily close to `a`.                                 | EN: In `\lim_{x\to 0}\sin x`, `x` approaches `0`.<br>FR: Dans `\lim_{x\to 0}\sin x`, `x` tend vers `0`.<br>中：在 `\lim_{x\to 0}\sin x` 中，`x` 趋于 `0`。                                                                         |
| `\lim_{x\to a^-}f(x)`                     | left-hand limit, limite à gauche, 左极限            | The value approached by `f(x)` as `x` approaches `a` from values less than `a`.                  | EN: `\lim_{x\to 0^-}\operatorname{sgn}(x)=-1`.<br>FR: `\lim_{x\to 0^-}\operatorname{sgn}(x)=-1`.<br>中：`\lim_{x\to 0^-}\operatorname{sgn}(x)=-1`。                                                                           |
| `\lim_{x\to a^+}f(x)`                     | right-hand limit, limite à droite, 右极限           | The value approached by `f(x)` as `x` approaches `a` from values greater than `a`.               | EN: `\lim_{x\to 0^+}\operatorname{sgn}(x)=1`.<br>FR: `\lim_{x\to 0^+}\operatorname{sgn}(x)=1`.<br>中：`\lim_{x\to 0^+}\operatorname{sgn}(x)=1`。                                                                              |
| `\lim_{x\to a^-}f(x)=\lim_{x\to a^+}f(x)` | two-sided limit, limite bilatérale, 双侧极限         | A limit exists when the left-hand and right-hand limits exist and are equal.                     | EN: `\lim_{x\to 1}x^2=1` because both one-sided limits equal `1`.<br>FR: `\lim_{x\to 1}x^2=1`, car les deux limites latérales valent `1`.<br>中：`\lim_{x\to 1}x^2=1`，因为左右极限都等于 `1`。                                         |
| `\lim_{x\to\infty}f(x)`                   | limit at infinity, limite à l’infini, 无穷远处极限     | The value approached by a function as the input grows without bound.                             | EN: `\lim_{x\to\infty}\frac{1}{x}=0`.<br>FR: `\lim_{x\to\infty}\frac{1}{x}=0`.<br>中：`\lim_{x\to\infty}\frac{1}{x}=0`。                                                                                                      |
| `\lim_{x\to a}f(x)=\infty`                | infinite limit, limite infinie, 无穷极限             | A limit statement indicating that function values grow without bound near `a`.                   | EN: `\lim_{x\to 0^+}\frac{1}{x}=\infty`.<br>FR: `\lim_{x\to 0^+}\frac{1}{x}=\infty`.<br>中：`\lim_{x\to 0^+}\frac{1}{x}=\infty`。                                                                                             |
| `f(x)\to L`                               | tends to L, tend vers L, 趋于 L                    | A compact way of saying that the values of a function or sequence approach `L`.                  | EN: As `x\to 0`, `\sin x\to 0`.<br>FR: Quand `x\to 0`, `\sin x\to 0`.<br>中：当 `x\to 0` 时，`\sin x\to 0`。                                                                                                                     |
| `\epsilon`                                | epsilon, epsilon, ε / 任意小正数                      | A small positive number used to express closeness in rigorous limit definitions.                 | EN: Given any `\epsilon>0`, choose `\delta` small enough.<br>FR: Pour tout `\epsilon>0`, choisir `\delta` assez petit.<br>中：对任意 `\epsilon>0`，选择足够小的 `\delta`。                                                              |
| `\delta`                                  | delta, delta, δ / 输入邻域半径                         | A positive number controlling how close `x` must be to `a` in an epsilon-delta definition.       | EN: If `0<\lvert x-a\rvert<\delta`, then `\lvert f(x)-L\rvert<\epsilon`.<br>FR: Si `0<\lvert x-a\rvert<\delta`, alors `\lvert f(x)-L\rvert<\epsilon`.<br>中：若 `0<\lvert x-a\rvert<\delta`，则 `\lvert f(x)-L\rvert<\epsilon`。 |
| `o(1)`                                    | tends to zero term, terme tendant vers zéro, 趋零项 | A quantity that tends to zero in a specified limit process.                                      | EN: As `x\to 0`, `x=o(1)`.<br>FR: Quand `x\to 0`, `x=o(1)`.<br>中：当 `x\to 0` 时，`x=o(1)`。                                                                                                                                    |

## 8.2 Limit laws and standard limits

| Symbol (LaTeX)                          | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                             | Definition                                                                                       | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                         |
| --------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `\lim(f+g)=\lim f+\lim g`               | sum law for limits, règle de la somme, 极限加法法则                                 | The limit of a sum equals the sum of the limits when both limits exist.                          | EN: `\lim_{x\to 1}(x+x^2)=1+1=2`.<br>FR: `\lim_{x\to 1}(x+x^2)=1+1=2`.<br>中：`\lim_{x\to 1}(x+x^2)=1+1=2`。                                                                                        |
| `\lim(fg)=(\lim f)(\lim g)`             | product law for limits, règle du produit, 极限乘法法则                              | The limit of a product equals the product of the limits when both limits exist.                  | EN: `\lim_{x\to 2}x(x+1)=2\cdot3=6`.<br>FR: `\lim_{x\to 2}x(x+1)=2\cdot3=6`.<br>中：`\lim_{x\to 2}x(x+1)=2\cdot3=6`。                                                                               |
| `\lim\frac{f}{g}=\frac{\lim f}{\lim g}` | quotient law for limits, règle du quotient, 极限商法则                             | The limit of a quotient equals the quotient of the limits when the denominator limit is nonzero. | EN: `\lim_{x\to 1}\frac{x+1}{x+2}=\frac{2}{3}`.<br>FR: `\lim_{x\to 1}\frac{x+1}{x+2}=\frac{2}{3}`.<br>中：`\lim_{x\to 1}\frac{x+1}{x+2}=\frac{2}{3}`。                                              |
| `\lim c f=c\lim f`                      | constant multiple law, règle du facteur constant, 常数倍极限法则                     | A constant factor may be pulled outside a limit.                                                 | EN: `\lim_{x\to 3}5x=5\cdot3=15`.<br>FR: `\lim_{x\to 3}5x=5\cdot3=15`.<br>中：`\lim_{x\to 3}5x=5\cdot3=15`。                                                                                        |
| `\lim_{x\to a}p(x)=p(a)`                | polynomial limit law, limite d’un polynôme, 多项式极限法则                           | A polynomial limit is found by direct substitution.                                              | EN: `\lim_{x\to 2}(x^2+1)=5`.<br>FR: `\lim_{x\to 2}(x^2+1)=5`.<br>中：`\lim_{x\to 2}(x^2+1)=5`。                                                                                                    |
| `\lim_{x\to a}r(x)=r(a)`                | rational function limit law, limite d’une fonction rationnelle, 有理函数极限法则      | A rational function limit is found by direct substitution when the denominator is nonzero.       | EN: `\lim_{x\to 1}\frac{x+2}{x+3}=\frac{3}{4}`.<br>FR: `\lim_{x\to 1}\frac{x+2}{x+3}=\frac{3}{4}`.<br>中：`\lim_{x\to 1}\frac{x+2}{x+3}=\frac{3}{4}`。                                              |
| `\lim_{x\to 0}\frac{\sin x}{x}=1`       | fundamental trigonometric limit, limite trigonométrique fondamentale, 基本三角极限  | A standard limit central to differentiating trigonometric functions.                             | EN: `\lim_{x\to 0}\frac{\sin x}{x}=1` when `x` is measured in radians.<br>FR: `\lim_{x\to 0}\frac{\sin x}{x}=1` lorsque `x` est en radians.<br>中：当 `x` 用弧度表示时，`\lim_{x\to 0}\frac{\sin x}{x}=1`。 |
| `\lim_{x\to 0}\frac{1-\cos x}{x}=0`     | cosine small-angle limit, limite du cosinus près de zéro, 余弦小角极限              | A standard limit describing the first-order behavior of cosine near zero.                        | EN: `1-\cos x` is much smaller than `x` near `0`.<br>FR: `1-\cos x` est beaucoup plus petit que `x` près de `0`.<br>中：在 `0` 附近，`1-\cos x` 比 `x` 小得多。                                             |
| `\lim_{x\to 0}\frac{e^x-1}{x}=1`        | exponential fundamental limit, limite fondamentale de l’exponentielle, 指数基本极限 | A standard limit characterizing the natural exponential function.                                | EN: `\lim_{x\to 0}\frac{e^x-1}{x}=1`.<br>FR: `\lim_{x\to 0}\frac{e^x-1}{x}=1`.<br>中：`\lim_{x\to 0}\frac{e^x-1}{x}=1`。                                                                            |
| `\lim_{x\to 0}\frac{\ln(1+x)}{x}=1`     | logarithmic fundamental limit, limite fondamentale du logarithme, 对数基本极限      | A standard limit characterizing the natural logarithm near `1`.                                  | EN: `\ln(1+x)` behaves like `x` near `0`.<br>FR: `\ln(1+x)` se comporte comme `x` près de `0`.<br>中：在 `0` 附近，`\ln(1+x)` 与 `x` 渐近等价。                                                              |
| —                                       | squeeze theorem, théorème des gendarmes, 夹逼定理                                 | If a function is trapped between two functions with the same limit, it has that limit too.       | EN: The squeeze theorem proves `\lim_{x\to 0}x^2\sin(1/x)=0`.<br>FR: Le théorème des gendarmes prouve `\lim_{x\to 0}x^2\sin(1/x)=0`.<br>中：夹逼定理可证明 `\lim_{x\to 0}x^2\sin(1/x)=0`。                 |

## 8.3 Continuity and discontinuity

| Symbol (LaTeX)           | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                     | Definition                                                                          | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| ------------------------ | --------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `\lim_{x\to a}f(x)=f(a)` | continuity at a point, continuité en un point, 点连续                    | A function is continuous at `a` if its limit at `a` equals its value at `a`.        | EN: `f(x)=x^2` is continuous at every real `a`.<br>FR: `f(x)=x^2` est continue en tout réel `a`.<br>中：`f(x)=x^2` 在每个实数点都连续。                                                                     |
| —                        | continuous function, fonction continue, 连续函数                          | A function continuous at every point of its domain.                                 | EN: Polynomial functions are continuous on `\mathbb{R}`.<br>FR: Les fonctions polynomiales sont continues sur `\mathbb{R}`.<br>中：多项式函数在 `\mathbb{R}` 上连续。                                       |
| —                        | discontinuity, discontinuité, 间断点                                     | A point where a function fails to be continuous.                                    | EN: `f(x)=1/x` is discontinuous at `x=0` if considered near zero.<br>FR: `f(x)=1/x` est discontinue en `x=0` si on l’étudie près de zéro.<br>中：若考察 `0` 附近，`f(x)=1/x` 在 `x=0` 处间断。               |
| —                        | removable discontinuity, discontinuité amovible, 可去间断点                | A discontinuity where redefining the function at one point can make it continuous.  | EN: `\frac{x^2-1}{x-1}` has a removable discontinuity at `x=1`.<br>FR: `\frac{x^2-1}{x-1}` a une discontinuité amovible en `x=1`.<br>中：`\frac{x^2-1}{x-1}` 在 `x=1` 处有可去间断点。                     |
| —                        | jump discontinuity, discontinuité par saut, 跳跃间断点                     | A discontinuity where left-hand and right-hand limits exist but are unequal.        | EN: The sign function has a jump discontinuity at `0`.<br>FR: La fonction signe a une discontinuité par saut en `0`.<br>中：符号函数在 `0` 处有跳跃间断点。                                                    |
| —                        | infinite discontinuity, discontinuité infinie, 无穷间断点                  | A discontinuity where function values grow without bound near a point.              | EN: `f(x)=1/x^2` has an infinite discontinuity at `0`.<br>FR: `f(x)=1/x^2` a une discontinuité infinie en `0`.<br>中：`f(x)=1/x^2` 在 `0` 处有无穷间断点。                                                 |
| —                        | oscillatory discontinuity, discontinuité oscillante, 振荡间断点            | A discontinuity where function values oscillate without approaching a single limit. | EN: `f(x)=\sin(1/x)` oscillates near `0`.<br>FR: `f(x)=\sin(1/x)` oscille près de `0`.<br>中：`f(x)=\sin(1/x)` 在 `0` 附近振荡。                                                                        |
| `[a,b]`                  | closed interval continuity, continuité sur un intervalle fermé, 闭区间连续 | Continuity at all interior points and one-sided continuity at endpoints.            | EN: `f(x)=x^2` is continuous on `[0,1]`.<br>FR: `f(x)=x^2` est continue sur `[0,1]`.<br>中：`f(x)=x^2` 在 `[0,1]` 上连续。                                                                             |
| —                        | intermediate value theorem, théorème des valeurs intermédiaires, 介值定理 | A continuous function on `[a,b]` takes every value between `f(a)` and `f(b)`.       | EN: If `f(0)<0` and `f(1)>0`, continuity implies a zero in `(0,1)`.<br>FR: Si `f(0)<0` et `f(1)>0`, la continuité implique un zéro dans `(0,1)`.<br>中：若 `f(0)<0` 且 `f(1)>0`，连续性推出 `(0,1)` 内有零点。 |
| —                        | extreme value theorem, théorème des bornes atteintes, 最值定理            | A continuous function on a closed bounded interval attains a maximum and a minimum. | EN: A continuous function on `[a,b]` has both a maximum and a minimum.<br>FR: Une fonction continue sur `[a,b]` atteint un maximum et un minimum.<br>中：闭区间 `[a,b]` 上的连续函数一定取得最大值和最小值。           |

## 8.4 Derivatives and rates of change

| Symbol (LaTeX)                                  | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                 | Definition                                                                | Examples                                                                                                                                                                 |
| ----------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `f'(a)`                                         | derivative at a point, dérivée en un point, 一点处导数                 | The instantaneous rate of change of `f` at `a`, when the limit exists.    | EN: If `f(x)=x^2`, then `f'(3)=6`.<br>FR: Si `f(x)=x^2`, alors `f'(3)=6`.<br>中：若 `f(x)=x^2`，则 `f'(3)=6`。                                                                 |
| `f'(x)`                                         | derivative function, fonction dérivée, 导函数                        | The function assigning to each point the derivative of `f` at that point. | EN: If `f(x)=x^2`, then `f'(x)=2x`.<br>FR: Si `f(x)=x^2`, alors `f'(x)=2x`.<br>中：若 `f(x)=x^2`，则 `f'(x)=2x`。                                                              |
| `\frac{d}{dx}`                                  | differentiation operator, opérateur de dérivation, 求导算子           | An operator indicating differentiation with respect to `x`.               | EN: `\frac{d}{dx}x^3=3x^2`.<br>FR: `\frac{d}{dx}x^3=3x^2`.<br>中：`\frac{d}{dx}x^3=3x^2`。                                                                                  |
| `\frac{dy}{dx}`                                 | derivative notation, notation de Leibniz, 导数记号                    | Leibniz notation for the derivative of `y` with respect to `x`.           | EN: If `y=x^2`, then `\frac{dy}{dx}=2x`.<br>FR: Si `y=x^2`, alors `\frac{dy}{dx}=2x`.<br>中：若 `y=x^2`，则 `\frac{dy}{dx}=2x`。                                               |
| `\frac{f(a+h)-f(a)}{h}`                         | difference quotient, quotient différentiel, 差商                    | The average rate of change over a small input change `h`.                 | EN: For `f(x)=x^2`, the difference quotient at `a` is `2a+h`.<br>FR: Pour `f(x)=x^2`, le quotient différentiel en `a` est `2a+h`.<br>中：对于 `f(x)=x^2`，在 `a` 处的差商是 `2a+h`。 |
| `f'(a)=\lim_{h\to 0}\frac{f(a+h)-f(a)}{h}`      | derivative definition, définition de la dérivée, 导数定义             | The derivative defined as the limit of the difference quotient.           | EN: The derivative is the limit of average rates of change.<br>FR: La dérivée est la limite des taux de variation moyens.<br>中：导数是平均变化率的极限。                              |
| `\frac{\Delta y}{\Delta x}`                     | average rate of change, taux de variation moyen, 平均变化率            | The ratio of output change to input change over an interval.              | EN: On `[1,3]`, `f(x)=x^2` has average rate of change `4`.<br>FR: Sur `[1,3]`, `f(x)=x^2` a un taux de variation moyen de `4`.<br>中：`f(x)=x^2` 在 `[1,3]` 上的平均变化率是 `4`。   |
| `\lim_{\Delta x\to 0}\frac{\Delta y}{\Delta x}` | instantaneous rate of change, taux de variation instantané, 瞬时变化率 | The limiting rate of change at a single point.                            | EN: Velocity is the instantaneous rate of change of position.<br>FR: La vitesse est le taux de variation instantané de la position.<br>中：速度是位置关于时间的瞬时变化率。                |
| —                                               | tangent line, tangente, 切线                                        | A line whose slope at a point equals the derivative there.                | EN: The tangent line to `y=x^2` at `x=1` has slope `2`.<br>FR: La tangente à `y=x^2` en `x=1` a pour pente `2`.<br>中：`y=x^2` 在 `x=1` 处的切线斜率是 `2`。                        |
| `m=f'(a)`                                       | slope of the tangent, pente de la tangente, 切线斜率                  | The slope of the tangent line to a graph at a point.                      | EN: If `f'(a)=3`, the tangent slope at `a` is `3`.<br>FR: Si `f'(a)=3`, la pente de la tangente en `a` est `3`.<br>中：若 `f'(a)=3`，则 `a` 处切线斜率为 `3`。                       |

## 8.5 Differentiability and local behavior

| Symbol (LaTeX) | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                              | Definition                                                                                    | Examples                                                                                                                                                                     |
| -------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| —              | differentiable function, fonction dérivable, 可导函数                              | A function whose derivative exists at points in its domain.                                   | EN: `f(x)=x^2` is differentiable on `\mathbb{R}`.<br>FR: `f(x)=x^2` est dérivable sur `\mathbb{R}`.<br>中：`f(x)=x^2` 在 `\mathbb{R}` 上可导。                                      |
| —              | differentiability at a point, dérivabilité en un point, 一点处可导                  | A function is differentiable at a point if its derivative exists there.                       | EN: `f(x)=\lvert x\rvert` is not differentiable at `0`.<br>FR: `f(x)=\lvert x\rvert` n’est pas dérivable en `0`.<br>中：`f(x)=\lvert x\rvert` 在 `0` 处不可导。                      |
| —              | differentiability implies continuity, dérivabilité implique continuité, 可导推出连续 | If a function is differentiable at a point, it is continuous there.                           | EN: If `f` is differentiable at `a`, then `f` is continuous at `a`.<br>FR: Si `f` est dérivable en `a`, alors `f` est continue en `a`.<br>中：若 `f` 在 `a` 处可导，则 `f` 在 `a` 处连续。 |
| —              | corner, point anguleux, 角点                                                     | A point where one-sided slopes are finite but unequal.                                        | EN: `f(x)=\lvert x\rvert` has a corner at `0`.<br>FR: `f(x)=\lvert x\rvert` a un point anguleux en `0`.<br>中：`f(x)=\lvert x\rvert` 在 `0` 处有角点。                               |
| —              | cusp, rebroussement, 尖点                                                        | A sharp point where slopes may become unbounded or direction changes abruptly.                | EN: `f(x)=x^{2/3}` has a cusp-like point at `0`.<br>FR: `f(x)=x^{2/3}` a un point de rebroussement près de `0`.<br>中：`f(x)=x^{2/3}` 在 `0` 附近有尖点。                             |
| —              | vertical tangent, tangente verticale, 垂直切线                                     | A tangent line with undefined or infinite slope.                                              | EN: `y=\sqrt[3]{x}` has a vertical tangent at `0`.<br>FR: `y=\sqrt[3]{x}` a une tangente verticale en `0`.<br>中：`y=\sqrt[3]{x}` 在 `0` 处有垂直切线。                                |
| `f'(x)>0`      | increasing by derivative, croissance par la dérivée, 导数判增                      | A positive derivative on an interval usually indicates that the function is increasing there. | EN: If `f'(x)>0` on an interval, then `f` is increasing there.<br>FR: Si `f'(x)>0` sur un intervalle, alors `f` y est croissante.<br>中：若 `f'(x)>0` 在某区间成立，则 `f` 在该区间递增。      |
| `f'(x)<0`      | decreasing by derivative, décroissance par la dérivée, 导数判减                    | A negative derivative on an interval usually indicates that the function is decreasing there. | EN: If `f'(x)<0` on an interval, then `f` is decreasing there.<br>FR: Si `f'(x)<0` sur un intervalle, alors `f` y est décroissante.<br>中：若 `f'(x)<0` 在某区间成立，则 `f` 在该区间递减。    |
| `f'(c)=0`      | stationary point, point stationnaire, 驻点                                       | A point where the derivative equals zero.                                                     | EN: For `f(x)=x^2`, `x=0` is a stationary point.<br>FR: Pour `f(x)=x^2`, `x=0` est un point stationnaire.<br>中：对于 `f(x)=x^2`，`x=0` 是驻点。                                      |
| —              | critical point, point critique, 临界点                                            | A point where the derivative is zero or undefined, often relevant for extrema.                | EN: Critical points are candidates for local maxima and minima.<br>FR: Les points critiques sont des candidats pour les maxima et minima locaux.<br>中：临界点是局部最大值和局部最小值的候选点。   |

## 8.6 Differentiation rules

| Symbol (LaTeX)                                  | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                                     | Definition                                                                                     | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
| ----------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `\frac{d}{dx}c=0`                               | constant rule, règle de la constante, 常数求导法则                                          | The derivative of a constant is zero.                                                          | EN: `\frac{d}{dx}7=0`.<br>FR: `\frac{d}{dx}7=0`.<br>中：`\frac{d}{dx}7=0`。                                                                                                                                             |
| `\frac{d}{dx}x^n=nx^{n-1}`                      | power rule, règle de puissance, 幂函数求导法则                                               | A rule for differentiating powers of `x`.                                                      | EN: `\frac{d}{dx}x^5=5x^4`.<br>FR: `\frac{d}{dx}x^5=5x^4`.<br>中：`\frac{d}{dx}x^5=5x^4`。                                                                                                                              |
| `(cf)'=cf'`                                     | constant multiple rule, règle du facteur constant, 常数倍求导法则                            | A constant factor can be pulled outside differentiation.                                       | EN: `\frac{d}{dx}(3x^2)=6x`.<br>FR: `\frac{d}{dx}(3x^2)=6x`.<br>中：`\frac{d}{dx}(3x^2)=6x`。                                                                                                                           |
| `(f+g)'=f'+g'`                                  | sum rule, règle de la somme, 和的求导法则                                                   | The derivative of a sum is the sum of the derivatives.                                         | EN: `\frac{d}{dx}(x^2+\sin x)=2x+\cos x`.<br>FR: `\frac{d}{dx}(x^2+\sin x)=2x+\cos x`.<br>中：`\frac{d}{dx}(x^2+\sin x)=2x+\cos x`。                                                                                    |
| `(f-g)'=f'-g'`                                  | difference rule, règle de la différence, 差的求导法则                                       | The derivative of a difference is the difference of the derivatives.                           | EN: `\frac{d}{dx}(x^3-x)=3x^2-1`.<br>FR: `\frac{d}{dx}(x^3-x)=3x^2-1`.<br>中：`\frac{d}{dx}(x^3-x)=3x^2-1`。                                                                                                            |
| `(fg)'=f'g+fg'`                                 | product rule, règle du produit, 乘积求导法则                                                | The derivative of a product combines derivatives of both factors.                              | EN: `\frac{d}{dx}(x^2\sin x)=2x\sin x+x^2\cos x`.<br>FR: `\frac{d}{dx}(x^2\sin x)=2x\sin x+x^2\cos x`.<br>中：`\frac{d}{dx}(x^2\sin x)=2x\sin x+x^2\cos x`。                                                            |
| `\left(\frac{f}{g}\right)'=\frac{f'g-fg'}{g^2}` | quotient rule, règle du quotient, 商的求导法则                                              | The derivative rule for a quotient, valid where `g\neq 0`.                                     | EN: `\frac{d}{dx}\frac{x}{x+1}=\frac{1}{(x+1)^2}`.<br>FR: `\frac{d}{dx}\frac{x}{x+1}=\frac{1}{(x+1)^2}`.<br>中：`\frac{d}{dx}\frac{x}{x+1}=\frac{1}{(x+1)^2}`。                                                         |
| `(f\circ g)'=(f'\circ g)g'`                     | chain rule, règle de la chaîne, 链式法则                                                  | The derivative rule for composite functions.                                                   | EN: `\frac{d}{dx}\sin(x^2)=2x\cos(x^2)`.<br>FR: `\frac{d}{dx}\sin(x^2)=2x\cos(x^2)`.<br>中：`\frac{d}{dx}\sin(x^2)=2x\cos(x^2)`。                                                                                       |
| `\frac{dy}{dx}=\frac{dy}{du}\frac{du}{dx}`      | chain rule in Leibniz notation, règle de la chaîne en notation de Leibniz, 莱布尼茨记号链式法则 | A notation expressing how derivatives multiply through an intermediate variable.               | EN: If `y=\sin u` and `u=x^2`, then `dy/dx=2x\cos(x^2)`.<br>FR: Si `y=\sin u` et `u=x^2`, alors `dy/dx=2x\cos(x^2)`.<br>中：若 `y=\sin u` 且 `u=x^2`，则 `dy/dx=2x\cos(x^2)`。                                              |
| —                                               | logarithmic differentiation, dérivation logarithmique, 对数求导                           | A method using logarithms to differentiate products, quotients, powers, or variable exponents. | EN: Logarithmic differentiation helps differentiate `x^x`.<br>FR: La dérivation logarithmique aide à dériver `x^x`.<br>中：对数求导可用于求 `x^x` 的导数。                                                                         |
| —                                               | implicit differentiation, dérivation implicite, 隐函数求导                                 | Differentiating an equation where `y` is defined implicitly as a function of `x`.              | EN: From `x^2+y^2=1`, implicit differentiation gives `2x+2y\frac{dy}{dx}=0`.<br>FR: À partir de `x^2+y^2=1`, la dérivation implicite donne `2x+2y\frac{dy}{dx}=0`.<br>中：由 `x^2+y^2=1` 隐函数求导得 `2x+2y\frac{dy}{dx}=0`。 |

## 8.7 Derivatives of common functions

| Symbol (LaTeX)                                  | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                                 | Definition                                                                | Examples                                                                                                                                                           |
| ----------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `\frac{d}{dx}e^x=e^x`                           | derivative of exponential, dérivée de l’exponentielle, 自然指数函数导数                   | The natural exponential function is its own derivative.                   | EN: `\frac{d}{dx}e^x=e^x`.<br>FR: `\frac{d}{dx}e^x=e^x`.<br>中：`\frac{d}{dx}e^x=e^x`。                                                                               |
| `\frac{d}{dx}a^x=a^x\ln a`                      | derivative of general exponential, dérivée de l’exponentielle de base a, 一般指数函数导数 | The derivative of an exponential with positive base `a`.                  | EN: `\frac{d}{dx}2^x=2^x\ln 2`.<br>FR: `\frac{d}{dx}2^x=2^x\ln 2`.<br>中：`\frac{d}{dx}2^x=2^x\ln 2`。                                                                |
| `\frac{d}{dx}\ln x=\frac{1}{x}`                 | derivative of natural logarithm, dérivée du logarithme naturel, 自然对数导数            | The derivative of `\ln x` for `x>0`.                                      | EN: `\frac{d}{dx}\ln x=\frac{1}{x}`.<br>FR: `\frac{d}{dx}\ln x=\frac{1}{x}`.<br>中：`\frac{d}{dx}\ln x=\frac{1}{x}`。                                                 |
| `\frac{d}{dx}\log_a x=\frac{1}{x\ln a}`         | derivative of logarithm base a, dérivée du logarithme en base a, a 底对数导数          | The derivative of the logarithm with base `a`, where `a>0` and `a\neq 1`. | EN: `\frac{d}{dx}\log_2 x=\frac{1}{x\ln 2}`.<br>FR: `\frac{d}{dx}\log_2 x=\frac{1}{x\ln 2}`.<br>中：`\frac{d}{dx}\log_2 x=\frac{1}{x\ln 2}`。                         |
| `\frac{d}{dx}\sin x=\cos x`                     | derivative of sine, dérivée du sinus, 正弦导数                                        | The derivative of sine when `x` is measured in radians.                   | EN: `\frac{d}{dx}\sin x=\cos x`.<br>FR: `\frac{d}{dx}\sin x=\cos x`.<br>中：`\frac{d}{dx}\sin x=\cos x`。                                                             |
| `\frac{d}{dx}\cos x=-\sin x`                    | derivative of cosine, dérivée du cosinus, 余弦导数                                    | The derivative of cosine when `x` is measured in radians.                 | EN: `\frac{d}{dx}\cos x=-\sin x`.<br>FR: `\frac{d}{dx}\cos x=-\sin x`.<br>中：`\frac{d}{dx}\cos x=-\sin x`。                                                          |
| `\frac{d}{dx}\tan x=\sec^2 x`                   | derivative of tangent, dérivée de la tangente, 正切导数                               | The derivative of tangent where tangent is defined.                       | EN: `\frac{d}{dx}\tan x=\sec^2 x`.<br>FR: `\frac{d}{dx}\tan x=\sec^2 x`.<br>中：`\frac{d}{dx}\tan x=\sec^2 x`。                                                       |
| `\frac{d}{dx}\arcsin x=\frac{1}{\sqrt{1-x^2}}`  | derivative of arcsine, dérivée de l’arcsinus, 反正弦导数                               | The derivative of arcsine for `-1<x<1`.                                   | EN: `\frac{d}{dx}\arcsin x=\frac{1}{\sqrt{1-x^2}}`.<br>FR: `\frac{d}{dx}\arcsin x=\frac{1}{\sqrt{1-x^2}}`.<br>中：`\frac{d}{dx}\arcsin x=\frac{1}{\sqrt{1-x^2}}`。    |
| `\frac{d}{dx}\arccos x=-\frac{1}{\sqrt{1-x^2}}` | derivative of arccosine, dérivée de l’arccosinus, 反余弦导数                           | The derivative of arccosine for `-1<x<1`.                                 | EN: `\frac{d}{dx}\arccos x=-\frac{1}{\sqrt{1-x^2}}`.<br>FR: `\frac{d}{dx}\arccos x=-\frac{1}{\sqrt{1-x^2}}`.<br>中：`\frac{d}{dx}\arccos x=-\frac{1}{\sqrt{1-x^2}}`。 |
| `\frac{d}{dx}\arctan x=\frac{1}{1+x^2}`         | derivative of arctangent, dérivée de l’arctangente, 反正切导数                         | The derivative of arctangent for all real `x`.                            | EN: `\frac{d}{dx}\arctan x=\frac{1}{1+x^2}`.<br>FR: `\frac{d}{dx}\arctan x=\frac{1}{1+x^2}`.<br>中：`\frac{d}{dx}\arctan x=\frac{1}{1+x^2}`。                         |
| `\frac{d}{dx}\lvert x\rvert`                    | derivative of absolute value, dérivée de la valeur absolue, 绝对值函数导数               | The derivative is `1` for `x>0`, `-1` for `x<0`, and undefined at `0`.    | EN: `\lvert x\rvert` is not differentiable at `0`.<br>FR: `\lvert x\rvert` n’est pas dérivable en `0`.<br>中：`\lvert x\rvert` 在 `0` 处不可导。                           |

## 8.8 Higher derivatives and curve shape

| Symbol (LaTeX)           | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                                       | Definition                                                                                  | Examples                                                                                                                                   |
| ------------------------ | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `f''(x)`                 | second derivative, dérivée seconde, 二阶导数                                                | The derivative of the derivative, often measuring concavity or acceleration.                | EN: If `f(x)=x^3`, then `f''(x)=6x`.<br>FR: Si `f(x)=x^3`, alors `f''(x)=6x`.<br>中：若 `f(x)=x^3`，则 `f''(x)=6x`。                             |
| `f^{(n)}(x)`             | nth derivative, dérivée n-ième, n 阶导数                                                   | The derivative obtained after differentiating `n` times.                                    | EN: For `f(x)=e^x`, every derivative is `e^x`.<br>FR: Pour `f(x)=e^x`, toutes les dérivées valent `e^x`.<br>中：对于 `f(x)=e^x`，任意阶导数都是 `e^x`。 |
| `\frac{d^2y}{dx^2}`      | second derivative notation, notation de la dérivée seconde, 二阶导数记号                      | Leibniz notation for the second derivative of `y` with respect to `x`.                      | EN: If `y=x^2`, then `\frac{d^2y}{dx^2}=2`.<br>FR: Si `y=x^2`, alors `\frac{d^2y}{dx^2}=2`.<br>中：若 `y=x^2`，则 `\frac{d^2y}{dx^2}=2`。        |
| `f''(x)>0`               | concave up, convexe, 上凸 / 凹向上                                                           | A graph bends upward when the second derivative is positive.                                | EN: `f(x)=x^2` is concave up on `\mathbb{R}`.<br>FR: `f(x)=x^2` est convexe sur `\mathbb{R}`.<br>中：`f(x)=x^2` 在 `\mathbb{R}` 上凹向上。         |
| `f''(x)<0`               | concave down, concave, 下凸 / 凹向下                                                         | A graph bends downward when the second derivative is negative.                              | EN: `f(x)=-x^2` is concave down on `\mathbb{R}`.<br>FR: `f(x)=-x^2` est concave sur `\mathbb{R}`.<br>中：`f(x)=-x^2` 在 `\mathbb{R}` 上凹向下。    |
| —                        | inflection point, point d’inflexion, 拐点                                                 | A point where concavity changes.                                                            | EN: `f(x)=x^3` has an inflection point at `0`.<br>FR: `f(x)=x^3` a un point d’inflexion en `0`.<br>中：`f(x)=x^3` 在 `0` 处有拐点。                |
| `f'(c)=0` and `f''(c)>0` | second derivative test for minimum, test de la dérivée seconde pour un minimum, 二阶导数判极小 | A criterion for a local minimum when the second derivative is positive at a critical point. | EN: For `f(x)=x^2`, `x=0` is a local minimum.<br>FR: Pour `f(x)=x^2`, `x=0` est un minimum local.<br>中：对于 `f(x)=x^2`，`x=0` 是局部极小点。         |
| `f'(c)=0` and `f''(c)<0` | second derivative test for maximum, test de la dérivée seconde pour un maximum, 二阶导数判极大 | A criterion for a local maximum when the second derivative is negative at a critical point. | EN: For `f(x)=-x^2`, `x=0` is a local maximum.<br>FR: Pour `f(x)=-x^2`, `x=0` est un maximum local.<br>中：对于 `f(x)=-x^2`，`x=0` 是局部极大点。      |
| —                        | acceleration, accélération, 加速度                                                         | The second derivative of position with respect to time.                                     | EN: If `s(t)=t^2`, then acceleration is `2`.<br>FR: Si `s(t)=t^2`, alors l’accélération vaut `2`.<br>中：若 `s(t)=t^2`，则加速度为 `2`。             |

## 8.9 Mean value theorem and derivative theorems

| Symbol (LaTeX)                | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                 | Definition                                                                                                      | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
| ----------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| —                             | Rolle’s theorem, théorème de Rolle, 罗尔定理                          | If `f` is continuous on `[a,b]`, differentiable on `(a,b)`, and `f(a)=f(b)`, then some `c` satisfies `f'(c)=0`. | EN: Rolle’s theorem guarantees a horizontal tangent somewhere between equal endpoint values.<br>FR: Le théorème de Rolle garantit une tangente horizontale entre deux valeurs terminales égales.<br>中：罗尔定理保证在两个端点函数值相等时，中间存在水平切线。 |
| `f'(c)=\frac{f(b)-f(a)}{b-a}` | mean value theorem, théorème des accroissements finis, 拉格朗日中值定理   | If `f` is continuous on `[a,b]` and differentiable on `(a,b)`, some tangent slope equals the secant slope.      | EN: The mean value theorem links average rate and instantaneous rate.<br>FR: Le théorème des accroissements finis relie taux moyen et taux instantané.<br>中：中值定理把平均变化率与瞬时变化率联系起来。                                                 |
| `\frac{f(b)-f(a)}{b-a}`       | secant slope, pente de la sécante, 割线斜率                           | The average rate of change between two points on a graph.                                                       | EN: The secant slope of `x^2` from `1` to `3` is `4`.<br>FR: La pente de la sécante de `x^2` de `1` à `3` vaut `4`.<br>中：`x^2` 从 `1` 到 `3` 的割线斜率是 `4`。                                                                            |
| `f'(c)`                       | tangent slope, pente de la tangente, 切线斜率                         | The instantaneous slope at a point.                                                                             | EN: At `x=2`, the tangent slope of `x^2` is `4`.<br>FR: En `x=2`, la pente de la tangente à `x^2` vaut `4`.<br>中：在 `x=2` 处，`x^2` 的切线斜率是 `4`。                                                                                      |
| —                             | increasing function test, critère de croissance, 递增判别法            | If `f'(x)>0` on an interval, then `f` is increasing there.                                                      | EN: Since `\frac{d}{dx}e^x=e^x>0`, `e^x` is increasing.<br>FR: Puisque `\frac{d}{dx}e^x=e^x>0`, `e^x` est croissante.<br>中：因为 `\frac{d}{dx}e^x=e^x>0`，所以 `e^x` 递增。                                                                |
| —                             | decreasing function test, critère de décroissance, 递减判别法          | If `f'(x)<0` on an interval, then `f` is decreasing there.                                                      | EN: Since `\frac{d}{dx}(-x)=-1`, `-x` is decreasing.<br>FR: Puisque `\frac{d}{dx}(-x)=-1`, `-x` est décroissante.<br>中：因为 `\frac{d}{dx}(-x)=-1`，所以 `-x` 递减。                                                                       |
| —                             | first derivative test, test de la dérivée première, 一阶导数判别法       | A method using sign changes of `f'` to classify local extrema.                                                  | EN: If `f'` changes from positive to negative, `f` has a local maximum.<br>FR: Si `f'` passe du positif au négatif, `f` a un maximum local.<br>中：若 `f'` 从正变负，则 `f` 有局部极大值。                                                        |
| —                             | Fermat’s theorem on stationary points, théorème de Fermat, 费马驻点定理 | If a differentiable function has a local extremum at an interior point, its derivative there is zero.           | EN: A differentiable local maximum inside the domain must satisfy `f'(c)=0`.<br>FR: Un maximum local dérivable à l’intérieur du domaine vérifie `f'(c)=0`.<br>中：可导函数若在内部点取得局部极值，则该点导数为 `0`。                                       |

## 8.10 Linear approximation and differentials

| Symbol (LaTeX)                | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                               | Definition                                                                 | Examples                                                                                                                                                                           |
| ----------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `L(x)=f(a)+f'(a)(x-a)`        | linear approximation, approximation linéaire, 线性近似              | An approximation of a function near `a` by its tangent line.               | EN: Near `0`, `e^x\approx 1+x`.<br>FR: Près de `0`, `e^x\approx 1+x`.<br>中：在 `0` 附近，`e^x\approx 1+x`。                                                                              |
| —                             | tangent line approximation, approximation par la tangente, 切线近似 | Using the tangent line to approximate nearby function values.              | EN: Use the tangent line to approximate `\sqrt{4.1}` near `4`.<br>FR: Utiliser la tangente pour approximer `\sqrt{4,1}` près de `4`.<br>中：可用切线近似估计 `\sqrt{4.1}`。                   |
| `dy=f'(x)\,dx`                | differential, différentielle, 微分                                | A linear approximation to the change in `y` caused by a small change `dx`. | EN: If `y=x^2`, then `dy=2x\,dx`.<br>FR: Si `y=x^2`, alors `dy=2x\,dx`.<br>中：若 `y=x^2`，则 `dy=2x\,dx`。                                                                              |
| `dx`                          | differential of x, différentielle de x, x 的微分                   | A small change in the independent variable.                                | EN: In `dy=f'(x)\,dx`, `dx` represents a small input change.<br>FR: Dans `dy=f'(x)\,dx`, `dx` représente une petite variation de l’entrée.<br>中：在 `dy=f'(x)\,dx` 中，`dx` 表示自变量的小变化。 |
| `dy`                          | differential of y, différentielle de y, y 的微分                   | The approximate change in `y` predicted by the tangent line.               | EN: For small `dx`, `dy` approximates `\Delta y`.<br>FR: Pour un petit `dx`, `dy` approxime `\Delta y`.<br>中：当 `dx` 很小时，`dy` 近似 `\Delta y`。                                        |
| `\Delta y`                    | actual change in y, variation réelle de y, y 的实际变化              | The exact change in output over a finite input change.                     | EN: `\Delta y=f(x+\Delta x)-f(x)`.<br>FR: `\Delta y=f(x+\Delta x)-f(x)`.<br>中：`\Delta y=f(x+\Delta x)-f(x)`。                                                                       |
| `R(x)`                        | remainder, reste, 余项                                            | The error left after an approximation.                                     | EN: In a linear approximation, the remainder measures the error.<br>FR: Dans une approximation linéaire, le reste mesure l’erreur.<br>中：在线性近似中，余项表示误差。                             |
| `f(x)=f(a)+f'(a)(x-a)+o(x-a)` | first-order expansion, développement au premier ordre, 一阶展开     | A local representation of a differentiable function near `a`.              | EN: Near `a`, a differentiable function is approximately linear.<br>FR: Près de `a`, une fonction dérivable est approximativement linéaire.<br>中：在 `a` 附近，可导函数近似为线性函数。             |

## 8.11 Optimization and applied derivatives

| Symbol (LaTeX)  | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                                     | Definition                                                                  | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| --------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| —               | optimization, optimisation, 最优化                                                       | The process of finding maximum or minimum values under given conditions.    | EN: Minimizing cost is an optimization problem.<br>FR: Minimiser le coût est un problème d’optimisation.<br>中：最小化成本是一个最优化问题。                                                                    |
| —               | objective function, fonction objectif, 目标函数                                           | The function to be maximized or minimized.                                  | EN: In area maximization, area is the objective function.<br>FR: Dans une maximisation d’aire, l’aire est la fonction objectif.<br>中：在面积最大化问题中，面积是目标函数。                                         |
| —               | constraint, contrainte, 约束条件                                                          | A condition limiting the possible values in an optimization problem.        | EN: Fixed perimeter is a constraint in maximizing area.<br>FR: Un périmètre fixé est une contrainte dans la maximisation de l’aire.<br>中：固定周长是面积最大化问题中的约束条件。                                    |
| —               | local maximum, maximum local, 局部最大值                                                   | A value greater than or equal to nearby function values.                    | EN: `f(x)=-x^2` has a local maximum at `x=0`.<br>FR: `f(x)=-x^2` a un maximum local en `x=0`.<br>中：`f(x)=-x^2` 在 `x=0` 处有局部最大值。                                                                 |
| —               | local minimum, minimum local, 局部最小值                                                   | A value less than or equal to nearby function values.                       | EN: `f(x)=x^2` has a local minimum at `x=0`.<br>FR: `f(x)=x^2` a un minimum local en `x=0`.<br>中：`f(x)=x^2` 在 `x=0` 处有局部最小值。                                                                    |
| —               | absolute maximum, maximum global, 全局最大值                                               | The greatest value of a function over its entire domain or a specified set. | EN: On `[0,1]`, `f(x)=x^2` has absolute maximum `1`.<br>FR: Sur `[0,1]`, `f(x)=x^2` a pour maximum global `1`.<br>中：在 `[0,1]` 上，`f(x)=x^2` 的全局最大值是 `1`。                                         |
| —               | absolute minimum, minimum global, 全局最小值                                               | The smallest value of a function over its entire domain or a specified set. | EN: On `[0,1]`, `f(x)=x^2` has absolute minimum `0`.<br>FR: Sur `[0,1]`, `f(x)=x^2` a pour minimum global `0`.<br>中：在 `[0,1]` 上，`f(x)=x^2` 的全局最小值是 `0`。                                         |
| `f'(c)=0`       | first-order condition, condition du premier ordre, 一阶必要条件                             | A necessary condition for an interior differentiable extremum.              | EN: At an interior differentiable optimum, usually `f'(c)=0`.<br>FR: À un optimum intérieur dérivable, on a généralement `f'(c)=0`.<br>中：在内部可导极值点，通常有 `f'(c)=0`。                                |
| `f''(c)>0`      | second-order condition for minimum, condition du second ordre pour un minimum, 二阶极小条件 | A sufficient local minimum condition at a critical point.                   | EN: If `f'(c)=0` and `f''(c)>0`, then `c` is a local minimum.<br>FR: Si `f'(c)=0` et `f''(c)>0`, alors `c` est un minimum local.<br>中：若 `f'(c)=0` 且 `f''(c)>0`，则 `c` 是局部极小点。                    |
| `f''(c)<0`      | second-order condition for maximum, condition du second ordre pour un maximum, 二阶极大条件 | A sufficient local maximum condition at a critical point.                   | EN: If `f'(c)=0` and `f''(c)<0`, then `c` is a local maximum.<br>FR: Si `f'(c)=0` et `f''(c)<0`, alors `c` est un maximum local.<br>中：若 `f'(c)=0` 且 `f''(c)<0`，则 `c` 是局部极大点。                    |
| —               | related rates, taux liés, 相关变化率                                                       | Problems involving rates of change of related quantities.                   | EN: If a circle’s radius changes over time, its area changes at a related rate.<br>FR: Si le rayon d’un cercle varie dans le temps, son aire varie à un taux lié.<br>中：若圆的半径随时间变化，则面积也以相关变化率变化。 |
| `\frac{dA}{dt}` | rate with respect to time, taux par rapport au temps, 对时间的变化率                         | A derivative measuring how a quantity changes over time.                    | EN: If `A=\pi r^2`, then `\frac{dA}{dt}=2\pi r\frac{dr}{dt}`.<br>FR: Si `A=\pi r^2`, alors `\frac{dA}{dt}=2\pi r\frac{dr}{dt}`.<br>中：若 `A=\pi r^2`，则 `\frac{dA}{dt}=2\pi r\frac{dr}{dt}`。       |
# 9. Integral Calculus, Accumulation, and Applications

## 9.1 Antiderivatives and indefinite integrals

| Symbol (LaTeX)                             | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                          | Definition                                                                             | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                     |
| ------------------------------------------ | -------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `F'(x)=f(x)`                               | antiderivative, primitive, 原函数                                             | A function `F` whose derivative is `f`.                                                | EN: Since `\frac{d}{dx}x^2=2x`, `x^2` is an antiderivative of `2x`.<br>FR: Comme `\frac{d}{dx}x^2=2x`, `x^2` est une primitive de `2x`.<br>中：因为 `\frac{d}{dx}x^2=2x`，所以 `x^2` 是 `2x` 的一个原函数。 |
| `\int f(x)\,dx`                            | indefinite integral, intégrale indéfinie, 不定积分                             | The family of all antiderivatives of `f`.                                              | EN: `\int 2x\,dx=x^2+C`.<br>FR: `\int 2x\,dx=x^2+C`.<br>中：`\int 2x\,dx=x^2+C`。                                                                                                               |
| `\int`                                     | integral sign, signe intégral, 积分号                                         | The symbol indicating integration.                                                     | EN: The symbol `\int` comes from a long form of `S`, for sum.<br>FR: Le symbole `\int` vient d’une forme allongée de `S`, pour somme.<br>中：积分号 `\int` 与“求和”的记号来源有关。                          |
| `f(x)` in `\int f(x)\,dx`                  | integrand, intégrande, 被积函数                                                | The function or expression being integrated.                                           | EN: In `\int x^2\,dx`, the integrand is `x^2`.<br>FR: Dans `\int x^2\,dx`, l’intégrande est `x^2`.<br>中：在 `\int x^2\,dx` 中，被积函数是 `x^2`。                                                      |
| `dx`                                       | differential of integration, différentielle d’intégration, 积分变量微分          | Indicates the variable of integration and the infinitesimal direction of accumulation. | EN: In `\int x^2\,dx`, integration is with respect to `x`.<br>FR: Dans `\int x^2\,dx`, on intègre par rapport à `x`.<br>中：在 `\int x^2\,dx` 中，积分变量是 `x`。                                      |
| `C`                                        | constant of integration, constante d’intégration, 积分常数                     | An arbitrary constant added because antiderivatives differ by constants.               | EN: `\int \cos x\,dx=\sin x+C`.<br>FR: `\int \cos x\,dx=\sin x+C`.<br>中：`\int \cos x\,dx=\sin x+C`。                                                                                          |
| `\int x^n\,dx=\frac{x^{n+1}}{n+1}+C`       | power rule for integration, règle de puissance pour l’intégration, 幂函数积分法则 | A rule for integrating powers of `x`, valid for `n\neq -1`.                            | EN: `\int x^3\,dx=\frac{x^4}{4}+C`.<br>FR: `\int x^3\,dx=\frac{x^4}{4}+C`.<br>中：`\int x^3\,dx=\frac{x^4}{4}+C`。                                                                              |
| `\int \frac{1}{x}\,dx=\ln\lvert x\rvert+C` | logarithmic integral, intégrale logarithmique, 对数型积分                       | The antiderivative of `1/x` on intervals not containing zero.                          | EN: `\int \frac{1}{x}\,dx=\ln\lvert x\rvert+C`.<br>FR: `\int \frac{1}{x}\,dx=\ln\lvert x\rvert+C`.<br>中：`\int \frac{1}{x}\,dx=\ln\lvert x\rvert+C`。                                          |
