| —                                                    | simply connected domain, domaine simplement connexe, 单连通区域                        | A connected region with no holes, informally.                                           | EN: A disk is simply connected, but an annulus is not.<br>FR: Un disque est simplement connexe, mais un anneau ne l’est pas.<br>中：圆盘是单连通的，圆环不是单连通的。                                                      |
| —                                                    | exact differential form, forme différentielle exacte, 恰当微分形式                      | A differential form that is the differential of some scalar potential.                  | EN: `2x\,dx+2y\,dy` is exact because it equals `d(x^2+y^2)`.<br>FR: `2x\,dx+2y\,dy` est exacte car elle vaut `d(x^2+y^2)`.<br>中：`2x\,dx+2y\,dy` 是恰当的，因为它等于 `d(x^2+y^2)`。                                 |

## 10.8 Divergence, curl, and vector calculus operators

| Symbol (LaTeX)                          | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                     | Definition                                                                              | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| --------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `\nabla`                                | del operator, opérateur nabla, nabla 算子                               | A vector differential operator used to define gradient, divergence, and curl.           | EN: `\nabla` acts like a vector of partial derivative operators.<br>FR: `\nabla` agit comme un vecteur d’opérateurs de dérivation partielle.<br>中：`\nabla` 可看作由偏导算子组成的向量。                     |
| `\nabla f`                              | gradient, gradient, 梯度                                                | The vector field of first derivatives of a scalar function.                             | EN: `\nabla f` points in the direction of steepest increase.<br>FR: `\nabla f` pointe dans la direction de croissance maximale.<br>中：`\nabla f` 指向函数增长最快的方向。                                  |
| `\nabla\cdot\mathbf{F}`                 | divergence, divergence, 散度                                            | A scalar measuring local source or sink strength of a vector field.                     | EN: Positive divergence indicates local outflow.<br>FR: Une divergence positive indique un écoulement sortant local.<br>中：正散度表示局部向外流出。                                                        |
| `\nabla\times\mathbf{F}`                | curl, rotationnel, 旋度                                                 | A vector measuring local rotational tendency of a vector field.                         | EN: Curl measures local spinning of a vector field.<br>FR: Le rotationnel mesure la rotation locale d’un champ de vecteurs.<br>中：旋度度量向量场的局部旋转趋势。                                              |
| `\nabla^2 f`                            | Laplacian, laplacien, 拉普拉斯算子                                          | The divergence of the gradient, often written as the sum of second partial derivatives. | EN: In two variables, `\nabla^2 f=f_{xx}+f_{yy}`.<br>FR: En deux variables, `\nabla^2 f=f_{xx}+f_{yy}`.<br>中：在二元情形，`\nabla^2 f=f_{xx}+f_{yy}`。                                                |
| `\nabla\cdot(\nabla\times\mathbf{F})=0` | divergence of curl identity, identité divergence-rotationnel, 旋度散度恒等式 | The divergence of a curl is zero under smoothness assumptions.                          | EN: `\nabla\cdot(\nabla\times\mathbf{F})=0` for smooth fields.<br>FR: `\nabla\cdot(\nabla\times\mathbf{F})=0` pour les champs réguliers.<br>中：对光滑向量场，`\nabla\cdot(\nabla\times\mathbf{F})=0`。 |
| `\nabla\times(\nabla f)=\mathbf{0}`     | curl of gradient identity, identité rotationnel-gradient, 梯度旋度恒等式     | The curl of a gradient is zero under smoothness assumptions.                            | EN: Gradient fields are irrotational.<br>FR: Les champs de gradient sont irrotationnels.<br>中：梯度场是无旋场。                                                                                        |
| `\nabla\cdot\mathbf{F}=0`               | divergence-free field, champ à divergence nulle, 无散场                  | A vector field with zero divergence.                                                    | EN: An incompressible fluid velocity field is often divergence-free.<br>FR: Un champ de vitesse d’un fluide incompressible est souvent à divergence nulle.<br>中：不可压流体的速度场通常是无散场。              |
| `\nabla\times\mathbf{F}=\mathbf{0}`     | irrotational field, champ irrotationnel, 无旋场                          | A vector field with zero curl.                                                          | EN: A conservative field is irrotational on suitable domains.<br>FR: Un champ conservatif est irrotationnel sur des domaines appropriés.<br>中：在适当区域上，保守场是无旋场。                                 |
| `\mathbf{n}`                            | normal vector, vecteur normal, 法向量                                    | A vector perpendicular to a surface.                                                    | EN: A plane has a constant normal vector.<br>FR: Un plan possède un vecteur normal constant.<br>中：平面有固定的法向量。                                                                                  |

## 10.9 Surface integrals and flux

| Symbol (LaTeX)                                       | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                        | Definition                                                               | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| ---------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------ | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `\iint_S f\,dS`                                      | scalar surface integral, intégrale de surface scalaire, 标量曲面积分           | An integral of a scalar function over a surface.                         | EN: `\iint_S 1\,dS` gives the area of surface `S`.<br>FR: `\iint_S 1\,dS` donne l’aire de la surface `S`.<br>中：`\iint_S 1\,dS` 给出曲面 `S` 的面积。                                                                                            |
| `dS`                                                 | surface area element, élément d’aire de surface, 曲面面积元                   | An infinitesimal element of surface area.                                | EN: `dS` measures a tiny piece of surface area.<br>FR: `dS` mesure un petit élément d’aire de surface.<br>中：`dS` 表示曲面上的微小面积元。                                                                                                           |
| `\iint_S \mathbf{F}\cdot\mathbf{n}\,dS`              | flux integral, intégrale de flux, 通量积分                                   | An integral measuring how much a vector field passes through a surface.  | EN: Fluid flow through a membrane can be modeled by a flux integral.<br>FR: L’écoulement d’un fluide à travers une membrane peut être modélisé par une intégrale de flux.<br>中：流体穿过膜面的流量可用通量积分建模。                                       |
| `\mathbf{n}\,dS`                                     | oriented surface element, élément de surface orienté, 定向曲面元              | A vector area element combining magnitude and normal direction.          | EN: `\mathbf{n}\,dS` points normal to the surface.<br>FR: `\mathbf{n}\,dS` pointe normalement à la surface.<br>中：`\mathbf{n}\,dS` 指向曲面的法向。                                                                                              |
| —                                                    | orientation of a surface, orientation d’une surface, 曲面定向                | A consistent choice of normal direction on a surface.                    | EN: A closed surface is often oriented outward.<br>FR: Une surface fermée est souvent orientée vers l’extérieur.<br>中：闭曲面通常取外法向。                                                                                                        |
| `\partial E`                                         | boundary surface, surface frontière, 边界曲面                                | The surface forming the boundary of a three-dimensional region.          | EN: If `E` is a ball, `\partial E` is a sphere.<br>FR: Si `E` est une boule, `\partial E` est une sphère.<br>中：若 `E` 是球体，则 `\partial E` 是球面。                                                                                            |
| `S:\mathbf{r}(u,v)`                                  | parametrized surface, surface paramétrée, 参数曲面                           | A surface described by two parameters.                                   | EN: A parametrized surface uses `\mathbf{r}(u,v)`.<br>FR: Une surface paramétrée utilise `\mathbf{r}(u,v)`.<br>中：参数曲面用 `\mathbf{r}(u,v)` 描述。                                                                                            |
| `\lVert\mathbf{r}_u\times\mathbf{r}_v\rVert\,du\,dv` | parametrized surface element, élément d’aire paramétré, 参数曲面面积元          | The area scaling factor for a parametrized surface.                      | EN: The surface element is `\lVert\mathbf{r}_u\times\mathbf{r}_v\rVert\,du\,dv`.<br>FR: L’élément d’aire est `\lVert\mathbf{r}_u\times\mathbf{r}_v\rVert\,du\,dv`.<br>中：参数曲面的面积元是 `\lVert\mathbf{r}_u\times\mathbf{r}_v\rVert\,du\,dv`。 |
| `\mathbf{r}_u\times\mathbf{r}_v`                     | normal from parametrization, normale issue de la paramétrisation, 参数化法向量 | A normal vector obtained from tangent vectors of a parametrized surface. | EN: `\mathbf{r}_u\times\mathbf{r}_v` is perpendicular to the surface patch.<br>FR: `\mathbf{r}_u\times\mathbf{r}_v` est perpendiculaire au morceau de surface.<br>中：`\mathbf{r}_u\times\mathbf{r}_v` 垂直于曲面片。                            |
| —                                                    | outward normal, normale extérieure, 外法向量                                 | The normal direction pointing out of a closed surface.                   | EN: For a sphere, the outward normal points away from the center.<br>FR: Pour une sphère, la normale extérieure pointe loin du centre.<br>中：对球面，外法向量指向远离球心的方向。                                                                          |

## 10.10 Green, Stokes, and divergence theorems

| Symbol (LaTeX)                                                                                    | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                                                | Definition                                                                                            | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                            |
| ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `\oint_C P\,dx+Q\,dy=\iint_D(Q_x-P_y)\,dA`                                                        | Green’s theorem, théorème de Green, Green 定理                                                     | A theorem relating circulation around a plane curve to a double integral over the region it encloses. | EN: Green’s theorem converts a closed line integral into a double integral.<br>FR: Le théorème de Green transforme une intégrale curviligne fermée en intégrale double.<br>中：Green 定理把闭路线积分转化为二重积分。 |
| `C=\partial D`                                                                                    | positively oriented boundary, frontière orientée positivement, 正向边界                              | The boundary orientation that keeps the region on the left as one travels along the curve.            | EN: For a simple plane region, positive orientation is counterclockwise.<br>FR: Pour un domaine plan simple, l’orientation positive est antihoraire.<br>中：对简单平面区域，正向通常是逆时针。                         |
| `\iint_D(Q_x-P_y)\,dA`                                                                            | scalar curl in the plane, rotationnel scalaire dans le plan, 平面标量旋度                              | The scalar quantity measuring rotation in a two-dimensional vector field.                             | EN: For `\mathbf{F}=(P,Q)`, the scalar curl is `Q_x-P_y`.<br>FR: Pour `\mathbf{F}=(P,Q)`, le rotationnel scalaire est `Q_x-P_y`.<br>中：对 `\mathbf{F}=(P,Q)`，平面标量旋度是 `Q_x-P_y`。                       |
| `\iint_D(P_x+Q_y)\,dA`                                                                            | flux form of Green’s theorem, forme flux du théorème de Green, Green 定理通量形式                      | A form of Green’s theorem relating flux through a boundary to divergence over a region.               | EN: The flux form uses `P_x+Q_y`.<br>FR: La forme flux utilise `P_x+Q_y`.<br>中：通量形式使用 `P_x+Q_y`。                                                                                                    |
| `\int_{\partial S}\mathbf{F}\cdot d\mathbf{r}=\iint_S(\nabla\times\mathbf{F})\cdot\mathbf{n}\,dS` | Stokes’ theorem, théorème de Stokes, Stokes 定理                                                   | A theorem relating circulation along a boundary curve to curl through a surface.                      | EN: Stokes’ theorem generalizes Green’s theorem to surfaces in space.<br>FR: Le théorème de Stokes généralise le théorème de Green aux surfaces dans l’espace.<br>中：Stokes 定理把 Green 定理推广到空间曲面。     |
| `\partial S`                                                                                      | boundary curve of a surface, bord d’une surface, 曲面边界曲线                                          | The curve forming the boundary of an oriented surface.                                                | EN: A disk has a circular boundary curve.<br>FR: Un disque a un cercle comme bord.<br>中：圆盘的边界曲线是圆。                                                                                                  |
| `\iiint_E\nabla\cdot\mathbf{F}\,dV=\iint_{\partial E}\mathbf{F}\cdot\mathbf{n}\,dS`               | divergence theorem, théorème de la divergence, 散度定理                                              | A theorem relating flux through a closed surface to divergence inside the enclosed region.            | EN: The divergence theorem converts outward flux into a volume integral.<br>FR: Le théorème de la divergence transforme le flux sortant en intégrale de volume.<br>中：散度定理把外通量转化为体积分。                |
| `\partial E`                                                                                      | closed boundary surface, surface fermée frontière, 闭边界曲面                                         | The closed surface enclosing a solid region.                                                          | EN: The boundary of a solid ball is a sphere.<br>FR: La frontière d’une boule solide est une sphère.<br>中：实心球的边界是球面。                                                                                |
| —                                                                                                 | outward flux, flux sortant, 外通量                                                                  | The flux measured using the outward normal direction.                                                 | EN: Positive outward flux means net flow leaves the region.<br>FR: Un flux sortant positif signifie qu’un flux net quitte la région.<br>中：正外通量表示净流量流出区域。                                            |
| —                                                                                                 | fundamental theorem for line integrals, théorème fondamental des intégrales curvilignes, 线积分基本定理 | A theorem evaluating line integrals of gradient fields by potential differences.                      | EN: It is the line-integral analogue of the fundamental theorem of calculus.<br>FR: C’est l’analogue curviligne du théorème fondamental de l’analyse.<br>中：它是微积分基本定理在线积分中的对应形式。                     |

## 10.11 Multivariable optimization

| Symbol (LaTeX)               | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                                      | Definition                                                                                           | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| ---------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `\nabla f=\mathbf{0}`        | first-order condition, condition du premier ordre, 一阶必要条件                              | A necessary condition for an interior differentiable local extremum.                                 | EN: For `f(x,y)=x^2+y^2`, `\nabla f=\mathbf{0}` at `(0,0)`.<br>FR: Pour `f(x,y)=x^2+y^2`, `\nabla f=\mathbf{0}` en `(0,0)`.<br>中：对于 `f(x,y)=x^2+y^2`，在 `(0,0)` 处 `\nabla f=\mathbf{0}`。                            |
| —                            | critical point, point critique, 临界点                                                    | A point where the gradient is zero or undefined.                                                     | EN: Critical points are candidates for local extrema.<br>FR: Les points critiques sont des candidats aux extrema locaux.<br>中：临界点是局部极值的候选点。                                                                        |
| —                            | local maximum, maximum local, 局部最大值                                                    | A value greater than nearby values of the function.                                                  | EN: A hilltop on a surface is a local maximum.<br>FR: Un sommet local d’une surface est un maximum local.<br>中：曲面上的局部山顶是局部最大值。                                                                                     |
| —                            | local minimum, minimum local, 局部最小值                                                    | A value smaller than nearby values of the function.                                                  | EN: A bowl bottom is a local minimum.<br>FR: Le fond d’un bol est un minimum local.<br>中：碗底形状的最低点是局部最小值。                                                                                                           |
| —                            | saddle point, point selle, 鞍点                                                          | A critical point that is not a local maximum or local minimum.                                       | EN: `f(x,y)=x^2-y^2` has a saddle point at `(0,0)`.<br>FR: `f(x,y)=x^2-y^2` a un point selle en `(0,0)`.<br>中：`f(x,y)=x^2-y^2` 在 `(0,0)` 处有鞍点。                                                                     |
| `D=f_{xx}f_{yy}-f_{xy}^2`    | second derivative test discriminant, discriminant du test de la dérivée seconde, 二阶判别式 | A quantity used to classify critical points of functions of two variables.                           | EN: If `D>0` and `f_{xx}>0`, the critical point is a local minimum.<br>FR: Si `D>0` et `f_{xx}>0`, le point critique est un minimum local.<br>中：若 `D>0` 且 `f_{xx}>0`，临界点是局部极小点。                                    |
| `H_f`                        | Hessian in optimization, hessienne en optimisation, 最优化中的 Hessian                      | The second-derivative matrix used to analyze curvature near a critical point.                        | EN: A positive definite Hessian indicates a local minimum.<br>FR: Une hessienne définie positive indique un minimum local.<br>中：正定 Hessian 表示局部极小值。                                                                |
| —                            | constrained optimization, optimisation sous contrainte, 约束优化                           | Optimization subject to one or more constraints.                                                     | EN: Maximizing area with fixed perimeter is constrained optimization.<br>FR: Maximiser une aire avec un périmètre fixé est une optimisation sous contrainte.<br>中：在固定周长下最大化面积是约束优化。                                |
| `g(x,y)=c`                   | constraint equation, équation de contrainte, 约束方程                                      | An equation restricting the allowed input points.                                                    | EN: The constraint `x^2+y^2=1` restricts points to the unit circle.<br>FR: La contrainte `x^2+y^2=1` restreint les points au cercle unité.<br>中：约束 `x^2+y^2=1` 将点限制在单位圆上。                                          |
| `\nabla f=\lambda\nabla g`   | Lagrange multiplier condition, condition du multiplicateur de Lagrange, 拉格朗日乘子条件       | A condition for constrained extrema where gradients are parallel.                                    | EN: Use `\nabla f=\lambda\nabla g` to optimize `f` subject to `g=c`.<br>FR: Utiliser `\nabla f=\lambda\nabla g` pour optimiser `f` sous la contrainte `g=c`.<br>中：在约束 `g=c` 下优化 `f`，可用 `\nabla f=\lambda\nabla g`。 |
| `\lambda`                    | Lagrange multiplier, multiplicateur de Lagrange, 拉格朗日乘子                                | An auxiliary variable measuring the relation between the objective gradient and constraint gradient. | EN: The multiplier `\lambda` appears in constrained optimization equations.<br>FR: Le multiplicateur `\lambda` apparaît dans les équations d’optimisation sous contrainte.<br>中：乘子 `\lambda` 出现在约束优化方程中。           |
| `\mathcal{L}=f-\lambda(g-c)` | Lagrangian, lagrangien, 拉格朗日函数                                                         | An auxiliary function combining objective and constraints.                                           | EN: Critical points of `\mathcal{L}` give candidates for constrained extrema.<br>FR: Les points critiques de `\mathcal{L}` donnent des candidats aux extrema sous contrainte.<br>中：`\mathcal{L}` 的临界点给出约束极值的候选点。   |

## 10.12 Taylor approximation in several variables

| Symbol (LaTeX)                                                                | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                                  | Definition                                                                                         | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                         |
| ----------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `f(\mathbf{x})`                                                               | multivariable function notation, notation de fonction multivariable, 多元函数记号        | A compact notation for a function whose input is a vector.                                         | EN: Write `f(\mathbf{x})` when `\mathbf{x}=(x_1,\ldots,x_n)`.<br>FR: On écrit `f(\mathbf{x})` lorsque `\mathbf{x}=(x_1,\ldots,x_n)`.<br>中：当 `\mathbf{x}=(x_1,\ldots,x_n)` 时，可写作 `f(\mathbf{x})`。 |
| `f(\mathbf{a})+\nabla f(\mathbf{a})\cdot(\mathbf{x}-\mathbf{a})`              | first-order Taylor approximation, approximation de Taylor d’ordre un, 一阶 Taylor 近似 | A linear approximation of a multivariable function near a point.                                   | EN: First-order Taylor approximation is the tangent-plane approximation.<br>FR: L’approximation de Taylor d’ordre un est l’approximation par plan tangent.<br>中：一阶 Taylor 近似就是切平面近似。             |
| `\frac{1}{2}(\mathbf{x}-\mathbf{a})^T H_f(\mathbf{a})(\mathbf{x}-\mathbf{a})` | quadratic Taylor term, terme quadratique de Taylor, Taylor 二次项                     | The second-order term involving the Hessian matrix.                                                | EN: The quadratic term describes curvature near the point.<br>FR: Le terme quadratique décrit la courbure près du point.<br>中：二次项描述点附近的曲率。                                                       |
| `R_n`                                                                         | remainder term, reste, 余项                                                          | The error term left after truncating a Taylor expansion.                                           | EN: The remainder measures the error of the approximation.<br>FR: Le reste mesure l’erreur de l’approximation.<br>中：余项度量近似误差。                                                                    |
| —                                                                             | local approximation, approximation locale, 局部近似                                    | An approximation valid near a chosen point.                                                        | EN: Taylor expansion gives local information near `\mathbf{a}`.<br>FR: Le développement de Taylor donne une information locale près de `\mathbf{a}`.<br>中：Taylor 展开给出 `\mathbf{a}` 附近的局部信息。      |
| —                                                                             | quadratic approximation, approximation quadratique, 二次近似                           | A second-order approximation using gradient and Hessian information.                               | EN: Quadratic approximation is central in optimization algorithms.<br>FR: L’approximation quadratique est centrale dans les algorithmes d’optimisation.<br>中：二次近似在最优化算法中很重要。                     |
| —                                                                             | convex function, fonction convexe, 凸函数                                             | A function whose graph lies below chords connecting points on the graph, in the appropriate sense. | EN: `f(x,y)=x^2+y^2` is convex.<br>FR: `f(x,y)=x^2+y^2` est convexe.<br>中：`f(x,y)=x^2+y^2` 是凸函数。                                                                                                 |
| —                                                                             | positive definite Hessian, hessienne définie positive, 正定 Hessian                  | A Hessian matrix indicating positive curvature in every direction.                                 | EN: A positive definite Hessian at a critical point gives a local minimum.<br>FR: Une hessienne définie positive en un point critique donne un minimum local.<br>中：临界点处 Hessian 正定意味着局部极小。       |
| —                                                                             | negative definite Hessian, hessienne définie négative, 负定 Hessian                  | A Hessian matrix indicating negative curvature in every direction.                                 | EN: A negative definite Hessian at a critical point gives a local maximum.<br>FR: Une hessienne définie négative en un point critique donne un maximum local.<br>中：临界点处 Hessian 负定意味着局部极大。       |
| —                                                                             | indefinite Hessian, hessienne indéfinie, 不定 Hessian                                | A Hessian matrix with both positive and negative curvature directions.                             | EN: An indefinite Hessian often indicates a saddle point.<br>FR: Une hessienne indéfinie indique souvent un point selle.<br>中：Hessian 不定通常表示鞍点。                                                  |
# 11. Linear Algebra, Matrices, and Vector Spaces

## 11.1 Vectors and basic vector operations

| Symbol (LaTeX)                                               | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                         | Definition                                                                                                            | Examples                                                                                                                                                                            |
| ------------------------------------------------------------ | --------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `\mathbf{v}`                                                 | vector, vecteur, 向量                                       | An object with components that can represent magnitude and direction, or more generally an element of a vector space. | EN: `\mathbf{v}=(3,4)` is a vector in `\mathbb{R}^2`.<br>FR: `\mathbf{v}=(3,4)` est un vecteur de `\mathbb{R}^2`.<br>中：`\mathbf{v}=(3,4)` 是 `\mathbb{R}^2` 中的向量。                    |
| `v_i`                                                        | component, composante, 分量                                 | One coordinate entry of a vector.                                                                                     | EN: In `\mathbf{v}=(3,4,5)`, the second component is `4`.<br>FR: Dans `\mathbf{v}=(3,4,5)`, la deuxième composante est `4`.<br>中：在 `\mathbf{v}=(3,4,5)` 中，第二个分量是 `4`。               |
| `\mathbf{0}`                                                 | zero vector, vecteur nul, 零向量                             | The vector whose components are all zero.                                                                             | EN: In `\mathbb{R}^3`, the zero vector is `(0,0,0)`.<br>FR: Dans `\mathbb{R}^3`, le vecteur nul est `(0,0,0)`.<br>中：在 `\mathbb{R}^3` 中，零向量是 `(0,0,0)`。                              |
| `\mathbf{u}+\mathbf{v}`                                      | vector addition, addition vectorielle, 向量加法               | Addition of vectors by adding corresponding components.                                                               | EN: `(1,2)+(3,4)=(4,6)`.<br>FR: `(1,2)+(3,4)=(4,6)`.<br>中：`(1,2)+(3,4)=(4,6)`。                                                                                                      |
| `\mathbf{u}-\mathbf{v}`                                      | vector subtraction, soustraction vectorielle, 向量减法        | Subtraction of vectors by subtracting corresponding components.                                                       | EN: `(5,7)-(2,3)=(3,4)`.<br>FR: `(5,7)-(2,3)=(3,4)`.<br>中：`(5,7)-(2,3)=(3,4)`。                                                                                                      |
| `c\mathbf{v}`                                                | scalar multiplication, multiplication par un scalaire, 数乘 | Multiplication of a vector by a scalar.                                                                               | EN: `2(3,4)=(6,8)`.<br>FR: `2(3,4)=(6,8)`.<br>中：`2(3,4)=(6,8)`。                                                                                                                     |
| `-\mathbf{v}`                                                | additive inverse, opposé d’un vecteur, 相反向量               | The vector that adds to `\mathbf{v}` to give the zero vector.                                                         | EN: If `\mathbf{v}=(3,-1)`, then `-\mathbf{v}=(-3,1)`.<br>FR: Si `\mathbf{v}=(3,-1)`, alors `-\mathbf{v}=(-3,1)`.<br>中：若 `\mathbf{v}=(3,-1)`，则 `-\mathbf{v}=(-3,1)`。                |
| `\lVert\mathbf{v}\rVert`                                     | norm, norme, 范数 / 向量长度                                    | A measure of the size or length of a vector.                                                                          | EN: If `\mathbf{v}=(3,4)`, then `\lVert\mathbf{v}\rVert=5`.<br>FR: Si `\mathbf{v}=(3,4)`, alors `\lVert\mathbf{v}\rVert=5`.<br>中：若 `\mathbf{v}=(3,4)`，则 `\lVert\mathbf{v}\rVert=5`。 |
| `\hat{\mathbf{u}}`                                           | unit vector, vecteur unitaire, 单位向量                       | A vector with norm `1`.                                                                                               | EN: `(\frac{3}{5},\frac{4}{5})` is a unit vector.<br>FR: `(\frac{3}{5},\frac{4}{5})` est un vecteur unitaire.<br>中：`(\frac{3}{5},\frac{4}{5})` 是单位向量。                               |
| `\frac{\mathbf{v}}{\lVert\mathbf{v}\rVert}`                  | normalization, normalisation, 单位化 / 归一化                   | The process of turning a nonzero vector into a unit vector in the same direction.                                     | EN: Normalizing `(3,4)` gives `(\frac{3}{5},\frac{4}{5})`.<br>FR: Normaliser `(3,4)` donne `(\frac{3}{5},\frac{4}{5})`.<br>中：将 `(3,4)` 单位化得到 `(\frac{3}{5},\frac{4}{5})`。           |
| `d(\mathbf{u},\mathbf{v})=\lVert\mathbf{u}-\mathbf{v}\rVert` | distance between vectors, distance entre vecteurs, 向量距离   | The norm of the difference between two vectors.                                                                       | EN: The distance between `(1,1)` and `(4,5)` is `5`.<br>FR: La distance entre `(1,1)` et `(4,5)` est `5`.<br>中：`(1,1)` 与 `(4,5)` 之间的距离是 `5`。                                        |

## 11.2 Dot product, cross product, and angles

| Symbol (LaTeX)                                                                     | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                                    | Definition                                                                                     | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `\mathbf{u}\cdot\mathbf{v}`                                                        | dot product, produit scalaire, 点积 / 数量积                                              | A scalar product measuring alignment between two vectors.                                      | EN: `(1,2)\cdot(3,4)=11`.<br>FR: `(1,2)\cdot(3,4)=11`.<br>中：`(1,2)\cdot(3,4)=11`。                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| `\mathbf{u}\cdot\mathbf{v}=\sum_i u_iv_i`                                          | component formula for dot product, formule composante du produit scalaire, 点积分量公式    | The dot product computed by multiplying corresponding components and adding.                   | EN: `(1,2,3)\cdot(4,5,6)=32`.<br>FR: `(1,2,3)\cdot(4,5,6)=32`.<br>中：`(1,2,3)\cdot(4,5,6)=32`。                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
| `\mathbf{u}\cdot\mathbf{v}=\lVert\mathbf{u}\rVert\lVert\mathbf{v}\rVert\cos\theta` | angle formula for dot product, formule angulaire du produit scalaire, 点积夹角公式         | A formula relating dot product to vector lengths and the angle between them.                   | EN: If `\mathbf{u}\cdot\mathbf{v}=0`, nonzero vectors are perpendicular.<br>FR: Si `\mathbf{u}\cdot\mathbf{v}=0`, les vecteurs non nuls sont perpendiculaires.<br>中：若 `\mathbf{u}\cdot\mathbf{v}=0`，非零向量互相垂直。                                                                                                                                            |
| `\theta`                                                                           | angle between vectors, angle entre vecteurs, 向量夹角                                    | The angle determined by two nonzero vectors, usually taken between `0` and `\pi`.              | EN: The angle between `(1,0)` and `(0,1)` is `\frac{\pi}{2}`.<br>FR: L’angle entre `(1,0)` et `(0,1)` est `\frac{\pi}{2}`.<br>中：`(1,0)` 与 `(0,1)` 的夹角是 `\frac{\pi}{2}`。                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| `\mathbf{u}\perp\mathbf{v}`                                                        | orthogonal vectors, vecteurs orthogonaux, 正交向量                                       | Vectors whose dot product is zero.                                                             | EN: `(1,0)` and `(0,1)` are orthogonal.<br>FR: `(1,0)` et `(0,1)` sont orthogonaux.<br>中：`(1,0)` 与 `(0,1)` 正交。                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| `\operatorname{proj}_{\mathbf{u}}\mathbf{v}`                                       | projection, projection, 投影                                                           | The component of one vector in the direction of another.                                       | EN: The projection of `(3,4)` onto the `x`-axis is `(3,0)`.<br>FR: La projection de `(3,4)` sur l’axe des abscisses est `(3,0)`.<br>中：`(3,4)` 在 `x` 轴上的投影是 `(3,0)`。                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| `\frac{\mathbf{v}\cdot\mathbf{u}}{\lVert\mathbf{u}\rVert^2}\mathbf{u}`             | vector projection formula, formule de projection vectorielle, 向量投影公式                 | The formula for projecting `\mathbf{v}` onto a nonzero vector `\mathbf{u}`.                    | EN: Projecting onto a unit vector `\mathbf{u}` gives `(\mathbf{v}\cdot\mathbf{u})\mathbf{u}`.<br>FR: La projection sur un vecteur unitaire `\mathbf{u}` donne `(\mathbf{v}\cdot\mathbf{u})\mathbf{u}`.<br>中：投影到单位向量 `\mathbf{u}` 上得到 `(\mathbf{v}\cdot\mathbf{u})\mathbf{u}`。                                                                            |
| `\mathbf{u}\times\mathbf{v}`                                                       | cross product, produit vectoriel, 叉积 / 向量积                                           | In three dimensions, a vector perpendicular to both input vectors.                             | EN: `\mathbf{i}\times\mathbf{j}=\mathbf{k}`.<br>FR: `\mathbf{i}\times\mathbf{j}=\mathbf{k}`.<br>中：`\mathbf{i}\times\mathbf{j}=\mathbf{k}`。                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
| `\lVert\mathbf{u}\times\mathbf{v}\rVert`                                           | magnitude of cross product, norme du produit vectoriel, 叉积长度                         | The area of the parallelogram spanned by two vectors.                                          | EN: If `\mathbf{u}` and `\mathbf{v}` are perpendicular unit vectors, then `\lVert\mathbf{u}\times\mathbf{v}\rVert=1`.<br>FR: Si `\mathbf{u}` et `\mathbf{v}` sont des vecteurs unitaires perpendiculaires, alors `\lVert\mathbf{u}\times\mathbf{v}\rVert=1`.<br>中：若 `\mathbf{u}` 和 `\mathbf{v}` 是互相垂直的单位向量，则 `\lVert\mathbf{u}\times\mathbf{v}\rVert=1`。 |
| `\mathbf{u}\times\mathbf{v}=\mathbf{0}`                                            | parallel test by cross product, critère de parallélisme par produit vectoriel, 叉积判平行 | Two nonzero vectors in `\mathbb{R}^3` are parallel if and only if their cross product is zero. | EN: If `\mathbf{u}\times\mathbf{v}=\mathbf{0}`, the vectors are parallel.<br>FR: Si `\mathbf{u}\times\mathbf{v}=\mathbf{0}`, les vecteurs sont parallèles.<br>中：若 `\mathbf{u}\times\mathbf{v}=\mathbf{0}`，则两个向量平行。                                                                                                                                       |
| `\det[\mathbf{u},\mathbf{v},\mathbf{w}]`                                           | scalar triple product, produit mixte, 混合积                                            | A scalar measuring signed volume of the parallelepiped spanned by three vectors.               | EN: The scalar triple product gives volume up to sign.<br>FR: Le produit mixte donne le volume signé.<br>中：混合积给出有向体积。                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |

## 11.3 Matrices and matrix operations

| Symbol (LaTeX) | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                                                       | Definition                                                                   | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| -------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `A`            | matrix, matrice, 矩阵                                                                                     | A rectangular array of numbers, symbols, or functions.                       | EN: `A=\begin{pmatrix}1&2\\3&4\end{pmatrix}` is a `2\times2` matrix.<br>FR: `A=\begin{pmatrix}1&2\\3&4\end{pmatrix}` est une matrice `2\times2`.<br>中：`A=\begin{pmatrix}1&2\\3&4\end{pmatrix}` 是 `2\times2` 矩阵。                                                                                                          |
| `a_{ij}`       | matrix entry, coefficient d’une matrice, 矩阵元素                                                           | The entry of a matrix in row `i` and column `j`.                             | EN: In `A=(a_{ij})`, `a_{23}` is the entry in row `2`, column `3`.<br>FR: Dans `A=(a_{ij})`, `a_{23}` est l’entrée de la ligne `2`, colonne `3`.<br>中：在 `A=(a_{ij})` 中，`a_{23}` 是第 `2` 行第 `3` 列元素。                                                                                                                       |
| `m\times n`    | matrix size, taille d’une matrice, 矩阵大小 / 阶数                                                            | The number of rows and columns of a matrix.                                  | EN: A `3\times2` matrix has `3` rows and `2` columns.<br>FR: Une matrice `3\times2` a `3` lignes et `2` colonnes.<br>中：`3\times2` 矩阵有 `3` 行 `2` 列。                                                                                                                                                                       |
| `A+B`          | matrix addition, addition de matrices, 矩阵加法                                                             | Addition of matrices of the same size by adding corresponding entries.       | EN: `\begin{pmatrix}1&2\end{pmatrix}+\begin{pmatrix}3&4\end{pmatrix}=\begin{pmatrix}4&6\end{pmatrix}`.<br>FR: `\begin{pmatrix}1&2\end{pmatrix}+\begin{pmatrix}3&4\end{pmatrix}=\begin{pmatrix}4&6\end{pmatrix}`.<br>中：`\begin{pmatrix}1&2\end{pmatrix}+\begin{pmatrix}3&4\end{pmatrix}=\begin{pmatrix}4&6\end{pmatrix}`。 |
| `cA`           | scalar multiplication of a matrix, multiplication scalaire d’une matrice, 矩阵数乘                          | Multiplication of every matrix entry by a scalar.                            | EN: `2\begin{pmatrix}1&3\\0&-1\end{pmatrix}=\begin{pmatrix}2&6\\0&-2\end{pmatrix}`.<br>FR: `2\begin{pmatrix}1&3\\0&-1\end{pmatrix}=\begin{pmatrix}2&6\\0&-2\end{pmatrix}`.<br>中：`2\begin{pmatrix}1&3\\0&-1\end{pmatrix}=\begin{pmatrix}2&6\\0&-2\end{pmatrix}`。                                                          |
| `AB`           | matrix multiplication, multiplication de matrices, 矩阵乘法                                                 | A product defined by row-column dot products when dimensions are compatible. | EN: If `A` is `2\times3` and `B` is `3\times4`, then `AB` is `2\times4`.<br>FR: Si `A` est `2\times3` et `B` est `3\times4`, alors `AB` est `2\times4`.<br>中：若 `A` 是 `2\times3`，`B` 是 `3\times4`，则 `AB` 是 `2\times4`。                                                                                                    |
| `AB\neq BA`    | noncommutativity of matrix multiplication, non-commutativité de la multiplication matricielle, 矩阵乘法不可交换 | Matrix multiplication generally depends on order.                            | EN: In general, `AB` is not equal to `BA`.<br>FR: En général, `AB` n’est pas égal à `BA`.<br>中：一般来说，`AB` 不等于 `BA`。                                                                                                                                                                                                       |
| `A^T`          | transpose, transposée, 转置                                                                               | The matrix obtained by switching rows and columns.                           | EN: If `A=\begin{pmatrix}1&2\\3&4\end{pmatrix}`, then `A^T=\begin{pmatrix}1&3\\2&4\end{pmatrix}`.<br>FR: Si `A=\begin{pmatrix}1&2\\3&4\end{pmatrix}`, alors `A^T=\begin{pmatrix}1&3\\2&4\end{pmatrix}`.<br>中：若 `A=\begin{pmatrix}1&2\\3&4\end{pmatrix}`，则 `A^T=\begin{pmatrix}1&3\\2&4\end{pmatrix}`。                    |
| `I_n`          | identity matrix, matrice identité, 单位矩阵                                                                 | A square matrix with ones on the diagonal and zeros elsewhere.               | EN: `AI_n=A` when dimensions match.<br>FR: `AI_n=A` lorsque les dimensions conviennent.<br>中：维度匹配时，`AI_n=A`。                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
| `O`            | zero matrix, matrice nulle, 零矩阵                                                                         | A matrix whose entries are all zero.                                         | EN: `A+O=A`.<br>FR: `A+O=A`.<br>中：`A+O=A`。                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
| `A^k`          | matrix power, puissance d’une matrice, 矩阵幂                                                              | Repeated multiplication of a square matrix by itself.                        | EN: `A^2=AA`.<br>FR: `A^2=AA`.<br>中：`A^2=AA`。                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
| `A^{-1}`       | inverse matrix, matrice inverse, 逆矩阵                                                                    | A matrix satisfying `AA^{-1}=A^{-1}A=I`.                                     | EN: If `A^{-1}` exists, then `Ax=b` has solution `x=A^{-1}b`.<br>FR: Si `A^{-1}` existe, alors `Ax=b` a pour solution `x=A^{-1}b`.<br>中：若 `A^{-1}` 存在，则 `Ax=b` 的解为 `x=A^{-1}b`。                                                                                                                                          |

## 11.4 Special matrices

| Symbol (LaTeX)                          | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                        | Definition                                                                                 | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| --------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `n\times n`                             | square matrix, matrice carrée, 方阵                                        | A matrix with the same number of rows and columns.                                         | EN: `\begin{pmatrix}1&2\\3&4\end{pmatrix}` is a square matrix.<br>FR: `\begin{pmatrix}1&2\\3&4\end{pmatrix}` est une matrice carrée.<br>中：`\begin{pmatrix}1&2\\3&4\end{pmatrix}` 是方阵。                                     |
| `D=\operatorname{diag}(d_1,\ldots,d_n)` | diagonal matrix, matrice diagonale, 对角矩阵                                 | A square matrix whose off-diagonal entries are all zero.                                   | EN: `\operatorname{diag}(2,3)` has diagonal entries `2` and `3`.<br>FR: `\operatorname{diag}(2,3)` a pour éléments diagonaux `2` et `3`.<br>中：`\operatorname{diag}(2,3)` 的对角元素是 `2` 和 `3`。                                |
| `A^T=A`                                 | symmetric matrix, matrice symétrique, 对称矩阵                               | A square matrix equal to its transpose.                                                    | EN: `\begin{pmatrix}1&2\\2&3\end{pmatrix}` is symmetric.<br>FR: `\begin{pmatrix}1&2\\2&3\end{pmatrix}` est symétrique.<br>中：`\begin{pmatrix}1&2\\2&3\end{pmatrix}` 是对称矩阵。                                                 |
| `A^T=-A`                                | skew-symmetric matrix, matrice antisymétrique, 反对称矩阵                     | A square matrix whose transpose is its negative.                                           | EN: A real skew-symmetric matrix has zeros on the diagonal.<br>FR: Une matrice réelle antisymétrique a des zéros sur la diagonale.<br>中：实反对称矩阵的对角线元素都是零。                                                                  |
| `A^TA=I`                                | orthogonal matrix, matrice orthogonale, 正交矩阵                             | A real square matrix whose columns form an orthonormal basis.                              | EN: Orthogonal matrices preserve lengths.<br>FR: Les matrices orthogonales préservent les longueurs.<br>中：正交矩阵保持向量长度。                                                                                                     |
| `A^*A=I`                                | unitary matrix, matrice unitaire, 酉矩阵                                    | A complex square matrix whose conjugate transpose is its inverse.                          | EN: Unitary matrices preserve complex inner products.<br>FR: Les matrices unitaires préservent les produits hermitiens.<br>中：酉矩阵保持复内积。                                                                                    |
| `A^*=A`                                 | Hermitian matrix, matrice hermitienne, Hermitian 矩阵 / 厄米矩阵               | A complex matrix equal to its conjugate transpose.                                         | EN: Hermitian matrices have real eigenvalues.<br>FR: Les matrices hermitiennes ont des valeurs propres réelles.<br>中：Hermitian 矩阵的特征值为实数。                                                                                 |
| `A^2=A`                                 | idempotent matrix, matrice idempotente, 幂等矩阵                             | A matrix that equals its square.                                                           | EN: Projection matrices are often idempotent.<br>FR: Les matrices de projection sont souvent idempotentes.<br>中：投影矩阵通常是幂等矩阵。                                                                                              |
| `A_{ij}=0` for `i>j`                    | upper triangular matrix, matrice triangulaire supérieure, 上三角矩阵          | A square matrix with zero entries below the main diagonal.                                 | EN: The determinant of an upper triangular matrix is the product of its diagonal entries.<br>FR: Le déterminant d’une matrice triangulaire supérieure est le produit de ses éléments diagonaux.<br>中：上三角矩阵的行列式等于对角线元素的乘积。 |
| `A_{ij}=0` for `i<j`                    | lower triangular matrix, matrice triangulaire inférieure, 下三角矩阵          | A square matrix with zero entries above the main diagonal.                                 | EN: Lower triangular systems are solved by forward substitution.<br>FR: Les systèmes triangulaires inférieurs se résolvent par substitution avant.<br>中：下三角线性方程组可用前代法求解。                                                  |
| `A^T=A^{-1}`                            | rotation or reflection matrix, matrice de rotation ou réflexion, 旋转或反射矩阵 | In Euclidean space, many rotations and reflections are represented by orthogonal matrices. | EN: A plane rotation matrix is orthogonal.<br>FR: Une matrice de rotation plane est orthogonale.<br>中：平面旋转矩阵是正交矩阵。                                                                                                        |

## 11.5 Linear systems and row operations

| Symbol (LaTeX)           | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                               | Definition                                                                              | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| ------------------------ | --------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `Ax=b`                   | linear system, système linéaire, 线性方程组                          | A system of linear equations represented in matrix form.                                | EN: `Ax=b` represents several linear equations at once.<br>FR: `Ax=b` représente plusieurs équations linéaires à la fois.<br>中：`Ax=b` 同时表示若干个线性方程。                                              |
| `[A\mid b]`              | augmented matrix, matrice augmentée, 增广矩阵                       | A matrix combining the coefficient matrix and right-hand side of a linear system.       | EN: The augmented matrix of `Ax=b` is `[A\mid b]`.<br>FR: La matrice augmentée de `Ax=b` est `[A\mid b]`.<br>中：`Ax=b` 的增广矩阵是 `[A\mid b]`。                                                       |
| —                        | coefficient matrix, matrice des coefficients, 系数矩阵              | The matrix containing coefficients of the variables in a linear system.                 | EN: In `Ax=b`, `A` is the coefficient matrix.<br>FR: Dans `Ax=b`, `A` est la matrice des coefficients.<br>中：在 `Ax=b` 中，`A` 是系数矩阵。                                                               |
| —                        | right-hand side vector, second membre, 右端向量                     | The vector on the right side of a matrix equation.                                      | EN: In `Ax=b`, `b` is the right-hand side vector.<br>FR: Dans `Ax=b`, `b` est le second membre.<br>中：在 `Ax=b` 中，`b` 是右端向量。                                                                      |
| —                        | solution vector, vecteur solution, 解向量                          | A vector satisfying a matrix equation.                                                  | EN: If `Ax=b`, a solution vector is a vector `x` that makes the equation true.<br>FR: Si `Ax=b`, un vecteur solution est un vecteur `x` qui vérifie l’équation.<br>中：若 `Ax=b`，解向量是使方程成立的向量 `x`。 |
| `R_i\leftrightarrow R_j` | row swap, échange de lignes, 交换两行                               | An elementary row operation exchanging two rows.                                        | EN: Swap `R_1` and `R_2` to move a nonzero pivot upward.<br>FR: Échanger `R_1` et `R_2` pour placer un pivot non nul plus haut.<br>中：交换 `R_1` 和 `R_2` 可把非零主元移到上方。                               |
| `cR_i`                   | row scaling, multiplication d’une ligne par un scalaire, 行倍乘    | Multiplying a row by a nonzero scalar.                                                  | EN: Replace `R_1` by `\frac{1}{2}R_1` to make the pivot equal to `1`.<br>FR: Remplacer `R_1` par `\frac{1}{2}R_1` pour rendre le pivot égal à `1`.<br>中：用 `\frac{1}{2}R_1` 替换 `R_1`，可使主元变为 `1`。 |
| `R_i+cR_j`               | row replacement, remplacement de ligne, 行加倍法                    | Replacing one row by itself plus a scalar multiple of another row.                      | EN: Use `R_2-3R_1` to eliminate an entry below a pivot.<br>FR: Utiliser `R_2-3R_1` pour éliminer une entrée sous un pivot.<br>中：用 `R_2-3R_1` 可消去主元下方元素。                                         |
| —                        | Gaussian elimination, élimination de Gauss, 高斯消元法               | A method using row operations to solve linear systems.                                  | EN: Gaussian elimination transforms a system into row echelon form.<br>FR: L’élimination de Gauss transforme un système en forme échelonnée.<br>中：高斯消元把方程组化为阶梯形。                                |
| —                        | Gauss-Jordan elimination, élimination de Gauss-Jordan, 高斯-约旦消元法 | A method reducing a matrix to reduced row echelon form.                                 | EN: Gauss-Jordan elimination can compute an inverse matrix.<br>FR: L’élimination de Gauss-Jordan peut calculer une matrice inverse.<br>中：高斯-约旦消元可用于求逆矩阵。                                        |
| —                        | row echelon form, forme échelonnée, 行阶梯形                        | A row-reduced form where pivots move rightward as rows go downward.                     | EN: Row echelon form helps identify pivots and free variables.<br>FR: La forme échelonnée aide à identifier pivots et variables libres.<br>中：行阶梯形有助于识别主元和自由变量。                                  |
| —                        | reduced row echelon form, forme échelonnée réduite, 简化行阶梯形      | A row echelon form where each pivot is `1` and is the only nonzero entry in its column. | EN: Reduced row echelon form gives solutions directly.<br>FR: La forme échelonnée réduite donne directement les solutions.<br>中：简化行阶梯形可直接读出解。                                                   |
| —                        | pivot, pivot, 主元                                                | A leading nonzero entry used to eliminate other entries in its column.                  | EN: Each pivot column corresponds to a basic variable.<br>FR: Chaque colonne pivot correspond à une variable de base.<br>中：每个主元列对应一个基本变量。                                                       |
| —                        | free variable, variable libre, 自由变量                             | A variable not corresponding to a pivot column.                                         | EN: Free variables appear when a system has infinitely many solutions.<br>FR: Les variables libres apparaissent lorsqu’un système a une infinité de solutions.<br>中：当方程组有无穷多解时，会出现自由变量。         |
| —                        | inconsistent system, système incompatible, 不相容方程组               | A linear system with no solution.                                                       | EN: A row `0=1` indicates an inconsistent system.<br>FR: Une ligne `0=1` indique un système incompatible.<br>中：出现 `0=1` 的行表示方程组无解。                                                              |
| —                        | homogeneous system, système homogène, 齐次线性方程组                   | A linear system of the form `Ax=0`.                                                     | EN: A homogeneous system always has the zero solution.<br>FR: Un système homogène possède toujours la solution nulle.<br>中：齐次线性方程组总有零解。                                                         |

## 11.6 Determinants and invertibility

| Symbol (LaTeX)                                   | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                        | Definition                                                                                         | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| ------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------ | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `\det A`                                         | determinant, déterminant, 行列式                                            | A scalar associated with a square matrix, measuring signed volume scaling and invertibility.       | EN: If `\det A\neq 0`, then `A` is invertible.<br>FR: Si `\det A\neq 0`, alors `A` est inversible.<br>中：若 `\det A\neq 0`，则 `A` 可逆。                                                                                                 |
| `\left\lvert A\right\rvert`                      | determinant notation, notation du déterminant, 行列式记号                     | A common notation for the determinant of a square matrix, distinct from absolute value by context. | EN: For a matrix `A`, `\left\lvert A\right\rvert` may mean `\det A`.<br>FR: Pour une matrice `A`, `\left\lvert A\right\rvert` peut désigner `\det A`.<br>中：对矩阵 `A`，`\left\lvert A\right\rvert` 可表示 `\det A`。                       |
| `\det\begin{pmatrix}a&b\\c&d\end{pmatrix}=ad-bc` | two-by-two determinant, déterminant deux par deux, 二阶行列式                 | The determinant formula for a `2\times2` matrix.                                                   | EN: `\det\begin{pmatrix}1&2\\3&4\end{pmatrix}=-2`.<br>FR: `\det\begin{pmatrix}1&2\\3&4\end{pmatrix}=-2`.<br>中：`\det\begin{pmatrix}1&2\\3&4\end{pmatrix}=-2`。                                                                       |
| —                                                | cofactor expansion, développement par cofacteurs, 余子式展开                  | A method for computing determinants by expanding along a row or column.                            | EN: Cofactor expansion is useful for matrices with many zeros.<br>FR: Le développement par cofacteurs est utile pour les matrices contenant beaucoup de zéros.<br>中：余子式展开适合含有许多零元素的矩阵。                                             |
| `M_{ij}`                                         | minor, mineur, 余子式                                                       | The determinant obtained by deleting row `i` and column `j`.                                       | EN: A minor is used in cofactor expansion.<br>FR: Un mineur est utilisé dans le développement par cofacteurs.<br>中：余子式用于行列式展开。                                                                                                     |
| `C_{ij}=(-1)^{i+j}M_{ij}`                        | cofactor, cofacteur, 代数余子式                                               | A signed minor used in determinant expansion.                                                      | EN: The cofactor includes the sign `(-1)^{i+j}`.<br>FR: Le cofacteur inclut le signe `(-1)^{i+j}`.<br>中：代数余子式包含符号因子 `(-1)^{i+j}`。                                                                                                  |
| `\operatorname{adj}(A)`                          | adjugate matrix, comatrice / matrice adjointe, 伴随矩阵                      | The transpose of the cofactor matrix.                                                              | EN: If `A` is invertible, then `A^{-1}=\frac{1}{\det A}\operatorname{adj}(A)`.<br>FR: Si `A` est inversible, alors `A^{-1}=\frac{1}{\det A}\operatorname{adj}(A)`.<br>中：若 `A` 可逆，则 `A^{-1}=\frac{1}{\det A}\operatorname{adj}(A)`。 |
| `\det(AB)=\det A\det B`                          | multiplicativity of determinant, multiplicativité du déterminant, 行列式乘法性 | The determinant of a matrix product is the product of determinants.                                | EN: `\det(AB)=\det A\det B`.<br>FR: `\det(AB)=\det A\det B`.<br>中：`\det(AB)=\det A\det B`。                                                                                                                                         |
| `\det A=0`                                       | singular matrix, matrice singulière, 奇异矩阵                                | A square matrix that is not invertible.                                                            | EN: A matrix with determinant zero is singular.<br>FR: Une matrice de déterminant nul est singulière.<br>中：行列式为零的矩阵是奇异矩阵。                                                                                                          |
| `\det A\neq 0`                                   | nonsingular matrix, matrice non singulière, 非奇异矩阵                        | A square matrix that is invertible.                                                                | EN: A nonsingular matrix has linearly independent columns.<br>FR: Une matrice non singulière a des colonnes linéairement indépendantes.<br>中：非奇异矩阵的列向量线性无关。                                                                        |
| —                                                | invertible matrix theorem, théorème de la matrice inversible, 可逆矩阵定理     | A collection of equivalent conditions for a square matrix to be invertible.                        | EN: For a square matrix, invertibility, nonzero determinant, and full rank are equivalent.<br>FR: Pour une matrice carrée, inversibilité, déterminant non nul et rang maximal sont équivalents.<br>中：对方阵，可逆、行列式非零、满秩是等价条件。         |

## 11.7 Vector spaces and subspaces

| Symbol (LaTeX)                          | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                | Definition                                                                                                 | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
| --------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------ | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `V`                                     | vector space, espace vectoriel, 向量空间             | A set whose elements can be added and multiplied by scalars according to specific axioms.                  | EN: `\mathbb{R}^n` is a vector space over `\mathbb{R}`.<br>FR: `\mathbb{R}^n` est un espace vectoriel sur `\mathbb{R}`.<br>中：`\mathbb{R}^n` 是实数域上的向量空间。                                                                |
| `\mathbb{F}`                            | field of scalars, corps des scalaires, 标量域       | The field from which scalar coefficients are taken.                                                        | EN: Complex vector spaces use scalars from `\mathbb{C}`.<br>FR: Les espaces vectoriels complexes utilisent des scalaires de `\mathbb{C}`.<br>中：复向量空间使用来自 `\mathbb{C}` 的标量。                                             |
| `W\leq V`                               | subspace, sous-espace vectoriel, 子空间             | A subset of a vector space that is itself a vector space under the same operations.                        | EN: A line through the origin in `\mathbb{R}^2` is a subspace.<br>FR: Une droite passant par l’origine dans `\mathbb{R}^2` est un sous-espace vectoriel.<br>中：`\mathbb{R}^2` 中经过原点的直线是子空间。                             |
| `\operatorname{span}\{v_1,\ldots,v_k\}` | span, sous-espace engendré, 张成 / 生成空间            | The set of all linear combinations of given vectors.                                                       | EN: `\operatorname{span}\{(1,0),(0,1)\}=\mathbb{R}^2`.<br>FR: `\operatorname{span}\{(1,0),(0,1)\}=\mathbb{R}^2`.<br>中：`\operatorname{span}\{(1,0),(0,1)\}=\mathbb{R}^2`。                                               |
| `c_1v_1+\cdots+c_kv_k`                  | linear combination, combinaison linéaire, 线性组合   | A sum of scalar multiples of vectors.                                                                      | EN: `3(1,0)+2(0,1)=(3,2)` is a linear combination.<br>FR: `3(1,0)+2(0,1)=(3,2)` est une combinaison linéaire.<br>中：`3(1,0)+2(0,1)=(3,2)` 是线性组合。                                                                        |
| —                                       | spanning set, famille génératrice, 张成集 / 生成集     | A set of vectors whose span equals the whole space or a target subspace.                                   | EN: The standard basis spans `\mathbb{R}^n`.<br>FR: La base canonique engendre `\mathbb{R}^n`.<br>中：标准基张成 `\mathbb{R}^n`。                                                                                              |
| —                                       | linear independence, indépendance linéaire, 线性无关 | A set of vectors is linearly independent if no vector is a linear combination of the others.               | EN: `(1,0)` and `(0,1)` are linearly independent.<br>FR: `(1,0)` et `(0,1)` sont linéairement indépendants.<br>中：`(1,0)` 和 `(0,1)` 线性无关。                                                                               |
| —                                       | linear dependence, dépendance linéaire, 线性相关     | A set of vectors is linearly dependent if one vector can be written as a linear combination of the others. | EN: `(1,0)`, `(0,1)`, and `(1,1)` are linearly dependent in `\mathbb{R}^2`.<br>FR: `(1,0)`, `(0,1)` et `(1,1)` sont linéairement dépendants dans `\mathbb{R}^2`.<br>中：在 `\mathbb{R}^2` 中，`(1,0)`、`(0,1)`、`(1,1)` 线性相关。 |
| `B=\{v_1,\ldots,v_n\}`                  | basis, base, 基                                   | A linearly independent spanning set of a vector space.                                                     | EN: `\{(1,0),(0,1)\}` is a basis of `\mathbb{R}^2`.<br>FR: `\{(1,0),(0,1)\}` est une base de `\mathbb{R}^2`.<br>中：`\{(1,0),(0,1)\}` 是 `\mathbb{R}^2` 的一组基。                                                             |
| `\dim V`                                | dimension, dimension, 维数                         | The number of vectors in any basis of a vector space.                                                      | EN: `\dim\mathbb{R}^3=3`.<br>FR: `\dim\mathbb{R}^3=3`.<br>中：`\dim\mathbb{R}^3=3`。                                                                                                                                      |
| `[v]_B`                                 | coordinate vector, vecteur de coordonnées, 坐标向量  | The vector of coefficients of `v` relative to a chosen basis `B`.                                          | EN: In the standard basis, `[ (3,4) ]_B=(3,4)`.<br>FR: Dans la base canonique, `[ (3,4) ]_B=(3,4)`.<br>中：在标准基下，`[(3,4)]_B=(3,4)`。                                                                                      |

## 11.8 Fundamental subspaces and rank

| Symbol (LaTeX)                                       | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                       | Definition                                                                  | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| ---------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `\operatorname{Col}(A)`                              | column space, espace colonne, 列空间                       | The span of the columns of a matrix.                                        | EN: `\operatorname{Col}(A)` is the set of all vectors `Ax`.<br>FR: `\operatorname{Col}(A)` est l’ensemble des vecteurs `Ax`.<br>中：`\operatorname{Col}(A)` 是所有 `Ax` 组成的集合。                                |
| `\operatorname{Row}(A)`                              | row space, espace ligne, 行空间                            | The span of the rows of a matrix.                                           | EN: Row operations preserve the row space up to basis change.<br>FR: Les opérations élémentaires sur les lignes préservent l’espace ligne à changement de base près.<br>中：初等行变换在基变换意义下保持行空间。             |
| `\operatorname{Null}(A)`                             | null space, noyau, 零空间 / 核                              | The set of all solutions to `Ax=0`.                                         | EN: `\operatorname{Null}(A)=\{x:Ax=0\}`.<br>FR: `\operatorname{Null}(A)=\{x:Ax=0\}`.<br>中：`\operatorname{Null}(A)=\{x:Ax=0\}`。                                                                           |
| `\operatorname{rank}(A)`                             | rank, rang, 秩                                           | The dimension of the column space, equal to the dimension of the row space. | EN: A `3\times3` invertible matrix has rank `3`.<br>FR: Une matrice inversible `3\times3` a un rang `3`.<br>中：`3\times3` 可逆矩阵的秩是 `3`。                                                                    |
| `\operatorname{nullity}(A)`                          | nullity, nullité, 零度 / 核维数                              | The dimension of the null space of a matrix.                                | EN: Nullity counts the number of independent free directions in `Ax=0`.<br>FR: La nullité compte le nombre de directions libres indépendantes dans `Ax=0`.<br>中：零度表示 `Ax=0` 中独立自由方向的数量。                  |
| `\operatorname{rank}(A)+\operatorname{nullity}(A)=n` | rank-nullity theorem, théorème du rang, 秩-零度定理          | For an `m\times n` matrix, rank plus nullity equals the number of columns.  | EN: If `A` has `5` columns and rank `3`, then its nullity is `2`.<br>FR: Si `A` a `5` colonnes et un rang `3`, alors sa nullité est `2`.<br>中：若 `A` 有 `5` 列且秩为 `3`，则零度为 `2`。                             |
| —                                                    | pivot column, colonne pivot, 主元列                        | A column containing a pivot in row echelon form.                            | EN: Pivot columns form a basis for the column space when taken from the original matrix.<br>FR: Les colonnes pivot de la matrice originale forment une base de l’espace colonne.<br>中：原矩阵中的主元列构成列空间的一组基。 |
| —                                                    | column rank, rang colonne, 列秩                           | The dimension of the column space.                                          | EN: Column rank equals row rank.<br>FR: Le rang colonne est égal au rang ligne.<br>中：列秩等于行秩。                                                                                                             |
| —                                                    | row rank, rang ligne, 行秩                                | The dimension of the row space.                                             | EN: Row rank equals the number of pivots.<br>FR: Le rang ligne est égal au nombre de pivots.<br>中：行秩等于主元个数。                                                                                              |
| `Ax=b` consistent                                    | consistency condition, condition de compatibilité, 相容条件 | A condition that the right-hand side must lie in the column space of `A`.   | EN: `Ax=b` is consistent exactly when `b\in\operatorname{Col}(A)`.<br>FR: `Ax=b` est compatible exactement lorsque `b\in\operatorname{Col}(A)`.<br>中：`Ax=b` 相容当且仅当 `b\in\operatorname{Col}(A)`。          |

## 11.9 Linear transformations

| Symbol (LaTeX)       | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                             | Definition                                                                  | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| -------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
