| —              | adjacency list, liste d’adjacence, 邻接表                                        | A representation storing neighbors of each vertex.                              | EN: Sparse graphs are often stored as adjacency lists.<br>FR: Les graphes creux sont souvent stockés sous forme de listes d’adjacence.<br>中：稀疏图常用邻接表存储。                                                     |
| —              | incidence matrix, matrice d’incidence, 关联矩阵                                   | A matrix describing which vertices are incident to which edges.                 | EN: An incidence matrix links vertices and edges.<br>FR: Une matrice d’incidence relie sommets et arêtes.<br>中：关联矩阵描述顶点与边的关联关系。                                                                             |
| `w(e)`         | edge weight, poids d’une arête, 边权                                            | A numerical value assigned to an edge, such as cost, length, or capacity.       | EN: In a road graph, edge weight may represent distance.<br>FR: Dans un graphe routier, le poids d’une arête peut représenter la distance.<br>中：在道路图中，边权可以表示距离。                                             |
| —              | weighted graph, graphe pondéré, 加权图                                           | A graph with weights assigned to edges or vertices.                             | EN: Shortest-path problems usually use weighted graphs.<br>FR: Les problèmes de plus court chemin utilisent souvent des graphes pondérés.<br>中：最短路径问题通常使用加权图。                                               |
| —              | breadth-first search, parcours en largeur, 广度优先搜索                             | A graph traversal method exploring vertices layer by layer.                     | EN: Breadth-first search finds shortest paths in unweighted graphs.<br>FR: Le parcours en largeur trouve les plus courts chemins dans les graphes non pondérés.<br>中：广度优先搜索可求无权图中的最短路径。                     |
| —              | depth-first search, parcours en profondeur, 深度优先搜索                            | A graph traversal method exploring as far as possible before backtracking.      | EN: Depth-first search can detect cycles.<br>FR: Le parcours en profondeur peut détecter des cycles.<br>中：深度优先搜索可用于检测环。                                                                                     |
| —              | shortest path, plus court chemin, 最短路径                                        | A path of minimum total length or weight between vertices.                      | EN: Dijkstra’s algorithm finds shortest paths with nonnegative edge weights.<br>FR: L’algorithme de Dijkstra trouve les plus courts chemins avec poids non négatifs.<br>中：Dijkstra 算法可求非负边权图中的最短路径。         |
| —              | minimum spanning tree algorithm, algorithme d’arbre couvrant minimal, 最小生成树算法 | An algorithm finding a minimum-weight spanning tree.                            | EN: Kruskal’s and Prim’s algorithms find minimum spanning trees.<br>FR: Les algorithmes de Kruskal et de Prim trouvent des arbres couvrants minimaux.<br>中：Kruskal 和 Prim 算法可求最小生成树。                        |
| —              | greedy algorithm, algorithme glouton, 贪心算法                                    | An algorithm making locally optimal choices at each step.                       | EN: Kruskal’s algorithm is greedy.<br>FR: L’algorithme de Kruskal est glouton.<br>中：Kruskal 算法是贪心算法。                                                                                                        |
| —              | dynamic programming, programmation dynamique, 动态规划                            | A method solving problems by combining solutions to overlapping subproblems.    | EN: Dynamic programming is used for shortest paths and sequence alignment.<br>FR: La programmation dynamique est utilisée pour les plus courts chemins et l’alignement de séquences.<br>中：动态规划可用于最短路径和序列比对。 |
| —              | backtracking, retour sur trace, 回溯                                            | A search method that builds partial solutions and abandons impossible branches. | EN: Sudoku solvers often use backtracking.<br>FR: Les solveurs de sudoku utilisent souvent le retour sur trace.<br>中：数独求解器常使用回溯。                                                                            |

## 13.10 Boolean algebra and logic circuits

| Symbol (LaTeX)                    | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                | Definition                                                                          | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| --------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `\{0,1\}`                         | Boolean domain, domaine booléen, 布尔域                             | The set of truth values, often interpreted as false and true.                       | EN: In Boolean algebra, `0` means false and `1` means true.<br>FR: En algèbre booléenne, `0` signifie faux et `1` signifie vrai.<br>中：在布尔代数中，`0` 表示假，`1` 表示真。                                      |
| `\neg p`                          | NOT, NON, 非                                                      | The Boolean operation that reverses a truth value.                                  | EN: If `p=1`, then `\neg p=0`.<br>FR: Si `p=1`, alors `\neg p=0`.<br>中：若 `p=1`，则 `\neg p=0`。                                                                                                       |
| `p\land q`                        | AND, ET, 与                                                       | A Boolean operation true only when both inputs are true.                            | EN: `1\land 0=0`.<br>FR: `1\land 0=0`.<br>中：`1\land 0=0`。                                                                                                                                          |
| `p\lor q`                         | OR, OU, 或                                                        | A Boolean operation true when at least one input is true.                           | EN: `1\lor 0=1`.<br>FR: `1\lor 0=1`.<br>中：`1\lor 0=1`。                                                                                                                                             |
| `p\oplus q`                       | XOR, OU exclusif, 异或                                             | A Boolean operation true when exactly one input is true.                            | EN: `1\oplus 1=0`.<br>FR: `1\oplus 1=0`.<br>中：`1\oplus 1=0`。                                                                                                                                       |
| `p\Rightarrow q`                  | implication, implication, 蕴含                                     | A Boolean operation false only when `p` is true and `q` is false.                   | EN: `p\Rightarrow q` is equivalent to `\neg p\lor q`.<br>FR: `p\Rightarrow q` est équivalent à `\neg p\lor q`.<br>中：`p\Rightarrow q` 等价于 `\neg p\lor q`。                                           |
| `p\Leftrightarrow q`              | equivalence, équivalence, 等价                                     | A Boolean operation true when two propositions have the same truth value.           | EN: `p\Leftrightarrow q` is true when both are true or both are false.<br>FR: `p\Leftrightarrow q` est vrai lorsque les deux ont la même valeur de vérité.<br>中：当两者同真或同假时，`p\Leftrightarrow q` 为真。 |
| `\neg(p\land q)=\neg p\lor\neg q` | De Morgan’s law for AND, loi de De Morgan pour ET, De Morgan 与式律 | The negation of a conjunction equals the disjunction of negations.                  | EN: “Not both” means “at least one not”.<br>FR: “Pas les deux” signifie “au moins l’un des deux non”.<br>中：“并非两者都成立”等价于“至少一个不成立”。                                                                  |
| `\neg(p\lor q)=\neg p\land\neg q` | De Morgan’s law for OR, loi de De Morgan pour OU, De Morgan 或式律  | The negation of a disjunction equals the conjunction of negations.                  | EN: “Neither `p` nor `q`” means `\neg p\land\neg q`.<br>FR: “Ni `p` ni `q`” signifie `\neg p\land\neg q`.<br>中：“既非 `p` 也非 `q`”表示 `\neg p\land\neg q`。                                              |
| `p\lor(p\land q)=p`               | absorption law, loi d’absorption, 吸收律                            | A Boolean simplification law where a stronger expression absorbs a weaker one.      | EN: `p\lor(p\land q)` simplifies to `p`.<br>FR: `p\lor(p\land q)` se simplifie en `p`.<br>中：`p\lor(p\land q)` 可化简为 `p`。                                                                            |
| —                                 | truth table, table de vérité, 真值表                                | A table listing the truth value of a compound statement for all input combinations. | EN: A truth table for two variables has four rows.<br>FR: Une table de vérité à deux variables a quatre lignes.<br>中：两个变量的真值表有四行。                                                                  |
| —                                 | logic gate, porte logique, 逻辑门                                   | A circuit component implementing a Boolean operation.                               | EN: An AND gate outputs `1` only when both inputs are `1`.<br>FR: Une porte ET produit `1` seulement si les deux entrées valent `1`.<br>中：与门只有在两个输入都为 `1` 时输出 `1`。                                 |
| —                                 | Boolean expression, expression booléenne, 布尔表达式                  | An expression built from Boolean variables and Boolean operations.                  | EN: `p\land(\neg q\lor r)` is a Boolean expression.<br>FR: `p\land(\neg q\lor r)` est une expression booléenne.<br>中：`p\land(\neg q\lor r)` 是布尔表达式。                                                |
| —                                 | satisfiability, satisfiabilité, 可满足性                             | A Boolean formula is satisfiable if some assignment makes it true.                  | EN: `p\lor q` is satisfiable.<br>FR: `p\lor q` est satisfiable.<br>中：`p\lor q` 是可满足的。                                                                                                              |
| —                                 | tautology, tautologie, 重言式                                       | A formula true under every truth assignment.                                        | EN: `p\lor\neg p` is a tautology.<br>FR: `p\lor\neg p` est une tautologie.<br>中：`p\lor\neg p` 是重言式。                                                                                                |
| —                                 | contradiction, contradiction, 矛盾式                                | A formula false under every truth assignment.                                       | EN: `p\land\neg p` is a contradiction.<br>FR: `p\land\neg p` est une contradiction.<br>中：`p\land\neg p` 是矛盾式。                                                                                      |

## 13.11 Formal languages and automata

| Symbol (LaTeX)       | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                 | Definition                                                                            | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                    |
| -------------------- | ------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `\Sigma`             | alphabet, alphabet, 字母表                           | A finite set of symbols.                                                              | EN: `\Sigma=\{0,1\}` is the binary alphabet.<br>FR: `\Sigma=\{0,1\}` est l’alphabet binaire.<br>中：`\Sigma=\{0,1\}` 是二进制字母表。                                                                                 |
| `w`                  | word, mot, 字符串 / 字                                | A finite sequence of symbols from an alphabet.                                        | EN: `0101` is a word over `\{0,1\}`.<br>FR: `0101` est un mot sur `\{0,1\}`.<br>中：`0101` 是字母表 `\{0,1\}` 上的字符串。                                                                                              |
| `\lvert w\rvert`     | length of a word, longueur d’un mot, 字符串长度        | The number of symbols in a word.                                                      | EN: If `w=0101`, then `\lvert w\rvert=4`.<br>FR: Si `w=0101`, alors `\lvert w\rvert=4`.<br>中：若 `w=0101`，则 `\lvert w\rvert=4`。                                                                               |
| `uv`                 | concatenation, concaténation, 连接                  | The operation of joining two strings end to end.                                      | EN: If `u=ab` and `v=ba`, then `uv=abba`.<br>FR: Si `u=ab` et `v=ba`, alors `uv=abba`.<br>中：若 `u=ab` 且 `v=ba`，则 `uv=abba`。                                                                                  |
| `w^R`                | reversal, renversement, 反转串                       | The string obtained by reversing the order of symbols.                                | EN: If `w=abc`, then `w^R=cba`.<br>FR: Si `w=abc`, alors `w^R=cba`.<br>中：若 `w=abc`，则 `w^R=cba`。                                                                                                             |
| `L\subseteq\Sigma^*` | formal language, langage formel, 形式语言             | A set of finite strings over an alphabet.                                             | EN: The set of binary strings with even length is a language.<br>FR: L’ensemble des chaînes binaires de longueur paire est un langage.<br>中：所有偶数长度二进制串构成一种形式语言。                                             |
| —                    | regular language, langage régulier, 正则语言          | A language recognizable by a finite automaton.                                        | EN: Binary strings ending in `1` form a regular language.<br>FR: Les chaînes binaires se terminant par `1` forment un langage régulier.<br>中：以 `1` 结尾的二进制串构成正则语言。                                           |
| —                    | finite automaton, automate fini, 有限自动机            | A model of computation with finitely many states recognizing regular languages.       | EN: A finite automaton can recognize strings with an even number of zeros.<br>FR: Un automate fini peut reconnaître les chaînes avec un nombre pair de zéros.<br>中：有限自动机可以识别含偶数个 `0` 的字符串。                  |
| `Q`                  | state set, ensemble des états, 状态集                | The finite set of states in an automaton.                                             | EN: An automaton changes state after reading each symbol.<br>FR: Un automate change d’état après avoir lu chaque symbole.<br>中：自动机每读取一个符号后改变状态。                                                             |
| `q_0`                | start state, état initial, 初始状态                   | The state where an automaton begins computation.                                      | EN: The machine starts in state `q_0`.<br>FR: La machine commence dans l’état `q_0`.<br>中：机器从状态 `q_0` 开始。                                                                                                   |
| `F`                  | accepting states, états acceptants, 接受状态          | States in which an automaton accepts the input string.                                | EN: A string is accepted if the final state lies in `F`.<br>FR: Une chaîne est acceptée si l’état final appartient à `F`.<br>中：若最终状态属于 `F`，则字符串被接受。                                                         |
| `\delta`             | transition function, fonction de transition, 转移函数 | The rule describing how an automaton moves from state to state.                       | EN: `\delta(q,a)` gives the next state after reading symbol `a` in state `q`.<br>FR: `\delta(q,a)` donne l’état suivant après lecture de `a` dans l’état `q`.<br>中：`\delta(q,a)` 给出在状态 `q` 读入符号 `a` 后的下一状态。 |
| —                    | regular expression, expression régulière, 正则表达式   | A symbolic description of a regular language.                                         | EN: `(0\lor1)^*1` describes binary strings ending in `1`.<br>FR: `(0\lor1)^*1` décrit les chaînes binaires finissant par `1`.<br>中：`(0\lor1)^*1` 描述以 `1` 结尾的二进制串。                                           |
| `L^*`                | Kleene star, étoile de Kleene, Kleene 星号          | The set of all finite concatenations of strings from `L`, including the empty string. | EN: If `L=\{ab\}`, then `L^*=\{\varepsilon,ab,abab,\ldots\}`.<br>FR: Si `L=\{ab\}`, alors `L^*=\{\varepsilon,ab,abab,\ldots\}`.<br>中：若 `L=\{ab\}`，则 `L^*=\{\varepsilon,ab,abab,\ldots\}`。                   |

## 13.12 Discrete probability on finite structures

| Symbol (LaTeX)                                   | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                                           | Definition                                                                                           | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                    |
| ------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `P(A)=\frac{\lvert A\rvert}{\lvert\Omega\rvert}` | uniform finite probability, probabilité uniforme finie, 有限均匀概率                              | Probability under equally likely outcomes in a finite sample space.                                  | EN: Drawing one card from `52` cards gives probability `4/52` of an ace.<br>FR: Tirer une carte parmi `52` donne une probabilité `4/52` d’obtenir un as.<br>中：从 `52` 张牌中抽一张，抽到 A 的概率是 `4/52`。               |
| —                                                | random graph, graphe aléatoire, 随机图                                                         | A graph selected according to a probability model.                                                   | EN: In one model, each possible edge appears independently with probability `p`.<br>FR: Dans un modèle, chaque arête possible apparaît indépendamment avec probabilité `p`.<br>中：在一种模型中，每条可能的边以概率 `p` 独立出现。 |
| `G(n,p)`                                         | Erdős-Rényi random graph, graphe aléatoire d’Erdős-Rényi, Erdős-Rényi 随机图                   | A random graph on `n` vertices where each edge appears independently with probability `p`.           | EN: `G(n,p)` becomes more connected as `p` increases.<br>FR: `G(n,p)` devient plus connexe lorsque `p` augmente.<br>中：随着 `p` 增大，`G(n,p)` 更可能连通。                                                             |
| `\mathbb{E}[X]`                                  | expected count, nombre espéré, 期望计数                                                         | The average number of objects satisfying a property in a random structure.                           | EN: The expected number of heads in `10` fair coin flips is `5`.<br>FR: Le nombre espéré de piles en `10` lancers équilibrés est `5`.<br>中：公平硬币抛 `10` 次，正面次数的期望是 `5`。                                       |
| `I_A`                                            | indicator variable, variable indicatrice, 指示变量                                              | A variable equal to `1` if event `A` occurs and `0` otherwise.                                       | EN: If `I_A` indicates success, then `\mathbb{E}[I_A]=P(A)`.<br>FR: Si `I_A` indique un succès, alors `\mathbb{E}[I_A]=P(A)`.<br>中：若 `I_A` 指示事件成功，则 `\mathbb{E}[I_A]=P(A)`。                                 |
| `X=\sum_i I_i`                                   | sum of indicators, somme d’indicatrices, 指示变量求和                                             | A method for counting random objects by adding indicator variables.                                  | EN: Count selected edges by summing edge indicators.<br>FR: Compter les arêtes sélectionnées en sommant des indicatrices d’arêtes.<br>中：可通过边的指示变量求和来计算被选中的边数。                                               |
| `\mathbb{E}[X]=\sum_i\mathbb{E}[I_i]`            | linearity of expectation for counting, linéarité de l’espérance pour le comptage, 计数中的期望线性性 | Expected counts can be computed by summing expected indicators, even without independence.           | EN: Linearity of expectation does not require independence.<br>FR: La linéarité de l’espérance ne nécessite pas l’indépendance.<br>中：期望线性性不要求独立性。                                                           |
| —                                                | random walk on a graph, marche aléatoire sur un graphe, 图上的随机游走                             | A stochastic process moving from vertex to neighboring vertex according to transition probabilities. | EN: A random walk on a graph models diffusion or browsing.<br>FR: Une marche aléatoire sur un graphe modélise la diffusion ou la navigation.<br>中：图上的随机游走可建模扩散或浏览行为。                                        |
| `P_{ij}`                                         | transition probability, probabilité de transition, 转移概率                                     | The probability of moving from state or vertex `i` to state or vertex `j`.                           | EN: In a Markov chain, `P_{ij}` is the one-step transition probability.<br>FR: Dans une chaîne de Markov, `P_{ij}` est la probabilité de transition en un pas.<br>中：在马尔可夫链中，`P_{ij}` 是一步转移概率。               |
| `\pi`                                            | stationary distribution, distribution stationnaire, 平稳分布                                    | A probability distribution unchanged by the transition rule of a Markov chain.                       | EN: A stationary distribution satisfies `\pi P=\pi`.<br>FR: Une distribution stationnaire vérifie `\pi P=\pi`.<br>中：平稳分布满足 `\pi P=\pi`。                                                                     |

# 14. Abstract Algebra, Algebraic Structures, and Symmetry

## 14.1 Algebraic structures and binary operations

| Symbol (LaTeX)      | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                      | Definition                                                                                         | Examples                                                                                                                                                                         |
| ------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `S`                 | set with structure, ensemble muni d’une structure, 带结构的集合              | A set together with one or more operations or relations satisfying specified rules.                | EN: `\mathbb{Z}` with addition is an algebraic structure.<br>FR: `\mathbb{Z}` muni de l’addition est une structure algébrique.<br>中：带有加法的 `\mathbb{Z}` 是一种代数结构。                  |
| `*:S\times S\to S`  | binary operation, opération binaire, 二元运算                              | A rule combining two elements of a set to produce another element of the same set.                 | EN: Addition is a binary operation on `\mathbb{Z}`.<br>FR: L’addition est une opération binaire sur `\mathbb{Z}`.<br>中：加法是 `\mathbb{Z}` 上的二元运算。                                  |
| `a*b`               | operation value, valeur d’une opération, 运算结果                          | The result of applying a binary operation to `a` and `b`.                                          | EN: If `*=+`, then `3*4=7`.<br>FR: Si `*=+`, alors `3*4=7`.<br>中：若 `*=+`，则 `3*4=7`。                                                                                              |
| `a*b\in S`          | closure, stabilité / fermeture, 封闭性                                    | An operation is closed on a set if combining elements of the set always stays in the set.          | EN: Integers are closed under addition.<br>FR: Les entiers sont stables par addition.<br>中：整数对加法封闭。                                                                              |
| `(a*b)*c=a*(b*c)`   | associativity, associativité, 结合律                                      | An operation is associative if grouping does not affect the result.                                | EN: Addition of real numbers is associative.<br>FR: L’addition des réels est associative.<br>中：实数加法满足结合律。                                                                        |
| `a*b=b*a`           | commutativity, commutativité, 交换律                                      | An operation is commutative if switching the order of two inputs does not change the result.       | EN: Multiplication of real numbers is commutative.<br>FR: La multiplication des réels est commutative.<br>中：实数乘法满足交换律。                                                           |
| `e*a=a*e=a`         | identity element, élément neutre, 单位元                                  | An element that leaves every element unchanged under an operation.                                 | EN: `0` is the identity element for addition.<br>FR: `0` est l’élément neutre pour l’addition.<br>中：`0` 是加法的单位元。                                                                 |
| `a^{-1}`            | inverse element, élément inverse, 逆元                                   | An element that combines with `a` to give the identity element.                                    | EN: Under addition, the inverse of `5` is `-5`.<br>FR: Pour l’addition, l’inverse de `5` est `-5`.<br>中：在加法下，`5` 的逆元是 `-5`。                                                      |
| `a^{-1}a=aa^{-1}=e` | inverse law, loi d’inversion, 逆元律                                      | An element and its inverse combine to produce the identity.                                        | EN: In multiplication, `2\cdot\frac{1}{2}=1`.<br>FR: Pour la multiplication, `2\cdot\frac{1}{2}=1`.<br>中：在乘法中，`2\cdot\frac{1}{2}=1`。                                             |
| `a(bc)=(ab)c`       | omitted operation notation, notation sans symbole d’opération, 省略运算符记号 | In many algebraic structures, multiplication-like operations are written without a visible symbol. | EN: `abc` usually means `(ab)c` when the operation is associative.<br>FR: `abc` signifie souvent `(ab)c` lorsque l’opération est associative.<br>中：当运算满足结合律时，`abc` 通常表示 `(ab)c`。 |
| `a^n`               | repeated product, puissance itérée, 重复乘积 / 幂                           | Repeated application of a multiplication-like operation to the same element.                       | EN: In a group, `a^3=aaa`.<br>FR: Dans un groupe, `a^3=aaa`.<br>中：在群中，`a^3=aaa`。                                                                                                 |
| `na`                | repeated sum, somme itérée, 重复加和                                       | Repeated addition of the same element.                                                             | EN: In additive notation, `3a=a+a+a`.<br>FR: En notation additive, `3a=a+a+a`.<br>中：在加法记号中，`3a=a+a+a`。                                                                           |

## 14.2 Groups and subgroups

| Symbol (LaTeX)     | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                  | Definition                                                                                      | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| ------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------ | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `(G,*)`            | group, groupe, 群                                                   | A set with an associative binary operation, an identity element, and inverses for all elements. | EN: `(\mathbb{Z},+)` is a group.<br>FR: `(\mathbb{Z},+)` est un groupe.<br>中：`(\mathbb{Z},+)` 是一个群。                                                                                                      |
| `G`                | underlying set of a group, ensemble sous-jacent d’un groupe, 群的底集合 | The set of elements on which the group operation is defined.                                    | EN: In `(\mathbb{Z},+)`, the underlying set is `\mathbb{Z}`.<br>FR: Dans `(\mathbb{Z},+)`, l’ensemble sous-jacent est `\mathbb{Z}`.<br>中：在 `(\mathbb{Z},+)` 中，底集合是 `\mathbb{Z}`。                         |
| `e`                | group identity, élément neutre du groupe, 群单位元                     | The identity element of a group.                                                                | EN: In `(\mathbb{R}^{\times},\cdot)`, the identity is `1`.<br>FR: Dans `(\mathbb{R}^{\times},\cdot)`, l’élément neutre est `1`.<br>中：在 `(\mathbb{R}^{\times},\cdot)` 中，单位元是 `1`。                         |
| `g^{-1}`           | inverse in a group, inverse dans un groupe, 群中逆元                   | The unique element that combines with `g` to give the identity.                                 | EN: In a multiplicative group, the inverse of `2` is `1/2`.<br>FR: Dans un groupe multiplicatif, l’inverse de `2` est `1/2`.<br>中：在乘法群中，`2` 的逆元是 `1/2`。                                                  |
| `ab`               | group product, produit dans un groupe, 群乘积                         | The result of applying the group operation to two elements.                                     | EN: In matrix groups, `AB` is matrix multiplication.<br>FR: Dans les groupes de matrices, `AB` est la multiplication matricielle.<br>中：在矩阵群中，`AB` 是矩阵乘法。                                                 |
| `ab\neq ba`        | nonabelian group, groupe non abélien, 非阿贝尔群                        | A group whose operation is not necessarily commutative.                                         | EN: Matrix multiplication usually gives nonabelian groups.<br>FR: La multiplication matricielle donne souvent des groupes non abéliens.<br>中：矩阵乘法通常产生非阿贝尔群。                                              |
| `ab=ba`            | abelian group, groupe abélien, 阿贝尔群 / 交换群                          | A group whose operation is commutative.                                                         | EN: `(\mathbb{Z},+)` is an abelian group.<br>FR: `(\mathbb{Z},+)` est un groupe abélien.<br>中：`(\mathbb{Z},+)` 是阿贝尔群。                                                                                    |
| `H\leq G`          | subgroup, sous-groupe, 子群                                          | A subset of a group that is itself a group under the inherited operation.                       | EN: `2\mathbb{Z}` is a subgroup of `(\mathbb{Z},+)`.<br>FR: `2\mathbb{Z}` est un sous-groupe de `(\mathbb{Z},+)`.<br>中：`2\mathbb{Z}` 是 `(\mathbb{Z},+)` 的子群。                                             |
| `\{e\}`            | trivial subgroup, sous-groupe trivial, 平凡子群                        | The subgroup containing only the identity element.                                              | EN: Every group has the trivial subgroup `\{e\}`.<br>FR: Tout groupe possède le sous-groupe trivial `\{e\}`.<br>中：每个群都有平凡子群 `\{e\}`。                                                                     |
| `G`                | improper subgroup, sous-groupe impropre, 非真子群                      | The whole group considered as a subgroup of itself.                                             | EN: `G` is always a subgroup of `G`.<br>FR: `G` est toujours un sous-groupe de `G`.<br>中：`G` 总是自身的一个子群。                                                                                                  |
| `H<G`              | proper subgroup, sous-groupe propre, 真子群                           | A subgroup strictly smaller than the whole group.                                               | EN: `2\mathbb{Z}<\mathbb{Z}` under addition.<br>FR: `2\mathbb{Z}<\mathbb{Z}` pour l’addition.<br>中：在加法下，`2\mathbb{Z}<\mathbb{Z}`。                                                                        |
| `\langle S\rangle` | subgroup generated by S, sous-groupe engendré par S, 由 S 生成的子群     | The smallest subgroup containing a given subset `S`.                                            | EN: In `\mathbb{Z}`, `\langle 3\rangle=3\mathbb{Z}`.<br>FR: Dans `\mathbb{Z}`, `\langle 3\rangle=3\mathbb{Z}`.<br>中：在 `\mathbb{Z}` 中，`\langle 3\rangle=3\mathbb{Z}`。                                     |
| `\lvert G\rvert`   | order of a group, ordre d’un groupe, 群的阶                           | The number of elements in a finite group.                                                       | EN: The group `\mathbb{Z}/5\mathbb{Z}` has order `5`.<br>FR: Le groupe `\mathbb{Z}/5\mathbb{Z}` a pour ordre `5`.<br>中：群 `\mathbb{Z}/5\mathbb{Z}` 的阶是 `5`。                                               |
| `\lvert g\rvert`   | order of an element, ordre d’un élément, 元素的阶                      | The smallest positive integer `n` such that `g^n=e`, if it exists.                              | EN: In `\mathbb{Z}/6\mathbb{Z}`, the element `2` has additive order `3`.<br>FR: Dans `\mathbb{Z}/6\mathbb{Z}`, l’élément `2` a un ordre additif `3`.<br>中：在 `\mathbb{Z}/6\mathbb{Z}` 中，元素 `2` 的加法阶是 `3`。 |

## 14.3 Cyclic groups and generated structures

| Symbol (LaTeX)                    | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                            | Definition                                                                  | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                         |
| --------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------ | --------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `\langle g\rangle`                | cyclic subgroup, sous-groupe cyclique, 循环子群                  | The subgroup consisting of all powers or multiples of a single element `g`. | EN: In additive notation, `\langle g\rangle=\{ng:n\in\mathbb{Z}\}`.<br>FR: En notation additive, `\langle g\rangle=\{ng:n\in\mathbb{Z}\}`.<br>中：在加法记号下，`\langle g\rangle=\{ng:n\in\mathbb{Z}\}`。 |
| `G=\langle g\rangle`              | cyclic group, groupe cyclique, 循环群                           | A group generated by one element.                                           | EN: `\mathbb{Z}` is cyclic, generated by `1`.<br>FR: `\mathbb{Z}` est cyclique, engendré par `1`.<br>中：`\mathbb{Z}` 是由 `1` 生成的循环群。                                                               |
| `g`                               | generator, générateur, 生成元                                   | An element whose powers or multiples generate a group or subgroup.          | EN: In `\mathbb{Z}/5\mathbb{Z}`, `1` is a generator.<br>FR: Dans `\mathbb{Z}/5\mathbb{Z}`, `1` est un générateur.<br>中：在 `\mathbb{Z}/5\mathbb{Z}` 中，`1` 是生成元。                                    |
| `g^0=e`                           | zero power, puissance zéro, 零次幂                              | The convention that the zeroth power of a group element is the identity.    | EN: For any group element `g`, `g^0=e`.<br>FR: Pour tout élément de groupe `g`, `g^0=e`.<br>中：对任意群元素 `g`，`g^0=e`。                                                                                |
| `g^{-n}=(g^{-1})^n`               | negative power, puissance négative, 负次幂                      | A power defined using the inverse element.                                  | EN: `g^{-2}=g^{-1}g^{-1}`.<br>FR: `g^{-2}=g^{-1}g^{-1}`.<br>中：`g^{-2}=g^{-1}g^{-1}`。                                                                                                             |
| `C_n`                             | cyclic group of order n, groupe cyclique d’ordre n, n 阶循环群   | A cyclic group with `n` elements.                                           | EN: Rotations of a regular pentagon form a group isomorphic to `C_5`.<br>FR: Les rotations d’un pentagone régulier forment un groupe isomorphe à `C_5`.<br>中：正五边形的旋转群同构于 `C_5`。                  |
| `\mathbb{Z}/n\mathbb{Z}`          | integers modulo n, entiers modulo n, 模 n 整数群                 | The additive cyclic group of residue classes modulo `n`.                    | EN: `\mathbb{Z}/4\mathbb{Z}` has elements `0,1,2,3` modulo `4`.<br>FR: `\mathbb{Z}/4\mathbb{Z}` a pour éléments `0,1,2,3` modulo `4`.<br>中：`\mathbb{Z}/4\mathbb{Z}` 的元素是模 `4` 的 `0,1,2,3`。       |
| `a\equiv b\pmod n`                | congruence class relation, relation de congruence, 同余类关系     | A relation identifying integers with the same remainder modulo `n`.         | EN: `7\equiv 1\pmod 3`.<br>FR: `7\equiv 1\pmod 3`.<br>中：`7\equiv 1\pmod 3`。                                                                                                                      |
| `[a]`                             | residue class, classe résiduelle, 剩余类 / 同余类                  | The set of all integers congruent to `a` modulo `n`.                        | EN: Modulo `4`, `[1]=\{\ldots,-3,1,5,9,\ldots\}`.<br>FR: Modulo `4`, `[1]=\{\ldots,-3,1,5,9,\ldots\}`.<br>中：模 `4` 下，`[1]=\{\ldots,-3,1,5,9,\ldots\}`。                                            |
| `\varphi(n)`                      | Euler phi function, indicatrice d’Euler, 欧拉函数                | The number of integers from `1` to `n` that are coprime to `n`.             | EN: `\varphi(10)=4` because `1,3,7,9` are coprime to `10`.<br>FR: `\varphi(10)=4` car `1,3,7,9` sont premiers avec `10`.<br>中：`\varphi(10)=4`，因为 `1,3,7,9` 与 `10` 互素。                            |
| `(\mathbb{Z}/n\mathbb{Z})^\times` | group of units modulo n, groupe des unités modulo n, 模 n 单位群 | The multiplicative group of invertible residue classes modulo `n`.          | EN: `(\mathbb{Z}/5\mathbb{Z})^\times=\{1,2,3,4\}`.<br>FR: `(\mathbb{Z}/5\mathbb{Z})^\times=\{1,2,3,4\}`.<br>中：`(\mathbb{Z}/5\mathbb{Z})^\times=\{1,2,3,4\}`。                                     |
| `a^{\varphi(n)}\equiv1\pmod n`    | Euler’s theorem, théorème d’Euler, 欧拉定理                      | If `a` and `n` are coprime, then `a^{\varphi(n)}\equiv1\pmod n`.            | EN: Since `\gcd(3,10)=1`, `3^4\equiv1\pmod{10}`.<br>FR: Comme `\gcd(3,10)=1`, `3^4\equiv1\pmod{10}`.<br>中：因为 `\gcd(3,10)=1`，所以 `3^4\equiv1\pmod{10}`。                                            |

## 14.4 Cosets, normal subgroups, and quotient groups

| Symbol (LaTeX)                        | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                            | Definition                                                                 | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
| ------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------ | -------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `gH`                                  | left coset, classe à gauche, 左陪集                             | The set of products `gh` with `h\in H`.                                    | EN: In an additive group, a coset has form `g+H`.<br>FR: Dans un groupe additif, une classe a la forme `g+H`.<br>中：在加法群中，陪集形式为 `g+H`。                                                                                               |
| `Hg`                                  | right coset, classe à droite, 右陪集                            | The set of products `hg` with `h\in H`.                                    | EN: In abelian groups, left and right cosets are the same.<br>FR: Dans les groupes abéliens, les classes à gauche et à droite coïncident.<br>中：在阿贝尔群中，左陪集和右陪集相同。                                                                    |
| `[G:H]`                               | index of a subgroup, indice d’un sous-groupe, 子群指数           | The number of distinct left cosets of `H` in `G`.                          | EN: In `\mathbb{Z}`, the subgroup `2\mathbb{Z}` has index `2`.<br>FR: Dans `\mathbb{Z}`, le sous-groupe `2\mathbb{Z}` a pour indice `2`.<br>中：在 `\mathbb{Z}` 中，子群 `2\mathbb{Z}` 的指数是 `2`。                                           |
| `\lvert G\rvert=\lvert H\rvert [G:H]` | Lagrange’s theorem, théorème de Lagrange, Lagrange 定理        | In a finite group, the order of a subgroup divides the order of the group. | EN: A subgroup of a group of order `12` may have order `1,2,3,4,6,12`.<br>FR: Un sous-groupe d’un groupe d’ordre `12` peut avoir ordre `1,2,3,4,6,12`.<br>中：`12` 阶群的子群阶数可能是 `1,2,3,4,6,12`。                                         |
| `N\trianglelefteq G`                  | normal subgroup, sous-groupe normal, 正规子群                    | A subgroup invariant under conjugation by elements of the group.           | EN: Every subgroup of an abelian group is normal.<br>FR: Tout sous-groupe d’un groupe abélien est normal.<br>中：阿贝尔群的每个子群都是正规子群。                                                                                                     |
| `gNg^{-1}=N`                          | conjugation invariance, invariance par conjugaison, 共轭不变性    | A criterion for a subgroup to be normal.                                   | EN: Normality means conjugation does not move the subgroup outside itself.<br>FR: La normalité signifie que la conjugaison ne sort pas du sous-groupe.<br>中：正规性表示共轭不会把子群移出自身。                                                       |
| `G/N`                                 | quotient group, groupe quotient, 商群                          | The group whose elements are cosets of a normal subgroup `N`.              | EN: `\mathbb{Z}/n\mathbb{Z}` is the quotient of `\mathbb{Z}` by `n\mathbb{Z}`.<br>FR: `\mathbb{Z}/n\mathbb{Z}` est le quotient de `\mathbb{Z}` par `n\mathbb{Z}`.<br>中：`\mathbb{Z}/n\mathbb{Z}` 是 `\mathbb{Z}` 对 `n\mathbb{Z}` 的商群。 |
| `gN\cdot hN=ghN`                      | quotient group operation, opération du groupe quotient, 商群运算 | The operation on cosets induced by the group operation.                    | EN: In a quotient group, multiply representatives and then take the coset.<br>FR: Dans un groupe quotient, on multiplie les représentants puis on prend la classe.<br>中：在商群中，先乘代表元，再取陪集。                                            |
| `ghg^{-1}`                            | conjugate element, élément conjugué, 共轭元素                    | The element obtained by conjugating `h` by `g`.                            | EN: In matrix groups, `ABA^{-1}` is conjugate to `B`.<br>FR: Dans les groupes de matrices, `ABA^{-1}` est conjugué à `B`.<br>中：在矩阵群中，`ABA^{-1}` 与 `B` 共轭。                                                                           |
| `C_G(g)`                              | centralizer, centralisateur, 中心化子                            | The set of group elements that commute with `g`.                           | EN: `C_G(g)=\{x\in G:xg=gx\}`.<br>FR: `C_G(g)=\{x\in G:xg=gx\}`.<br>中：`C_G(g)=\{x\in G:xg=gx\}`。                                                                                                                                    |
| `Z(G)`                                | center of a group, centre d’un groupe, 群中心                   | The set of elements that commute with every element of the group.          | EN: An abelian group equals its center.<br>FR: Un groupe abélien est égal à son centre.<br>中：阿贝尔群等于自己的中心。                                                                                                                           |
| `G/Z(G)`                              | quotient by the center, quotient par le centre, 中心商群         | The quotient group formed by dividing by the center.                       | EN: Quotients by the center measure noncommutativity in some settings.<br>FR: Les quotients par le centre mesurent parfois la non-commutativité.<br>中：中心商群在某些情形下可度量非交换性。                                                            |

## 14.5 Group homomorphisms and isomorphisms

| Symbol (LaTeX)                               | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                  | Definition                                                                            | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
| -------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `\varphi:G\to H`                             | group homomorphism, homomorphisme de groupes, 群同态                  | A map between groups preserving the group operation.                                  | EN: A homomorphism satisfies `\varphi(ab)=\varphi(a)\varphi(b)`.<br>FR: Un homomorphisme vérifie `\varphi(ab)=\varphi(a)\varphi(b)`.<br>中：群同态满足 `\varphi(ab)=\varphi(a)\varphi(b)`。                                  |
| `\ker\varphi`                                | kernel, noyau, 核                                                   | The set of elements mapped to the identity element.                                   | EN: `\ker\varphi=\{g\in G:\varphi(g)=e_H\}`.<br>FR: `\ker\varphi=\{g\in G:\varphi(g)=e_H\}`.<br>中：`\ker\varphi=\{g\in G:\varphi(g)=e_H\}`。                                                                           |
| `\operatorname{im}\varphi`                   | image, image, 像                                                    | The set of elements in the codomain reached by the homomorphism.                      | EN: The image of a group homomorphism is a subgroup of the codomain.<br>FR: L’image d’un homomorphisme de groupes est un sous-groupe du codomaine.<br>中：群同态的像是陪域中的一个子群。                                              |
| `\varphi(e_G)=e_H`                           | identity preservation, préservation de l’élément neutre, 单位元保持     | A group homomorphism maps the identity of the domain to the identity of the codomain. | EN: Every group homomorphism sends identity to identity.<br>FR: Tout homomorphisme de groupes envoie le neutre sur le neutre.<br>中：每个群同态都把单位元映到单位元。                                                                  |
| `\varphi(g^{-1})=\varphi(g)^{-1}`            | inverse preservation, préservation des inverses, 逆元保持              | A group homomorphism sends inverses to inverses.                                      | EN: Homomorphisms preserve inverse structure.<br>FR: Les homomorphismes préservent la structure des inverses.<br>中：群同态保持逆元结构。                                                                                        |
| `G\cong H`                                   | isomorphic groups, groupes isomorphes, 同构群                         | Groups that are structurally the same through a bijective homomorphism.               | EN: `(\mathbb{Z}/3\mathbb{Z},+)` is isomorphic to `C_3`.<br>FR: `(\mathbb{Z}/3\mathbb{Z},+)` est isomorphe à `C_3`.<br>中：`(\mathbb{Z}/3\mathbb{Z},+)` 同构于 `C_3`。                                                     |
| `\varphi^{-1}`                               | inverse isomorphism, isomorphisme inverse, 逆同构                     | The inverse map of a group isomorphism, also preserving group structure.              | EN: If `\varphi` is an isomorphism, then `\varphi^{-1}` is also an isomorphism.<br>FR: Si `\varphi` est un isomorphisme, alors `\varphi^{-1}` est aussi un isomorphisme.<br>中：若 `\varphi` 是同构，则 `\varphi^{-1}` 也是同构。 |
| `\operatorname{Aut}(G)`                      | automorphism group, groupe des automorphismes, 自同构群                | The group of isomorphisms from `G` to itself.                                         | EN: `\operatorname{Aut}(G)` describes symmetries of the group structure.<br>FR: `\operatorname{Aut}(G)` décrit les symétries de la structure de groupe.<br>中：`\operatorname{Aut}(G)` 描述群结构自身的对称性。                    |
| `\operatorname{End}(G)`                      | endomorphism set, ensemble des endomorphismes, 自同态集合               | The set of homomorphisms from a group to itself.                                      | EN: Every automorphism is an endomorphism, but not every endomorphism is invertible.<br>FR: Tout automorphisme est un endomorphisme, mais tout endomorphisme n’est pas inversible.<br>中：每个自同构都是自同态，但并非每个自同态都可逆。      |
| `G/\ker\varphi\cong\operatorname{im}\varphi` | first isomorphism theorem, premier théorème d’isomorphisme, 第一同构定理 | A theorem identifying a quotient group with the image of a homomorphism.              | EN: The first isomorphism theorem explains why kernels produce quotient groups.<br>FR: Le premier théorème d’isomorphisme explique pourquoi les noyaux produisent des groupes quotients.<br>中：第一同构定理说明为什么核会产生商群。     |
| `\ker\varphi=\{e\}`                          | injectivity criterion, critère d’injectivité, 单射判别                 | A group homomorphism is injective exactly when its kernel is trivial.                 | EN: A homomorphism with trivial kernel is one-to-one.<br>FR: Un homomorphisme de noyau trivial est injectif.<br>中：核平凡的群同态是单射。                                                                                        |
| `\operatorname{im}\varphi=H`                 | surjectivity criterion, critère de surjectivité, 满射判别              | A homomorphism is surjective exactly when its image equals the codomain.              | EN: If every element of `H` is reached, then `\varphi` is onto.<br>FR: Si tout élément de `H` est atteint, alors `\varphi` est surjectif.<br>中：若 `H` 中每个元素都被映到，则 `\varphi` 是满射。                                      |

## 14.6 Symmetry groups and permutation groups

| Symbol (LaTeX)                                      | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                 | Definition                                                                            | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                       |
| --------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `S_n`                                               | symmetric group, groupe symétrique, 对称群                           | The group of all permutations of `n` elements.                                        | EN: `S_3` contains all permutations of three objects.<br>FR: `S_3` contient toutes les permutations de trois objets.<br>中：`S_3` 包含三个对象的所有排列。                                                   |
| `\sigma`                                            | permutation, permutation, 置换                                      | A bijection from a finite set to itself.                                              | EN: A permutation rearranges elements without losing any.<br>FR: Une permutation réarrange les éléments sans en perdre.<br>中：置换重新排列元素且不遗漏元素。                                                   |
| `\sigma\tau`                                        | composition of permutations, composition de permutations, 置换复合    | The product operation in a permutation group, applying one permutation after another. | EN: In permutation groups, order of composition matters.<br>FR: Dans les groupes de permutations, l’ordre de composition compte.<br>中：在置换群中，复合顺序很重要。                                           |
| `(1\ 2\ 3)`                                         | cycle notation, notation cyclique, 轮换记号                           | A notation describing a permutation by how elements move in cycles.                   | EN: `(1\ 2\ 3)` sends `1` to `2`, `2` to `3`, and `3` to `1`.<br>FR: `(1\ 2\ 3)` envoie `1` sur `2`, `2` sur `3`, et `3` sur `1`.<br>中：`(1\ 2\ 3)` 把 `1` 映到 `2`，`2` 映到 `3`，`3` 映到 `1`。         |
| `(a\ b)`                                            | transposition, transposition, 对换                                  | A permutation that swaps two elements and fixes all others.                           | EN: `(1\ 3)` swaps `1` and `3`.<br>FR: `(1\ 3)` échange `1` et `3`.<br>中：`(1\ 3)` 交换 `1` 和 `3`。                                                                                                |
| `\operatorname{sgn}(\sigma)`                        | sign of a permutation, signature d’une permutation, 置换符号          | `1` for even permutations and `-1` for odd permutations.                              | EN: A transposition has sign `-1`.<br>FR: Une transposition a pour signature `-1`.<br>中：一个对换的符号是 `-1`。                                                                                         |
| `A_n`                                               | alternating group, groupe alterné, 交错群                            | The subgroup of `S_n` consisting of even permutations.                                | EN: `A_3` has three elements.<br>FR: `A_3` possède trois éléments.<br>中：`A_3` 有三个元素。                                                                                                           |
| `D_n`                                               | dihedral group, groupe diédral, 二面体群                              | The symmetry group of a regular `n`-gon, including rotations and reflections.         | EN: `D_4` is the symmetry group of a square.<br>FR: `D_4` est le groupe des symétries d’un carré.<br>中：`D_4` 是正方形的对称群。                                                                         |
| `C_n`                                               | rotation group, groupe des rotations, 旋转群                         | The cyclic subgroup of rotations of a regular `n`-gon.                                | EN: The rotations of a regular hexagon form `C_6`.<br>FR: Les rotations d’un hexagone régulier forment `C_6`.<br>中：正六边形的旋转构成 `C_6`。                                                            |
| —                                                   | symmetry group, groupe de symétries, 对称群                          | A group of transformations preserving the structure of an object.                     | EN: The symmetries of a square form a group under composition.<br>FR: Les symétries d’un carré forment un groupe par composition.<br>中：正方形的对称变换在复合下构成群。                                        |
| —                                                   | group action, action de groupe, 群作用                               | A way for group elements to act as transformations on a set.                          | EN: `S_n` acts on the set `\{1,\ldots,n\}` by permuting elements.<br>FR: `S_n` agit sur `\{1,\ldots,n\}` en permutant les éléments.<br>中：`S_n` 通过置换元素作用在 `\{1,\ldots,n\}` 上。                   |
| `Gx`                                                | orbit, orbite, 轨道                                                 | The set of all points reachable from `x` by the action of elements of `G`.            | EN: The orbit of a vertex under square symmetries contains all four vertices.<br>FR: L’orbite d’un sommet sous les symétries du carré contient les quatre sommets.<br>中：正方形顶点在对称群作用下的轨道包含四个顶点。 |
| `G_x`                                               | stabilizer, stabilisateur, 稳定子                                    | The subgroup of elements that fix a point `x`.                                        | EN: The stabilizer of a vertex contains symmetries that leave it fixed.<br>FR: Le stabilisateur d’un sommet contient les symétries qui le fixent.<br>中：顶点的稳定子包含保持该顶点不动的对称。                     |
| `\lvert Gx\rvert [\lvert G_x\rvert]=\lvert G\rvert` | orbit-stabilizer theorem, théorème orbite-stabilisateur, 轨道-稳定子定理 | For a finite group action, orbit size times stabilizer size equals group order.       | EN: Orbit-stabilizer links symmetry counts to fixed-point counts.<br>FR: Le théorème orbite-stabilisateur relie les tailles d’orbites et de stabilisateurs.<br>中：轨道-稳定子定理把轨道大小与稳定子大小联系起来。      |

## 14.7 Rings, ideals, and quotient rings

| Symbol (LaTeX)                               | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                                           | Definition                                                                                                                               | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
| -------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `(R,+,\cdot)`                                | ring, anneau, 环                                                                             | A set with addition and multiplication where addition forms an abelian group, multiplication is associative, and distributive laws hold. | EN: `\mathbb{Z}` is a ring.<br>FR: `\mathbb{Z}` est un anneau.<br>中：`\mathbb{Z}` 是一个环。                                                                                                                                              |
| `0_R`                                        | additive identity, élément neutre additif, 加法单位元 / 零元                                       | The identity element for addition in a ring.                                                                                             | EN: In any ring, `a+0_R=a`.<br>FR: Dans tout anneau, `a+0_R=a`.<br>中：在任意环中，`a+0_R=a`。                                                                                                                                               |
| `1_R`                                        | multiplicative identity, élément unité, 乘法单位元                                               | An element satisfying `1_Ra=a1_R=a`, when the ring has identity.                                                                         | EN: In `\mathbb{Z}`, the multiplicative identity is `1`.<br>FR: Dans `\mathbb{Z}`, l’unité multiplicative est `1`.<br>中：在 `\mathbb{Z}` 中，乘法单位元是 `1`。                                                                                |
| `a(b+c)=ab+ac`                               | left distributive law, distributivité à gauche, 左分配律                                        | Multiplication distributes over addition from the left.                                                                                  | EN: `2(3+4)=2\cdot3+2\cdot4`.<br>FR: `2(3+4)=2\cdot3+2\cdot4`.<br>中：`2(3+4)=2\cdot3+2\cdot4`。                                                                                                                                       |
| `(a+b)c=ac+bc`                               | right distributive law, distributivité à droite, 右分配律                                       | Multiplication distributes over addition from the right.                                                                                 | EN: `(3+4)2=3\cdot2+4\cdot2`.<br>FR: `(3+4)2=3\cdot2+4\cdot2`.<br>中：`(3+4)2=3\cdot2+4\cdot2`。                                                                                                                                       |
| `ab=ba`                                      | commutative ring, anneau commutatif, 交换环                                                    | A ring whose multiplication is commutative.                                                                                              | EN: Polynomial rings over fields are commutative.<br>FR: Les anneaux de polynômes sur des corps sont commutatifs.<br>中：域上的多项式环是交换环。                                                                                                 |
| `R^\times`                                   | group of units, groupe des unités, 单位群                                                      | The multiplicative group of invertible elements in a ring with identity.                                                                 | EN: In `\mathbb{Z}`, the units are `1` and `-1`.<br>FR: Dans `\mathbb{Z}`, les unités sont `1` et `-1`.<br>中：在 `\mathbb{Z}` 中，单位是 `1` 和 `-1`。                                                                                       |
| `I\trianglelefteq R`                         | ideal, idéal, 理想                                                                            | A subset closed under addition and absorbing multiplication by ring elements.                                                            | EN: `n\mathbb{Z}` is an ideal of `\mathbb{Z}`.<br>FR: `n\mathbb{Z}` est un idéal de `\mathbb{Z}`.<br>中：`n\mathbb{Z}` 是 `\mathbb{Z}` 的理想。                                                                                            |
| `(a)`                                        | principal ideal, idéal principal, 主理想                                                       | The ideal generated by one element.                                                                                                      | EN: In `\mathbb{Z}`, `(6)=6\mathbb{Z}`.<br>FR: Dans `\mathbb{Z}`, `(6)=6\mathbb{Z}`.<br>中：在 `\mathbb{Z}` 中，`(6)=6\mathbb{Z}`。                                                                                                       |
| `R/I`                                        | quotient ring, anneau quotient, 商环                                                          | The ring of cosets modulo an ideal `I`.                                                                                                  | EN: `\mathbb{Z}/n\mathbb{Z}` is a quotient ring.<br>FR: `\mathbb{Z}/n\mathbb{Z}` est un anneau quotient.<br>中：`\mathbb{Z}/n\mathbb{Z}` 是商环。                                                                                         |
| `a+I`                                        | coset modulo an ideal, classe modulo un idéal, 模理想陪集                                        | The equivalence class of `a` in a quotient ring.                                                                                         | EN: In `R/I`, elements have form `a+I`.<br>FR: Dans `R/I`, les éléments ont la forme `a+I`.<br>中：在 `R/I` 中，元素形式为 `a+I`。                                                                                                             |
| `\varphi:R\to S`                             | ring homomorphism, homomorphisme d’anneaux, 环同态                                             | A map preserving addition and multiplication, often also preserving identity when required.                                              | EN: Evaluation at a point is a ring homomorphism from polynomials to numbers.<br>FR: L’évaluation en un point est un homomorphisme d’anneaux des polynômes vers les nombres.<br>中：在一点处求值是从多项式到数的环同态。                                |
| `\ker\varphi`                                | kernel of a ring homomorphism, noyau d’un homomorphisme d’anneaux, 环同态的核                    | The set of ring elements mapped to zero.                                                                                                 | EN: The kernel of a ring homomorphism is an ideal.<br>FR: Le noyau d’un homomorphisme d’anneaux est un idéal.<br>中：环同态的核是一个理想。                                                                                                      |
| `R/\ker\varphi\cong\operatorname{im}\varphi` | first isomorphism theorem for rings, premier théorème d’isomorphisme pour anneaux, 环的第一同构定理 | A theorem identifying a quotient ring with the image of a ring homomorphism.                                                             | EN: Ring kernels create quotient rings just as group kernels create quotient groups.<br>FR: Les noyaux d’anneaux créent des anneaux quotients comme les noyaux de groupes créent des groupes quotients.<br>中：环同态的核产生商环，类似群同态的核产生商群。 |

## 14.8 Integral domains, fields, and polynomial rings

| Symbol (LaTeX)             | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                       | Definition                                                                                                                            | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| -------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `ab=0`                     | zero product, produit nul, 零乘积                                          | A product equal to the zero element of a ring.                                                                                        | EN: In an integral domain, `ab=0` implies `a=0` or `b=0`.<br>FR: Dans un anneau intègre, `ab=0` implique `a=0` ou `b=0`.<br>中：在整环中，`ab=0` 推出 `a=0` 或 `b=0`。                                                                                                                |
| —                          | zero divisor, diviseur de zéro, 零因子                                     | A nonzero element that multiplies with another nonzero element to give zero.                                                          | EN: In `\mathbb{Z}/6\mathbb{Z}`, `2\cdot3=0`, so `2` and `3` are zero divisors.<br>FR: Dans `\mathbb{Z}/6\mathbb{Z}`, `2\cdot3=0`, donc `2` et `3` sont des diviseurs de zéro.<br>中：在 `\mathbb{Z}/6\mathbb{Z}` 中，`2\cdot3=0`，所以 `2` 和 `3` 是零因子。                            |
| —                          | integral domain, anneau intègre, 整环                                     | A nonzero commutative ring with identity and no zero divisors.                                                                        | EN: `\mathbb{Z}` is an integral domain.<br>FR: `\mathbb{Z}` est un anneau intègre.<br>中：`\mathbb{Z}` 是整环。                                                                                                                                                                  |
| `F`                        | field, corps, 域                                                         | A commutative ring with identity in which every nonzero element has a multiplicative inverse.                                         | EN: `\mathbb{Q}`, `\mathbb{R}`, and `\mathbb{C}` are fields.<br>FR: `\mathbb{Q}`, `\mathbb{R}` et `\mathbb{C}` sont des corps.<br>中：`\mathbb{Q}`、`\mathbb{R}`、`\mathbb{C}` 都是域。                                                                                            |
| `F^\times=F\setminus\{0\}` | multiplicative group of a field, groupe multiplicatif d’un corps, 域的乘法群 | The group of all nonzero elements of a field under multiplication.                                                                    | EN: `\mathbb{R}^{\times}` is the multiplicative group of nonzero real numbers.<br>FR: `\mathbb{R}^{\times}` est le groupe multiplicatif des réels non nuls.<br>中：`\mathbb{R}^{\times}` 是非零实数乘法群。                                                                           |
| `\mathbb{F}_p`             | finite field with p elements, corps fini à p éléments, p 元有限域           | The field of integers modulo a prime `p`.                                                                                             | EN: `\mathbb{F}_5` has elements `0,1,2,3,4`.<br>FR: `\mathbb{F}_5` a pour éléments `0,1,2,3,4`.<br>中：`\mathbb{F}_5` 的元素是 `0,1,2,3,4`。                                                                                                                                      |
| `K/F`                      | field extension, extension de corps, 域扩张                                | A field `K` containing a subfield `F`.                                                                                                | EN: `\mathbb{C}/\mathbb{R}` is a field extension.<br>FR: `\mathbb{C}/\mathbb{R}` est une extension de corps.<br>中：`\mathbb{C}/\mathbb{R}` 是一个域扩张。                                                                                                                          |
| `[K:F]`                    | degree of field extension, degré d’une extension de corps, 域扩张次数        | The dimension of `K` as a vector space over `F`.                                                                                      | EN: `[\mathbb{C}:\mathbb{R}]=2`.<br>FR: `[\mathbb{C}:\mathbb{R}]=2`.<br>中：`[\mathbb{C}:\mathbb{R}]=2`。                                                                                                                                                                     |
| `R[x]`                     | polynomial ring, anneau de polynômes, 多项式环                              | The ring of polynomials in variable `x` with coefficients in `R`.                                                                     | EN: `\mathbb{R}[x]` is the ring of real polynomials.<br>FR: `\mathbb{R}[x]` est l’anneau des polynômes réels.<br>中：`\mathbb{R}[x]` 是实系数多项式环。                                                                                                                               |
| `\deg f`                   | degree of a polynomial, degré d’un polynôme, 多项式次数                      | The highest exponent with nonzero coefficient in a nonzero polynomial.                                                                | EN: `\deg(3x^4+x+1)=4`.<br>FR: `\deg(3x^4+x+1)=4`.<br>中：`\deg(3x^4+x+1)=4`。                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| `f(a)=0`                   | root of a polynomial, racine d’un polynôme, 多项式根                        | A value making a polynomial equal to zero.                                                                                            | EN: `2` is a root of `x^2-4`.<br>FR: `2` est une racine de `x^2-4`.<br>中：`2` 是 `x^2-4` 的一个根。                                                                                                                                                                               |
| `(x-a)\mid f(x)`           | factor theorem, théorème du facteur, 因式定理                               | A polynomial `f(x)` has root `a` exactly when `x-a` divides `f(x)`.                                                                   | EN: Since `f(2)=0`, `x-2` divides `f(x)`.<br>FR: Puisque `f(2)=0`, `x-2` divise `f(x)`.<br>中：因为 `f(2)=0`，所以 `x-2` 整除 `f(x)`。                                                                                                                                               |
| —                          | irreducible polynomial, polynôme irréductible, 不可约多项式                   | A nonconstant polynomial that cannot be factored into lower-degree nonconstant polynomials over the chosen coefficient ring or field. | EN: `x^2+1` is irreducible over `\mathbb{R}`? No, over `\mathbb{R}` it factors only if real roots exist; over `\mathbb{C}` it factors.<br>FR: `x^2+1` est irréductible sur `\mathbb{R}`, mais pas sur `\mathbb{C}`.<br>中：`x^2+1` 在 `\mathbb{R}` 上不可约，但在 `\mathbb{C}` 上可分解。 |
| `F[x]/(f)`                 | quotient by a polynomial, quotient par un polynôme, 多项式商环               | The quotient ring obtained by identifying multiples of a polynomial `f` with zero.                                                    | EN: `\mathbb{R}[x]/(x^2+1)` behaves like `\mathbb{C}`.<br>FR: `\mathbb{R}[x]/(x^2+1)` se comporte comme `\mathbb{C}`.<br>中：`\mathbb{R}[x]/(x^2+1)` 的结构类似 `\mathbb{C}`。                                                                                                     |

## 14.9 Modules, algebras, and linear structure over rings

| Symbol (LaTeX)              | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                                | Definition                                                                                        | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
| --------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `M`                         | module, module, 模                                                                | A generalization of vector spaces where scalars come from a ring rather than necessarily a field. | EN: Every vector space is a module, but not every module is a vector space.<br>FR: Tout espace vectoriel est un module, mais tout module n’est pas un espace vectoriel.<br>中：每个向量空间都是模，但并非每个模都是向量空间。                                                  |
| `R\text{-Mod}`              | category of R-modules, catégorie des R-modules, R 模范畴                            | The collection of modules over a ring `R` together with module homomorphisms.                     | EN: `\mathbb{Z}`-modules are abelian groups.<br>FR: Les `\mathbb{Z}`-modules sont des groupes abéliens.<br>中：`\mathbb{Z}`-模就是阿贝尔群。                                                                                                                    |
| `rm`                        | scalar multiplication in a module, multiplication scalaire dans un module, 模中的数乘 | The action of a ring element `r` on a module element `m`.                                         | EN: In an `R`-module, scalars from `R` act on elements of `M`.<br>FR: Dans un `R`-module, les scalaires de `R` agissent sur les éléments de `M`.<br>中：在 `R`-模中，来自 `R` 的标量作用在 `M` 的元素上。                                                                |
| `N\leq M`                   | submodule, sous-module, 子模                                                       | A subset of a module that is itself a module under the inherited operations.                      | EN: Subspaces are submodules over a field.<br>FR: Les sous-espaces sont des sous-modules sur un corps.<br>中：域上的子空间就是子模。                                                                                                                               |
| `M/N`                       | quotient module, module quotient, 商模                                             | The module whose elements are cosets modulo a submodule `N`.                                      | EN: Quotient modules generalize quotient vector spaces.<br>FR: Les modules quotients généralisent les espaces vectoriels quotients.<br>中：商模推广了商向量空间。                                                                                                  |
| `\operatorname{Hom}_R(M,N)` | module homomorphisms, homomorphismes de modules, 模同态集合                           | The set of structure-preserving maps from `M` to `N`.                                             | EN: Module homomorphisms preserve addition and scalar multiplication.<br>FR: Les homomorphismes de modules préservent l’addition et la multiplication scalaire.<br>中：模同态保持加法和数乘。                                                                      |
| `\ker f`                    | kernel of a module homomorphism, noyau d’un homomorphisme de modules, 模同态的核      | The submodule of elements mapped to zero.                                                         | EN: The kernel of a linear map is a subspace.<br>FR: Le noyau d’une application linéaire est un sous-espace.<br>中：线性映射的核是子空间。                                                                                                                         |
| `\operatorname{im} f`       | image of a module homomorphism, image d’un homomorphisme de modules, 模同态的像       | The submodule consisting of all outputs of a homomorphism.                                        | EN: The image of a linear map is a subspace of the codomain.<br>FR: L’image d’une application linéaire est un sous-espace du codomaine.<br>中：线性映射的像是陪域中的子空间。                                                                                          |
| `M\oplus N`                 | direct sum, somme directe, 直和                                                    | A construction combining modules so each element decomposes uniquely into components.             | EN: `\mathbb{R}^2=\mathbb{R}\oplus\mathbb{R}`.<br>FR: `\mathbb{R}^2=\mathbb{R}\oplus\mathbb{R}`.<br>中：`\mathbb{R}^2=\mathbb{R}\oplus\mathbb{R}`。                                                                                                      |
