| `M\otimes_R N`              | tensor product, produit tensoriel, 张量积                                           | A construction encoding bilinear interactions between modules.                                    | EN: Tensor products turn bilinear maps into linear maps.<br>FR: Les produits tensoriels transforment les applications bilinéaires en applications linéaires.<br>中：张量积把双线性映射转化为线性映射。                                                                   |
| `A` over `R`                | algebra over a ring, algèbre sur un anneau, R 上的代数                               | A module over `R` with a compatible multiplication.                                               | EN: Matrix rings over a field are algebras over that field.<br>FR: Les anneaux de matrices sur un corps sont des algèbres sur ce corps.<br>中：域上的矩阵环是该域上的代数。                                                                                           |
| `R^n`                       | free module, module libre, 自由模                                                   | A module with a basis, analogous to a vector space basis.                                         | EN: `\mathbb{Z}^n` is a free `\mathbb{Z}`-module.<br>FR: `\mathbb{Z}^n` est un `\mathbb{Z}`-module libre.<br>中：`\mathbb{Z}^n` 是自由 `\mathbb{Z}`-模。                                                                                                     |
| —                           | torsion element, élément de torsion, 挠元素                                         | A module element killed by multiplication by a nonzero ring element.                              | EN: In `\mathbb{Z}/6\mathbb{Z}`, the element `1` is torsion as a `\mathbb{Z}`-module.<br>FR: Dans `\mathbb{Z}/6\mathbb{Z}`, l’élément `1` est de torsion comme `\mathbb{Z}`-module.<br>中：在 `\mathbb{Z}/6\mathbb{Z}` 中，元素 `1` 作为 `\mathbb{Z}`-模元素是挠元素。 |

## 14.10 Lattices, algebraic order, and universal algebra

| Symbol (LaTeX)                              | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                   | Definition                                                                          | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| ------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `(P,\leq)`                                  | partially ordered set, ensemble partiellement ordonné, 偏序集          | A set equipped with a reflexive, antisymmetric, and transitive relation.            | EN: Subsets of a set ordered by inclusion form a poset.<br>FR: Les sous-ensembles d’un ensemble ordonnés par inclusion forment un ensemble partiellement ordonné.<br>中：一个集合的所有子集按包含关系构成偏序集。                                                                                     |
| `a\vee b`                                   | join, borne supérieure / supremum, 并 / 上确界                          | The least upper bound of two elements in a lattice.                                 | EN: For sets ordered by inclusion, join is union.<br>FR: Pour les ensembles ordonnés par inclusion, le supremum est l’union.<br>中：在按包含排序的集合中，并运算对应上确界。                                                                                                                          |
| `a\wedge b`                                 | meet, borne inférieure / infimum, 交 / 下确界                           | The greatest lower bound of two elements in a lattice.                              | EN: For sets ordered by inclusion, meet is intersection.<br>FR: Pour les ensembles ordonnés par inclusion, l’infimum est l’intersection.<br>中：在按包含排序的集合中，交运算对应下确界。                                                                                                              |
| `L`                                         | lattice, treillis, 格                                                | A partially ordered set where every pair of elements has a join and a meet.         | EN: The power set `\mathcal{P}(S)` is a lattice under inclusion.<br>FR: L’ensemble des parties `\mathcal{P}(S)` est un treillis pour l’inclusion.<br>中：幂集 `\mathcal{P}(S)` 在包含关系下是格。                                                                                            |
| `a\vee(b\wedge c)=(a\vee b)\wedge(a\vee c)` | distributive lattice law, loi distributive des treillis, 分配格律       | A lattice law resembling distributivity of union and intersection.                  | EN: Boolean algebras are distributive lattices.<br>FR: Les algèbres booléennes sont des treillis distributifs.<br>中：布尔代数是分配格。                                                                                                                                                   |
| `0,1`                                       | bottom and top elements, plus petit et plus grand éléments, 最小元与最大元 | Distinguished least and greatest elements of a bounded lattice.                     | EN: In `\mathcal{P}(S)`, the bottom is `\varnothing` and the top is `S`.<br>FR: Dans `\mathcal{P}(S)`, le plus petit élément est `\varnothing` et le plus grand est `S`.<br>中：在 `\mathcal{P}(S)` 中，最小元是 `\varnothing`，最大元是 `S`。                                                 |
| `a^c`                                       | complement, complément, 补元                                          | An element that joins with `a` to the top and meets with `a` to the bottom.         | EN: In a power set, the complement of `A` is `S\setminus A`.<br>FR: Dans un ensemble de parties, le complément de `A` est `S\setminus A`.<br>中：在幂集中，`A` 的补集是 `S\setminus A`。                                                                                                    |
| `B`                                         | Boolean algebra, algèbre de Boole, 布尔代数                             | A complemented distributive lattice, often modeling logical operations.             | EN: Sets under union, intersection, and complement form a Boolean algebra.<br>FR: Les ensembles avec union, intersection et complément forment une algèbre de Boole.<br>中：集合在并、交、补运算下形成布尔代数。                                                                                    |
| —                                           | algebraic structure, structure algébrique, 代数结构                     | A set equipped with operations satisfying specified identities or axioms.           | EN: Groups, rings, fields, and lattices are algebraic structures.<br>FR: Les groupes, anneaux, corps et treillis sont des structures algébriques.<br>中：群、环、域、格都是代数结构。                                                                                                           |
| —                                           | signature, signature, 符号型 / 运算型                                     | The list of operation symbols and their arities in a class of algebraic structures. | EN: Group theory uses one binary operation, an identity, and an inverse operation in one common signature.<br>FR: La théorie des groupes utilise une opération binaire, un neutre et une opération d’inverse dans une signature courante.<br>中：群论的一种常见符号型包含一个二元运算、一个单位元和一个逆元运算。 |
| —                                           | identity law, identité algébrique, 恒等式律                             | An equation required to hold for all elements of an algebraic structure.            | EN: Associativity is an identity law.<br>FR: L’associativité est une identité algébrique.<br>中：结合律是一种代数恒等式律。                                                                                                                                                                    |
| —                                           | variety of algebras, variété d’algèbres, 代数簇                        | A class of algebraic structures defined by identities.                              | EN: Groups form a variety in universal algebra.<br>FR: Les groupes forment une variété en algèbre universelle.<br>中：群在泛代数中构成一个代数簇。                                                                                                                                              |

## 14.11 Category-theoretic language for algebra

| Symbol (LaTeX)                          | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                                  | Definition                                                                                                   | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| --------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `\mathcal{C}`                           | category, catégorie, 范畴                                                            | A structure consisting of objects and arrows between them, with associative composition and identity arrows. | EN: Groups and group homomorphisms form a category.<br>FR: Les groupes et homomorphismes de groupes forment une catégorie.<br>中：群与群同态构成一个范畴。                                                                              |
| `\operatorname{Ob}(\mathcal{C})`        | objects of a category, objets d’une catégorie, 范畴对象                                | The entities inside a category.                                                                              | EN: In the category of groups, objects are groups.<br>FR: Dans la catégorie des groupes, les objets sont les groupes.<br>中：在群范畴中，对象是群。                                                                                    |
| `\operatorname{Hom}_{\mathcal{C}}(A,B)` | morphisms, morphismes, 态射                                                          | The arrows from object `A` to object `B` in a category.                                                      | EN: In vector spaces, morphisms are linear maps.<br>FR: Dans les espaces vectoriels, les morphismes sont les applications linéaires.<br>中：在向量空间范畴中，态射是线性映射。                                                               |
| `g\circ f`                              | composition of morphisms, composition de morphismes, 态射复合                          | The operation of applying one morphism after another.                                                        | EN: If `f:A\to B` and `g:B\to C`, then `g\circ f:A\to C`.<br>FR: Si `f:A\to B` et `g:B\to C`, alors `g\circ f:A\to C`.<br>中：若 `f:A\to B` 且 `g:B\to C`，则 `g\circ f:A\to C`。                                                |
| `1_A`                                   | identity morphism, morphisme identité, 恒等态射                                        | The morphism from an object to itself that acts as an identity under composition.                            | EN: Every object has an identity morphism.<br>FR: Tout objet possède un morphisme identité.<br>中：每个对象都有恒等态射。                                                                                                              |
| `F:\mathcal{C}\to\mathcal{D}`           | functor, foncteur, 函子                                                              | A map between categories preserving objects, morphisms, identities, and composition.                         | EN: A forgetful functor sends groups to their underlying sets.<br>FR: Un foncteur d’oubli envoie les groupes vers leurs ensembles sous-jacents.<br>中：遗忘函子把群送到其底集合。                                                        |
| `F(f)`                                  | image of a morphism under a functor, image d’un morphisme par un foncteur, 函子下的态射像 | The morphism obtained after applying a functor to an arrow.                                                  | EN: A functor sends `f:A\to B` to `F(f):F(A)\to F(B)`.<br>FR: Un foncteur envoie `f:A\to B` sur `F(f):F(A)\to F(B)`.<br>中：函子把 `f:A\to B` 映到 `F(f):F(A)\to F(B)`。                                                          |
| `\eta:F\Rightarrow G`                   | natural transformation, transformation naturelle, 自然变换                             | A coherent family of morphisms between two functors.                                                         | EN: Natural transformations compare functors in a structure-preserving way.<br>FR: Les transformations naturelles comparent des foncteurs de manière compatible avec la structure.<br>中：自然变换以保持结构的方式比较两个函子。               |
| `A\times B`                             | product object, produit catégorique, 积对象                                           | An object representing pairs of maps into `A` and `B` through a universal property.                          | EN: In sets, the categorical product is the Cartesian product.<br>FR: Dans les ensembles, le produit catégorique est le produit cartésien.<br>中：在集合范畴中，范畴积就是笛卡尔积。                                                         |
| `A\coprod B`                            | coproduct object, coproduit, 余积对象                                                  | A dual construction representing maps out of `A` and `B` through a universal property.                       | EN: In sets, the coproduct is disjoint union.<br>FR: Dans les ensembles, le coproduit est l’union disjointe.<br>中：在集合范畴中，余积是不交并。                                                                                          |
| —                                       | universal property, propriété universelle, 泛性质                                     | A property characterizing an object uniquely up to unique isomorphism by its maps.                           | EN: Products, quotients, and tensor products can be described by universal properties.<br>FR: Les produits, quotients et produits tensoriels peuvent être décrits par des propriétés universelles.<br>中：积、商、张量积都可以用泛性质描述。 |
| `A\cong B`                              | isomorphism in a category, isomorphisme dans une catégorie, 范畴中的同构                 | A morphism with a two-sided inverse.                                                                         | EN: Isomorphic objects are structurally identical inside a category.<br>FR: Les objets isomorphes sont structurellement identiques dans une catégorie.<br>中：同构对象在该范畴内部结构相同。                                               |
# 15. Real Analysis, Metric Spaces, and Topology

## 15.1 Metric spaces and distance

| Symbol (LaTeX)                                              | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                      | Definition                                                                                                                                   | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
| ----------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------ | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `(X,d)`                                                     | metric space, espace métrique, 度量空间                    | A set `X` together with a distance function `d` satisfying nonnegativity, identity of indiscernibles, symmetry, and the triangle inequality. | EN: `(\mathbb{R},d)` with `d(x,y)=\lvert x-y\rvert` is a metric space.<br>FR: `(\mathbb{R},d)` avec `d(x,y)=\lvert x-y\rvert` est un espace métrique.<br>中：带有 `d(x,y)=\lvert x-y\rvert` 的 `(\mathbb{R},d)` 是度量空间。 |
| `d(x,y)`                                                    | distance function, fonction distance, 距离函数             | A function measuring the distance between two points of a metric space.                                                                      | EN: In the plane, the usual distance between `(0,0)` and `(3,4)` is `5`.<br>FR: Dans le plan, la distance usuelle entre `(0,0)` et `(3,4)` vaut `5`.<br>中：在平面中，`(0,0)` 与 `(3,4)` 的通常距离是 `5`。                      |
| `d(x,y)\geq 0`                                              | nonnegativity, positivité, 非负性                         | Distance is always nonnegative.                                                                                                              | EN: A distance cannot be negative.<br>FR: Une distance ne peut pas être négative.<br>中：距离不能为负。                                                                                                                    |
| `d(x,y)=0\Leftrightarrow x=y`                               | identity of indiscernibles, séparation des points, 同一性 | Two points have zero distance exactly when they are the same point.                                                                          | EN: If `d(x,y)=0`, then `x=y` in a metric space.<br>FR: Si `d(x,y)=0`, alors `x=y` dans un espace métrique.<br>中：在度量空间中，若 `d(x,y)=0`，则 `x=y`。                                                                     |
| `d(x,y)=d(y,x)`                                             | symmetry of distance, symétrie de la distance, 距离对称性   | Distance from `x` to `y` equals distance from `y` to `x`.                                                                                    | EN: The distance from Tokyo to Osaka equals the distance from Osaka to Tokyo.<br>FR: La distance de Tokyo à Osaka égale la distance d’Osaka à Tokyo.<br>中：东京到大阪的距离等于大阪到东京的距离。                                     |
| `d(x,z)\leq d(x,y)+d(y,z)`                                  | triangle inequality, inégalité triangulaire, 三角不等式     | Going directly from `x` to `z` is no longer than going through an intermediate point `y`.                                                    | EN: In Euclidean space, direct distance is at most broken-line distance.<br>FR: Dans l’espace euclidien, la distance directe est au plus la distance brisée.<br>中：在欧氏空间中，两点直线距离不超过折线路径距离。                         |
| `d_p(x,y)=\left(\sum_i \lvert x_i-y_i\rvert^p\right)^{1/p}` | p-metric, métrique p, p 度量                             | A family of metrics on finite-dimensional spaces for `p\geq 1`.                                                                              | EN: When `p=2`, `d_p` is the Euclidean distance.<br>FR: Quand `p=2`, `d_p` est la distance euclidienne.<br>中：当 `p=2` 时，`d_p` 是欧氏距离。                                                                               |
| `d_\infty(x,y)=\max_i\lvert x_i-y_i\rvert`                  | sup metric, métrique uniforme, 上确界度量                   | A metric measuring the largest coordinate difference.                                                                                        | EN: Between `(1,4)` and `(3,0)`, the sup distance is `4`.<br>FR: Entre `(1,4)` et `(3,0)`, la distance uniforme vaut `4`.<br>中：`(1,4)` 与 `(3,0)` 的上确界距离是 `4`。                                                     |
| `d(x,y)=1` for `x\neq y`                                    | discrete metric, métrique discrète, 离散度量               | A metric where distinct points all have distance `1`.                                                                                        | EN: Every subset is open under the discrete metric.<br>FR: Tout sous-ensemble est ouvert pour la métrique discrète.<br>中：在离散度量下，每个子集都是开集。                                                                         |
| `B(x,r)`                                                    | open ball, boule ouverte, 开球                           | The set of points whose distance from `x` is less than `r`.                                                                                  | EN: In `\mathbb{R}`, `B(0,1)=(-1,1)`.<br>FR: Dans `\mathbb{R}`, `B(0,1)=(-1,1)`.<br>中：在 `\mathbb{R}` 中，`B(0,1)=(-1,1)`。                                                                                           |
| `\overline{B}(x,r)`                                         | closed ball, boule fermée, 闭球                          | The set of points whose distance from `x` is at most `r`.                                                                                    | EN: In `\mathbb{R}`, `\overline{B}(0,1)=[-1,1]`.<br>FR: Dans `\mathbb{R}`, `\overline{B}(0,1)=[-1,1]`.<br>中：在 `\mathbb{R}` 中，`\overline{B}(0,1)=[-1,1]`。                                                          |
| `S(x,r)`                                                    | sphere, sphère, 球面                                     | The set of points whose distance from `x` equals `r`.                                                                                        | EN: In `\mathbb{R}^2`, `S(0,1)` is the unit circle.<br>FR: Dans `\mathbb{R}^2`, `S(0,1)` est le cercle unité.<br>中：在 `\mathbb{R}^2` 中，`S(0,1)` 是单位圆。                                                              |

## 15.2 Open sets, closed sets, and neighborhoods

| Symbol (LaTeX)             | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                     | Definition                                                                                         | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| -------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `U` open                   | open set, ensemble ouvert, 开集                                         | A set containing a small open ball around each of its points.                                      | EN: `(0,1)` is open in `\mathbb{R}`.<br>FR: `(0,1)` est ouvert dans `\mathbb{R}`.<br>中：`(0,1)` 是 `\mathbb{R}` 中的开集。                                                                                                             |
| `F` closed                 | closed set, ensemble fermé, 闭集                                        | A set whose complement is open, equivalently one containing all its limit points in metric spaces. | EN: `[0,1]` is closed in `\mathbb{R}`.<br>FR: `[0,1]` est fermé dans `\mathbb{R}`.<br>中：`[0,1]` 是 `\mathbb{R}` 中的闭集。                                                                                                            |
| `X\setminus A`             | complement, complémentaire, 补集                                        | The set of points in the ambient space that are not in `A`.                                        | EN: The complement of `(0,1)` in `\mathbb{R}` is `(-\infty,0]\cup[1,\infty)`.<br>FR: Le complément de `(0,1)` dans `\mathbb{R}` est `(-\infty,0]\cup[1,\infty)`.<br>中：`(0,1)` 在 `\mathbb{R}` 中的补集是 `(-\infty,0]\cup[1,\infty)`。 |
| `N(x)`                     | neighborhood, voisinage, 邻域                                           | A set containing an open set that contains `x`.                                                    | EN: `(-1,1)` is a neighborhood of `0`.<br>FR: `(-1,1)` est un voisinage de `0`.<br>中：`(-1,1)` 是 `0` 的一个邻域。                                                                                                                      |
| `B(x,\epsilon)`            | epsilon-neighborhood, voisinage epsilon, ε 邻域                         | An open ball of radius `\epsilon` around `x`.                                                      | EN: `B(2,0.1)` contains numbers within `0.1` of `2`.<br>FR: `B(2,0,1)` contient les nombres à distance inférieure à `0,1` de `2`.<br>中：`B(2,0.1)` 包含所有距离 `2` 小于 `0.1` 的数。                                                       |
| `x\in U`                   | interior point, point intérieur, 内点                                   | A point of a set that has some open ball contained entirely in the set.                            | EN: `1/2` is an interior point of `(0,1)`.<br>FR: `1/2` est un point intérieur de `(0,1)`.<br>中：`1/2` 是 `(0,1)` 的内点。                                                                                                            |
| `x\notin U^\circ`          | non-interior point, point non intérieur, 非内点                          | A point for which no sufficiently small open ball is fully contained in the set.                   | EN: `0` is not an interior point of `[0,1]` in `\mathbb{R}`.<br>FR: `0` n’est pas un point intérieur de `[0,1]` dans `\mathbb{R}`.<br>中：在 `\mathbb{R}` 中，`0` 不是 `[0,1]` 的内点。                                                    |
| `\bigcup_{\alpha}U_\alpha` | union of open sets, réunion d’ouverts, 开集并                            | Any union of open sets is open.                                                                    | EN: A union of intervals like `(0,1)\cup(2,3)` is open.<br>FR: Une réunion d’intervalles comme `(0,1)\cup(2,3)` est ouverte.<br>中：像 `(0,1)\cup(2,3)` 这样的开区间并是开集。                                                                |
| `U_1\cap\cdots\cap U_n`    | finite intersection of open sets, intersection finie d’ouverts, 有限开集交 | A finite intersection of open sets is open.                                                        | EN: `(0,2)\cap(1,3)=(1,2)` is open.<br>FR: `(0,2)\cap(1,3)=(1,2)` est ouvert.<br>中：`(0,2)\cap(1,3)=(1,2)` 是开集。                                                                                                                  |
| `\bigcap_{\alpha}F_\alpha` | intersection of closed sets, intersection de fermés, 闭集交              | Any intersection of closed sets is closed.                                                         | EN: The intersection of closed intervals `[0,1/n]` is `{0}`.<br>FR: L’intersection des intervalles fermés `[0,1/n]` est `{0}`.<br>中：闭区间 `[0,1/n]` 的交集是 `{0}`。                                                                   |
| `F_1\cup\cdots\cup F_n`    | finite union of closed sets, réunion finie de fermés, 有限闭集并           | A finite union of closed sets is closed.                                                           | EN: `[0,1]\cup[2,3]` is closed.<br>FR: `[0,1]\cup[2,3]` est fermé.<br>中：`[0,1]\cup[2,3]` 是闭集。                                                                                                                                   |
| `\varnothing,X`            | clopen basic examples, exemples ouverts-fermés, 既开又闭的基本例子             | The empty set and the whole space are both open and closed in every topological space.             | EN: In every space, `\varnothing` and `X` are clopen.<br>FR: Dans tout espace, `\varnothing` et `X` sont à la fois ouverts et fermés.<br>中：在任意空间中，`\varnothing` 和 `X` 都既开又闭。                                                    |

## 15.3 Interior, closure, boundary, and dense sets

| Symbol (LaTeX)             | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                   | Definition                                                                          | Examples                                                                                                                                                                  |
| -------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `A^\circ`                  | interior, intérieur, 内部                             | The set of all interior points of `A`.                                              | EN: The interior of `[0,1]` in `\mathbb{R}` is `(0,1)`.<br>FR: L’intérieur de `[0,1]` dans `\mathbb{R}` est `(0,1)`.<br>中：`[0,1]` 在 `\mathbb{R}` 中的内部是 `(0,1)`。           |
| `\overline{A}`             | closure, adhérence, 闭包                              | The smallest closed set containing `A`, equivalently `A` plus its limit points.     | EN: The closure of `(0,1)` is `[0,1]`.<br>FR: L’adhérence de `(0,1)` est `[0,1]`.<br>中：`(0,1)` 的闭包是 `[0,1]`。                                                              |
| `\partial A`               | boundary, frontière, 边界                             | The set of points where every neighborhood meets both `A` and its complement.       | EN: The boundary of `(0,1)` in `\mathbb{R}` is `\{0,1\}`.<br>FR: La frontière de `(0,1)` dans `\mathbb{R}` est `\{0,1\}`.<br>中：`(0,1)` 在 `\mathbb{R}` 中的边界是 `\{0,1\}`。    |
| `A'`                       | derived set, ensemble dérivé, 导集                    | The set of all limit points of `A`.                                                 | EN: The derived set of `\{1/n:n\in\mathbb{N}\}` is `\{0\}`.<br>FR: L’ensemble dérivé de `\{1/n:n\in\mathbb{N}\}` est `\{0\}`.<br>中：`\{1/n:n\in\mathbb{N}\}` 的导集是 `\{0\}`。 |
| —                          | limit point, point d’accumulation, 聚点               | A point whose every neighborhood contains a point of the set different from itself. | EN: `0` is a limit point of `\{1/n:n\in\mathbb{N}\}`.<br>FR: `0` est un point d’accumulation de `\{1/n:n\in\mathbb{N}\}`.<br>中：`0` 是 `\{1/n:n\in\mathbb{N}\}` 的聚点。        |
| —                          | isolated point, point isolé, 孤立点                    | A point of a set that has a neighborhood containing no other points of the set.     | EN: Each `1/n` is isolated in `\{1/n:n\in\mathbb{N}\}`.<br>FR: Chaque `1/n` est isolé dans `\{1/n:n\in\mathbb{N}\}`.<br>中：每个 `1/n` 都是 `\{1/n:n\in\mathbb{N}\}` 中的孤立点。     |
| `\overline{A}=X`           | dense set, ensemble dense, 稠密集                      | A subset whose closure is the whole space.                                          | EN: `\mathbb{Q}` is dense in `\mathbb{R}`.<br>FR: `\mathbb{Q}` est dense dans `\mathbb{R}`.<br>中：`\mathbb{Q}` 在 `\mathbb{R}` 中稠密。                                         |
| —                          | nowhere dense set, ensemble nulle part dense, 无处稠密集 | A set whose closure has empty interior.                                             | EN: A single point is nowhere dense in `\mathbb{R}`.<br>FR: Un singleton est nulle part dense dans `\mathbb{R}`.<br>中：单点集在 `\mathbb{R}` 中无处稠密。                            |
| `A^\circ=\varnothing`      | empty interior, intérieur vide, 空内部                 | A set has empty interior if it contains no nonempty open set.                       | EN: `\mathbb{Q}` has empty interior in `\mathbb{R}`.<br>FR: `\mathbb{Q}` a un intérieur vide dans `\mathbb{R}`.<br>中：`\mathbb{Q}` 在 `\mathbb{R}` 中内部为空。                   |
| `A\setminus A^\circ`       | edge part of a set, partie frontalière, 集合边缘部分      | Points of a set that are not interior points.                                       | EN: In `[0,1]`, the points `0` and `1` are not interior points.<br>FR: Dans `[0,1]`, les points `0` et `1` ne sont pas intérieurs.<br>中：在 `[0,1]` 中，`0` 与 `1` 不是内点。       |
| `\operatorname{dist}(x,A)` | distance to a set, distance à un ensemble, 到集合的距离   | The infimum of distances from `x` to points of `A`.                                 | EN: The distance from `2` to `[0,1]` is `1`.<br>FR: La distance de `2` à `[0,1]` vaut `1`.<br>中：`2` 到 `[0,1]` 的距离是 `1`。                                                   |

## 15.4 Sequences, convergence, and Cauchy behavior

| Symbol (LaTeX)        | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                                 | Definition                                                                              | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| --------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `x_n\to x`            | convergence in a metric space, convergence dans un espace métrique, 度量空间中的收敛      | A sequence converges to `x` if its distance from `x` tends to zero.                     | EN: In `\mathbb{R}`, `1/n\to 0`.<br>FR: Dans `\mathbb{R}`, `1/n\to 0`.<br>中：在 `\mathbb{R}` 中，`1/n\to 0`。                                                                                                                        |
| `d(x_n,x)\to 0`       | metric convergence criterion, critère métrique de convergence, 度量收敛判据             | A sequence converges exactly when the distance to the limit tends to zero.              | EN: `x_n\to x` means `d(x_n,x)\to0`.<br>FR: `x_n\to x` signifie `d(x_n,x)\to0`.<br>中：`x_n\to x` 表示 `d(x_n,x)\to0`。                                                                                                              |
| `\epsilon`-definition | epsilon definition of convergence, définition epsilon de la convergence, 收敛的 ε 定义 | For every `\epsilon>0`, all sufficiently late terms lie within `\epsilon` of the limit. | EN: Eventually, `x_n` stays inside every ball around `x`.<br>FR: À partir d’un certain rang, `x_n` reste dans toute boule autour de `x`.<br>中：从某一项以后，`x_n` 留在 `x` 的任意小邻域内。                                                      |
| `(x_{n_k})`           | subsequence, sous-suite, 子列                                                       | A sequence formed by selecting terms of another sequence in increasing index order.     | EN: `1,1/3,1/5,\ldots` is a subsequence of `1/n`.<br>FR: `1,1/3,1/5,\ldots` est une sous-suite de `1/n`.<br>中：`1,1/3,1/5,\ldots` 是 `1/n` 的一个子列。                                                                                 |
| `x_{n_k}\to x`        | subsequential limit, valeur d’adhérence, 子列极限                                     | A limit of some subsequence of a given sequence.                                        | EN: The sequence `(-1)^n` has subsequential limits `1` and `-1`.<br>FR: La suite `(-1)^n` a pour valeurs d’adhérence `1` et `-1`.<br>中：数列 `(-1)^n` 的子列极限有 `1` 和 `-1`。                                                           |
| —                     | cluster point of a sequence, point d’accumulation d’une suite, 数列聚点               | A point approached by some subsequence.                                                 | EN: Both `1` and `-1` are cluster points of `(-1)^n`.<br>FR: `1` et `-1` sont des points d’accumulation de `(-1)^n`.<br>中：`1` 和 `-1` 都是 `(-1)^n` 的聚点。                                                                           |
| —                     | Cauchy sequence, suite de Cauchy, Cauchy 列 / 柯西列                                  | A sequence whose terms eventually become arbitrarily close to each other.               | EN: Every convergent sequence in a metric space is Cauchy.<br>FR: Toute suite convergente dans un espace métrique est de Cauchy.<br>中：度量空间中的每个收敛列都是 Cauchy 列。                                                                   |
| `d(x_m,x_n)<\epsilon` | Cauchy criterion, critère de Cauchy, Cauchy 判据                                    | For every `\epsilon>0`, sufficiently late terms are mutually within `\epsilon`.         | EN: A Cauchy sequence stabilizes internally, even before knowing its limit.<br>FR: Une suite de Cauchy se stabilise intérieurement, même sans connaître sa limite.<br>中：Cauchy 列的后期项彼此接近，即使尚不知道极限。                              |
| —                     | complete metric space, espace métrique complet, 完备度量空间                            | A metric space in which every Cauchy sequence converges to a point in the space.        | EN: `\mathbb{R}` is complete, but `\mathbb{Q}` is not complete under the usual metric.<br>FR: `\mathbb{R}` est complet, mais `\mathbb{Q}` ne l’est pas pour la métrique usuelle.<br>中：通常度量下，`\mathbb{R}` 完备，而 `\mathbb{Q}` 不完备。 |
| —                     | sequentially closed set, ensemble séquentiellement fermé, 序列闭集                    | A set that contains the limits of all convergent sequences from the set.                | EN: In metric spaces, closed sets are sequentially closed.<br>FR: Dans les espaces métriques, les fermés sont séquentiellement fermés.<br>中：在度量空间中，闭集是序列闭的。                                                                     |
| —                     | sequential compactness, compacité séquentielle, 序列紧性                              | Every sequence has a convergent subsequence whose limit lies in the space.              | EN: Closed bounded intervals in `\mathbb{R}` are sequentially compact.<br>FR: Les intervalles fermés bornés de `\mathbb{R}` sont séquentiellement compacts.<br>中：`\mathbb{R}` 中的有界闭区间是序列紧的。                                     |

## 15.5 Compactness, connectedness, and covering properties

| Symbol (LaTeX)               | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                            | Definition                                                                          | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                     |
| ---------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------ | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `\{U_\alpha\}_{\alpha\in A}` | open cover, recouvrement ouvert, 开覆盖                         | A family of open sets whose union contains a given space or set.                    | EN: The intervals `(-n,n)` cover `\mathbb{R}`.<br>FR: Les intervalles `(-n,n)` recouvrent `\mathbb{R}`.<br>中：区间 `(-n,n)` 覆盖 `\mathbb{R}`。                                                                    |
| —                            | subcover, sous-recouvrement, 子覆盖                             | A subfamily of a cover that still covers the set.                                   | EN: A finite subcover uses only finitely many sets from the original cover.<br>FR: Un sous-recouvrement fini utilise seulement un nombre fini d’ensembles du recouvrement initial.<br>中：有限子覆盖只使用原覆盖中的有限多个集合。 |
| —                            | compact set, ensemble compact, 紧集                            | A set for which every open cover has a finite subcover.                             | EN: `[0,1]` is compact in `\mathbb{R}`.<br>FR: `[0,1]` est compact dans `\mathbb{R}`.<br>中：`[0,1]` 是 `\mathbb{R}` 中的紧集。                                                                                      |
| —                            | finite subcover, sous-recouvrement fini, 有限子覆盖               | A finite collection of open sets still covering the original set.                   | EN: Compactness guarantees finite subcovers for all open covers.<br>FR: La compacité garantit des sous-recouvrements finis pour tous les recouvrements ouverts.<br>中：紧性保证每个开覆盖都有有限子覆盖。                       |
| —                            | Heine-Borel theorem, théorème de Heine-Borel, Heine-Borel 定理 | In Euclidean space, a set is compact exactly when it is closed and bounded.         | EN: In `\mathbb{R}^n`, closed and bounded sets are compact.<br>FR: Dans `\mathbb{R}^n`, les ensembles fermés et bornés sont compacts.<br>中：在 `\mathbb{R}^n` 中，闭且有界的集合是紧的。                                    |
| —                            | bounded set, ensemble borné, 有界集                             | A set contained inside some ball of finite radius.                                  | EN: `(0,1)` is bounded in `\mathbb{R}`.<br>FR: `(0,1)` est borné dans `\mathbb{R}`.<br>中：`(0,1)` 在 `\mathbb{R}` 中有界。                                                                                         |
| —                            | totally bounded set, ensemble totalement borné, 全有界集         | A set that can be covered by finitely many balls of any prescribed positive radius. | EN: `[0,1]` is totally bounded.<br>FR: `[0,1]` est totalement borné.<br>中：`[0,1]` 是全有界的。                                                                                                                     |
| —                            | connected set, ensemble connexe, 连通集                         | A set that cannot be separated into two disjoint nonempty open pieces.              | EN: An interval in `\mathbb{R}` is connected.<br>FR: Un intervalle de `\mathbb{R}` est connexe.<br>中：`\mathbb{R}` 中的区间是连通集。                                                                                  |
| —                            | disconnected set, ensemble non connexe, 非连通集                 | A set that can be split into two separated nonempty pieces.                         | EN: `(0,1)\cup(2,3)` is disconnected.<br>FR: `(0,1)\cup(2,3)` est non connexe.<br>中：`(0,1)\cup(2,3)` 是非连通集。                                                                                                  |
| —                            | path connected set, ensemble connexe par arcs, 路径连通集         | A set where any two points can be joined by a continuous path lying in the set.     | EN: A disk in the plane is path connected.<br>FR: Un disque dans le plan est connexe par arcs.<br>中：平面中的圆盘是路径连通的。                                                                                            |
| `\gamma:[0,1]\to X`          | path, chemin, 路径                                             | A continuous map from an interval into a space.                                     | EN: A path from `a` to `b` satisfies `\gamma(0)=a` and `\gamma(1)=b`.<br>FR: Un chemin de `a` à `b` vérifie `\gamma(0)=a` et `\gamma(1)=b`.<br>中：从 `a` 到 `b` 的路径满足 `\gamma(0)=a` 且 `\gamma(1)=b`。            |
| —                            | connected component, composante connexe, 连通分支                | A maximal connected subset of a space.                                              | EN: The connected components of `(0,1)\cup(2,3)` are `(0,1)` and `(2,3)`.<br>FR: Les composantes connexes de `(0,1)\cup(2,3)` sont `(0,1)` et `(2,3)`.<br>中：`(0,1)\cup(2,3)` 的连通分支是 `(0,1)` 和 `(2,3)`。       |

## 15.6 Continuity, uniform continuity, and homeomorphisms

| Symbol (LaTeX)                                 | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                       | Definition                                                                                                                | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| ---------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `f:X\to Y`                                     | map between spaces, application entre espaces, 空间之间的映射                  | A function from one space to another.                                                                                     | EN: `f(x)=x^2` maps `\mathbb{R}` to `\mathbb{R}`.<br>FR: `f(x)=x^2` envoie `\mathbb{R}` dans `\mathbb{R}`.<br>中：`f(x)=x^2` 是从 `\mathbb{R}` 到 `\mathbb{R}` 的映射。                                  |
| `f` continuous                                 | continuous map, application continue, 连续映射                              | A map preserving limits or, equivalently in topology, whose preimage of every open set is open.                           | EN: Polynomial functions are continuous on `\mathbb{R}`.<br>FR: Les fonctions polynomiales sont continues sur `\mathbb{R}`.<br>中：多项式函数在 `\mathbb{R}` 上连续。                                       |
| `f^{-1}(U)`                                    | preimage, image réciproque, 原像                                          | The set of domain points mapped into a set `U` in the codomain.                                                           | EN: For continuous `f`, the preimage of an open set is open.<br>FR: Pour une fonction continue `f`, l’image réciproque d’un ouvert est ouverte.<br>中：对连续映射 `f`，开集的原像是开集。                        |
| `f(A)`                                         | image of a set, image d’un ensemble, 像集                                 | The set of all values of `f(x)` with `x\in A`.                                                                            | EN: If `f(x)=x^2`, then `f([-1,1])=[0,1]`.<br>FR: Si `f(x)=x^2`, alors `f([-1,1])=[0,1]`.<br>中：若 `f(x)=x^2`，则 `f([-1,1])=[0,1]`。                                                                |
| `\epsilon`-`\delta` continuity                 | epsilon-delta continuity, continuité epsilon-delta, ε-δ 连续性             | A pointwise condition saying small input changes produce small output changes.                                            | EN: `f(x)=x^2` is continuous by the epsilon-delta definition.<br>FR: `f(x)=x^2` est continue par la définition epsilon-delta.<br>中：`f(x)=x^2` 满足 ε-δ 连续性定义。                                     |
| —                                              | sequential continuity, continuité séquentielle, 序列连续性                   | A function is sequentially continuous if it sends convergent sequences to convergent sequences with corresponding limits. | EN: In metric spaces, continuity and sequential continuity are equivalent.<br>FR: Dans les espaces métriques, continuité et continuité séquentielle sont équivalentes.<br>中：在度量空间中，连续性与序列连续性等价。 |
| —                                              | uniform continuity, continuité uniforme, 一致连续                           | A stronger form of continuity where one input tolerance works uniformly across the domain.                                | EN: `f(x)=x^2` is uniformly continuous on `[0,1]`.<br>FR: `f(x)=x^2` est uniformément continue sur `[0,1]`.<br>中：`f(x)=x^2` 在 `[0,1]` 上一致连续。                                                    |
| `\lvert f(x)-f(y)\rvert\leq L\lvert x-y\rvert` | Lipschitz continuity, continuité lipschitzienne, Lipschitz 连续           | A condition bounding output distance by a constant multiple of input distance.                                            | EN: `f(x)=3x` is Lipschitz with constant `3`.<br>FR: `f(x)=3x` est lipschitzienne de constante `3`.<br>中：`f(x)=3x` 是 Lipschitz 连续的，常数为 `3`。                                                     |
| —                                              | contraction, contraction, 压缩映射                                          | A Lipschitz map with constant strictly less than `1`.                                                                     | EN: `f(x)=x/2` is a contraction on `\mathbb{R}`.<br>FR: `f(x)=x/2` est une contraction sur `\mathbb{R}`.<br>中：`f(x)=x/2` 是 `\mathbb{R}` 上的压缩映射。                                                 |
| `f:X\cong Y`                                   | homeomorphism, homéomorphisme, 同胚                                       | A bijective continuous map whose inverse is also continuous.                                                              | EN: An open interval is homeomorphic to the real line.<br>FR: Un intervalle ouvert est homéomorphe à la droite réelle.<br>中：开区间与实数直线同胚。                                                         |
| —                                              | topological invariant, invariant topologique, 拓扑不变量                     | A property preserved under homeomorphism.                                                                                 | EN: Compactness is a topological invariant.<br>FR: La compacité est un invariant topologique.<br>中：紧性是拓扑不变量。                                                                                    |
| —                                              | continuous image of compact set, image continue d’un compact, 紧集的连续像    | The image of a compact set under a continuous map is compact.                                                             | EN: A continuous function on `[0,1]` has compact image.<br>FR: Une fonction continue sur `[0,1]` a une image compacte.<br>中：`[0,1]` 上连续函数的像是紧集。                                                 |
| —                                              | continuous image of connected set, image continue d’un connexe, 连通集的连续像 | The image of a connected set under a continuous map is connected.                                                         | EN: The continuous image of an interval is an interval.<br>FR: L’image continue d’un intervalle est un intervalle.<br>中：区间的连续像仍是区间。                                                             |

## 15.7 Complete spaces, fixed points, and functional limits

| Symbol (LaTeX)                       | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                                   | Definition                                                                                      | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
| ------------------------------------ | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| —                                    | Banach fixed-point theorem, théorème du point fixe de Banach, Banach 不动点定理          | A contraction on a complete metric space has a unique fixed point.                              | EN: Iterating a contraction converges to its fixed point.<br>FR: L’itération d’une contraction converge vers son point fixe.<br>中：反复迭代压缩映射会收敛到它的不动点。                                                                                                                                  |
| `f(x)=x`                             | fixed point, point fixe, 不动点                                                        | A point left unchanged by a function.                                                           | EN: The map `f(x)=x/2` has fixed point `0`.<br>FR: L’application `f(x)=x/2` a pour point fixe `0`.<br>中：映射 `f(x)=x/2` 的不动点是 `0`。                                                                                                                                                      |
| `f_n\to f` pointwise                 | pointwise convergence, convergence simple, 逐点收敛                                     | A sequence of functions converges point by point to a limiting function.                        | EN: `f_n(x)=x^n` converges pointwise on `[0,1]` to a function that is `0` on `[0,1)` and `1` at `1`.<br>FR: `f_n(x)=x^n` converge simplement sur `[0,1]` vers une fonction valant `0` sur `[0,1)` et `1` en `1`.<br>中：`f_n(x)=x^n` 在 `[0,1]` 上逐点收敛到在 `[0,1)` 上为 `0`、在 `1` 处为 `1` 的函数。 |
| `\sup_x\lvert f_n(x)-f(x)\rvert\to0` | uniform convergence, convergence uniforme, 一致收敛                                     | A sequence of functions converges uniformly if the maximum error over the domain tends to zero. | EN: Uniform convergence controls error over the whole domain at once.<br>FR: La convergence uniforme contrôle l’erreur sur tout le domaine à la fois.<br>中：一致收敛同时控制整个定义域上的误差。                                                                                                         |
| `\lVert f\rVert_\infty`              | sup norm, norme uniforme, 上确界范数                                                     | The maximum or supremum of the absolute value of a function.                                    | EN: On `[0,1]`, `\lVert x\rVert_\infty=1`.<br>FR: Sur `[0,1]`, `\lVert x\rVert_\infty=1`.<br>中：在 `[0,1]` 上，`\lVert x\rVert_\infty=1`。                                                                                                                                                 |
| `C(X)`                               | continuous function space, espace des fonctions continues, 连续函数空间                   | The set of continuous real- or complex-valued functions on a space `X`.                         | EN: `C([0,1])` contains all continuous functions on `[0,1]`.<br>FR: `C([0,1])` contient toutes les fonctions continues sur `[0,1]`.<br>中：`C([0,1])` 包含 `[0,1]` 上所有连续函数。                                                                                                               |
| `C([a,b])` complete                  | completeness of continuous functions, complétude des fonctions continues, 连续函数空间完备性 | With the sup norm, the space of continuous functions on a compact interval is complete.         | EN: A uniformly Cauchy sequence of continuous functions has a continuous limit.<br>FR: Une suite uniformément de Cauchy de fonctions continues a une limite continue.<br>中：连续函数的一致 Cauchy 列有连续极限。                                                                                     |
| —                                    | uniform limit theorem, théorème de limite uniforme, 一致极限定理                          | A uniform limit of continuous functions is continuous.                                          | EN: Uniform convergence preserves continuity.<br>FR: La convergence uniforme préserve la continuité.<br>中：一致收敛保持连续性。                                                                                                                                                                  |
| —                                    | interchange of limit and integral, échange limite-intégrale, 极限与积分交换                | Under suitable conditions, the limit of integrals equals the integral of the limit.             | EN: Uniform convergence allows `\lim\int f_n=\int\lim f_n` on finite intervals.<br>FR: La convergence uniforme permet `\lim\int f_n=\int\lim f_n` sur des intervalles finis.<br>中：在有限区间上，一致收敛允许 `\lim\int f_n=\int\lim f_n`。                                                          |
| —                                    | interchange of limit and derivative, échange limite-dérivée, 极限与导数交换                | Passing a limit through derivatives requires stronger hypotheses than continuity alone.         | EN: Derivatives of `f_n` may fail to converge to the derivative of the limit.<br>FR: Les dérivées de `f_n` peuvent ne pas converger vers la dérivée de la limite.<br>中：`f_n` 的导数未必收敛到极限函数的导数。                                                                                         |

## 15.8 Normed spaces, Banach spaces, and Hilbert spaces

| Symbol (LaTeX)                                      | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                             | Definition                                                                                            | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| --------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `(V,\lVert\cdot\rVert)`                             | normed vector space, espace vectoriel normé, 赋范向量空间                           | A vector space equipped with a norm measuring vector size.                                            | EN: `\mathbb{R}^n` with Euclidean norm is a normed vector space.<br>FR: `\mathbb{R}^n` avec la norme euclidienne est un espace vectoriel normé.<br>中：带欧氏范数的 `\mathbb{R}^n` 是赋范向量空间。                      |
| `\lVert x\rVert`                                    | norm, norme, 范数                                                               | A function measuring vector size and satisfying positivity, homogeneity, and the triangle inequality. | EN: In `\mathbb{R}^2`, `\lVert(3,4)\rVert=5` under the Euclidean norm.<br>FR: Dans `\mathbb{R}^2`, `\lVert(3,4)\rVert=5` pour la norme euclidienne.<br>中：在 `\mathbb{R}^2` 中，欧氏范数下 `\lVert(3,4)\rVert=5`。 |
| `\lVert cx\rVert=\lvert c\rvert\lVert x\rVert`      | homogeneity of norm, homogénéité de la norme, 范数齐次性                           | Scaling a vector by `c` scales its norm by `\lvert c\rvert`.                                          | EN: Doubling a vector doubles its norm.<br>FR: Doubler un vecteur double sa norme.<br>中：向量翻倍时，其范数也翻倍。                                                                                                    |
| `\lVert x+y\rVert\leq\lVert x\rVert+\lVert y\rVert` | norm triangle inequality, inégalité triangulaire de la norme, 范数三角不等式         | The norm of a sum is no larger than the sum of norms.                                                 | EN: The triangle inequality controls vector addition.<br>FR: L’inégalité triangulaire contrôle l’addition vectorielle.<br>中：三角不等式控制向量加法后的大小。                                                             |
| `d(x,y)=\lVert x-y\rVert`                           | metric induced by a norm, métrique induite par une norme, 范数诱导度量              | A norm defines a distance by applying the norm to differences.                                        | EN: Every normed vector space is a metric space.<br>FR: Tout espace vectoriel normé est un espace métrique.<br>中：每个赋范向量空间都是度量空间。                                                                         |
| —                                                   | Banach space, espace de Banach, Banach 空间                                     | A complete normed vector space.                                                                       | EN: `C([0,1])` with the sup norm is a Banach space.<br>FR: `C([0,1])` avec la norme uniforme est un espace de Banach.<br>中：带上确界范数的 `C([0,1])` 是 Banach 空间。                                               |
| `\langle x,y\rangle`                                | inner product, produit intérieur, 内积                                          | A generalized dot product that induces a norm.                                                        | EN: In `\mathbb{R}^n`, the standard inner product is the dot product.<br>FR: Dans `\mathbb{R}^n`, le produit intérieur standard est le produit scalaire.<br>中：在 `\mathbb{R}^n` 中，标准内积就是点积。               |
| `\lVert x\rVert=\sqrt{\langle x,x\rangle}`          | norm induced by inner product, norme induite par un produit intérieur, 内积诱导范数 | A norm obtained from an inner product.                                                                | EN: The Euclidean norm comes from the standard inner product.<br>FR: La norme euclidienne provient du produit intérieur standard.<br>中：欧氏范数来自标准内积。                                                       |
| —                                                   | Hilbert space, espace de Hilbert, Hilbert 空间                                  | A complete inner product space.                                                                       | EN: `\ell^2` is a Hilbert space.<br>FR: `\ell^2` est un espace de Hilbert.<br>中：`\ell^2` 是 Hilbert 空间。                                                                                                   |
| `\ell^p`                                            | sequence space, espace de suites, 序列空间                                        | A space of sequences whose `p`-powers are summable, with standard variants for `1\leq p\leq\infty`.   | EN: `\ell^2` contains square-summable sequences.<br>FR: `\ell^2` contient les suites de carré sommable.<br>中：`\ell^2` 包含平方可和序列。                                                                          |
| `L^p`                                               | function space, espace Lp, Lp 函数空间                                            | A space of functions whose absolute value to the `p`th power is integrable.                           | EN: `L^2` is central in Fourier analysis and quantum mechanics.<br>FR: `L^2` est central en analyse de Fourier et en mécanique quantique.<br>中：`L^2` 在傅里叶分析和量子力学中很重要。                                    |
| `T:X\to Y`                                          | bounded linear operator, opérateur linéaire borné, 有界线性算子                     | A linear map between normed spaces that sends bounded sets to bounded sets.                           | EN: In normed spaces, bounded linear maps are continuous.<br>FR: Dans les espaces normés, les applications linéaires bornées sont continues.<br>中：在赋范空间中，有界线性映射是连续的。                                     |

## 15.9 Topological spaces and bases

| Symbol (LaTeX)                   | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                             | Definition                                                                                                | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
| -------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `(X,\tau)`                       | topological space, espace topologique, 拓扑空间                   | A set `X` together with a collection `\tau` of open sets satisfying union and finite-intersection axioms. | EN: Every metric space induces a topological space.<br>FR: Tout espace métrique induit un espace topologique.<br>中：每个度量空间都会诱导一个拓扑空间。                                                                                                                 |
| `\tau`                           | topology, topologie, 拓扑                                       | The collection of open sets of a topological space.                                                       | EN: A topology specifies which sets count as open.<br>FR: Une topologie précise quels ensembles sont ouverts.<br>中：拓扑指定哪些集合被视为开集。                                                                                                                    |
| `\varnothing,X\in\tau`           | topology axiom, axiome topologique, 拓扑公理                      | The empty set and the whole set must be open.                                                             | EN: Every topology contains `\varnothing` and `X`.<br>FR: Toute topologie contient `\varnothing` et `X`.<br>中：每个拓扑都包含 `\varnothing` 和 `X`。                                                                                                           |
| `\bigcup_\alpha U_\alpha\in\tau` | arbitrary union axiom, axiome de réunion arbitraire, 任意并公理    | Any union of open sets is open.                                                                           | EN: Infinite unions of open sets remain open.<br>FR: Les réunions infinies d’ouverts restent ouvertes.<br>中：开集的任意并仍是开集。                                                                                                                              |
| `U_1\cap\cdots\cap U_n\in\tau`   | finite intersection axiom, axiome d’intersection finie, 有限交公理 | Any finite intersection of open sets is open.                                                             | EN: Finite intersections of open sets remain open.<br>FR: Les intersections finies d’ouverts restent ouvertes.<br>中：开集的有限交仍是开集。                                                                                                                      |
| `\mathcal{B}`                    | basis for a topology, base d’une topologie, 拓扑基               | A collection of open sets from which all open sets can be formed by unions.                               | EN: Open intervals form a basis for the usual topology on `\mathbb{R}`.<br>FR: Les intervalles ouverts forment une base de la topologie usuelle sur `\mathbb{R}`.<br>中：开区间构成 `\mathbb{R}` 上通常拓扑的一组基。                                                 |
| —                                | subbasis, sous-base, 子基                                       | A collection whose finite intersections form a basis for a topology.                                      | EN: Subbases provide compact descriptions of topologies.<br>FR: Les sous-bases donnent des descriptions compactes des topologies.<br>中：子基可以更简洁地描述拓扑。                                                                                                 |
| `\tau_{\text{disc}}`             | discrete topology, topologie discrète, 离散拓扑                   | The topology in which every subset is open.                                                               | EN: In a discrete topology, every function out of the space is continuous.<br>FR: Dans une topologie discrète, toute fonction depuis l’espace est continue.<br>中：在离散拓扑中，从该空间出发的任意函数都是连续的。                                                            |
| `\tau_{\text{indisc}}`           | indiscrete topology, topologie grossière, 平凡拓扑 / 不分明拓扑        | The topology consisting only of `\varnothing` and `X`.                                                    | EN: The indiscrete topology has the fewest open sets.<br>FR: La topologie grossière possède le moins d’ouverts.<br>中：平凡拓扑含有最少的开集。                                                                                                                    |
| `A\subseteq X`                   | subspace topology, topologie induite, 子空间拓扑                   | The topology on a subset formed by intersecting open sets of the ambient space with the subset.           | EN: `[0,1]` inherits a topology from `\mathbb{R}`.<br>FR: `[0,1]` hérite d’une topologie de `\mathbb{R}`.<br>中：`[0,1]` 从 `\mathbb{R}` 继承子空间拓扑。                                                                                                       |
| `X/\sim`                         | quotient topology, topologie quotient, 商拓扑                    | The topology on a quotient set making the quotient map continuous in the strongest natural way.           | EN: Identifying the endpoints of `[0,1]` produces a circle.<br>FR: Identifier les extrémités de `[0,1]` produit un cercle.<br>中：把 `[0,1]` 的两个端点粘合会得到圆。                                                                                               |
| `X\times Y`                      | product topology, topologie produit, 积拓扑                      | The natural topology on a product space built from open sets of the factors.                              | EN: The usual topology on `\mathbb{R}^2` is the product topology of `\mathbb{R}` with itself.<br>FR: La topologie usuelle de `\mathbb{R}^2` est la topologie produit de `\mathbb{R}` avec lui-même.<br>中：`\mathbb{R}^2` 的通常拓扑是 `\mathbb{R}` 与自身的积拓扑。 |

## 15.10 Separation, countability, and structural properties

| Symbol (LaTeX) | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                      | Definition                                                                                         | Examples                                                                                                                                                                            |
| -------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `T_0`          | Kolmogorov space, espace de Kolmogorov, T0 空间                          | A space where any two distinct points are topologically distinguishable.                           | EN: `T_0` is the weakest common separation axiom.<br>FR: `T_0` est l’axiome de séparation courant le plus faible.<br>中：`T_0` 是常见分离公理中最弱的一种。                                         |
| `T_1`          | T1 space, espace T1, T1 空间                                             | A space where singletons are closed.                                                               | EN: Every metric space is `T_1`.<br>FR: Tout espace métrique est `T_1`.<br>中：每个度量空间都是 `T_1` 空间。                                                                                     |
| `T_2`          | Hausdorff space, espace de Hausdorff, Hausdorff 空间                     | A space where distinct points can be separated by disjoint neighborhoods.                          | EN: Metric spaces are Hausdorff.<br>FR: Les espaces métriques sont de Hausdorff.<br>中：度量空间都是 Hausdorff 空间。                                                                          |
| —              | regular space, espace régulier, 正则空间                                   | A space where points and closed sets can be separated by neighborhoods under suitable assumptions. | EN: Many familiar spaces in analysis are regular.<br>FR: Beaucoup d’espaces familiers en analyse sont réguliers.<br>中：分析中常见的许多空间都是正则的。                                              |
| —              | normal space, espace normal, 正规空间                                      | A space where disjoint closed sets can be separated by disjoint open neighborhoods.                | EN: Metric spaces are normal.<br>FR: Les espaces métriques sont normaux.<br>中：度量空间是正规空间。                                                                                            |
| —              | first countable space, espace à base dénombrable de voisinages, 第一可数空间 | A space where each point has a countable neighborhood basis.                                       | EN: Metric spaces are first countable.<br>FR: Les espaces métriques sont à base dénombrable de voisinages.<br>中：度量空间都是第一可数空间。                                                       |
| —              | second countable space, espace à base dénombrable, 第二可数空间              | A space with a countable basis for its topology.                                                   | EN: `\mathbb{R}^n` is second countable.<br>FR: `\mathbb{R}^n` est à base dénombrable.<br>中：`\mathbb{R}^n` 是第二可数空间。                                                                  |
| —              | separable space, espace séparable, 可分空间                                | A space containing a countable dense subset.                                                       | EN: `\mathbb{Q}` is a countable dense subset of `\mathbb{R}`.<br>FR: `\mathbb{Q}` est un sous-ensemble dénombrable dense de `\mathbb{R}`.<br>中：`\mathbb{Q}` 是 `\mathbb{R}` 的可数稠密子集。 |
| —              | metrizable space, espace métrisable, 可度量化空间                            | A topological space whose topology comes from some metric.                                         | EN: Not every topological space is metrizable.<br>FR: Tout espace topologique n’est pas métrisable.<br>中：不是每个拓扑空间都可度量化。                                                             |
| —              | locally compact space, espace localement compact, 局部紧空间                | A space where every point has a compact neighborhood, under common conventions.                    | EN: `\mathbb{R}^n` is locally compact.<br>FR: `\mathbb{R}^n` est localement compact.<br>中：`\mathbb{R}^n` 是局部紧空间。                                                                    |
| —              | Lindelöf space, espace de Lindelöf, Lindelöf 空间                        | A space where every open cover has a countable subcover.                                           | EN: Every second countable space is Lindelöf.<br>FR: Tout espace à base dénombrable est de Lindelöf.<br>中：每个第二可数空间都是 Lindelöf 空间。                                                   |

## 15.11 Manifolds, surfaces, and local coordinates

| Symbol (LaTeX)             | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                      | Definition                                                                            | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| -------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `M`                        | manifold, variété, 流形                                  | A space that locally looks like Euclidean space.                                      | EN: A circle is a one-dimensional manifold.<br>FR: Un cercle est une variété de dimension un.<br>中：圆是一维流形。                                                                                               |
| `\dim M`                   | dimension of a manifold, dimension d’une variété, 流形维数 | The Euclidean dimension of local coordinate charts.                                   | EN: A sphere surface has dimension `2`.<br>FR: Une surface sphérique a dimension `2`.<br>中：球面是二维流形。                                                                                                      |
| `(U,\varphi)`              | chart, carte locale, 坐标图                               | A local coordinate description mapping an open part of a manifold to Euclidean space. | EN: Latitude and longitude give local coordinates on much of a sphere.<br>FR: Latitude et longitude donnent des coordonnées locales sur une grande partie d’une sphère.<br>中：经纬度在球面大部分区域上给出局部坐标。         |
| `\varphi:U\to\mathbb{R}^n` | coordinate map, application de coordonnées, 坐标映射       | A map assigning Euclidean coordinates to points in a chart domain.                    | EN: A coordinate map turns local geometry into coordinates.<br>FR: Une application de coordonnées transforme la géométrie locale en coordonnées.<br>中：坐标映射把局部几何转化为坐标。                                    |
| —                          | atlas, atlas, 图册                                       | A collection of charts covering a manifold.                                           | EN: A sphere cannot be covered by one global coordinate chart without singularities.<br>FR: Une sphère ne peut pas être couverte par une seule carte globale sans singularités.<br>中：球面不能由一个无奇点的全局坐标图覆盖。 |
| —                          | transition map, changement de cartes, 坐标变换             | A map relating coordinates on overlapping charts.                                     | EN: Smooth transition maps define a smooth manifold.<br>FR: Des changements de cartes lisses définissent une variété lisse.<br>中：光滑坐标变换定义光滑流形。                                                           |
| `T_pM`                     | tangent space, espace tangent, 切空间                     | The vector space of tangent directions at a point `p` on a manifold.                  | EN: The tangent space to a surface at a point is a plane-like vector space.<br>FR: L’espace tangent à une surface en un point ressemble à un plan vectoriel.<br>中：曲面上一点的切空间类似一个平面向量空间。                   |
| `TM`                       | tangent bundle, fibré tangent, 切丛                      | The collection of all tangent spaces of a manifold.                                   | EN: A vector field is often viewed as a section of the tangent bundle.<br>FR: Un champ de vecteurs est souvent vu comme une section du fibré tangent.<br>中：向量场常被看作切丛的截面。                                 |
| —                          | smooth manifold, variété lisse, 光滑流形                   | A manifold whose coordinate changes are smooth.                                       | EN: Curves and surfaces used in calculus are often smooth manifolds.<br>FR: Les courbes et surfaces du calcul différentiel sont souvent des variétés lisses.<br>中：微积分中的曲线和曲面通常是光滑流形。                     |
| `S^n`                      | n-sphere, sphère n-dimensionnelle, n 维球面               | The set of points at unit distance from the origin in `\mathbb{R}^{n+1}`.             | EN: `S^1` is the circle and `S^2` is the usual sphere surface.<br>FR: `S^1` est le cercle et `S^2` est la sphère usuelle.<br>中：`S^1` 是圆，`S^2` 是通常的球面。                                                    |
| `\mathbb{T}^2`             | torus, tore, 环面 / 甜甜圈面                                 | A surface shaped like a donut, often represented as a product of two circles.         | EN: The torus can be written as `S^1\times S^1`.<br>FR: Le tore peut s’écrire `S^1\times S^1`.<br>中：环面可写为 `S^1\times S^1`。                                                                               |
| —                          | boundary of a manifold, bord d’une variété, 流形边界       | Points locally modeled on a half-space rather than all of Euclidean space.            | EN: A closed disk is a manifold with boundary.<br>FR: Un disque fermé est une variété à bord.<br>中：闭圆盘是带边界流形。                                                                                            |

## 15.12 Measure-theoretic language for analysis

| Symbol (LaTeX)                                 | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                      | Definition                                                                                                   | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                    |
