| `y(x_0)=y_0`               | initial condition, condition initiale, 初始条件                      | A value of the unknown function specified at an initial point.                       | EN: `y(0)=1` fixes one solution from a family.<br>FR: `y(0)=1` choisit une solution dans une famille.<br>中：`y(0)=1` 从一族解中确定一个解。                                                                         |
| `y'(x_0)=v_0`              | initial velocity condition, condition initiale de vitesse, 初速度条件 | A specified initial value of the first derivative.                                   | EN: For motion, `x'(0)=v_0` gives initial velocity.<br>FR: Pour un mouvement, `x'(0)=v_0` donne la vitesse initiale.<br>中：对运动问题，`x'(0)=v_0` 给出初速度。                                                      |
| `y'=f(x,y),\ y(x_0)=y_0`   | initial value problem, problème de Cauchy, 初值问题                  | A differential equation together with data at an initial point.                      | EN: `y'=y`, `y(0)=2` has solution `y=2e^x`.<br>FR: `y'=y`, `y(0)=2` a pour solution `y=2e^x`.<br>中：`y'=y`，`y(0)=2` 的解是 `y=2e^x`。                                                                        |
| `y(a)=\alpha,\ y(b)=\beta` | boundary conditions, conditions aux limites, 边界条件                | Conditions specified at boundary points of an interval or region.                    | EN: `y(0)=0` and `y(1)=1` are boundary conditions.<br>FR: `y(0)=0` et `y(1)=1` sont des conditions aux limites.<br>中：`y(0)=0` 与 `y(1)=1` 是边界条件。                                                         |
| —                          | boundary value problem, problème aux limites, 边值问题               | A differential equation together with conditions imposed at the boundary.            | EN: A vibrating string problem often has boundary conditions at both endpoints.<br>FR: Un problème de corde vibrante a souvent des conditions aux limites aux deux extrémités.<br>中：振动弦问题通常在两个端点都有边界条件。 |
| —                          | Dirichlet condition, condition de Dirichlet, Dirichlet 条件        | A boundary condition specifying the value of the unknown function.                   | EN: `u=0` on the boundary is a Dirichlet condition.<br>FR: `u=0` sur le bord est une condition de Dirichlet.<br>中：边界上 `u=0` 是 Dirichlet 条件。                                                             |
| —                          | Neumann condition, condition de Neumann, Neumann 条件              | A boundary condition specifying the normal derivative of the unknown function.       | EN: `\frac{\partial u}{\partial n}=0` can represent insulation.<br>FR: `\frac{\partial u}{\partial n}=0` peut représenter une isolation.<br>中：`\frac{\partial u}{\partial n}=0` 可表示绝热边界。                |
| —                          | Robin condition, condition de Robin, Robin 条件                    | A boundary condition combining function value and derivative value.                  | EN: `au+b\frac{\partial u}{\partial n}=0` is a Robin condition.<br>FR: `au+b\frac{\partial u}{\partial n}=0` est une condition de Robin.<br>中：`au+b\frac{\partial u}{\partial n}=0` 是 Robin 条件。         |
| —                          | existence, existence, 存在性                                        | The property that at least one solution satisfies the given equation and conditions. | EN: An existence theorem states that a solution exists under specified assumptions.<br>FR: Un théorème d’existence affirme qu’une solution existe sous certaines hypothèses.<br>中：存在性定理说明在特定假设下解存在。     |
| —                          | uniqueness, unicité, 唯一性                                         | The property that at most one solution satisfies the given equation and conditions.  | EN: `y'=y`, `y(0)=1` has a unique solution.<br>FR: `y'=y`, `y(0)=1` a une solution unique.<br>中：`y'=y`，`y(0)=1` 有唯一解。                                                                                   |
| —                          | continuous dependence, dépendance continue, 连续依赖性                | Small changes in data produce small changes in the solution.                         | EN: Well-posed models require solutions to depend continuously on initial data.<br>FR: Les modèles bien posés exigent une dépendance continue aux données initiales.<br>中：适定模型要求解连续依赖初始数据。              |
| —                          | well-posed problem, problème bien posé, 适定问题                     | A problem with existence, uniqueness, and continuous dependence on data.             | EN: A well-posed initial value problem has a stable mathematical meaning.<br>FR: Un problème de Cauchy bien posé possède un sens mathématique stable.<br>中：适定初值问题具有稳定的数学意义。                             |
| —                          | ill-posed problem, problème mal posé, 不适定问题                      | A problem lacking existence, uniqueness, or continuous dependence.                   | EN: Some inverse problems are ill-posed without regularization.<br>FR: Certains problèmes inverses sont mal posés sans régularisation.<br>中：某些反问题若无正则化就是不适定的。                                           |

## 17.3 First-order differential equations

| Symbol (LaTeX)                | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                                                         | Definition                                                                           | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                             |
| ----------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `\frac{dy}{dx}=g(x)h(y)`      | separable equation, équation séparable, 可分离变量方程                                                           | A first-order equation whose variables can be separated onto different sides.        | EN: `y'=xy` is separable.<br>FR: `y'=xy` est séparable.<br>中：`y'=xy` 是可分离变量方程。                                                                                                                       |
| `\frac{1}{h(y)}\,dy=g(x)\,dx` | separation of variables, séparation des variables, 分离变量法                                                  | A solution method moving all `y` terms to one side and all `x` terms to the other.   | EN: From `y'=xy`, write `\frac{1}{y}\,dy=x\,dx`.<br>FR: À partir de `y'=xy`, écrire `\frac{1}{y}\,dy=x\,dx`.<br>中：由 `y'=xy` 可写成 `\frac{1}{y}\,dy=x\,dx`。                                             |
| `y'+p(x)y=q(x)`               | first-order linear equation, équation linéaire du premier ordre, 一阶线性方程                                   | A first-order differential equation linear in the unknown function.                  | EN: `y'+2y=e^x` is first-order linear.<br>FR: `y'+2y=e^x` est linéaire du premier ordre.<br>中：`y'+2y=e^x` 是一阶线性方程。                                                                                   |
| `\mu(x)=e^{\int p(x)\,dx}`    | integrating factor, facteur intégrant, 积分因子                                                               | A function used to turn a first-order linear equation into an exact derivative.      | EN: For `y'+2y=e^x`, an integrating factor is `e^{2x}`.<br>FR: Pour `y'+2y=e^x`, un facteur intégrant est `e^{2x}`.<br>中：对 `y'+2y=e^x`，积分因子是 `e^{2x}`。                                               |
| `M(x,y)\,dx+N(x,y)\,dy=0`     | differential form of first-order equation, forme différentielle d’une équation du premier ordre, 一阶方程微分形式 | A first-order equation written as a differential form.                               | EN: `2x\,dx+2y\,dy=0` is a differential form equation.<br>FR: `2x\,dx+2y\,dy=0` est une équation sous forme différentielle.<br>中：`2x\,dx+2y\,dy=0` 是微分形式方程。                                          |
| `M_y=N_x`                     | exactness condition, condition d’exactitude, 恰当条件                                                         | A condition under which `M\,dx+N\,dy` is the differential of a potential function.   | EN: If `M_y=N_x`, the equation may be exact on suitable domains.<br>FR: Si `M_y=N_x`, l’équation peut être exacte sur des domaines appropriés.<br>中：若 `M_y=N_x`，在适当区域上方程可能是恰当的。                      |
| `dF=M\,dx+N\,dy`              | exact differential equation, équation différentielle exacte, 恰当微分方程                                       | A differential equation expressible as `dF=0`.                                       | EN: `2x\,dx+2y\,dy=0` gives `d(x^2+y^2)=0`.<br>FR: `2x\,dx+2y\,dy=0` donne `d(x^2+y^2)=0`.<br>中：`2x\,dx+2y\,dy=0` 对应 `d(x^2+y^2)=0`。                                                                 |
| `y'=F(y/x)`                   | homogeneous first-order equation, équation homogène du premier ordre, 一阶齐次方程                              | A first-order equation depending on the ratio `y/x`.                                 | EN: Use substitution `v=y/x` for equations of this form.<br>FR: Utiliser la substitution `v=y/x` pour ce type d’équation.<br>中：这类方程常用换元 `v=y/x`。                                                     |
| `y'+p(x)y=q(x)y^n`            | Bernoulli equation, équation de Bernoulli, Bernoulli 方程                                                   | A nonlinear first-order equation reducible to a linear equation by substitution.     | EN: The substitution `v=y^{1-n}` linearizes a Bernoulli equation.<br>FR: La substitution `v=y^{1-n}` linéarise une équation de Bernoulli.<br>中：换元 `v=y^{1-n}` 可把 Bernoulli 方程线性化。                    |
| `y'=f(ax+by+c)`               | reducible first-order equation, équation réductible du premier ordre, 可化简一阶方程                             | A first-order equation simplified by a change of variables.                          | EN: Setting `u=ax+by+c` may reduce the equation.<br>FR: Poser `u=ax+by+c` peut réduire l’équation.<br>中：令 `u=ax+by+c` 可能化简方程。                                                                        |
| —                             | direction field, champ de directions, 方向场                                                                 | A visual representation of slopes determined by a first-order differential equation. | EN: A direction field sketches solution behavior without solving explicitly.<br>FR: Un champ de directions esquisse le comportement des solutions sans résolution explicite.<br>中：方向场可在不显式求解时描绘解的走势。 |

## 17.4 Linear differential equations of higher order

| Symbol (LaTeX)                               | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                                 | Definition                                                                                           | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| -------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `a_n(x)y^{(n)}+\cdots+a_1(x)y'+a_0(x)y=g(x)` | linear differential equation, équation différentielle linéaire, 线性微分方程            | A differential equation linear in the unknown function and its derivatives.                          | EN: `y''+3y'+2y=\sin x` is linear.<br>FR: `y''+3y'+2y=\sin x` est linéaire.<br>中：`y''+3y'+2y=\sin x` 是线性方程。                                                                                                                                |
| `g(x)=0`                                     | homogeneous linear equation, équation linéaire homogène, 齐次线性方程                   | A linear differential equation with zero forcing term.                                               | EN: `y''+y=0` is homogeneous.<br>FR: `y''+y=0` est homogène.<br>中：`y''+y=0` 是齐次线性方程。                                                                                                                                                       |
| `g(x)\neq0`                                  | nonhomogeneous linear equation, équation linéaire non homogène, 非齐次线性方程           | A linear differential equation with a nonzero forcing term.                                          | EN: `y''+y=\cos x` is nonhomogeneous.<br>FR: `y''+y=\cos x` est non homogène.<br>中：`y''+y=\cos x` 是非齐次线性方程。                                                                                                                                |
| `y=c_1y_1+\cdots+c_ny_n`                     | superposition principle, principe de superposition, 叠加原理                          | Linear combinations of homogeneous solutions are again homogeneous solutions.                        | EN: If `y_1` and `y_2` solve a homogeneous linear equation, so does `c_1y_1+c_2y_2`.<br>FR: Si `y_1` et `y_2` résolvent une équation linéaire homogène, alors `c_1y_1+c_2y_2` aussi.<br>中：若 `y_1` 和 `y_2` 是齐次线性方程的解，则 `c_1y_1+c_2y_2` 也是解。 |
| `y_h`                                        | complementary solution, solution complémentaire, 齐次通解                             | The general solution of the associated homogeneous equation.                                         | EN: For `y''+y=\cos x`, `y_h` solves `y''+y=0`.<br>FR: Pour `y''+y=\cos x`, `y_h` résout `y''+y=0`.<br>中：对 `y''+y=\cos x`，`y_h` 解齐次方程 `y''+y=0`。                                                                                           |
| `y_p`                                        | particular solution, solution particulière, 特解                                    | One solution of a nonhomogeneous linear equation.                                                    | EN: The full solution is `y=y_h+y_p`.<br>FR: La solution complète est `y=y_h+y_p`.<br>中：完整解为 `y=y_h+y_p`。                                                                                                                                  |
| `ar^2+br+c=0`                                | characteristic equation, équation caractéristique, 特征方程                           | An algebraic equation used to solve constant-coefficient linear equations.                           | EN: For `y''-3y'+2y=0`, the characteristic equation is `r^2-3r+2=0`.<br>FR: Pour `y''-3y'+2y=0`, l’équation caractéristique est `r^2-3r+2=0`.<br>中：对 `y''-3y'+2y=0`，特征方程是 `r^2-3r+2=0`。                                                    |
| `e^{rx}`                                     | exponential trial solution, solution d’essai exponentielle, 指数试探解                 | A trial solution used for constant-coefficient linear differential equations.                        | EN: Substitute `y=e^{rx}` into `y''+ay'+by=0`.<br>FR: Substituer `y=e^{rx}` dans `y''+ay'+by=0`.<br>中：将 `y=e^{rx}` 代入 `y''+ay'+by=0`。                                                                                                      |
| `W(y_1,\ldots,y_n)`                          | Wronskian, wronskien, Wronski 行列式                                                 | A determinant used to test linear independence of solutions.                                         | EN: A nonzero Wronskian implies local linear independence.<br>FR: Un wronskien non nul implique l’indépendance linéaire locale.<br>中：Wronski 行列式非零推出局部线性无关。                                                                                |
| —                                            | method of undetermined coefficients, méthode des coefficients indéterminés, 待定系数法 | A method for finding particular solutions for selected forcing terms.                                | EN: It works well for polynomial, exponential, sine, and cosine forcing.<br>FR: Elle fonctionne bien pour des termes polynomiaux, exponentiels, sinus et cosinus.<br>中：待定系数法适合多项式、指数、正弦、余弦型非齐次项。                                           |
| —                                            | variation of parameters, variation des constantes, 常数变易法                          | A method for finding particular solutions by allowing constants in the homogeneous solution to vary. | EN: Variation of parameters applies more broadly than undetermined coefficients.<br>FR: La variation des constantes s’applique plus largement que les coefficients indéterminés.<br>中：常数变易法比待定系数法适用范围更广。                                   |

## 17.5 Systems of differential equations

| Symbol (LaTeX)                         | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                            | Definition                                                                        | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| -------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `\mathbf{x}'=\mathbf{f}(t,\mathbf{x})` | system of differential equations, système d’équations différentielles, 微分方程组 | A collection of differential equations for several unknown functions.             | EN: Predator-prey models are systems of differential equations.<br>FR: Les modèles proie-prédateur sont des systèmes d’équations différentielles.<br>中：捕食者-猎物模型是微分方程组。                                                                                   |
| `\mathbf{x}'=A\mathbf{x}`              | linear autonomous system, système linéaire autonome, 线性自治系统                  | A system with constant matrix `A` and no explicit time dependence.                | EN: `\mathbf{x}'=A\mathbf{x}` is solved using eigenvalues and eigenvectors.<br>FR: `\mathbf{x}'=A\mathbf{x}` se résout avec valeurs propres et vecteurs propres.<br>中：`\mathbf{x}'=A\mathbf{x}` 可用特征值和特征向量求解。                                            |
| `\mathbf{x}'=A(t)\mathbf{x}`           | time-varying linear system, système linéaire non autonome, 时变线性系统            | A linear system whose coefficient matrix depends on time.                         | EN: `\mathbf{x}'=A(t)\mathbf{x}` has coefficients changing with `t`.<br>FR: `\mathbf{x}'=A(t)\mathbf{x}` a des coefficients dépendant de `t`.<br>中：`\mathbf{x}'=A(t)\mathbf{x}` 的系数随 `t` 改变。                                                             |
| `e^{At}`                               | matrix exponential, exponentielle matricielle, 矩阵指数                          | The matrix function used to solve linear systems with constant coefficients.      | EN: The solution of `\mathbf{x}'=A\mathbf{x}` is `\mathbf{x}(t)=e^{At}\mathbf{x}(0)`.<br>FR: La solution de `\mathbf{x}'=A\mathbf{x}` est `\mathbf{x}(t)=e^{At}\mathbf{x}(0)`.<br>中：`\mathbf{x}'=A\mathbf{x}` 的解是 `\mathbf{x}(t)=e^{At}\mathbf{x}(0)`。   |
| `\lambda`                              | eigenvalue in a system, valeur propre dans un système, 系统中的特征值               | A scalar determining growth, decay, or oscillation directions of a linear system. | EN: Negative real eigenvalues indicate decay along eigen-directions.<br>FR: Des valeurs propres réelles négatives indiquent une décroissance le long des directions propres.<br>中：负实特征值表示沿特征方向衰减。                                                        |
| `\mathbf{v}`                           | eigenvector direction, direction propre, 特征向量方向                              | A direction preserved by the linear flow of a system.                             | EN: Solutions starting on an eigenvector direction stay on that line.<br>FR: Les solutions partant dans une direction propre restent sur cette droite.<br>中：从特征向量方向出发的解会留在该直线上。                                                                          |
| `\mathbf{x}^*`                         | equilibrium point, point d’équilibre, 平衡点                                    | A state where the derivative is zero and the system does not move.                | EN: If `\mathbf{f}(\mathbf{x}^*)=\mathbf{0}`, then `\mathbf{x}^*` is an equilibrium.<br>FR: Si `\mathbf{f}(\mathbf{x}^*)=\mathbf{0}`, alors `\mathbf{x}^*` est un point d’équilibre.<br>中：若 `\mathbf{f}(\mathbf{x}^*)=\mathbf{0}`，则 `\mathbf{x}^*` 是平衡点。 |
| —                                      | phase plane, plan de phase, 相平面                                              | A plane showing trajectories of a two-dimensional autonomous system.              | EN: The phase plane shows solution paths without plotting time directly.<br>FR: Le plan de phase montre les trajectoires sans représenter directement le temps.<br>中：相平面显示解轨线，而不直接绘制时间。                                                                  |
| —                                      | trajectory, trajectoire, 轨线                                                  | The path traced by a solution in state space.                                     | EN: A trajectory shows how the state evolves.<br>FR: Une trajectoire montre l’évolution de l’état.<br>中：轨线表示状态如何演化。                                                                                                                                      |
| —                                      | nullcline, nullcline, 零增长线                                                   | A curve where one component of the vector field is zero.                          | EN: In a two-variable system, nullclines help locate equilibria.<br>FR: Dans un système à deux variables, les nullclines aident à trouver les équilibres.<br>中：在二元系统中，零增长线有助于寻找平衡点。                                                                      |
| `J(\mathbf{x}^*)`                      | Jacobian at equilibrium, jacobienne à l’équilibre, 平衡点处雅可比矩阵                 | The derivative matrix used to linearize a nonlinear system near equilibrium.      | EN: Eigenvalues of `J(\mathbf{x}^*)` describe local behavior.<br>FR: Les valeurs propres de `J(\mathbf{x}^*)` décrivent le comportement local.<br>中：`J(\mathbf{x}^*)` 的特征值描述局部行为。                                                                        |

## 17.6 Stability and qualitative analysis

| Symbol (LaTeX)  | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                                 | Definition                                                                                              | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                       |
| --------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| —               | stable equilibrium, équilibre stable, 稳定平衡                                        | An equilibrium where nearby solutions remain nearby.                                                    | EN: A pendulum hanging downward has a stable equilibrium.<br>FR: Un pendule suspendu vers le bas a un équilibre stable.<br>中：向下悬挂的摆具有稳定平衡。                                                     |
| —               | unstable equilibrium, équilibre instable, 不稳定平衡                                   | An equilibrium where nearby solutions can move away.                                                    | EN: A pendulum balanced upright has an unstable equilibrium.<br>FR: Un pendule équilibré vers le haut a un équilibre instable.<br>中：倒立摆的竖直上方位置是不稳定平衡。                                          |
| —               | asymptotic stability, stabilité asymptotique, 渐近稳定                                | Stability together with convergence of nearby solutions to the equilibrium.                             | EN: Damped motion often approaches a stable equilibrium.<br>FR: Un mouvement amorti s’approche souvent d’un équilibre stable.<br>中：阻尼运动通常趋近稳定平衡。                                               |
| —               | Lyapunov stability, stabilité de Lyapunov, Lyapunov 稳定性                           | A rigorous notion of stability defined by small initial disturbances producing small future deviations. | EN: Lyapunov stability does not necessarily require convergence.<br>FR: La stabilité de Lyapunov n’exige pas nécessairement la convergence.<br>中：Lyapunov 稳定性不一定要求解收敛到平衡点。                     |
| `V(\mathbf{x})` | Lyapunov function, fonction de Lyapunov, Lyapunov 函数                              | A scalar function used to prove stability by decreasing along trajectories.                             | EN: Energy-like functions often serve as Lyapunov functions.<br>FR: Les fonctions de type énergie servent souvent de fonctions de Lyapunov.<br>中：类似能量的函数常可作为 Lyapunov 函数。                      |
| `\dot{V}\leq0`  | Lyapunov decrease condition, condition de décroissance de Lyapunov, Lyapunov 下降条件 | A condition showing that `V` does not increase along system trajectories.                               | EN: If `\dot{V}<0` away from equilibrium, asymptotic stability may follow.<br>FR: Si `\dot{V}<0` hors de l’équilibre, la stabilité asymptotique peut suivre.<br>中：若在平衡点外 `\dot{V}<0`，可推出渐近稳定性。 |
| —               | phase portrait, portrait de phase, 相图                                             | A qualitative diagram of trajectories and equilibria of a dynamical system.                             | EN: A phase portrait shows sinks, sources, saddles, and cycles.<br>FR: Un portrait de phase montre puits, sources, selles et cycles.<br>中：相图显示汇点、源点、鞍点和周期轨道。                                   |
| —               | sink, puits, 汇点                                                                   | An equilibrium attracting nearby trajectories.                                                          | EN: A sink corresponds to local asymptotic stability.<br>FR: Un puits correspond à une stabilité asymptotique locale.<br>中：汇点对应局部渐近稳定。                                                         |
| —               | source, source, 源点                                                                | An equilibrium repelling nearby trajectories.                                                           | EN: A source is unstable.<br>FR: Une source est instable.<br>中：源点是不稳定的。                                                                                                                        |
| —               | saddle point, point selle, 鞍点                                                     | An equilibrium with both attracting and repelling directions.                                           | EN: A saddle is unstable even though some trajectories approach it.<br>FR: Un point selle est instable même si certaines trajectoires s’en approchent.<br>中：鞍点是不稳定的，即使某些轨线会趋近它。                |
| —               | limit cycle, cycle limite, 极限环                                                    | An isolated closed trajectory that nearby trajectories may approach or move away from.                  | EN: Stable limit cycles model sustained oscillations.<br>FR: Les cycles limites stables modélisent des oscillations entretenues.<br>中：稳定极限环可建模持续振荡。                                            |
| —               | bifurcation, bifurcation, 分岔                                                      | A qualitative change in system behavior as a parameter varies.                                          | EN: A small parameter change may create or destroy equilibria.<br>FR: Un petit changement de paramètre peut créer ou détruire des équilibres.<br>中：参数小变化可能产生或消灭平衡点。                            |

## 17.7 Laplace transforms and transform methods

| Symbol (LaTeX)                           | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                                         | Definition                                                                                         | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |
| ---------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `\mathcal{L}\{f(t)\}`                    | Laplace transform, transformée de Laplace, Laplace 变换                                     | An integral transform converting a time-domain function into a function of a complex variable `s`. | EN: Laplace transforms turn many differential equations into algebraic equations.<br>FR: Les transformées de Laplace transforment beaucoup d’équations différentielles en équations algébriques.<br>中：Laplace 变换可把许多微分方程转化为代数方程。 |
| `F(s)=\int_0^\infty e^{-st}f(t)\,dt`     | Laplace transform definition, définition de la transformée de Laplace, Laplace 变换定义       | The defining integral for the Laplace transform when it converges.                                 | EN: The transform of `1` is `1/s` for suitable `s`.<br>FR: La transformée de `1` est `1/s` pour `s` convenable.<br>中：在适当条件下，`1` 的变换是 `1/s`。                                                                                      |
| `s`                                      | transform variable, variable de transformation, 变换变量                                      | The complex or real variable in the transformed domain.                                            | EN: The variable `s` often represents growth and oscillation information.<br>FR: La variable `s` représente souvent croissance et oscillation.<br>中：变量 `s` 常表示增长与振荡信息。                                                           |
| `\mathcal{L}^{-1}\{F(s)\}`               | inverse Laplace transform, transformée de Laplace inverse, Laplace 逆变换                    | The operation recovering a time-domain function from its Laplace transform.                        | EN: If `F(s)=1/(s-a)`, then `\mathcal{L}^{-1}\{F\}=e^{at}`.<br>FR: Si `F(s)=1/(s-a)`, alors `\mathcal{L}^{-1}\{F\}=e^{at}`.<br>中：若 `F(s)=1/(s-a)`，则 `\mathcal{L}^{-1}\{F\}=e^{at}`。                                              |
| `\mathcal{L}\{f'\}=sF(s)-f(0)`           | derivative transform rule, règle de transformation de la dérivée, 导数变换公式                  | A rule converting derivatives into algebraic expressions involving initial values.                 | EN: This rule makes Laplace transforms useful for initial value problems.<br>FR: Cette règle rend les transformées de Laplace utiles pour les problèmes de Cauchy.<br>中：该公式使 Laplace 变换适合处理初值问题。                                 |
| `\mathcal{L}\{f''\}=s^2F(s)-sf(0)-f'(0)` | second derivative transform rule, règle de transformation de la dérivée seconde, 二阶导数变换公式 | A transform rule for second derivatives including two initial data terms.                          | EN: Second-order equations become algebraic equations in `F(s)`.<br>FR: Les équations du second ordre deviennent des équations algébriques en `F(s)`.<br>中：二阶方程会变成关于 `F(s)` 的代数方程。                                               |
| `u(t-a)`                                 | Heaviside step function, fonction échelon de Heaviside, Heaviside 阶跃函数                    | A function that turns on at time `a`.                                                              | EN: Step functions model sudden switching.<br>FR: Les fonctions échelon modélisent des commutations brusques.<br>中：阶跃函数可建模突然开关。                                                                                                  |
| `\delta(t-a)`                            | Dirac delta, delta de Dirac, Dirac δ 函数                                                   | An idealized impulse concentrated at time `a`.                                                     | EN: Impulse forces are often modeled using `\delta(t-a)`.<br>FR: Les forces impulsionnelles sont souvent modélisées par `\delta(t-a)`.<br>中：冲击力常用 `\delta(t-a)` 建模。                                                              |
| `(f*g)(t)`                               | convolution, convolution, 卷积                                                              | An operation combining two functions through an integral over shifted products.                    | EN: Convolution describes accumulated response to past input.<br>FR: La convolution décrit la réponse accumulée aux entrées passées.<br>中：卷积描述系统对过去输入的累积响应。                                                                      |
| `\mathcal{L}\{f*g\}=F(s)G(s)`            | convolution theorem, théorème de convolution, 卷积定理                                        | A theorem turning convolution in time into multiplication in the Laplace domain.                   | EN: Convolution becomes simpler after transforming.<br>FR: La convolution devient plus simple après transformation.<br>中：变换后卷积会化为乘法。                                                                                             |
| —                                        | transfer function, fonction de transfert, 传递函数                                            | A transformed-domain ratio describing the input-output behavior of a linear system.                | EN: In control theory, poles of a transfer function reveal stability information.<br>FR: En théorie du contrôle, les pôles d’une fonction de transfert donnent des informations de stabilité.<br>中：在控制理论中，传递函数的极点揭示稳定性信息。        |

## 17.8 Series solutions and special functions

| Symbol (LaTeX)                      | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                       | Definition                                                                               | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
| ----------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `y=\sum_{n=0}^{\infty}a_n(x-x_0)^n` | power series solution, solution en série entière, 幂级数解  | A solution expressed as a power series around a point.                                   | EN: Series methods solve equations that lack elementary closed forms.<br>FR: Les méthodes en série résolvent des équations sans forme fermée élémentaire.<br>中：级数法可求解没有初等闭式解的方程。                                  |
| `x_0`                               | ordinary point, point ordinaire, 常点                     | A point where coefficients of a linear differential equation behave regularly.           | EN: Power series solutions are natural near ordinary points.<br>FR: Les solutions en série entière sont naturelles près des points ordinaires.<br>中：常点附近适合使用幂级数解。                                                 |
| —                                   | singular point, point singulier, 奇点                     | A point where the coefficient functions of a differential equation fail to be regular.   | EN: `x=0` is a singular point for many equations with coefficients `1/x`.<br>FR: `x=0` est un point singulier pour beaucoup d’équations avec coefficients `1/x`.<br>中：含 `1/x` 系数的许多方程在 `x=0` 处有奇点。                |
| —                                   | regular singular point, point singulier régulier, 正则奇点  | A singular point mild enough for Frobenius series methods.                               | EN: Bessel’s equation has a regular singular point at `0`.<br>FR: L’équation de Bessel a un point singulier régulier en `0`.<br>中：Bessel 方程在 `0` 处有正则奇点。                                                          |
| `y=x^r\sum_{n=0}^{\infty}a_nx^n`    | Frobenius method, méthode de Frobenius, Frobenius 方法    | A series method allowing a leading power `x^r` near a regular singular point.            | EN: Frobenius method extends ordinary power series methods.<br>FR: La méthode de Frobenius étend les méthodes de séries entières ordinaires.<br>中：Frobenius 方法推广了普通幂级数法。                                          |
| —                                   | indicial equation, équation indicielle, 指标方程            | An algebraic equation determining possible leading exponents in the Frobenius method.    | EN: Roots of the indicial equation determine candidate exponents `r`.<br>FR: Les racines de l’équation indicielle déterminent les exposants candidats `r`.<br>中：指标方程的根决定候选指数 `r`。                                 |
| `J_\nu(x)`                          | Bessel function, fonction de Bessel, Bessel 函数          | A special function arising from Bessel’s differential equation.                          | EN: Bessel functions appear in circular vibration and wave problems.<br>FR: Les fonctions de Bessel apparaissent dans les problèmes d’ondes et de vibrations circulaires.<br>中：Bessel 函数出现在圆形振动与波动问题中。            |
| `P_n(x)`                            | Legendre polynomial, polynôme de Legendre, Legendre 多项式 | A family of orthogonal polynomials solving Legendre’s differential equation.             | EN: Legendre polynomials appear in spherical symmetry problems.<br>FR: Les polynômes de Legendre apparaissent dans les problèmes à symétrie sphérique.<br>中：Legendre 多项式出现在球对称问题中。                                |
| `H_n(x)`                            | Hermite polynomial, polynôme de Hermite, Hermite 多项式    | A family of orthogonal polynomials used in analysis, probability, and quantum mechanics. | EN: Hermite polynomials occur in the quantum harmonic oscillator.<br>FR: Les polynômes de Hermite apparaissent dans l’oscillateur harmonique quantique.<br>中：Hermite 多项式出现在量子谐振子中。                                |
| `L_n(x)`                            | Laguerre polynomial, polynôme de Laguerre, Laguerre 多项式 | A family of orthogonal polynomials common in radial quantum and approximation problems.  | EN: Laguerre polynomials appear in hydrogen atom calculations.<br>FR: Les polynômes de Laguerre apparaissent dans les calculs de l’atome d’hydrogène.<br>中：Laguerre 多项式出现在氢原子计算中。                                 |
| —                                   | orthogonal functions, fonctions orthogonales, 正交函数      | Functions whose inner product is zero on a given interval or domain.                     | EN: Sine and cosine functions form orthogonal families under suitable intervals.<br>FR: Les fonctions sinus et cosinus forment des familles orthogonales sur des intervalles appropriés.<br>中：正弦和余弦函数在适当区间上构成正交族。 |

## 17.9 Partial differential equations

| Symbol (LaTeX)     | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                 | Definition                                                                     | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| ------------------ | ------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `u(x,t)`           | unknown field, champ inconnu, 未知场函数               | A function of space and time or several spatial variables.                     | EN: Temperature can be modeled as `u(x,t)`.<br>FR: La température peut être modélisée par `u(x,t)`.<br>中：温度可建模为 `u(x,t)`。                                                                                |
| `u_t=ku_{xx}`      | heat equation, équation de la chaleur, 热方程        | A parabolic PDE modeling diffusion of heat or similar quantities.              | EN: Heat spreads from hot regions to cold regions.<br>FR: La chaleur diffuse des régions chaudes vers les régions froides.<br>中：热量从高温区域向低温区域扩散。                                                          |
| `u_{tt}=c^2u_{xx}` | wave equation, équation des ondes, 波方程            | A hyperbolic PDE modeling wave propagation.                                    | EN: A vibrating string satisfies a wave equation.<br>FR: Une corde vibrante satisfait une équation des ondes.<br>中：振动弦满足波方程。                                                                             |
| `\Delta u=0`       | Laplace equation, équation de Laplace, Laplace 方程 | An elliptic PDE describing harmonic functions and equilibrium states.          | EN: Steady-state temperature without sources satisfies `\Delta u=0`.<br>FR: Une température stationnaire sans source satisfait `\Delta u=0`.<br>中：无热源的稳态温度满足 `\Delta u=0`。                               |
| `-\Delta u=f`      | Poisson equation, équation de Poisson, Poisson 方程 | An elliptic PDE describing potentials with sources.                            | EN: Electrostatic potential with charge density satisfies a Poisson equation.<br>FR: Le potentiel électrostatique avec densité de charge satisfait une équation de Poisson.<br>中：带电荷密度的静电势满足 Poisson 方程。 |
| `\Delta`           | Laplacian, laplacien, Laplace 算子                  | The sum of second spatial partial derivatives.                                 | EN: In two variables, `\Delta u=u_{xx}+u_{yy}`.<br>FR: En deux variables, `\Delta u=u_{xx}+u_{yy}`.<br>中：在二元情形，`\Delta u=u_{xx}+u_{yy}`。                                                                 |
| —                  | elliptic PDE, équation elliptique, 椭圆型偏微分方程       | A class of PDEs often associated with equilibrium and boundary value problems. | EN: Laplace’s equation is elliptic.<br>FR: L’équation de Laplace est elliptique.<br>中：Laplace 方程是椭圆型方程。                                                                                                  |
| —                  | parabolic PDE, équation parabolique, 抛物型偏微分方程     | A class of PDEs often associated with diffusion and time evolution.            | EN: The heat equation is parabolic.<br>FR: L’équation de la chaleur est parabolique.<br>中：热方程是抛物型方程。                                                                                                     |
| —                  | hyperbolic PDE, équation hyperbolique, 双曲型偏微分方程   | A class of PDEs often associated with waves and finite propagation speed.      | EN: The wave equation is hyperbolic.<br>FR: L’équation des ondes est hyperbolique.<br>中：波方程是双曲型方程。                                                                                                       |
| —                  | weak solution, solution faible, 弱解                | A generalized solution satisfying an integral form of the equation.            | EN: Weak solutions allow less regular functions than classical solutions.<br>FR: Les solutions faibles permettent des fonctions moins régulières que les solutions classiques.<br>中：弱解允许比经典解更低的光滑性。      |
| —                  | classical solution, solution classique, 经典解       | A sufficiently differentiable function satisfying the PDE pointwise.           | EN: A classical solution has the derivatives required by the equation.<br>FR: Une solution classique possède les dérivées requises par l’équation.<br>中：经典解具有方程所需的导数并逐点满足方程。                             |

## 17.10 Separation of variables and Fourier methods

| Symbol (LaTeX)                                             | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                           | Definition                                                                               | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| ---------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `u(x,t)=X(x)T(t)`                                          | separation ansatz, hypothèse de séparation, 分离变量假设                          | An assumed product form used to separate a PDE into ODEs.                                | EN: For the heat equation, try `u(x,t)=X(x)T(t)`.<br>FR: Pour l’équation de la chaleur, essayer `u(x,t)=X(x)T(t)`.<br>中：对热方程，可尝试 `u(x,t)=X(x)T(t)`。                                                                                                |
| `\lambda`                                                  | separation constant, constante de séparation, 分离常数                          | A constant introduced when separated expressions depend on different variables.          | EN: Separation often yields equations like `X''+\lambda X=0`.<br>FR: La séparation donne souvent des équations comme `X''+\lambda X=0`.<br>中：分离变量常得到 `X''+\lambda X=0` 这类方程。                                                                       |
| —                                                          | eigenvalue problem, problème aux valeurs propres, 特征值问题                     | A boundary value problem where only certain parameter values allow nontrivial solutions. | EN: Boundary conditions select allowed eigenvalues.<br>FR: Les conditions aux limites sélectionnent les valeurs propres permises.<br>中：边界条件会筛选允许的特征值。                                                                                              |
| `X''+\lambda X=0`                                          | spatial eigenvalue equation, équation spatiale aux valeurs propres, 空间特征值方程 | An ODE obtained from separating the spatial variable.                                    | EN: On an interval, sine functions often solve this equation with zero endpoint conditions.<br>FR: Sur un intervalle, les fonctions sinus résolvent souvent cette équation avec conditions nulles aux extrémités.<br>中：在区间上，正弦函数常满足零端点条件下的空间特征值方程。 |
| `\sum_{n=1}^{\infty}b_n\sin(nx)`                           | Fourier sine series, série de Fourier en sinus, Fourier 正弦级数                | An expansion using sine functions, often suited to zero boundary conditions.             | EN: Sine series appear for strings fixed at both ends.<br>FR: Les séries en sinus apparaissent pour les cordes fixées aux deux extrémités.<br>中：两端固定的弦常出现正弦级数。                                                                                     |
| `\frac{a_0}{2}+\sum_{n=1}^{\infty}(a_n\cos nx+b_n\sin nx)` | Fourier series, série de Fourier, Fourier 级数                                | An expansion of a periodic function using sines and cosines.                             | EN: Fourier series decomposes periodic signals into frequencies.<br>FR: La série de Fourier décompose les signaux périodiques en fréquences.<br>中：Fourier 级数把周期信号分解为不同频率成分。                                                                        |
| `a_n,b_n`                                                  | Fourier coefficients, coefficients de Fourier, Fourier 系数                   | Coefficients measuring the contribution of each sine or cosine mode.                     | EN: Fourier coefficients are computed by integrals over one period.<br>FR: Les coefficients de Fourier se calculent par des intégrales sur une période.<br>中：Fourier 系数通过一个周期上的积分计算。                                                               |
| `e^{inx}`                                                  | complex Fourier mode, mode de Fourier complexe, 复 Fourier 模态                | A complex exponential basis function for periodic analysis.                              | EN: Complex notation often simplifies Fourier calculations.<br>FR: La notation complexe simplifie souvent les calculs de Fourier.<br>中：复数记号常简化 Fourier 计算。                                                                                         |
| `\hat{f}(\xi)`                                             | Fourier transform, transformée de Fourier, Fourier 变换                       | An integral transform representing a function by continuous frequencies.                 | EN: Fourier transform converts differentiation into multiplication by frequency.<br>FR: La transformée de Fourier transforme la dérivation en multiplication par la fréquence.<br>中：Fourier 变换把微分转化为频率乘法。                                          |
| —                                                          | normal mode, mode normal, 正则模态 / 正常模                                        | A characteristic oscillation pattern of a system.                                        | EN: A vibrating string decomposes into normal modes.<br>FR: Une corde vibrante se décompose en modes normaux.<br>中：振动弦可分解为正常模。                                                                                                                     |
| —                                                          | superposition of modes, superposition des modes, 模态叠加                       | Building a solution by summing separated solutions.                                      | EN: Heat equation solutions can be written as sums of decaying modes.<br>FR: Les solutions de l’équation de la chaleur peuvent s’écrire comme sommes de modes décroissants.<br>中：热方程的解可写成衰减模态之和。                                                   |

## 17.11 Numerical methods for differential equations

| Symbol (LaTeX)                     | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                             | Definition                                                                                                       | Examples                                                                                                                                                                       |
| ---------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `h`                                | step size, pas, 步长                                            | The time or space increment used in a numerical method.                                                          | EN: Smaller `h` often improves accuracy but increases computation.<br>FR: Un plus petit `h` améliore souvent la précision mais augmente le calcul.<br>中：较小的 `h` 通常提高精度，但增加计算量。 |
| `t_n=t_0+nh`                       | time grid, grille temporelle, 时间网格                            | Discrete time points used to approximate a solution.                                                             | EN: Numerical solvers compute approximations at grid points `t_n`.<br>FR: Les solveurs numériques calculent des approximations aux points `t_n`.<br>中：数值求解器在网格点 `t_n` 上计算近似解。  |
| `y_{n+1}=y_n+h f(t_n,y_n)`         | Euler method, méthode d’Euler, Euler 方法                       | A first-order explicit method for approximating solutions of `y'=f(t,y)`.                                        | EN: Euler method uses the current slope to step forward.<br>FR: La méthode d’Euler utilise la pente actuelle pour avancer.<br>中：Euler 方法使用当前斜率向前推进。                            |
| —                                  | explicit method, méthode explicite, 显式方法                      | A numerical method computing the next value directly from known previous values.                                 | EN: Euler’s method is explicit.<br>FR: La méthode d’Euler est explicite.<br>中：Euler 方法是显式方法。                                                                                   |
| —                                  | implicit method, méthode implicite, 隐式方法                      | A numerical method where the next value is defined through an equation that may need solving.                    | EN: Backward Euler is implicit.<br>FR: Euler rétrograde est implicite.<br>中：后退 Euler 方法是隐式方法。                                                                                  |
| `y_{n+1}=y_n+h f(t_{n+1},y_{n+1})` | backward Euler method, méthode d’Euler implicite, 后退 Euler 方法 | An implicit first-order method often more stable for stiff equations.                                            | EN: Backward Euler is useful for stiff decay problems.<br>FR: Euler implicite est utile pour les problèmes raides de décroissance.<br>中：后退 Euler 方法适合刚性衰减问题。                   |
| `k_1,k_2,k_3,k_4`                  | Runge-Kutta stages, étapes de Runge-Kutta, Runge-Kutta 阶段量    | Intermediate slopes used in Runge-Kutta methods.                                                                 | EN: Classical RK4 uses four slope evaluations per step.<br>FR: Le RK4 classique utilise quatre évaluations de pente par pas.<br>中：经典 RK4 每步使用四次斜率计算。                           |
| —                                  | Runge-Kutta method, méthode de Runge-Kutta, Runge-Kutta 方法    | A family of numerical methods using weighted combinations of intermediate slopes.                                | EN: RK4 is a widely used fourth-order method.<br>FR: RK4 est une méthode d’ordre quatre très utilisée.<br>中：RK4 是常用的四阶方法。                                                      |
| —                                  | local truncation error, erreur de troncature locale, 局部截断误差   | The error made in one step assuming previous values are exact.                                                   | EN: Local error measures one-step approximation quality.<br>FR: L’erreur locale mesure la qualité d’une approximation en un pas.<br>中：局部误差衡量单步近似质量。                            |
| —                                  | global error, erreur globale, 全局误差                            | The accumulated error over many numerical steps.                                                                 | EN: Global error includes propagation of earlier errors.<br>FR: L’erreur globale inclut la propagation des erreurs précédentes.<br>中：全局误差包含此前误差的传播。                            |
| —                                  | numerical stability, stabilité numérique, 数值稳定性               | A property describing whether errors remain controlled during computation.                                       | EN: Stable methods prevent small errors from growing uncontrollably.<br>FR: Les méthodes stables empêchent les petites erreurs de croître sans contrôle.<br>中：稳定方法防止小误差失控增长。   |
| —                                  | stiffness, raideur, 刚性                                        | A situation where some components evolve much faster than others, forcing very small steps for explicit methods. | EN: Chemical reaction systems can be stiff.<br>FR: Les systèmes de réactions chimiques peuvent être raides.<br>中：化学反应系统可能具有刚性。                                                 |

## 17.12 Modeling, applications, and interpretation

| Symbol (LaTeX)                    | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                     | Definition                                                                                                       | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                          |
| --------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `\frac{dP}{dt}=kP`                | exponential growth model, modèle de croissance exponentielle, 指数增长模型  | A model where the growth rate is proportional to the current amount.                                             | EN: Ideal population growth can be modeled by `P'=kP`.<br>FR: Une croissance idéale de population peut être modélisée par `P'=kP`.<br>中：理想人口增长可由 `P'=kP` 建模。                                      |
| `\frac{dP}{dt}=-kP`               | exponential decay model, modèle de décroissance exponentielle, 指数衰减模型 | A model where the quantity decreases at a rate proportional to itself.                                           | EN: Radioactive decay follows an exponential decay model.<br>FR: La désintégration radioactive suit un modèle de décroissance exponentielle.<br>中：放射性衰变服从指数衰减模型。                                  |
| `\frac{dP}{dt}=rP(1-\frac{P}{K})` | logistic equation, équation logistique, Logistic 方程                   | A growth model with carrying capacity `K`.                                                                       | EN: Logistic growth slows as `P` approaches `K`.<br>FR: La croissance logistique ralentit lorsque `P` approche `K`.<br>中：当 `P` 接近 `K` 时，Logistic 增长变慢。                                            |
| `K`                               | carrying capacity, capacité de charge, 环境容纳量                          | The limiting population level supported by an environment in the logistic model.                                 | EN: In the logistic model, `K` is the long-term population limit.<br>FR: Dans le modèle logistique, `K` est la limite de population à long terme.<br>中：在 Logistic 模型中，`K` 是长期人口上限。                |
| `m x''+c x'+kx=0`                 | damped harmonic oscillator, oscillateur harmonique amorti, 阻尼谐振子      | A second-order model for oscillation with damping and restoring force.                                           | EN: A mass-spring-damper system satisfies this equation.<br>FR: Un système masse-ressort-amortisseur satisfait cette équation.<br>中：质量-弹簧-阻尼系统满足这个方程。                                             |
| `m x''+kx=0`                      | simple harmonic oscillator, oscillateur harmonique simple, 简谐振子       | A model for undamped oscillation around equilibrium.                                                             | EN: An ideal spring-mass system oscillates sinusoidally.<br>FR: Un système masse-ressort idéal oscille sinusoïdalement.<br>中：理想弹簧-质量系统作正弦振荡。                                                      |
| `F=ma`                            | Newtonian dynamics, dynamique newtonienne, 牛顿动力学                      | A physical modeling principle relating force to acceleration.                                                    | EN: Newton’s second law often produces second-order differential equations.<br>FR: La deuxième loi de Newton produit souvent des équations différentielles du second ordre.<br>中：牛顿第二定律常产生二阶微分方程。 |
| `SIR`                             | SIR model, modèle SIR, SIR 模型                                         | An epidemic model dividing a population into susceptible, infected, and recovered compartments.                  | EN: SIR models describe infection spread and recovery.<br>FR: Les modèles SIR décrivent la propagation d’une infection et la guérison.<br>中：SIR 模型描述感染传播与康复过程。                                    |
| `R_0`                             | basic reproduction number, nombre de reproduction de base, 基本再生数      | The expected number of secondary infections caused by one infected individual in a fully susceptible population. | EN: If `R_0>1`, an outbreak may grow initially.<br>FR: Si `R_0>1`, une épidémie peut croître initialement.<br>中：若 `R_0>1`，疫情初期可能增长。                                                               |
| —                                 | compartment model, modèle compartimental, 舱室模型                        | A model dividing a system into interacting compartments or states.                                               | EN: Epidemiology and pharmacokinetics often use compartment models.<br>FR: L’épidémiologie et la pharmacocinétique utilisent souvent des modèles compartimentaux.<br>中：流行病学和药代动力学常使用舱室模型。         |
| —                                 | nondimensionalization, adimensionnement, 无量纲化                         | Rescaling variables to reduce units and reveal essential parameters.                                             | EN: Nondimensionalization can reduce the number of parameters.<br>FR: L’adimensionnement peut réduire le nombre de paramètres.<br>中：无量纲化可以减少参数数量。                                                 |
| —                                 | parameter estimation, estimation de paramètres, 参数估计                  | The process of fitting model parameters to observed data.                                                        | EN: Growth-rate parameters can be estimated from time-series data.<br>FR: Les paramètres de croissance peuvent être estimés à partir de séries temporelles.<br>中：增长率参数可由时间序列数据估计。                 |
| —                                 | model validation, validation du modèle, 模型验证                          | Checking whether model predictions agree with observed behavior or intended use.                                 | EN: A model should be tested against data not used for fitting.<br>FR: Un modèle devrait être testé sur des données non utilisées pour l’ajustement.<br>中：模型应在未用于拟合的数据上测试。                        |
# 18. Numerical Analysis, Approximation, and Scientific Computing

## 18.1 Numerical error and floating-point arithmetic

| Symbol (LaTeX)                                                              | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                           | Definition                                                                                             | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
| --------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `x_{\text{true}}`                                                           | true value, valeur exacte, 真值                               | The exact mathematical value being approximated.                                                       | EN: If the exact integral is `2`, then `x_{\text{true}}=2`.<br>FR: Si l’intégrale exacte vaut `2`, alors `x_{\text{true}}=2`.<br>中：若精确积分值为 `2`，则 `x_{\text{true}}=2`。                                                               |
| `\hat{x}`                                                                   | approximate value, valeur approchée, 近似值                    | A computed or estimated value used in place of the exact value.                                        | EN: If a calculator gives `3.1416` for `\pi`, then `3.1416` is an approximate value.<br>FR: Si une calculatrice donne `3,1416` pour `\pi`, alors `3,1416` est une valeur approchée.<br>中：若计算器给出 `\pi\approx3.1416`，则 `3.1416` 是近似值。 |
| `e=\hat{x}-x_{\text{true}}`                                                 | absolute error with sign, erreur signée, 带符号误差              | The difference between an approximate value and the exact value.                                       | EN: If `\hat{x}=2.01` and `x_{\text{true}}=2`, then `e=0.01`.<br>FR: Si `\hat{x}=2,01` et `x_{\text{true}}=2`, alors `e=0,01`.<br>中：若 `\hat{x}=2.01` 且 `x_{\text{true}}=2`，则 `e=0.01`。                                              |
| `\lvert \hat{x}-x_{\text{true}}\rvert`                                      | absolute error, erreur absolue, 绝对误差                        | The magnitude of the difference between an approximation and the exact value.                          | EN: The absolute error of `3.14` as an approximation to `\pi` is about `0.00159`.<br>FR: L’erreur absolue de `3,14` comme approximation de `\pi` est environ `0,00159`.<br>中：用 `3.14` 近似 `\pi` 的绝对误差约为 `0.00159`。                   |
| `\frac{\lvert \hat{x}-x_{\text{true}}\rvert}{\lvert x_{\text{true}}\rvert}` | relative error, erreur relative, 相对误差                       | Absolute error scaled by the size of the exact value.                                                  | EN: Relative error is more informative when comparing quantities with different scales.<br>FR: L’erreur relative est plus informative pour comparer des grandeurs d’échelles différentes.<br>中：比较不同尺度的量时，相对误差更有信息量。                 |
| —                                                                           | round-off error, erreur d’arrondi, 舍入误差                     | Error caused by representing real numbers with finitely many digits.                                   | EN: Floating-point arithmetic introduces round-off error.<br>FR: L’arithmétique en virgule flottante introduit des erreurs d’arrondi.<br>中：浮点运算会引入舍入误差。                                                                             |
| —                                                                           | truncation error, erreur de troncature, 截断误差                | Error caused by replacing an infinite or exact process with a finite approximation.                    | EN: Replacing a Taylor series by its first three terms creates truncation error.<br>FR: Remplacer une série de Taylor par ses trois premiers termes crée une erreur de troncature.<br>中：用 Taylor 级数前三项代替完整级数会产生截断误差。                |
| `O(h^p)`                                                                    | order of error, ordre de l’erreur, 误差阶                      | A notation describing how error scales as a parameter `h` tends to zero.                               | EN: Error `O(h^2)` decreases about fourfold when `h` is halved.<br>FR: Une erreur `O(h^2)` diminue environ par quatre lorsque `h` est divisé par deux.<br>中：`O(h^2)` 误差在 `h` 减半时约缩小为四分之一。                                           |
| `\epsilon_{\text{mach}}`                                                    | machine epsilon, epsilon machine, 机器精度                      | The smallest scale at which floating-point arithmetic can distinguish `1` from a nearby larger number. | EN: Machine epsilon measures floating-point resolution near `1`.<br>FR: L’epsilon machine mesure la résolution flottante près de `1`.<br>中：机器精度度量浮点数在 `1` 附近的分辨能力。                                                                  |
| —                                                                           | floating-point number, nombre à virgule flottante, 浮点数      | A finite-precision representation of real numbers using sign, significand, base, and exponent.         | EN: Computers approximate most real numbers by floating-point numbers.<br>FR: Les ordinateurs approchent la plupart des réels par des nombres à virgule flottante.<br>中：计算机通常用浮点数近似大多数实数。                                           |
| —                                                                           | overflow, dépassement de capacité, 上溢                       | A numerical event where a computed value is too large to represent.                                    | EN: Computing `e^{1000}` may cause overflow in standard floating-point arithmetic.<br>FR: Calculer `e^{1000}` peut provoquer un dépassement de capacité en virgule flottante standard.<br>中：计算 `e^{1000}` 可能导致标准浮点运算上溢。             |
| —                                                                           | underflow, soupassement de capacité, 下溢                     | A numerical event where a nonzero value is too small to represent normally.                            | EN: Very tiny probabilities may underflow to zero.<br>FR: Des probabilités très petites peuvent subir un sous-dépassement vers zéro.<br>中：极小概率可能下溢为零。                                                                               |
| —                                                                           | catastrophic cancellation, annulation catastrophique, 灾难性抵消 | Severe loss of significant digits when subtracting nearly equal numbers.                               | EN: Computing `\sqrt{x+1}-\sqrt{x}` for large `x` may suffer cancellation.<br>FR: Calculer `\sqrt{x+1}-\sqrt{x}` pour grand `x` peut subir une annulation catastrophique.<br>中：当 `x` 很大时，计算 `\sqrt{x+1}-\sqrt{x}` 可能发生灾难性抵消。        |

## 18.2 Conditioning, stability, and sensitivity

| Symbol (LaTeX)                                | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                | Definition                                                                                            | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| --------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| —                                             | conditioning, conditionnement, 条件性                               | A property of a mathematical problem measuring how sensitive the answer is to small changes in input. | EN: Solving nearly singular linear systems is poorly conditioned.<br>FR: Résoudre des systèmes presque singuliers est mal conditionné.<br>中：求解近似奇异的线性方程组条件性较差。                                                                            |
| `\kappa`                                      | condition number, nombre de condition, 条件数                       | A numerical measure of sensitivity of a problem or matrix.                                            | EN: A large condition number means small input errors can cause large output errors.<br>FR: Un grand nombre de condition signifie que de petites erreurs d’entrée peuvent causer de grandes erreurs de sortie.<br>中：条件数大表示输入小误差可能导致输出大误差。 |
| `\kappa(A)=\lVert A\rVert\lVert A^{-1}\rVert` | matrix condition number, nombre de condition matriciel, 矩阵条件数    | A measure of sensitivity for solving `Ax=b` when `A` is invertible.                                   | EN: If `\kappa(A)` is large, solving `Ax=b` may be numerically unreliable.<br>FR: Si `\kappa(A)` est grand, résoudre `Ax=b` peut être numériquement peu fiable.<br>中：若 `\kappa(A)` 很大，求解 `Ax=b` 可能数值不可靠。                                  |
| —                                             | well-conditioned problem, problème bien conditionné, 良态问题        | A problem whose solution changes only mildly under small input perturbations.                         | EN: Adding two ordinary numbers is usually well-conditioned.<br>FR: Additionner deux nombres ordinaires est généralement bien conditionné.<br>中：普通数值加法通常是良态问题。                                                                            |
| —                                             | ill-conditioned problem, problème mal conditionné, 病态问题          | A problem whose solution is highly sensitive to small input perturbations.                            | EN: Polynomial root finding can be ill-conditioned for multiple roots.<br>FR: La recherche de racines de polynômes peut être mal conditionnée pour les racines multiples.<br>中：多重根附近的多项式求根可能是病态问题。                                        |
| —                                             | stability of an algorithm, stabilité d’un algorithme, 算法稳定性      | A property describing whether an algorithm controls the growth of numerical errors.                   | EN: A stable algorithm does not unnecessarily amplify round-off errors.<br>FR: Un algorithme stable n’amplifie pas inutilement les erreurs d’arrondi.<br>中：稳定算法不会无谓放大舍入误差。                                                                |
| —                                             | forward error, erreur directe, 前向误差                              | The difference between the computed answer and the exact answer.                                      | EN: Forward error measures how wrong the final result is.<br>FR: L’erreur directe mesure l’écart du résultat final.<br>中：前向误差度量最终结果与真解相差多少。                                                                                               |
| —                                             | backward error, erreur inverse, 后向误差                             | The smallest input perturbation that would make the computed answer exact.                            | EN: Backward error asks what nearby problem the algorithm solved exactly.<br>FR: L’erreur inverse demande quel problème voisin l’algorithme a résolu exactement.<br>中：后向误差询问算法精确求解了哪个邻近问题。                                                |
| —                                             | backward stable algorithm, algorithme inversement stable, 后向稳定算法 | An algorithm whose computed result is the exact solution to a slightly perturbed input problem.       | EN: Gaussian elimination with pivoting is often backward stable in practice.<br>FR: L’élimination de Gauss avec pivotement est souvent inversement stable en pratique.<br>中：带主元选取的高斯消元在实践中通常后向稳定。                                         |
| `r=b-A\hat{x}`                                | residual, résidu, 残差                                             | The amount by which an approximate solution fails to satisfy an equation.                             | EN: A small residual does not always imply a small solution error for ill-conditioned systems.<br>FR: Un petit résidu n’implique pas toujours une petite erreur de solution pour les systèmes mal conditionnés.<br>中：对病态系统，小残差不一定意味着小解误差。 |
| `\lVert r\rVert`                              | residual norm, norme du résidu, 残差范数                             | A scalar measure of the size of the residual.                                                         | EN: Iterative solvers often stop when the residual norm is small.<br>FR: Les solveurs itératifs s’arrêtent souvent lorsque la norme du résidu est petite.<br>中：迭代求解器常在残差范数足够小时停止。                                                         |
| —                                             | perturbation analysis, analyse de perturbation, 扰动分析             | The study of how small changes in input affect output.                                                | EN: Perturbation analysis explains sensitivity before computation begins.<br>FR: L’analyse de perturbation explique la sensibilité avant le calcul.<br>中：扰动分析在计算前解释问题的敏感性。                                                                |

## 18.3 Root-finding and nonlinear equations

| Symbol (LaTeX)                                            | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                             | Definition                                                                                     | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| --------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `f(x)=0`                                                  | nonlinear equation, équation non linéaire, 非线性方程              | An equation whose solution is a root of a function.                                            | EN: Solving `\cos x-x=0` is a nonlinear root-finding problem.<br>FR: Résoudre `\cos x-x=0` est un problème non linéaire de recherche de racine.<br>中：求解 `\cos x-x=0` 是非线性求根问题。                                                                |
| `x^*`                                                     | root, racine, 根                                               | A value satisfying `f(x^*)=0`.                                                                 | EN: `x=2` is a root of `x^2-4=0`.<br>FR: `x=2` est une racine de `x^2-4=0`.<br>中：`x=2` 是 `x^2-4=0` 的一个根。                                                                                                                                      |
| `x_{n+1}`                                                 | next iterate, itéré suivant, 下一迭代值                            | The next approximation produced by an iterative method.                                        | EN: Root-finding methods generate a sequence `x_0,x_1,x_2,\ldots`.<br>FR: Les méthodes de recherche de racines génèrent une suite `x_0,x_1,x_2,\ldots`.<br>中：求根方法生成序列 `x_0,x_1,x_2,\ldots`。                                                   |
| —                                                         | bisection method, méthode de dichotomie, 二分法                  | A root-finding method repeatedly halving an interval where a continuous function changes sign. | EN: Bisection is slow but reliable when `f(a)f(b)<0`.<br>FR: La dichotomie est lente mais fiable lorsque `f(a)f(b)<0`.<br>中：当 `f(a)f(b)<0` 时，二分法较慢但可靠。                                                                                        |
| `f(a)f(b)<0`                                              | sign-change condition, condition de changement de signe, 变号条件 | A condition guaranteeing a root in `[a,b]` for continuous `f`.                                 | EN: If `f` is continuous and changes sign, the intermediate value theorem gives a root.<br>FR: Si `f` est continue et change de signe, le théorème des valeurs intermédiaires donne une racine.<br>中：若 `f` 连续且变号，中值定理保证区间内有根。                 |
