| `x_{n+1}=x_n-\frac{f(x_n)}{f'(x_n)}`                      | Newton’s method, méthode de Newton, Newton 法                  | An iterative method using tangent-line approximation to find roots.                            | EN: Newton’s method is fast near a simple root when started well.<br>FR: La méthode de Newton est rapide près d’une racine simple avec un bon point initial.<br>中：初值合适且接近简单根时，Newton 法收敛很快。                                                   |
| `x_{n+1}=x_n-f(x_n)\frac{x_n-x_{n-1}}{f(x_n)-f(x_{n-1})}` | secant method, méthode de la sécante, 割线法                     | A root-finding method replacing the derivative in Newton’s method by a finite difference.      | EN: The secant method avoids computing `f'` explicitly.<br>FR: La méthode de la sécante évite de calculer explicitement `f'`.<br>中：割线法避免显式计算 `f'`。                                                                                            |
| `x=g(x)`                                                  | fixed-point equation, équation de point fixe, 不动点方程           | An equation reformulated so that the solution is a fixed point of `g`.                         | EN: Solving `f(x)=0` can sometimes be rewritten as `x=g(x)`.<br>FR: Résoudre `f(x)=0` peut parfois se réécrire `x=g(x)`.<br>中：求解 `f(x)=0` 有时可改写为 `x=g(x)`。                                                                                    |
| `x_{n+1}=g(x_n)`                                          | fixed-point iteration, itération de point fixe, 不动点迭代         | An iterative process repeatedly applying `g`.                                                  | EN: If `g` is a contraction, fixed-point iteration converges.<br>FR: Si `g` est une contraction, l’itération de point fixe converge.<br>中：若 `g` 是压缩映射，不动点迭代收敛。                                                                                |
| —                                                         | convergence order, ordre de convergence, 收敛阶                  | A measure of how quickly iterates approach the solution.                                       | EN: Newton’s method has quadratic convergence near a simple root under standard assumptions.<br>FR: La méthode de Newton a une convergence quadratique près d’une racine simple sous des hypothèses usuelles.<br>中：在通常条件下，Newton 法在简单根附近二阶收敛。 |
| —                                                         | linear convergence, convergence linéaire, 线性收敛                | Convergence where the error is reduced by roughly a constant factor each step.                 | EN: Bisection has linear convergence.<br>FR: La dichotomie a une convergence linéaire.<br>中：二分法具有线性收敛。                                                                                                                                        |
| —                                                         | quadratic convergence, convergence quadratique, 二次收敛          | Convergence where the next error is roughly proportional to the square of the current error.   | EN: Quadratic convergence can double the number of correct digits at each step.<br>FR: La convergence quadratique peut doubler le nombre de chiffres corrects à chaque étape.<br>中：二次收敛可能每步使正确数字数量翻倍。                                         |

## 18.4 Interpolation and polynomial approximation

| Symbol (LaTeX)                 | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                                  | Definition                                                                                          | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| ------------------------------ | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `p_n(x)`                       | interpolating polynomial, polynôme d’interpolation, 插值多项式                          | A polynomial passing through specified data points.                                                 | EN: Through `n+1` distinct points, there is a unique polynomial of degree at most `n`.<br>FR: Par `n+1` points distincts passe un unique polynôme de degré au plus `n`.<br>中：经过 `n+1` 个横坐标互异的点，存在唯一次数不超过 `n` 的插值多项式。                                     |
| `(x_i,y_i)`                    | interpolation node and value, nœud et valeur d’interpolation, 插值节点与函数值             | A data pair used to construct an interpolant.                                                       | EN: The point `(x_i,y_i)` imposes the condition `p(x_i)=y_i`.<br>FR: Le point `(x_i,y_i)` impose la condition `p(x_i)=y_i`.<br>中：点 `(x_i,y_i)` 要求 `p(x_i)=y_i`。                                                                                          |
| `L_i(x)`                       | Lagrange basis polynomial, polynôme de base de Lagrange, Lagrange 基多项式             | A polynomial equal to `1` at one interpolation node and `0` at the others.                          | EN: Lagrange interpolation writes `p(x)=\sum_i y_iL_i(x)`.<br>FR: L’interpolation de Lagrange écrit `p(x)=\sum_i y_iL_i(x)`.<br>中：Lagrange 插值写作 `p(x)=\sum_i y_iL_i(x)`。                                                                                 |
| `p(x)=\sum_{i=0}^{n}y_iL_i(x)` | Lagrange interpolation formula, formule d’interpolation de Lagrange, Lagrange 插值公式 | A formula for the polynomial interpolating given data values.                                       | EN: This formula is conceptually simple but can be numerically inefficient for repeated evaluation.<br>FR: Cette formule est conceptuellement simple mais peut être numériquement inefficace pour des évaluations répétées.<br>中：该公式概念简单，但重复求值时数值效率可能不高。 |
| —                              | Newton interpolation, interpolation de Newton, Newton 插值                           | An interpolation form using divided differences and nested multiplication.                          | EN: Newton interpolation is convenient when new nodes are added.<br>FR: L’interpolation de Newton est pratique lorsque de nouveaux nœuds sont ajoutés.<br>中：添加新节点时，Newton 插值较方便。                                                                         |
| `f[x_0,\ldots,x_k]`            | divided difference, différence divisée, 差商                                         | A recursive quantity used in Newton interpolation.                                                  | EN: First divided differences are slopes between data points.<br>FR: Les différences divisées d’ordre un sont des pentes entre points de données.<br>中：一阶差商是数据点之间的斜率。                                                                                    |
| —                              | spline, spline, 样条                                                                 | A piecewise polynomial function joined smoothly at nodes.                                           | EN: Cubic splines often produce smooth curves through data.<br>FR: Les splines cubiques produisent souvent des courbes lisses passant par les données.<br>中：三次样条常生成穿过数据的光滑曲线。                                                                            |
| —                              | cubic spline, spline cubique, 三次样条                                                 | A spline made of cubic polynomials with smoothness conditions at knots.                             | EN: Cubic splines avoid some oscillations of high-degree polynomial interpolation.<br>FR: Les splines cubiques évitent certaines oscillations des interpolations de haut degré.<br>中：三次样条可避免高次多项式插值的一些振荡。                                                |
| —                              | knot, nœud, 结点                                                                     | A point where spline pieces meet.                                                                   | EN: Smoothness conditions are imposed at spline knots.<br>FR: Des conditions de régularité sont imposées aux nœuds de spline.<br>中：样条结点处施加光滑性条件。                                                                                                         |
| —                              | Runge phenomenon, phénomène de Runge, Runge 现象                                     | Oscillation that can occur when using high-degree polynomial interpolation at equally spaced nodes. | EN: Equally spaced high-degree interpolation may behave badly near interval endpoints.<br>FR: L’interpolation de haut degré à nœuds équidistants peut mal se comporter près des extrémités.<br>中：等距节点高次插值在区间端点附近可能表现很差。                                  |
| `T_n(x)`                       | Chebyshev polynomial, polynôme de Tchebychev, Chebyshev 多项式                        | A family of orthogonal polynomials useful in approximation and stable interpolation.                | EN: Chebyshev nodes reduce interpolation oscillation.<br>FR: Les nœuds de Tchebychev réduisent les oscillations d’interpolation.<br>中：Chebyshev 节点可减少插值振荡。                                                                                               |
| —                              | best approximation, meilleure approximation, 最佳逼近                                  | The closest approximation to a function under a chosen norm and approximation space.                | EN: Least-squares approximation minimizes error in an average-square sense.<br>FR: L’approximation aux moindres carrés minimise l’erreur au sens quadratique moyen.<br>中：最小二乘逼近在均方意义下最小化误差。                                                              |

## 18.5 Numerical differentiation and finite differences

| Symbol (LaTeX)                    | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                                | Definition                                                                                            | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| --------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `f'(x)`                           | derivative to approximate, dérivée à approcher, 待近似导数                            | The exact derivative estimated by numerical differentiation.                                          | EN: Numerical differentiation estimates `f'(x)` from nearby function values.<br>FR: La différentiation numérique estime `f'(x)` à partir de valeurs voisines.<br>中：数值微分用附近函数值估计 `f'(x)`。                                   |
| `h`                               | step size, pas, 步长                                                               | The small increment used in a finite difference formula.                                              | EN: Smaller `h` reduces truncation error but may increase round-off error.<br>FR: Un plus petit `h` réduit l’erreur de troncature mais peut augmenter l’erreur d’arrondi.<br>中：较小的 `h` 降低截断误差，但可能增加舍入误差。                   |
| `\frac{f(x+h)-f(x)}{h}`           | forward difference, différence avant, 前向差分                                       | A first-order approximation to the derivative using a point ahead.                                    | EN: Forward difference uses `x` and `x+h`.<br>FR: La différence avant utilise `x` et `x+h`.<br>中：前向差分使用 `x` 与 `x+h`。                                                                                                       |
| `\frac{f(x)-f(x-h)}{h}`           | backward difference, différence arrière, 后向差分                                    | A first-order approximation to the derivative using a point behind.                                   | EN: Backward difference is useful near a right endpoint.<br>FR: La différence arrière est utile près d’une extrémité droite.<br>中：后向差分适合右端点附近。                                                                             |
| `\frac{f(x+h)-f(x-h)}{2h}`        | central difference, différence centrée, 中心差分                                     | A derivative approximation using symmetric points around `x`.                                         | EN: Central difference is usually more accurate than forward difference for the same `h`.<br>FR: La différence centrée est généralement plus précise que la différence avant pour le même `h`.<br>中：在相同步长下，中心差分通常比前向差分更准确。 |
| `\frac{f(x+h)-2f(x)+f(x-h)}{h^2}` | second derivative finite difference, différence finie de dérivée seconde, 二阶导数差分 | A finite difference approximation to `f''(x)`.                                                        | EN: This formula appears in finite-difference methods for the heat equation.<br>FR: Cette formule apparaît dans les méthodes aux différences finies pour l’équation de la chaleur.<br>中：该公式出现在热方程的有限差分法中。                  |
| —                                 | finite difference method, méthode des différences finies, 有限差分法                  | A numerical method replacing derivatives by difference quotients.                                     | EN: Finite differences convert differential equations into algebraic equations.<br>FR: Les différences finies transforment des équations différentielles en équations algébriques.<br>中：有限差分把微分方程转化为代数方程。                  |
| —                                 | stencil, stencil / schéma local, 差分模板                                            | The pattern of grid points used in a finite difference approximation.                                 | EN: A three-point stencil uses `x-h`, `x`, and `x+h`.<br>FR: Un stencil à trois points utilise `x-h`, `x` et `x+h`.<br>中：三点模板使用 `x-h`、`x` 和 `x+h`。                                                                         |
| —                                 | consistency, consistance, 相容性                                                    | A property that the discrete approximation approaches the continuous operator as the grid is refined. | EN: A consistent derivative formula becomes exact in the limit `h\to0`.<br>FR: Une formule de dérivée consistante devient exacte lorsque `h\to0`.<br>中：相容的导数公式在 `h\to0` 极限下趋于精确算子。                                         |
| —                                 | discretization error, erreur de discrétisation, 离散化误差                            | Error introduced by replacing a continuous problem with a discrete one.                               | EN: Finite difference grids introduce discretization error.<br>FR: Les grilles de différences finies introduisent une erreur de discrétisation.<br>中：有限差分网格会引入离散化误差。                                                       |

## 18.6 Numerical integration and quadrature

| Symbol (LaTeX)                                                 | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                       | Definition                                                                                      | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| -------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `\int_a^b f(x)\,dx`                                            | definite integral to approximate, intégrale définie à approcher, 待近似定积分 | An exact integral estimated by numerical quadrature.                                            | EN: Numerical integration approximates areas under curves.<br>FR: L’intégration numérique approche les aires sous les courbes.<br>中：数值积分近似曲线下方的面积。                                                        |
| —                                                              | quadrature, quadrature, 求积                                              | Numerical approximation of definite integrals, usually by weighted sums of function values.     | EN: Quadrature replaces an integral by a finite sum.<br>FR: La quadrature remplace une intégrale par une somme finie.<br>中：求积法用有限和代替积分。                                                                   |
| `\sum_i w_i f(x_i)`                                            | quadrature rule, formule de quadrature, 求积公式                            | A weighted sum used to approximate an integral.                                                 | EN: The nodes `x_i` and weights `w_i` define a quadrature rule.<br>FR: Les nœuds `x_i` et poids `w_i` définissent une formule de quadrature.<br>中：节点 `x_i` 与权重 `w_i` 定义一个求积公式。                            |
| `h=\frac{b-a}{n}`                                              | mesh width, largeur de maille, 网格宽度                                     | The spacing between equally spaced integration nodes.                                           | EN: Composite rules divide `[a,b]` into subintervals of width `h`.<br>FR: Les formules composées divisent `[a,b]` en sous-intervalles de largeur `h`.<br>中：复合求积公式把 `[a,b]` 分成宽度为 `h` 的小区间。                |
| `h\sum_{i=0}^{n-1}f(a+ih)`                                     | left rectangle rule, règle des rectangles à gauche, 左矩形公式               | A quadrature rule using left endpoints of subintervals.                                         | EN: The left rectangle rule is simple but low-order.<br>FR: La règle des rectangles à gauche est simple mais d’ordre faible.<br>中：左矩形公式简单但阶数较低。                                                           |
| `h\sum_{i=1}^{n}f(a+ih)`                                       | right rectangle rule, règle des rectangles à droite, 右矩形公式              | A quadrature rule using right endpoints of subintervals.                                        | EN: Right rectangles approximate the integral using endpoint heights.<br>FR: Les rectangles à droite approchent l’intégrale avec les hauteurs aux extrémités droites.<br>中：右矩形公式使用右端点函数值近似积分。             |
| `h\sum_{i=0}^{n-1}f(a+(i+\frac12)h)`                           | midpoint rule, règle du point milieu, 中点公式                              | A quadrature rule using midpoint values of subintervals.                                        | EN: The midpoint rule is often more accurate than endpoint rectangle rules.<br>FR: La règle du point milieu est souvent plus précise que les règles des rectangles aux extrémités.<br>中：中点公式通常比端点矩形公式更准确。 |
| `\frac{h}{2}\left(f(a)+2\sum_{i=1}^{n-1}f(x_i)+f(b)\right)`    | composite trapezoidal rule, règle des trapèzes composée, 复合梯形公式         | A quadrature rule approximating the curve by line segments.                                     | EN: The trapezoidal rule is effective for smooth periodic functions.<br>FR: La règle des trapèzes est efficace pour les fonctions périodiques lisses.<br>中：梯形公式对光滑周期函数常很有效。                               |
| `\frac{h}{3}\left(f(x_0)+4f(x_1)+2f(x_2)+\cdots+f(x_n)\right)` | Simpson’s rule, règle de Simpson, Simpson 公式                            | A quadrature rule using quadratic interpolation on pairs of subintervals.                       | EN: Simpson’s rule often gives high accuracy for smooth functions.<br>FR: La règle de Simpson donne souvent une grande précision pour les fonctions lisses.<br>中：Simpson 公式对光滑函数常有较高精度。                   |
| —                                                              | Gaussian quadrature, quadrature de Gauss, Gauss 求积                      | A quadrature method choosing nodes and weights to integrate polynomials of high degree exactly. | EN: Gaussian quadrature is efficient when function evaluations are expensive.<br>FR: La quadrature de Gauss est efficace lorsque les évaluations de fonction sont coûteuses.<br>中：当函数求值代价高时，Gauss 求积很有效。  |
| —                                                              | adaptive quadrature, quadrature adaptative, 自适应求积                       | A numerical integration method refining intervals where the integrand is difficult.             | EN: Adaptive quadrature uses more points near sharp peaks.<br>FR: La quadrature adaptative utilise plus de points près des pics marqués.<br>中：自适应求积会在尖峰附近使用更多节点。                                          |
| —                                                              | Monte Carlo integration, intégration de Monte-Carlo, Monte Carlo 积分     | Integration by random sampling, especially useful in high dimensions.                           | EN: Monte Carlo error often decreases like `1/\sqrt{N}`.<br>FR: L’erreur de Monte-Carlo décroît souvent comme `1/\sqrt{N}`.<br>中：Monte Carlo 误差通常按 `1/\sqrt{N}` 下降。                                       |

## 18.7 Numerical linear algebra

| Symbol (LaTeX) | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                           | Definition                                                                               | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |
| -------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `Ax=b`         | linear system, système linéaire, 线性方程组                                      | A matrix equation solved numerically in many scientific computations.                    | EN: Discretized differential equations often produce systems `Ax=b`.<br>FR: Les équations différentielles discrétisées produisent souvent des systèmes `Ax=b`.<br>中：离散化微分方程常产生 `Ax=b` 线性系统。                                 |
| `PA=LU`        | LU factorization with pivoting, factorisation LU avec pivotement, 带主元 LU 分解 | A factorization using row permutations, lower triangular, and upper triangular matrices. | EN: LU factorization solves many systems with the same matrix efficiently.<br>FR: La factorisation LU résout efficacement plusieurs systèmes avec la même matrice.<br>中：LU 分解适合高效求解多个同系数矩阵系统。                               |
| `L`            | lower triangular matrix, matrice triangulaire inférieure, 下三角矩阵             | A matrix with zero entries above the diagonal.                                           | EN: Lower triangular systems are solved by forward substitution.<br>FR: Les systèmes triangulaires inférieurs se résolvent par substitution avant.<br>中：下三角系统用前代法求解。                                                        |
| `U`            | upper triangular matrix, matrice triangulaire supérieure, 上三角矩阵             | A matrix with zero entries below the diagonal.                                           | EN: Upper triangular systems are solved by back substitution.<br>FR: Les systèmes triangulaires supérieurs se résolvent par substitution arrière.<br>中：上三角系统用回代法求解。                                                         |
| `P`            | permutation matrix, matrice de permutation, 置换矩阵                            | A matrix that reorders rows or columns.                                                  | EN: Pivoting uses permutation matrices to improve numerical stability.<br>FR: Le pivotement utilise des matrices de permutation pour améliorer la stabilité numérique.<br>中：主元选取使用置换矩阵提高数值稳定性。                              |
| —              | pivoting, pivotement, 主元选取                                                  | A strategy of swapping rows or columns to avoid small pivots.                            | EN: Partial pivoting reduces numerical instability in Gaussian elimination.<br>FR: Le pivotement partiel réduit l’instabilité numérique dans l’élimination de Gauss.<br>中：部分主元选取减少高斯消元中的数值不稳定。                              |
| `A=QR`         | QR factorization, factorisation QR, QR 分解                                   | A factorization into an orthogonal or unitary matrix and an upper triangular matrix.     | EN: QR factorization is central for least-squares problems.<br>FR: La factorisation QR est centrale pour les problèmes de moindres carrés.<br>中：QR 分解是最小二乘问题的核心工具。                                                          |
| `A=LL^T`       | Cholesky factorization, factorisation de Cholesky, Cholesky 分解              | A factorization for symmetric positive definite matrices.                                | EN: Cholesky factorization is faster than general LU for positive definite systems.<br>FR: La factorisation de Cholesky est plus rapide que LU général pour les systèmes définis positifs.<br>中：对正定系统，Cholesky 分解比一般 LU 更快。 |
| —              | sparse matrix, matrice creuse, 稀疏矩阵                                         | A matrix with mostly zero entries.                                                       | EN: Large finite-difference problems often produce sparse matrices.<br>FR: Les grands problèmes aux différences finies produisent souvent des matrices creuses.<br>中：大型有限差分问题常产生稀疏矩阵。                                       |
| —              | dense matrix, matrice dense, 稠密矩阵                                           | A matrix with many nonzero entries.                                                      | EN: Dense matrix multiplication requires substantial memory for large dimensions.<br>FR: La multiplication de matrices denses exige beaucoup de mémoire en grande dimension.<br>中：大维度稠密矩阵乘法需要大量内存。                          |
| —              | iterative solver, solveur itératif, 迭代求解器                                   | A method that approximates the solution of a linear system through repeated updates.     | EN: Iterative solvers are common for huge sparse systems.<br>FR: Les solveurs itératifs sont courants pour les très grands systèmes creux.<br>中：巨大稀疏系统常用迭代求解器。                                                              |
| `M^{-1}A`      | preconditioned matrix, matrice préconditionnée, 预条件矩阵                       | A transformed matrix intended to improve convergence of iterative solvers.               | EN: Good preconditioning can greatly reduce iteration counts.<br>FR: Un bon préconditionnement peut fortement réduire le nombre d’itérations.<br>中：良好的预条件可显著减少迭代次数。                                                         |

## 18.8 Iterative methods for linear systems

| Symbol (LaTeX)         | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                                            | Definition                                                                             | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |
| ---------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `x^{(k)}`              | kth iterate, k-ième itéré, 第 k 次迭代值                                                          | The approximation produced at iteration `k`.                                           | EN: Iterative methods update `x^{(k)}` until convergence.<br>FR: Les méthodes itératives mettent à jour `x^{(k)}` jusqu’à convergence.<br>中：迭代法不断更新 `x^{(k)}` 直到收敛。                                                                          |
| `r^{(k)}=b-Ax^{(k)}`   | residual at iteration k, résidu à l’itération k, 第 k 次残差                                     | The residual corresponding to the current approximate solution.                        | EN: A decreasing residual often indicates progress.<br>FR: Un résidu décroissant indique souvent un progrès.<br>中：残差下降通常表示迭代正在推进。                                                                                                            |
| —                      | Jacobi method, méthode de Jacobi, Jacobi 迭代法                                                 | An iterative method updating each variable using values from the previous iteration.   | EN: Jacobi iteration is easy to parallelize.<br>FR: L’itération de Jacobi est facile à paralléliser.<br>中：Jacobi 迭代易于并行化。                                                                                                                    |
| —                      | Gauss-Seidel method, méthode de Gauss-Seidel, Gauss-Seidel 迭代法                               | An iterative method using newly updated values immediately within the same iteration.  | EN: Gauss-Seidel often converges faster than Jacobi.<br>FR: Gauss-Seidel converge souvent plus vite que Jacobi.<br>中：Gauss-Seidel 往往比 Jacobi 收敛更快。                                                                                           |
| `\omega`               | relaxation parameter, paramètre de relaxation, 松弛参数                                          | A parameter controlling update size in relaxation methods.                             | EN: Choosing `\omega` well can accelerate convergence.<br>FR: Bien choisir `\omega` peut accélérer la convergence.<br>中：适当选择 `\omega` 可加速收敛。                                                                                                 |
| —                      | successive over-relaxation, sur-relaxation successive, 逐次超松弛法                                | A Gauss-Seidel-type method using a relaxation parameter to accelerate convergence.     | EN: SOR can be effective for structured linear systems.<br>FR: La SOR peut être efficace pour les systèmes linéaires structurés.<br>中：SOR 对结构化线性系统可能很有效。                                                                                     |
| —                      | gradient descent for linear systems, descente de gradient pour systèmes linéaires, 线性系统的梯度下降 | An iterative method minimizing a quadratic residual-related objective.                 | EN: Solving `Ax=b` with positive definite `A` can be viewed as minimizing a quadratic function.<br>FR: Résoudre `Ax=b` avec `A` définie positive peut être vu comme minimiser une fonction quadratique.<br>中：当 `A` 正定时，求解 `Ax=b` 可看作最小化二次函数。 |
| —                      | conjugate gradient method, méthode du gradient conjugué, 共轭梯度法                               | An efficient iterative method for symmetric positive definite linear systems.          | EN: Conjugate gradient is widely used for large sparse positive definite systems.<br>FR: Le gradient conjugué est très utilisé pour les grands systèmes creux définis positifs.<br>中：共轭梯度法广泛用于大型稀疏正定系统。                                      |
| —                      | Krylov subspace, sous-espace de Krylov, Krylov 子空间                                           | A subspace generated by repeatedly applying a matrix to a vector.                      | EN: Many modern iterative solvers search in Krylov subspaces.<br>FR: Beaucoup de solveurs itératifs modernes cherchent dans des sous-espaces de Krylov.<br>中：许多现代迭代求解器在 Krylov 子空间中搜索。                                                       |
| `\mathcal{K}_k(A,r_0)` | kth Krylov subspace, k-ième sous-espace de Krylov, 第 k 阶 Krylov 子空间                          | The span of `r_0,Ar_0,\ldots,A^{k-1}r_0`.                                              | EN: GMRES builds approximations from Krylov subspaces.<br>FR: GMRES construit des approximations à partir de sous-espaces de Krylov.<br>中：GMRES 从 Krylov 子空间中构造近似解。                                                                          |
| —                      | GMRES, GMRES, GMRES 方法                                                                       | A Krylov method for general nonsymmetric linear systems.                               | EN: GMRES minimizes the residual over a Krylov subspace.<br>FR: GMRES minimise le résidu sur un sous-espace de Krylov.<br>中：GMRES 在 Krylov 子空间上最小化残差。                                                                                        |
| —                      | multigrid method, méthode multigrille, 多重网格法                                                 | A method accelerating convergence by solving error components on multiple grid scales. | EN: Multigrid can solve certain PDE-derived systems nearly optimally.<br>FR: La multigrille peut résoudre certains systèmes issus de PDE presque optimalement.<br>中：多重网格法可近似最优地求解某些 PDE 离散系统。                                                |

## 18.9 Eigenvalue computation and matrix decompositions

| Symbol (LaTeX)   | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                         | Definition                                                                               | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                    |
| ---------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `Av=\lambda v`   | eigenvalue problem, problème aux valeurs propres, 特征值问题                   | The problem of finding scalars and nonzero vectors satisfying the eigenvalue equation.   | EN: Vibration modes are often eigenvectors of a matrix or operator.<br>FR: Les modes de vibration sont souvent des vecteurs propres d’une matrice ou d’un opérateur.<br>中：振动模态常是矩阵或算子的特征向量。                 |
| `\lambda_{\max}` | dominant eigenvalue, valeur propre dominante, 主特征值                        | The eigenvalue with largest magnitude under common usage.                                | EN: The power method estimates the dominant eigenvalue.<br>FR: La méthode de la puissance estime la valeur propre dominante.<br>中：幂法估计主特征值。                                                                 |
| —                | power method, méthode de la puissance, 幂法                                 | An iterative method for estimating a dominant eigenvalue and eigenvector.                | EN: Power iteration repeatedly multiplies by the matrix and normalizes.<br>FR: L’itération de puissance multiplie répétitivement par la matrice puis normalise.<br>中：幂迭代反复乘以矩阵并归一化。                         |
| —                | inverse iteration, itération inverse, 反迭代                                 | An eigenvalue method applying inverse-like solves to focus on selected eigenvalues.      | EN: Inverse iteration can find eigenvectors near a chosen shift.<br>FR: L’itération inverse peut trouver des vecteurs propres près d’un décalage choisi.<br>中：反迭代可寻找接近给定移位的特征向量。                            |
| `A-\mu I`        | shifted matrix, matrice décalée, 移位矩阵                                     | A matrix shifted by scalar `\mu`, used in eigenvalue algorithms.                         | EN: Shifted inverse iteration targets eigenvalues near `\mu`.<br>FR: L’itération inverse décalée vise les valeurs propres proches de `\mu`.<br>中：移位反迭代针对接近 `\mu` 的特征值。                                      |
| —                | QR algorithm, algorithme QR, QR 算法                                        | A standard algorithm for computing eigenvalues through repeated QR factorizations.       | EN: The QR algorithm is fundamental in dense eigenvalue computation.<br>FR: L’algorithme QR est fondamental pour le calcul dense des valeurs propres.<br>中：QR 算法是稠密矩阵特征值计算的基础方法。                            |
| `A=U\Sigma V^T`  | singular value decomposition, décomposition en valeurs singulières, 奇异值分解 | A factorization representing a matrix through orthogonal directions and singular values. | EN: SVD is used in compression, least squares, and data analysis.<br>FR: La SVD est utilisée en compression, moindres carrés et analyse de données.<br>中：SVD 用于压缩、最小二乘和数据分析。                                |
| `\sigma_i`       | singular value, valeur singulière, 奇异值                                    | A nonnegative value measuring stretching by a matrix in special directions.              | EN: Small singular values indicate nearly lost directions.<br>FR: De petites valeurs singulières indiquent des directions presque perdues.<br>中：小奇异值表示某些方向几乎被压扁。                                            |
| —                | low-rank approximation, approximation de rang faible, 低秩近似                | Approximation of a matrix by another matrix with smaller rank.                           | EN: Truncated SVD gives an optimal low-rank approximation under common norms.<br>FR: La SVD tronquée donne une approximation de rang faible optimale pour des normes courantes.<br>中：截断 SVD 在常见范数下给出最优低秩近似。 |
| `A_k`            | rank-k approximation, approximation de rang k, k 秩近似                      | A matrix approximation with rank at most `k`.                                            | EN: Image compression often stores only a rank-`k` approximation.<br>FR: La compression d’image stocke souvent seulement une approximation de rang `k`.<br>中：图像压缩常只存储 `k` 秩近似。                              |
| —                | spectral radius, rayon spectral, 谱半径                                      | The largest absolute value of the eigenvalues of a matrix.                               | EN: The spectral radius controls convergence of some iterations.<br>FR: Le rayon spectral contrôle la convergence de certaines itérations.<br>中：谱半径控制某些迭代方法的收敛性。                                            |

## 18.10 Numerical optimization

| Symbol (LaTeX)                           | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                         | Definition                                                                                 | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| ---------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `\min_x f(x)`                            | optimization problem, problème d’optimisation, 最优化问题                      | The problem of finding an input minimizing an objective function.                          | EN: Fitting a model often requires minimizing a loss function.<br>FR: Ajuster un modèle exige souvent de minimiser une fonction de perte.<br>中：拟合模型常需要最小化损失函数。                                           |
| `f(x)`                                   | objective function, fonction objectif, 目标函数                               | The function being minimized or maximized.                                                 | EN: In least squares, the objective is the sum of squared residuals.<br>FR: En moindres carrés, l’objectif est la somme des résidus carrés.<br>中：在最小二乘中，目标函数是残差平方和。                                      |
| `\nabla f(x)`                            | gradient, gradient, 梯度                                                    | The vector of first derivatives used to indicate the direction of steepest increase.       | EN: Optimization methods often move against the gradient.<br>FR: Les méthodes d’optimisation se déplacent souvent contre le gradient.<br>中：优化方法常沿梯度反方向移动。                                                |
| `x_{k+1}=x_k-\alpha_k\nabla f(x_k)`      | gradient descent, descente de gradient, 梯度下降                              | An iterative method moving in the negative gradient direction.                             | EN: Gradient descent is simple but may be slow on ill-conditioned problems.<br>FR: La descente de gradient est simple mais peut être lente pour les problèmes mal conditionnés.<br>中：梯度下降简单，但在病态问题上可能很慢。 |
| `\alpha_k`                               | step size / learning rate, pas / taux d’apprentissage, 步长 / 学习率           | The scalar controlling how far an optimization method moves at iteration `k`.              | EN: Too large a step size can cause divergence.<br>FR: Un pas trop grand peut provoquer la divergence.<br>中：步长过大可能导致发散。                                                                                  |
| —                                        | line search, recherche linéaire, 线搜索                                      | A procedure for choosing a step size along a search direction.                             | EN: Line search balances progress and stability.<br>FR: La recherche linéaire équilibre progrès et stabilité.<br>中：线搜索在推进速度与稳定性之间取平衡。                                                                    |
| `H_f(x)`                                 | Hessian matrix, matrice hessienne, Hessian 矩阵                             | The matrix of second derivatives used to describe curvature.                               | EN: Newton’s method uses the Hessian for second-order information.<br>FR: La méthode de Newton utilise la hessienne pour l’information du second ordre.<br>中：Newton 法使用 Hessian 获取二阶信息。                  |
| `x_{k+1}=x_k-H_f(x_k)^{-1}\nabla f(x_k)` | Newton optimization method, méthode de Newton en optimisation, Newton 优化法 | An optimization method using a local quadratic approximation.                              | EN: Newton’s method can converge rapidly near a well-behaved minimum.<br>FR: La méthode de Newton peut converger rapidement près d’un minimum régulier.<br>中：Newton 优化法在良性极小点附近可快速收敛。                    |
| —                                        | quasi-Newton method, méthode quasi-Newton, 拟 Newton 法                     | An optimization method approximating Hessian information rather than computing it exactly. | EN: BFGS is a common quasi-Newton method.<br>FR: BFGS est une méthode quasi-Newton courante.<br>中：BFGS 是常见的拟 Newton 方法。                                                                                  |
| —                                        | constrained optimization, optimisation sous contraintes, 约束优化             | Optimization subject to equations, inequalities, or feasible-region restrictions.          | EN: Portfolio optimization often includes budget and risk constraints.<br>FR: L’optimisation de portefeuille inclut souvent des contraintes de budget et de risque.<br>中：投资组合优化常包含预算与风险约束。               |
| `g_i(x)\leq0`                            | inequality constraint, contrainte d’inégalité, 不等式约束                      | A constraint requiring a function to be nonpositive or nonnegative.                        | EN: The constraint `x\geq0` can be written as `-x\leq0`.<br>FR: La contrainte `x\geq0` peut s’écrire `-x\leq0`.<br>中：约束 `x\geq0` 可写为 `-x\leq0`。                                                          |
| —                                        | convex optimization, optimisation convexe, 凸优化                            | Optimization where the objective and feasible region have convex structure.                | EN: Convex problems avoid bad local minima under standard assumptions.<br>FR: Les problèmes convexes évitent les mauvais minima locaux sous des hypothèses usuelles.<br>中：在通常假设下，凸问题没有坏的局部极小值。           |

## 18.11 Discretization of differential equations

| Symbol (LaTeX)      | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                               | Definition                                                                                                     | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                              |
| ------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| —                   | discretization, discrétisation, 离散化                             | The process of replacing a continuous problem by a finite-dimensional approximation.                           | EN: A PDE on a domain can be discretized on a grid.<br>FR: Une PDE sur un domaine peut être discrétisée sur une grille.<br>中：定义域上的 PDE 可在网格上离散化。                                                      |
| `x_i=a+ih`          | grid point, point de grille, 网格点                                | A discrete point used to approximate a continuous domain.                                                      | EN: Finite difference methods compute unknowns at grid points.<br>FR: Les méthodes aux différences finies calculent les inconnues aux points de grille.<br>中：有限差分法在网格点上计算未知量。                         |
| `u_i\approx u(x_i)` | grid function value, valeur de fonction sur grille, 网格函数值       | A discrete approximation to a function value at a grid point.                                                  | EN: `u_i` stores the approximate value at `x_i`.<br>FR: `u_i` stocke la valeur approchée en `x_i`.<br>中：`u_i` 存储在 `x_i` 处的近似值。                                                                        |
| —                   | finite difference method, méthode des différences finies, 有限差分法 | A discretization method replacing derivatives by finite differences.                                           | EN: The heat equation can be solved by finite differences in space and time.<br>FR: L’équation de la chaleur peut être résolue par différences finies en espace et en temps.<br>中：热方程可用空间和时间上的有限差分求解。 |
| —                   | finite element method, méthode des éléments finis, 有限元法         | A method approximating solutions by piecewise functions over a mesh.                                           | EN: Finite elements are effective on complex geometries.<br>FR: Les éléments finis sont efficaces sur des géométries complexes.<br>中：有限元法适合复杂几何区域。                                                    |
| —                   | mesh, maillage, 网格 / 剖分                                         | A subdivision of a domain into elements or cells.                                                              | EN: A triangular mesh can approximate an irregular two-dimensional region.<br>FR: Un maillage triangulaire peut approcher une région bidimensionnelle irrégulière.<br>中：三角形网格可近似不规则二维区域。              |
| —                   | element, élément, 单元                                            | A basic piece of a finite element mesh.                                                                        | EN: In two dimensions, elements are often triangles or quadrilaterals.<br>FR: En dimension deux, les éléments sont souvent des triangles ou quadrilatères.<br>中：二维中，单元常为三角形或四边形。                      |
| —                   | basis function, fonction de base, 基函数                           | A function used to build an approximate solution.                                                              | EN: Finite element solutions are linear combinations of basis functions.<br>FR: Les solutions par éléments finis sont des combinaisons linéaires de fonctions de base.<br>中：有限元解是基函数的线性组合。            |
| —                   | weak form, formulation faible, 弱形式                              | An integral formulation of a differential equation used in finite element methods.                             | EN: The weak form reduces differentiability requirements on the solution.<br>FR: La formulation faible réduit les exigences de différentiabilité de la solution.<br>中：弱形式降低了对解的可微性要求。                 |
| —                   | spectral method, méthode spectrale, 谱方法                         | A method approximating solutions using global basis functions such as trigonometric or orthogonal polynomials. | EN: Spectral methods can be highly accurate for smooth solutions.<br>FR: Les méthodes spectrales peuvent être très précises pour les solutions lisses.<br>中：谱方法对光滑解可具有很高精度。                           |
| —                   | finite volume method, méthode des volumes finis, 有限体积法          | A method based on conservation laws over small control volumes.                                                | EN: Finite volume methods are common in fluid dynamics.<br>FR: Les méthodes de volumes finis sont courantes en dynamique des fluides.<br>中：有限体积法常用于流体动力学。                                             |
| —                   | CFL condition, condition CFL, CFL 条件                            | A stability condition relating time step size to spatial mesh size for time-dependent PDEs.                    | EN: Explicit wave solvers often require a CFL condition.<br>FR: Les solveurs explicites d’ondes exigent souvent une condition CFL.<br>中：显式波动方程求解器通常需要满足 CFL 条件。                                       |

## 18.12 Simulation, uncertainty, and reproducibility

| Symbol (LaTeX)                    | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                       | Definition                                                                                                   | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
| --------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| —                                 | simulation, simulation, 模拟 / 仿真                                         | Numerical imitation of a mathematical, physical, or statistical system.                                      | EN: Weather prediction uses large-scale numerical simulation.<br>FR: La prévision météorologique utilise de grandes simulations numériques.<br>中：天气预报使用大规模数值模拟。                                                                                       |
| `N`                               | number of samples or steps, nombre d’échantillons ou d’étapes, 样本数 / 步数 | A count controlling computational resolution or statistical accuracy.                                        | EN: Increasing `N` often improves accuracy but raises cost.<br>FR: Augmenter `N` améliore souvent la précision mais augmente le coût.<br>中：增大 `N` 通常提高精度，但增加成本。                                                                                       |
| —                                 | Monte Carlo method, méthode de Monte-Carlo, Monte Carlo 方法              | A computational method using random sampling to estimate quantities.                                         | EN: Monte Carlo methods estimate expectations by averaging samples.<br>FR: Les méthodes de Monte-Carlo estiment des espérances par moyenne d’échantillons.<br>中：Monte Carlo 方法通过样本平均估计期望。                                                             |
| `\frac{1}{N}\sum_{i=1}^{N}f(X_i)` | Monte Carlo estimator, estimateur de Monte-Carlo, Monte Carlo 估计量       | A sample-average estimator for an expected value or integral.                                                | EN: The estimator converges to `\mathbb{E}[f(X)]` under standard assumptions.<br>FR: L’estimateur converge vers `\mathbb{E}[f(X)]` sous des hypothèses usuelles.<br>中：在通常假设下，该估计量收敛到 `\mathbb{E}[f(X)]`。                                              |
| —                                 | variance reduction, réduction de variance, 方差缩减                         | Techniques for reducing Monte Carlo estimator variance without simply increasing sample size.                | EN: Importance sampling is a variance reduction technique.<br>FR: L’échantillonnage préférentiel est une technique de réduction de variance.<br>中：重要性抽样是一种方差缩减技术。                                                                                     |
| —                                 | importance sampling, échantillonnage préférentiel, 重要性抽样                | A Monte Carlo method sampling more frequently from important regions and reweighting estimates.              | EN: Importance sampling helps estimate rare-event probabilities.<br>FR: L’échantillonnage préférentiel aide à estimer des probabilités d’événements rares.<br>中：重要性抽样有助于估计小概率事件。                                                                      |
| —                                 | quasi-Monte Carlo, quasi-Monte-Carlo, 拟 Monte Carlo                     | A sampling method using low-discrepancy deterministic sequences instead of random samples.                   | EN: Quasi-Monte Carlo can improve integration accuracy for some smooth high-dimensional problems.<br>FR: Le quasi-Monte-Carlo peut améliorer l’intégration pour certains problèmes lisses de grande dimension.<br>中：拟 Monte Carlo 可改善某些高维光滑积分问题的精度。   |
| —                                 | sensitivity analysis, analyse de sensibilité, 敏感性分析                     | The study of how model outputs change when parameters or inputs vary.                                        | EN: Sensitivity analysis identifies influential parameters.<br>FR: L’analyse de sensibilité identifie les paramètres influents.<br>中：敏感性分析识别影响较大的参数。                                                                                                  |
| —                                 | uncertainty quantification, quantification de l’incertitude, 不确定性量化     | The analysis of how uncertainty in inputs affects uncertainty in outputs.                                    | EN: Engineering simulations often report uncertainty ranges, not only point predictions.<br>FR: Les simulations d’ingénierie rapportent souvent des intervalles d’incertitude, pas seulement des prédictions ponctuelles.<br>中：工程仿真常报告不确定性区间，而不只是点预测。 |
| —                                 | verification, vérification, 验证计算正确性                                     | Checking whether the numerical method correctly solves the mathematical model.                               | EN: Grid refinement tests help verify numerical PDE solvers.<br>FR: Les tests de raffinement de grille aident à vérifier les solveurs numériques de PDE.<br>中：网格加密测试有助于验证 PDE 数值求解器。                                                                  |
| —                                 | validation, validation, 验证模型适用性                                         | Checking whether the mathematical model adequately represents the real system or data.                       | EN: A fluid simulation should be validated against experiments when possible.<br>FR: Une simulation de fluide devrait être validée par des expériences lorsque c’est possible.<br>中：流体仿真应尽可能用实验数据验证模型适用性。                                             |
| —                                 | reproducibility, reproductibilité, 可复现性                                 | The ability to obtain the same or consistent computational results from documented code, data, and settings. | EN: Fixing random seeds supports reproducible simulations.<br>FR: Fixer les graines aléatoires soutient les simulations reproductibles.<br>中：固定随机种子有助于仿真结果可复现。                                                                                        |
| —                                 | benchmark problem, problème de référence, 基准问题                          | A standard test problem used to compare methods or implementations.                                          | EN: Benchmark problems reveal accuracy, speed, and robustness differences.<br>FR: Les problèmes de référence révèlent les différences de précision, vitesse et robustesse.<br>中：基准问题揭示方法在精度、速度和鲁棒性上的差异。                                               |
# 19. Mathematical Logic, Set Theory, and Foundations

## 19.1 Propositions, truth values, and logical connectives

| Symbol (LaTeX)       | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                 | Definition                                                                                              | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| -------------------- | ------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `P`                  | proposition, proposition, 命题                      | A statement that has a definite truth value, either true or false.                                      | EN: “`2+2=4`” is a proposition.<br>FR: “`2+2=4`” est une proposition.<br>中：“`2+2=4`” 是一个命题。                                                                                               |
| `\top`               | truth, vrai, 真                                    | The truth value representing a true statement.                                                          | EN: The proposition `3>1` has truth value `\top`.<br>FR: La proposition `3>1` a pour valeur de vérité `\top`.<br>中：命题 `3>1` 的真值是 `\top`。                                                  |
| `\bot`               | falsity, faux, 假                                  | The truth value representing a false statement.                                                         | EN: The proposition `3<1` has truth value `\bot`.<br>FR: La proposition `3<1` a pour valeur de vérité `\bot`.<br>中：命题 `3<1` 的真值是 `\bot`。                                                  |
| `\neg P`             | negation, négation, 否定                            | The proposition that is true exactly when `P` is false.                                                 | EN: If `P` is “`x` is even,” then `\neg P` is “`x` is not even.”<br>FR: Si `P` est “`x` est pair,” alors `\neg P` est “`x` n’est pas pair.”<br>中：若 `P` 是“`x` 是偶数”，则 `\neg P` 是“`x` 不是偶数”。 |
| `P\land Q`           | conjunction, conjonction, 合取 / 且                  | The proposition true exactly when both `P` and `Q` are true.                                            | EN: `x>0\land x<1` means `x` lies between `0` and `1`.<br>FR: `x>0\land x<1` signifie que `x` est entre `0` et `1`.<br>中：`x>0\land x<1` 表示 `x` 在 `0` 与 `1` 之间。                            |
| `P\lor Q`            | disjunction, disjonction, 析取 / 或                  | The proposition true when at least one of `P` and `Q` is true.                                          | EN: `x=0\lor x=1` means `x` is either `0` or `1`.<br>FR: `x=0\lor x=1` signifie que `x` vaut `0` ou `1`.<br>中：`x=0\lor x=1` 表示 `x` 等于 `0` 或 `1`。                                          |
| `P\oplus Q`          | exclusive disjunction, disjonction exclusive, 异或  | The proposition true when exactly one of `P` and `Q` is true.                                           | EN: `P\oplus Q` is false when both propositions are true.<br>FR: `P\oplus Q` est fausse lorsque les deux propositions sont vraies.<br>中：当两个命题都为真时，`P\oplus Q` 为假。                         |
| `P\Rightarrow Q`     | implication, implication, 蕴含                      | The proposition false only when `P` is true and `Q` is false.                                           | EN: `x>2\Rightarrow x>1` is true for real `x`.<br>FR: `x>2\Rightarrow x>1` est vraie pour `x` réel.<br>中：对实数 `x`，`x>2\Rightarrow x>1` 为真。                                                 |
| `P\Leftarrow Q`      | reverse implication, implication réciproque, 反向蕴含 | A notation meaning `Q\Rightarrow P`.                                                                    | EN: `x>1\Leftarrow x>2` means `x>2\Rightarrow x>1`.<br>FR: `x>1\Leftarrow x>2` signifie `x>2\Rightarrow x>1`.<br>中：`x>1\Leftarrow x>2` 表示 `x>2\Rightarrow x>1`。                           |
| `P\Leftrightarrow Q` | biconditional, équivalence, 当且仅当                  | The proposition true when `P` and `Q` have the same truth value.                                        | EN: An integer `n` is even if and only if `n=2k` for some integer `k`.<br>FR: Un entier `n` est pair si et seulement si `n=2k` pour un entier `k`.<br>中：整数 `n` 是偶数，当且仅当存在整数 `k` 使 `n=2k`。 |
| `P\equiv Q`          | logical equivalence, équivalence logique, 逻辑等价    | Two propositions are logically equivalent if they have the same truth value under every interpretation. | EN: `P\Rightarrow Q` is equivalent to `\neg P\lor Q`.<br>FR: `P\Rightarrow Q` est équivalent à `\neg P\lor Q`.<br>中：`P\Rightarrow Q` 与 `\neg P\lor Q` 逻辑等价。                               |
| `\neg\neg P\equiv P` | double negation, double négation, 双重否定            | In classical logic, a proposition is equivalent to its double negation.                                 | EN: “It is not false that `P`” is equivalent to `P` in classical logic.<br>FR: “Il n’est pas faux que `P`” équivaut à `P` en logique classique.<br>中：在经典逻辑中，“并非 `P` 为假”等价于 `P`。           |

## 19.2 Truth tables, tautologies, and logical laws

| Symbol (LaTeX)                                | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                                      | Definition                                                                                         | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| --------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| —                                             | truth table, table de vérité, 真值表                                                      | A table listing the truth values of a compound proposition under all assignments of its variables. | EN: A truth table for two variables has four rows.<br>FR: Une table de vérité pour deux variables a quatre lignes.<br>中：两个命题变量的真值表有四行。                                                                    |
| —                                             | tautology, tautologie, 重言式                                                             | A proposition true under every truth assignment.                                                   | EN: `P\lor\neg P` is a tautology.<br>FR: `P\lor\neg P` est une tautologie.<br>中：`P\lor\neg P` 是重言式。                                                                                                       |
| —                                             | contradiction, contradiction, 矛盾式                                                      | A proposition false under every truth assignment.                                                  | EN: `P\land\neg P` is a contradiction.<br>FR: `P\land\neg P` est une contradiction.<br>中：`P\land\neg P` 是矛盾式。                                                                                             |
| —                                             | contingency, contingence, 偶然式                                                          | A proposition true under some truth assignments and false under others.                            | EN: `P\land Q` is contingent because its value depends on `P` and `Q`.<br>FR: `P\land Q` est contingente car sa valeur dépend de `P` et `Q`.<br>中：`P\land Q` 是偶然式，因为其真值取决于 `P` 和 `Q`。                     |
| `\neg(P\land Q)\equiv\neg P\lor\neg Q`        | De Morgan’s law for conjunction, loi de De Morgan pour la conjonction, 合取的 De Morgan 律 | The negation of a conjunction is the disjunction of the negations.                                 | EN: “Not both `P` and `Q`” means “not `P` or not `Q`.”<br>FR: “Non pas `P` et `Q`” signifie “non `P` ou non `Q`.”<br>中：“并非 `P` 且 `Q`”等价于“非 `P` 或非 `Q`”。                                                   |
| `\neg(P\lor Q)\equiv\neg P\land\neg Q`        | De Morgan’s law for disjunction, loi de De Morgan pour la disjonction, 析取的 De Morgan 律 | The negation of a disjunction is the conjunction of the negations.                                 | EN: “Neither `P` nor `Q`” means `\neg P\land\neg Q`.<br>FR: “Ni `P` ni `Q`” signifie `\neg P\land\neg Q`.<br>中：“既非 `P` 也非 `Q`”表示 `\neg P\land\neg Q`。                                                     |
| `P\land Q\equiv Q\land P`                     | commutativity of conjunction, commutativité de la conjonction, 合取交换律                   | The order of conjunction does not affect truth value.                                              | EN: `P\land Q` and `Q\land P` say the same thing logically.<br>FR: `P\land Q` et `Q\land P` disent logiquement la même chose.<br>中：`P\land Q` 与 `Q\land P` 在逻辑上相同。                                        |
| `P\lor Q\equiv Q\lor P`                       | commutativity of disjunction, commutativité de la disjonction, 析取交换律                   | The order of disjunction does not affect truth value.                                              | EN: `P\lor Q` is equivalent to `Q\lor P`.<br>FR: `P\lor Q` est équivalent à `Q\lor P`.<br>中：`P\lor Q` 等价于 `Q\lor P`。                                                                                      |
| `P\land(P\lor Q)\equiv P`                     | absorption law, loi d’absorption, 吸收律                                                  | A logical law where a stronger component absorbs a weaker disjunction.                             | EN: `P\land(P\lor Q)` simplifies to `P`.<br>FR: `P\land(P\lor Q)` se simplifie en `P`.<br>中：`P\land(P\lor Q)` 可化简为 `P`。                                                                                   |
| `P\lor(P\land Q)\equiv P`                     | dual absorption law, loi d’absorption duale, 对偶吸收律                                     | A logical law where a proposition absorbs a stronger conjunction.                                  | EN: `P\lor(P\land Q)` simplifies to `P`.<br>FR: `P\lor(P\land Q)` se simplifie en `P`.<br>中：`P\lor(P\land Q)` 可化简为 `P`。                                                                                   |
| `P\Rightarrow Q\equiv\neg Q\Rightarrow\neg P` | contrapositive equivalence, équivalence contraposée, 逆否等价                              | An implication is logically equivalent to its contrapositive.                                      | EN: “If `n^2` is even, then `n` is even” is often proved by contrapositive.<br>FR: “Si `n^2` est pair, alors `n` est pair” se prouve souvent par contraposée.<br>中：“若 `n^2` 为偶数，则 `n` 为偶数”常用逆否命题证明。       |
| `P\Rightarrow Q\not\equiv Q\Rightarrow P`     | converse is not equivalent, réciproque non équivalente, 逆命题不等价                         | An implication is generally not equivalent to its converse.                                        | EN: `x>2\Rightarrow x>1` is true, but `x>1\Rightarrow x>2` is false.<br>FR: `x>2\Rightarrow x>1` est vraie, mais `x>1\Rightarrow x>2` est fausse.<br>中：`x>2\Rightarrow x>1` 为真，但 `x>1\Rightarrow x>2` 为假。 |

## 19.3 Predicates, quantifiers, and formal statements

| Symbol (LaTeX)                                  | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                            | Definition                                                              | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| ----------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `P(x)`                                          | predicate, prédicat, 谓词                                                      | A statement form whose truth value depends on one or more variables.    | EN: `P(x)` may mean “`x` is prime.”<br>FR: `P(x)` peut signifier “`x` est premier.”<br>中：`P(x)` 可以表示“`x` 是质数”。                                                                                                                                                                            |
| `\forall x`                                     | universal quantifier, quantificateur universel, 全称量词                         | A quantifier meaning “for every `x`.”                                   | EN: `\forall x\in\mathbb{R}, x^2\geq0`.<br>FR: `\forall x\in\mathbb{R}, x^2\geq0`.<br>中：`\forall x\in\mathbb{R}, x^2\geq0` 表示每个实数平方非负。                                                                                                                                                    |
| `\exists x`                                     | existential quantifier, quantificateur existentiel, 存在量词                     | A quantifier meaning “there exists at least one `x`.”                   | EN: `\exists x\in\mathbb{R}, x^2=4`.<br>FR: `\exists x\in\mathbb{R}, x^2=4`.<br>中：`\exists x\in\mathbb{R}, x^2=4` 表示存在实数平方等于 `4`。                                                                                                                                                         |
| `\exists! x`                                    | unique existence quantifier, quantificateur d’existence unique, 唯一存在量词       | A quantifier meaning “there exists exactly one `x`.”                    | EN: `\exists! x\in\mathbb{R}, x+1=0`.<br>FR: `\exists! x\in\mathbb{R}, x+1=0`.<br>中：`\exists! x\in\mathbb{R}, x+1=0` 表示唯一存在一个实数满足方程。                                                                                                                                                      |
| `\forall x\in A`                                | bounded universal quantifier, quantificateur universel borné, 有界全称量词         | A universal quantifier restricted to a specified set.                   | EN: `\forall x\in[0,1], x\leq1`.<br>FR: `\forall x\in[0,1], x\leq1`.<br>中：`\forall x\in[0,1], x\leq1` 表示区间内每个数都不超过 `1`。                                                                                                                                                                   |
| `\exists x\in A`                                | bounded existential quantifier, quantificateur existentiel borné, 有界存在量词     | An existential quantifier restricted to a specified set.                | EN: `\exists x\in\mathbb{Z}, x^2=9`.<br>FR: `\exists x\in\mathbb{Z}, x^2=9`.<br>中：`\exists x\in\mathbb{Z}, x^2=9` 表示存在整数平方等于 `9`。                                                                                                                                                         |
| `\neg\forall x\,P(x)\equiv\exists x\,\neg P(x)` | negation of universal statement, négation d’un énoncé universel, 全称命题的否定     | The denial of “all `x` satisfy `P`” is “some `x` does not satisfy `P`.” | EN: The negation of “all numbers are positive” is “some number is not positive.”<br>FR: La négation de “tous les nombres sont positifs” est “un nombre n’est pas positif.”<br>中：“所有数都是正数”的否定是“存在某个数不是正数”。                                                                                 |
| `\neg\exists x\,P(x)\equiv\forall x\,\neg P(x)` | negation of existential statement, négation d’un énoncé existentiel, 存在命题的否定 | The denial of “some `x` satisfies `P`” is “no `x` satisfies `P`.”       | EN: The negation of “some integer is between `0` and `1`” is “no integer is between `0` and `1`.”<br>FR: La négation de “un entier est entre `0` et `1`” est “aucun entier n’est entre `0` et `1`.”<br>中：“存在整数在 `0` 与 `1` 之间”的否定是“没有整数在 `0` 与 `1` 之间”。                                    |
| `\forall x\,\exists y`                          | nested quantifiers, quantificateurs imbriqués, 嵌套量词                          | Multiple quantifiers whose order affects meaning.                       | EN: `\forall x\in\mathbb{R}\,\exists y\in\mathbb{R}, y>x` says there is always a larger real number.<br>FR: `\forall x\in\mathbb{R}\,\exists y\in\mathbb{R}, y>x` dit qu’il existe toujours un réel plus grand.<br>中：`\forall x\in\mathbb{R}\,\exists y\in\mathbb{R}, y>x` 表示任意实数都有更大的实数。 |
| `\exists y\,\forall x`                          | changed quantifier order, ordre des quantificateurs modifié, 改变量词顺序          | Reversing quantifiers often changes the truth conditions.               | EN: `\exists y\,\forall x, y>x` is false over `\mathbb{R}`.<br>FR: `\exists y\,\forall x, y>x` est faux sur `\mathbb{R}`.<br>中：在 `\mathbb{R}` 上，`\exists y\,\forall x, y>x` 为假。                                                                                                           |
| `x` free                                        | free variable, variable libre, 自由变量                                          | A variable not bound by a quantifier.                                   | EN: In `x>0`, the variable `x` is free.<br>FR: Dans `x>0`, la variable `x` est libre.<br>中：在 `x>0` 中，变量 `x` 是自由变量。                                                                                                                                                                        |
| `x` bound                                       | bound variable, variable liée, 约束变量                                          | A variable governed by a quantifier.                                    | EN: In `\forall x, x^2\geq0`, the variable `x` is bound.<br>FR: Dans `\forall x, x^2\geq0`, la variable `x` est liée.<br>中：在 `\forall x, x^2\geq0` 中，变量 `x` 是约束变量。                                                                                                                        |

## 19.4 Proof methods and argument patterns

| Symbol (LaTeX) | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                       | Definition                                                                                        | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| -------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| —              | direct proof, preuve directe, 直接证明                                      | A proof that starts from assumptions and derives the conclusion straightforwardly.                | EN: To prove even plus even is even, write `2a+2b=2(a+b)`.<br>FR: Pour prouver que pair plus pair est pair, écrire `2a+2b=2(a+b)`.<br>中：证明偶数加偶数仍为偶数，可写 `2a+2b=2(a+b)`。                                                                                  |
| —              | proof by contrapositive, preuve par contraposée, 逆否证明                   | A proof of `P\Rightarrow Q` by proving `\neg Q\Rightarrow\neg P`.                                 | EN: To prove `n^2` even implies `n` even, prove if `n` is odd then `n^2` is odd.<br>FR: Pour prouver que `n^2` pair implique `n` pair, prouver que `n` impair implique `n^2` impair.<br>中：证明 `n^2` 为偶数推出 `n` 为偶数，可证明 `n` 为奇数推出 `n^2` 为奇数。               |
| —              | proof by contradiction, preuve par l’absurde, 反证法                       | A proof that assumes the negation of the desired conclusion and derives a contradiction.          | EN: The irrationality of `\sqrt{2}` is commonly proved by contradiction.<br>FR: L’irrationalité de `\sqrt{2}` se prouve souvent par l’absurde.<br>中：`\sqrt{2}` 的无理性常用反证法证明。                                                                             |
| —              | proof by cases, preuve par cas, 分类讨论证明                                  | A proof that splits the argument into exhaustive cases.                                           | EN: To prove a statement about integers, separate even and odd cases.<br>FR: Pour prouver un énoncé sur les entiers, séparer les cas pair et impair.<br>中：证明整数命题时，可分偶数和奇数两类讨论。                                                                          |
| —              | proof by exhaustion, preuve par épuisement, 穷举证明                        | A proof checking every case in a finite list.                                                     | EN: A property of all elements of `{1,2,3}` can be proved by checking all three.<br>FR: Une propriété de tous les éléments de `{1,2,3}` se prouve en vérifiant les trois cas.<br>中：关于 `{1,2,3}` 中所有元素的性质可通过逐一检查三种情况证明。                                  |
| —              | counterexample, contre-exemple, 反例                                      | An example showing that a universal statement is false.                                           | EN: `x=0` is a counterexample to “all real numbers are positive.”<br>FR: `x=0` est un contre-exemple à “tous les réels sont positifs.”<br>中：`x=0` 是“所有实数都是正数”的反例。                                                                                       |
