| —              | constructive proof, preuve constructive, 构造性证明                          | A proof of existence that explicitly constructs an object.                                        | EN: To prove an even prime exists, give the example `2`.<br>FR: Pour prouver qu’un nombre premier pair existe, donner l’exemple `2`.<br>中：证明存在偶质数，可直接给出例子 `2`。                                                                                          |
| —              | nonconstructive proof, preuve non constructive, 非构造性证明                  | A proof of existence without explicitly producing the object.                                     | EN: Some existence proofs use contradiction without locating the object.<br>FR: Certaines preuves d’existence utilisent l’absurde sans localiser l’objet.<br>中：某些存在性证明用反证法，但不明确给出对象。                                                                    |
| —              | induction, récurrence, 数学归纳法                                            | A proof method for statements indexed by natural numbers using a base case and an induction step. | EN: Prove `1+\cdots+n=\frac{n(n+1)}{2}` by induction on `n`.<br>FR: Prouver `1+\cdots+n=\frac{n(n+1)}{2}` par récurrence sur `n`.<br>中：可对 `n` 归纳证明 `1+\cdots+n=\frac{n(n+1)}{2}`。                                                                       |
| —              | strong induction, récurrence forte, 强归纳法                                | An induction method where the induction step may use all previous cases.                          | EN: Strong induction is useful for proving every integer greater than `1` factors into primes.<br>FR: La récurrence forte est utile pour prouver que tout entier supérieur à `1` se factorise en nombres premiers.<br>中：证明每个大于 `1` 的整数都可分解为素数时，强归纳法很有用。 |
| —              | minimal counterexample method, méthode du contre-exemple minimal, 最小反例法 | A proof method assuming a smallest counterexample exists and deriving a contradiction.            | EN: Many divisibility claims can be proved by ruling out the smallest counterexample.<br>FR: Beaucoup d’énoncés de divisibilité se prouvent en excluant le plus petit contre-exemple.<br>中：许多整除命题可通过排除最小反例来证明。                                          |
| —              | invariant argument, argument par invariant, 不变量论证                       | A proof using a quantity or property that remains unchanged under allowed operations.             | EN: Parity is often used as an invariant in puzzle proofs.<br>FR: La parité sert souvent d’invariant dans les preuves de casse-tête.<br>中：奇偶性常作为谜题证明中的不变量。                                                                                              |

## 19.5 Sets, classes, and membership

| Symbol (LaTeX)    | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                      | Definition                                                                     | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
| ----------------- | ------------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `x\in A`          | membership, appartenance, 属于                           | A relation meaning that `x` is an element of set `A`.                          | EN: `3\in\{1,2,3\}`.<br>FR: `3\in\{1,2,3\}`.<br>中：`3\in\{1,2,3\}`。                                                                                                                                                                   |
| `x\notin A`       | non-membership, non-appartenance, 不属于                  | A relation meaning that `x` is not an element of set `A`.                      | EN: `4\notin\{1,2,3\}`.<br>FR: `4\notin\{1,2,3\}`.<br>中：`4\notin\{1,2,3\}`。                                                                                                                                                          |
| `A\subseteq B`    | subset, sous-ensemble, 子集                              | Every element of `A` is also an element of `B`.                                | EN: `\{1,2\}\subseteq\{1,2,3\}`.<br>FR: `\{1,2\}\subseteq\{1,2,3\}`.<br>中：`\{1,2\}\subseteq\{1,2,3\}`。                                                                                                                               |
| `A\subset B`      | proper subset, sous-ensemble propre, 真子集               | `A` is a subset of `B` and is not equal to `B`.                                | EN: `\{1\}\subset\{1,2\}`.<br>FR: `\{1\}\subset\{1,2\}`.<br>中：`\{1\}\subset\{1,2\}`。                                                                                                                                                 |
| `A=B`             | equality of sets, égalité d’ensembles, 集合相等            | Two sets are equal if they have exactly the same elements.                     | EN: `A=B` means `A\subseteq B` and `B\subseteq A`.<br>FR: `A=B` signifie `A\subseteq B` et `B\subseteq A`.<br>中：`A=B` 表示 `A\subseteq B` 且 `B\subseteq A`。                                                                            |
| `\varnothing`     | empty set, ensemble vide, 空集                           | The set containing no elements.                                                | EN: `\varnothing\subseteq A` for every set `A`.<br>FR: `\varnothing\subseteq A` pour tout ensemble `A`.<br>中：对任意集合 `A`，都有 `\varnothing\subseteq A`。                                                                                  |
| `\{x\}`           | singleton, singleton, 单元素集                             | A set containing exactly one element.                                          | EN: `\{0\}` is not the same object as `0`.<br>FR: `\{0\}` n’est pas le même objet que `0`.<br>中：`\{0\}` 与 `0` 不是同一个对象。                                                                                                               |
| `\{x,y\}`         | unordered pair, paire non ordonnée, 无序对                | A set containing `x` and `y`, without order.                                   | EN: `\{1,2\}=\{2,1\}`.<br>FR: `\{1,2\}=\{2,1\}`.<br>中：`\{1,2\}=\{2,1\}`。                                                                                                                                                             |
| `(x,y)`           | ordered pair, paire ordonnée, 有序对                      | A pair where order matters.                                                    | EN: `(1,2)\neq(2,1)`.<br>FR: `(1,2)\neq(2,1)`.<br>中：`(1,2)\neq(2,1)`。                                                                                                                                                                |
| `\{x\in A:P(x)\}` | set-builder notation, notation en compréhension, 集合构造式 | A notation for the subset of elements of `A` satisfying a predicate `P`.       | EN: `\{x\in\mathbb{Z}:x\text{ is even}\}` is the set of even integers.<br>FR: `\{x\in\mathbb{Z}:x\text{ est pair}\}` est l’ensemble des entiers pairs.<br>中：`\{x\in\mathbb{Z}:x\text{ 是偶数}\}` 是偶整数集合。                                |
| `\mathcal{C}`     | class, classe, 类                                       | A collection of objects that may be too large to be a set in some foundations. | EN: The collection of all sets is treated as a proper class in standard set theory.<br>FR: La collection de tous les ensembles est traitée comme une classe propre dans la théorie usuelle des ensembles.<br>中：在标准集合论中，所有集合的总体被视为真类。 |
| —                 | proper class, classe propre, 真类                        | A class that is not a set.                                                     | EN: The class of all ordinals is a proper class.<br>FR: La classe de tous les ordinaux est une classe propre.<br>中：所有序数构成的类是真类。                                                                                                      |

## 19.6 Set operations and algebra of sets

| Symbol (LaTeX)        | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                | Definition                                                          | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |
| --------------------- | ------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `A\cup B`             | union, union / réunion, 并集                       | The set of elements belonging to `A`, `B`, or both.                 | EN: `\{1,2\}\cup\{2,3\}=\{1,2,3\}`.<br>FR: `\{1,2\}\cup\{2,3\}=\{1,2,3\}`.<br>中：`\{1,2\}\cup\{2,3\}=\{1,2,3\}`。                                                                                                                              |
| `A\cap B`             | intersection, intersection, 交集                   | The set of elements belonging to both `A` and `B`.                  | EN: `\{1,2\}\cap\{2,3\}=\{2\}`.<br>FR: `\{1,2\}\cap\{2,3\}=\{2\}`.<br>中：`\{1,2\}\cap\{2,3\}=\{2\}`。                                                                                                                                          |
| `A\setminus B`        | set difference, différence d’ensembles, 差集       | The set of elements in `A` that are not in `B`.                     | EN: `\{1,2,3\}\setminus\{2\}=\{1,3\}`.<br>FR: `\{1,2,3\}\setminus\{2\}=\{1,3\}`.<br>中：`\{1,2,3\}\setminus\{2\}=\{1,3\}`。                                                                                                                     |
| `A^c`                 | complement, complémentaire, 补集                   | The set of elements in the ambient universe that are not in `A`.    | EN: If the universe is `\mathbb{R}`, then `(\mathbb{Q})^c` is the set of irrational numbers.<br>FR: Si l’univers est `\mathbb{R}`, alors `(\mathbb{Q})^c` est l’ensemble des irrationnels.<br>中：若全集是 `\mathbb{R}`，则 `(\mathbb{Q})^c` 是无理数集合。 |
| `A\triangle B`        | symmetric difference, différence symétrique, 对称差 | The set of elements belonging to exactly one of `A` and `B`.        | EN: `\{1,2\}\triangle\{2,3\}=\{1,3\}`.<br>FR: `\{1,2\}\triangle\{2,3\}=\{1,3\}`.<br>中：`\{1,2\}\triangle\{2,3\}=\{1,3\}`。                                                                                                                     |
| `A\times B`           | Cartesian product, produit cartésien, 笛卡尔积       | The set of ordered pairs `(a,b)` with `a\in A` and `b\in B`.        | EN: If `A=\{1,2\}` and `B=\{x\}`, then `A\times B=\{(1,x),(2,x)\}`.<br>FR: Si `A=\{1,2\}` et `B=\{x\}`, alors `A\times B=\{(1,x),(2,x)\}`.<br>中：若 `A=\{1,2\}` 且 `B=\{x\}`，则 `A\times B=\{(1,x),(2,x)\}`。                                     |
| `\mathcal{P}(A)`      | power set, ensemble des parties, 幂集              | The set of all subsets of `A`.                                      | EN: If `A=\{1,2\}`, then `\mathcal{P}(A)=\{\varnothing,\{1\},\{2\},\{1,2\}\}`.<br>FR: Si `A=\{1,2\}`, alors `\mathcal{P}(A)=\{\varnothing,\{1\},\{2\},\{1,2\}\}`.<br>中：若 `A=\{1,2\}`，则 `\mathcal{P}(A)=\{\varnothing,\{1\},\{2\},\{1,2\}\}`。 |
| `\bigcup_{i\in I}A_i` | indexed union, réunion indexée, 指标并              | The union of a family of sets indexed by `I`.                       | EN: `\bigcup_{n\geq1}(0,1-\frac{1}{n})=(0,1)`.<br>FR: `\bigcup_{n\geq1}(0,1-\frac{1}{n})=(0,1)`.<br>中：`\bigcup_{n\geq1}(0,1-\frac{1}{n})=(0,1)`。                                                                                             |
| `\bigcap_{i\in I}A_i` | indexed intersection, intersection indexée, 指标交  | The intersection of a family of sets indexed by `I`.                | EN: `\bigcap_{n\geq1}(-\frac{1}{n},\frac{1}{n})=\{0\}`.<br>FR: `\bigcap_{n\geq1}(-\frac{1}{n},\frac{1}{n})=\{0\}`.<br>中：`\bigcap_{n\geq1}(-\frac{1}{n},\frac{1}{n})=\{0\}`。                                                                  |
| `A\cap B=\varnothing` | disjoint sets, ensembles disjoints, 不交集合         | Sets with no common elements.                                       | EN: `{1,2}` and `{3,4}` are disjoint.<br>FR: `{1,2}` et `{3,4}` sont disjoints.<br>中：`{1,2}` 与 `{3,4}` 不相交。                                                                                                                                  |
| `\{A_i\}_{i\in I}`    | family of sets, famille d’ensembles, 集合族         | A collection of sets indexed by another set.                        | EN: Intervals `(n,n+1)` form a family indexed by integers.<br>FR: Les intervalles `(n,n+1)` forment une famille indexée par les entiers.<br>中：区间 `(n,n+1)` 构成由整数指标化的集合族。                                                                     |
| —                     | partition, partition, 划分                         | A family of nonempty disjoint subsets whose union is the whole set. | EN: Even and odd integers form a partition of `\mathbb{Z}`.<br>FR: Les entiers pairs et impairs forment une partition de `\mathbb{Z}`.<br>中：偶整数和奇整数构成 `\mathbb{Z}` 的划分。                                                                      |

## 19.7 Functions, relations, and equivalence structures

| Symbol (LaTeX)         | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                         | Definition                                                         | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| ---------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------ | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `f:A\to B`             | function, fonction, 函数                                                    | A rule assigning each element of `A` exactly one element of `B`.   | EN: `f(x)=x^2` defines a function from `\mathbb{R}` to `\mathbb{R}`.<br>FR: `f(x)=x^2` définit une fonction de `\mathbb{R}` vers `\mathbb{R}`.<br>中：`f(x)=x^2` 定义了从 `\mathbb{R}` 到 `\mathbb{R}` 的函数。            |
| `A`                    | domain, domaine, 定义域                                                      | The set of inputs of a function.                                   | EN: For `f:A\to B`, the domain is `A`.<br>FR: Pour `f:A\to B`, le domaine est `A`.<br>中：对 `f:A\to B`，定义域是 `A`。                                                                                                  |
| `B`                    | codomain, codomaine, 陪域                                                   | The target set in which function values are required to lie.       | EN: For `f:A\to B`, the codomain is `B`.<br>FR: Pour `f:A\to B`, le codomaine est `B`.<br>中：对 `f:A\to B`，陪域是 `B`。                                                                                               |
| `f(A)`                 | image, image, 像                                                           | The set of actual output values of a function.                     | EN: If `f(x)=x^2` on `\mathbb{R}`, then `f(\mathbb{R})=[0,\infty)`.<br>FR: Si `f(x)=x^2` sur `\mathbb{R}`, alors `f(\mathbb{R})=[0,\infty)`.<br>中：若 `f(x)=x^2` 定义在 `\mathbb{R}` 上，则 `f(\mathbb{R})=[0,\infty)`。 |
| `f^{-1}(S)`            | preimage, image réciproque, 原像                                            | The set of inputs mapped into subset `S` of the codomain.          | EN: For `f(x)=x^2`, `f^{-1}(\{4\})=\{-2,2\}`.<br>FR: Pour `f(x)=x^2`, `f^{-1}(\{4\})=\{-2,2\}`.<br>中：对 `f(x)=x^2`，`f^{-1}(\{4\})=\{-2,2\}`。                                                                     |
| —                      | injective function, fonction injective, 单射                                | A function sending distinct inputs to distinct outputs.            | EN: `f(x)=2x` is injective on `\mathbb{R}`.<br>FR: `f(x)=2x` est injective sur `\mathbb{R}`.<br>中：`f(x)=2x` 在 `\mathbb{R}` 上是单射。                                                                                |
| —                      | surjective function, fonction surjective, 满射                              | A function whose image equals its codomain.                        | EN: `f:\mathbb{R}\to[0,\infty)`, `f(x)=x^2`, is surjective.<br>FR: `f:\mathbb{R}\to[0,\infty)`, `f(x)=x^2`, est surjective.<br>中：`f:\mathbb{R}\to[0,\infty)`，`f(x)=x^2` 是满射。                                    |
| —                      | bijective function, fonction bijective, 双射                                | A function that is both injective and surjective.                  | EN: `f(x)=x+1` is a bijection from `\mathbb{R}` to `\mathbb{R}`.<br>FR: `f(x)=x+1` est une bijection de `\mathbb{R}` vers `\mathbb{R}`.<br>中：`f(x)=x+1` 是从 `\mathbb{R}` 到 `\mathbb{R}` 的双射。                     |
| `g\circ f`             | composition, composition, 复合                                              | The function obtained by applying `f` first and then `g`.          | EN: If `f(x)=x+1` and `g(x)=x^2`, then `(g\circ f)(x)=(x+1)^2`.<br>FR: Si `f(x)=x+1` et `g(x)=x^2`, alors `(g\circ f)(x)=(x+1)^2`.<br>中：若 `f(x)=x+1` 且 `g(x)=x^2`，则 `(g\circ f)(x)=(x+1)^2`。                    |
| `f^{-1}`               | inverse function, fonction inverse, 反函数                                   | The function reversing a bijection.                                | EN: If `f(x)=x+1`, then `f^{-1}(x)=x-1`.<br>FR: Si `f(x)=x+1`, alors `f^{-1}(x)=x-1`.<br>中：若 `f(x)=x+1`，则 `f^{-1}(x)=x-1`。                                                                                      |
| `R\subseteq A\times B` | relation, relation, 关系                                                    | A subset of a Cartesian product connecting elements of two sets.   | EN: The relation `<` is a subset of `\mathbb{R}\times\mathbb{R}`.<br>FR: La relation `<` est un sous-ensemble de `\mathbb{R}\times\mathbb{R}`.<br>中：关系 `<` 是 `\mathbb{R}\times\mathbb{R}` 的子集。                  |
| `a\sim b`              | equivalence relation notation, notation de relation d’équivalence, 等价关系记号 | A notation meaning that `a` is equivalent to `b` under a relation. | EN: Modulo `3`, `7\sim1` because `7\equiv1\pmod3`.<br>FR: Modulo `3`, `7\sim1` car `7\equiv1\pmod3`.<br>中：模 `3` 下，`7\sim1`，因为 `7\equiv1\pmod3`。                                                                 |
| `A/\sim`               | quotient set, ensemble quotient, 商集                                       | The set of equivalence classes under an equivalence relation.      | EN: Integers modulo `n` form the quotient set `\mathbb{Z}/n\mathbb{Z}`.<br>FR: Les entiers modulo `n` forment l’ensemble quotient `\mathbb{Z}/n\mathbb{Z}`.<br>中：模 `n` 整数构成商集 `\mathbb{Z}/n\mathbb{Z}`。         |

## 19.8 Cardinality, countability, and infinity

| Symbol (LaTeX)                              | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                            | Definition                                                                                              | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
| ------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `\lvert A\rvert`                            | cardinality, cardinalité, 基数 / 势                                             | The size of a set, generalized to finite and infinite sets.                                             | EN: `\lvert\{a,b,c\}\rvert=3`.<br>FR: `\lvert\{a,b,c\}\rvert=3`.<br>中：`\lvert\{a,b,c\}\rvert=3`。                                                                                                                     |
| `A\sim B`                                   | equinumerous sets, ensembles équipotents, 等势集合                               | Sets having a bijection between them.                                                                   | EN: `\mathbb{N}` and the even positive integers are equinumerous.<br>FR: `\mathbb{N}` et les entiers positifs pairs sont équipotents.<br>中：`\mathbb{N}` 与正偶数集合等势。                                                    |
| `\lvert A\rvert\leq\lvert B\rvert`          | cardinal inequality, inégalité cardinale, 基数不等式                              | There exists an injection from `A` to `B`.                                                              | EN: `\lvert\mathbb{N}\rvert\leq\lvert\mathbb{R}\rvert`.<br>FR: `\lvert\mathbb{N}\rvert\leq\lvert\mathbb{R}\rvert`.<br>中：`\lvert\mathbb{N}\rvert\leq\lvert\mathbb{R}\rvert`。                                          |
| —                                           | finite set, ensemble fini, 有限集                                               | A set with exactly `n` elements for some natural number `n`.                                            | EN: `{1,2,3}` is finite.<br>FR: `{1,2,3}` est fini.<br>中：`{1,2,3}` 是有限集。                                                                                                                                             |
| —                                           | infinite set, ensemble infini, 无限集                                           | A set that is not finite.                                                                               | EN: `\mathbb{N}` is infinite.<br>FR: `\mathbb{N}` est infini.<br>中：`\mathbb{N}` 是无限集。                                                                                                                                |
| —                                           | countable set, ensemble dénombrable, 可数集                                     | A finite set or a set in bijection with a subset of `\mathbb{N}`.                                       | EN: The integers `\mathbb{Z}` are countable.<br>FR: Les entiers `\mathbb{Z}` sont dénombrables.<br>中：整数集 `\mathbb{Z}` 是可数集。                                                                                          |
| —                                           | countably infinite set, ensemble infini dénombrable, 可数无限集                   | A set in bijection with `\mathbb{N}`.                                                                   | EN: The rational numbers `\mathbb{Q}` are countably infinite.<br>FR: Les rationnels `\mathbb{Q}` sont infinis dénombrables.<br>中：有理数集 `\mathbb{Q}` 是可数无限集。                                                           |
| —                                           | uncountable set, ensemble non dénombrable, 不可数集                              | A set not in bijection with any subset of `\mathbb{N}`.                                                 | EN: The real numbers `\mathbb{R}` are uncountable.<br>FR: Les réels `\mathbb{R}` sont non dénombrables.<br>中：实数集 `\mathbb{R}` 不可数。                                                                                   |
| `\aleph_0`                                  | aleph-null, aleph-zéro, 阿列夫零                                                 | The cardinality of the natural numbers.                                                                 | EN: `\lvert\mathbb{N}\rvert=\aleph_0`.<br>FR: `\lvert\mathbb{N}\rvert=\aleph_0`.<br>中：`\lvert\mathbb{N}\rvert=\aleph_0`。                                                                                             |
| `\mathfrak{c}`                              | cardinality of the continuum, cardinal du continu, 连续统基数                     | The cardinality of the real numbers.                                                                    | EN: `\lvert\mathbb{R}\rvert=\mathfrak{c}`.<br>FR: `\lvert\mathbb{R}\rvert=\mathfrak{c}`.<br>中：`\lvert\mathbb{R}\rvert=\mathfrak{c}`。                                                                                 |
| `2^{\aleph_0}`                              | continuum as power set size, cardinal du continu comme puissance, 作为幂集大小的连续统 | The cardinality of the power set of the natural numbers, equal to the cardinality of the reals.         | EN: `\lvert\mathcal{P}(\mathbb{N})\rvert=2^{\aleph_0}`.<br>FR: `\lvert\mathcal{P}(\mathbb{N})\rvert=2^{\aleph_0}`.<br>中：`\lvert\mathcal{P}(\mathbb{N})\rvert=2^{\aleph_0}`。                                          |
| —                                           | Cantor diagonal argument, argument diagonal de Cantor, Cantor 对角线论证          | A method showing that certain sets, such as real numbers or infinite binary sequences, are uncountable. | EN: Cantor’s argument proves that binary sequences cannot be listed completely.<br>FR: L’argument de Cantor prouve que les suites binaires ne peuvent pas être entièrement listées.<br>中：Cantor 对角线论证证明二进制序列不能被完整列举。 |
| `\lvert A\rvert<\lvert\mathcal{P}(A)\rvert` | Cantor’s theorem, théorème de Cantor, Cantor 定理                              | Every set has strictly smaller cardinality than its power set.                                          | EN: There is no surjection from `A` onto `\mathcal{P}(A)`.<br>FR: Il n’existe pas de surjection de `A` vers `\mathcal{P}(A)`.<br>中：不存在从 `A` 到 `\mathcal{P}(A)` 的满射。                                                  |

## 19.9 Ordinals, well-orders, and transfinite methods

| Symbol (LaTeX)            | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                       | Definition                                                                             | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| ------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| —                         | well-order, bon ordre, 良序                               | A total order in which every nonempty subset has a least element.                      | EN: The usual order on `\mathbb{N}` is a well-order.<br>FR: L’ordre usuel sur `\mathbb{N}` est un bon ordre.<br>中：`\mathbb{N}` 上的通常顺序是良序。                                                                                                                                  |
| —                         | least element, plus petit élément, 最小元                  | An element less than or equal to every element in a set.                               | EN: The least element of `{3,5,7}` is `3`.<br>FR: Le plus petit élément de `{3,5,7}` est `3`.<br>中：`{3,5,7}` 的最小元是 `3`。                                                                                                                                                    |
| —                         | minimal element, élément minimal, 极小元                   | An element with no strictly smaller element in a partially ordered set.                | EN: A partial order can have several minimal elements.<br>FR: Un ordre partiel peut avoir plusieurs éléments minimaux.<br>中：偏序集可以有多个极小元。                                                                                                                                   |
| `\alpha,\beta`            | ordinal, ordinal, 序数                                    | An object representing the order type of a well-ordered set.                           | EN: Finite ordinals correspond to `0,1,2,\ldots`.<br>FR: Les ordinaux finis correspondent à `0,1,2,\ldots`.<br>中：有限序数对应 `0,1,2,\ldots`。                                                                                                                                    |
| `\omega`                  | first infinite ordinal, premier ordinal infini, 第一个无限序数 | The order type of the natural numbers in their usual order.                            | EN: `\omega` comes after all finite ordinals.<br>FR: `\omega` vient après tous les ordinaux finis.<br>中：`\omega` 位于所有有限序数之后。                                                                                                                                               |
| `\alpha+1`                | successor ordinal, ordinal successeur, 后继序数             | The ordinal immediately after `\alpha`.                                                | EN: `3+1=4` is a finite successor ordinal.<br>FR: `3+1=4` est un ordinal successeur fini.<br>中：`3+1=4` 是有限后继序数。                                                                                                                                                            |
| —                         | limit ordinal, ordinal limite, 极限序数                     | A nonzero ordinal that is not a successor ordinal.                                     | EN: `\omega` is a limit ordinal.<br>FR: `\omega` est un ordinal limite.<br>中：`\omega` 是极限序数。                                                                                                                                                                               |
| —                         | transfinite induction, récurrence transfinie, 超限归纳法     | An induction principle extending ordinary induction to well-ordered classes.           | EN: To prove a property for all ordinals, prove it assuming it holds for all smaller ordinals.<br>FR: Pour prouver une propriété pour tous les ordinaux, la prouver en supposant qu’elle vaut pour tous les ordinaux plus petits.<br>中：要证明所有序数满足某性质，可假设所有更小序数满足该性质后证明当前序数。 |
| —                         | transfinite recursion, récursion transfinie, 超限递归       | A method defining objects along ordinals using earlier values.                         | EN: Many hierarchies in set theory are defined by transfinite recursion.<br>FR: Beaucoup de hiérarchies en théorie des ensembles sont définies par récursion transfinie.<br>中：集合论中的许多层级由超限递归定义。                                                                            |
| `\operatorname{otp}(X,<)` | order type, type d’ordre, 序型                            | The abstract order represented by an ordered set.                                      | EN: The order type of the natural numbers is `\omega`.<br>FR: Le type d’ordre des entiers naturels est `\omega`.<br>中：自然数通常顺序的序型是 `\omega`。                                                                                                                                |
| —                         | well-ordering theorem, théorème du bon ordre, 良序定理      | The statement that every set can be well-ordered.                                      | EN: The well-ordering theorem is equivalent to the axiom of choice in standard set theory.<br>FR: Le théorème du bon ordre est équivalent à l’axiome du choix dans la théorie usuelle des ensembles.<br>中：在标准集合论中，良序定理与选择公理等价。                                             |
| —                         | ordinal arithmetic, arithmétique ordinale, 序数算术         | Addition, multiplication, and exponentiation defined for ordinals, sensitive to order. | EN: Ordinal addition is not generally commutative.<br>FR: L’addition ordinale n’est généralement pas commutative.<br>中：序数加法通常不可交换。                                                                                                                                         |

## 19.10 Axiomatic set theory and choice principles

| Symbol (LaTeX) | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                              | Definition                                                                                          | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| -------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `ZF`           | Zermelo-Fraenkel set theory, théorie des ensembles de Zermelo-Fraenkel, ZF 集合论 | A standard axiomatic foundation for set theory without the axiom of choice.                         | EN: `ZF` formalizes ordinary set construction through axioms.<br>FR: `ZF` formalise la construction ordinaire des ensembles par des axiomes.<br>中：`ZF` 通过公理形式化通常的集合构造。                                  |
| `ZFC`          | Zermelo-Fraenkel set theory with Choice, théorie ZF avec choix, ZFC 集合论        | Zermelo-Fraenkel set theory together with the axiom of choice.                                      | EN: Most standard mathematics is developed inside `ZFC`.<br>FR: La plupart des mathématiques usuelles se développent dans `ZFC`.<br>中：大多数标准数学可在 `ZFC` 中展开。                                              |
| —              | axiom of extensionality, axiome d’extensionnalité, 外延公理                        | Sets with the same elements are equal.                                                              | EN: If two sets contain exactly the same elements, they are the same set.<br>FR: Si deux ensembles contiennent exactement les mêmes éléments, ils sont le même ensemble.<br>中：若两个集合含有完全相同的元素，则它们相等。     |
| —              | axiom of pairing, axiome de la paire, 配对公理                                     | For any two objects, there exists a set containing exactly them as elements.                        | EN: Pairing guarantees the existence of `{a,b}`.<br>FR: L’axiome de la paire garantit l’existence de `{a,b}`.<br>中：配对公理保证 `{a,b}` 存在。                                                                   |
| —              | axiom of union, axiome de l’union, 并集公理                                        | For a set of sets, there exists a set containing all elements of those sets.                        | EN: The union axiom justifies `\bigcup A`.<br>FR: L’axiome de l’union justifie `\bigcup A`.<br>中：并集公理保证 `\bigcup A` 的存在。                                                                                |
| —              | axiom schema of separation, schéma d’axiomes de séparation, 分离公理模式             | Subsets defined by a property can be formed from an already existing set.                           | EN: Separation allows `{x\in A:P(x)}`.<br>FR: La séparation permet `{x\in A:P(x)}`.<br>中：分离公理模式允许构造 `{x\in A:P(x)}`。                                                                                    |
| —              | axiom schema of replacement, schéma d’axiomes de remplacement, 替换公理模式          | The image of a set under a definable functional rule is a set.                                      | EN: Replacement supports transfinite recursive constructions.<br>FR: Le remplacement soutient les constructions par récursion transfinie.<br>中：替换公理模式支撑超限递归构造。                                          |
| —              | axiom of infinity, axiome de l’infini, 无穷公理                                    | An axiom asserting the existence of an infinite set.                                                | EN: The axiom of infinity permits construction of `\mathbb{N}`.<br>FR: L’axiome de l’infini permet de construire `\mathbb{N}`.<br>中：无穷公理允许构造 `\mathbb{N}`。                                              |
| —              | axiom of power set, axiome de l’ensemble des parties, 幂集公理                     | For every set, its power set exists.                                                                | EN: The power set axiom gives `\mathcal{P}(A)` for any set `A`.<br>FR: L’axiome de l’ensemble des parties donne `\mathcal{P}(A)` pour tout ensemble `A`.<br>中：幂集公理保证任意集合 `A` 的 `\mathcal{P}(A)` 存在。     |
| `AC`           | axiom of choice, axiome du choix, 选择公理                                         | A principle asserting that one can choose one element from each set in any family of nonempty sets. | EN: The axiom of choice is often used to prove every vector space has a basis.<br>FR: L’axiome du choix sert souvent à prouver que tout espace vectoriel possède une base.<br>中：选择公理常用于证明每个向量空间都有基。     |
| —              | Zorn’s lemma, lemme de Zorn, Zorn 引理                                           | A maximal-element principle equivalent to the axiom of choice.                                      | EN: Zorn’s lemma is used to prove existence of maximal ideals.<br>FR: Le lemme de Zorn sert à prouver l’existence d’idéaux maximaux.<br>中：Zorn 引理可用于证明极大理想存在。                                           |
| —              | continuum hypothesis, hypothèse du continu, 连续统假设                              | The statement that there is no cardinal strictly between `\aleph_0` and `\mathfrak{c}`.             | EN: The continuum hypothesis is independent of `ZFC`, assuming `ZFC` is consistent.<br>FR: L’hypothèse du continu est indépendante de `ZFC`, si `ZFC` est consistante.<br>中：若 `ZFC` 相容，则连续统假设独立于 `ZFC`。 |

## 19.11 Formal proof systems and inference

| Symbol (LaTeX)                        | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                  | Definition                                                                                  | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |
| ------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `\Gamma`                              | set of assumptions, ensemble d’hypothèses, 假设集     | A collection of formulas used as premises in a derivation.                                  | EN: `\Gamma` may contain axioms and previously proved statements.<br>FR: `\Gamma` peut contenir des axiomes et des énoncés déjà prouvés.<br>中：`\Gamma` 可包含公理和已证明命题。                                                                                         |
| `\Gamma\vdash\varphi`                 | syntactic entailment, conséquence syntaxique, 语法推出 | Formula `\varphi` is derivable from assumptions `\Gamma` in a formal proof system.          | EN: `\Gamma\vdash\varphi` means there is a formal proof of `\varphi` from `\Gamma`.<br>FR: `\Gamma\vdash\varphi` signifie qu’il existe une preuve formelle de `\varphi` à partir de `\Gamma`.<br>中：`\Gamma\vdash\varphi` 表示存在从 `\Gamma` 推出 `\varphi` 的形式证明。 |
| `\Gamma\models\varphi`                | semantic entailment, conséquence sémantique, 语义蕴涵  | Every model satisfying `\Gamma` also satisfies `\varphi`.                                   | EN: `\Gamma\models\varphi` is about truth in all models.<br>FR: `\Gamma\models\varphi` concerne la vérité dans tous les modèles.<br>中：`\Gamma\models\varphi` 讨论所有模型中的真值保持。                                                                                  |
| —                                     | axiom, axiome, 公理                                  | A formula accepted as a starting point of a formal system.                                  | EN: Group theory begins from group axioms.<br>FR: La théorie des groupes commence par les axiomes de groupe.<br>中：群论从群公理开始。                                                                                                                                 |
| —                                     | inference rule, règle d’inférence, 推理规则            | A rule allowing new formulas to be derived from existing formulas.                          | EN: Modus ponens is a basic inference rule.<br>FR: Le modus ponens est une règle d’inférence fondamentale.<br>中：假言推理是基本推理规则。                                                                                                                                |
| `P,\ P\Rightarrow Q\vdash Q`          | modus ponens, modus ponens, 假言推理                   | From `P` and `P\Rightarrow Q`, infer `Q`.                                                   | EN: If `n` is even and even numbers are divisible by `2`, infer `n` is divisible by `2`.<br>FR: Si `n` est pair et les nombres pairs sont divisibles par `2`, conclure que `n` est divisible par `2`.<br>中：若 `n` 是偶数，且偶数可被 `2` 整除，则推出 `n` 可被 `2` 整除。        |
| `\neg Q,\ P\Rightarrow Q\vdash\neg P` | modus tollens, modus tollens, 否定后件式                | From `P\Rightarrow Q` and `\neg Q`, infer `\neg P`.                                         | EN: If rain implies wet ground, and the ground is not wet, infer it did not rain.<br>FR: Si la pluie implique un sol mouillé, et le sol n’est pas mouillé, conclure qu’il n’a pas plu.<br>中：若下雨推出地面湿，而地面不湿，则推出没有下雨。                                         |
| —                                     | natural deduction, déduction naturelle, 自然演绎       | A proof system organized around introduction and elimination rules for logical connectives. | EN: Natural deduction mirrors ordinary mathematical reasoning.<br>FR: La déduction naturelle reflète le raisonnement mathématique ordinaire.<br>中：自然演绎贴近日常数学推理。                                                                                             |
| —                                     | sequent calculus, calcul des séquents, 相继式演算       | A formal proof system using sequents to represent derivability.                             | EN: Sequent calculus makes structural rules explicit.<br>FR: Le calcul des séquents rend explicites les règles structurelles.<br>中：相继式演算明确呈现结构规则。                                                                                                           |
| —                                     | Hilbert system, système de Hilbert, Hilbert 系统     | A proof system using many axiom schemas and few inference rules.                            | EN: Hilbert systems are compact but less close to informal proof style.<br>FR: Les systèmes de Hilbert sont compacts mais moins proches du style informel.<br>中：Hilbert 系统形式简洁，但不太接近日常证明风格。                                                                 |
| —                                     | soundness, correction, 可靠性                         | A proof system is sound if every derivable statement is semantically valid.                 | EN: Soundness says formal proof does not derive false semantic consequences.<br>FR: La correction dit qu’une preuve formelle ne dérive pas de fausses conséquences sémantiques.<br>中：可靠性表示形式证明不会推出语义上错误的结论。                                                 |
| —                                     | completeness, complétude, 完备性                      | A proof system is complete if every semantic consequence is derivable.                      | EN: First-order logic has a completeness theorem.<br>FR: La logique du premier ordre possède un théorème de complétude.<br>中：一阶逻辑有完备性定理。                                                                                                                    |

## 19.12 Models, theories, and metamathematics

| Symbol (LaTeX)              | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                           | Definition                                                                                                              | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| --------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `\mathcal{M}`               | model, modèle, 模型                                                           | A mathematical structure interpreting the symbols of a formal language and satisfying specified sentences.              | EN: The natural numbers with addition and multiplication form a model of arithmetic axioms.<br>FR: Les entiers naturels avec addition et multiplication forment un modèle d’axiomes arithmétiques.<br>中：带加法和乘法的自然数结构是算术公理的一个模型。                                           |
| `\mathcal{M}\models\varphi` | satisfaction, satisfaction, 满足关系                                            | A model `\mathcal{M}` makes formula `\varphi` true.                                                                     | EN: `\mathbb{R}\models\exists x(x^2=2)`.<br>FR: `\mathbb{R}\models\exists x(x^2=2)`.<br>中：`\mathbb{R}\models\exists x(x^2=2)` 表示实数结构中存在平方为 `2` 的数。                                                                                                                        |
| `T`                         | theory, théorie, 理论                                                         | A set of sentences in a formal language, often closed under logical consequence.                                        | EN: Group theory can be formalized as a first-order theory.<br>FR: La théorie des groupes peut être formalisée comme une théorie du premier ordre.<br>中：群论可被形式化为一阶理论。                                                                                                     |
| `\operatorname{Mod}(T)`     | class of models of a theory, classe des modèles d’une théorie, 理论的模型类       | The collection of structures satisfying every sentence of `T`.                                                          | EN: `\operatorname{Mod}(T)` for group axioms is the class of all groups.<br>FR: `\operatorname{Mod}(T)` pour les axiomes de groupe est la classe de tous les groupes.<br>中：群公理的 `\operatorname{Mod}(T)` 是所有群构成的类。                                                         |
| —                           | consistency, consistance, 相容性                                               | A theory is consistent if it does not prove a contradiction.                                                            | EN: A consistent theory does not prove both `P` and `\neg P`.<br>FR: Une théorie consistante ne prouve pas à la fois `P` et `\neg P`.<br>中：相容理论不会同时证明 `P` 与 `\neg P`。                                                                                                     |
| —                           | syntactic consistency, consistance syntaxique, 语法相容性                        | A theory is syntactically consistent if no contradiction is derivable from it.                                          | EN: `T` is syntactically consistent if `T\not\vdash\bot`.<br>FR: `T` est syntaxiquement consistante si `T\not\vdash\bot`.<br>中：若 `T\not\vdash\bot`，则 `T` 语法相容。                                                                                                            |
| —                           | satisfiability, satisfiabilité, 可满足性                                        | A set of sentences is satisfiable if some model satisfies all of them.                                                  | EN: The axioms of fields are satisfiable because `\mathbb{Q}` is a field.<br>FR: Les axiomes des corps sont satisfiables car `\mathbb{Q}` est un corps.<br>中：域公理可满足，因为 `\mathbb{Q}` 是域。                                                                                   |
| —                           | compactness theorem, théorème de compacité, 紧致性定理                           | In first-order logic, if every finite subset of a theory is satisfiable, then the whole theory is satisfiable.          | EN: Compactness explains why first-order logic cannot fully control infinite size properties.<br>FR: La compacité explique pourquoi la logique du premier ordre ne contrôle pas entièrement certaines propriétés infinies.<br>中：紧致性说明一阶逻辑不能完全控制某些无限性质。                    |
| —                           | Löwenheim-Skolem theorem, théorème de Löwenheim-Skolem, Löwenheim-Skolem 定理 | A theorem stating that first-order theories with infinite models often have models of different infinite cardinalities. | EN: First-order set theory can have countable models if it has any infinite model under suitable assumptions.<br>FR: La théorie des ensembles du premier ordre peut avoir des modèles dénombrables sous des hypothèses appropriées.<br>中：在适当假设下，一阶集合论若有无限模型，则也可有可数模型。     |
| —                           | categoricity, catégoricité, 范畴性                                             | A theory is categorical in a cardinality if all its models of that cardinality are isomorphic.                          | EN: Some theories determine their models uniquely up to isomorphism in a fixed size.<br>FR: Certaines théories déterminent leurs modèles à isomorphisme près dans une taille fixée.<br>中：某些理论在给定基数下能唯一确定模型到同构为止。                                                          |
| —                           | incompleteness theorem, théorème d’incomplétude, 不完备性定理                     | Gödel’s result showing limits of sufficiently strong, consistent, effectively axiomatized formal systems.               | EN: Arithmetic truth cannot be fully captured by one suitable computable consistent axiom system.<br>FR: La vérité arithmétique ne peut pas être entièrement capturée par un seul système axiomatique calculable et consistant approprié.<br>中：算术真理不能被一个适当的可计算相容公理系统完全捕捉。 |
| —                           | decidability, décidabilité, 可判定性                                            | A property of a theory or problem having an algorithm that always determines the correct answer.                        | EN: Propositional logic validity is decidable.<br>FR: La validité en logique propositionnelle est décidable.<br>中：命题逻辑有效性是可判定的。                                                                                                                                           |
| —                           | undecidability, indécidabilité, 不可判定性                                       | The absence of an algorithm that always decides a problem correctly.                                                    | EN: The halting problem is undecidable.<br>FR: Le problème de l’arrêt est indécidable.<br>中：停机问题不可判定。                                                                                                                                                                     |

# 20. Algorithms, Computability, Complexity, and Information

## 20.1 Algorithms, inputs, and computation

| Symbol (LaTeX) | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                         | Definition                                                                                          | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |
| -------------- | --------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| —              | algorithm, algorithme, 算法                                 | A finite, precise procedure for solving a class of problems.                                        | EN: Euclid’s algorithm computes the greatest common divisor.<br>FR: L’algorithme d’Euclide calcule le plus grand commun diviseur.<br>中：欧几里得算法计算最大公约数。                                                                       |
| `I`            | input, entrée, 输入                                         | The data supplied to an algorithm.                                                                  | EN: For sorting, the input is a list of items.<br>FR: Pour le tri, l’entrée est une liste d’éléments.<br>中：对排序问题，输入是一列元素。                                                                                                   |
| `O`            | output, sortie, 输出                                        | The result produced by an algorithm.                                                                | EN: A sorting algorithm outputs the list in sorted order.<br>FR: Un algorithme de tri produit la liste triée.<br>中：排序算法输出排好序的列表。                                                                                            |
| `n`            | input size, taille de l’entrée, 输入规模                      | A numerical measure of the size of the input.                                                       | EN: For an array, `n` is often the number of elements.<br>FR: Pour un tableau, `n` est souvent le nombre d’éléments.<br>中：对数组，`n` 通常表示元素个数。                                                                                 |
| —              | instance, instance, 实例                                    | A specific input of a general computational problem.                                                | EN: A particular graph with `10` vertices is an instance of the shortest-path problem.<br>FR: Un graphe particulier avec `10` sommets est une instance du problème du plus court chemin.<br>中：一个有 `10` 个顶点的具体图是最短路径问题的一个实例。 |
| —              | problem, problème, 问题                                     | A general computational task mapping possible inputs to desired outputs or answers.                 | EN: Sorting is a problem; merge sort is an algorithm for that problem.<br>FR: Le tri est un problème ; le tri fusion est un algorithme pour ce problème.<br>中：排序是问题；归并排序是解决该问题的算法。                                          |
| —              | deterministic algorithm, algorithme déterministe, 确定性算法   | An algorithm whose next step is completely determined by its current state and input.               | EN: Standard binary search is deterministic.<br>FR: La recherche binaire standard est déterministe.<br>中：标准二分查找是确定性算法。                                                                                                      |
| —              | randomized algorithm, algorithme randomisé, 随机算法          | An algorithm that uses random choices during computation.                                           | EN: Randomized quicksort chooses pivots randomly.<br>FR: Le tri rapide randomisé choisit les pivots au hasard.<br>中：随机化快速排序随机选择枢轴。                                                                                          |
| —              | exact algorithm, algorithme exact, 精确算法                   | An algorithm guaranteed to return an exact correct solution.                                        | EN: Dijkstra’s algorithm gives exact shortest paths with nonnegative weights.<br>FR: L’algorithme de Dijkstra donne des plus courts chemins exacts avec poids non négatifs.<br>中：Dijkstra 算法在非负权图中给出精确最短路径。                 |
| —              | approximation algorithm, algorithme d’approximation, 近似算法 | An algorithm designed to return a solution close to optimal when exact optimization is too costly.  | EN: Some scheduling problems use approximation algorithms.<br>FR: Certains problèmes d’ordonnancement utilisent des algorithmes d’approximation.<br>中：某些调度问题使用近似算法。                                                         |
| —              | heuristic, heuristique, 启发式方法                             | A practical rule or method that often works well but may lack guaranteed correctness or optimality. | EN: Greedy choices can be useful heuristics for hard problems.<br>FR: Les choix gloutons peuvent servir d’heuristiques pour des problèmes difficiles.<br>中：贪心选择可作为困难问题的启发式方法。                                               |
| —              | termination, terminaison, 终止性                             | The property that an algorithm eventually stops on its intended inputs.                             | EN: A correct sorting algorithm must terminate after producing a sorted list.<br>FR: Un algorithme de tri correct doit terminer après avoir produit une liste triée.<br>中：正确排序算法必须在输出有序列表后终止。                               |

## 20.2 Asymptotic notation and algorithm analysis

| Symbol (LaTeX) | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                           | Definition                                                                                           | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
| -------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `T(n)`         | running time, temps d’exécution, 运行时间                       | A function describing how many computational steps an algorithm uses as input size grows.            | EN: Linear search has running time proportional to `n` in the worst case.<br>FR: La recherche linéaire a un temps proportionnel à `n` dans le pire cas.<br>中：线性查找在最坏情况下运行时间与 `n` 成正比。                                 |
| `S(n)`         | space complexity, complexité spatiale, 空间复杂度                | A function describing how much memory an algorithm uses as input size grows.                         | EN: In-place sorting aims to use small extra space.<br>FR: Le tri en place cherche à utiliser peu d’espace supplémentaire.<br>中：原地排序试图使用很少的额外空间。                                                                      |
| `O(g(n))`      | big-O notation, notation grand O, 大 O 记号                    | An asymptotic upper bound up to constant factors.                                                    | EN: Binary search runs in `O(\log n)` time.<br>FR: La recherche binaire s’exécute en temps `O(\log n)`.<br>中：二分查找的时间复杂度是 `O(\log n)`。                                                                                 |
| `\Omega(g(n))` | big-Omega notation, notation grand Oméga, 大 Ω 记号            | An asymptotic lower bound up to constant factors.                                                    | EN: Any comparison sort needs `\Omega(n\log n)` comparisons in the worst case.<br>FR: Tout tri par comparaisons nécessite `\Omega(n\log n)` comparaisons dans le pire cas.<br>中：任何比较排序在最坏情况下需要 `\Omega(n\log n)` 次比较。 |
| `\Theta(g(n))` | big-Theta notation, notation grand Thêta, 大 Θ 记号            | A tight asymptotic bound, both upper and lower.                                                      | EN: Merge sort runs in `\Theta(n\log n)` time.<br>FR: Le tri fusion s’exécute en temps `\Theta(n\log n)`.<br>中：归并排序的时间复杂度是 `\Theta(n\log n)`。                                                                         |
| `o(g(n))`      | little-o notation, notation petit o, 小 o 记号                 | A strict asymptotic upper bound where the ratio tends to zero.                                       | EN: `\log n=o(n)`.<br>FR: `\log n=o(n)`.<br>中：`\log n=o(n)`。                                                                                                                                                          |
| `\omega(g(n))` | little-omega notation, notation petit oméga, 小 ω 记号         | A strict asymptotic lower bound where the ratio tends to infinity.                                   | EN: `n^2=\omega(n\log n)`.<br>FR: `n^2=\omega(n\log n)`.<br>中：`n^2=\omega(n\log n)`。                                                                                                                                  |
| —              | worst-case complexity, complexité dans le pire cas, 最坏情况复杂度 | The maximum resource use over all inputs of size `n`.                                                | EN: Quicksort has worst-case time `O(n^2)` without safeguards.<br>FR: Le tri rapide a un temps `O(n^2)` dans le pire cas sans précautions.<br>中：未经保护的快速排序最坏时间复杂度为 `O(n^2)`。                                           |
| —              | average-case complexity, complexité moyenne, 平均情况复杂度        | The expected resource use over a specified distribution of inputs.                                   | EN: Randomized quicksort has expected time `O(n\log n)`.<br>FR: Le tri rapide randomisé a un temps espéré `O(n\log n)`.<br>中：随机化快速排序的期望时间复杂度是 `O(n\log n)`。                                                           |
| —              | amortized complexity, complexité amortie, 摊还复杂度             | The average cost per operation over a sequence of operations, even if some operations are expensive. | EN: Dynamic arrays have amortized `O(1)` append operations.<br>FR: Les tableaux dynamiques ont des ajouts amortis en `O(1)`.<br>中：动态数组的追加操作摊还复杂度为 `O(1)`。                                                             |
| `\log n`       | logarithmic growth, croissance logarithmique, 对数增长          | A growth rate much slower than linear growth.                                                        | EN: Halving the search interval each step gives logarithmic time.<br>FR: Diviser l’intervalle de recherche par deux à chaque étape donne un temps logarithmique.<br>中：每步把搜索区间减半会得到对数时间。                               |
| `2^n`          | exponential growth, croissance exponentielle, 指数增长          | A growth rate where work doubles with each unit increase in input size.                              | EN: Brute-force search over all subsets often takes `2^n` time.<br>FR: La recherche exhaustive sur tous les sous-ensembles prend souvent un temps `2^n`.<br>中：穷举所有子集通常需要 `2^n` 时间。                                    |

## 20.3 Data structures and abstract data types

| Symbol (LaTeX) | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                     | Definition                                                                                        | Examples                                                                                                                                                              |
| -------------- | ----------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| —              | data structure, structure de données, 数据结构            | A way of organizing data to support efficient operations.                                         | EN: Arrays and linked lists are basic data structures.<br>FR: Les tableaux et les listes chaînées sont des structures de données de base.<br>中：数组和链表是基本数据结构。          |
| `A[i]`         | array access, accès à un tableau, 数组访问                | Retrieving the element at index `i` in an array.                                                  | EN: Array access is usually `O(1)`.<br>FR: L’accès à un tableau est généralement en `O(1)`.<br>中：数组访问通常是 `O(1)`。                                                      |
| —              | linked list, liste chaînée, 链表                        | A sequence of nodes where each node stores data and a reference to another node.                  | EN: Linked lists allow efficient insertion after a known node.<br>FR: Les listes chaînées permettent une insertion efficace après un nœud connu.<br>中：链表允许在已知节点后高效插入。 |
| `LIFO`         | stack, pile, 栈                                        | A data structure where the last inserted item is removed first.                                   | EN: Function calls are often managed using a stack.<br>FR: Les appels de fonctions sont souvent gérés par une pile.<br>中：函数调用通常用栈管理。                                  |
| `FIFO`         | queue, file, 队列                                       | A data structure where the first inserted item is removed first.                                  | EN: Breadth-first search uses a queue.<br>FR: Le parcours en largeur utilise une file.<br>中：广度优先搜索使用队列。                                                               |
| —              | priority queue, file de priorité, 优先队列                | A data structure that removes items according to priority.                                        | EN: Dijkstra’s algorithm often uses a priority queue.<br>FR: L’algorithme de Dijkstra utilise souvent une file de priorité.<br>中：Dijkstra 算法常使用优先队列。                  |
| —              | heap, tas, 堆                                          | A tree-like data structure supporting efficient minimum or maximum extraction.                    | EN: Binary heaps implement priority queues.<br>FR: Les tas binaires implémentent des files de priorité.<br>中：二叉堆可实现优先队列。                                              |
| —              | hash table, table de hachage, 哈希表                     | A data structure mapping keys to values using a hash function.                                    | EN: Hash tables often support expected `O(1)` lookup.<br>FR: Les tables de hachage offrent souvent une recherche espérée en `O(1)`.<br>中：哈希表常支持期望 `O(1)` 查找。          |
| `h(k)`         | hash function, fonction de hachage, 哈希函数              | A function mapping keys to table positions or hash values.                                        | EN: A good hash function spreads keys evenly.<br>FR: Une bonne fonction de hachage répartit les clés uniformément.<br>中：好的哈希函数使键分布均匀。                                 |
| —              | collision, collision, 冲突                              | A situation where different keys map to the same hash location.                                   | EN: Hash tables need collision-handling strategies.<br>FR: Les tables de hachage ont besoin de stratégies de gestion des collisions.<br>中：哈希表需要处理冲突的策略。               |
| —              | binary search tree, arbre binaire de recherche, 二叉搜索树 | A binary tree storing keys so that left descendants are smaller and right descendants are larger. | EN: Balanced search trees support logarithmic lookup.<br>FR: Les arbres de recherche équilibrés permettent une recherche logarithmique.<br>中：平衡搜索树支持对数时间查找。           |
| —              | graph representation, représentation de graphe, 图表示   | A way to store vertices and edges, commonly by adjacency lists or adjacency matrices.             | EN: Sparse graphs are usually stored with adjacency lists.<br>FR: Les graphes creux sont généralement stockés par listes d’adjacence.<br>中：稀疏图通常用邻接表存储。               |

## 20.4 Recursion, divide-and-conquer, and recurrence relations

| Symbol (LaTeX)      | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                           | Definition                                                                                              | Examples                                                                                                                                                                              |
| ------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| —                   | recursion, récursion, 递归                                    | A method where a function or procedure calls itself on smaller or simpler inputs.                       | EN: Computing factorial can be written recursively.<br>FR: Le calcul de la factorielle peut s’écrire récursivement.<br>中：阶乘计算可以递归表示。                                                  |
| —                   | base case, cas de base, 基本情形                                | A simple case in recursion that is solved directly and stops further recursive calls.                   | EN: In factorial recursion, `0!=1` is the base case.<br>FR: Dans la récursion de la factorielle, `0!=1` est le cas de base.<br>中：在阶乘递归中，`0!=1` 是基本情形。                                 |
| —                   | recursive case, cas récursif, 递归情形                          | The part of a recursive definition that reduces the problem to smaller instances.                       | EN: `n!=n(n-1)!` is the recursive case.<br>FR: `n!=n(n-1)!` est le cas récursif.<br>中：`n!=n(n-1)!` 是递归情形。                                                                             |
| —                   | divide and conquer, diviser pour régner, 分治法                | An algorithmic strategy that divides a problem into subproblems, solves them, and combines the results. | EN: Merge sort is a divide-and-conquer algorithm.<br>FR: Le tri fusion est un algorithme diviser-pour-régner.<br>中：归并排序是分治算法。                                                         |
| `T(n)=2T(n/2)+O(n)` | merge sort recurrence, récurrence du tri fusion, 归并排序递推式    | A recurrence describing the time complexity of merge sort.                                              | EN: This recurrence solves to `T(n)=O(n\log n)`.<br>FR: Cette récurrence donne `T(n)=O(n\log n)`.<br>中：该递推式解得 `T(n)=O(n\log n)`。                                                      |
| `T(n)=aT(n/b)+f(n)` | master theorem form, forme du théorème maître, 主定理形式        | A common recurrence form for divide-and-conquer algorithms.                                             | EN: The master theorem estimates many divide-and-conquer running times.<br>FR: Le théorème maître estime beaucoup de temps d’algorithmes diviser-pour-régner.<br>中：主定理可估计许多分治算法的运行时间。 |
| —                   | recursion tree, arbre de récursion, 递归树                     | A tree representation of recursive calls and their costs.                                               | EN: A recursion tree explains why merge sort costs `n\log n`.<br>FR: Un arbre de récursion explique pourquoi le tri fusion coûte `n\log n`.<br>中：递归树解释了为什么归并排序代价为 `n\log n`。          |
| —                   | tail recursion, récursion terminale, 尾递归                    | A recursion where the recursive call is the final operation.                                            | EN: Tail recursion can sometimes be optimized into iteration.<br>FR: La récursion terminale peut parfois être optimisée en itération.<br>中：尾递归有时可优化为迭代。                               |
| —                   | memoization, mémoïsation, 记忆化                               | A technique storing results of subproblems to avoid repeated computation.                               | EN: Memoization speeds up recursive Fibonacci computation.<br>FR: La mémoïsation accélère le calcul récursif de Fibonacci.<br>中：记忆化加速递归 Fibonacci 计算。                                 |
| —                   | dynamic programming, programmation dynamique, 动态规划          | A method solving problems by combining stored solutions to overlapping subproblems.                     | EN: Edit distance can be computed by dynamic programming.<br>FR: La distance d’édition se calcule par programmation dynamique.<br>中：编辑距离可用动态规划计算。                                     |
| —                   | overlapping subproblems, sous-problèmes chevauchants, 重叠子问题 | A property where the same smaller problems occur repeatedly.                                            | EN: Fibonacci recursion has many overlapping subproblems.<br>FR: La récursion de Fibonacci contient beaucoup de sous-problèmes chevauchants.<br>中：Fibonacci 递归包含大量重叠子问题。              |
| —                   | optimal substructure, sous-structure optimale, 最优子结构        | A property where optimal solutions can be built from optimal solutions of subproblems.                  | EN: Shortest paths have optimal substructure.<br>FR: Les plus courts chemins possèdent une sous-structure optimale.<br>中：最短路径具有最优子结构。                                                 |

## 20.5 Sorting, searching, and selection

| Symbol (LaTeX) | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                              | Definition                                                                                     | Examples                                                                                                                                                                             |
| -------------- | ---------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| —              | sorting, tri, 排序                               | The problem of rearranging items according to an order relation.                               | EN: Sorting numbers from smallest to largest is a basic task.<br>FR: Trier des nombres du plus petit au plus grand est une tâche fondamentale.<br>中：把数字从小到大排列是基本任务。                  |
| —              | comparison sort, tri par comparaisons, 比较排序    | A sorting algorithm that obtains order information only by comparing pairs of elements.        | EN: Merge sort and heapsort are comparison sorts.<br>FR: Le tri fusion et le tri par tas sont des tris par comparaisons.<br>中：归并排序和堆排序是比较排序。                                         |
| —              | stable sort, tri stable, 稳定排序                  | A sorting algorithm preserving the relative order of equal keys.                               | EN: Stable sorting is useful when sorting records by multiple fields.<br>FR: Le tri stable est utile pour trier des enregistrements selon plusieurs champs.<br>中：按多个字段排序记录时，稳定排序很有用。 |
| —              | in-place sort, tri en place, 原地排序              | A sorting algorithm using only small extra memory beyond the input array.                      | EN: Heapsort is commonly implemented as an in-place sort.<br>FR: Le tri par tas est souvent implémenté comme tri en place.<br>中：堆排序常被实现为原地排序。                                        |
| —              | merge sort, tri fusion, 归并排序                   | A stable divide-and-conquer sorting algorithm with `O(n\log n)` time.                          | EN: Merge sort splits, sorts, and merges lists.<br>FR: Le tri fusion divise, trie, puis fusionne les listes.<br>中：归并排序先拆分、排序，再合并列表。                                                  |
| —              | quicksort, tri rapide, 快速排序                    | A sorting algorithm that partitions elements around a pivot.                                   | EN: Randomized quicksort has good expected performance.<br>FR: Le tri rapide randomisé a de bonnes performances espérées.<br>中：随机化快速排序具有良好的期望性能。                                     |
| —              | heapsort, tri par tas, 堆排序                     | A sorting algorithm using a heap to repeatedly extract maximum or minimum elements.            | EN: Heapsort runs in `O(n\log n)` time.<br>FR: Le tri par tas s’exécute en `O(n\log n)`.<br>中：堆排序时间复杂度为 `O(n\log n)`。                                                                |
| —              | counting sort, tri par comptage, 计数排序          | A non-comparison sorting algorithm for keys from a limited integer range.                      | EN: Counting sort can be linear when the key range is small.<br>FR: Le tri par comptage peut être linéaire lorsque l’intervalle des clés est petit.<br>中：当键范围较小时，计数排序可以是线性时间。        |
| —              | linear search, recherche linéaire, 线性查找        | A search method checking items one by one.                                                     | EN: Linear search works on unsorted lists.<br>FR: La recherche linéaire fonctionne sur des listes non triées.<br>中：线性查找适用于未排序列表。                                                     |
| —              | binary search, recherche binaire, 二分查找         | A search method repeatedly halving a sorted search interval.                                   | EN: Binary search needs sorted data.<br>FR: La recherche binaire exige des données triées.<br>中：二分查找需要已排序数据。                                                                         |
| —              | selection problem, problème de sélection, 选择问题 | The problem of finding the kth smallest or largest element.                                    | EN: Finding the median is a selection problem.<br>FR: Trouver la médiane est un problème de sélection.<br>中：寻找中位数是选择问题。                                                              |
| —              | median, médiane, 中位数                           | The middle element after sorting, or an average of two middle elements under some conventions. | EN: The median is robust to extreme values.<br>FR: La médiane est robuste aux valeurs extrêmes.<br>中：中位数对极端值较稳健。                                                                     |

## 20.6 Graph algorithms and network problems

| Symbol (LaTeX) | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                         | Definition                                                                                          | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| -------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `G=(V,E)`      | graph input, graphe en entrée, 图输入                                        | A graph given as input to an algorithm.                                                             | EN: A route-planning algorithm takes a road graph as input.<br>FR: Un algorithme de planification d’itinéraire prend un graphe routier en entrée.<br>中：路线规划算法以道路图作为输入。                                   |
