| —              | graph traversal, parcours de graphe, 图遍历                                  | A systematic procedure for visiting vertices or edges of a graph.                                   | EN: BFS and DFS are graph traversal methods.<br>FR: BFS et DFS sont des méthodes de parcours de graphe.<br>中：BFS 和 DFS 是图遍历方法。                                                                           |
| `BFS`          | breadth-first search, parcours en largeur, 广度优先搜索                         | A graph search method exploring vertices by distance layers.                                        | EN: BFS finds shortest paths in unweighted graphs.<br>FR: BFS trouve les plus courts chemins dans les graphes non pondérés.<br>中：BFS 可求无权图中的最短路径。                                                        |
| `DFS`          | depth-first search, parcours en profondeur, 深度优先搜索                        | A graph search method exploring as far as possible before backtracking.                             | EN: DFS can detect cycles and connected components.<br>FR: DFS peut détecter des cycles et des composantes connexes.<br>中：DFS 可检测环和连通分量。                                                                 |
| —              | shortest path problem, problème du plus court chemin, 最短路径问题              | The problem of finding a minimum-length or minimum-cost path between vertices.                      | EN: Navigation systems solve shortest-path problems.<br>FR: Les systèmes de navigation résolvent des problèmes de plus court chemin.<br>中：导航系统求解最短路径问题。                                                  |
| —              | Dijkstra’s algorithm, algorithme de Dijkstra, Dijkstra 算法                 | An algorithm for shortest paths with nonnegative edge weights.                                      | EN: Dijkstra’s algorithm uses a priority queue in many implementations.<br>FR: L’algorithme de Dijkstra utilise une file de priorité dans beaucoup d’implémentations.<br>中：Dijkstra 算法在许多实现中使用优先队列。      |
| —              | Bellman-Ford algorithm, algorithme de Bellman-Ford, Bellman-Ford 算法       | An algorithm for shortest paths that can handle negative edge weights and detect negative cycles.   | EN: Bellman-Ford is slower than Dijkstra but more general.<br>FR: Bellman-Ford est plus lent que Dijkstra mais plus général.<br>中：Bellman-Ford 比 Dijkstra 慢，但适用范围更广。                                     |
| —              | Floyd-Warshall algorithm, algorithme de Floyd-Warshall, Floyd-Warshall 算法 | A dynamic-programming algorithm for all-pairs shortest paths.                                       | EN: Floyd-Warshall computes shortest paths between every pair of vertices.<br>FR: Floyd-Warshall calcule les plus courts chemins entre toutes les paires de sommets.<br>中：Floyd-Warshall 计算每一对顶点之间的最短路径。 |
| —              | minimum spanning tree, arbre couvrant minimal, 最小生成树                      | A minimum-weight tree connecting all vertices of a connected weighted graph.                        | EN: Network design often uses minimum spanning trees.<br>FR: La conception de réseaux utilise souvent des arbres couvrants minimaux.<br>中：网络设计常使用最小生成树。                                                  |
| —              | Kruskal’s algorithm, algorithme de Kruskal, Kruskal 算法                    | A greedy algorithm building a minimum spanning tree by adding safe edges.                           | EN: Kruskal’s algorithm sorts edges by weight.<br>FR: L’algorithme de Kruskal trie les arêtes par poids.<br>中：Kruskal 算法按边权排序。                                                                           |
| —              | Prim’s algorithm, algorithme de Prim, Prim 算法                             | A greedy algorithm growing a minimum spanning tree from a starting vertex.                          | EN: Prim’s algorithm expands the tree one edge at a time.<br>FR: L’algorithme de Prim agrandit l’arbre une arête à la fois.<br>中：Prim 算法逐边扩展生成树。                                                         |
| —              | maximum flow, flot maximal, 最大流                                           | The problem of sending as much flow as possible from a source to a sink under capacity constraints. | EN: Maximum flow models transportation and communication capacity.<br>FR: Le flot maximal modélise les capacités de transport et de communication.<br>中：最大流可建模运输和通信容量。                                   |
| `s,t`          | source and sink, source et puits, 源点与汇点                                   | Distinguished vertices where flow starts and ends.                                                  | EN: In a flow network, flow moves from source `s` to sink `t`.<br>FR: Dans un réseau de flot, le flot va de la source `s` au puits `t`.<br>中：在流网络中，流从源点 `s` 到汇点 `t`。                                     |

## 20.7 Computability and Turing machines

| Symbol (LaTeX) | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                              | Definition                                                                                         | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| -------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| —              | computability, calculabilité, 可计算性                             | The study of which functions or problems can be solved by mechanical procedures.                   | EN: Computability asks whether an algorithm exists at all.<br>FR: La calculabilité demande s’il existe un algorithme.<br>中：可计算性研究算法是否存在。                                                 |
| `M`            | Turing machine, machine de Turing, 图灵机                         | A formal model of computation using states, a tape, and transition rules.                          | EN: Turing machines formalize the idea of algorithmic computation.<br>FR: Les machines de Turing formalisent l’idée de calcul algorithmique.<br>中：图灵机形式化了算法计算的概念。                        |
| `q`            | state, état, 状态                                                | A control condition of an abstract machine during computation.                                     | EN: A Turing machine changes state according to its transition function.<br>FR: Une machine de Turing change d’état selon sa fonction de transition.<br>中：图灵机根据转移函数改变状态。                 |
| `\Gamma`       | tape alphabet, alphabet de bande, 纸带字母表                        | The finite set of symbols that may appear on a Turing machine tape.                                | EN: The tape alphabet includes blank symbols.<br>FR: L’alphabet de bande inclut des symboles blancs.<br>中：纸带字母表包含空白符号。                                                                   |
| `\delta`       | transition function, fonction de transition, 转移函数              | The rule determining the next state, written symbol, and head movement.                            | EN: The transition function is the program of a Turing machine.<br>FR: La fonction de transition est le programme d’une machine de Turing.<br>中：转移函数就是图灵机的程序。                            |
| —              | decidable problem, problème décidable, 可判定问题                   | A decision problem for which an algorithm always halts with the correct yes-or-no answer.          | EN: Testing whether an integer is even is decidable.<br>FR: Tester si un entier est pair est décidable.<br>中：判断整数是否为偶数是可判定问题。                                                            |
| —              | semidecidable problem, problème semi-décidable, 半可判定问题         | A problem where yes-instances can be recognized by an algorithm that may not halt on no-instances. | EN: Program halting is semidecidable but not decidable.<br>FR: L’arrêt des programmes est semi-décidable mais non décidable.<br>中：程序停机问题是半可判定但不可判定的。                                     |
| —              | undecidable problem, problème indécidable, 不可判定问题              | A problem for which no algorithm always gives the correct yes-or-no answer.                        | EN: The halting problem is undecidable.<br>FR: Le problème de l’arrêt est indécidable.<br>中：停机问题不可判定。                                                                                    |
| `HALT`         | halting problem, problème de l’arrêt, 停机问题                     | The problem of deciding whether a given program halts on a given input.                            | EN: No algorithm can solve `HALT` for all programs and inputs.<br>FR: Aucun algorithme ne résout `HALT` pour tous les programmes et entrées.<br>中：不存在算法能对所有程序和输入解决 `HALT`。               |
| —              | Church-Turing thesis, thèse de Church-Turing, Church-Turing 论题 | The thesis that effectively computable functions are exactly those computable by a Turing machine. | EN: The thesis is not a theorem but a foundational identification.<br>FR: La thèse n’est pas un théorème mais une identification fondatrice.<br>中：该论题不是定理，而是基础性的识别原则。                    |
| —              | universal Turing machine, machine de Turing universelle, 通用图灵机 | A Turing machine capable of simulating any other Turing machine given its description.             | EN: A universal machine is an abstract ancestor of programmable computers.<br>FR: Une machine universelle est un ancêtre abstrait des ordinateurs programmables.<br>中：通用图灵机是可编程计算机的抽象先祖。 |
| —              | reduction, réduction, 归约                                       | A transformation from one problem to another used to compare difficulty.                           | EN: Reductions prove undecidability by transferring hardness.<br>FR: Les réductions prouvent l’indécidabilité en transférant la difficulté.<br>中：归约通过转移困难性证明不可判定性。                       |

## 20.8 Complexity classes and computational hardness

| Symbol (LaTeX)       | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                               | Definition                                                                                              | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| -------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `P`                  | polynomial time, temps polynomial, 多项式时间类                                       | The class of decision problems solvable by deterministic algorithms in polynomial time.                 | EN: Graph connectivity is in `P`.<br>FR: La connexité d’un graphe est dans `P`.<br>中：图连通性问题属于 `P`。                                                                                            |
| `NP`                 | nondeterministic polynomial time, temps polynomial non déterministe, 非确定性多项式时间类 | The class of decision problems whose yes-certificates can be verified in polynomial time.               | EN: Sudoku solution verification is in `NP`.<br>FR: La vérification d’une solution de sudoku est dans `NP`.<br>中：数独解的验证属于 `NP`。                                                               |
| `P\subseteq NP`      | P is contained in NP, P est inclus dans NP, P 包含于 NP                            | Every problem solvable in polynomial time is verifiable in polynomial time.                             | EN: If a problem is easy to solve, its solution is easy to verify.<br>FR: Si un problème est facile à résoudre, sa solution est facile à vérifier.<br>中：若问题容易求解，则其解也容易验证。                     |
| `P\stackrel{?}{=}NP` | P versus NP problem, problème P contre NP, P 与 NP 问题                            | The open question asking whether every efficiently verifiable problem is efficiently solvable.          | EN: `P=NP` remains one of the central open problems in theoretical computer science.<br>FR: `P=NP` reste un problème ouvert central en informatique théorique.<br>中：`P=NP` 仍是理论计算机科学中的核心开放问题。 |
| `NP`-hard            | NP-hard, NP-difficile, NP 困难                                                    | A problem at least as hard as every problem in `NP` under a chosen reduction.                           | EN: Some optimization versions of satisfiability are `NP`-hard.<br>FR: Certaines versions d’optimisation de la satisfiabilité sont `NP`-difficiles.<br>中：某些可满足性优化版本是 `NP` 困难的。                |
| `NP`-complete        | NP-complete, NP-complet, NP 完全                                                  | A problem that is both in `NP` and `NP`-hard.                                                           | EN: Boolean satisfiability is `NP`-complete.<br>FR: La satisfiabilité booléenne est `NP`-complète.<br>中：布尔可满足性问题是 `NP` 完全的。                                                                   |
| `SAT`                | Boolean satisfiability problem, problème de satisfiabilité booléenne, 布尔可满足性问题  | The problem of deciding whether a Boolean formula has a satisfying assignment.                          | EN: `SAT` was the first problem proved `NP`-complete.<br>FR: `SAT` fut le premier problème prouvé `NP`-complet.<br>中：`SAT` 是第一个被证明为 `NP` 完全的问题。                                               |
| `3SAT`               | 3-satisfiability, 3-satisfiabilité, 3-可满足性                                      | A satisfiability problem where clauses contain at most or exactly three literals under common variants. | EN: `3SAT` is a standard `NP`-complete problem.<br>FR: `3SAT` est un problème `NP`-complet standard.<br>中：`3SAT` 是标准的 `NP` 完全问题。                                                              |
| `coNP`               | co-NP, co-NP, coNP 类                                                            | The class of problems whose no-instances have polynomial-time verifiable certificates.                  | EN: Tautology testing belongs naturally to `coNP`.<br>FR: Le test de tautologie appartient naturellement à `coNP`.<br>中：重言式检验自然属于 `coNP`。                                                     |
| `PSPACE`             | polynomial space, espace polynomial, 多项式空间类                                     | The class of decision problems solvable using polynomial memory.                                        | EN: Some game-solving problems are `PSPACE`-complete.<br>FR: Certains problèmes de résolution de jeux sont `PSPACE`-complets.<br>中：某些游戏求解问题是 `PSPACE` 完全的。                                    |
| `EXPTIME`            | exponential time, temps exponentiel, 指数时间类                                      | The class of problems solvable in exponential time.                                                     | EN: Some decision procedures are correct but require exponential time.<br>FR: Certaines procédures de décision sont correctes mais exigent un temps exponentiel.<br>中：某些判定程序正确但需要指数时间。        |
| —                    | polynomial-time reduction, réduction en temps polynomial, 多项式时间归约               | A transformation computable in polynomial time that converts instances of one problem into another.     | EN: Polynomial reductions compare computational difficulty.<br>FR: Les réductions polynomiales comparent la difficulté computationnelle.<br>中：多项式时间归约用于比较计算困难度。                               |

## 20.9 Formal languages, grammars, and automata

| Symbol (LaTeX)       | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                            | Definition                                                                              | Examples                                                                                                                                                                      |
| -------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `\Sigma`             | alphabet, alphabet, 字母表                                                      | A finite set of symbols.                                                                | EN: `\Sigma=\{0,1\}` is the binary alphabet.<br>FR: `\Sigma=\{0,1\}` est l’alphabet binaire.<br>中：`\Sigma=\{0,1\}` 是二进制字母表。                                                   |
| `w\in\Sigma^*`       | string, mot, 字符串                                                             | A finite sequence of symbols from an alphabet.                                          | EN: `0101` is a string over `\{0,1\}`.<br>FR: `0101` est un mot sur `\{0,1\}`.<br>中：`0101` 是 `\{0,1\}` 上的字符串。                                                                 |
| `L\subseteq\Sigma^*` | language, langage, 语言                                                        | A set of strings over an alphabet.                                                      | EN: Binary strings with even length form a language.<br>FR: Les mots binaires de longueur paire forment un langage.<br>中：偶数长度的二进制串构成一种语言。                                     |
| `G=(V,\Sigma,R,S)`   | grammar, grammaire, 文法                                                       | A formal system generating strings by production rules.                                 | EN: A grammar can define the syntax of arithmetic expressions.<br>FR: Une grammaire peut définir la syntaxe des expressions arithmétiques.<br>中：文法可以定义算术表达式的语法。               |
| `S`                  | start symbol, symbole initial, 开始符号                                          | The symbol from which grammar derivations begin.                                        | EN: Derivation begins with the start symbol `S`.<br>FR: La dérivation commence par le symbole initial `S`.<br>中：推导从开始符号 `S` 开始。                                               |
| `A\to \alpha`        | production rule, règle de production, 产生式规则                                  | A rule replacing a symbol or pattern with another string of symbols.                    | EN: The rule `S\to aSb` generates matching `a` and `b` pairs.<br>FR: La règle `S\to aSb` génère des paires correspondantes de `a` et `b`.<br>中：规则 `S\to aSb` 生成匹配的 `a` 与 `b`。 |
| —                    | regular language, langage régulier, 正则语言                                     | A language recognized by a finite automaton or described by a regular expression.       | EN: Strings ending in `1` form a regular language.<br>FR: Les mots finissant par `1` forment un langage régulier.<br>中：以 `1` 结尾的字符串构成正则语言。                                    |
| —                    | context-free language, langage hors contexte, 上下文无关语言                        | A language generated by a context-free grammar.                                         | EN: Balanced parentheses form a context-free language.<br>FR: Les parenthèses équilibrées forment un langage hors contexte.<br>中：括号匹配语言是上下文无关语言。                              |
| `DFA`                | deterministic finite automaton, automate fini déterministe, 确定性有限自动机         | A finite automaton with exactly one transition for each state-symbol pair.              | EN: A `DFA` can recognize strings with an even number of zeros.<br>FR: Un `DFA` peut reconnaître les mots avec un nombre pair de zéros.<br>中：`DFA` 可识别含偶数个 `0` 的字符串。          |
| `NFA`                | nondeterministic finite automaton, automate fini non déterministe, 非确定性有限自动机 | A finite automaton that may have multiple possible transitions for a state-symbol pair. | EN: Every `NFA` has an equivalent `DFA`.<br>FR: Tout `NFA` possède un `DFA` équivalent.<br>中：每个 `NFA` 都有等价的 `DFA`。                                                            |
| `PDA`                | pushdown automaton, automate à pile, 下推自动机                                   | An automaton with a stack, recognizing context-free languages.                          | EN: A `PDA` can recognize balanced parentheses.<br>FR: Un `PDA` peut reconnaître les parenthèses équilibrées.<br>中：`PDA` 可以识别括号匹配语言。                                          |
| —                    | Chomsky hierarchy, hiérarchie de Chomsky, Chomsky 层级                         | A classification of formal grammars and languages by generative power.                  | EN: Regular languages are weaker than context-free languages.<br>FR: Les langages réguliers sont moins puissants que les langages hors contexte.<br>中：正则语言的表达能力弱于上下文无关语言。     |

## 20.10 Information theory and coding

| Symbol (LaTeX)               | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                  | Definition                                                                    | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
| ---------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------ | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `I(x)`                       | information content, contenu informationnel, 信息量                   | A measure of surprise associated with an event or message.                    | EN: Rare events carry more information when observed.<br>FR: Les événements rares portent plus d’information lorsqu’ils sont observés.<br>中：罕见事件被观察到时携带更多信息。                                                        |
| `-\log p(x)`                 | self-information, auto-information, 自信息                            | The information content of an event with probability `p(x)`.                  | EN: A less likely symbol has larger self-information.<br>FR: Un symbole moins probable a une auto-information plus grande.<br>中：概率更低的符号具有更大的自信息。                                                                    |
| `H(X)`                       | entropy, entropie, 熵                                               | The expected information content of a random variable.                        | EN: A fair coin has higher entropy than a biased coin.<br>FR: Une pièce équilibrée a une entropie plus grande qu’une pièce biaisée.<br>中：公平硬币的熵高于偏置硬币。                                                              |
| `H(X)=-\sum_x p(x)\log p(x)` | Shannon entropy, entropie de Shannon, Shannon 熵                    | A formula measuring average uncertainty in a discrete distribution.           | EN: For a fair binary variable, entropy is `1` bit with base-2 logarithms.<br>FR: Pour une variable binaire équilibrée, l’entropie vaut `1` bit avec logarithmes en base `2`.<br>中：对公平二元变量，用以 `2` 为底的对数时熵为 `1` bit。 |
| `H(X\mid Y)`                 | conditional entropy, entropie conditionnelle, 条件熵                  | The remaining uncertainty about `X` after observing `Y`.                      | EN: If `Y` fully determines `X`, then `H(X\mid Y)=0`.<br>FR: Si `Y` détermine entièrement `X`, alors `H(X\mid Y)=0`.<br>中：若 `Y` 完全决定 `X`，则 `H(X\mid Y)=0`。                                                          |
| `I(X;Y)`                     | mutual information, information mutuelle, 互信息                      | A measure of how much knowing one variable reduces uncertainty about another. | EN: Independent variables have mutual information `0`.<br>FR: Des variables indépendantes ont une information mutuelle nulle.<br>中：独立变量的互信息为 `0`。                                                                   |
| `D_{\mathrm{KL}}(P\Vert Q)`  | Kullback-Leibler divergence, divergence de Kullback-Leibler, KL 散度 | A measure of how one probability distribution differs from another.           | EN: KL divergence is not symmetric.<br>FR: La divergence KL n’est pas symétrique.<br>中：KL 散度不对称。                                                                                                                    |
| —                            | source coding, codage de source, 信源编码                              | Encoding data efficiently by exploiting statistical structure.                | EN: Compression is a source coding problem.<br>FR: La compression est un problème de codage de source.<br>中：压缩是信源编码问题。                                                                                              |
| —                            | channel coding, codage de canal, 信道编码                              | Encoding messages to protect them from noise during transmission.             | EN: Error-correcting codes are used in channel coding.<br>FR: Les codes correcteurs d’erreurs sont utilisés en codage de canal.<br>中：纠错码用于信道编码。                                                                     |
| `C`                          | channel capacity, capacité du canal, 信道容量                          | The maximum reliable communication rate of a channel.                         | EN: Shannon capacity sets a theoretical limit on communication.<br>FR: La capacité de Shannon fixe une limite théorique à la communication.<br>中：Shannon 容量给出通信的理论极限。                                               |
| —                            | prefix code, code préfixe, 前缀码                                     | A code where no codeword is a prefix of another codeword.                     | EN: Huffman coding produces prefix codes.<br>FR: Le codage de Huffman produit des codes préfixes.<br>中：Huffman 编码产生前缀码。                                                                                             |
| —                            | Hamming distance, distance de Hamming, Hamming 距离                  | The number of positions where two equal-length strings differ.                | EN: The Hamming distance between `1011` and `1001` is `1`.<br>FR: La distance de Hamming entre `1011` et `1001` vaut `1`.<br>中：`1011` 与 `1001` 的 Hamming 距离是 `1`。                                                   |

## 20.11 Cryptography and computational security

| Symbol (LaTeX) | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                        | Definition                                                                                               | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| -------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------ | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `m`            | plaintext message, message clair, 明文                                     | The original message before encryption.                                                                  | EN: The plaintext is what the sender wants to protect.<br>FR: Le message clair est ce que l’expéditeur veut protéger.<br>中：明文是发送者想保护的原始信息。                                                                    |
| `c`            | ciphertext, texte chiffré, 密文                                            | The encrypted form of a message.                                                                         | EN: An attacker sees ciphertext rather than plaintext.<br>FR: Un attaquant voit le texte chiffré plutôt que le message clair.<br>中：攻击者看到的是密文而不是明文。                                                            |
| `k`            | key, clé, 密钥                                                             | Secret or public information used by cryptographic algorithms.                                           | EN: Encryption and decryption depend on keys.<br>FR: Le chiffrement et le déchiffrement dépendent des clés.<br>中：加密和解密依赖密钥。                                                                                   |
| `E_k(m)`       | encryption function, fonction de chiffrement, 加密函数                       | A function transforming plaintext into ciphertext using a key.                                           | EN: `E_k(m)=c` means key `k` encrypts message `m` to ciphertext `c`.<br>FR: `E_k(m)=c` signifie que la clé `k` chiffre le message `m` en texte chiffré `c`.<br>中：`E_k(m)=c` 表示密钥 `k` 把明文 `m` 加密为密文 `c`。       |
| `D_k(c)`       | decryption function, fonction de déchiffrement, 解密函数                     | A function recovering plaintext from ciphertext using a key.                                             | EN: Correct decryption gives `D_k(E_k(m))=m`.<br>FR: Le déchiffrement correct donne `D_k(E_k(m))=m`.<br>中：正确解密满足 `D_k(E_k(m))=m`。                                                                             |
| —              | symmetric-key cryptography, cryptographie symétrique, 对称密钥密码学            | Cryptography where encryption and decryption use the same secret key.                                    | EN: AES is a symmetric-key encryption standard.<br>FR: AES est une norme de chiffrement symétrique.<br>中：AES 是对称密钥加密标准。                                                                                       |
| —              | public-key cryptography, cryptographie à clé publique, 公钥密码学             | Cryptography using a public key and a private key.                                                       | EN: Public-key systems allow encryption without sharing a secret key first.<br>FR: Les systèmes à clé publique permettent le chiffrement sans partager d’abord une clé secrète.<br>中：公钥系统允许在未预先共享秘密密钥时进行加密。   |
| `pk,sk`        | public key and secret key, clé publique et clé secrète, 公钥与私钥            | A key pair where `pk` may be public and `sk` must remain secret.                                         | EN: Anyone may know `pk`, but only the owner should know `sk`.<br>FR: Tout le monde peut connaître `pk`, mais seul le propriétaire doit connaître `sk`.<br>中：任何人都可知道 `pk`，但只有持有人应知道 `sk`。                     |
| —              | digital signature, signature numérique, 数字签名                             | A cryptographic method for verifying message origin and integrity.                                       | EN: Digital signatures help authenticate software updates.<br>FR: Les signatures numériques aident à authentifier les mises à jour logicielles.<br>中：数字签名有助于验证软件更新的来源和完整性。                                    |
| `H(m)`         | cryptographic hash function, fonction de hachage cryptographique, 密码哈希函数 | A function mapping messages to fixed-size digests with security properties such as collision resistance. | EN: Hash functions are used to verify data integrity.<br>FR: Les fonctions de hachage servent à vérifier l’intégrité des données.<br>中：哈希函数用于验证数据完整性。                                                         |
| —              | collision resistance, résistance aux collisions, 抗碰撞性                    | The difficulty of finding two distinct inputs with the same hash output.                                 | EN: A collision-resistant hash function should make collisions hard to find.<br>FR: Une fonction de hachage résistante aux collisions doit rendre les collisions difficiles à trouver.<br>中：抗碰撞哈希函数应使碰撞难以被找到。 |
| —              | one-way function, fonction à sens unique, 单向函数                           | A function easy to compute but hard to invert.                                                           | EN: One-way functions are a central assumption in cryptography.<br>FR: Les fonctions à sens unique sont une hypothèse centrale en cryptographie.<br>中：单向函数是密码学中的核心假设。                                         |

## 20.12 Verification, correctness, and program semantics

| Symbol (LaTeX)    | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                        | Definition                                                                                                           | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| ----------------- | -------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| —                 | program correctness, correction de programme, 程序正确性      | The property that a program satisfies its specification.                                                             | EN: A sorting program is correct if it returns a sorted permutation of the input.<br>FR: Un programme de tri est correct s’il renvoie une permutation triée de l’entrée.<br>中：排序程序若返回输入的有序排列，则是正确的。                                     |
| —                 | specification, spécification, 规格说明                       | A precise description of what a program or algorithm is required to do.                                              | EN: A specification for search says what output is required when the key is present or absent.<br>FR: Une spécification de recherche dit quelle sortie est requise lorsque la clé est présente ou absente.<br>中：查找规格说明会规定键存在或不存在时应输出什么。 |
| `P`               | precondition, précondition, 前置条件                         | A condition required to hold before a program fragment executes.                                                     | EN: Binary search has the precondition that the array is sorted.<br>FR: La recherche binaire a pour précondition que le tableau soit trié.<br>中：二分查找的前置条件是数组已排序。                                                                        |
| `Q`               | postcondition, postcondition, 后置条件                       | A condition required to hold after a program fragment executes.                                                      | EN: After sorting, the array should be ordered and contain the same elements.<br>FR: Après le tri, le tableau doit être ordonné et contenir les mêmes éléments.<br>中：排序后，数组应有序且包含同样的元素。                                                 |
| `\{P\}\ C\ \{Q\}` | Hoare triple, triplet de Hoare, Hoare 三元组                | A formal assertion that command `C`, started under precondition `P`, establishes postcondition `Q` if it terminates. | EN: Hoare triples express partial correctness.<br>FR: Les triplets de Hoare expriment la correction partielle.<br>中：Hoare 三元组表达部分正确性。                                                                                                   |
| —                 | partial correctness, correction partielle, 部分正确性         | The property that if a program terminates, its result satisfies the specification.                                   | EN: Partial correctness does not by itself prove termination.<br>FR: La correction partielle ne prouve pas à elle seule la terminaison.<br>中：部分正确性本身不证明程序会终止。                                                                           |
| —                 | total correctness, correction totale, 完全正确性              | Partial correctness plus termination.                                                                                | EN: Total correctness proves both the right answer and eventual stopping.<br>FR: La correction totale prouve à la fois la bonne réponse et l’arrêt.<br>中：完全正确性同时证明答案正确和程序终止。                                                            |
| —                 | loop invariant, invariant de boucle, 循环不变式               | A property that remains true before and after each loop iteration.                                                   | EN: Loop invariants are used to prove iterative algorithms correct.<br>FR: Les invariants de boucle servent à prouver les algorithmes itératifs corrects.<br>中：循环不变式用于证明迭代算法正确。                                                         |
| —                 | variant function, fonction variante, 变元函数 / 终止度量         | A quantity that decreases toward a lower bound to prove loop termination.                                            | EN: A loop counter decreasing to zero can be a variant function.<br>FR: Un compteur de boucle décroissant vers zéro peut être une fonction variante.<br>中：递减到零的循环计数器可作为终止度量。                                                            |
| —                 | operational semantics, sémantique opérationnelle, 操作语义   | A semantics describing program meaning by execution steps.                                                           | EN: Operational semantics explains how expressions evaluate.<br>FR: La sémantique opérationnelle explique comment les expressions s’évaluent.<br>中：操作语义解释表达式如何求值。                                                                       |
| —                 | denotational semantics, sémantique dénotationnelle, 指称语义 | A semantics assigning mathematical objects to programs.                                                              | EN: Denotational semantics interprets programs as functions or domains.<br>FR: La sémantique dénotationnelle interprète les programmes comme fonctions ou domaines.<br>中：指称语义把程序解释为函数或论域中的对象。                                           |
| —                 | type system, système de types, 类型系统                      | A formal system classifying program expressions to prevent certain errors and structure computation.                 | EN: Type systems can catch many errors before execution.<br>FR: Les systèmes de types peuvent détecter beaucoup d’erreurs avant l’exécution.<br>中：类型系统可在运行前发现许多错误。                                                                      |
# 21. Probability Theory and Stochastic Processes

## 21.1 Probability spaces, events, and measures

| Symbol (LaTeX)                                                      | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                               | Definition                                                                                                    | Examples                                                                                                                                                                               |
| ------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `\Omega`                                                            | sample space, univers, 样本空间                                                     | The set of all possible outcomes of a random experiment.                                                      | EN: For one die roll, `\Omega=\{1,2,3,4,5,6\}`.<br>FR: Pour un lancer de dé, `\Omega=\{1,2,3,4,5,6\}`.<br>中：掷一次骰子时，`\Omega=\{1,2,3,4,5,6\}`。                                           |
| `\omega`                                                            | outcome, issue / réalisation, 样本点                                               | A single element of the sample space.                                                                         | EN: The outcome `\omega=4` means the die shows `4`.<br>FR: L’issue `\omega=4` signifie que le dé montre `4`.<br>中：样本点 `\omega=4` 表示骰子点数为 `4`。                                          |
| `\mathcal{F}`                                                       | sigma-algebra of events, tribu des événements, 事件 σ-代数                          | A collection of subsets of `\Omega` closed under complements and countable unions.                            | EN: Events must belong to `\mathcal{F}` to have probabilities.<br>FR: Les événements doivent appartenir à `\mathcal{F}` pour avoir des probabilités.<br>中：事件必须属于 `\mathcal{F}` 才能赋予概率。 |
| `A\in\mathcal{F}`                                                   | event, événement, 事件                                                            | A measurable subset of the sample space.                                                                      | EN: “The die roll is even” is the event `A=\{2,4,6\}`.<br>FR: “Le lancer est pair” est l’événement `A=\{2,4,6\}`.<br>中：“骰子点数为偶数”是事件 `A=\{2,4,6\}`。                                     |
| `P`                                                                 | probability measure, mesure de probabilité, 概率测度                                | A measure on `\mathcal{F}` with total mass `1`.                                                               | EN: A fair coin gives `P(\text{heads})=1/2`.<br>FR: Une pièce équilibrée donne `P(\text{pile})=1/2`.<br>中：公平硬币满足 `P(\text{正面})=1/2`。                                                   |
| `(\Omega,\mathcal{F},P)`                                            | probability space, espace probabilisé, 概率空间                                     | A sample space, a sigma-algebra of events, and a probability measure.                                         | EN: Every rigorous probability model begins with a probability space.<br>FR: Tout modèle probabiliste rigoureux commence par un espace probabilisé.<br>中：严格的概率模型从概率空间开始。               |
| `P(\Omega)=1`                                                       | normalization axiom, axiome de normalisation, 规范化公理                             | The probability of the whole sample space is one.                                                             | EN: Something in the sample space must occur with probability `1`.<br>FR: Quelque chose dans l’univers se produit avec probabilité `1`.<br>中：样本空间中的某个结果必然以概率 `1` 发生。                   |
| `P(\varnothing)=0`                                                  | probability of impossible event, probabilité de l’événement impossible, 不可能事件概率 | The empty event has probability zero.                                                                         | EN: An impossible event has probability `0`.<br>FR: Un événement impossible a probabilité `0`.<br>中：不可能事件的概率为 `0`。                                                                     |
| `P(A^c)=1-P(A)`                                                     | complement rule, règle du complément, 补事件公式                                     | The probability of the complement equals one minus the probability of the event.                              | EN: If rain has probability `0.3`, no rain has probability `0.7`.<br>FR: Si la pluie a probabilité `0,3`, l’absence de pluie a probabilité `0,7`.<br>中：若下雨概率为 `0.3`，则不下雨概率为 `0.7`。     |
| `P\left(\bigcup_{n=1}^{\infty}A_n\right)=\sum_{n=1}^{\infty}P(A_n)` | countable additivity, additivité dénombrable, 可数可加性                             | For pairwise disjoint events, the probability of their countable union equals the sum of their probabilities. | EN: Disjoint alternatives have probabilities that add.<br>FR: Les alternatives disjointes ont des probabilités qui s’additionnent.<br>中：互不相交的备选事件概率可以相加。                               |
| `P(A\cup B)=P(A)+P(B)-P(A\cap B)`                                   | addition rule, règle d’addition, 加法公式                                           | A formula correcting double-counting in the probability of a union.                                           | EN: If two events overlap, subtract the probability of the overlap.<br>FR: Si deux événements se chevauchent, soustraire la probabilité du chevauchement.<br>中：若两个事件重叠，需要减去交集概率。       |
| `A_n\uparrow A`                                                     | increasing sequence of events, suite croissante d’événements, 递增事件列             | A sequence of events with `A_n\subseteq A_{n+1}` and union `A`.                                               | EN: For `A_n\uparrow A`, `P(A_n)\to P(A)`.<br>FR: Pour `A_n\uparrow A`, `P(A_n)\to P(A)`.<br>中：若 `A_n\uparrow A`，则 `P(A_n)\to P(A)`。                                                   |
| `A_n\downarrow A`                                                   | decreasing sequence of events, suite décroissante d’événements, 递减事件列           | A sequence of events with `A_{n+1}\subseteq A_n` and intersection `A`.                                        | EN: For `A_n\downarrow A`, `P(A_n)\to P(A)`.<br>FR: Pour `A_n\downarrow A`, `P(A_n)\to P(A)`.<br>中：若 `A_n\downarrow A`，则 `P(A_n)\to P(A)`。                                             |

## 21.2 Random variables, distributions, and laws

| Symbol (LaTeX)                             | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                              | Definition                                                                                 | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| ------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `X:\Omega\to E`                            | random variable, variable aléatoire, 随机变量                                      | A measurable function from a probability space to a measurable state space.                | EN: The number shown by a die is a random variable.<br>FR: Le nombre montré par un dé est une variable aléatoire.<br>中：骰子的点数是一个随机变量。                                                                                      |
| `X(\omega)`                                | realization of a random variable, réalisation d’une variable aléatoire, 随机变量取值 | The value of `X` at an outcome `\omega`.                                                   | EN: If the die outcome is `4`, then `X(\omega)=4`.<br>FR: Si l’issue du dé est `4`, alors `X(\omega)=4`.<br>中：若骰子样本点为 `4`，则 `X(\omega)=4`。                                                                                |
| `P_X`                                      | distribution / law, loi de probabilité, 分布 / 律                                 | The probability measure induced by a random variable on its state space.                   | EN: The law of a fair die assigns probability `1/6` to each face.<br>FR: La loi d’un dé équilibré attribue probabilité `1/6` à chaque face.<br>中：公平骰子的分布给每个点数概率 `1/6`。                                                    |
| `X\sim\mu`                                 | distributed according to, suit la loi, 服从分布                                    | A notation meaning that the law of `X` is `\mu`.                                           | EN: `X\sim\operatorname{Bernoulli}(p)` means `X` has Bernoulli law.<br>FR: `X\sim\operatorname{Bernoulli}(p)` signifie que `X` suit une loi de Bernoulli.<br>中：`X\sim\operatorname{Bernoulli}(p)` 表示 `X` 服从 Bernoulli 分布。 |
| `F_X(x)=P(X\leq x)`                        | cumulative distribution function, fonction de répartition, 分布函数                | A function giving the probability that `X` is at most `x`.                                 | EN: Every real random variable has a distribution function.<br>FR: Toute variable aléatoire réelle possède une fonction de répartition.<br>中：每个实值随机变量都有分布函数。                                                              |
| `p_X(x)`                                   | probability mass function, fonction de masse, 概率质量函数                           | A function giving probabilities of individual values for a discrete random variable.       | EN: For a fair die, `p_X(3)=1/6`.<br>FR: Pour un dé équilibré, `p_X(3)=1/6`.<br>中：对公平骰子，`p_X(3)=1/6`。                                                                                                                     |
| `f_X(x)`                                   | probability density function, densité de probabilité, 概率密度函数                   | A function whose integral over a set gives probability for a continuous distribution.      | EN: A normal random variable is described by a density.<br>FR: Une variable normale est décrite par une densité.<br>中：正态随机变量由密度函数描述。                                                                                      |
| `P(a\leq X\leq b)=\int_a^b f_X(x)\,dx`     | density integration rule, règle d’intégration de densité, 密度积分公式               | Probabilities for continuous variables are obtained by integrating density over intervals. | EN: The probability of an interval equals the area under the density.<br>FR: La probabilité d’un intervalle égale l’aire sous la densité.<br>中：区间概率等于密度曲线下的面积。                                                            |
| `X\sim\operatorname{Bernoulli}(p)`         | Bernoulli distribution, loi de Bernoulli, Bernoulli 分布                         | A distribution on `\{0,1\}` with success probability `p`.                                  | EN: A single coin toss can be modeled by a Bernoulli variable.<br>FR: Un seul lancer de pièce peut être modélisé par une variable de Bernoulli.<br>中：一次抛硬币可建模为 Bernoulli 随机变量。                                            |
| `X\sim\operatorname{Binomial}(n,p)`        | binomial distribution, loi binomiale, 二项分布                                     | The distribution of the number of successes in `n` independent Bernoulli trials.           | EN: The number of heads in `10` coin tosses has a binomial distribution.<br>FR: Le nombre de piles en `10` lancers suit une loi binomiale.<br>中：抛硬币 `10` 次的正面次数服从二项分布。                                                    |
| `X\sim\operatorname{Poisson}(\lambda)`     | Poisson distribution, loi de Poisson, Poisson 分布                               | A distribution often modeling counts of rare or independent events in a fixed interval.    | EN: The number of calls arriving in one minute can be modeled by a Poisson variable.<br>FR: Le nombre d’appels reçus en une minute peut être modélisé par une variable de Poisson.<br>中：一分钟内到达的电话数可用 Poisson 随机变量建模。      |
| `X\sim\mathcal{N}(\mu,\sigma^2)`           | normal distribution, loi normale, 正态分布                                         | A continuous bell-shaped distribution with mean `\mu` and variance `\sigma^2`.             | EN: Measurement errors are often approximated by normal distributions.<br>FR: Les erreurs de mesure sont souvent approchées par des lois normales.<br>中：测量误差常用正态分布近似。                                                     |
| `X\sim\operatorname{Exponential}(\lambda)` | exponential distribution, loi exponentielle, 指数分布                              | A continuous distribution often modeling waiting times with memoryless behavior.           | EN: Waiting time between Poisson arrivals is exponential.<br>FR: Le temps d’attente entre arrivées de Poisson est exponentiel.<br>中：Poisson 到达之间的等待时间服从指数分布。                                                              |

## 21.3 Expectation, variance, moments, and transforms

| Symbol (LaTeX)                                        | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                             | Definition                                                                           | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| ----------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `\mathbb{E}[X]`                                       | expectation, espérance, 期望                                                    | The probability-weighted average value of a random variable, when defined.           | EN: A fair die has expectation `3.5`.<br>FR: Un dé équilibré a une espérance `3,5`.<br>中：公平骰子的期望是 `3.5`。                                                                                                      |
| `\int X\,dP`                                          | expectation as integral, espérance comme intégrale, 作为积分的期望                   | Expectation defined as the integral of a random variable over the probability space. | EN: Writing expectation as `\int X\,dP` connects probability with measure theory.<br>FR: Écrire l’espérance comme `\int X\,dP` relie probabilité et théorie de la mesure.<br>中：把期望写成 `\int X\,dP` 可连接概率论与测度论。 |
| `\mathbb{E}[g(X)]`                                    | expected transformed value, espérance d’une transformation, 变换后的期望            | The expectation of a function applied to a random variable.                          | EN: `\mathbb{E}[X^2]` is the second raw moment.<br>FR: `\mathbb{E}[X^2]` est le moment brut d’ordre deux.<br>中：`\mathbb{E}[X^2]` 是二阶原点矩。                                                                      |
| `\operatorname{Var}(X)`                               | variance, variance, 方差                                                        | The expected squared deviation from the mean.                                        | EN: Variance measures spread around the expectation.<br>FR: La variance mesure la dispersion autour de l’espérance.<br>中：方差度量围绕期望的离散程度。                                                                       |
| `\operatorname{Var}(X)=\mathbb{E}[(X-\mathbb{E}X)^2]` | variance formula, formule de variance, 方差公式                                   | The defining formula for variance.                                                   | EN: Variance is nonnegative.<br>FR: La variance est non négative.<br>中：方差非负。                                                                                                                                  |
| `\sigma_X=\sqrt{\operatorname{Var}(X)}`               | standard deviation, écart-type, 标准差                                           | The square root of variance, measured in the same units as `X`.                      | EN: Standard deviation is easier to interpret than variance in original units.<br>FR: L’écart-type est plus facile à interpréter que la variance dans les unités originales.<br>中：标准差与原变量单位相同，通常比方差更易解释。      |
| `\operatorname{Cov}(X,Y)`                             | covariance, covariance, 协方差                                                   | A measure of joint linear variation between two random variables.                    | EN: Positive covariance means variables tend to move together.<br>FR: Une covariance positive signifie que les variables tendent à varier ensemble.<br>中：正协方差表示两个变量倾向于同向变化。                                   |
| `\rho_{X,Y}`                                          | correlation coefficient, coefficient de corrélation, 相关系数                     | A normalized covariance lying between `-1` and `1` when variances are positive.      | EN: Correlation is dimensionless.<br>FR: La corrélation est sans dimension.<br>中：相关系数是无量纲的。                                                                                                                   |
| `\mathbb{E}[X^k]`                                     | kth moment, moment d’ordre k, k 阶矩                                            | The expectation of the `k`th power of a random variable.                             | EN: Moments summarize distributional shape.<br>FR: Les moments résument la forme d’une distribution.<br>中：矩可以概括分布形状。                                                                                          |
| `\mathbb{E}[(X-\mu)^k]`                               | central moment, moment centré, 中心矩                                            | A moment of deviations from the mean `\mu`.                                          | EN: The second central moment is variance.<br>FR: Le moment centré d’ordre deux est la variance.<br>中：二阶中心矩就是方差。                                                                                              |
| `M_X(t)=\mathbb{E}[e^{tX}]`                           | moment generating function, fonction génératrice des moments, 矩母函数            | A transform that can encode all moments when finite near zero.                       | EN: Differentiating `M_X(t)` at `0` gives moments.<br>FR: Dériver `M_X(t)` en `0` donne les moments.<br>中：在 `0` 处对 `M_X(t)` 求导可得到矩。                                                                           |
| `\varphi_X(t)=\mathbb{E}[e^{itX}]`                    | characteristic function, fonction caractéristique, 特征函数                       | A Fourier-transform-like function that uniquely determines the distribution.         | EN: Every real random variable has a characteristic function.<br>FR: Toute variable aléatoire réelle possède une fonction caractéristique.<br>中：每个实随机变量都有特征函数。                                                |
| `G_X(s)=\mathbb{E}[s^X]`                              | probability generating function, fonction génératrice des probabilités, 概率母函数 | A generating function used mainly for nonnegative integer-valued random variables.   | EN: Probability generating functions are useful for branching processes.<br>FR: Les fonctions génératrices des probabilités sont utiles pour les processus de branchement.<br>中：概率母函数常用于分枝过程。                 |

## 21.4 Conditional probability, independence, and conditioning

| Symbol (LaTeX)                                                                         | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                                                           | Definition                                                                                                | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `P(A\mid B)`                                                                           | conditional probability, probabilité conditionnelle, 条件概率                                                   | The probability of `A` given that `B` has occurred, when `P(B)>0`.                                        | EN: `P(A\mid B)` updates probability after learning `B`.<br>FR: `P(A\mid B)` met à jour la probabilité après l’information `B`.<br>中：`P(A\mid B)` 表示知道 `B` 后对 `A` 的概率更新。                                                                   |
| `P(A\mid B)=\frac{P(A\cap B)}{P(B)}`                                                   | conditional probability formula, formule de probabilité conditionnelle, 条件概率公式                              | The defining formula for conditional probability when `P(B)>0`.                                           | EN: Conditional probability divides joint probability by the conditioning event probability.<br>FR: La probabilité conditionnelle divise la probabilité conjointe par la probabilité de l’événement conditionnant.<br>中：条件概率用联合概率除以条件事件概率。 |
| `P(A\cap B)=P(A)P(B)`                                                                  | independent events, événements indépendants, 独立事件                                                           | Events whose joint probability equals the product of their probabilities.                                 | EN: Two fair coin tosses are independent.<br>FR: Deux lancers d’une pièce équilibrée sont indépendants.<br>中：两次公平抛硬币相互独立。                                                                                                                  |
| `X\perp Y`                                                                             | independent random variables, variables aléatoires indépendantes, 独立随机变量                                    | Random variables whose joint law factors into the product of their marginal laws.                         | EN: Independent measurement errors are often modeled as independent variables.<br>FR: Des erreurs de mesure indépendantes sont souvent modélisées par des variables indépendantes.<br>中：相互独立的测量误差常建模为独立随机变量。                               |
| `X\perp Y\mid Z`                                                                       | conditional independence, indépendance conditionnelle, 条件独立                                                 | Variables `X` and `Y` are independent after conditioning on `Z`.                                          | EN: In graphical models, conditional independence encodes structure.<br>FR: Dans les modèles graphiques, l’indépendance conditionnelle encode la structure.<br>中：在图模型中，条件独立编码结构信息。                                                         |
| `P(A)=\sum_i P(A\mid B_i)P(B_i)`                                                       | law of total probability, formule des probabilités totales, 全概率公式                                           | A formula decomposing probability over a partition.                                                       | EN: Diagnose by summing over possible hidden causes.<br>FR: Diagnostiquer en sommant sur les causes cachées possibles.<br>中：可对潜在原因分类求和得到总概率。                                                                                               |
| `P(B_i\mid A)=\frac{P(A\mid B_i)P(B_i)}{\sum_jP(A\mid B_j)P(B_j)}`                     | Bayes’ theorem, théorème de Bayes, Bayes 定理                                                                 | A formula for reversing conditional probabilities using prior probabilities and likelihoods.              | EN: Bayes’ theorem updates beliefs after evidence.<br>FR: Le théorème de Bayes met à jour les croyances après observation.<br>中：Bayes 定理用于根据证据更新信念。                                                                                        |
| `\mathbb{E}[X\mid B]`                                                                  | conditional expectation given an event, espérance conditionnelle sachant un événement, 给定事件的条件期望            | The average value of `X` under the conditional probability measure given `B`.                             | EN: Expected loss given an accident is a conditional expectation.<br>FR: La perte espérée sachant un accident est une espérance conditionnelle.<br>中：给定事故发生时的期望损失是条件期望。                                                                    |
| `\mathbb{E}[X\mid Y]`                                                                  | conditional expectation given a random variable, espérance conditionnelle sachant une variable, 给定随机变量的条件期望 | A random variable representing the best mean-square prediction of `X` from `Y` under suitable conditions. | EN: `\mathbb{E}[X\mid Y]` is a function of `Y`.<br>FR: `\mathbb{E}[X\mid Y]` est une fonction de `Y`.<br>中：`\mathbb{E}[X\mid Y]` 是 `Y` 的函数。                                                                                                |
| `\mathbb{E}[X\mid\mathcal{G}]`                                                         | conditional expectation given a sigma-algebra, espérance conditionnelle sachant une tribu, 给定 σ-代数的条件期望     | The projection of `X` onto the information represented by `\mathcal{G}`.                                  | EN: Conditioning on `\mathcal{G}` means using only information in `\mathcal{G}`.<br>FR: Conditionner par `\mathcal{G}` signifie utiliser seulement l’information dans `\mathcal{G}`.<br>中：给定 `\mathcal{G}` 表示只使用 `\mathcal{G}` 中的信息。       |
| `\mathbb{E}[\mathbb{E}[X\mid\mathcal{G}]]=\mathbb{E}[X]`                               | tower property base form, propriété de la tour, 塔式性质基本式                                                     | The expectation of a conditional expectation equals the original expectation.                             | EN: Averaging a conditional average gives the overall average.<br>FR: La moyenne d’une moyenne conditionnelle donne la moyenne totale.<br>中：条件平均再取平均得到总体平均。                                                                                |
| `\mathbb{E}[\mathbb{E}[X\mid\mathcal{G}]\mid\mathcal{H}]=\mathbb{E}[X\mid\mathcal{H}]` | tower property, propriété de la tour, 塔式性质                                                                  | If `\mathcal{H}\subseteq\mathcal{G}`, conditioning in stages collapses to the coarser information.        | EN: Coarser information absorbs later averaging.<br>FR: L’information plus grossière absorbe la moyenne ultérieure.<br>中：较粗的信息会吸收后续条件平均。                                                                                                   |

## 21.5 Convergence of random variables and limit theorems

| Symbol (LaTeX)                                                           | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                      | Definition                                                                                                 | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| ------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ---------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `X_n\to X` a.s.                                                          | almost sure convergence, convergence presque sûre, 几乎必然收敛              | Convergence that holds with probability one.                                                               | EN: Almost sure convergence is a pathwise form of convergence.<br>FR: La convergence presque sûre est une convergence trajectorielle.<br>中：几乎必然收敛是一种逐样本路径的收敛。                                      |
| `X_n\to X` in `P`                                                        | convergence in probability, convergence en probabilité, 依概率收敛          | Convergence where the probability of a non-negligible deviation tends to zero.                             | EN: Sample averages converge in probability under weak laws.<br>FR: Les moyennes empiriques convergent en probabilité dans les lois faibles.<br>中：样本均值在弱大数定律下依概率收敛。                                |
| `X_n\Rightarrow X`                                                       | convergence in distribution, convergence en loi, 依分布收敛                 | Convergence of distribution functions at continuity points of the limit distribution.                      | EN: Normal approximation uses convergence in distribution.<br>FR: L’approximation normale utilise la convergence en loi.<br>中：正态近似使用依分布收敛。                                                         |
| `X_n\to X` in `L^p`                                                      | Lp convergence, convergence dans Lp, Lp 收敛                             | Convergence measured by `\mathbb{E}[\lvert X_n-X\rvert^p]\to0`.                                            | EN: `L^2` convergence controls mean squared error.<br>FR: La convergence dans `L^2` contrôle l’erreur quadratique moyenne.<br>中：`L^2` 收敛控制均方误差。                                                    |
| `X_n=O_P(a_n)`                                                           | bounded in probability rate, borné en probabilité, 概率有界阶               | A notation describing stochastic boundedness after scaling by `a_n`.                                       | EN: Statistical estimators often have error `O_P(n^{-1/2})`.<br>FR: Les estimateurs statistiques ont souvent une erreur `O_P(n^{-1/2})`.<br>中：统计估计量的误差常为 `O_P(n^{-1/2})`。                          |
| `X_n=o_P(a_n)`                                                           | negligible in probability rate, négligeable en probabilité, 概率小 o      | A notation meaning `X_n/a_n` converges to zero in probability.                                             | EN: Remainder terms in asymptotic statistics are often written as `o_P(1)`.<br>FR: Les restes en statistique asymptotique s’écrivent souvent `o_P(1)`.<br>中：渐近统计中的余项常写作 `o_P(1)`。                  |
| `\bar{X}_n=\frac{1}{n}\sum_{i=1}^nX_i`                                   | sample average, moyenne empirique, 样本均值                                | The average of the first `n` random variables.                                                             | EN: Sample averages estimate population means.<br>FR: Les moyennes empiriques estiment les moyennes de population.<br>中：样本均值估计总体均值。                                                                |
| —                                                                        | weak law of large numbers, loi faible des grands nombres, 弱大数定律        | A theorem stating that sample averages converge in probability to the mean under suitable assumptions.     | EN: Repeated independent measurements average out noise.<br>FR: Les mesures indépendantes répétées moyennent le bruit.<br>中：重复独立测量会平均掉噪声。                                                          |
| —                                                                        | strong law of large numbers, loi forte des grands nombres, 强大数定律       | A theorem stating that sample averages converge almost surely to the mean under suitable assumptions.      | EN: The strong law gives almost sure long-run frequency stabilization.<br>FR: La loi forte donne une stabilisation presque sûre des fréquences à long terme.<br>中：强大数定律给出长期频率的几乎必然稳定。              |
| `\frac{\sum_{i=1}^nX_i-n\mu}{\sigma\sqrt{n}}\Rightarrow\mathcal{N}(0,1)` | central limit theorem, théorème central limite, 中心极限定理                 | A theorem stating that normalized sums often converge in distribution to a normal law.                     | EN: Many averages are approximately normal for large samples.<br>FR: Beaucoup de moyennes sont approximativement normales pour de grands échantillons.<br>中：大样本下许多平均量近似服从正态分布。                     |
| —                                                                        | Slutsky’s theorem, théorème de Slutsky, Slutsky 定理                     | A theorem allowing limits in distribution to combine with convergence in probability.                      | EN: Slutsky’s theorem is standard in asymptotic statistics.<br>FR: Le théorème de Slutsky est standard en statistique asymptotique.<br>中：Slutsky 定理是渐近统计中的标准工具。                                    |
| —                                                                        | continuous mapping theorem, théorème de l’application continue, 连续映射定理 | If random variables converge and a function is continuous, the transformed variables converge accordingly. | EN: Apply the theorem to pass convergence through smooth transformations.<br>FR: Appliquer le théorème pour faire passer la convergence par des transformations continues.<br>中：可用该定理把收敛传递到连续变换之后。 |

## 21.6 Stochastic processes, filtrations, and adaptedness

| Symbol (LaTeX)                        | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                  | Definition                                                                                                             | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
| ------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------ | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `(X_t)_{t\in T}`                      | stochastic process, processus stochastique, 随机过程                   | A family of random variables indexed by time or another parameter.                                                     | EN: Stock price over time can be modeled as a stochastic process.<br>FR: Le prix d’une action dans le temps peut être modélisé par un processus stochastique.<br>中：股票价格随时间变化可建模为随机过程。                                 |
| `T`                                   | index set, ensemble d’indices, 指标集                                 | The set of times or parameters indexing a stochastic process.                                                          | EN: `T=\mathbb{N}` gives discrete time; `T=[0,\infty)` gives continuous time.<br>FR: `T=\mathbb{N}` donne le temps discret ; `T=[0,\infty)` donne le temps continu.<br>中：`T=\mathbb{N}` 表示离散时间；`T=[0,\infty)` 表示连续时间。 |
| `X_t`                                 | state at time t, état au temps t, t 时刻状态                           | The random variable describing the process value at index `t`.                                                         | EN: `X_t` may represent a queue length at time `t`.<br>FR: `X_t` peut représenter la longueur d’une file au temps `t`.<br>中：`X_t` 可表示 `t` 时刻的队列长度。                                                                    |
| `E`                                   | state space, espace d’états, 状态空间                                  | The set of possible values of a stochastic process.                                                                    | EN: A weather chain may have state space `{sunny,rainy}`.<br>FR: Une chaîne météo peut avoir pour espace d’états `{ensoleillé, pluvieux}`.<br>中：天气链的状态空间可为 `{晴, 雨}`。                                                  |
| `\mathcal{F}_t`                       | filtration, filtration, 滤过                                         | An increasing family of sigma-algebras representing information available up to time `t`.                              | EN: `\mathcal{F}_t` encodes what is known by time `t`.<br>FR: `\mathcal{F}_t` encode ce qui est connu au temps `t`.<br>中：`\mathcal{F}_t` 编码截至 `t` 时刻已知的信息。                                                            |
| `\mathcal{F}_s\subseteq\mathcal{F}_t` | increasing information, information croissante, 信息递增               | Filtrations grow with time: later information contains earlier information.                                            | EN: Future observations contain past observations.<br>FR: Les observations futures contiennent les observations passées.<br>中：未来观测包含过去观测。                                                                             |
| `(X_t)` adapted                       | adapted process, processus adapté, 适应过程                            | A process where `X_t` is measurable with respect to `\mathcal{F}_t`.                                                   | EN: An adapted trading strategy uses only current and past information.<br>FR: Une stratégie de trading adaptée utilise seulement l’information présente et passée.<br>中：适应的交易策略只使用当前与过去信息。                           |
| —                                     | predictable process, processus prévisible, 可预测过程                   | A process whose value is determined by information just before the current time, under the relevant formal definition. | EN: Integrands in stochastic integration are often required to be predictable.<br>FR: Les intégrands en intégration stochastique doivent souvent être prévisibles.<br>中：随机积分中的被积过程通常要求可预测。                            |
| —                                     | sample path, trajectoire, 样本路径                                     | The function of time obtained by fixing one outcome `\omega`.                                                          | EN: A Brownian sample path is continuous but highly irregular.<br>FR: Une trajectoire brownienne est continue mais très irrégulière.<br>中：Brownian 样本路径连续但极不规则。                                                       |
| —                                     | finite-dimensional distributions, lois fini-dimensionnelles, 有限维分布 | The joint laws of `(X_{t_1},\ldots,X_{t_n})` for finite collections of times.                                          | EN: A process is partly specified by its finite-dimensional distributions.<br>FR: Un processus est en partie spécifié par ses lois fini-dimensionnelles.<br>中：随机过程可由有限维分布部分刻画。                                        |
| —                                     | modification, modification, 修正版本                                   | A process equal to another process at each fixed time almost surely.                                                   | EN: Two modifications may differ on path properties.<br>FR: Deux modifications peuvent différer par leurs propriétés de trajectoires.<br>中：两个修正版本可能在路径性质上不同。                                                          |
| —                                     | indistinguishable processes, processus indistinguables, 不可区分过程     | Two processes whose sample paths agree for all times outside a null event.                                             | EN: Indistinguishability is stronger than being modifications.<br>FR: L’indistinguabilité est plus forte que la modification.<br>中：不可区分性强于逐时刻几乎处处相等。                                                                  |

## 21.7 Martingales, stopping times, and optional sampling

| Symbol (LaTeX)                         | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                              | Definition                                                                                                                    | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |
