| —                    | test set, ensemble de test, 测试集                                               | Data held out for final evaluation of predictive performance.                                     | EN: The test set should not be used for model tuning.<br>FR: L’ensemble de test ne doit pas être utilisé pour ajuster le modèle.<br>中：测试集不应参与模型调参。                                                                                                                                   |
| —                    | cross-validation, validation croisée, 交叉验证                                    | A resampling method estimating prediction performance by repeated training and validation splits. | EN: Cross-validation helps choose models when data are limited.<br>FR: La validation croisée aide à choisir des modèles lorsque les données sont limitées.<br>中：数据有限时，交叉验证有助于模型选择。                                                                                                   |
| `K`-fold CV          | K-fold cross-validation, validation croisée en K blocs, K 折交叉验证               | Cross-validation dividing data into `K` folds and rotating the validation fold.                   | EN: `5`-fold or `10`-fold cross-validation is common.<br>FR: La validation croisée à `5` ou `10` blocs est courante.<br>中：`5` 折或 `10` 折交叉验证很常见。                                                                                                                                      |
| —                    | leave-one-out cross-validation, validation croisée leave-one-out, 留一交叉验证      | Cross-validation using one observation as validation data at a time.                              | EN: Leave-one-out can be computationally expensive for complex models.<br>FR: Le leave-one-out peut être coûteux pour des modèles complexes.<br>中：对复杂模型，留一交叉验证可能计算代价很高。                                                                                                              |
| `\operatorname{AIC}` | Akaike information criterion, critère d’information d’Akaike, Akaike 信息准则     | A model selection criterion balancing likelihood fit and model complexity.                        | EN: Lower `\operatorname{AIC}` is often preferred among candidate models.<br>FR: Un `\operatorname{AIC}` plus faible est souvent préféré parmi les modèles candidats.<br>中：在候选模型中，较低的 `\operatorname{AIC}` 通常更优。                                                                     |
| `\operatorname{BIC}` | Bayesian information criterion, critère d’information bayésien, Bayesian 信息准则 | A model selection criterion with a stronger sample-size-dependent penalty for complexity.         | EN: `\operatorname{BIC}` often favors simpler models than `\operatorname{AIC}` in large samples.<br>FR: Le `\operatorname{BIC}` favorise souvent des modèles plus simples que l’`AIC` dans les grands échantillons.<br>中：大样本下，`\operatorname{BIC}` 往往比 `\operatorname{AIC}` 更偏向简单模型。 |
| —                    | overfitting, surapprentissage, 过拟合                                            | A model fits training data too closely and performs poorly on new data.                           | EN: A very flexible model may overfit noise.<br>FR: Un modèle très flexible peut surapprendre le bruit.<br>中：过于灵活的模型可能拟合噪声。                                                                                                                                                          |
| —                    | underfitting, sous-apprentissage, 欠拟合                                         | A model is too simple to capture important structure in the data.                                 | EN: A linear model may underfit a strongly nonlinear relationship.<br>FR: Un modèle linéaire peut sous-apprendre une relation fortement non linéaire.<br>中：线性模型可能欠拟合强非线性关系。                                                                                                          |
| —                    | data leakage, fuite de données, 数据泄漏                                          | Improper use of information from validation or test data during training.                         | EN: Data leakage makes performance estimates overly optimistic.<br>FR: La fuite de données rend les estimations de performance trop optimistes.<br>中：数据泄漏会使性能评估过于乐观。                                                                                                                 |

## 22.8 Statistical learning theory and empirical risk

| Symbol (LaTeX)                                         | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                      | Definition                                                                                      | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| ------------------------------------------------------ | ---------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `\mathcal{H}`                                          | hypothesis class, classe d’hypothèses, 假设类                             | A set of candidate functions considered by a learning algorithm.                                | EN: Linear classifiers form a hypothesis class.<br>FR: Les classificateurs linéaires forment une classe d’hypothèses.<br>中：线性分类器构成一个假设类。                                                                                |
| `h\in\mathcal{H}`                                      | hypothesis, hypothèse, 假设函数                                            | A candidate prediction function in a hypothesis class.                                          | EN: A classifier `h` maps features to labels.<br>FR: Un classificateur `h` envoie des variables vers des étiquettes.<br>中：分类器 `h` 把特征映射到标签。                                                                             |
| `\ell(h(X),Y)`                                         | loss function, fonction de perte, 损失函数                                 | A function measuring prediction error for one example.                                          | EN: Squared loss penalizes large errors more strongly.<br>FR: La perte quadratique pénalise plus fortement les grandes erreurs.<br>中：平方损失对大误差惩罚更强。                                                                      |
| `R(h)=\mathbb{E}[\ell(h(X),Y)]`                        | risk, risque, 风险                                                       | The expected loss of a prediction rule under the data distribution.                             | EN: The true risk is usually unknown because the distribution is unknown.<br>FR: Le vrai risque est généralement inconnu car la distribution est inconnue.<br>中：由于数据分布未知，真实风险通常未知。                                      |
| `\hat{R}_n(h)=\frac{1}{n}\sum_{i=1}^n\ell(h(X_i),Y_i)` | empirical risk, risque empirique, 经验风险                                 | The average loss on the observed sample.                                                        | EN: Training often minimizes empirical risk.<br>FR: L’entraînement minimise souvent le risque empirique.<br>中：训练通常最小化经验风险。                                                                                              |
| `\hat{h}\in\arg\min_{h\in\mathcal{H}}\hat{R}_n(h)`     | empirical risk minimization, minimisation du risque empirique, 经验风险最小化 | A learning principle choosing a hypothesis with minimal sample loss.                            | EN: Least squares is an example of empirical risk minimization.<br>FR: Les moindres carrés sont un exemple de minimisation du risque empirique.<br>中：最小二乘是经验风险最小化的例子。                                                   |
| `R(\hat{h})-R(h^*)`                                    | excess risk, excès de risque, 超额风险                                     | The additional risk of a learned rule compared with an optimal benchmark.                       | EN: Learning theory often bounds excess risk.<br>FR: La théorie de l’apprentissage borne souvent l’excès de risque.<br>中：学习理论常研究超额风险界。                                                                                  |
| —                                                      | generalization, généralisation, 泛化                                     | The ability of a model trained on sample data to perform well on new data.                      | EN: Low training error does not guarantee good generalization.<br>FR: Une faible erreur d’entraînement ne garantit pas une bonne généralisation.<br>中：低训练误差不保证良好泛化。                                                     |
| —                                                      | generalization error, erreur de généralisation, 泛化误差                   | The gap between training performance and performance on new data, or the true prediction error. | EN: Cross-validation estimates generalization error.<br>FR: La validation croisée estime l’erreur de généralisation.<br>中：交叉验证估计泛化误差。                                                                                   |
| —                                                      | uniform convergence, convergence uniforme, 一致收敛                        | A property where empirical risk approximates true risk uniformly over a function class.         | EN: Uniform convergence supports empirical risk minimization.<br>FR: La convergence uniforme soutient la minimisation du risque empirique.<br>中：一致收敛支撑经验风险最小化。                                                          |
| `\operatorname{VCdim}(\mathcal{H})`                    | VC dimension, dimension VC, VC 维                                       | A combinatorial measure of the capacity of a hypothesis class.                                  | EN: Finite VC dimension can imply learnability under standard settings.<br>FR: Une dimension VC finie peut impliquer l’apprenabilité dans des cadres standards.<br>中：在标准情形下，有限 VC 维可推出可学习性。                             |
| —                                                      | PAC learning, apprentissage PAC, PAC 学习                                | A framework requiring probably approximately correct learning from samples.                     | EN: PAC learning formalizes sample complexity for supervised learning.<br>FR: L’apprentissage PAC formalise la complexité en échantillons de l’apprentissage supervisé.<br>中：PAC 学习形式化监督学习中的样本复杂度。                      |
| `\mathfrak{R}_n(\mathcal{H})`                          | Rademacher complexity, complexité de Rademacher, Rademacher 复杂度        | A data-dependent measure of how well a function class can fit random noise.                     | EN: Lower Rademacher complexity often supports better generalization bounds.<br>FR: Une complexité de Rademacher plus faible soutient souvent de meilleures bornes de généralisation.<br>中：较低 Rademacher 复杂度通常对应更好的泛化界。 |

## 22.9 Classification, evaluation metrics, and decision thresholds

| Symbol (LaTeX)              | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                | Definition                                                                    | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                         |
| --------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------ | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `Y\in\{0,1\}`               | binary label, étiquette binaire, 二分类标签           | A response variable taking one of two class labels.                           | EN: Spam detection uses binary labels.<br>FR: La détection de spam utilise des étiquettes binaires.<br>中：垃圾邮件检测使用二分类标签。                                                                          |
| `\hat{Y}`                   | predicted label, étiquette prédite, 预测标签         | The class label output by a classifier.                                       | EN: A classifier predicts `\hat{Y}=1` for a positive case.<br>FR: Un classificateur prédit `\hat{Y}=1` pour un cas positif.<br>中：分类器对正类样本预测 `\hat{Y}=1`。                                         |
| `P(Y=1\mid X=x)`            | class probability, probabilité de classe, 类别概率   | The conditional probability of a class given features.                        | EN: Probabilistic classifiers estimate `P(Y=1\mid X=x)`.<br>FR: Les classificateurs probabilistes estiment `P(Y=1\mid X=x)`.<br>中：概率分类器估计 `P(Y=1\mid X=x)`。                                      |
| —                           | decision threshold, seuil de décision, 决策阈值      | A cutoff used to convert probabilities or scores into class labels.           | EN: Changing the threshold changes precision and recall.<br>FR: Changer le seuil modifie la précision et le rappel.<br>中：改变阈值会改变精确率和召回率。                                                         |
| —                           | confusion matrix, matrice de confusion, 混淆矩阵     | A table comparing true labels and predicted labels.                           | EN: A confusion matrix summarizes classification errors.<br>FR: Une matrice de confusion résume les erreurs de classification.<br>中：混淆矩阵概括分类错误。                                                  |
| `TP`                        | true positive, vrai positif, 真阳性                 | A positive instance correctly predicted as positive.                          | EN: A correctly detected disease case is a true positive.<br>FR: Un cas de maladie correctement détecté est un vrai positif.<br>中：被正确检出的患病样本是真阳性。                                                |
| `FP`                        | false positive, faux positif, 假阳性                | A negative instance incorrectly predicted as positive.                        | EN: A false alarm is a false positive.<br>FR: Une fausse alerte est un faux positif.<br>中：误报是假阳性。                                                                                                |
| `FN`                        | false negative, faux négatif, 假阴性                | A positive instance incorrectly predicted as negative.                        | EN: A missed disease case is a false negative.<br>FR: Un cas de maladie manqué est un faux négatif.<br>中：漏检患病样本是假阴性。                                                                             |
| `TN`                        | true negative, vrai négatif, 真阴性                 | A negative instance correctly predicted as negative.                          | EN: Correctly identifying a healthy case is a true negative.<br>FR: Identifier correctement un cas sain est un vrai négatif.<br>中：正确识别健康样本是真阴性。                                                  |
| `\frac{TP+TN}{TP+FP+FN+TN}` | accuracy, exactitude, 准确率                        | The proportion of correct predictions among all predictions.                  | EN: Accuracy can be misleading with imbalanced classes.<br>FR: L’exactitude peut être trompeuse avec des classes déséquilibrées.<br>中：类别不平衡时，准确率可能误导。                                            |
| `\frac{TP}{TP+FP}`          | precision, précision, 精确率                        | The proportion of predicted positives that are truly positive.                | EN: High precision means few false positives.<br>FR: Une précision élevée signifie peu de faux positifs.<br>中：高精确率表示假阳性较少。                                                                       |
| `\frac{TP}{TP+FN}`          | recall, rappel, 召回率                              | The proportion of actual positives correctly identified.                      | EN: High recall means few false negatives.<br>FR: Un rappel élevé signifie peu de faux négatifs.<br>中：高召回率表示假阴性较少。                                                                               |
| `F_1`                       | F1 score, score F1, F1 分数                        | The harmonic mean of precision and recall.                                    | EN: `F_1` balances precision and recall.<br>FR: Le score `F_1` équilibre précision et rappel.<br>中：`F_1` 平衡精确率与召回率。                                                                              |
| `\operatorname{AUC}`        | area under the curve, aire sous la courbe, 曲线下面积 | A threshold-independent summary of ranking performance, often for ROC curves. | EN: AUC evaluates how well scores rank positive cases above negative cases.<br>FR: L’AUC évalue la capacité des scores à classer les positifs au-dessus des négatifs.<br>中：AUC 衡量分数把正类排在负类前面的能力。 |

## 22.10 Regularization, high-dimensional statistics, and sparsity

| Symbol (LaTeX)                                         | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                                                 | Definition                                                                                   | Examples                                                                                                                                                                            |
| ------------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `\lambda`                                              | regularization parameter, paramètre de régularisation, 正则化参数                                      | A parameter controlling the strength of a penalty or constraint.                             | EN: Larger `\lambda` usually imposes stronger regularization.<br>FR: Un plus grand `\lambda` impose généralement une régularisation plus forte.<br>中：较大的 `\lambda` 通常表示更强正则化。       |
| `\Omega(\theta)`                                       | regularization penalty, pénalité de régularisation, 正则化惩罚项                                        | A penalty added to an objective to control model complexity.                                 | EN: Regularization penalties discourage overly complex models.<br>FR: Les pénalités de régularisation découragent les modèles trop complexes.<br>中：正则化惩罚项抑制过度复杂模型。                  |
| `\min_{\theta}\hat{R}_n(\theta)+\lambda\Omega(\theta)` | regularized empirical risk minimization, minimisation du risque empirique régularisée, 正则化经验风险最小化 | Empirical risk minimization with an added complexity penalty.                                | EN: Regularized objectives balance fit and simplicity.<br>FR: Les objectifs régularisés équilibrent ajustement et simplicité.<br>中：正则化目标在拟合度与简单性之间平衡。                               |
| `\lVert\beta\rVert_2^2`                                | L2 penalty, pénalité L2, L2 惩罚                                                                    | A squared Euclidean norm penalty on parameters.                                              | EN: Ridge regression uses an `L^2` penalty.<br>FR: La régression ridge utilise une pénalité `L^2`.<br>中：岭回归使用 `L^2` 惩罚。                                                             |
| `\lVert\beta\rVert_1`                                  | L1 penalty, pénalité L1, L1 惩罚                                                                    | A sum-of-absolute-values penalty encouraging sparsity.                                       | EN: Lasso uses an `L^1` penalty.<br>FR: Le lasso utilise une pénalité `L^1`.<br>中：Lasso 使用 `L^1` 惩罚。                                                                                |
| —                                                      | ridge regression, régression ridge, 岭回归                                                           | Linear regression with an L2 penalty on coefficients.                                        | EN: Ridge regression stabilizes estimates under multicollinearity.<br>FR: La régression ridge stabilise les estimations en présence de multicolinéarité.<br>中：岭回归在多重共线性下稳定估计。       |
| —                                                      | lasso, lasso, Lasso 回归                                                                            | Linear regression with an L1 penalty, often producing sparse coefficients.                   | EN: Lasso can perform variable selection.<br>FR: Le lasso peut effectuer une sélection de variables.<br>中：Lasso 可进行变量选择。                                                            |
| —                                                      | sparsity, parcimonie / sparsité, 稀疏性                                                              | A property where only a small number of components are nonzero or important.                 | EN: Sparse models are easier to interpret in high dimensions.<br>FR: Les modèles parcimonieux sont plus faciles à interpréter en grande dimension.<br>中：高维中稀疏模型更易解释。                |
| `p\gg n`                                               | high-dimensional regime, régime de grande dimension, 高维情形                                         | A setting where the number of variables is much larger than the sample size.                 | EN: Genomics often has `p\gg n`.<br>FR: La génomique a souvent `p\gg n`.<br>中：基因组学中常出现 `p\gg n`。                                                                                    |
| —                                                      | curse of dimensionality, fléau de la dimension, 维数灾难                                              | The difficulty caused by volume, sparsity, and complexity increasing rapidly with dimension. | EN: Nearest-neighbor methods can degrade in high dimensions.<br>FR: Les méthodes des plus proches voisins peuvent se dégrader en grande dimension.<br>中：最近邻方法在高维中可能退化。              |
| —                                                      | feature selection, sélection de variables, 特征选择                                                   | The process of choosing relevant variables for modeling or prediction.                       | EN: Feature selection can reduce noise and improve interpretability.<br>FR: La sélection de variables peut réduire le bruit et améliorer l’interprétabilité.<br>中：特征选择可降低噪声并提高可解释性。 |
| —                                                      | shrinkage, rétrécissement, 收缩估计                                                                   | Estimation that pulls coefficients toward zero or another target to reduce variance.         | EN: Shrinkage can lower prediction error despite adding bias.<br>FR: Le rétrécissement peut réduire l’erreur de prédiction malgré l’ajout de biais.<br>中：收缩估计虽引入偏差，却可能降低预测误差。       |

## 22.11 Causal inference and treatment effects

| Symbol (LaTeX)          | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                                            | Definition                                                                                                      | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                         |
| ----------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `A`                     | treatment, traitement, 处理 / 干预                                                               | A variable representing an intervention, exposure, or assigned condition.                                       | EN: A drug dose can be a treatment variable.<br>FR: Une dose de médicament peut être une variable de traitement.<br>中：药物剂量可以是处理变量。                                                                               |
| `Y`                     | outcome, résultat, 结果变量                                                                      | The variable whose causal response is studied.                                                                  | EN: Blood pressure after medication is an outcome.<br>FR: La pression artérielle après médicament est un résultat.<br>中：服药后的血压是结果变量。                                                                             |
| `Y(1),Y(0)`             | potential outcomes, résultats potentiels, 潜在结果                                               | The outcomes that would occur under treatment and control conditions.                                           | EN: Causal effects compare potential outcomes for the same unit.<br>FR: Les effets causaux comparent les résultats potentiels pour la même unité.<br>中：因果效应比较同一单位在不同处理下的潜在结果。                                    |
| `Y(1)-Y(0)`             | individual treatment effect, effet individuel du traitement, 个体处理效应                          | The difference between a unit’s potential outcomes under treatment and control.                                 | EN: Individual treatment effects are usually not directly observable.<br>FR: Les effets individuels du traitement ne sont généralement pas directement observables.<br>中：个体处理效应通常不能被直接观测。                        |
| `\mathbb{E}[Y(1)-Y(0)]` | average treatment effect, effet moyen du traitement, 平均处理效应                                  | The expected causal effect in a population.                                                                     | EN: A randomized trial often estimates the average treatment effect.<br>FR: Un essai randomisé estime souvent l’effet moyen du traitement.<br>中：随机试验常估计平均处理效应。                                                   |
| —                       | randomized controlled trial, essai contrôlé randomisé, 随机对照试验                                | A study design assigning treatment randomly to support causal inference.                                        | EN: Randomization helps balance confounders on average.<br>FR: La randomisation aide à équilibrer les facteurs de confusion en moyenne.<br>中：随机化有助于平均平衡混杂因素。                                                     |
| —                       | confounder, facteur de confusion, 混杂变量                                                       | A variable associated with both treatment and outcome that can bias causal estimates.                           | EN: Age can confound the relation between treatment and health outcome.<br>FR: L’âge peut confondre la relation entre traitement et résultat de santé.<br>中：年龄可能混杂处理与健康结果之间的关系。                                  |
| `do(A=a)`               | intervention operator, opérateur d’intervention, 干预算子                                        | Pearl’s notation for setting a treatment variable by intervention rather than conditioning.                     | EN: `P(Y\mid do(A=a))` is causal, not merely observational.<br>FR: `P(Y\mid do(A=a))` est causal, non simplement observationnel.<br>中：`P(Y\mid do(A=a))` 是因果量，而非单纯观测条件概率。                                        |
| `P(Y\mid A=a)`          | observational conditional distribution, distribution conditionnelle observationnelle, 观测条件分布 | The distribution of `Y` among units observed with treatment value `a`.                                          | EN: `P(Y\mid A=a)` can differ from `P(Y\mid do(A=a))` under confounding.<br>FR: `P(Y\mid A=a)` peut différer de `P(Y\mid do(A=a))` en présence de confusion.<br>中：存在混杂时，`P(Y\mid A=a)` 可能不同于 `P(Y\mid do(A=a))`。 |
| —                       | ignorability, ignorabilité, 可忽略性                                                             | An assumption that treatment assignment is independent of potential outcomes after conditioning on covariates.  | EN: Ignorability supports adjustment-based causal estimation.<br>FR: L’ignorabilité soutient l’estimation causale par ajustement.<br>中：可忽略性支撑基于调整的因果估计。                                                          |
| —                       | propensity score, score de propension, 倾向得分                                                  | The probability of receiving treatment given observed covariates.                                               | EN: Propensity scores help balance treated and control groups.<br>FR: Les scores de propension aident à équilibrer groupes traités et témoins.<br>中：倾向得分有助于平衡处理组与对照组。                                            |
| —                       | instrumental variable, variable instrumentale, 工具变量                                          | A variable that affects treatment but influences the outcome only through treatment under specific assumptions. | EN: Instrumental variables can address unobserved confounding in some settings.<br>FR: Les variables instrumentales peuvent traiter la confusion non observée dans certains cadres.<br>中：在某些情形下，工具变量可处理未观测混杂。    |

## 22.12 Graphical models, latent variables, and probabilistic modeling

| Symbol (LaTeX)                                               | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                                 | Definition                                                                                               | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| ------------------------------------------------------------ | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `G=(V,E)`                                                    | probabilistic graphical model, modèle graphique probabiliste, 概率图模型               | A graph-based representation of dependencies among random variables.                                     | EN: Graphical models encode conditional independence structure.<br>FR: Les modèles graphiques encodent des structures d’indépendance conditionnelle.<br>中：概率图模型编码条件独立结构。                                                        |
| `X_v`                                                        | node random variable, variable aléatoire de nœud, 节点随机变量                          | A random variable associated with a graph node.                                                          | EN: In a medical graph, a node may represent a symptom.<br>FR: Dans un graphe médical, un nœud peut représenter un symptôme.<br>中：在医学图中，一个节点可表示症状。                                                                              |
| —                                                            | Bayesian network, réseau bayésien, Bayesian 网络                                    | A directed acyclic graphical model factorizing a joint distribution by parent-child relations.           | EN: Bayesian networks model directed probabilistic dependence.<br>FR: Les réseaux bayésiens modélisent une dépendance probabiliste dirigée.<br>中：Bayesian 网络建模有向概率依赖。                                                           |
| `P(X_1,\ldots,X_d)=\prod_iP(X_i\mid \operatorname{Pa}(X_i))` | Bayesian network factorization, factorisation d’un réseau bayésien, Bayesian 网络分解 | A product representation of a joint distribution using parents in a directed graph.                      | EN: Factorization reduces a large joint model into local conditional models.<br>FR: La factorisation réduit un grand modèle conjoint en modèles conditionnels locaux.<br>中：该分解把大型联合模型化为局部条件模型。                                  |
| `\operatorname{Pa}(X_i)`                                     | parents of a node, parents d’un nœud, 父节点                                         | Variables with directed edges into a given node.                                                         | EN: Parent variables directly condition the node distribution.<br>FR: Les variables parentes conditionnent directement la distribution du nœud.<br>中：父节点变量直接条件化该节点分布。                                                           |
| —                                                            | Markov random field, champ aléatoire de Markov, Markov 随机场                        | An undirected graphical model representing local dependence structure.                                   | EN: Markov random fields are used in spatial statistics and image models.<br>FR: Les champs aléatoires de Markov sont utilisés en statistique spatiale et modèles d’image.<br>中：Markov 随机场用于空间统计与图像模型。                          |
| —                                                            | factor graph, graphe factoriel, 因子图                                               | A bipartite graph representing how a joint distribution factors into local functions.                    | EN: Factor graphs clarify message-passing algorithms.<br>FR: Les graphes factoriels clarifient les algorithmes de passage de messages.<br>中：因子图有助于理解消息传递算法。                                                                     |
| `Z`                                                          | latent variable, variable latente, 潜变量                                            | An unobserved variable introduced to explain observed data structure.                                    | EN: Topic models use latent variables for hidden topics.<br>FR: Les modèles thématiques utilisent des variables latentes pour les sujets cachés.<br>中：主题模型用潜变量表示隐藏主题。                                                           |
| —                                                            | mixture model, modèle de mélange, 混合模型                                            | A model representing data as coming from several component distributions.                                | EN: Gaussian mixture models cluster data through latent components.<br>FR: Les mélanges gaussiens regroupent les données par composantes latentes.<br>中：Gaussian 混合模型通过潜在成分聚类数据。                                                |
| `\sum_z P(X,z)`                                              | marginalization, marginalisation, 边缘化                                             | Summing or integrating out hidden variables to obtain a distribution over observed variables.            | EN: Latent-variable models require marginalizing over hidden states.<br>FR: Les modèles à variables latentes exigent de marginaliser les états cachés.<br>中：潜变量模型需要对隐藏状态边缘化。                                                    |
| —                                                            | expectation-maximization algorithm, algorithme espérance-maximisation, EM 算法      | An iterative algorithm for maximum likelihood estimation with latent variables.                          | EN: EM alternates between estimating latent structure and updating parameters.<br>FR: L’EM alterne entre estimation de la structure latente et mise à jour des paramètres.<br>中：EM 在估计潜在结构与更新参数之间交替。                            |
| —                                                            | variational inference, inférence variationnelle, 变分推断                             | An approximate Bayesian inference method replacing a difficult posterior with a tractable approximation. | EN: Variational inference turns posterior approximation into optimization.<br>FR: L’inférence variationnelle transforme l’approximation a posteriori en optimisation.<br>中：变分推断把后验近似转化为优化问题。                                    |
| —                                                            | Markov chain Monte Carlo, Monte-Carlo par chaînes de Markov, Markov 链 Monte Carlo | A family of sampling methods using Markov chains to approximate target distributions.                    | EN: MCMC is often used when posterior distributions are hard to compute directly.<br>FR: Le MCMC est souvent utilisé lorsque les distributions a posteriori sont difficiles à calculer directement.<br>中：当后验分布难以直接计算时，常使用 MCMC。 |
# 23. Optimization, Convex Analysis, and Variational Methods

## 23.1 Optimization problems and objective functions

| Symbol (LaTeX)                        | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                     | Definition                                                                                        | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| ------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `\min_{x\in X} f(x)`                  | minimization problem, problème de minimisation, 最小化问题 | A problem of finding a point in `X` where the objective function has the smallest possible value. | EN: `\min_x (x-2)^2` has minimizer `x=2`.<br>FR: `\min_x (x-2)^2` a pour minimiseur `x=2`.<br>中：`\min_x (x-2)^2` 的极小点是 `x=2`。                                                              |
| `\max_{x\in X} f(x)`                  | maximization problem, problème de maximisation, 最大化问题 | A problem of finding a point in `X` where the objective function has the largest possible value.  | EN: Maximizing profit is a standard optimization problem.<br>FR: Maximiser le profit est un problème d’optimisation standard.<br>中：利润最大化是标准优化问题。                                           |
| `f(x)`                                | objective function, fonction objectif, 目标函数           | The function whose value is minimized or maximized.                                               | EN: In least squares, the objective is the sum of squared residuals.<br>FR: En moindres carrés, la fonction objectif est la somme des résidus carrés.<br>中：在最小二乘中，目标函数是残差平方和。              |
| `x`                                   | decision variable, variable de décision, 决策变量         | The variable chosen to optimize the objective.                                                    | EN: In portfolio optimization, asset weights are decision variables.<br>FR: En optimisation de portefeuille, les poids des actifs sont des variables de décision.<br>中：在投资组合优化中，资产权重是决策变量。 |
| `X`                                   | feasible set, ensemble admissible, 可行集                | The set of points allowed by the constraints of an optimization problem.                          | EN: If `x\geq0`, the feasible set is `[0,\infty)`.<br>FR: Si `x\geq0`, l’ensemble admissible est `[0,\infty)`.<br>中：若 `x\geq0`，可行集是 `[0,\infty)`。                                          |
| `x^\star`                             | optimal solution, solution optimale, 最优解              | A feasible point attaining the optimal objective value.                                           | EN: For `f(x)=(x-1)^2`, the optimal solution is `x^\star=1`.<br>FR: Pour `f(x)=(x-1)^2`, la solution optimale est `x^\star=1`.<br>中：对 `f(x)=(x-1)^2`，最优解是 `x^\star=1`。                     |
| `p^\star`                             | optimal value, valeur optimale, 最优值                   | The value of the objective function at an optimal solution.                                       | EN: For `\min_x (x-1)^2`, the optimal value is `0`.<br>FR: Pour `\min_x (x-1)^2`, la valeur optimale est `0`.<br>中：对 `\min_x (x-1)^2`，最优值是 `0`。                                            |
| `\operatorname{argmin}_{x\in X} f(x)` | set of minimizers, ensemble des minimiseurs, 极小点集合    | The set of feasible points at which the minimum is attained.                                      | EN: `\operatorname{argmin}_{x\in\mathbb{R}} x^2=\{0\}`.<br>FR: `\operatorname{argmin}_{x\in\mathbb{R}} x^2=\{0\}`.<br>中：`\operatorname{argmin}_{x\in\mathbb{R}} x^2=\{0\}`。                |
| `\operatorname{argmax}_{x\in X} f(x)` | set of maximizers, ensemble des maximiseurs, 极大点集合    | The set of feasible points at which the maximum is attained.                                      | EN: `\operatorname{argmax}` may contain more than one point.<br>FR: `\operatorname{argmax}` peut contenir plusieurs points.<br>中：`\operatorname{argmax}` 可能包含多个点。                          |
| —                                     | feasible solution, solution admissible, 可行解           | A point satisfying all constraints of an optimization problem.                                    | EN: A feasible solution need not be optimal.<br>FR: Une solution admissible n’est pas nécessairement optimale.<br>中：可行解不一定是最优解。                                                            |
| —                                     | infeasible problem, problème infaisable, 不可行问题        | An optimization problem whose constraints have no common solution.                                | EN: The constraints `x\geq1` and `x\leq0` make a problem infeasible.<br>FR: Les contraintes `x\geq1` et `x\leq0` rendent un problème infaisable.<br>中：约束 `x\geq1` 与 `x\leq0` 使问题不可行。       |
| —                                     | unbounded problem, problème non borné, 无界问题           | An optimization problem whose objective can improve without finite limit.                         | EN: `\min_x x` over `\mathbb{R}` is unbounded below.<br>FR: `\min_x x` sur `\mathbb{R}` est non borné inférieurement.<br>中：在 `\mathbb{R}` 上，`\min_x x` 下无界。                                |

## 23.2 Constraints, feasible regions, and problem classes

| Symbol (LaTeX)                 | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                         | Definition                                                                                     | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| ------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `g_i(x)\leq0`                  | inequality constraint, contrainte d’inégalité, 不等式约束                      | A constraint requiring a function to be nonpositive.                                           | EN: `x^2-1\leq0` restricts `x` to `[-1,1]`.<br>FR: `x^2-1\leq0` restreint `x` à `[-1,1]`.<br>中：`x^2-1\leq0` 把 `x` 限制在 `[-1,1]`。                                                                           |
| `h_j(x)=0`                     | equality constraint, contrainte d’égalité, 等式约束                           | A constraint requiring a function to equal zero.                                               | EN: `x+y-1=0` restricts points to a line.<br>FR: `x+y-1=0` restreint les points à une droite.<br>中：`x+y-1=0` 把点限制在一条直线上。                                                                                  |
| `\{x:g_i(x)\leq0,\ h_j(x)=0\}` | constraint set, ensemble de contraintes, 约束集                              | The set of all points satisfying the specified constraints.                                    | EN: Optimization is often the search for the best point in a constraint set.<br>FR: L’optimisation cherche souvent le meilleur point dans un ensemble de contraintes.<br>中：优化通常是在约束集中寻找最优点。               |
| —                              | active constraint, contrainte active, 起作用约束                               | An inequality constraint satisfied with equality at a given feasible point.                    | EN: At `x=1`, the constraint `x\leq1` is active.<br>FR: En `x=1`, la contrainte `x\leq1` est active.<br>中：在 `x=1` 处，约束 `x\leq1` 是起作用约束。                                                                   |
| —                              | inactive constraint, contrainte inactive, 非起作用约束                          | An inequality constraint satisfied strictly at a given feasible point.                         | EN: At `x=0`, the constraint `x\leq1` is inactive.<br>FR: En `x=0`, la contrainte `x\leq1` est inactive.<br>中：在 `x=0` 处，约束 `x\leq1` 是非起作用约束。                                                              |
| —                              | unconstrained optimization, optimisation sans contraintes, 无约束优化          | Optimization without explicit restrictions on the decision variable beyond its ambient space.  | EN: Minimizing a smooth function over `\mathbb{R}^n` is often unconstrained.<br>FR: Minimiser une fonction lisse sur `\mathbb{R}^n` est souvent sans contraintes.<br>中：在 `\mathbb{R}^n` 上最小化光滑函数通常是无约束优化。 |
| —                              | constrained optimization, optimisation sous contraintes, 约束优化             | Optimization with equality, inequality, set, or structural constraints.                        | EN: Budget limits create constrained optimization problems.<br>FR: Les limites budgétaires créent des problèmes d’optimisation sous contraintes.<br>中：预算限制产生约束优化问题。                                       |
| `\min c^Tx`                    | linear objective, objectif linéaire, 线性目标                                 | An objective function linear in the decision variable.                                         | EN: Linear objectives appear in linear programming.<br>FR: Les objectifs linéaires apparaissent en programmation linéaire.<br>中：线性目标出现在线性规划中。                                                             |
| —                              | linear programming, programmation linéaire, 线性规划                          | Optimization of a linear objective subject to linear equality and inequality constraints.      | EN: Resource allocation is often modeled by linear programming.<br>FR: L’allocation de ressources est souvent modélisée par programmation linéaire.<br>中：资源分配常用线性规划建模。                                    |
| —                              | quadratic programming, programmation quadratique, 二次规划                    | Optimization of a quadratic objective subject to linear or convex constraints in common forms. | EN: Support vector machines lead to quadratic programming problems.<br>FR: Les machines à vecteurs de support mènent à des problèmes quadratiques.<br>中：支持向量机会导向二次规划问题。                                   |
| —                              | nonlinear programming, programmation non linéaire, 非线性规划                  | Optimization involving nonlinear objectives or nonlinear constraints.                          | EN: Many engineering design problems are nonlinear programs.<br>FR: Beaucoup de problèmes de conception d’ingénierie sont des programmes non linéaires.<br>中：许多工程设计问题是非线性规划。                              |
| —                              | integer programming, programmation en nombres entiers, 整数规划               | Optimization where some or all variables are constrained to take integer values.               | EN: Scheduling with yes-or-no decisions often becomes integer programming.<br>FR: L’ordonnancement avec décisions oui-non devient souvent une programmation entière.<br>中：含是非决策的调度问题常变成整数规划。              |
| —                              | mixed-integer programming, programmation mixte en nombres entiers, 混合整数规划 | Optimization with both continuous and integer variables.                                       | EN: Facility location can be modeled as mixed-integer programming.<br>FR: La localisation d’installations peut être modélisée par programmation mixte entière.<br>中：设施选址可建模为混合整数规划。                       |

## 23.3 Convex sets and geometric language

| Symbol (LaTeX)             | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                  | Definition                                                                             | Examples                                                                                                                                                                              |
| -------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `C` convex                 | convex set, ensemble convexe, 凸集                   | A set containing the line segment between any two of its points.                       | EN: Intervals in `\mathbb{R}` are convex.<br>FR: Les intervalles de `\mathbb{R}` sont convexes.<br>中：`\mathbb{R}` 中的区间是凸集。                                                            |
| `\theta x+(1-\theta)y`     | convex combination, combinaison convexe, 凸组合       | A weighted combination with nonnegative weights summing to one.                        | EN: The midpoint is a convex combination with weights `1/2` and `1/2`.<br>FR: Le milieu est une combinaison convexe de poids `1/2` et `1/2`.<br>中：中点是权重为 `1/2` 与 `1/2` 的凸组合。          |
| `\operatorname{conv}(S)`   | convex hull, enveloppe convexe, 凸包                 | The smallest convex set containing `S`.                                                | EN: The convex hull of three noncollinear points is a triangle.<br>FR: L’enveloppe convexe de trois points non alignés est un triangle.<br>中：三个不共线点的凸包是三角形。                           |
| `\operatorname{aff}(S)`    | affine hull, enveloppe affine, 仿射包                 | The smallest affine set containing `S`.                                                | EN: The affine hull of two distinct points is the line through them.<br>FR: L’enveloppe affine de deux points distincts est la droite qui les traverse.<br>中：两个不同点的仿射包是经过它们的直线。       |
| `\operatorname{relint}(C)` | relative interior, intérieur relatif, 相对内部         | The interior of `C` relative to its affine hull.                                       | EN: A line segment has nonempty relative interior inside its supporting line.<br>FR: Un segment a un intérieur relatif non vide dans sa droite support.<br>中：线段在其所在直线中有非空相对内部。        |
| `\operatorname{cl}(C)`     | closure of a set, adhérence d’un ensemble, 闭包      | The smallest closed set containing `C`.                                                | EN: The closure of an open ball is the corresponding closed ball.<br>FR: L’adhérence d’une boule ouverte est la boule fermée correspondante.<br>中：开球的闭包是对应的闭球。                        |
| —                          | hyperplane, hyperplan, 超平面                         | A set of points satisfying one affine equality.                                        | EN: In `\mathbb{R}^3`, a hyperplane is an ordinary plane.<br>FR: Dans `\mathbb{R}^3`, un hyperplan est un plan ordinaire.<br>中：在 `\mathbb{R}^3` 中，超平面就是普通平面。                          |
| —                          | halfspace, demi-espace, 半空间                        | A set of points satisfying one affine inequality.                                      | EN: The set `\{x:a^Tx\leq b\}` is a halfspace.<br>FR: L’ensemble `\{x:a^Tx\leq b\}` est un demi-espace.<br>中：集合 `\{x:a^Tx\leq b\}` 是半空间。                                              |
| —                          | separating hyperplane, hyperplan séparateur, 分离超平面 | A hyperplane that separates two convex sets under suitable conditions.                 | EN: Separation theorems support convex duality.<br>FR: Les théorèmes de séparation soutiennent la dualité convexe.<br>中：分离定理支撑凸对偶理论。                                                  |
| —                          | supporting hyperplane, hyperplan d’appui, 支撑超平面    | A hyperplane touching a convex set while keeping the set on one side.                  | EN: At a smooth boundary point, the tangent hyperplane may support the set.<br>FR: En un point lisse du bord, l’hyperplan tangent peut soutenir l’ensemble.<br>中：在光滑边界点，切超平面可能是支撑超平面。 |
| —                          | cone, cône, 锥                                      | A set closed under multiplication by nonnegative scalars.                              | EN: The nonnegative orthant is a convex cone.<br>FR: L’orthant positif est un cône convexe.<br>中：非负正交象限是凸锥。                                                                           |
| `K^\ast`                   | dual cone, cône dual, 对偶锥                          | The set of vectors forming nonnegative inner products with every vector in a cone `K`. | EN: Dual cones appear in conic optimization.<br>FR: Les cônes duaux apparaissent en optimisation conique.<br>中：对偶锥出现在锥优化中。                                                            |
| —                          | polyhedron, polyèdre, 多面体                          | An intersection of finitely many halfspaces and affine hyperplanes.                    | EN: Feasible regions of linear programs are polyhedra.<br>FR: Les régions admissibles des programmes linéaires sont des polyèdres.<br>中：线性规划的可行域是多面体。                                 |

## 23.4 Convex functions, smoothness, and curvature

| Symbol (LaTeX)                                              | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                                 | Definition                                                                                                          | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| ----------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `f` convex                                                  | convex function, fonction convexe, 凸函数                                            | A function whose value at a convex combination is at most the corresponding convex combination of values.           | EN: `f(x)=x^2` is convex on `\mathbb{R}`.<br>FR: `f(x)=x^2` est convexe sur `\mathbb{R}`.<br>中：`f(x)=x^2` 在 `\mathbb{R}` 上是凸函数。                                                                                    |
| `f(\theta x+(1-\theta)y)\leq\theta f(x)+(1-\theta)f(y)`     | convexity inequality, inégalité de convexité, 凸性不等式                               | The defining inequality for convexity.                                                                              | EN: The graph of a convex function lies below its chords.<br>FR: Le graphe d’une fonction convexe est sous ses cordes.<br>中：凸函数图像位于其弦线下方。                                                                          |
| `f` concave                                                 | concave function, fonction concave, 凹函数                                           | A function whose negative is convex.                                                                                | EN: `\log x` is concave on `(0,\infty)`.<br>FR: `\log x` est concave sur `(0,\infty)`.<br>中：`\log x` 在 `(0,\infty)` 上是凹函数。                                                                                         |
| —                                                           | strictly convex function, fonction strictement convexe, 严格凸函数                     | A convex function with strict inequality between distinct points under nontrivial convex combinations.              | EN: A strictly convex objective has at most one minimizer on a convex set.<br>FR: Un objectif strictement convexe a au plus un minimiseur sur un ensemble convexe.<br>中：严格凸目标在凸集上至多有一个极小点。                         |
| —                                                           | strongly convex function, fonction fortement convexe, 强凸函数                        | A convex function with a uniform positive quadratic curvature lower bound.                                          | EN: Strong convexity gives uniqueness and stable optimization behavior.<br>FR: La forte convexité donne unicité et stabilité en optimisation.<br>中：强凸性带来唯一性和稳定的优化行为。                                               |
| `\nabla^2 f(x)\succeq0`                                     | second-order convexity condition, condition convexe du second ordre, 二阶凸性条件       | For twice differentiable functions, convexity on a convex domain is characterized by positive semidefinite Hessian. | EN: If `\nabla^2 f(x)\succeq0` everywhere, then `f` is convex.<br>FR: Si `\nabla^2 f(x)\succeq0` partout, alors `f` est convexe.<br>中：若处处有 `\nabla^2 f(x)\succeq0`，则 `f` 是凸函数。                                     |
| `\nabla f`                                                  | gradient, gradient, 梯度                                                            | The vector of first partial derivatives of a scalar function.                                                       | EN: The gradient points in the direction of steepest increase.<br>FR: Le gradient pointe vers la direction de plus forte croissance.<br>中：梯度指向函数增长最快的方向。                                                           |
| `\nabla^2 f`                                                | Hessian, matrice hessienne, Hessian 矩阵                                            | The matrix of second partial derivatives of a scalar function.                                                      | EN: The Hessian describes local curvature.<br>FR: La hessienne décrit la courbure locale.<br>中：Hessian 矩阵描述局部曲率。                                                                                                   |
| `L`-smooth                                                  | L-smooth function, fonction L-lisse, L-光滑函数                                       | A differentiable function whose gradient is Lipschitz continuous with constant `L`.                                 | EN: Smoothness controls how fast the gradient can change.<br>FR: La lissité contrôle la vitesse de variation du gradient.<br>中：光滑性控制梯度变化速度。                                                                        |
| `\lVert\nabla f(x)-\nabla f(y)\rVert\leq L\lVert x-y\rVert` | Lipschitz gradient condition, condition de gradient lipschitzien, 梯度 Lipschitz 条件 | A condition bounding gradient variation by distance between points.                                                 | EN: Gradient descent analysis often assumes Lipschitz gradients.<br>FR: L’analyse de la descente de gradient suppose souvent des gradients lipschitziens.<br>中：梯度下降分析常假设梯度 Lipschitz。                              |
| `\mu`-strongly convex                                       | mu-strongly convex, mu-fortement convexe, μ-强凸                                    | A function whose curvature is bounded below by `\mu>0` in the convex sense.                                         | EN: Strong convexity yields linear convergence for gradient descent under smoothness.<br>FR: La forte convexité donne une convergence linéaire de la descente de gradient sous lissité.<br>中：在光滑条件下，强凸性可使梯度下降线性收敛。 |
| `\operatorname{epi}(f)`                                     | epigraph, épigraphe, 上图像                                                          | The set of points lying on or above the graph of a function.                                                        | EN: A function is convex exactly when its epigraph is convex.<br>FR: Une fonction est convexe exactement lorsque son épigraphe est convexe.<br>中：函数凸当且仅当其上图像为凸集。                                                   |

## 23.5 Optimality conditions and local analysis

| Symbol (LaTeX)                | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                               | Definition                                                                                                                  | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                            |
| ----------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `x^\star` local min           | local minimizer, minimiseur local, 局部极小点                                        | A point whose objective value is no larger than nearby feasible points.                                                     | EN: A local minimizer may fail to be globally optimal.<br>FR: Un minimiseur local peut ne pas être globalement optimal.<br>中：局部极小点未必是全局最优点。                                                         |
| `x^\star` global min          | global minimizer, minimiseur global, 全局极小点                                      | A point whose objective value is no larger than all feasible points.                                                        | EN: Convex optimization aims to make local minima global.<br>FR: L’optimisation convexe vise à rendre les minima locaux globaux.<br>中：凸优化使局部极小点成为全局极小点。                                             |
| `\nabla f(x^\star)=0`         | first-order necessary condition, condition nécessaire du premier ordre, 一阶必要条件  | For unconstrained differentiable optimization, an interior local minimizer must have zero gradient.                         | EN: At an unconstrained smooth local minimum, the gradient vanishes.<br>FR: En un minimum local lisse sans contraintes, le gradient s’annule.<br>中：无约束光滑局部极小点处梯度为零。                                 |
| `\nabla^2 f(x^\star)\succeq0` | second-order necessary condition, condition nécessaire du second ordre, 二阶必要条件  | At an unconstrained twice differentiable local minimizer, the Hessian is positive semidefinite.                             | EN: A negative curvature direction rules out a local minimum.<br>FR: Une direction de courbure négative exclut un minimum local.<br>中：负曲率方向排除局部极小。                                                  |
| `\nabla^2 f(x^\star)\succ0`   | second-order sufficient condition, condition suffisante du second ordre, 二阶充分条件 | If the gradient vanishes and the Hessian is positive definite, the point is a strict local minimizer.                       | EN: Positive definite Hessian gives a strict local minimum.<br>FR: Une hessienne définie positive donne un minimum local strict.<br>中：正定 Hessian 给出严格局部极小。                                          |
| —                             | stationary point, point stationnaire, 驻点                                        | A point where the gradient of a differentiable function is zero.                                                            | EN: A stationary point can be a minimum, maximum, or saddle.<br>FR: Un point stationnaire peut être un minimum, un maximum ou un point selle.<br>中：驻点可能是极小点、极大点或鞍点。                                 |
| —                             | saddle point, point selle, 鞍点                                                   | A stationary point that is not a local minimum or local maximum.                                                            | EN: `f(x,y)=x^2-y^2` has a saddle point at the origin.<br>FR: `f(x,y)=x^2-y^2` a un point selle à l’origine.<br>中：`f(x,y)=x^2-y^2` 在原点有鞍点。                                                          |
| —                             | descent direction, direction de descente, 下降方向                                  | A direction along which the objective decreases locally.                                                                    | EN: The negative gradient is a descent direction when the gradient is nonzero.<br>FR: Le gradient négatif est une direction de descente si le gradient est non nul.<br>中：当梯度非零时，负梯度是下降方向。           |
| `d^T\nabla f(x)<0`            | descent condition, condition de descente, 下降条件                                  | A first-order condition ensuring that direction `d` locally decreases `f`.                                                  | EN: If `d^T\nabla f(x)<0`, a small step along `d` lowers the objective.<br>FR: Si `d^T\nabla f(x)<0`, un petit pas selon `d` diminue l’objectif.<br>中：若 `d^T\nabla f(x)<0`，沿 `d` 小步移动会降低目标。         |
| —                             | critical point, point critique, 临界点                                             | A point where first-order optimality may fail to determine behavior, often because the derivative vanishes or is undefined. | EN: Nonsmooth functions may have critical points without classical gradients.<br>FR: Les fonctions non lisses peuvent avoir des points critiques sans gradients classiques.<br>中：非光滑函数可能有无经典梯度的临界点。 |
| —                             | coercive function, fonction coercive, 强制函数                                      | A function tending to infinity as the norm of the variable tends to infinity.                                               | EN: Coercivity helps prove existence of minimizers.<br>FR: La coercivité aide à prouver l’existence de minimiseurs.<br>中：强制性有助于证明极小点存在。                                                             |
| —                             | existence theorem, théorème d’existence, 存在性定理                                  | A result guaranteeing that an optimal solution exists under specified compactness or coercivity assumptions.                | EN: A continuous function on a compact set attains its minimum.<br>FR: Une fonction continue sur un compact atteint son minimum.<br>中：紧集上的连续函数必达到最小值。                                               |

## 23.6 Lagrange multipliers and KKT conditions

| Symbol (LaTeX)                                 | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                                        | Definition                                                                                                        | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
| ---------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `\lambda`                                      | Lagrange multiplier, multiplicateur de Lagrange, Lagrange 乘子                             | A variable attached to a constraint to express constrained optimality conditions.                                 | EN: Multipliers measure sensitivity to constraints.<br>FR: Les multiplicateurs mesurent la sensibilité aux contraintes.<br>中：乘子度量目标对约束的敏感性。                                                                                                       |
| `\mathcal{L}(x,\lambda)`                       | Lagrangian, lagrangien, Lagrange 函数                                                      | A function combining objective and constraints with multipliers.                                                  | EN: Equality-constrained problems use `\mathcal{L}(x,\lambda)=f(x)+\lambda^Th(x)`.<br>FR: Les problèmes à contraintes d’égalité utilisent `\mathcal{L}(x,\lambda)=f(x)+\lambda^Th(x)`.<br>中：等式约束问题使用 `\mathcal{L}(x,\lambda)=f(x)+\lambda^Th(x)`。 |
| `\nabla_x\mathcal{L}(x^\star,\lambda^\star)=0` | stationarity condition, condition de stationnarité, 平稳性条件                                | The condition that the derivative of the Lagrangian with respect to primal variables vanishes at the optimum.     | EN: Stationarity balances objective gradient against constraint gradients.<br>FR: La stationnarité équilibre le gradient objectif avec les gradients des contraintes.<br>中：平稳性使目标梯度与约束梯度平衡。                                                       |
| `h(x^\star)=0`                                 | primal feasibility for equality constraints, faisabilité primale des égalités, 等式约束原始可行性 | The equality constraints must hold at the optimal point.                                                          | EN: A constrained optimum must satisfy its constraints.<br>FR: Un optimum contraint doit satisfaire ses contraintes.<br>中：约束最优点必须满足约束。                                                                                                            |
| `g_i(x^\star)\leq0`                            | primal feasibility for inequalities, faisabilité primale des inégalités, 不等式约束原始可行性      | The inequality constraints must hold at the optimal point.                                                        | EN: Feasibility is required before optimality is meaningful.<br>FR: La faisabilité est requise avant que l’optimalité ait un sens.<br>中：先有可行性，最优性才有意义。                                                                                            |
| `\lambda_i^\star\geq0`                         | dual feasibility, faisabilité duale, 对偶可行性                                               | Inequality multipliers must be nonnegative in standard minimization form.                                         | EN: KKT multipliers for `g_i(x)\leq0` are nonnegative.<br>FR: Les multiplicateurs KKT pour `g_i(x)\leq0` sont non négatifs.<br>中：对 `g_i(x)\leq0`，KKT 乘子非负。                                                                                        |
| `\lambda_i^\star g_i(x^\star)=0`               | complementary slackness, complémentarité, 互补松弛                                           | At optimality, each inequality multiplier or its constraint slack must be zero.                                   | EN: Inactive constraints have zero multipliers under KKT conditions.<br>FR: Les contraintes inactives ont des multiplicateurs nuls sous les conditions KKT.<br>中：KKT 条件下，非起作用约束的乘子为零。                                                             |
| —                                              | KKT conditions, conditions KKT, KKT 条件                                                   | Karush-Kuhn-Tucker conditions combining stationarity, feasibility, dual feasibility, and complementary slackness. | EN: For convex problems under regularity, KKT conditions can be sufficient for optimality.<br>FR: Pour les problèmes convexes sous régularité, les conditions KKT peuvent suffire à l’optimalité.<br>中：在正则条件下，凸问题的 KKT 条件可作为最优性充分条件。              |
| —                                              | constraint qualification, qualification des contraintes, 约束规范条件                          | A regularity condition ensuring that optimality conditions such as KKT apply.                                     | EN: Constraint qualifications prevent degenerate constraint geometry.<br>FR: Les qualifications des contraintes évitent une géométrie dégénérée des contraintes.<br>中：约束规范条件避免退化的约束几何。                                                            |
| —                                              | Slater’s condition, condition de Slater, Slater 条件                                       | A constraint qualification requiring a strictly feasible point for convex inequality constraints.                 | EN: Slater’s condition often guarantees strong duality in convex optimization.<br>FR: La condition de Slater garantit souvent la dualité forte en optimisation convexe.<br>中：Slater 条件常保证凸优化中的强对偶。                                                |
| —                                              | LICQ, qualification d’indépendance linéaire, 线性无关约束规范                                    | A condition requiring gradients of active constraints to be linearly independent.                                 | EN: LICQ supports uniqueness of Lagrange multipliers locally.<br>FR: La LICQ soutient localement l’unicité des multiplicateurs de Lagrange.<br>中：LICQ 支持局部 Lagrange 乘子的唯一性。                                                                       |
| —                                              | sensitivity analysis, analyse de sensibilité, 敏感性分析                                      | The study of how optimal values and solutions change when problem data change.                                    | EN: Lagrange multipliers often describe marginal value of relaxing constraints.<br>FR: Les multiplicateurs de Lagrange décrivent souvent la valeur marginale du relâchement des contraintes.<br>中：Lagrange 乘子常描述放松约束的边际价值。                        |

## 23.7 Duality and saddle-point formulations

| Symbol (LaTeX)                               | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                | Definition                                                                                                 | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| -------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------ | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `d(\lambda)=\inf_x\mathcal{L}(x,\lambda)`    | dual function, fonction duale, 对偶函数              | A function giving lower bounds on the primal optimal value in standard minimization problems.              | EN: The dual function is always concave, even when the primal problem is not convex.<br>FR: La fonction duale est toujours concave, même si le problème primal n’est pas convexe.<br>中：即使原问题非凸，对偶函数也总是凹函数。 |
| `\max_{\lambda\geq0} d(\lambda)`             | dual problem, problème dual, 对偶问题                | An optimization problem derived from the Lagrangian dual function.                                         | EN: Solving the dual can give bounds on the primal value.<br>FR: Résoudre le dual peut donner des bornes sur la valeur primale.<br>中：求解对偶问题可给出原问题最优值的界。                                                    |
| —                                            | primal problem, problème primal, 原问题             | The original optimization problem before forming its dual.                                                 | EN: The primal problem contains the original variables and constraints.<br>FR: Le problème primal contient les variables et contraintes originales.<br>中：原问题包含原始变量和约束。                                     |
| `p^\star-d^\star`                            | duality gap, écart de dualité, 对偶间隙              | The difference between primal and dual optimal values.                                                     | EN: A zero duality gap means primal and dual optimal values match.<br>FR: Un écart de dualité nul signifie que les valeurs optimales primale et duale coïncident.<br>中：对偶间隙为零表示原问题与对偶问题最优值相同。              |
| —                                            | weak duality, dualité faible, 弱对偶                | The principle that the dual value bounds the primal value in the appropriate direction.                    | EN: Weak duality holds under very general conditions.<br>FR: La dualité faible vaut sous des conditions très générales.<br>中：弱对偶在非常一般的条件下成立。                                                               |
| —                                            | strong duality, dualité forte, 强对偶               | The property that the primal and dual optimal values are equal.                                            | EN: Convex problems often have strong duality under constraint qualifications.<br>FR: Les problèmes convexes ont souvent une dualité forte sous qualifications de contraintes.<br>中：凸问题在约束规范条件下常有强对偶。      |
| `\sup_{\lambda}\inf_x\mathcal{L}(x,\lambda)` | max-min formulation, formulation max-min, 极大极小形式 | A dual formulation maximizing the lower bound obtained by minimizing the Lagrangian over primal variables. | EN: Dual problems often appear as max-min problems.<br>FR: Les problèmes duaux apparaissent souvent comme des problèmes max-min.<br>中：对偶问题常表现为极大极小问题。                                                      |
| `\inf_x\sup_{\lambda}\mathcal{L}(x,\lambda)` | min-max formulation, formulation min-max, 极小极大形式 | A formulation enforcing constraints through maximization over multipliers.                                 | EN: Under strong duality, min-max and max-min values coincide.<br>FR: Sous dualité forte, les valeurs min-max et max-min coïncident.<br>中：强对偶下，极小极大值与极大极小值相同。                                              |
| —                                            | saddle point, point selle, 鞍点                    | A point minimizing a function in one variable and maximizing it in another.                                | EN: Lagrangian saddle points encode primal-dual optimality.<br>FR: Les points selles lagrangiens encodent l’optimalité primale-duale.<br>中：Lagrange 鞍点编码原始-对偶最优性。                                          |
| `f^\ast(y)`                                  | convex conjugate, conjuguée convexe, 凸共轭         | A function defined by the supremum of affine lower representations relative to `f`.                        | EN: Convex conjugates are central in Fenchel duality.<br>FR: Les conjuguées convexes sont centrales dans la dualité de Fenchel.<br>中：凸共轭是 Fenchel 对偶的核心。                                                   |
| `f^{\ast\ast}`                               | biconjugate, biconjuguée, 双共轭                    | The convex conjugate of the convex conjugate.                                                              | EN: For closed proper convex functions, `f^{\ast\ast}=f`.<br>FR: Pour les fonctions convexes propres fermées, `f^{\ast\ast}=f`.<br>中：对闭真凸函数，`f^{\ast\ast}=f`。                                              |
| —                                            | Fenchel duality, dualité de Fenchel, Fenchel 对偶  | A duality theory based on convex conjugates.                                                               | EN: Fenchel duality links primal optimization to conjugate functions.<br>FR: La dualité de Fenchel relie l’optimisation primale aux fonctions conjuguées.<br>中：Fenchel 对偶把原始优化与共轭函数联系起来。                   |

## 23.8 Subgradients, nonsmooth analysis, and proximal methods
