| —                                                                             | weak solution of an SDE, solution faible d’une EDS, 随机微分方程弱解                                         | A solution where the probability space and Brownian motion may be part of the unknown.                      | EN: Weak solutions specify laws rather than fixed sample paths on a pre-given space.<br>FR: Les solutions faibles spécifient des lois plutôt que des trajectoires sur un espace fixé.<br>中：弱解指定分布，而不固定在预先给定概率空间上的路径。                             |
| —                                                                             | strong solution of an SDE, solution forte d’une EDS, 随机微分方程强解                                        | A solution adapted to a given Brownian motion on a given probability space.                                 | EN: Strong solutions are constructed from the given noise.<br>FR: Les solutions fortes sont construites à partir du bruit donné.<br>中：强解由给定噪声构造出来。                                                                                               |
| —                                                                             | pathwise uniqueness, unicité trajectorielle, 路径唯一性                                                   | Any two solutions driven by the same Brownian motion with the same initial condition are indistinguishable. | EN: Pathwise uniqueness is stronger than uniqueness in law.<br>FR: L’unicité trajectorielle est plus forte que l’unicité en loi.<br>中：路径唯一性强于分布唯一性。                                                                                              |
| —                                                                             | uniqueness in law, unicité en loi, 分布唯一性                                                             | All solutions have the same probability law.                                                                | EN: Weak uniqueness means the distribution of the process is uniquely determined.<br>FR: L’unicité faible signifie que la loi du processus est déterminée de manière unique.<br>中：弱唯一性表示过程分布被唯一确定。                                               |

## 31.11 Point processes, Poisson processes, and jump processes

| Symbol (LaTeX)                             | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                                            | Definition                                                                                            | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| ------------------------------------------ | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `N_t`                                      | counting process, processus de comptage, 计数过程                                                | A process counting the number of events occurring up to time `t`.                                     | EN: The number of arrivals by time `t` is a counting process.<br>FR: Le nombre d’arrivées avant le temps `t` est un processus de comptage.<br>中：截至时间 `t` 的到达次数是计数过程。                                            |
| —                                          | point process, processus ponctuel, 点过程                                                       | A random collection of points in time, space, or a general state space.                               | EN: Earthquake times can be modeled by a point process.<br>FR: Les temps de séismes peuvent être modélisés par un processus ponctuel.<br>中：地震发生时间可建模为点过程。                                                       |
| `(N_t)_{t\geq0}`                           | Poisson process, processus de Poisson, Poisson 过程                                            | A counting process with independent stationary increments and Poisson-distributed counts.             | EN: The Poisson process models random arrivals at a constant average rate.<br>FR: Le processus de Poisson modélise des arrivées aléatoires à taux moyen constant.<br>中：Poisson 过程建模以恒定平均速率发生的随机到达。              |
| `\lambda`                                  | intensity rate, intensité, 强度率                                                               | The average event rate per unit time in a Poisson process or related process.                         | EN: If `\lambda=3`, the expected number of arrivals per unit time is `3`.<br>FR: Si `\lambda=3`, le nombre moyen d’arrivées par unité de temps est `3`.<br>中：若 `\lambda=3`，单位时间内期望到达次数为 `3`。                    |
| `N_t\sim\operatorname{Poisson}(\lambda t)` | Poisson process marginal distribution, loi marginale du processus de Poisson, Poisson 过程边际分布 | The number of events by time `t` has a Poisson distribution with mean `\lambda t`.                    | EN: Counts grow in expectation linearly with time.<br>FR: L’espérance des comptages croît linéairement avec le temps.<br>中：计数的期望随时间线性增长。                                                                        |
| —                                          | exponential waiting time, temps d’attente exponentiel, 指数等待时间                                | The time between consecutive events in a homogeneous Poisson process.                                 | EN: Poisson process interarrival times are independent exponential variables.<br>FR: Les temps inter-arrivées du processus de Poisson sont exponentiels indépendants.<br>中：Poisson 过程的相邻到达间隔是独立指数随机变量。          |
| —                                          | compound Poisson process, processus de Poisson composé, 复合 Poisson 过程                        | A jump process formed by summing random jump sizes at Poisson arrival times.                          | EN: Insurance claim totals are often modeled by compound Poisson processes.<br>FR: Les montants totaux de sinistres sont souvent modélisés par des processus de Poisson composés.<br>中：保险理赔总额常用复合 Poisson 过程建模。 |
| `\nu`                                      | Lévy measure, mesure de Lévy, Lévy 测度                                                        | A measure describing jump intensity by jump size in a Lévy process.                                   | EN: The Lévy measure controls frequency of small and large jumps.<br>FR: La mesure de Lévy contrôle la fréquence des petits et grands sauts.<br>中：Lévy 测度控制小跳和大跳的频率。                                            |
| —                                          | Lévy process, processus de Lévy, Lévy 过程                                                     | A process with stationary independent increments and stochastic continuity.                           | EN: Brownian motion and Poisson processes are Lévy processes.<br>FR: Le mouvement brownien et les processus de Poisson sont des processus de Lévy.<br>中：Brownian 运动和 Poisson 过程都是 Lévy 过程。                      |
| —                                          | càdlàg path, trajectoire càdlàg, 右连续左极限路径                                                    | A path that is right-continuous and has left limits everywhere.                                       | EN: Many jump processes have càdlàg paths.<br>FR: Beaucoup de processus à sauts ont des trajectoires càdlàg.<br>中：许多跳过程具有右连续左极限路径。                                                                              |
| `X_{t-}`                                   | left limit, limite à gauche, 左极限                                                             | The value approached by a process immediately before time `t`.                                        | EN: Jump size is `\Delta X_t=X_t-X_{t-}`.<br>FR: La taille du saut est `\Delta X_t=X_t-X_{t-}`.<br>中：跳跃大小为 `\Delta X_t=X_t-X_{t-}`。                                                                             |
| —                                          | compensator, compensateur, 补偿子                                                               | A predictable increasing process used to subtract the expected drift from a counting or jump process. | EN: A compensated Poisson process is a martingale.<br>FR: Un processus de Poisson compensé est une martingale.<br>中：补偿 Poisson 过程是鞅。                                                                            |

## 31.12 Ergodic, mixing, and long-time behavior of stochastic processes

| Symbol (LaTeX) | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                       | Definition                                                                                               | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
| -------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| —              | stationary process, processus stationnaire, 平稳过程                        | A process whose finite-dimensional distributions are invariant under time shifts.                        | EN: Stationary processes have time-invariant statistical behavior.<br>FR: Les processus stationnaires ont un comportement statistique invariant dans le temps.<br>中：平稳过程具有时间不变的统计行为。                                 |
| —              | strictly stationary process, processus strictement stationnaire, 严格平稳过程 | A process whose all finite-dimensional distributions are invariant under time shifts.                    | EN: Strict stationarity concerns full distributions, not just means and covariances.<br>FR: La stationnarité stricte concerne les lois complètes, non seulement moyennes et covariances.<br>中：严格平稳涉及完整分布，而不只是均值和协方差。 |
| —              | weakly stationary process, processus faiblement stationnaire, 弱平稳过程     | A process with constant mean and covariance depending only on time lag.                                  | EN: Weak stationarity is common in time series analysis.<br>FR: La stationnarité faible est courante dans l’analyse des séries temporelles.<br>中：弱平稳常见于时间序列分析。                                                       |
| `\gamma(h)`    | autocovariance function, fonction d’autocovariance, 自协方差函数              | The covariance of a stationary process at time lag `h`.                                                  | EN: Autocovariance measures dependence across time lags.<br>FR: L’autocovariance mesure la dépendance selon les retards temporels.<br>中：自协方差度量不同时间滞后的相关性。                                                            |
| `\rho(h)`      | autocorrelation function, fonction d’autocorrélation, 自相关函数             | The normalized autocovariance at lag `h`.                                                                | EN: Autocorrelation is dimensionless temporal dependence.<br>FR: L’autocorrélation est une dépendance temporelle sans dimension.<br>中：自相关是无量纲的时间依赖度量。                                                                |
| —              | ergodic process, processus ergodique, 遍历过程                              | A stationary process whose time averages equal ensemble averages under suitable conditions.              | EN: Ergodicity justifies estimating expectations from one long trajectory.<br>FR: L’ergodicité justifie l’estimation des espérances à partir d’une longue trajectoire.<br>中：遍历性说明可由一条长路径估计总体期望。                      |
| —              | ergodic theorem, théorème ergodique, 遍历定理                               | A theorem relating long-time averages to conditional or invariant expectations.                          | EN: Birkhoff’s ergodic theorem is foundational for dynamical probability.<br>FR: Le théorème ergodique de Birkhoff est fondamental pour la probabilité dynamique.<br>中：Birkhoff 遍历定理是动力概率的基础。                        |
| —              | mixing, mélange, 混合性                                                    | A strong asymptotic independence property for events separated by long time gaps.                        | EN: Mixing processes gradually lose memory of initial events.<br>FR: Les processus mélangeants perdent progressivement la mémoire des événements initiaux.<br>中：混合过程会逐渐遗忘初始事件。                                       |
| —              | invariant distribution, loi invariante, 不变分布                            | A distribution preserved by the dynamics of a Markov process.                                            | EN: Invariant distributions describe equilibrium states.<br>FR: Les lois invariantes décrivent les états d’équilibre.<br>中：不变分布描述平衡状态。                                                                               |
| —              | convergence to equilibrium, convergence vers l’équilibre, 向平衡收敛         | The tendency of a Markov process distribution to approach an invariant distribution.                     | EN: Many ergodic Markov chains converge to a stationary distribution.<br>FR: Beaucoup de chaînes de Markov ergodiques convergent vers une loi stationnaire.<br>中：许多遍历 Markov 链收敛到平稳分布。                               |
| —              | mixing time, temps de mélange, 混合时间                                     | The time needed for a Markov chain to get close to its stationary distribution under a chosen distance.  | EN: Mixing time quantifies how fast a chain forgets its initial state.<br>FR: Le temps de mélange quantifie la vitesse à laquelle une chaîne oublie son état initial.<br>中：混合时间量化 Markov 链遗忘初始状态的速度。                 |
| —              | coupling, couplage, 耦合                                                  | A construction putting random variables or processes on a common probability space to compare them.      | EN: Coupling can prove convergence to equilibrium.<br>FR: Le couplage peut prouver la convergence vers l’équilibre.<br>中：耦合可用于证明向平衡收敛。                                                                               |
| —              | total variation distance, distance en variation totale, 总变差距离           | A distance measuring the largest probability difference assigned to the same event by two distributions. | EN: Markov chain mixing is often measured in total variation distance.<br>FR: Le mélange des chaînes de Markov est souvent mesuré en distance de variation totale.<br>中：Markov 链混合常用总变差距离度量。                         |
# 32. Statistics, Inference, and Learning Theory

## 32.1 Data, samples, models, and parameters

| Symbol (LaTeX)                     | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                                        | Definition                                                                              | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| ---------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `X_1,\ldots,X_n`                   | sample, échantillon, 样本                                                                  | A finite collection of observed random variables or data points.                        | EN: `X_1,\ldots,X_n` may represent observed heights.<br>FR: `X_1,\ldots,X_n` peut représenter des tailles observées.<br>中：`X_1,\ldots,X_n` 可表示观测到的身高数据。                                         |
| `n`                                | sample size, taille de l’échantillon, 样本量                                                | The number of observations in a sample.                                                 | EN: Larger `n` often reduces sampling variability.<br>FR: Un `n` plus grand réduit souvent la variabilité d’échantillonnage.<br>中：更大的 `n` 通常降低抽样波动。                                             |
| `x_i`                              | observed value, valeur observée, 观测值                                                     | A realized value of a random variable `X_i`.                                            | EN: If `X_i` is a test score, `x_i=87` is one observed value.<br>FR: Si `X_i` est une note d’examen, `x_i=87` est une valeur observée.<br>中：若 `X_i` 是考试分数，则 `x_i=87` 是一个观测值。                    |
| `\mathcal{X}`                      | sample space of observations, espace des observations, 观测空间                              | The set where individual observations take values.                                      | EN: For binary labels, `\mathcal{X}` may be `{0,1}`.<br>FR: Pour des étiquettes binaires, `\mathcal{X}` peut être `{0,1}`.<br>中：对二元标签，`\mathcal{X}` 可为 `{0,1}`。                                 |
| `P`                                | data-generating distribution, loi génératrice des données, 数据生成分布                        | The probability distribution assumed to generate observations.                          | EN: Statistical inference estimates features of `P` from data.<br>FR: L’inférence statistique estime des propriétés de `P` à partir des données.<br>中：统计推断从数据中估计 `P` 的性质。                       |
| `P_\theta`                         | parametric model, modèle paramétrique, 参数模型                                              | A family of probability distributions indexed by parameter `\theta`.                    | EN: Normal models are often written as `P_{\mu,\sigma^2}`.<br>FR: Les modèles normaux s’écrivent souvent `P_{\mu,\sigma^2}`.<br>中：正态模型常写作 `P_{\mu,\sigma^2}`。                                   |
| `\theta`                           | parameter, paramètre, 参数                                                                 | An unknown quantity indexing a statistical model.                                       | EN: In a Bernoulli model, `\theta` may be the success probability.<br>FR: Dans un modèle de Bernoulli, `\theta` peut être la probabilité de succès.<br>中：在 Bernoulli 模型中，`\theta` 可表示成功概率。      |
| `\Theta`                           | parameter space, espace des paramètres, 参数空间                                             | The set of all possible parameter values.                                               | EN: For a probability parameter, `\Theta=[0,1]`.<br>FR: Pour un paramètre de probabilité, `\Theta=[0,1]`.<br>中：对概率参数，`\Theta=[0,1]`。                                                            |
| `\theta_0`                         | true parameter, vrai paramètre, 真参数                                                      | The parameter value assumed to generate the data when the model is correctly specified. | EN: Consistency means an estimator approaches `\theta_0`.<br>FR: La consistance signifie qu’un estimateur approche `\theta_0`.<br>中：一致性表示估计量趋近真参数 `\theta_0`。                                   |
| `\mathcal{P}`                      | statistical model, modèle statistique, 统计模型                                              | A set of candidate probability distributions for the data.                              | EN: A nonparametric model may allow a very large class `\mathcal{P}`.<br>FR: Un modèle non paramétrique peut permettre une très grande classe `\mathcal{P}`.<br>中：非参数模型可允许很大的分布类 `\mathcal{P}`。 |
| `X_i\overset{\mathrm{iid}}{\sim}P` | independent and identically distributed, indépendants et identiquement distribués, 独立同分布 | Observations are independent and share the same distribution `P`.                       | EN: Many classical estimators assume iid samples.<br>FR: Beaucoup d’estimateurs classiques supposent des échantillons iid.<br>中：许多经典估计量假设样本独立同分布。                                               |
| `T(X_1,\ldots,X_n)`                | statistic, statistique, 统计量                                                              | A function of the sample, not depending on unknown parameters.                          | EN: The sample mean is a statistic.<br>FR: La moyenne empirique est une statistique.<br>中：样本均值是统计量。                                                                                             |

## 32.2 Estimators, bias, variance, and risk

| Symbol (LaTeX)                                                                                          | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                  | Definition                                                                     | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                         |
| ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `\hat{\theta}`                                                                                          | estimator, estimateur, 估计量                                         | A statistic used to estimate an unknown parameter.                             | EN: `\hat{\theta}` may estimate a population mean.<br>FR: `\hat{\theta}` peut estimer une moyenne de population.<br>中：`\hat{\theta}` 可用于估计总体均值。                                                  |
| `\hat{\theta}(x_1,\ldots,x_n)`                                                                          | estimate, estimation, 估计值                                          | The realized numerical value of an estimator after observing data.             | EN: The estimator is random; the estimate is observed.<br>FR: L’estimateur est aléatoire ; l’estimation est observée.<br>中：估计量是随机的，估计值是观测后的数值。                                                   |
| `\bar{X}`                                                                                               | sample mean, moyenne empirique, 样本均值                               | The average of sample observations.                                            | EN: `\bar{X}=n^{-1}\sum_iX_i` estimates the population mean.<br>FR: `\bar{X}=n^{-1}\sum_iX_i` estime la moyenne de population.<br>中：`\bar{X}=n^{-1}\sum_iX_i` 估计总体均值。                            |
| `S^2`                                                                                                   | sample variance, variance empirique, 样本方差                          | A statistic estimating the population variance.                                | EN: The unbiased sample variance divides by `n-1`.<br>FR: La variance empirique sans biais divise par `n-1`.<br>中：无偏样本方差使用 `n-1` 作分母。                                                            |
| `\operatorname{Bias}(\hat{\theta})`                                                                     | bias, biais, 偏差                                                    | The difference between the estimator’s expectation and the target parameter.   | EN: Bias measures systematic error.<br>FR: Le biais mesure l’erreur systématique.<br>中：偏差度量系统性误差。                                                                                                |
| `\mathbb{E}[\hat{\theta}]=\theta`                                                                       | unbiased estimator, estimateur sans biais, 无偏估计量                   | An estimator whose expectation equals the target parameter.                    | EN: The sample mean is unbiased for the population mean under iid sampling.<br>FR: La moyenne empirique est sans biais pour la moyenne sous échantillonnage iid.<br>中：在 iid 抽样下，样本均值是总体均值的无偏估计量。 |
| `\operatorname{Var}(\hat{\theta})`                                                                      | estimator variance, variance d’un estimateur, 估计量方差                | The variability of an estimator across repeated samples.                       | EN: Lower variance means the estimator fluctuates less across samples.<br>FR: Une variance plus faible signifie que l’estimateur fluctue moins entre échantillons.<br>中：较低方差表示估计量在不同样本中波动较小。     |
| `\operatorname{MSE}(\hat{\theta})`                                                                      | mean squared error, erreur quadratique moyenne, 均方误差               | The expected squared distance between an estimator and the target.             | EN: MSE combines bias and variance.<br>FR: L’erreur quadratique moyenne combine biais et variance.<br>中：均方误差结合偏差与方差。                                                                             |
| `\operatorname{MSE}(\hat{\theta})=\operatorname{Var}(\hat{\theta})+\operatorname{Bias}(\hat{\theta})^2` | bias-variance decomposition, décomposition biais-variance, 偏差-方差分解 | A decomposition of mean squared error into variance and squared bias.          | EN: Regularization often increases bias but decreases variance.<br>FR: La régularisation augmente souvent le biais mais diminue la variance.<br>中：正则化常增加偏差但降低方差。                                 |
| `R(\hat{\theta},\theta)`                                                                                | risk, risque, 风险                                                   | The expected loss of an estimator under parameter value `\theta`.              | EN: Risk compares estimators under a chosen loss function.<br>FR: Le risque compare des estimateurs sous une fonction de perte choisie.<br>中：风险在给定损失函数下比较估计量。                                    |
| `L(\hat{\theta},\theta)`                                                                                | loss function, fonction de perte, 损失函数                             | A function measuring the cost of estimating `\theta` by `\hat{\theta}`.        | EN: Squared loss gives mean squared error.<br>FR: La perte quadratique donne l’erreur quadratique moyenne.<br>中：平方损失给出均方误差。                                                                      |
| —                                                                                                       | consistency, consistance, 一致性                                      | An estimator is consistent if it converges to the target as sample size grows. | EN: The sample mean is consistent under standard assumptions.<br>FR: La moyenne empirique est consistante sous hypothèses standards.<br>中：在标准假设下，样本均值是一致估计量。                                     |

## 32.3 Likelihood, maximum likelihood, and estimating equations

| Symbol (LaTeX)                             | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                             | Definition                                                                            | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                             |
| ------------------------------------------ | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `L(\theta;x)`                              | likelihood function, fonction de vraisemblance, 似然函数                          | The probability or density of observed data as a function of the parameter.           | EN: Likelihood treats data as fixed and parameter as variable.<br>FR: La vraisemblance traite les données comme fixes et le paramètre comme variable.<br>中：似然把数据视为固定，把参数视为变量。                        |
| `\ell(\theta;x)`                           | log-likelihood, log-vraisemblance, 对数似然                                       | The logarithm of the likelihood function.                                             | EN: Log-likelihood turns products into sums.<br>FR: La log-vraisemblance transforme les produits en sommes.<br>中：对数似然把乘积转化为求和。                                                                       |
| `\hat{\theta}_{\mathrm{MLE}}`              | maximum likelihood estimator, estimateur du maximum de vraisemblance, 最大似然估计量 | A parameter value maximizing the likelihood or log-likelihood.                        | EN: In a Bernoulli model, the MLE is the sample proportion.<br>FR: Dans un modèle de Bernoulli, le MLE est la proportion empirique.<br>中：在 Bernoulli 模型中，最大似然估计量是样本比例。                               |
| `\nabla_\theta\ell(\theta)=0`              | likelihood score equation, équation du score, 似然得分方程                          | The first-order condition for maximizing log-likelihood under smoothness assumptions. | EN: Solving the score equation often gives the MLE.<br>FR: Résoudre l’équation du score donne souvent le MLE.<br>中：求解得分方程常得到最大似然估计量。                                                                 |
| `U(\theta)`                                | score function, fonction score, 得分函数                                          | The derivative of the log-likelihood with respect to the parameter.                   | EN: The score has mean zero under regular models.<br>FR: Le score a une moyenne nulle dans les modèles réguliers.<br>中：在正则模型中，得分函数期望为零。                                                              |
| `I(\theta)`                                | Fisher information, information de Fisher, Fisher 信息                          | A measure of local sensitivity of the likelihood to parameter changes.                | EN: More Fisher information means more precise estimation is possible.<br>FR: Plus d’information de Fisher permet une estimation plus précise.<br>中：Fisher 信息越多，估计越可能精确。                             |
| `I(\theta)=\mathbb{E}_\theta[U(\theta)^2]` | Fisher information formula, formule de l’information de Fisher, Fisher 信息公式   | A common scalar definition of Fisher information in regular one-parameter models.     | EN: Fisher information controls asymptotic variance of efficient estimators.<br>FR: L’information de Fisher contrôle la variance asymptotique des estimateurs efficaces.<br>中：Fisher 信息控制有效估计量的渐近方差。 |
| `\mathcal{I}_n(\theta)`                    | observed information, information observée, 观测信息                              | The negative second derivative of log-likelihood evaluated from observed data.        | EN: Observed information is used to approximate standard errors.<br>FR: L’information observée sert à approximer les erreurs standards.<br>中：观测信息用于近似标准误。                                            |
| —                                          | estimating equation, équation d’estimation, 估计方程                              | An equation whose solution defines an estimator.                                      | EN: Moment conditions can define estimating equations.<br>FR: Les conditions de moments peuvent définir des équations d’estimation.<br>中：矩条件可定义估计方程。                                                 |
| `GMM`                                      | generalized method of moments, méthode généralisée des moments, 广义矩估计         | An estimation method based on matching theoretical and empirical moment conditions.   | EN: GMM is common in econometrics.<br>FR: La GMM est courante en économétrie.<br>中：广义矩估计常见于计量经济学。                                                                                                    |
| `\hat{\theta}_{\mathrm{MAP}}`              | maximum a posteriori estimator, estimateur du maximum a posteriori, 最大后验估计量   | A parameter value maximizing posterior density in Bayesian inference.                 | EN: MAP combines likelihood with a prior.<br>FR: Le MAP combine vraisemblance et loi a priori.<br>中：最大后验估计结合似然与先验。                                                                                   |
| —                                          | identifiability, identifiabilité, 可识别性                                        | A model property where different parameter values imply different distributions.      | EN: Without identifiability, data cannot distinguish parameters.<br>FR: Sans identifiabilité, les données ne distinguent pas les paramètres.<br>中：没有可识别性，数据无法区分参数。                                   |

## 32.4 Hypothesis testing and p-values

| Symbol (LaTeX) | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                        | Definition                                                                                              | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                     |
| -------------- | -------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `H_0`          | null hypothesis, hypothèse nulle, 原假设                    | The baseline hypothesis tested against alternatives.                                                    | EN: `H_0` may state that a treatment has no effect.<br>FR: `H_0` peut affirmer qu’un traitement n’a aucun effet.<br>中：`H_0` 可表示治疗没有效果。                                                       |
| `H_1`          | alternative hypothesis, hypothèse alternative, 备择假设      | The hypothesis considered when the null is rejected.                                                    | EN: `H_1` may state that the mean is not zero.<br>FR: `H_1` peut affirmer que la moyenne n’est pas nulle.<br>中：`H_1` 可表示均值不为零。                                                               |
| `T(X)`         | test statistic, statistique de test, 检验统计量               | A statistic used to decide whether to reject the null hypothesis.                                       | EN: A t-test uses a t-statistic.<br>FR: Un test t utilise une statistique t.<br>中：t 检验使用 t 统计量。                                                                                              |
| `\alpha`       | significance level, niveau de signification, 显著性水平       | The maximum probability of Type I error allowed by the test.                                            | EN: A common choice is `\alpha=0.05`.<br>FR: Un choix courant est `\alpha=0.05`.<br>中：常见选择是 `\alpha=0.05`。                                                                                   |
| —              | Type I error, erreur de type I, 第一类错误                    | Rejecting the null hypothesis when it is true.                                                          | EN: A false alarm is a Type I error.<br>FR: Une fausse alarme est une erreur de type I.<br>中：误报是第一类错误。                                                                                       |
| —              | Type II error, erreur de type II, 第二类错误                  | Failing to reject the null hypothesis when the alternative is true.                                     | EN: Missing a real effect is a Type II error.<br>FR: Manquer un effet réel est une erreur de type II.<br>中：漏掉真实效应是第二类错误。                                                                     |
| `1-\beta`      | power, puissance, 检验功效                                   | The probability of rejecting the null when the alternative is true.                                     | EN: Larger samples usually increase power.<br>FR: Des échantillons plus grands augmentent généralement la puissance.<br>中：更大的样本通常提高检验功效。                                                     |
| `p`-value      | p-value, valeur p, p 值                                   | The probability, under the null, of observing a test statistic at least as extreme as the observed one. | EN: A small p-value indicates data unusual under `H_0`.<br>FR: Une petite valeur p indique des données inhabituelles sous `H_0`.<br>中：较小 p 值表示数据在 `H_0` 下较不寻常。                               |
| —              | rejection region, région de rejet, 拒绝域                   | The set of test statistic values for which the null is rejected.                                        | EN: The rejection region is chosen to control Type I error.<br>FR: La région de rejet est choisie pour contrôler l’erreur de type I.<br>中：拒绝域用于控制第一类错误。                                      |
| —              | one-sided test, test unilatéral, 单侧检验                    | A hypothesis test where the alternative specifies one direction.                                        | EN: Testing whether a mean is greater than zero is one-sided.<br>FR: Tester si une moyenne est supérieure à zéro est unilatéral.<br>中：检验均值是否大于零是单侧检验。                                        |
| —              | two-sided test, test bilatéral, 双侧检验                     | A hypothesis test where deviations in both directions count against the null.                           | EN: Testing whether a mean differs from zero is two-sided.<br>FR: Tester si une moyenne diffère de zéro est bilatéral.<br>中：检验均值是否不同于零是双侧检验。                                                 |
| —              | multiple testing, tests multiples, 多重检验                  | The problem of performing many tests simultaneously and controlling error rates.                        | EN: Multiple testing arises in genomics and large-scale experiments.<br>FR: Les tests multiples apparaissent en génomique et dans les expériences à grande échelle.<br>中：多重检验出现在基因组学和大规模实验中。 |
| `FDR`          | false discovery rate, taux de fausses découvertes, 错误发现率 | The expected proportion of false rejections among all rejections.                                       | EN: FDR control is common in high-dimensional testing.<br>FR: Le contrôle du FDR est courant dans les tests de grande dimension.<br>中：错误发现率控制常用于高维检验。                                        |

## 32.5 Confidence intervals, asymptotics, and efficiency

| Symbol (LaTeX)                                      | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                               | Definition                                                                              | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                               |
| --------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `CI`                                                | confidence interval, intervalle de confiance, 置信区间                              | A data-dependent interval procedure with a specified long-run coverage probability.     | EN: A 95% confidence interval has 95% coverage under repeated sampling.<br>FR: Un intervalle de confiance à 95% a une couverture de 95% sous échantillonnage répété.<br>中：95% 置信区间在重复抽样中具有 95% 覆盖率。    |
| `1-\alpha`                                          | confidence level, niveau de confiance, 置信水平                                     | The nominal coverage probability of a confidence interval procedure.                    | EN: `1-\alpha=0.95` is a common confidence level.<br>FR: `1-\alpha=0.95` est un niveau de confiance courant.<br>中：`1-\alpha=0.95` 是常见置信水平。                                                             |
| `SE(\hat{\theta})`                                  | standard error, erreur standard, 标准误                                            | An estimate of the standard deviation of an estimator.                                  | EN: Standard error measures uncertainty of an estimate.<br>FR: L’erreur standard mesure l’incertitude d’une estimation.<br>中：标准误度量估计值的不确定性。                                                            |
| `\hat{\theta}\pm z_{\alpha/2}SE(\hat{\theta})`      | Wald interval, intervalle de Wald, Wald 区间                                      | A confidence interval based on approximate normality of an estimator.                   | EN: Wald intervals are common but can perform poorly in small samples.<br>FR: Les intervalles de Wald sont courants mais peuvent mal fonctionner avec petits échantillons.<br>中：Wald 区间很常见，但小样本表现可能较差。 |
| `\sqrt{n}(\hat{\theta}-\theta_0)\Rightarrow N(0,V)` | asymptotic normality, normalité asymptotique, 渐近正态性                             | A property where a normalized estimator converges in distribution to a normal law.      | EN: Asymptotic normality justifies large-sample confidence intervals.<br>FR: La normalité asymptotique justifie les intervalles de confiance en grand échantillon.<br>中：渐近正态性支持大样本置信区间。                |
| `o_p(1)`                                            | convergence to zero in probability, convergence vers zéro en probabilité, 依概率趋零 | A random sequence converging to zero in probability.                                    | EN: Remainder terms in asymptotic expansions are often written as `o_p(1)`.<br>FR: Les termes de reste dans les développements asymptotiques s’écrivent souvent `o_p(1)`.<br>中：渐近展开中的余项常写作 `o_p(1)`。   |
| `O_p(1)`                                            | bounded in probability, borné en probabilité, 依概率有界                             | A random sequence that remains bounded with high probability.                           | EN: Tight estimator sequences are often denoted `O_p(1)` after scaling.<br>FR: Les suites d’estimateurs tendues sont souvent notées `O_p(1)` après normalisation.<br>中：适当缩放后的紧估计量列常记作 `O_p(1)`。        |
| —                                                   | delta method, méthode delta, Delta 方法                                           | A method for deriving asymptotic distributions of smooth transformations of estimators. | EN: The delta method gives the asymptotic distribution of `g(\hat{\theta})`.<br>FR: La méthode delta donne la loi asymptotique de `g(\hat{\theta})`.<br>中：Delta 方法给出 `g(\hat{\theta})` 的渐近分布。          |
| —                                                   | bootstrap, bootstrap, Bootstrap 方法                                              | A resampling method estimating sampling distributions from the observed data.           | EN: Bootstrap can approximate standard errors and confidence intervals.<br>FR: Le bootstrap peut approximer erreurs standards et intervalles de confiance.<br>中：Bootstrap 可近似标准误与置信区间。                 |
| `\mathcal{I}(\theta)^{-1}`                          | Cramér-Rao lower bound, borne inférieure de Cramér-Rao, Cramér-Rao 下界           | A lower bound on the variance of unbiased estimators under regularity conditions.       | EN: Efficient estimators attain the Cramér-Rao lower bound asymptotically.<br>FR: Les estimateurs efficaces atteignent asymptotiquement la borne de Cramér-Rao.<br>中：有效估计量渐近达到 Cramér-Rao 下界。          |
| —                                                   | efficiency, efficacité, 有效性                                                     | A property describing an estimator’s variance relative to an optimal lower bound.       | EN: An efficient estimator uses data with minimal asymptotic variance.<br>FR: Un estimateur efficace utilise les données avec variance asymptotique minimale.<br>中：有效估计量以最小渐近方差利用数据。                   |
| —                                                   | confidence set, ensemble de confiance, 置信集                                      | A higher-dimensional generalization of a confidence interval.                           | EN: A confidence ellipse is a confidence set for two parameters.<br>FR: Une ellipse de confiance est un ensemble de confiance pour deux paramètres.<br>中：置信椭圆是二参数的置信集。                                 |

## 32.6 Bayesian inference and posterior analysis

| Symbol (LaTeX)                                       | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                                 | Definition                                                                                          | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
| ---------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `\pi(\theta)`                                        | prior distribution, loi a priori, 先验分布                                            | A probability distribution expressing uncertainty about a parameter before observing data.          | EN: A beta prior is common for a Bernoulli probability.<br>FR: Une loi bêta a priori est courante pour une probabilité de Bernoulli.<br>中：Beta 先验常用于 Bernoulli 概率参数。                                                |
| `p(x\mid\theta)`                                     | likelihood, vraisemblance, 似然                                                     | The data density or probability under parameter `\theta`.                                           | EN: Bayes’ rule combines likelihood and prior.<br>FR: La règle de Bayes combine vraisemblance et a priori.<br>中：Bayes 公式结合似然与先验。                                                                                    |
| `\pi(\theta\mid x)`                                  | posterior distribution, loi a posteriori, 后验分布                                    | The updated distribution of a parameter after observing data.                                       | EN: Posterior distributions quantify uncertainty after data.<br>FR: Les lois a posteriori quantifient l’incertitude après les données.<br>中：后验分布量化观测数据后的不确定性。                                                       |
| `\pi(\theta\mid x)\propto p(x\mid\theta)\pi(\theta)` | Bayes rule, règle de Bayes, Bayes 公式                                              | The rule updating prior beliefs by likelihood to obtain the posterior.                              | EN: Posterior is proportional to likelihood times prior.<br>FR: Le posterior est proportionnel à la vraisemblance fois l’a priori.<br>中：后验正比于似然乘以先验。                                                                |
| `p(x)`                                               | marginal likelihood, vraisemblance marginale, 边际似然                                | The normalizing constant obtained by integrating likelihood against the prior.                      | EN: Marginal likelihood is used in Bayesian model comparison.<br>FR: La vraisemblance marginale sert à comparer les modèles bayésiens.<br>中：边际似然用于 Bayes 模型比较。                                                      |
| —                                                    | conjugate prior, loi a priori conjuguée, 共轭先验                                     | A prior family that yields a posterior in the same family as the prior.                             | EN: Beta prior is conjugate to Bernoulli likelihood.<br>FR: La loi bêta est conjuguée à la vraisemblance de Bernoulli.<br>中：Beta 先验与 Bernoulli 似然共轭。                                                                |
| `\mathbb{E}[\theta\mid x]`                           | posterior mean, moyenne a posteriori, 后验均值                                        | The expectation of the parameter under the posterior distribution.                                  | EN: Posterior mean is often used as a Bayesian point estimate.<br>FR: La moyenne a posteriori sert souvent d’estimation ponctuelle bayésienne.<br>中：后验均值常作为 Bayes 点估计。                                              |
| `\hat{\theta}_{\mathrm{MAP}}`                        | MAP estimator, estimateur MAP, 最大后验估计量                                            | The parameter value maximizing the posterior density.                                               | EN: MAP equals penalized likelihood under many priors.<br>FR: Le MAP équivaut souvent à une vraisemblance pénalisée.<br>中：在许多先验下，MAP 等价于惩罚似然。                                                                       |
| —                                                    | credible interval, intervalle crédible, 可信区间                                      | A posterior probability interval for a parameter.                                                   | EN: A 95% credible interval contains 95% posterior probability.<br>FR: Un intervalle crédible à 95% contient 95% de probabilité a posteriori.<br>中：95% 可信区间包含 95% 的后验概率。                                            |
| —                                                    | posterior predictive distribution, loi prédictive a posteriori, 后验预测分布            | The predictive distribution for future data after integrating over posterior parameter uncertainty. | EN: Posterior predictive checks compare simulated future data with observed data.<br>FR: Les vérifications prédictives a posteriori comparent des données simulées aux données observées.<br>中：后验预测检验比较模拟未来数据与观测数据。 |
| `MCMC`                                               | Markov chain Monte Carlo, Monte Carlo par chaînes de Markov, Markov 链 Monte Carlo | A class of algorithms sampling from complex distributions using Markov chains.                      | EN: MCMC is widely used to approximate posterior distributions.<br>FR: Le MCMC est largement utilisé pour approximer les lois a posteriori.<br>中：MCMC 广泛用于近似后验分布。                                                   |
| `VI`                                                 | variational inference, inférence variationnelle, 变分推断                             | An approximate Bayesian method that turns posterior approximation into an optimization problem.     | EN: Variational inference is often faster than MCMC but more biased.<br>FR: L’inférence variationnelle est souvent plus rapide que le MCMC mais plus biaisée.<br>中：变分推断通常比 MCMC 更快，但偏差更大。                           |

## 32.7 Regression, generalized linear models, and prediction

| Symbol (LaTeX)                  | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                                      | Definition                                                                                | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| ------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `Y`                             | response variable, variable réponse, 响应变量                                              | The variable to be explained or predicted.                                                | EN: In wage prediction, income may be the response variable.<br>FR: Dans la prédiction du salaire, le revenu peut être la variable réponse.<br>中：在工资预测中，收入可以是响应变量。                                        |
| `X`                             | covariate, covariable, 协变量                                                             | An explanatory variable used to predict or explain the response.                          | EN: Education and age may be covariates in a wage model.<br>FR: L’éducation et l’âge peuvent être des covariables dans un modèle de salaire.<br>中：教育和年龄可作为工资模型中的协变量。                                      |
| `Y=X\beta+\varepsilon`          | linear regression model, modèle de régression linéaire, 线性回归模型                         | A model representing the response as a linear function of covariates plus noise.          | EN: Linear regression estimates how predictors relate to an outcome.<br>FR: La régression linéaire estime comment les prédicteurs se relient à une réponse.<br>中：线性回归估计预测变量与结果之间的关系。                      |
| `\beta`                         | regression coefficient, coefficient de régression, 回归系数                                | A parameter measuring the association between a covariate and the response.               | EN: A positive coefficient means the predicted response increases with the covariate.<br>FR: Un coefficient positif signifie que la réponse prédite augmente avec la covariable.<br>中：正系数表示预测响应随协变量增加而增加。 |
| `\varepsilon`                   | error term, terme d’erreur, 误差项                                                        | The unobserved part of the response not explained by covariates.                          | EN: Regression assumptions often constrain the error term.<br>FR: Les hypothèses de régression contraignent souvent le terme d’erreur.<br>中：回归假设常对误差项施加约束。                                                |
| `\hat{\beta}_{OLS}`             | ordinary least squares estimator, estimateur des moindres carrés ordinaires, 普通最小二乘估计量 | The coefficient estimator minimizing residual sum of squares.                             | EN: OLS fits the best linear predictor under squared loss.<br>FR: Les MCO ajustent le meilleur prédicteur linéaire sous perte quadratique.<br>中：OLS 在平方损失下拟合最佳线性预测器。                                      |
| `\hat{y}`                       | fitted value, valeur ajustée, 拟合值                                                      | The predicted response produced by a fitted model.                                        | EN: Residuals compare observed values with fitted values.<br>FR: Les résidus comparent valeurs observées et valeurs ajustées.<br>中：残差比较观测值与拟合值。                                                           |
| `e_i=y_i-\hat{y}_i`             | residual, résidu, 残差                                                                   | The difference between an observed response and its fitted value.                         | EN: Residual plots diagnose model misspecification.<br>FR: Les graphiques de résidus diagnostiquent les erreurs de spécification.<br>中：残差图用于诊断模型设定错误。                                                     |
| `R^2`                           | coefficient of determination, coefficient de détermination, 决定系数                       | A measure of the proportion of response variation explained by a regression model.        | EN: High `R^2` does not by itself imply causal explanation.<br>FR: Un `R^2` élevé n’implique pas à lui seul une explication causale.<br>中：高 `R^2` 本身不意味着因果解释。                                             |
| `GLM`                           | generalized linear model, modèle linéaire généralisé, 广义线性模型                           | A model connecting a response distribution to a linear predictor through a link function. | EN: Logistic regression is a generalized linear model.<br>FR: La régression logistique est un modèle linéaire généralisé.<br>中：逻辑回归是广义线性模型。                                                               |
| `g(\mathbb{E}[Y\mid X])=X\beta` | link function equation, équation de fonction de lien, 连接函数方程                           | The defining relation connecting conditional mean to a linear predictor in a GLM.         | EN: The logit link is used for binary responses.<br>FR: Le lien logit est utilisé pour les réponses binaires.<br>中：logit 连接函数用于二元响应。                                                                      |
| `\mathbb{P}(Y=1\mid X=x)`       | conditional response probability, probabilité conditionnelle de réponse, 条件响应概率        | The probability of a binary outcome given covariates.                                     | EN: Logistic regression estimates `\mathbb{P}(Y=1\mid X=x)`.<br>FR: La régression logistique estime `\mathbb{P}(Y=1\mid X=x)`.<br>中：逻辑回归估计 `\mathbb{P}(Y=1\mid X=x)`。                                     |

## 32.8 Model selection, regularization, and validation

| Symbol (LaTeX)          | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                          | Definition                                                                                          | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                  |                                                                                                                                                                   |
| ----------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| —                       | overfitting, surapprentissage, 过拟合                                         | A model fits training data too closely and generalizes poorly.                                      | EN: A very deep tree may overfit noisy data.<br>FR: Un arbre très profond peut surapprendre des données bruitées.<br>中：很深的决策树可能过拟合噪声数据。                                                   |                                                                                                                                                                   |
| —                       | underfitting, sous-apprentissage, 欠拟合                                      | A model is too simple to capture the underlying pattern.                                            | EN: A linear model may underfit a highly nonlinear relation.<br>FR: Un modèle linéaire peut sous-apprendre une relation.                                                                  | EN: A linear model may underfit a highly nonlinear relation.<br>FR: Un modèle linéaire peut sous-apprendre une relation très non linéaire.<br>中：线性模型可能欠拟合高度非线性关系。 |
| `\lambda`               | regularization parameter, paramètre de régularisation, 正则化参数               | A parameter controlling penalty strength in a regularized model.                                    | EN: Larger `\lambda` usually imposes stronger shrinkage.<br>FR: Un `\lambda` plus grand impose généralement un rétrécissement plus fort.<br>中：更大的 `\lambda` 通常带来更强收缩。                     |                                                                                                                                                                   |
| `\lVert\beta\rVert_2^2` | ridge penalty, pénalité ridge, 岭惩罚                                         | An `L^2` penalty shrinking coefficients toward zero.                                                | EN: Ridge regression stabilizes estimates under collinearity.<br>FR: La régression ridge stabilise les estimations sous colinéarité.<br>中：岭回归在共线性下稳定估计。                                   |                                                                                                                                                                   |
| `\lVert\beta\rVert_1`   | lasso penalty, pénalité lasso, Lasso 惩罚                                    | An `L^1` penalty encouraging sparse coefficients.                                                   | EN: Lasso can perform variable selection.<br>FR: Le lasso peut effectuer une sélection de variables.<br>中：Lasso 可进行变量选择。                                                                  |                                                                                                                                                                   |
| `AIC`                   | Akaike information criterion, critère d’information d’Akaike, Akaike 信息准则  | A model selection criterion balancing fit and model complexity.                                     | EN: Lower AIC is often preferred among comparable models.<br>FR: Un AIC plus faible est souvent préféré entre modèles comparables.<br>中：在可比模型中通常偏好较低 AIC。                                 |                                                                                                                                                                   |
| `BIC`                   | Bayesian information criterion, critère d’information bayésien, Bayes 信息准则 | A model selection criterion with a stronger sample-size-dependent complexity penalty.               | EN: BIC tends to favor simpler models than AIC in large samples.<br>FR: Le BIC tend à favoriser des modèles plus simples que l’AIC en grands échantillons.<br>中：大样本中 BIC 往往比 AIC 更偏向简单模型。 |                                                                                                                                                                   |
| —                       | cross-validation, validation croisée, 交叉验证                                 | A procedure estimating predictive performance by splitting data into training and validation parts. | EN: Cross-validation helps choose tuning parameters.<br>FR: La validation croisée aide à choisir les paramètres de réglage.<br>中：交叉验证帮助选择调参参数。                                            |                                                                                                                                                                   |
| `K`-fold CV             | K-fold cross-validation, validation croisée en K blocs, K 折交叉验证            | Cross-validation dividing data into `K` folds and rotating validation folds.                        | EN: 5-fold and 10-fold cross-validation are common choices.<br>FR: Les validations croisées à 5 ou 10 blocs sont courantes.<br>中：5 折和 10 折交叉验证很常见。                                        |                                                                                                                                                                   |
| —                       | training set, ensemble d’entraînement, 训练集                                 | The data used to fit a model.                                                                       | EN: The training set determines model parameters.<br>FR: L’ensemble d’entraînement détermine les paramètres du modèle.<br>中：训练集用于决定模型参数。                                                  |                                                                                                                                                                   |
| —                       | validation set, ensemble de validation, 验证集                                | The data used to tune model choices without final evaluation.                                       | EN: Validation data help select hyperparameters.<br>FR: Les données de validation aident à choisir les hyperparamètres.<br>中：验证集帮助选择超参数。                                                  |                                                                                                                                                                   |
| —                       | test set, ensemble de test, 测试集                                            | Held-out data used for final evaluation of predictive performance.                                  | EN: The test set should not guide model selection.<br>FR: L’ensemble de test ne doit pas guider la sélection du modèle.<br>中：测试集不应参与模型选择。                                                 |                                                                                                                                                                   |

## 32.9 Statistical learning, empirical risk, and generalization

| Symbol (LaTeX)                | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                  | Definition                                                                                           | Examples                                                                                                                                                                               |
| ----------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------ | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `\mathcal{H}`                 | hypothesis class, classe d’hypothèses, 假设类                         | A set of candidate prediction functions or models.                                                   | EN: Linear classifiers form a hypothesis class.<br>FR: Les classifieurs linéaires forment une classe d’hypothèses.<br>中：线性分类器构成一个假设类。                                                  |
| `h\in\mathcal{H}`             | hypothesis, hypothèse, 假设函数                                        | A candidate function mapping inputs to predictions.                                                  | EN: A classifier `h` assigns labels to inputs.<br>FR: Un classifieur `h` attribue des étiquettes aux entrées.<br>中：分类器 `h` 给输入分配标签。                                                    |
| `\ell(h(x),y)`                | loss function, fonction de perte, 损失函数                             | A function measuring prediction error on an example.                                                 | EN: Squared loss is common for regression.<br>FR: La perte quadratique est courante en régression.<br>中：平方损失常用于回归。                                                                     |
| `R(h)`                        | population risk, risque populationnel, 总体风险                        | The expected loss under the true data-generating distribution.                                       | EN: Learning aims to minimize population risk.<br>FR: L’apprentissage vise à minimiser le risque populationnel.<br>中：学习的目标是最小化总体风险。                                                    |
| `\hat{R}_n(h)`                | empirical risk, risque empirique, 经验风险                             | The average loss over the training sample.                                                           | EN: Empirical risk is computable from data.<br>FR: Le risque empirique est calculable à partir des données.<br>中：经验风险可由数据计算。                                                           |
| `\hat{h}_{ERM}`               | empirical risk minimizer, minimiseur du risque empirique, 经验风险最小化器 | A hypothesis minimizing empirical risk over a hypothesis class.                                      | EN: ERM is a basic principle behind supervised learning.<br>FR: L’ERM est un principe fondamental de l’apprentissage supervisé.<br>中：经验风险最小化是监督学习的基本原则。                                |
| `R(\hat{h})-R(h^\ast)`        | excess risk, excès de risque, 超额风险                                 | The gap between the risk of a learned predictor and the best achievable risk in a class or globally. | EN: Excess risk measures how far learning is from optimal prediction.<br>FR: L’excès de risque mesure l’écart à la prédiction optimale.<br>中：超额风险度量学习结果距离最优预测的差距。                      |
| —                             | generalization error, erreur de généralisation, 泛化误差               | The gap between training performance and performance on new data.                                    | EN: Small training loss does not guarantee small generalization error.<br>FR: Une faible perte d’entraînement ne garantit pas une faible erreur de généralisation.<br>中：低训练损失不保证低泛化误差。 |
| —                             | uniform convergence, convergence uniforme, 一致收敛                    | A property where empirical risks converge uniformly to population risks over a class.                | EN: Uniform convergence justifies empirical risk minimization.<br>FR: La convergence uniforme justifie la minimisation du risque empirique.<br>中：一致收敛为经验风险最小化提供依据。                     |
| `VC(\mathcal{H})`             | VC dimension, dimension VC, VC 维                                   | A combinatorial measure of the capacity of a binary hypothesis class.                                | EN: Finite VC dimension gives generalization bounds.<br>FR: Une dimension VC finie donne des bornes de généralisation.<br>中：有限 VC 维给出泛化界。                                              |
| `\mathfrak{R}_n(\mathcal{H})` | Rademacher complexity, complexité de Rademacher, Rademacher 复杂度    | A data-dependent complexity measure of a function class.                                             | EN: Rademacher complexity bounds generalization error.<br>FR: La complexité de Rademacher borne l’erreur de généralisation.<br>中：Rademacher 复杂度可界定泛化误差。                                |
| —                             | PAC learning, apprentissage PAC, PAC 学习                            | A framework requiring probably approximately correct learning from finite samples.                   | EN: PAC learning formalizes sample complexity.<br>FR: L’apprentissage PAC formalise la complexité d’échantillonnage.<br>中：PAC 学习形式化样本复杂度。                                              |

## 32.10 Classification, calibration, and evaluation metrics

| Symbol (LaTeX) | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                 | Definition                                                                      | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
| -------------- | ------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `Y\in\{0,1\}`  | binary label, étiquette binaire, 二元标签             | A response variable with two possible classes.                                  | EN: Spam detection often uses binary labels.<br>FR: La détection de spam utilise souvent des étiquettes binaires.<br>中：垃圾邮件检测常使用二元标签。                                                                                |
| `\hat{Y}`      | predicted label, étiquette prédite, 预测标签          | The class label output by a classifier.                                         | EN: A classifier may predict `\hat{Y}=1` for a positive case.<br>FR: Un classifieur peut prédire `\hat{Y}=1` pour un cas positif.<br>中：分类器可对正例预测 `\hat{Y}=1`。                                                        |
| `\eta(x)`      | regression function, fonction de régression, 回归函数 | The conditional probability `\mathbb{P}(Y=1\mid X=x)` in binary classification. | EN: The Bayes classifier thresholds `\eta(x)` at `1/2` under 0-1 loss.<br>FR: Le classifieur de Bayes seuillle `\eta(x)` à `1/2` sous perte 0-1.<br>中：在 0-1 损失下，Bayes 分类器以 `1/2` 阈值切分 `\eta(x)`。                     |
| —              | Bayes classifier, classifieur de Bayes, Bayes 分类器 | The classifier minimizing population classification risk.                       | EN: The Bayes classifier is the optimal theoretical classifier.<br>FR: Le classifieur de Bayes est le classifieur théorique optimal.<br>中：Bayes 分类器是理论最优分类器。                                                         |
| —              | confusion matrix, matrice de confusion, 混淆矩阵      | A table comparing predicted labels with true labels.                            | EN: Confusion matrices summarize classification errors.<br>FR: Les matrices de confusion résument les erreurs de classification.<br>中：混淆矩阵总结分类错误。                                                                    |
| `TP`           | true positive, vrai positif, 真阳性                  | A positive example correctly predicted as positive.                             | EN: Correctly detecting a disease case is a true positive.<br>FR: Détecter correctement un cas de maladie est un vrai positif.<br>中：正确识别患病个体是真阳性。                                                                    |
| `FP`           | false positive, faux positif, 假阳性                 | A negative example incorrectly predicted as positive.                           | EN: A false alarm is a false positive.<br>FR: Une fausse alarme est un faux positif.<br>中：误报是假阳性。                                                                                                                    |
| `FN`           | false negative, faux négatif, 假阴性                 | A positive example incorrectly predicted as negative.                           | EN: Missing a disease case is a false negative.<br>FR: Manquer un cas de maladie est un faux négatif.<br>中：漏诊是假阴性。                                                                                                   |
| `TPR`          | true positive rate, taux de vrais positifs, 真阳性率  | The proportion of positives correctly classified.                               | EN: True positive rate is also called recall or sensitivity.<br>FR: Le taux de vrais positifs s’appelle aussi rappel ou sensibilité.<br>中：真阳性率也称召回率或灵敏度。                                                             |
| `FPR`          | false positive rate, taux de faux positifs, 假阳性率  | The proportion of negatives incorrectly classified as positive.                 | EN: ROC curves plot true positive rate against false positive rate.<br>FR: Les courbes ROC tracent le taux de vrais positifs contre le taux de faux positifs.<br>中：ROC 曲线绘制真阳性率与假阳性率的关系。                             |
| `AUC`          | area under the curve, aire sous la courbe, 曲线下面积  | A scalar summary of ranking performance, often under the ROC curve.             | EN: AUC measures how well scores rank positives above negatives.<br>FR: L’AUC mesure la capacité des scores à classer les positifs avant les négatifs.<br>中：AUC 度量评分把正例排在负例前面的能力。                                    |
| —              | calibration, calibration, 校准                      | Agreement between predicted probabilities and observed frequencies.             | EN: A calibrated model assigning probability `0.8` should be correct about 80% of the time.<br>FR: Un modèle calibré prédisant `0.8` devrait être correct environ 80% du temps.<br>中：校准模型若预测概率为 `0.8`，对应事件应约 80% 发生。 |

## 32.11 Causal inference, treatment effects, and identification

| Symbol (LaTeX)      | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                               | Definition                                                                                              | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                       |
| ------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `D`                 | treatment indicator, indicateur de traitement, 处理指示变量                           | A variable indicating whether a unit receives treatment.                                                | EN: `D=1` may mean receiving a policy intervention.<br>FR: `D=1` peut signifier recevoir une intervention politique.<br>中：`D=1` 可表示接受政策干预。                                                     |
| `Y(1),Y(0)`         | potential outcomes, résultats potentiels, 潜在结果                                  | Outcomes that would occur under treatment and control conditions.                                       | EN: Only one potential outcome is observed for each unit.<br>FR: Un seul résultat potentiel est observé pour chaque unité.<br>中：每个个体只能观测到一个潜在结果。                                               |
| `Y=DY(1)+(1-D)Y(0)` | observed outcome equation, équation du résultat observé, 观测结果方程                 | The equation connecting observed outcome to potential outcomes and treatment status.                    | EN: The missing potential outcome creates the causal inference problem.<br>FR: Le résultat potentiel manquant crée le problème d’inférence causale.<br>中：缺失的潜在结果造成因果推断问题。                      |
| `ATE`               | average treatment effect, effet moyen du traitement, 平均处理效应                     | The population average difference between treated and untreated potential outcomes.                     | EN: `ATE=\mathbb{E}[Y(1)-Y(0)]`.<br>FR: `ATE=\mathbb{E}[Y(1)-Y(0)]`.<br>中：`ATE=\mathbb{E}[Y(1)-Y(0)]`。                                                                                         |
| `ATT`               | average treatment effect on the treated, effet moyen sur les traités, 处理组平均处理效应 | The average treatment effect among units that actually received treatment.                              | EN: ATT asks how treated units benefited from treatment.<br>FR: L’ATT demande comment les unités traitées ont bénéficié du traitement.<br>中：ATT 询问实际接受处理者从处理中获得的平均效应。                          |
| —                   | randomized experiment, expérience randomisée, 随机实验                              | A study where treatment assignment is randomized.                                                       | EN: Randomization supports causal identification.<br>FR: La randomisation soutient l’identification causale.<br>中：随机化支持因果识别。                                                                   |
| —                   | confounding, confusion, 混杂                                                      | Distortion caused by variables affecting both treatment assignment and outcome.                         | EN: Confounding can make association differ from causation.<br>FR: La confusion peut faire différer association et causalité.<br>中：混杂会使相关不同于因果。                                                |
| `X`                 | covariates for adjustment, covariables d’ajustement, 调整协变量                      | Variables used to adjust for confounding or improve precision.                                          | EN: Age and baseline health may be adjustment covariates.<br>FR: L’âge et la santé initiale peuvent être des covariables d’ajustement.<br>中：年龄和基线健康状况可作为调整协变量。                                 |
