| `\nabla\cdot u=0`                          | incompressibility condition, condition d’incompressibilité, 不可压条件   | A divergence-free condition expressing constant fluid density.                              | EN: Incompressible flow satisfies `\nabla\cdot u=0`.<br>FR: Un écoulement incompressible satisfait `\nabla\cdot u=0`.<br>中：不可压流满足 `\nabla\cdot u=0`。                                                                            |
| `p`                                        | pressure, pression, 压强                                              | A scalar field enforcing constraints and representing mechanical stress in fluids.          | EN: Pressure enforces incompressibility in Navier-Stokes equations.<br>FR: La pression impose l’incompressibilité dans les équations de Navier-Stokes.<br>中：压强在 Navier-Stokes 方程中施加不可压约束。                                       |
| `f(t,x,v)`                                 | distribution function, fonction de distribution, 分布函数               | A kinetic density over time, position, and velocity.                                        | EN: Kinetic theory studies particle systems through `f(t,x,v)`.<br>FR: La théorie cinétique étudie les systèmes de particules par `f(t,x,v)`.<br>中：动力学理论通过 `f(t,x,v)` 研究粒子系统。                                                   |
| —                                          | Boltzmann equation, équation de Boltzmann, Boltzmann 方程             | A kinetic PDE describing evolution of particle distribution under transport and collisions. | EN: The Boltzmann equation links microscopic collisions with macroscopic fluid behavior.<br>FR: L’équation de Boltzmann relie collisions microscopiques et comportement fluide macroscopique.<br>中：Boltzmann 方程连接微观碰撞与宏观流体行为。   |
| —                                          | renormalized solution, solution renormalisée, 重整化解                  | A generalized solution concept adapted to low regularity or nonlinear transport structures. | EN: Renormalized solutions are important in kinetic equations and transport PDEs.<br>FR: Les solutions renormalisées sont importantes dans les équations cinétiques et de transport.<br>中：重整化解在动力学方程和输运 PDE 中很重要。               |
| —                                          | mean-field limit, limite de champ moyen, 平均场极限                      | A limit deriving effective equations from many-particle systems as particle number grows.   | EN: Vlasov-type equations arise from mean-field limits.<br>FR: Les équations de type Vlasov proviennent de limites de champ moyen.<br>中：Vlasov 型方程来自平均场极限。                                                                      |
# 34. Numerical Analysis and Scientific Computing

## 34.1 Numerical approximation, errors, and floating-point arithmetic

| Symbol (LaTeX)                                            | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                       | Definition                                                                                                  | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                     |
| --------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `x^\ast`                                                  | exact solution, solution exacte, 精确解                    | The mathematically exact value or function being approximated.                                              | EN: Numerical methods approximate `x^\ast` when no closed form is available.<br>FR: Les méthodes numériques approximent `x^\ast` lorsqu’aucune forme fermée n’est disponible.<br>中：当没有闭式表达时，数值方法近似 `x^\ast`。 |
| `\hat{x}`                                                 | numerical approximation, approximation numérique, 数值近似  | A computed value intended to approximate an exact quantity.                                                 | EN: A calculator gives `\hat{x}` rather than an exact real number.<br>FR: Une calculatrice donne `\hat{x}` plutôt qu’un nombre réel exact.<br>中：计算器给出的是 `\hat{x}`，而不是精确实数。                                   |
| `e=x^\ast-\hat{x}`                                        | error, erreur, 误差                                       | The difference between the exact value and the computed approximation.                                      | EN: Error measures how far the approximation is from the truth.<br>FR: L’erreur mesure l’écart entre l’approximation et la vraie valeur.<br>中：误差度量近似值与真实值之间的差距。                                              |
| `\lvert e\rvert`                                          | absolute error, erreur absolue, 绝对误差                    | The magnitude of the numerical error.                                                                       | EN: Absolute error is measured in the same unit as the quantity.<br>FR: L’erreur absolue se mesure dans la même unité que la quantité.<br>中：绝对误差与被计算量具有相同单位。                                                 |
| `\frac{\lvert x^\ast-\hat{x}\rvert}{\lvert x^\ast\rvert}` | relative error, erreur relative, 相对误差                   | Error normalized by the magnitude of the exact value, when meaningful.                                      | EN: Relative error compares error to the scale of the answer.<br>FR: L’erreur relative compare l’erreur à l’échelle de la réponse.<br>中：相对误差把误差与答案尺度进行比较。                                                    |
| `O(h^p)`                                                  | order of accuracy, ordre de précision, 精度阶              | A statement that error decreases proportionally to `h^p` as the discretization parameter `h` tends to zero. | EN: A second-order method has error `O(h^2)`.<br>FR: Une méthode d’ordre deux a une erreur `O(h^2)`.<br>中：二阶方法的误差为 `O(h^2)`。                                                                                 |
| `h`                                                       | step size, pas, 步长                                      | A discretization length in time, space, or another numerical grid.                                          | EN: Smaller `h` often improves accuracy but increases cost.<br>FR: Un pas plus petit améliore souvent la précision mais augmente le coût.<br>中：更小的步长通常提高精度，但增加计算成本。                                          |
| `\epsilon_{\mathrm{mach}}`                                | machine epsilon, epsilon machine, 机器精度                  | The smallest relative gap distinguishable near `1` in a floating-point system.                              | EN: Machine epsilon limits achievable numerical precision.<br>FR: L’epsilon machine limite la précision numérique atteignable.<br>中：机器精度限制可达到的数值精度。                                                          |
| —                                                         | floating-point arithmetic, arithmétique flottante, 浮点运算 | Computer arithmetic representing real numbers by finite precision sign, exponent, and significand.          | EN: Floating-point arithmetic introduces rounding error.<br>FR: L’arithmétique flottante introduit des erreurs d’arrondi.<br>中：浮点运算会引入舍入误差。                                                                  |
| —                                                         | rounding error, erreur d’arrondi, 舍入误差                  | Error caused by representing or computing numbers with finite precision.                                    | EN: Repeated operations can accumulate rounding error.<br>FR: Les opérations répétées peuvent accumuler les erreurs d’arrondi.<br>中：重复运算可能累积舍入误差。                                                            |
| —                                                         | truncation error, erreur de troncature, 截断误差            | Error caused by replacing an infinite or exact mathematical process by a finite approximation.              | EN: Taylor expansions create truncation error when cut after finitely many terms.<br>FR: Les développements de Taylor créent une erreur de troncature lorsqu’ils sont coupés.<br>中：Taylor 展开截断后会产生截断误差。      |
| —                                                         | cancellation error, erreur d’annulation, 相消误差           | Loss of significant digits when subtracting nearly equal numbers.                                           | EN: Computing `1-\cos x` for small `x` can suffer cancellation.<br>FR: Calculer `1-\cos x` pour petit `x` peut subir une annulation.<br>中：小 `x` 下计算 `1-\cos x` 可能出现相消误差。                                     |

## 34.2 Conditioning, stability, and convergence

| Symbol (LaTeX) | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                | Definition                                                                                                                   | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                          |
| -------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| —              | conditioning, conditionnement, 条件性                               | The sensitivity of a mathematical problem’s solution to perturbations in input data.                                         | EN: Ill-conditioned problems amplify small data errors.<br>FR: Les problèmes mal conditionnés amplifient les petites erreurs de données.<br>中：病态问题会放大小的数据误差。                                      |
| `\kappa(A)`    | condition number, nombre de condition, 条件数                       | A number measuring sensitivity of a problem, often for solving linear systems involving `A`.                                 | EN: A large condition number suggests unstable numerical behavior.<br>FR: Un grand nombre de condition suggère un comportement numérique instable.<br>中：较大的条件数暗示数值行为不稳定。                          |
| —              | well-conditioned problem, problème bien conditionné, 良态问题        | A problem whose solution changes moderately under small input perturbations.                                                 | EN: Well-conditioned systems are easier to solve accurately.<br>FR: Les systèmes bien conditionnés sont plus faciles à résoudre précisément.<br>中：良态系统更容易被精确求解。                                   |
| —              | ill-conditioned problem, problème mal conditionné, 病态问题          | A problem whose solution can change greatly under small input perturbations.                                                 | EN: Near-singular matrices create ill-conditioned linear systems.<br>FR: Les matrices presque singulières créent des systèmes linéaires mal conditionnés.<br>中：接近奇异的矩阵会产生病态线性系统。                  |
| —              | numerical stability, stabilité numérique, 数值稳定性                  | A property that an algorithm does not excessively amplify computational errors.                                              | EN: A stable algorithm controls rounding error growth.<br>FR: Un algorithme stable contrôle la croissance des erreurs d’arrondi.<br>中：稳定算法控制舍入误差增长。                                               |
| —              | forward error, erreur avant, 前向误差                                | The difference between the computed solution and the exact solution.                                                         | EN: Forward error directly measures answer accuracy.<br>FR: L’erreur avant mesure directement la précision de la réponse.<br>中：前向误差直接度量答案精度。                                                      |
| —              | backward error, erreur arrière, 后向误差                             | The smallest input perturbation that would make the computed solution exact.                                                 | EN: Backward stability means the algorithm solves a nearby problem exactly.<br>FR: La stabilité arrière signifie que l’algorithme résout exactement un problème voisin.<br>中：后向稳定表示算法精确求解了一个邻近问题。 |
| —              | backward stable algorithm, algorithme stable en arrière, 后向稳定算法  | An algorithm whose computed result is the exact solution to a slightly perturbed input problem.                              | EN: Gaussian elimination with pivoting is often backward stable.<br>FR: L’élimination de Gauss avec pivot est souvent stable en arrière.<br>中：带主元的 Gaussian 消元通常后向稳定。                             |
| —              | consistency, consistance, 相容性                                    | A numerical method is consistent if its discrete equations approximate the continuous equations locally.                     | EN: Finite difference schemes need consistency with the PDE.<br>FR: Les schémas aux différences finies doivent être consistants avec l’EDP.<br>中：有限差分格式需要与 PDE 相容。                                |
| —              | convergence, convergence, 收敛性                                    | A numerical method is convergent if its approximate solutions approach the exact solution as discretization is refined.      | EN: A convergent method gives correct solutions as `h\to0`.<br>FR: Une méthode convergente donne les bonnes solutions quand `h\to0`.<br>中：收敛方法在 `h\to0` 时给出正确解。                                   |
| —              | stability of a scheme, stabilité d’un schéma, 格式稳定性              | A property that numerical errors remain controlled during computation.                                                       | EN: Stability is essential for time-stepping methods.<br>FR: La stabilité est essentielle pour les méthodes de pas en temps.<br>中：稳定性对时间推进方法至关重要。                                                 |
| —              | Lax equivalence theorem, théorème d’équivalence de Lax, Lax 等价定理 | A theorem stating that, for suitable linear initial-value problems, consistency plus stability is equivalent to convergence. | EN: Lax equivalence explains why stability analysis matters.<br>FR: L’équivalence de Lax explique pourquoi l’analyse de stabilité importe.<br>中：Lax 等价定理解释了为什么稳定性分析很重要。                           |

## 34.3 Interpolation, approximation, and polynomial methods

| Symbol (LaTeX)            | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                      | Definition                                                                                       | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                               |
| ------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `p_n(x)`                  | interpolating polynomial, polynôme interpolateur, 插值多项式                | A polynomial matching given function values at specified nodes.                                  | EN: Polynomial interpolation estimates values between data points.<br>FR: L’interpolation polynomiale estime les valeurs entre points de données.<br>中：多项式插值估计数据点之间的函数值。                               |
| `x_i`                     | interpolation node, nœud d’interpolation, 插值节点                         | A point where interpolation conditions are imposed.                                              | EN: Chebyshev nodes reduce oscillation compared with equally spaced nodes.<br>FR: Les nœuds de Chebyshev réduisent les oscillations par rapport aux nœuds équidistants.<br>中：Chebyshev 节点相比等距节点可减少振荡。  |
| `L_i(x)`                  | Lagrange basis polynomial, polynôme de base de Lagrange, Lagrange 基多项式 | A basis polynomial equal to `1` at one interpolation node and `0` at the others.                 | EN: Lagrange interpolation writes `p_n(x)` as a weighted sum of data values.<br>FR: L’interpolation de Lagrange écrit `p_n(x)` comme somme pondérée des données.<br>中：Lagrange 插值把 `p_n(x)` 写成数据值的加权和。 |
| —                         | Newton interpolation, interpolation de Newton, Newton 插值               | A polynomial interpolation method using divided differences.                                     | EN: Newton form makes adding new interpolation nodes convenient.<br>FR: La forme de Newton facilite l’ajout de nouveaux nœuds.<br>中：Newton 形式便于加入新的插值节点。                                               |
| —                         | divided difference, différence divisée, 差商                             | A recursive quantity used to construct Newton interpolation polynomials.                         | EN: Divided differences generalize slopes to higher order.<br>FR: Les différences divisées généralisent les pentes à l’ordre supérieur.<br>中：差商把斜率推广到高阶情形。                                             |
| —                         | Runge phenomenon, phénomène de Runge, Runge 现象                         | Oscillation near interval endpoints caused by high-degree interpolation at equally spaced nodes. | EN: High-degree interpolation can behave badly on equally spaced grids.<br>FR: L’interpolation de haut degré peut mal se comporter sur des grilles équidistantes.<br>中：等距网格上的高次插值可能表现很差。               |
| `T_n(x)`                  | Chebyshev polynomial, polynôme de Chebyshev, Chebyshev 多项式             | A special polynomial useful for near-optimal approximation on intervals.                         | EN: Chebyshev polynomials help reduce maximum approximation error.<br>FR: Les polynômes de Chebyshev aident à réduire l’erreur maximale d’approximation.<br>中：Chebyshev 多项式有助于降低最大近似误差。                |
| —                         | spline, spline, 样条                                                     | A piecewise polynomial function joined with specified smoothness.                                | EN: Cubic splines interpolate data smoothly.<br>FR: Les splines cubiques interpolent les données de façon lisse.<br>中：三次样条可平滑插值数据。                                                                     |
| —                         | least squares approximation, approximation aux moindres carrés, 最小二乘近似 | Approximation minimizing the sum or integral of squared residuals.                               | EN: Least squares fits a curve when exact interpolation is undesirable.<br>FR: Les moindres carrés ajustent une courbe lorsque l’interpolation exacte est indésirable.<br>中：当不希望精确穿过所有点时，可用最小二乘拟合曲线。   |
| `\lVert f-p\rVert_\infty` | uniform approximation error, erreur d’approximation uniforme, 一致近似误差   | The maximum pointwise difference between a function and its approximation.                       | EN: Uniform approximation controls worst-case error over an interval.<br>FR: L’approximation uniforme contrôle l’erreur maximale sur un intervalle.<br>中：一致近似控制区间上的最坏误差。                               |
| —                         | best approximation, meilleure approximation, 最佳近似                      | An approximation minimizing an error norm over a chosen function class.                          | EN: Best polynomial approximation depends on the chosen norm.<br>FR: La meilleure approximation polynomiale dépend de la norme choisie.<br>中：最佳多项式近似取决于所选范数。                                           |
| —                         | spectral approximation, approximation spectrale, 谱近似                   | Approximation using global basis functions such as Fourier or orthogonal polynomials.            | EN: Spectral methods can converge very fast for smooth functions.<br>FR: Les méthodes spectrales peuvent converger très vite pour les fonctions lisses.<br>中：对光滑函数，谱方法可非常快速收敛。                         |

## 34.4 Numerical differentiation and integration

| Symbol (LaTeX)             | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                   | Definition                                                                                                    | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
| -------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `\frac{f(x+h)-f(x)}{h}`    | forward difference, différence avant, 前向差分                          | A finite difference approximation to the first derivative.                                                    | EN: Forward difference is simple but only first-order accurate.<br>FR: La différence avant est simple mais seulement d’ordre un.<br>中：前向差分简单，但只有一阶精度。                                                                                 |
| `\frac{f(x)-f(x-h)}{h}`    | backward difference, différence arrière, 后向差分                       | A finite difference approximation using a point behind `x`.                                                   | EN: Backward differences are common in implicit time schemes.<br>FR: Les différences arrière sont courantes dans les schémas temporels implicites.<br>中：后向差分常用于隐式时间格式。                                                                |
| `\frac{f(x+h)-f(x-h)}{2h}` | central difference, différence centrée, 中心差分                        | A symmetric finite difference approximation to the first derivative.                                          | EN: Central difference is usually more accurate than forward difference.<br>FR: La différence centrée est généralement plus précise que la différence avant.<br>中：中心差分通常比前向差分更精确。                                                     |
| `D_h f`                    | discrete derivative operator, opérateur de dérivée discrète, 离散导数算子 | An operator approximating a derivative on a discrete grid.                                                    | EN: Discrete derivative operators replace calculus by algebra on grids.<br>FR: Les opérateurs de dérivée discrète remplacent le calcul par l’algèbre sur grilles.<br>中：离散导数算子在网格上用代数替代微积分。                                            |
| —                          | numerical quadrature, quadrature numérique, 数值求积                    | Numerical approximation of definite integrals.                                                                | EN: Quadrature rules approximate areas under curves.<br>FR: Les formules de quadrature approximent les aires sous les courbes.<br>中：数值求积公式近似曲线下的面积。                                                                                   |
| `Q_n(f)`                   | quadrature rule, formule de quadrature, 求积公式                        | A weighted sum approximating an integral of `f`.                                                              | EN: A quadrature rule uses function values and weights.<br>FR: Une formule de quadrature utilise des valeurs de fonction et des poids.<br>中：求积公式使用函数值和权重。                                                                             |
| `\sum_i w_i f(x_i)`        | weighted quadrature sum, somme pondérée de quadrature, 加权求积和        | A finite weighted sum used to approximate an integral.                                                        | EN: Nodes `x_i` and weights `w_i` define the rule.<br>FR: Les nœuds `x_i` et les poids `w_i` définissent la règle.<br>中：节点 `x_i` 与权重 `w_i` 定义求积公式。                                                                                    |
| —                          | trapezoidal rule, règle des trapèzes, 梯形法则                          | A quadrature rule approximating an integral by trapezoids.                                                    | EN: The trapezoidal rule is effective for smooth periodic functions.<br>FR: La règle des trapèzes est efficace pour les fonctions périodiques lisses.<br>中：梯形法则对光滑周期函数效果较好。                                                           |
| —                          | Simpson rule, règle de Simpson, Simpson 法则                          | A quadrature rule using quadratic interpolation over subintervals.                                            | EN: Simpson rule has higher accuracy than the basic trapezoidal rule for smooth functions.<br>FR: La règle de Simpson est plus précise que la règle des trapèzes de base pour les fonctions lisses.<br>中：对光滑函数，Simpson 法则比基本梯形法则精度更高。 |
| —                          | Gaussian quadrature, quadrature de Gauss, Gaussian 求积               | A high-order quadrature method choosing nodes and weights to integrate polynomials exactly up to high degree. | EN: Gaussian quadrature is efficient for smooth integrands.<br>FR: La quadrature de Gauss est efficace pour les intégrandes lisses.<br>中：Gaussian 求积对光滑被积函数很高效。                                                                       |
| —                          | adaptive quadrature, quadrature adaptative, 自适应求积                   | A quadrature method refining intervals where the integrand is difficult.                                      | EN: Adaptive quadrature places more points near sharp features.<br>FR: La quadrature adaptative place plus de points près des zones abruptes.<br>中：自适应求积在剧烈变化区域放置更多节点。                                                                |
| —                          | Monte Carlo integration, intégration de Monte Carlo, Monte Carlo 积分 | Integral approximation using random samples.                                                                  | EN: Monte Carlo integration is useful in high dimensions.<br>FR: L’intégration de Monte Carlo est utile en grande dimension.<br>中：Monte Carlo 积分适用于高维问题。                                                                              |

## 34.5 Linear systems, matrix factorizations, and direct solvers

| Symbol (LaTeX)           | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                         | Definition                                                                               | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |
| ------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `Ax=b`                   | linear system, système linéaire, 线性方程组                                    | A system of linear equations with coefficient matrix `A` and right-hand side `b`.        | EN: Discretized PDEs often produce large linear systems.<br>FR: Les EDP discrétisées produisent souvent de grands systèmes linéaires.<br>中：离散化 PDE 常产生大型线性方程组。                                                              |
| `A^{-1}`                 | matrix inverse, inverse matricielle, 矩阵逆                                  | A matrix satisfying `A^{-1}A=I` when `A` is invertible.                                  | EN: Numerically, one usually solves `Ax=b` without explicitly forming `A^{-1}`.<br>FR: Numériquement, on résout souvent `Ax=b` sans former explicitement `A^{-1}`.<br>中：数值上通常不显式求 `A^{-1}`，而是直接解 `Ax=b`。                    |
| —                        | Gaussian elimination, élimination de Gauss, Gaussian 消元                   | A direct method reducing a linear system to triangular form.                             | EN: Gaussian elimination is a standard dense linear solver.<br>FR: L’élimination de Gauss est un solveur linéaire dense standard.<br>中：Gaussian 消元是标准稠密线性求解法。                                                               |
| —                        | pivoting, pivotage, 主元选取                                                  | A strategy of swapping rows or columns to improve numerical stability.                   | EN: Pivoting reduces the risk of division by tiny numbers.<br>FR: Le pivotage réduit le risque de division par de très petits nombres.<br>中：主元选取降低除以极小数的风险。                                                                 |
| `A=LU`                   | LU factorization, factorisation LU, LU 分解                                 | A factorization of a matrix into lower and upper triangular factors.                     | EN: LU factorization solves multiple systems with the same matrix efficiently.<br>FR: La factorisation LU résout efficacement plusieurs systèmes avec la même matrice.<br>中：LU 分解可高效求解同一矩阵对应的多个线性系统。                        |
| `A=QR`                   | QR factorization, factorisation QR, QR 分解                                 | A factorization into an orthogonal or unitary matrix and an upper triangular matrix.     | EN: QR factorization is important for least squares problems.<br>FR: La factorisation QR est importante pour les problèmes de moindres carrés.<br>中：QR 分解对最小二乘问题很重要。                                                        |
| `A=LL^\top`              | Cholesky factorization, factorisation de Cholesky, Cholesky 分解            | A factorization for symmetric positive definite matrices.                                | EN: Cholesky factorization is faster than general LU for positive definite systems.<br>FR: La factorisation de Cholesky est plus rapide que LU général pour les systèmes définis positifs.<br>中：对正定系统，Cholesky 分解比一般 LU 更快。 |
| `A=U\Sigma V^\top`       | singular value decomposition, décomposition en valeurs singulières, 奇异值分解 | A factorization expressing a matrix through singular values and orthogonal directions.   | EN: SVD is used for rank approximation and least squares.<br>FR: La SVD sert à l’approximation de rang et aux moindres carrés.<br>中：SVD 用于秩近似和最小二乘。                                                                         |
| `\operatorname{rank}(A)` | numerical rank, rang numérique, 数值秩                                       | The effective rank of a matrix under a tolerance, often determined from singular values. | EN: Numerical rank may differ from exact symbolic rank under noise.<br>FR: Le rang numérique peut différer du rang symbolique exact sous bruit.<br>中：有噪声时，数值秩可能不同于精确符号秩。                                                    |
| —                        | sparse matrix, matrice creuse, 稀疏矩阵                                       | A matrix with mostly zero entries.                                                       | EN: Finite element and finite difference methods often produce sparse matrices.<br>FR: Les méthodes d’éléments finis et de différences finies produisent souvent des matrices creuses.<br>中：有限元和有限差分方法常产生稀疏矩阵。              |
| —                        | fill-in, remplissage, 填充                                                  | New nonzero entries created during matrix factorization.                                 | EN: Reordering can reduce fill-in in sparse factorization.<br>FR: Le réordonnancement peut réduire le remplissage dans la factorisation creuse.<br>中：重排序可减少稀疏分解中的填充。                                                        |
| —                        | preconditioning, préconditionnement, 预条件化                                 | Transforming a linear system to improve conditioning and solver convergence.             | EN: Good preconditioners greatly accelerate iterative solvers.<br>FR: De bons préconditionneurs accélèrent fortement les solveurs itératifs.<br>中：好的预条件器能大幅加速迭代求解器。                                                         |

## 34.6 Iterative methods and Krylov subspace algorithms

| Symbol (LaTeX)         | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                                           | Definition                                                                                        | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                               |
| ---------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `x^{(k)}`              | kth iterate, k-ième itéré, 第 k 次迭代                                                          | The approximation produced after `k` iterations of an algorithm.                                  | EN: Iterative solvers refine `x^{(k)}` step by step.<br>FR: Les solveurs itératifs raffinent `x^{(k)}` étape par étape.<br>中：迭代求解器逐步改进 `x^{(k)}`。                                                      |
| `r^{(k)}=b-Ax^{(k)}`   | residual, résidu, 残差                                                                        | The mismatch remaining in a linear system for an approximate solution.                            | EN: Small residual often indicates a good approximate solution.<br>FR: Un petit résidu indique souvent une bonne solution approchée.<br>中：较小残差通常表示近似解较好。                                               |
| —                      | Jacobi method, méthode de Jacobi, Jacobi 方法                                                 | An iterative linear solver updating each component using previous-iteration values.               | EN: Jacobi iteration is simple but may converge slowly.<br>FR: L’itération de Jacobi est simple mais peut converger lentement.<br>中：Jacobi 迭代简单，但可能收敛较慢。                                               |
| —                      | Gauss-Seidel method, méthode de Gauss-Seidel, Gauss-Seidel 方法                               | An iterative solver using the newest available component values during updates.                   | EN: Gauss-Seidel often converges faster than Jacobi.<br>FR: Gauss-Seidel converge souvent plus vite que Jacobi.<br>中：Gauss-Seidel 通常比 Jacobi 收敛更快。                                                     |
| —                      | successive over-relaxation, sur-relaxation successive, 逐次超松弛                                | An accelerated Gauss-Seidel-type method using a relaxation parameter.                             | EN: SOR can be fast with a well-chosen relaxation parameter.<br>FR: La SOR peut être rapide avec un bon paramètre de relaxation.<br>中：选择合适松弛参数时，SOR 可较快收敛。                                             |
| `\mathcal{K}_k(A,r_0)` | Krylov subspace, sous-espace de Krylov, Krylov 子空间                                          | The span of `r_0,Ar_0,\ldots,A^{k-1}r_0`, used by many iterative solvers.                         | EN: Krylov methods search for solutions in expanding subspaces.<br>FR: Les méthodes de Krylov cherchent les solutions dans des sous-espaces croissants.<br>中：Krylov 方法在逐渐扩大的子空间中寻找解。                   |
| `CG`                   | conjugate gradient method, méthode du gradient conjugué, 共轭梯度法                              | A Krylov method for symmetric positive definite linear systems.                                   | EN: CG is standard for large sparse positive definite systems.<br>FR: Le gradient conjugué est standard pour les grands systèmes creux définis positifs.<br>中：共轭梯度法是大型稀疏正定系统的标准方法。                     |
| `GMRES`                | generalized minimal residual method, méthode du résidu minimal généralisé, 广义最小残差法          | A Krylov method minimizing residual norm over a Krylov subspace for general nonsymmetric systems. | EN: GMRES is widely used for nonsymmetric sparse systems.<br>FR: GMRES est largement utilisé pour les systèmes creux non symétriques.<br>中：GMRES 广泛用于非对称稀疏系统。                                          |
| `BiCGSTAB`             | stabilized biconjugate gradient method, méthode du gradient biconjugué stabilisée, 稳定双共轭梯度法 | An iterative method for nonsymmetric linear systems designed to improve convergence smoothness.   | EN: BiCGSTAB can be cheaper per iteration than GMRES.<br>FR: BiCGSTAB peut être moins coûteux par itération que GMRES.<br>中：BiCGSTAB 每次迭代成本可能低于 GMRES。                                                 |
| —                      | multigrid method, méthode multigrille, 多重网格法                                                | A solver using multiple grid resolutions to eliminate errors at different scales.                 | EN: Multigrid methods can solve elliptic problems in near-linear time.<br>FR: Les méthodes multigrilles peuvent résoudre des problèmes elliptiques en temps presque linéaire.<br>中：多重网格法可近似线性时间求解椭圆问题。 |
| —                      | relaxation method, méthode de relaxation, 松弛法                                               | An iterative smoothing method reducing high-frequency error components.                           | EN: Relaxation is used as a smoother in multigrid methods.<br>FR: La relaxation sert de lisseur dans les méthodes multigrilles.<br>中：松弛法在多重网格中用作平滑器。                                                   |
| —                      | convergence criterion, critère de convergence, 收敛准则                                         | A rule for stopping an iterative algorithm when accuracy is sufficient.                           | EN: A residual tolerance is a common convergence criterion.<br>FR: Une tolérance sur le résidu est un critère de convergence courant.<br>中：残差容差是常见收敛准则。                                                |

## 34.7 Nonlinear equations and root-finding methods

| Symbol (LaTeX)                       | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                           | Definition                                                                                  | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| ------------------------------------ | ----------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `f(x)=0`                             | root-finding problem, problème de recherche de racine, 求根问题 | The problem of finding an input where a function equals zero.                               | EN: Solving `f(x)=0` is central in nonlinear computation.<br>FR: Résoudre `f(x)=0` est central dans le calcul non linéaire.<br>中：求解 `f(x)=0` 是非线性计算中的核心问题。                                                              |
| `x^\ast`                             | root, racine, 根                                             | A value satisfying `f(x^\ast)=0`.                                                           | EN: The root of `x^2-2=0` is `\sqrt{2}` or `-\sqrt{2}`.<br>FR: Les racines de `x^2-2=0` sont `\sqrt{2}` et `-\sqrt{2}`.<br>中：`x^2-2=0` 的根是 `\sqrt{2}` 和 `-\sqrt{2}`。                                                    |
| —                                    | bisection method, méthode de dichotomie, 二分法                | A robust root-finding method repeatedly halving an interval where a sign change occurs.     | EN: Bisection is slow but reliable for continuous scalar equations.<br>FR: La dichotomie est lente mais fiable pour les équations scalaires continues.<br>中：二分法较慢，但对连续标量方程可靠。                                           |
| `x_{k+1}=x_k-\frac{f(x_k)}{f'(x_k)}` | Newton method, méthode de Newton, Newton 方法                 | An iterative root-finding method using local linearization.                                 | EN: Newton’s method can converge quadratically near a simple root.<br>FR: La méthode de Newton peut converger quadratiquement près d’une racine simple.<br>中：Newton 方法在简单根附近可二次收敛。                                      |
| —                                    | secant method, méthode de la sécante, 割线法                   | A root-finding method approximating derivatives by finite differences between two iterates. | EN: The secant method avoids computing exact derivatives.<br>FR: La méthode de la sécante évite de calculer les dérivées exactes.<br>中：割线法避免计算精确导数。                                                                     |
| `F(x)=0`                             | nonlinear system, système non linéaire, 非线性方程组              | A system of equations where unknowns enter nonlinearly.                                     | EN: Discretized nonlinear PDEs often produce nonlinear systems.<br>FR: Les EDP non linéaires discrétisées produisent souvent des systèmes non linéaires.<br>中：离散化非线性 PDE 常产生非线性方程组。                                     |
| `J_F(x)`                             | Jacobian matrix, matrice jacobienne, Jacobian 矩阵            | The matrix of first derivatives of a vector-valued function.                                | EN: Multivariate Newton methods solve linear systems with the Jacobian.<br>FR: Les méthodes de Newton multivariées résolvent des systèmes linéaires avec la jacobienne.<br>中：多变量 Newton 方法用 Jacobian 矩阵求解线性系统。          |
| `F'(x_k)s_k=-F(x_k)`                 | Newton step equation, équation du pas de Newton, Newton 步方程 | The linear system defining the Newton correction `s_k`.                                     | EN: Solving the Newton step is the main cost in large nonlinear systems.<br>FR: Résoudre le pas de Newton est le coût principal dans les grands systèmes non linéaires.<br>中：在大型非线性系统中，求 Newton 步是主要成本。                 |
| —                                    | fixed-point iteration, itération de point fixe, 不动点迭代       | An iteration of the form `x_{k+1}=g(x_k)` seeking `x=g(x)`.                                 | EN: Fixed-point iteration converges when `g` is contractive under suitable conditions.<br>FR: L’itération de point fixe converge lorsque `g` est contractante sous bonnes conditions.<br>中：在适当条件下，若 `g` 为压缩映射，则不动点迭代收敛。 |
| —                                    | contraction mapping, application contractante, 压缩映射         | A map bringing points closer by a uniform factor less than one.                             | EN: Contraction mappings have unique fixed points in complete metric spaces.<br>FR: Les applications contractantes ont un point fixe unique dans les espaces métriques complets.<br>中：完备度量空间中的压缩映射有唯一不动点。               |
| —                                    | line search, recherche linéaire, 线搜索                        | A globalization strategy choosing a step length to ensure sufficient decrease or progress.  | EN: Line search stabilizes Newton and quasi-Newton methods.<br>FR: La recherche linéaire stabilise les méthodes de Newton et quasi-Newton.<br>中：线搜索可稳定 Newton 和拟 Newton 方法。                                             |
| —                                    | trust-region method, méthode de région de confiance, 信赖域方法  | A method solving local subproblems inside a region where the model is trusted.              | EN: Trust-region methods control unreliable large Newton steps.<br>FR: Les méthodes de région de confiance contrôlent les grands pas de Newton peu fiables.<br>中：信赖域方法控制不可靠的大 Newton 步。                                 |

## 34.8 Numerical ODEs and time-stepping methods

| Symbol (LaTeX)                     | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                        | Definition                                                                                                              | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                     |
| ---------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `y'(t)=f(t,y)`                     | ordinary differential equation, équation différentielle ordinaire, 常微分方程 | An equation relating a function of one variable to its derivatives.                                                     | EN: Population models often use ODEs.<br>FR: Les modèles de population utilisent souvent des EDO.<br>中：种群模型常使用常微分方程。                                                                                         |
| `y_{n+1}=y_n+h f(t_n,y_n)`         | explicit Euler method, méthode d’Euler explicite, 显式 Euler 方法            | A first-order time-stepping method using the slope at the current step.                                                 | EN: Explicit Euler is simple but conditionally stable.<br>FR: Euler explicite est simple mais conditionnellement stable.<br>中：显式 Euler 方法简单，但通常条件稳定。                                                         |
| `y_{n+1}=y_n+h f(t_{n+1},y_{n+1})` | implicit Euler method, méthode d’Euler implicite, 隐式 Euler 方法            | A first-order method evaluating the slope at the next step, requiring an implicit solve.                                | EN: Implicit Euler is useful for stiff problems.<br>FR: Euler implicite est utile pour les problèmes raides.<br>中：隐式 Euler 方法适用于刚性问题。                                                                        |
| —                                  | Runge-Kutta method, méthode de Runge-Kutta, Runge-Kutta 方法               | A family of time-stepping methods using several intermediate slope evaluations.                                         | EN: Classical fourth-order Runge-Kutta is widely used for nonstiff ODEs.<br>FR: Runge-Kutta classique d’ordre quatre est largement utilisé pour les EDO non raides.<br>中：经典四阶 Runge-Kutta 广泛用于非刚性 ODE。       |
| `k_i`                              | stage derivative, dérivée d’étape, 阶段导数                                  | An intermediate slope evaluation in a Runge-Kutta method.                                                               | EN: Runge-Kutta methods combine stage derivatives by weighted sums.<br>FR: Les méthodes de Runge-Kutta combinent les dérivées d’étape par sommes pondérées.<br>中：Runge-Kutta 方法通过加权和组合阶段导数。                  |
| —                                  | multistep method, méthode multipas, 多步法                                  | A method using several previous time levels to advance the solution.                                                    | EN: Adams methods are multistep methods.<br>FR: Les méthodes d’Adams sont des méthodes multipas.<br>中：Adams 方法属于多步法。                                                                                         |
| —                                  | Adams-Bashforth method, méthode d’Adams-Bashforth, Adams-Bashforth 方法    | An explicit multistep method for ODE integration.                                                                       | EN: Adams-Bashforth methods extrapolate previous slopes.<br>FR: Les méthodes d’Adams-Bashforth extrapolent les pentes précédentes.<br>中：Adams-Bashforth 方法外推之前的斜率。                                           |
| —                                  | Adams-Moulton method, méthode d’Adams-Moulton, Adams-Moulton 方法          | An implicit multistep method for ODE integration.                                                                       | EN: Adams-Moulton methods are often used in predictor-corrector schemes.<br>FR: Les méthodes d’Adams-Moulton sont souvent utilisées dans des schémas prédicteur-correcteur.<br>中：Adams-Moulton 方法常用于预测-校正格式。 |
| —                                  | local truncation error, erreur de troncature locale, 局部截断误差              | The error made in one step assuming previous values are exact.                                                          | EN: Local truncation error determines the formal order of a time method.<br>FR: L’erreur de troncature locale détermine l’ordre formel d’une méthode temporelle.<br>中：局部截断误差决定时间方法的形式阶。                      |
| —                                  | global error, erreur globale, 全局误差                                       | The accumulated error over all time steps.                                                                              | EN: Global error includes both local errors and their propagation.<br>FR: L’erreur globale inclut les erreurs locales et leur propagation.<br>中：全局误差包括局部误差及其传播。                                              |
| —                                  | stiffness, raideur, 刚性                                                   | A property where some solution components evolve on much faster time scales than others, constraining explicit methods. | EN: Stiff equations often require implicit solvers.<br>FR: Les équations raides exigent souvent des solveurs implicites.<br>中：刚性方程通常需要隐式求解器。                                                                 |
| —                                  | adaptive time stepping, pas de temps adaptatif, 自适应时间步长                  | A strategy changing step size based on estimated error.                                                                 | EN: Adaptive time stepping uses small steps only when needed.<br>FR: Le pas adaptatif utilise de petits pas seulement si nécessaire.<br>中：自适应时间步长只在需要时使用小步长。                                                 |

## 34.9 Finite difference, finite volume, and finite element methods

| Symbol (LaTeX)            | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                               | Definition                                                                                    | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| ------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| —                         | discretization, discrétisation, 离散化                             | The process of replacing a continuous problem by a finite-dimensional numerical problem.      | EN: PDE discretization turns differential equations into algebraic systems.<br>FR: La discrétisation des EDP transforme les équations différentielles en systèmes algébriques.<br>中：PDE 离散化把微分方程转化为代数系统。 |
| `x_i=ih`                  | grid point, point de grille, 网格点                                | A point in a computational grid.                                                              | EN: Finite difference methods store unknowns at grid points.<br>FR: Les différences finies stockent les inconnues aux points de grille.<br>中：有限差分方法把未知量存储在网格点上。                                          |
| —                         | finite difference method, méthode des différences finies, 有限差分法 | A method approximating derivatives by difference quotients on grids.                          | EN: Finite difference methods are simple on rectangular domains.<br>FR: Les différences finies sont simples sur des domaines rectangulaires.<br>中：有限差分法在矩形区域上较简单。                                        |
| —                         | finite volume method, méthode des volumes finis, 有限体积法          | A method enforcing conservation laws over small control volumes.                              | EN: Finite volume methods are popular for fluid dynamics and shocks.<br>FR: Les volumes finis sont populaires en dynamique des fluides et pour les chocs.<br>中：有限体积法常用于流体动力学和激波问题。                       |
| —                         | finite element method, méthode des éléments finis, 有限元法         | A method using weak formulations and piecewise basis functions on meshes.                     | EN: Finite elements handle complex geometries well.<br>FR: Les éléments finis gèrent bien les géométries complexes.<br>中：有限元法很适合复杂几何。                                                                    |
| `V_h`                     | finite element space, espace d’éléments finis, 有限元空间            | A finite-dimensional function space used to approximate a solution space.                     | EN: `V_h` may consist of piecewise linear functions on a mesh.<br>FR: `V_h` peut être constitué de fonctions linéaires par morceaux sur un maillage.<br>中：`V_h` 可由网格上的分片线性函数组成。                          |
| `\phi_i`                  | basis function, fonction de base, 基函数                           | A function used as a basis element in a discrete approximation space.                         | EN: Finite element solutions are linear combinations of basis functions.<br>FR: Les solutions éléments finis sont des combinaisons linéaires de fonctions de base.<br>中：有限元解是基函数的线性组合。                   |
| `K_{ij}=a(\phi_j,\phi_i)` | stiffness matrix, matrice de rigidité, 刚度矩阵                     | A matrix representing the bilinear form of a differential operator in a finite element basis. | EN: Elliptic finite element methods produce stiffness matrices.<br>FR: Les méthodes éléments finis elliptiques produisent des matrices de rigidité.<br>中：椭圆有限元方法产生刚度矩阵。                                  |
| `M_{ij}=\int\phi_i\phi_j` | mass matrix, matrice de masse, 质量矩阵                             | A matrix representing inner products of basis functions.                                      | EN: Time-dependent finite element methods often use mass matrices.<br>FR: Les éléments finis dépendant du temps utilisent souvent des matrices de masse.<br>中：时间依赖有限元方法常使用质量矩阵。                          |
| —                         | mesh, maillage, 网格                                              | A partition of the computational domain into elements or cells.                               | EN: Mesh quality affects accuracy and conditioning.<br>FR: La qualité du maillage influence précision et conditionnement.<br>中：网格质量影响精度和条件性。                                                             |
| —                         | element, élément, 单元                                            | A small subdomain in a mesh, such as a triangle, quadrilateral, or tetrahedron.               | EN: Triangular elements are common in two-dimensional finite element meshes.<br>FR: Les éléments triangulaires sont courants dans les maillages éléments finis bidimensionnels.<br>中：三角形单元常见于二维有限元网格。    |
| —                         | CFL condition, condition CFL, CFL 条件                            | A stability condition relating time step to mesh size and wave speed.                         | EN: Explicit schemes for hyperbolic PDEs often require a CFL condition.<br>FR: Les schémas explicites pour EDP hyperboliques exigent souvent une condition CFL.<br>中：双曲型 PDE 的显式格式通常需要 CFL 条件。           |

## 34.10 Eigenvalue algorithms, SVD, and dimensional reduction

| Symbol (LaTeX) | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                          | Definition                                                                                            | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
| -------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `Ax=\lambda x` | matrix eigenvalue problem, problème aux valeurs propres matriciel, 矩阵特征值问题 | The problem of finding scalars `\lambda` and nonzero vectors `x` satisfying `Ax=\lambda x`.           | EN: Eigenvalue problems appear in vibration and stability analysis.<br>FR: Les problèmes aux valeurs propres apparaissent en vibration et analyse de stabilité.<br>中：特征值问题出现在振动和稳定性分析中。                              |
| `\lambda_i`    | eigenvalue, valeur propre, 特征值                                             | A scalar describing how a matrix stretches an eigenvector.                                            | EN: Eigenvalues of a covariance matrix determine principal component variances.<br>FR: Les valeurs propres d’une matrice de covariance déterminent les variances des composantes principales.<br>中：协方差矩阵的特征值决定主成分方差。 |
| `v_i`          | eigenvector, vecteur propre, 特征向量                                          | A nonzero vector whose direction is preserved by a matrix.                                            | EN: Eigenvectors give principal directions of a linear transformation.<br>FR: Les vecteurs propres donnent les directions principales d’une transformation linéaire.<br>中：特征向量给出线性变换的主方向。                            |
| —              | power iteration, méthode de la puissance, 幂迭代                              | An algorithm for approximating a dominant eigenvalue and eigenvector.                                 | EN: Power iteration repeatedly applies the matrix to a vector.<br>FR: La méthode de la puissance applique répétitivement la matrice à un vecteur.<br>中：幂迭代反复把矩阵作用到向量上。                                               |
| —              | inverse iteration, itération inverse, 反幂迭代                                 | An eigenvalue algorithm applying shifted inverse solves to approximate selected eigenvectors.         | EN: Inverse iteration can target eigenvalues near a chosen shift.<br>FR: L’itération inverse peut cibler des valeurs propres près d’un décalage choisi.<br>中：反幂迭代可针对接近选定移位的特征值。                                      |
| —              | Rayleigh quotient, quotient de Rayleigh, Rayleigh 商                        | The scalar `x^\top Ax/(x^\top x)` used to estimate eigenvalues of symmetric matrices.                 | EN: Rayleigh quotients appear in variational eigenvalue characterizations.<br>FR: Les quotients de Rayleigh apparaissent dans les caractérisations variationnelles des valeurs propres.<br>中：Rayleigh 商出现在特征值的变分刻画中。 |
| —              | QR algorithm, algorithme QR, QR 算法                                         | A fundamental algorithm for computing matrix eigenvalues.                                             | EN: QR iteration is a standard dense eigenvalue method.<br>FR: L’itération QR est une méthode dense standard pour les valeurs propres.<br>中：QR 迭代是标准的稠密矩阵特征值方法。                                                      |
| `\sigma_i`     | singular value, valeur singulière, 奇异值                                     | A nonnegative scalar measuring the action of a matrix along singular directions.                      | EN: Singular values measure stretching by a matrix.<br>FR: Les valeurs singulières mesurent l’étirement par une matrice.<br>中：奇异值度量矩阵的拉伸作用。                                                                          |
| —              | low-rank approximation, approximation de bas rang, 低秩近似                    | Approximation of a matrix by another matrix of smaller rank.                                          | EN: Truncated SVD gives optimal low-rank approximation in common norms.<br>FR: La SVD tronquée donne une approximation de bas rang optimale pour des normes usuelles.<br>中：截断 SVD 在常见范数下给出最优低秩近似。                    |
| `A_k`          | rank-k approximation, approximation de rang k, k 秩近似                       | An approximation to a matrix constrained to rank at most `k`.                                         | EN: Rank-`k` approximations compress high-dimensional data.<br>FR: Les approximations de rang `k` compressent les données de grande dimension.<br>中：`k` 秩近似压缩高维数据。                                                   |
| `PCA`          | principal component analysis, analyse en composantes principales, 主成分分析    | A dimensional reduction method based on eigenvectors or singular vectors of covariance-like matrices. | EN: PCA finds directions of maximum variance.<br>FR: L’ACP trouve les directions de variance maximale.<br>中：PCA 寻找方差最大的方向。                                                                                           |
| —              | randomized linear algebra, algèbre linéaire randomisée, 随机化线性代数            | Algorithms using random projections or sampling to approximate large matrix computations.             | EN: Randomized SVD accelerates low-rank approximation for large matrices.<br>FR: La SVD randomisée accélère l’approximation de bas rang pour grandes matrices.<br>中：随机化 SVD 加速大型矩阵的低秩近似。                             |

## 34.11 Scientific computing workflows, simulation, and uncertainty

| Symbol (LaTeX) | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                   | Definition                                                                                      | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
| -------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| —              | simulation, simulation, 仿真                                          | Computational imitation of a mathematical, physical, or statistical system.                     | EN: Climate models simulate coupled atmosphere-ocean dynamics.<br>FR: Les modèles climatiques simulent la dynamique couplée atmosphère-océan.<br>中：气候模型仿真大气-海洋耦合动力学。                                                  |
| —              | numerical experiment, expérience numérique, 数值实验                    | A computational experiment designed to test hypotheses or compare methods.                      | EN: Numerical experiments compare accuracy and runtime of algorithms.<br>FR: Les expériences numériques comparent précision et temps de calcul des algorithmes.<br>中：数值实验比较算法精度和运行时间。                                 |
| —              | benchmark problem, problème de référence, 基准问题                      | A standard test problem used to evaluate algorithms.                                            | EN: Benchmark problems make solver comparisons reproducible.<br>FR: Les problèmes de référence rendent les comparaisons de solveurs reproductibles.<br>中：基准问题使求解器比较可复现。                                               |
| —              | verification, vérification, 验证                                      | Checking whether the numerical implementation correctly solves the intended mathematical model. | EN: Code verification asks whether the equations are solved correctly.<br>FR: La vérification du code demande si les équations sont résolues correctement.<br>中：代码验证检查方程是否被正确求解。                                      |
| —              | validation, validation, 确证                                          | Checking whether the mathematical model adequately represents reality or target data.           | EN: Model validation compares simulations with experiments.<br>FR: La validation du modèle compare les simulations aux expériences.<br>中：模型确证把仿真结果与实验进行比较。                                                            |
| —              | grid refinement study, étude de raffinement de grille, 网格加密研究       | A study of how numerical solutions change as the mesh is refined.                               | EN: Grid refinement reveals convergence behavior.<br>FR: Le raffinement de grille révèle le comportement de convergence.<br>中：网格加密揭示收敛行为。                                                                             |
| —              | uncertainty quantification, quantification de l’incertitude, 不确定性量化 | The study of how input uncertainty affects output uncertainty in computational models.          | EN: Uncertainty quantification estimates confidence in simulations.<br>FR: La quantification de l’incertitude estime la confiance dans les simulations.<br>中：不确定性量化评估仿真的可信范围。                                         |
| —              | sensitivity analysis, analyse de sensibilité, 敏感性分析                 | Analysis of how outputs change when inputs or parameters change.                                | EN: Sensitivity analysis identifies influential parameters.<br>FR: L’analyse de sensibilité identifie les paramètres influents.<br>中：敏感性分析识别影响较大的参数。                                                                  |
| —              | surrogate model, modèle de substitution, 替代模型                       | A cheaper approximation to an expensive computational model.                                    | EN: Surrogate models speed up optimization and uncertainty studies.<br>FR: Les modèles de substitution accélèrent optimisation et études d’incertitude.<br>中：替代模型加速优化和不确定性研究。                                         |
| —              | reduced-order model, modèle d’ordre réduit, 降阶模型                    | A simplified model retaining dominant behavior with fewer degrees of freedom.                   | EN: Reduced-order models approximate large simulations efficiently.<br>FR: Les modèles d’ordre réduit approximent efficacement les grandes simulations.<br>中：降阶模型高效近似大型仿真。                                            |
| `DoF`          | degrees of freedom, degrés de liberté, 自由度                          | The number of independent unknowns in a discretized model.                                      | EN: More degrees of freedom usually increase resolution and cost.<br>FR: Plus de degrés de liberté augmentent généralement résolution et coût.<br>中：更多自由度通常提高分辨率，也增加成本。                                               |
| —              | reproducibility, reproductibilité, 可复现性                             | The ability to obtain the same computational results under documented conditions.               | EN: Reproducible simulations require code, data, parameters, and environment records.<br>FR: Les simulations reproductibles exigent code, données, paramètres et environnement documentés.<br>中：可复现仿真需要记录代码、数据、参数和环境。 |

## 34.12 High-performance computing, parallelism, and computational cost

| Symbol (LaTeX) | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                   | Definition                                                                                     | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                |
| -------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `O(n)`         | linear complexity, complexité linéaire, 线性复杂度                       | Computational cost growing proportionally to input size.                                       | EN: A linear-time algorithm scales well with large data.<br>FR: Un algorithme en temps linéaire passe bien à grande échelle.<br>中：线性时间算法对大规模数据扩展较好。                                     |
| `O(n^2)`       | quadratic complexity, complexité quadratique, 二次复杂度                 | Computational cost growing proportionally to the square of input size.                         | EN: Pairwise distance computation is often quadratic.<br>FR: Le calcul des distances par paires est souvent quadratique.<br>中：成对距离计算通常是二次复杂度。                                           |
| `O(n^3)`       | cubic complexity, complexité cubique, 三次复杂度                         | Computational cost growing proportionally to the cube of input size.                           | EN: Dense matrix factorization is often cubic in dimension.<br>FR: La factorisation de matrices denses est souvent cubique en dimension.<br>中：稠密矩阵分解通常对维数呈三次复杂度。                        |
| —              | memory complexity, complexité mémoire, 存储复杂度                        | The amount of memory required by an algorithm as problem size grows.                           | EN: Sparse methods reduce memory complexity for large PDE systems.<br>FR: Les méthodes creuses réduisent la complexité mémoire des grands systèmes d’EDP.<br>中：稀疏方法降低大型 PDE 系统的存储复杂度。   |
| —              | parallel computing, calcul parallèle, 并行计算                          | Computing by dividing work across multiple processors or cores.                                | EN: Large simulations rely on parallel computing.<br>FR: Les grandes simulations reposent sur le calcul parallèle.<br>中：大型仿真依赖并行计算。                                                     |
| —              | distributed computing, calcul distribué, 分布式计算                      | Computing across multiple networked machines with distributed memory.                          | EN: Distributed solvers split large matrices across nodes.<br>FR: Les solveurs distribués répartissent les grandes matrices entre nœuds.<br>中：分布式求解器把大型矩阵分配到多个节点上。                      |
| `MPI`          | message passing interface, interface de passage de messages, 消息传递接口 | A standard for communication between processes in distributed-memory computing.                | EN: MPI is widely used in high-performance scientific computing.<br>FR: MPI est largement utilisé dans le calcul scientifique haute performance.<br>中：MPI 广泛用于高性能科学计算。                  |
| `GPU`          | graphics processing unit, processeur graphique, 图形处理器               | A massively parallel processor used for numerical acceleration.                                | EN: GPUs accelerate dense linear algebra and deep learning workloads.<br>FR: Les GPU accélèrent l’algèbre linéaire dense et l’apprentissage profond.<br>中：GPU 加速稠密线性代数和深度学习工作负载。        |
| —              | vectorization, vectorisation, 向量化                                   | Rewriting computation to operate on arrays or vectors efficiently.                             | EN: Vectorized code is often faster than scalar loops.<br>FR: Le code vectorisé est souvent plus rapide que les boucles scalaires.<br>中：向量化代码通常比标量循环更快。                                 |
| —              | cache efficiency, efficacité du cache, 缓存效率                         | How well an algorithm uses fast memory close to the processor.                                 | EN: Matrix blocking improves cache efficiency.<br>FR: Le blocage matriciel améliore l’efficacité du cache.<br>中：矩阵分块可提高缓存效率。                                                            |
| —              | scalability, extensibilité, 可扩展性                                    | The ability of a computation to remain efficient as problem size or processor count increases. | EN: A scalable solver benefits from more processors.<br>FR: Un solveur extensible bénéficie de plus de processeurs.<br>中：可扩展求解器能从更多处理器中获益。                                              |
| —              | load balancing, équilibrage de charge, 负载均衡                         | Distributing work evenly among processors.                                                     | EN: Poor load balancing leaves some processors idle.<br>FR: Un mauvais équilibrage de charge laisse certains processeurs inactifs.<br>中：负载均衡差会导致部分处理器空闲。                                |
| —              | strong scaling, scalabilité forte, 强扩展                              | Speedup from using more processors for a fixed problem size.                                   | EN: Strong scaling measures how fast one fixed simulation becomes.<br>FR: La scalabilité forte mesure l’accélération d’une simulation fixe.<br>中：强扩展度量固定问题在更多处理器下变快多少。                  |
| —              | weak scaling, scalabilité faible, 弱扩展                               | Performance behavior when problem size grows proportionally with processor count.              | EN: Weak scaling measures whether per-processor workload remains efficient.<br>FR: La scalabilité faible mesure si la charge par processeur reste efficace.<br>中：弱扩展度量每个处理器负载保持时效率是否稳定。 |
# 35. Dynamical Systems, Ergodic Theory, and Chaos

## 35.1 Dynamical systems, phase space, and trajectories

| Symbol (LaTeX)   | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                        | Definition                                                                      | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| ---------------- | -------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `X`              | phase space, espace des phases, 相空间                      | The space of all possible states of a dynamical system.                         | EN: For a pendulum, phase space records angle and angular velocity.<br>FR: Pour un pendule, l’espace des phases enregistre l’angle et la vitesse angulaire.<br>中：对摆而言，相空间记录角度和角速度。                        |
| `x(t)`           | trajectory, trajectoire, 轨道 / 轨迹                         | The path followed by a state as time evolves.                                   | EN: A solution curve of an ODE is a trajectory.<br>FR: Une courbe solution d’une EDO est une trajectoire.<br>中：常微分方程的解曲线是一条轨道。                                                                            |
| `\Phi_t:X\to X`  | flow, flot, 流                                            | A family of maps describing continuous-time evolution.                          | EN: `\Phi_t(x_0)` gives the state after time `t`.<br>FR: `\Phi_t(x_0)` donne l’état après le temps `t`.<br>中：`\Phi_t(x_0)` 给出时间 `t` 后的状态。                                                                 |
| `T:X\to X`       | map dynamical system, système dynamique discret, 映射动力系统  | A discrete-time system generated by repeated application of a map.              | EN: Iterating `T` gives `x_n=T^n(x_0)`.<br>FR: Itérer `T` donne `x_n=T^n(x_0)`.<br>中：迭代 `T` 得到 `x_n=T^n(x_0)`。                                                                                            |
| `T^n`            | nth iterate, n-ième itéré, 第 n 次迭代                       | The map obtained by composing `T` with itself `n` times.                        | EN: Long-term behavior is studied through `T^n(x)` as `n\to\infty`.<br>FR: Le comportement à long terme s’étudie par `T^n(x)` quand `n\to\infty`.<br>中：长期行为通过 `n\to\infty` 时的 `T^n(x)` 研究。                |
| `\dot{x}=f(x)`   | autonomous system, système autonome, 自治系统                | A continuous-time system whose vector field does not explicitly depend on time. | EN: Autonomous systems have time-translation invariance.<br>FR: Les systèmes autonomes ont une invariance par translation temporelle.<br>中：自治系统具有时间平移不变性。                                                 |
| `\dot{x}=f(t,x)` | nonautonomous system, système non autonome, 非自治系统        | A system whose dynamics explicitly depend on time.                              | EN: A seasonally forced population model is nonautonomous.<br>FR: Un modèle de population forcé par les saisons est non autonome.<br>中：受季节强迫的种群模型是非自治系统。                                                  |
| `x_0`            | initial condition, condition initiale, 初始条件              | The starting state from which a trajectory evolves.                             | EN: Different initial conditions may lead to different long-term outcomes.<br>FR: Des conditions initiales différentes peuvent mener à des comportements asymptotiques différents.<br>中：不同初始条件可能导致不同长期结果。 |
| `\omega(x)`      | omega-limit set, ensemble omega-limite, ω 极限集            | The set of accumulation points of a forward trajectory.                         | EN: An omega-limit set describes where a trajectory accumulates in the future.<br>FR: Un ensemble omega-limite décrit où une trajectoire s’accumule dans le futur.<br>中：ω 极限集描述轨道未来会积聚到哪里。                |
| `\alpha(x)`      | alpha-limit set, ensemble alpha-limite, α 极限集            | The set of accumulation points of a backward trajectory.                        | EN: Alpha-limit sets describe possible past asymptotic behavior.<br>FR: Les ensembles alpha-limites décrivent le comportement asymptotique passé possible.<br>中：α 极限集描述可能的过去渐近行为。                         |
| —                | invariant set, ensemble invariant, 不变集                   | A subset mapped into itself, or onto itself, under the dynamics.                | EN: A periodic orbit is an invariant set.<br>FR: Une orbite périodique est un ensemble invariant.<br>中：周期轨道是不变集。                                                                                          |
| —                | forward invariant set, ensemble invariant positif, 正向不变集 | A set whose forward evolution remains inside itself.                            | EN: A trapping region is forward invariant.<br>FR: Une région piégeante est invariante positivement.<br>中：吸引区域通常是正向不变的。                                                                                   |

## 35.2 Equilibria, periodic orbits, and stability

| Symbol (LaTeX) | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                             | Definition                                                                       | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| -------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
