| `x^\ast`       | equilibrium point, point d’équilibre, 平衡点                                     | A state that does not change under the dynamics.                                 | EN: For `\dot{x}=f(x)`, an equilibrium satisfies `f(x^\ast)=0`.<br>FR: Pour `\dot{x}=f(x)`, un équilibre satisfait `f(x^\ast)=0`.<br>中：对 `\dot{x}=f(x)`，平衡点满足 `f(x^\ast)=0`。                                                                    |
| `f(x^\ast)=0`  | equilibrium condition, condition d’équilibre, 平衡条件                            | The condition that the vector field vanishes at an equilibrium.                  | EN: Solving `f(x)=0` finds equilibria.<br>FR: Résoudre `f(x)=0` trouve les équilibres.<br>中：求解 `f(x)=0` 可找到平衡点。                                                                                                                                 |
| —              | fixed point, point fixe, 不动点                                                  | A point unchanged by a map.                                                      | EN: For a discrete system, `x^\ast` is fixed if `T(x^\ast)=x^\ast`.<br>FR: Pour un système discret, `x^\ast` est fixe si `T(x^\ast)=x^\ast`.<br>中：对离散系统，若 `T(x^\ast)=x^\ast`，则 `x^\ast` 是不动点。                                                   |
| —              | periodic point, point périodique, 周期点                                         | A point returning to itself after finitely many iterations.                      | EN: If `T^p(x)=x`, then `x` is periodic with period dividing `p`.<br>FR: Si `T^p(x)=x`, alors `x` est périodique de période divisant `p`.<br>中：若 `T^p(x)=x`，则 `x` 是周期点，周期整除 `p`。                                                                |
| —              | periodic orbit, orbite périodique, 周期轨道                                       | A trajectory that repeats after a fixed time or number of iterations.            | EN: Planetary motion is often approximated by periodic orbits.<br>FR: Le mouvement planétaire est souvent approché par des orbites périodiques.<br>中：行星运动常被近似为周期轨道。                                                                             |
| `\tau`         | period, période, 周期                                                           | The time or number of iterations after which a trajectory repeats.               | EN: A solution satisfies `x(t+\tau)=x(t)` with period `\tau`.<br>FR: Une solution satisfait `x(t+\tau)=x(t)` avec période `\tau`.<br>中：周期为 `\tau` 的解满足 `x(t+\tau)=x(t)`。                                                                        |
| —              | stable equilibrium, équilibre stable, 稳定平衡点                                   | An equilibrium where nearby trajectories remain nearby.                          | EN: A ball at the bottom of a bowl has stable equilibrium.<br>FR: Une bille au fond d’un bol a un équilibre stable.<br>中：碗底小球处于稳定平衡。                                                                                                            |
| —              | asymptotically stable equilibrium, équilibre asymptotiquement stable, 渐近稳定平衡点 | A stable equilibrium that attracts nearby trajectories as time goes to infinity. | EN: Damped mechanical systems often approach asymptotically stable equilibria.<br>FR: Les systèmes mécaniques amortis approchent souvent des équilibres asymptotiquement stables.<br>中：阻尼机械系统常趋向渐近稳定平衡点。                                        |
| —              | unstable equilibrium, équilibre instable, 不稳定平衡点                              | An equilibrium where arbitrarily close initial states can move away.             | EN: A ball balanced on top of a hill is unstable.<br>FR: Une bille en équilibre au sommet d’une colline est instable.<br>中：山顶上的小球处于不稳定平衡。                                                                                                       |
| `J_f(x^\ast)`  | Jacobian at equilibrium, jacobienne à l’équilibre, 平衡点处 Jacobian 矩阵           | The derivative matrix used to linearize dynamics near an equilibrium.            | EN: Eigenvalues of `J_f(x^\ast)` determine local stability in many cases.<br>FR: Les valeurs propres de `J_f(x^\ast)` déterminent souvent la stabilité locale.<br>中：`J_f(x^\ast)` 的特征值常决定局部稳定性。                                                 |
| —              | linearization, linéarisation, 线性化                                             | Approximation of nonlinear dynamics by a linear system near a point.             | EN: Linearization replaces `\dot{x}=f(x)` by `\dot{y}=J_f(x^\ast)y` near equilibrium.<br>FR: La linéarisation remplace `\dot{x}=f(x)` par `\dot{y}=J_f(x^\ast)y` près de l’équilibre.<br>中：线性化在平衡点附近用 `\dot{y}=J_f(x^\ast)y` 代替 `\dot{x}=f(x)`。 |
| —              | hyperbolic equilibrium, équilibre hyperbolique, 双曲平衡点                         | An equilibrium whose linearization has no eigenvalues with zero real part.       | EN: Hyperbolic equilibria are structurally stable under small perturbations in many settings.<br>FR: Les équilibres hyperboliques sont souvent structurellement stables sous petites perturbations.<br>中：双曲平衡点在许多情形下对小扰动结构稳定。                   |

## 35.3 Linear systems, eigenvalues, and phase portraits

| Symbol (LaTeX) | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                           | Definition                                                                               | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| -------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `\dot{x}=Ax`   | linear dynamical system, système dynamique linéaire, 线性动力系统 | A continuous-time system governed by a matrix `A`.                                       | EN: Linear systems are solved using the matrix exponential.<br>FR: Les systèmes linéaires se résolvent avec l’exponentielle de matrice.<br>中：线性系统可用矩阵指数求解。                                              |
| `x_{n+1}=Ax_n` | discrete linear system, système linéaire discret, 离散线性系统    | A discrete-time system defined by repeated multiplication by a matrix.                   | EN: Powers of `A` determine the long-term behavior.<br>FR: Les puissances de `A` déterminent le comportement à long terme.<br>中：`A` 的幂决定长期行为。                                                           |
| `e^{tA}`       | matrix exponential, exponentielle de matrice, 矩阵指数          | The operator giving the flow of a linear continuous-time system.                         | EN: The solution of `\dot{x}=Ax` is `x(t)=e^{tA}x_0`.<br>FR: La solution de `\dot{x}=Ax` est `x(t)=e^{tA}x_0`.<br>中：`\dot{x}=Ax` 的解为 `x(t)=e^{tA}x_0`。                                                  |
| `\lambda`      | eigenvalue, valeur propre, 特征值                              | A scalar describing growth, decay, or rotation directions in linear dynamics.            | EN: Negative real eigenvalues produce decay in continuous time.<br>FR: Les valeurs propres réelles négatives produisent une décroissance en temps continu.<br>中：连续时间中，负实特征值导致衰减。                        |
| `v`            | eigenvector, vecteur propre, 特征向量                           | A direction preserved by the linear dynamics up to scaling.                              | EN: Eigenvectors give preferred directions in phase space.<br>FR: Les vecteurs propres donnent des directions privilégiées dans l’espace des phases.<br>中：特征向量给出相空间中的特殊方向。                              |
| —              | phase portrait, portrait de phase, 相图                       | A qualitative diagram of trajectories in phase space.                                    | EN: Phase portraits visualize equilibria and orbit directions.<br>FR: Les portraits de phase visualisent équilibres et directions d’orbites.<br>中：相图展示平衡点和轨道方向。                                         |
| —              | node, nœud, 结点                                              | An equilibrium type where trajectories approach or leave along non-spiraling directions. | EN: Stable nodes attract nearby trajectories without oscillation.<br>FR: Les nœuds stables attirent les trajectoires proches sans oscillation.<br>中：稳定结点吸引附近轨道且不振荡。                                     |
| —              | saddle point, point selle, 鞍点                               | An equilibrium with both stable and unstable directions.                                 | EN: Saddle points attract along some directions and repel along others.<br>FR: Les points selles attirent dans certaines directions et repoussent dans d’autres.<br>中：鞍点在某些方向吸引，在另一些方向排斥。               |
| —              | spiral point, foyer, 螺旋点 / 焦点                               | An equilibrium where trajectories rotate while approaching or leaving.                   | EN: Complex eigenvalues with negative real part produce stable spirals.<br>FR: Des valeurs propres complexes à partie réelle négative produisent des foyers stables.<br>中：实部为负的复特征值产生稳定螺旋。              |
| —              | center, centre, 中心                                          | An equilibrium surrounded by closed orbits in linear planar systems.                     | EN: Purely imaginary eigenvalues can produce a center in linear systems.<br>FR: Des valeurs propres purement imaginaires peuvent produire un centre dans les systèmes linéaires.<br>中：纯虚特征值在线性系统中可产生中心。 |
| `E^s`          | stable subspace, sous-espace stable, 稳定子空间                  | The subspace spanned by directions decaying forward in time.                             | EN: Stable subspaces contain initial conditions approaching equilibrium.<br>FR: Les sous-espaces stables contiennent les conditions initiales qui approchent l’équilibre.<br>中：稳定子空间包含趋向平衡点的初始条件。       |
| `E^u`          | unstable subspace, sous-espace instable, 不稳定子空间             | The subspace spanned by directions growing forward in time.                              | EN: Unstable directions move away from equilibrium.<br>FR: Les directions instables s’éloignent de l’équilibre.<br>中：不稳定方向远离平衡点。                                                                        |

## 35.4 Nonlinear dynamics, invariant manifolds, and normal forms

| Symbol (LaTeX) | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                        | Definition                                                                                                                  | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| -------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------ | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `W^s(x^\ast)`  | stable manifold, variété stable, 稳定流形                                    | The set of initial points whose trajectories converge to an invariant set or equilibrium.                                   | EN: The stable manifold of a saddle contains points attracted to it.<br>FR: La variété stable d’une selle contient les points attirés par elle.<br>中：鞍点的稳定流形包含被其吸引的点。                           |
| `W^u(x^\ast)`  | unstable manifold, variété instable, 不稳定流形                               | The set of points whose backward-time trajectories converge to an invariant set or equilibrium.                             | EN: Unstable manifolds organize escape directions.<br>FR: Les variétés instables organisent les directions d’échappement.<br>中：不稳定流形组织逃离方向。                                                     |
| —              | center manifold, variété centrale, 中心流形                                  | An invariant manifold tangent to the center subspace, capturing neutral dynamics.                                           | EN: Center manifolds reduce analysis near nonhyperbolic equilibria.<br>FR: Les variétés centrales réduisent l’analyse près des équilibres non hyperboliques.<br>中：中心流形简化非双曲平衡点附近的分析。            |
| —              | stable manifold theorem, théorème de la variété stable, 稳定流形定理           | A theorem guaranteeing stable and unstable manifolds near hyperbolic invariant sets.                                        | EN: The theorem explains local geometry near hyperbolic equilibria.<br>FR: Le théorème explique la géométrie locale près des équilibres hyperboliques.<br>中：稳定流形定理解释双曲平衡点附近的局部几何。               |
| —              | Hartman-Grobman theorem, théorème de Hartman-Grobman, Hartman-Grobman 定理 | A theorem saying that near a hyperbolic equilibrium, nonlinear dynamics are topologically conjugate to their linearization. | EN: Hyperbolic nonlinear systems locally resemble their linear parts.<br>FR: Les systèmes non linéaires hyperboliques ressemblent localement à leurs parties linéaires.<br>中：双曲非线性系统在局部类似其线性部分。 |
| —              | topological conjugacy, conjugaison topologique, 拓扑共轭                     | An equivalence between dynamical systems by a homeomorphism preserving time evolution.                                      | EN: Conjugate systems have the same qualitative dynamics.<br>FR: Les systèmes conjugués ont la même dynamique qualitative.<br>中：共轭系统具有相同的定性动力学。                                                 |
| —              | smooth conjugacy, conjugaison lisse, 光滑共轭                                | A conjugacy given by a smooth change of coordinates.                                                                        | EN: Smooth conjugacy preserves differentiable dynamical structure.<br>FR: La conjugaison lisse préserve la structure dynamique différentiable.<br>中：光滑共轭保持可微动力结构。                               |
| —              | normal form, forme normale, 标准形                                          | A simplified coordinate representation of a dynamical system near a special point.                                          | EN: Normal forms isolate the essential nonlinear terms.<br>FR: Les formes normales isolent les termes non linéaires essentiels.<br>中：标准形分离出关键非线性项。                                              |
| —              | resonance, résonance, 共振                                                 | A relation among eigenvalues preventing removal of certain nonlinear terms by coordinate change.                            | EN: Resonant terms remain in normal forms.<br>FR: Les termes résonants restent dans les formes normales.<br>中：共振项会保留在标准形中。                                                                      |
| —              | invariant foliation, feuilletage invariant, 不变叶状结构                       | A decomposition of phase space into invariant leaves under the dynamics.                                                    | EN: Hyperbolic systems often possess stable and unstable foliations.<br>FR: Les systèmes hyperboliques possèdent souvent des feuilletages stables et instables.<br>中：双曲系统常具有稳定和不稳定叶状结构。         |
| —              | basin of attraction, bassin d’attraction, 吸引盆                            | The set of initial conditions attracted to a given attractor.                                                               | EN: Different basins can lead to different long-term states.<br>FR: Différents bassins peuvent mener à différents états asymptotiques.<br>中：不同吸引盆可导向不同长期状态。                                     |
| —              | trapping region, région piégeante, 捕获区域                                  | A region whose forward image remains inside itself.                                                                         | EN: Trapping regions help prove attractor existence.<br>FR: Les régions piégeantes aident à prouver l’existence d’attracteurs.<br>中：捕获区域有助于证明吸引子存在。                                             |

## 35.5 Bifurcations and qualitative changes

| Symbol (LaTeX) | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                         | Definition                                                                                     | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                     |
| -------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `\mu`          | bifurcation parameter, paramètre de bifurcation, 分岔参数                     | A parameter whose variation changes the qualitative behavior of a system.                      | EN: Changing `\mu` can create or destroy equilibria.<br>FR: Changer `\mu` peut créer ou détruire des équilibres.<br>中：改变 `\mu` 可产生或消灭平衡点。                                                                    |
| —              | bifurcation, bifurcation, 分岔                                              | A qualitative change in dynamics as a parameter varies.                                        | EN: A stable equilibrium can lose stability at a bifurcation.<br>FR: Un équilibre stable peut perdre sa stabilité lors d’une bifurcation.<br>中：稳定平衡点可能在分岔处失去稳定性。                                             |
| —              | bifurcation diagram, diagramme de bifurcation, 分岔图                        | A diagram showing long-term states as a function of a parameter.                               | EN: Bifurcation diagrams reveal transitions to periodicity and chaos.<br>FR: Les diagrammes de bifurcation révèlent les transitions vers périodicité et chaos.<br>中：分岔图揭示通向周期性和混沌的转变。                        |
| —              | saddle-node bifurcation, bifurcation nœud-col, 鞍结分岔                       | A bifurcation where two equilibria collide and annihilate or are created.                      | EN: Saddle-node bifurcation creates a stable and an unstable equilibrium together.<br>FR: La bifurcation nœud-col crée ensemble un équilibre stable et un équilibre instable.<br>中：鞍结分岔同时产生一个稳定平衡点和一个不稳定平衡点。 |
| —              | transcritical bifurcation, bifurcation transcritique, 跨临界分岔               | A bifurcation where two equilibria exchange stability.                                         | EN: Population models with thresholds can show transcritical bifurcations.<br>FR: Les modèles de population avec seuils peuvent montrer des bifurcations transcritiques.<br>中：带阈值的种群模型可能出现跨临界分岔。             |
| —              | pitchfork bifurcation, bifurcation fourche, 叉形分岔                          | A bifurcation where one equilibrium splits into several branches under symmetry.               | EN: Symmetric systems often have pitchfork bifurcations.<br>FR: Les systèmes symétriques ont souvent des bifurcations en fourche.<br>中：对称系统常出现叉形分岔。                                                          |
| —              | Hopf bifurcation, bifurcation de Hopf, Hopf 分岔                            | A bifurcation where an equilibrium loses stability and a periodic orbit emerges or disappears. | EN: Hopf bifurcation explains onset of oscillations.<br>FR: La bifurcation de Hopf explique l’apparition d’oscillations.<br>中：Hopf 分岔解释振荡的出现。                                                                |
| —              | period-doubling bifurcation, bifurcation par doublement de période, 倍周期分岔 | A bifurcation where a periodic orbit gives rise to one with twice the period.                  | EN: Repeated period-doubling can lead to chaos.<br>FR: Les doublements de période répétés peuvent mener au chaos.<br>中：重复倍周期分岔可通向混沌。                                                                         |
| —              | codimension-one bifurcation, bifurcation de codimension un, 余维一分岔         | A bifurcation typically requiring one parameter to unfold.                                     | EN: Saddle-node and Hopf bifurcations are standard codimension-one bifurcations.<br>FR: Les bifurcations nœud-col et de Hopf sont des bifurcations standards de codimension un.<br>中：鞍结分岔和 Hopf 分岔是标准余维一分岔。  |
| —              | unfolding, déploiement, 展开                                                | A parameterized family describing how a degenerate system changes under perturbation.          | EN: Normal forms often provide unfoldings of bifurcations.<br>FR: Les formes normales fournissent souvent des déploiements de bifurcations.<br>中：标准形常给出分岔的展开。                                                |
| —              | hysteresis, hystérésis, 滞后                                                | A phenomenon where system state depends on parameter history.                                  | EN: Systems with multiple stable equilibria can show hysteresis.<br>FR: Les systèmes avec plusieurs équilibres stables peuvent montrer de l’hystérésis.<br>中：具有多个稳定平衡点的系统可能出现滞后。                             |
| —              | catastrophe, catastrophe, 突变                                              | A sudden qualitative change in equilibrium behavior under smooth parameter variation.          | EN: Catastrophe theory studies abrupt changes in parameterized systems.<br>FR: La théorie des catastrophes étudie les changements brusques dans les systèmes paramétrés.<br>中：突变理论研究参数化系统中的骤然变化。             |

## 35.6 Chaos, sensitivity, and symbolic dynamics

| Symbol (LaTeX) | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                                           | Definition                                                                                                   | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                       |
| -------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| —              | chaos, chaos, 混沌                                                                            | Deterministic dynamics with irregular long-term behavior, often involving sensitivity to initial conditions. | EN: Chaotic systems can be deterministic but unpredictable in detail.<br>FR: Les systèmes chaotiques peuvent être déterministes mais imprévisibles en détail.<br>中：混沌系统可以是确定性的，但细节上难以预测。       |
| —              | sensitive dependence on initial conditions, sensibilité aux conditions initiales, 对初始条件敏感依赖 | A property where nearby initial states eventually separate by a noticeable amount.                           | EN: Small measurement errors can grow rapidly in chaotic systems.<br>FR: De petites erreurs de mesure peuvent croître rapidement dans les systèmes chaotiques.<br>中：在混沌系统中，小测量误差可迅速放大。         |
| `\lambda_L`    | Lyapunov exponent, exposant de Lyapunov, Lyapunov 指数                                        | A number measuring exponential rates of separation or contraction of nearby trajectories.                    | EN: A positive Lyapunov exponent indicates sensitive dependence.<br>FR: Un exposant de Lyapunov positif indique une sensibilité aux conditions initiales.<br>中：正 Lyapunov 指数表示对初始条件敏感。         |
| —              | strange attractor, attracteur étrange, 奇异吸引子                                                | An attractor with complicated geometry and chaotic dynamics.                                                 | EN: The Lorenz attractor is a classical strange attractor.<br>FR: L’attracteur de Lorenz est un attracteur étrange classique.<br>中：Lorenz 吸引子是经典奇异吸引子。                                         |
| —              | topological transitivity, transitivité topologique, 拓扑传递性                                   | A property meaning that orbits can move between arbitrary open regions in the phase space.                   | EN: Topological transitivity expresses dynamical indecomposability.<br>FR: La transitivité topologique exprime l’indécomposabilité dynamique.<br>中：拓扑传递性表达动力系统的不可分解性。                          |
| —              | dense orbit, orbite dense, 稠密轨道                                                             | An orbit whose closure is the whole space or a specified invariant set.                                      | EN: Irrational rotations on the circle have dense orbits.<br>FR: Les rotations irrationnelles du cercle ont des orbites denses.<br>中：圆上的无理旋转具有稠密轨道。                                            |
| —              | symbolic dynamics, dynamique symbolique, 符号动力学                                              | The study of dynamical systems through sequences of symbols.                                                 | EN: Symbolic dynamics encodes complicated orbits as strings.<br>FR: La dynamique symbolique encode les orbites compliquées comme des suites de symboles.<br>中：符号动力学把复杂轨道编码为符号序列。               |
| `\Sigma_A`     | subshift of finite type, sous-décalage de type fini, 有限型子移位                                 | A symbolic system defined by allowed transitions encoded in a matrix `A`.                                    | EN: Subshifts of finite type model constrained symbolic evolution.<br>FR: Les sous-décalages de type fini modélisent une évolution symbolique contrainte.<br>中：有限型子移位建模受约束的符号演化。               |
| `\sigma`       | shift map, décalage, 移位映射                                                                   | The map moving every symbol in a sequence one position.                                                      | EN: The shift map is a basic model of symbolic time evolution.<br>FR: Le décalage est un modèle fondamental d’évolution symbolique.<br>中：移位映射是符号时间演化的基本模型。                                     |
| —              | topological entropy, entropie topologique, 拓扑熵                                              | A measure of orbit complexity and exponential growth of distinguishable trajectories.                        | EN: Positive topological entropy indicates complex dynamics.<br>FR: Une entropie topologique positive indique une dynamique complexe.<br>中：正拓扑熵表示复杂动力行为。                                       |
| —              | horseshoe map, application fer à cheval, 马蹄映射                                               | A map exhibiting stretching, folding, and chaotic invariant sets.                                            | EN: Smale horseshoes provide a geometric mechanism for chaos.<br>FR: Les fers à cheval de Smale fournissent un mécanisme géométrique du chaos.<br>中：Smale 马蹄给出混沌的几何机制。                         |
| —              | shadowing, pistage, 影随性质                                                                    | A property where approximate orbits are closely followed by true orbits.                                     | EN: Shadowing helps justify numerical simulations of hyperbolic systems.<br>FR: Le pistage aide à justifier les simulations numériques des systèmes hyperboliques.<br>中：影随性质有助于说明双曲系统数值仿真的合理性。 |

## 35.7 Attractors, recurrence, and long-time behavior

| Symbol (LaTeX) | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                              | Definition                                                                                                    | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| -------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `A`            | attractor, attracteur, 吸引子                                                     | An invariant set attracting nearby orbits.                                                                    | EN: A stable equilibrium is a simple attractor.<br>FR: Un équilibre stable est un attracteur simple.<br>中：稳定平衡点是简单吸引子。                                                                          |
| —              | global attractor, attracteur global, 全局吸引子                                     | An attractor that attracts all bounded sets or all relevant initial states.                                   | EN: Dissipative PDEs may possess global attractors.<br>FR: Les EDP dissipatives peuvent posséder des attracteurs globaux.<br>中：耗散型 PDE 可能具有全局吸引子。                                               |
| —              | limit cycle, cycle limite, 极限环                                                 | An isolated periodic orbit attracting or repelling nearby trajectories.                                       | EN: The Van der Pol oscillator has a stable limit cycle.<br>FR: L’oscillateur de Van der Pol a un cycle limite stable.<br>中：Van der Pol 振子具有稳定极限环。                                              |
| —              | recurrence, récurrence, 回归性                                                    | The tendency of trajectories to return arbitrarily close to previous states.                                  | EN: Recurrence is central in conservative dynamics.<br>FR: La récurrence est centrale dans la dynamique conservative.<br>中：回归性在保守动力系统中很核心。                                                      |
| —              | Poincaré recurrence theorem, théorème de récurrence de Poincaré, Poincaré 回归定理 | A theorem stating that measure-preserving systems of finite measure return near initial states almost surely. | EN: Finite measure-preserving systems cannot simply dissipate everywhere.<br>FR: Les systèmes préservant une mesure finie ne peuvent pas simplement se dissiper partout.<br>中：有限测度保持系统不能处处简单耗散。 |
| —              | recurrent point, point récurrent, 回归点                                          | A point whose trajectory returns arbitrarily close to itself.                                                 | EN: Many points in finite measure-preserving systems are recurrent.<br>FR: Beaucoup de points dans les systèmes préservant une mesure finie sont récurrents.<br>中：有限测度保持系统中的许多点是回归点。            |
| —              | wandering set, ensemble errant, 游荡集                                            | A set whose iterates eventually remain disjoint from itself.                                                  | EN: Conservative systems have limited wandering behavior.<br>FR: Les systèmes conservatifs ont un comportement errant limité.<br>中：保守系统中的游荡行为受到限制。                                              |
| —              | nonwandering point, point non errant, 非游荡点                                     | A point whose every neighborhood returns to itself under some future iterate.                                 | EN: Nonwandering sets contain recurrent and periodic behavior.<br>FR: Les ensembles non errants contiennent comportements récurrents et périodiques.<br>中：非游荡集包含回归和周期行为。                        |
| `\Omega(T)`    | nonwandering set, ensemble non errant, 非游荡集                                    | The set of all nonwandering points of a dynamical system.                                                     | EN: The nonwandering set captures essential recurrent dynamics.<br>FR: L’ensemble non errant capture la dynamique récurrente essentielle.<br>中：非游荡集捕捉核心回归动力学。                                   |
| —              | absorbing set, ensemble absorbant, 吸收集                                         | A set that eventually contains the forward images of all relevant bounded sets.                               | EN: Absorbing sets help prove existence of global attractors.<br>FR: Les ensembles absorbants aident à prouver l’existence d’attracteurs globaux.<br>中：吸收集有助于证明全局吸引子存在。                         |
| —              | dissipative system, système dissipatif, 耗散系统                                   | A system where phase volume or energy tends to decrease.                                                      | EN: Damped systems are typically dissipative.<br>FR: Les systèmes amortis sont typiquement dissipatifs.<br>中：阻尼系统通常是耗散系统。                                                                       |
| —              | conservative system, système conservatif, 保守系统                                 | A system preserving volume, energy, or another invariant quantity.                                            | EN: Hamiltonian systems are often conservative.<br>FR: Les systèmes hamiltoniens sont souvent conservatifs.<br>中：Hamilton 系统通常是保守系统。                                                            |

## 35.8 Measure-preserving systems and ergodic theory

| Symbol (LaTeX)                       | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                                             | Definition                                                                                 | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |
| ------------------------------------ | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `(X,\mathcal{B},\mu)`                | measure space, espace mesuré, 测度空间                                                            | A set with a sigma-algebra and a measure.                                                  | EN: Ergodic theory studies dynamics on measure spaces.<br>FR: La théorie ergodique étudie la dynamique sur des espaces mesurés.<br>中：遍历理论研究测度空间上的动力系统。                                                                           |
| `T:X\to X`                           | measure-preserving transformation, transformation préservant la mesure, 保测变换                  | A map satisfying `\mu(T^{-1}A)=\mu(A)` for measurable sets `A`.                            | EN: Rotations of the circle preserve Lebesgue measure.<br>FR: Les rotations du cercle préservent la mesure de Lebesgue.<br>中：圆周旋转保持 Lebesgue 测度。                                                                                 |
| `\mu(T^{-1}A)=\mu(A)`                | measure-preserving property, propriété de préservation de la mesure, 保测性质                     | The identity saying that the measure of every measurable set is unchanged by the dynamics. | EN: Probability-preserving maps model stationary evolution.<br>FR: Les applications préservant la probabilité modélisent une évolution stationnaire.<br>中：保概率映射建模平稳演化。                                                           |
| —                                    | invariant measure, mesure invariante, 不变测度                                                    | A measure preserved by a map or flow.                                                      | EN: Invariant measures describe statistical steady states.<br>FR: Les mesures invariantes décrivent des états statistiques stationnaires.<br>中：不变测度描述统计稳态。                                                                       |
| —                                    | ergodicity, ergodicité, 遍历性                                                                   | A property that invariant sets have either zero measure or full measure.                   | EN: Ergodicity means the system cannot be decomposed into two invariant statistical pieces.<br>FR: L’ergodicité signifie que le système ne se décompose pas en deux parties statistiques invariantes.<br>中：遍历性表示系统不能分解为两个不变统计部分。 |
| —                                    | ergodic system, système ergodique, 遍历系统                                                       | A measure-preserving system whose invariant measurable sets are trivial in measure.        | EN: Irrational circle rotations are ergodic for Lebesgue measure.<br>FR: Les rotations irrationnelles du cercle sont ergodiques pour la mesure de Lebesgue.<br>中：圆上的无理旋转对 Lebesgue 测度是遍历的。                                       |
| `f\circ T`                           | observable after transformation, observable après transformation, 变换后的观测量                     | The observable obtained after applying dynamics before measurement.                        | EN: Time series from a dynamical system are often `f(T^n x)`.<br>FR: Les séries temporelles d’un système dynamique sont souvent `f(T^n x)`.<br>中：动力系统产生的时间序列常写作 `f(T^n x)`。                                                      |
| `\frac{1}{n}\sum_{k=0}^{n-1}f(T^kx)` | time average, moyenne temporelle, 时间平均                                                        | The average of an observable along one trajectory.                                         | EN: Time averages are computed from a single long orbit.<br>FR: Les moyennes temporelles se calculent sur une longue orbite unique.<br>中：时间平均由一条长轨道计算。                                                                           |
| `\int_X f\,d\mu`                     | space average, moyenne spatiale, 空间平均                                                         | The average of an observable over the whole measure space.                                 | EN: Ergodic theorems relate time averages to space averages.<br>FR: Les théorèmes ergodiques relient moyennes temporelles et moyennes spatiales.<br>中：遍历定理联系时间平均与空间平均。                                                           |
| —                                    | Birkhoff ergodic theorem, théorème ergodique de Birkhoff, Birkhoff 遍历定理                       | A theorem giving almost sure convergence of time averages for integrable observables.      | EN: In an ergodic system, time averages equal space averages almost surely.<br>FR: Dans un système ergodique, les moyennes temporelles égalent presque sûrement les moyennes spatiales.<br>中：在遍历系统中，时间平均几乎处处等于空间平均。              |
| —                                    | von Neumann mean ergodic theorem, théorème ergodique moyen de von Neumann, von Neumann 平均遍历定理 | An ergodic theorem giving convergence of averages in `L^2`.                                | EN: Mean ergodic theorem is a Hilbert-space version of averaging dynamics.<br>FR: Le théorème ergodique moyen est une version hilbertienne de la moyenne dynamique.<br>中：平均遍历定理是动力平均的 Hilbert 空间版本。                              |
| —                                    | ergodic decomposition, décomposition ergodique, 遍历分解                                          | A representation of an invariant measure as a mixture of ergodic invariant measures.       | EN: Non-ergodic systems can be decomposed into ergodic components.<br>FR: Les systèmes non ergodiques se décomposent en composantes ergodiques.<br>中：非遍历系统可分解为遍历成分。                                                              |

## 35.9 Mixing, entropy, and statistical properties

| Symbol (LaTeX)                      | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                                                         | Definition                                                                                            | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |
| ----------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| —                                   | mixing, mélange, 混合性                                                                                      | A property expressing asymptotic statistical independence of distant events.                          | EN: Mixing is stronger than ergodicity in many settings.<br>FR: Le mélange est souvent plus fort que l’ergodicité.<br>中：在许多情形中，混合性强于遍历性。                                                                                    |
| `\mu(T^{-n}A\cap B)\to\mu(A)\mu(B)` | strong mixing, mélange fort, 强混合                                                                          | A condition saying that events become asymptotically independent under long time separation.          | EN: Strong mixing means distant observations nearly decorrelate.<br>FR: Le mélange fort signifie que les observations éloignées se décorrèlent presque.<br>中：强混合表示相隔很远的观测近似去相关。                                             |
| —                                   | weak mixing, mélange faible, 弱混合                                                                          | A mixing property weaker than strong mixing but stronger than ergodicity.                             | EN: Weak mixing rules out nontrivial eigenfunctions in many formulations.<br>FR: Le mélange faible exclut souvent les fonctions propres non triviales.<br>中：弱混合在许多表述中排除非平凡特征函数。                                             |
| —                                   | decay of correlations, décroissance des corrélations, 相关衰减                                                | The decrease of statistical dependence between observables as time separation grows.                  | EN: Fast decay of correlations supports central limit behavior.<br>FR: Une décroissance rapide des corrélations soutient un comportement de type théorème central limite.<br>中：快速相关衰减支持中心极限定理型行为。                           |
| `\operatorname{Corr}(f,g\circ T^n)` | correlation of observables, corrélation des observables, 观测量相关                                            | A measure of dependence between one observable and another after `n` iterations.                      | EN: Chaotic systems often show rapid correlation decay for smooth observables.<br>FR: Les systèmes chaotiques montrent souvent une décroissance rapide des corrélations pour observables lisses.<br>中：混沌系统对光滑观测量常表现出快速相关衰减。 |
| —                                   | Kolmogorov-Sinai entropy, entropie de Kolmogorov-Sinai, Kolmogorov-Sinai 熵                                | A measure-theoretic entropy quantifying average information production per unit time.                 | EN: Positive Kolmogorov-Sinai entropy indicates stochastic-like complexity.<br>FR: Une entropie de Kolmogorov-Sinai positive indique une complexité de type stochastique.<br>中：正 Kolmogorov-Sinai 熵表示类似随机的复杂性。              |
| `h_\mu(T)`                          | metric entropy, entropie métrique, 测度熵                                                                    | Entropy of a measure-preserving transformation relative to an invariant measure.                      | EN: Metric entropy depends on both dynamics and invariant measure.<br>FR: L’entropie métrique dépend à la fois de la dynamique et de la mesure invariante.<br>中：测度熵同时依赖动力系统和不变测度。                                           |
| —                                   | generating partition, partition génératrice, 生成划分                                                         | A partition whose iterates capture the measurable structure of the system.                            | EN: Generating partitions allow entropy to be computed from symbolic names.<br>FR: Les partitions génératrices permettent de calculer l’entropie à partir de noms symboliques.<br>中：生成划分允许通过符号名称计算熵。                        |
| —                                   | Bernoulli system, système de Bernoulli, Bernoulli 系统                                                      | A system isomorphic to an independent shift process.                                                  | EN: Bernoulli systems are strongly random in an ergodic-theoretic sense.<br>FR: Les systèmes de Bernoulli sont fortement aléatoires au sens ergodique.<br>中：Bernoulli 系统在遍历论意义上具有强随机性。                                      |
| —                                   | K-system, système K, K 系统                                                                                 | A system with a strong entropy and mixing structure related to Kolmogorov systems.                    | EN: K-systems sit between general mixing systems and Bernoulli systems.<br>FR: Les systèmes K se situent entre les systèmes mélangeants généraux et les systèmes de Bernoulli.<br>中：K 系统位于一般混合系统和 Bernoulli 系统之间。           |
| —                                   | central limit theorem for dynamical systems, théorème central limite pour systèmes dynamiques, 动力系统中心极限定理 | A theorem giving Gaussian fluctuations of Birkhoff sums under suitable chaotic or mixing assumptions. | EN: Some deterministic systems satisfy probabilistic limit laws.<br>FR: Certains systèmes déterministes satisfont des lois limites probabilistes.<br>中：某些确定性系统满足概率极限定律。                                                     |
| —                                   | large deviations, grandes déviations, 大偏差                                                                 | Theory estimating probabilities of rare deviations of time averages from typical values.              | EN: Large deviations quantify rare atypical dynamical behavior.<br>FR: Les grandes déviations quantifient les comportements dynamiques atypiques rares.<br>中：大偏差量化罕见的非典型动力行为。                                               |

## 35.10 Hamiltonian, symplectic, and conservative dynamics

| Symbol (LaTeX)                   | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                                     | Definition                                                                                                        | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
| -------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `(M,\omega)`                     | symplectic manifold, variété symplectique, 辛流形                                        | A manifold equipped with a closed nondegenerate two-form.                                                         | EN: Phase spaces in Hamiltonian mechanics are symplectic manifolds.<br>FR: Les espaces des phases en mécanique hamiltonienne sont des variétés symplectiques.<br>中：Hamilton 力学中的相空间是辛流形。                               |
| `\omega`                         | symplectic form, forme symplectique, 辛形式                                              | A closed nondegenerate two-form encoding geometric structure of phase space.                                      | EN: The symplectic form pairs position and momentum directions.<br>FR: La forme symplectique apparie directions de position et de moment.<br>中：辛形式配对位置与动量方向。                                                           |
| `H`                              | Hamiltonian, hamiltonien, Hamilton 函数                                                 | A function generating Hamiltonian time evolution, often representing energy.                                      | EN: The Hamiltonian determines the equations of motion.<br>FR: Le hamiltonien détermine les équations du mouvement.<br>中：Hamilton 函数决定运动方程。                                                                            |
| `X_H`                            | Hamiltonian vector field, champ hamiltonien, Hamilton 向量场                             | The vector field generated by a Hamiltonian through the symplectic form.                                          | EN: Trajectories of `X_H` preserve the Hamiltonian.<br>FR: Les trajectoires de `X_H` préservent le hamiltonien.<br>中：`X_H` 的轨道保持 Hamilton 函数。                                                                          |
| `\dot{q}=\partial H/\partial p`  | Hamilton equation for position, équation de Hamilton pour la position, 位置 Hamilton 方程 | The equation giving velocity of generalized coordinates.                                                          | EN: Position evolves according to derivative of energy with respect to momentum.<br>FR: La position évolue selon la dérivée de l’énergie par rapport au moment.<br>中：位置按能量对动量的导数演化。                                    |
| `\dot{p}=-\partial H/\partial q` | Hamilton equation for momentum, équation de Hamilton pour le moment, 动量 Hamilton 方程   | The equation giving evolution of generalized momentum.                                                            | EN: Forces appear as negative derivatives of the Hamiltonian with respect to position.<br>FR: Les forces apparaissent comme dérivées négatives du hamiltonien par rapport à la position.<br>中：力表现为 Hamilton 函数对位置的负导数。 |
| `\{f,g\}`                        | Poisson bracket, crochet de Poisson, Poisson 括号                                       | A bracket describing interaction of observables in Hamiltonian dynamics.                                          | EN: Time evolution satisfies `\dot{f}=\{f,H\}`.<br>FR: L’évolution temporelle satisfait `\dot{f}=\{f,H\}`.<br>中：时间演化满足 `\dot{f}=\{f,H\}`。                                                                              |
| —                                | symplectic map, application symplectique, 辛映射                                         | A map preserving the symplectic form.                                                                             | EN: Hamiltonian flows are symplectic maps.<br>FR: Les flots hamiltoniens sont des applications symplectiques.<br>中：Hamilton 流是辛映射。                                                                                     |
| —                                | Liouville theorem, théorème de Liouville, Liouville 定理                                | A theorem stating that Hamiltonian flows preserve phase-space volume.                                             | EN: Hamiltonian dynamics preserves volume in phase space.<br>FR: La dynamique hamiltonienne préserve le volume dans l’espace des phases.<br>中：Hamilton 动力学保持相空间体积。                                                     |
| —                                | action-angle variables, variables action-angle, 作用-角变量                                | Coordinates simplifying integrable Hamiltonian systems into linear motion on tori.                                | EN: Action-angle variables describe quasiperiodic motion.<br>FR: Les variables action-angle décrivent le mouvement quasi-périodique.<br>中：作用-角变量描述拟周期运动。                                                               |
| —                                | integrable system, système intégrable, 可积系统                                           | A system with enough conserved quantities to allow strong simplification of dynamics.                             | EN: Completely integrable Hamiltonian systems move on invariant tori.<br>FR: Les systèmes hamiltoniens complètement intégrables évoluent sur des tores invariants.<br>中：完全可积 Hamilton 系统在不变环面上运动。                      |
| —                                | KAM theorem, théorème KAM, KAM 定理                                                     | A theorem showing persistence of many invariant tori under small perturbations of integrable Hamiltonian systems. | EN: KAM theory explains partial stability in nearly integrable systems.<br>FR: La théorie KAM explique la stabilité partielle des systèmes presque intégrables.<br>中：KAM 理论解释近可积系统中的部分稳定性。                             |

## 35.11 Random dynamical systems and stochastic dynamics

| Symbol (LaTeX)        | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                       | Definition                                                                                | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| --------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| —                     | random dynamical system, système dynamique aléatoire, 随机动力系统            | A dynamical system whose evolution depends on randomness.                                 | EN: Random dynamical systems model dynamics under noisy environments.<br>FR: Les systèmes dynamiques aléatoires modélisent la dynamique sous environnements bruités.<br>中：随机动力系统建模噪声环境下的动力行为。                   |
| `\theta_t\omega`      | noise shift, décalage du bruit, 噪声移位                                    | A transformation describing time shift on the underlying randomness.                      | EN: Noise shifts encode how random environments evolve in time.<br>FR: Les décalages du bruit encodent l’évolution temporelle des environnements aléatoires.<br>中：噪声移位编码随机环境随时间的演化。                             |
| `\varphi(t,\omega,x)` | random cocycle, cocycle aléatoire, 随机余循环                                | A map describing random evolution satisfying a cocycle property.                          | EN: Random cocycles generalize deterministic flows.<br>FR: Les cocycles aléatoires généralisent les flots déterministes.<br>中：随机余循环推广确定性流。                                                                      |
| —                     | cocycle property, propriété de cocycle, 余循环性质                           | A composition rule expressing consistency of evolution through time.                      | EN: Evolving for `s` and then `t` equals evolving for `s+t` with shifted noise.<br>FR: Évoluer pendant `s` puis `t` équivaut à évoluer pendant `s+t` avec bruit décalé.<br>中：先演化 `s` 再演化 `t` 等价于在移位噪声下演化 `s+t`。 |
| —                     | random attractor, attracteur aléatoire, 随机吸引子                           | A random invariant set attracting random trajectories.                                    | EN: Random attractors describe long-time behavior under noise.<br>FR: Les attracteurs aléatoires décrivent le comportement à long terme sous bruit.<br>中：随机吸引子描述噪声下的长期行为。                                       |
| —                     | stochastic stability, stabilité stochastique, 随机稳定性                     | Persistence of statistical behavior under small random perturbations.                     | EN: Stochastic stability asks whether invariant measures survive noise.<br>FR: La stabilité stochastique demande si les mesures invariantes survivent au bruit.<br>中：随机稳定性考察不变测度是否能在噪声下保持。                      |
| —                     | Markov process, processus de Markov, Markov 过程                          | A stochastic process whose future depends on the past only through the present.           | EN: Diffusions are Markov processes generated by stochastic differential equations.<br>FR: Les diffusions sont des processus de Markov engendrés par des EDS.<br>中：扩散过程是由随机微分方程生成的 Markov 过程。                   |
| `P_t`                 | transition semigroup, semi-groupe de transition, 转移半群                   | Operators describing time evolution of distributions or observables for a Markov process. | EN: `P_t f(x)` is the expected value of `f(X_t)` starting from `x`.<br>FR: `P_t f(x)` est l’espérance de `f(X_t)` en partant de `x`.<br>中：`P_t f(x)` 是从 `x` 出发时 `f(X_t)` 的期望。                                   |
| —                     | invariant probability measure, mesure de probabilité invariante, 不变概率测度 | A probability measure unchanged by the Markov transition.                                 | EN: Invariant probability measures describe stationary stochastic regimes.<br>FR: Les mesures de probabilité invariantes décrivent les régimes stochastiques stationnaires.<br>中：不变概率测度描述平稳随机状态。                |
| —                     | Fokker-Planck equation, équation de Fokker-Planck, Fokker-Planck 方程     | A PDE describing evolution of probability densities for diffusion processes.              | EN: Fokker-Planck equations track distributional dynamics.<br>FR: Les équations de Fokker-Planck suivent la dynamique des distributions.<br>中：Fokker-Planck 方程追踪分布演化。                                           |
| —                     | Langevin equation, équation de Langevin, Langevin 方程                    | A stochastic differential equation modeling dynamics with damping and noise.              | EN: Langevin dynamics is used in statistical physics and sampling.<br>FR: La dynamique de Langevin est utilisée en physique statistique et échantillonnage.<br>中：Langevin 动力学用于统计物理和采样。                         |
| —                     | metastability, métastabilité, 亚稳态                                       | Long residence in quasi-stable states before rare transitions.                            | EN: Noisy systems can remain near one attractor before jumping to another.<br>FR: Les systèmes bruités peuvent rester près d’un attracteur avant de sauter vers un autre.<br>中：含噪系统可长时间停留在一个吸引子附近，然后跃迁到另一个吸引子。  |

## 35.12 Networks, coupled systems, and complex dynamics

| Symbol (LaTeX) | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                          | Definition                                                                                            | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| -------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `G=(V,E)`      | network, réseau, 网络                                        | A graph structure describing interacting components.                                                  | EN: Coupled oscillators can be placed on a network.<br>FR: Des oscillateurs couplés peuvent être placés sur un réseau.<br>中：耦合振子可放置在网络上。                                                                        |
| `x_i(t)`       | node state, état du nœud, 节点状态                             | The dynamical state associated with node `i`.                                                         | EN: In epidemic models, `x_i(t)` may represent infection status.<br>FR: Dans les modèles épidémiques, `x_i(t)` peut représenter l’état d’infection.<br>中：在流行病模型中，`x_i(t)` 可表示感染状态。                              |
| `A_{ij}`       | adjacency matrix, matrice d’adjacence, 邻接矩阵                | A matrix encoding which nodes are connected.                                                          | EN: `A_{ij}=1` can mean node `i` is connected to node `j`.<br>FR: `A_{ij}=1` peut signifier que le nœud `i` est connecté au nœud `j`.<br>中：`A_{ij}=1` 可表示节点 `i` 与节点 `j` 相连。                                     |
| `L`            | graph Laplacian, laplacien de graphe, 图 Laplace 算子         | A matrix encoding diffusion or coupling structure on a graph.                                         | EN: Consensus dynamics often use the graph Laplacian.<br>FR: La dynamique de consensus utilise souvent le laplacien de graphe.<br>中：一致性动力学常使用图 Laplace 算子。                                                      |
| —              | coupled dynamical system, système dynamique couplé, 耦合动力系统 | A system made of interacting subsystems.                                                              | EN: Neural, ecological, and power-grid models are coupled dynamical systems.<br>FR: Les modèles neuronaux, écologiques et de réseaux électriques sont des systèmes dynamiques couplés.<br>中：神经、生态和电网模型都是耦合动力系统。 |
| —              | synchronization, synchronisation, 同步                       | A phenomenon where interacting systems adjust to a common rhythm or state.                            | EN: Coupled oscillators may synchronize their phases.<br>FR: Des oscillateurs couplés peuvent synchroniser leurs phases.<br>中：耦合振子可能同步其相位。                                                                      |
| `\theta_i`     | phase variable, variable de phase, 相位变量                    | A variable representing position in an oscillatory cycle.                                             | EN: Phase models reduce oscillators to variables `\theta_i`.<br>FR: Les modèles de phase réduisent les oscillateurs aux variables `\theta_i`.<br>中：相位模型把振子化简为变量 `\theta_i`。                                     |
| —              | Kuramoto model, modèle de Kuramoto, Kuramoto 模型            | A model of coupled phase oscillators used to study synchronization.                                   | EN: The Kuramoto model shows a transition from incoherence to synchronization.<br>FR: Le modèle de Kuramoto montre une transition de l’incohérence à la synchronisation.<br>中：Kuramoto 模型展示从不相干到同步的转变。          |
| —              | consensus dynamics, dynamique de consensus, 一致性动力学         | Dynamics where agents update states toward agreement.                                                 | EN: Consensus models appear in distributed control and social dynamics.<br>FR: Les modèles de consensus apparaissent en contrôle distribué et dynamique sociale.<br>中：一致性模型出现在分布式控制和社会动力学中。                     |
| —              | pattern formation, formation de motifs, 模式形成               | Emergence of spatial or temporal structures from dynamical interactions.                              | EN: Reaction-diffusion systems can generate stripes and spots.<br>FR: Les systèmes réaction-diffusion peuvent produire rayures et taches.<br>中：反应扩散系统可产生条纹和斑点。                                                  |
| —              | Turing instability, instabilité de Turing, Turing 不稳定性     | A mechanism where diffusion destabilizes a homogeneous equilibrium and creates patterns.              | EN: Turing instability explains some biological pattern formation.<br>FR: L’instabilité de Turing explique certaines formations de motifs biologiques.<br>中：Turing 不稳定性解释某些生物图案形成。                              |
| —              | complex system, système complexe, 复杂系统                     | A system with many interacting parts producing collective behavior not obvious from components alone. | EN: Complex systems include ecosystems, economies, and neural networks.<br>FR: Les systèmes complexes incluent écosystèmes, économies et réseaux neuronaux.<br>中：复杂系统包括生态系统、经济系统和神经网络。                          |

# 36. Information Theory, Coding, and Statistical Physics Interface

## 36.1 Information, uncertainty, and entropy

| Symbol (LaTeX)               | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                   | Definition                                                                                 | Examples                                                                                                                                                                               |
| ---------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `X`                          | random variable, variable aléatoire, 随机变量                           | A variable whose value is determined by chance according to a probability distribution.    | EN: A coin toss outcome can be modeled as a random variable.<br>FR: Le résultat d’un lancer de pièce peut être modélisé comme une variable aléatoire.<br>中：一次抛硬币的结果可建模为随机变量。           |
| `\mathcal{X}`                | alphabet, alphabet, 字母表                                             | The set of possible symbols or values of a discrete random variable.                       | EN: For a binary source, `\mathcal{X}=\{0,1\}`.<br>FR: Pour une source binaire, `\mathcal{X}=\{0,1\}`.<br>中：对二元信源，`\mathcal{X}=\{0,1\}`。                                               |
| `p(x)`                       | probability mass function, fonction de masse de probabilité, 概率质量函数 | A function assigning probability to each discrete outcome.                                 | EN: A fair coin has `p(0)=p(1)=1/2`.<br>FR: Une pièce équilibrée a `p(0)=p(1)=1/2`.<br>中：公平硬币满足 `p(0)=p(1)=1/2`。                                                                       |
| `-\log p(x)`                 | self-information, auto-information, 自信息                             | The information content of observing outcome `x`; rarer events carry more information.     | EN: A rare event has larger self-information.<br>FR: Un événement rare a une auto-information plus grande.<br>中：罕见事件具有更大的自信息。                                                          |
| `H(X)`                       | entropy, entropie, 熵                                                | The expected uncertainty or average information content of a random variable.              | EN: Entropy is maximal for a uniform distribution on a finite alphabet.<br>FR: L’entropie est maximale pour une loi uniforme sur un alphabet fini.<br>中：有限字母表上的均匀分布具有最大熵。              |
| `H(X)=-\sum_x p(x)\log p(x)` | Shannon entropy, entropie de Shannon, Shannon 熵                     | The standard entropy formula for a discrete random variable.                               | EN: Shannon entropy measures average code length in ideal compression.<br>FR: L’entropie de Shannon mesure la longueur moyenne idéale d’un code compressé.<br>中：Shannon 熵度量理想压缩中的平均码长。 |
| `h(X)`                       | differential entropy, entropie différentielle, 微分熵                  | An entropy-like quantity for continuous random variables, defined using density functions. | EN: Differential entropy can be negative, unlike discrete entropy.<br>FR: L’entropie différentielle peut être négative, contrairement à l’entropie discrète.<br>中：微分熵可以为负，不同于离散熵。      |
| `H_b(p)`                     | binary entropy function, fonction d’entropie binaire, 二元熵函数         | The entropy of a Bernoulli random variable with success probability `p`.                   | EN: `H_b(p)` is largest at `p=1/2`.<br>FR: `H_b(p)` est maximale en `p=1/2`.<br>中：`H_b(p)` 在 `p=1/2` 处最大。                                                                              |
| —                            | information content, contenu informationnel, 信息量                    | A measure of how surprising or informative an observation is.                              | EN: Predictable messages contain little new information.<br>FR: Les messages prévisibles contiennent peu d’information nouvelle.<br>中：可预测消息包含的新信息较少。                                   |
| —                            | uncertainty, incertitude, 不确定性                                      | Lack of certainty about which outcome will occur.                                          | EN: Entropy formalizes uncertainty in a probability distribution.<br>FR: L’entropie formalise l’incertitude d’une distribution de probabilité.<br>中：熵形式化概率分布中的不确定性。                    |

## 36.2 Joint, conditional, and mutual information

| Symbol (LaTeX)                 | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                                 | Definition                                                                                           | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| ------------------------------ | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `H(X,Y)`                       | joint entropy, entropie jointe, 联合熵                                               | The entropy of a pair of random variables considered together.                                       | EN: Joint entropy measures uncertainty in both `X` and `Y`.<br>FR: L’entropie jointe mesure l’incertitude de `X` et `Y` ensemble.<br>中：联合熵度量 `X` 与 `Y` 的整体不确定性。                                                                            |
| `H(X\mid Y)`                   | conditional entropy, entropie conditionnelle, 条件熵                                 | The remaining uncertainty about `X` after observing `Y`.                                             | EN: If `Y` fully determines `X`, then `H(X\mid Y)=0`.<br>FR: Si `Y` détermine entièrement `X`, alors `H(X\mid Y)=0`.<br>中：若 `Y` 完全决定 `X`，则 `H(X\mid Y)=0`。                                                                                 |
| `H(X,Y)=H(X)+H(Y\mid X)`       | chain rule for entropy, règle de chaîne de l’entropie, 熵链式法则                      | A decomposition of joint entropy into marginal entropy and conditional entropy.                      | EN: The chain rule separates uncertainty into stages.<br>FR: La règle de chaîne sépare l’incertitude en étapes.<br>中：熵链式法则把不确定性分阶段分解。                                                                                                      |
| `I(X;Y)`                       | mutual information, information mutuelle, 互信息                                     | The amount of information shared between two random variables.                                       | EN: Mutual information is zero when variables are independent.<br>FR: L’information mutuelle est nulle lorsque les variables sont indépendantes.<br>中：当变量独立时，互信息为零。                                                                        |
| `I(X;Y)=H(X)-H(X\mid Y)`       | mutual information identity, identité de l’information mutuelle, 互信息恒等式           | A formula expressing mutual information as reduction in uncertainty.                                 | EN: Observing `Y` reduces uncertainty about `X` by `I(X;Y)`.<br>FR: Observer `Y` réduit l’incertitude sur `X` de `I(X;Y)`.<br>中：观察 `Y` 会使对 `X` 的不确定性减少 `I(X;Y)`。                                                                           |
| `I(X;Y\mid Z)`                 | conditional mutual information, information mutuelle conditionnelle, 条件互信息        | The information shared by `X` and `Y` after conditioning on `Z`.                                     | EN: Conditional mutual information tests dependence after controlling for `Z`.<br>FR: L’information mutuelle conditionnelle teste la dépendance après contrôle de `Z`.<br>中：条件互信息检验控制 `Z` 后 `X` 与 `Y` 的依赖。                                 |
| `X\to Y\to Z`                  | Markov chain, chaîne de Markov, Markov 链                                          | A dependence structure where `X` and `Z` are conditionally independent given `Y`.                    | EN: In `X\to Y\to Z`, information flows through `Y`.<br>FR: Dans `X\to Y\to Z`, l’information passe par `Y`.<br>中：在 `X\to Y\to Z` 中，信息通过 `Y` 传递。                                                                                           |
| —                              | data processing inequality, inégalité de traitement des données, 数据处理不等式          | A theorem stating that post-processing cannot increase information about the original variable.      | EN: No transformation of `Y` can create more information about `X` than `Y` already has.<br>FR: Aucune transformation de `Y` ne peut créer plus d’information sur `X` que `Y` n’en possède déjà.<br>中：对 `Y` 的后处理不能产生比 `Y` 原本更多的关于 `X` 的信息。 |
| `H(X_1,\ldots,X_n)`            | entropy of a random vector, entropie d’un vecteur aléatoire, 随机向量熵                | The joint entropy of multiple random variables.                                                      | EN: Entropy of a sequence measures uncertainty in the whole message.<br>FR: L’entropie d’une suite mesure l’incertitude du message entier.<br>中：序列熵度量整个消息的不确定性。                                                                            |
| `I(X;Y)=D(P_{XY}\Vert P_XP_Y)` | mutual information as divergence, information mutuelle comme divergence, 作为散度的互信息 | Mutual information equals KL divergence between the joint distribution and the product of marginals. | EN: This formula shows mutual information measures departure from independence.<br>FR: Cette formule montre que l’information mutuelle mesure l’écart à l’indépendance.<br>中：该公式说明互信息度量偏离独立性的程度。                                           |

## 36.3 Divergences, cross-entropy, and information geometry

| Symbol (LaTeX)                                 | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                             | Definition                                                                          | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| ---------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `D(P\Vert Q)`                                  | Kullback-Leibler divergence, divergence de Kullback-Leibler, KL 散度            | A nonnegative measure of discrepancy from distribution `Q` to distribution `P`.     | EN: KL divergence is zero exactly when the two distributions agree almost everywhere.<br>FR: La divergence KL est nulle exactement lorsque les deux lois coïncident presque partout.<br>中：KL 散度为零当且仅当两个分布几乎处处一致。                   |
| `D(P\Vert Q)=\sum_x P(x)\log\frac{P(x)}{Q(x)}` | discrete KL divergence formula, formule discrète de divergence KL, 离散 KL 散度公式 | The standard KL divergence formula for discrete distributions.                      | EN: KL divergence is not symmetric in `P` and `Q`.<br>FR: La divergence KL n’est pas symétrique en `P` et `Q`.<br>中：KL 散度关于 `P` 与 `Q` 不对称。                                                                                         |
| `H(P,Q)`                                       | cross-entropy, entropie croisée, 交叉熵                                          | The expected negative log probability assigned by `Q` to samples from `P`.          | EN: Cross-entropy loss is widely used in classification.<br>FR: La perte d’entropie croisée est largement utilisée en classification.<br>中：交叉熵损失广泛用于分类任务。                                                                          |
| `H(P,Q)=H(P)+D(P\Vert Q)`                      | cross-entropy decomposition, décomposition de l’entropie croisée, 交叉熵分解       | A decomposition showing that cross-entropy equals entropy plus KL divergence.       | EN: Minimizing cross-entropy over `Q` is equivalent to minimizing KL divergence from `P`.<br>FR: Minimiser l’entropie croisée en `Q` équivaut à minimiser la divergence KL depuis `P`.<br>中：对 `Q` 最小化交叉熵等价于最小化从 `P` 到 `Q` 的 KL 散度。 |
| `D_\alpha(P\Vert Q)`                           | Rényi divergence, divergence de Rényi, Rényi 散度                               | A one-parameter family of divergences generalizing KL divergence.                   | EN: Rényi divergences appear in privacy, hypothesis testing, and coding.<br>FR: Les divergences de Rényi apparaissent en confidentialité, tests d’hypothèses et codage.<br>中：Rényi 散度出现在隐私、假设检验和编码理论中。                             |
| `H_\alpha(X)`                                  | Rényi entropy, entropie de Rényi, Rényi 熵                                     | A one-parameter entropy family generalizing Shannon entropy.                        | EN: Rényi entropy emphasizes different parts of the distribution depending on `\alpha`.<br>FR: L’entropie de Rényi met l’accent sur différentes parties de la loi selon `\alpha`.<br>中：Rényi 熵根据 `\alpha` 的不同强调分布的不同部分。            |
| `D_{\mathrm{JS}}(P,Q)`                         | Jensen-Shannon divergence, divergence de Jensen-Shannon, Jensen-Shannon 散度    | A symmetrized and smoothed divergence based on KL divergence.                       | EN: Jensen-Shannon divergence is finite and symmetric for probability distributions.<br>FR: La divergence de Jensen-Shannon est finie et symétrique pour les lois de probabilité.<br>中：Jensen-Shannon 散度对概率分布有限且对称。                |
| `d_{\mathrm{TV}}(P,Q)`                         | total variation distance, distance en variation totale, 总变差距离                 | The largest probability difference assigned by two distributions to the same event. | EN: Total variation directly controls event-probability error.<br>FR: La variation totale contrôle directement l’erreur de probabilité des événements.<br>中：总变差距离直接控制事件概率误差。                                                       |
| `W_p(P,Q)`                                     | Wasserstein distance, distance de Wasserstein, Wasserstein 距离                 | An optimal-transport distance measuring the cost of moving mass from `P` to `Q`.    | EN: Wasserstein distance is sensitive to geometry of the sample space.<br>FR: La distance de Wasserstein est sensible à la géométrie de l’espace d’échantillonnage.<br>中：Wasserstein 距离对样本空间的几何结构敏感。                               |
