| `g_{ij}(\theta)`                               | Fisher information metric, métrique d’information de Fisher, Fisher 信息度量      | A Riemannian metric on statistical models derived from Fisher information.          | EN: Information geometry studies statistical models as curved spaces.<br>FR: La géométrie de l’information étudie les modèles statistiques comme des espaces courbes.<br>中：信息几何把统计模型研究为弯曲空间。                                       |

## 36.4 Source coding and lossless compression

| Symbol (LaTeX)             | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                           | Definition                                                                                             | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                     |
| -------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| —                          | source, source, 信源                                          | A mechanism producing symbols or messages according to a probability law.                              | EN: A text generator can be modeled as a source.<br>FR: Un générateur de texte peut être modélisé comme une source.<br>中：文本生成器可建模为信源。                                                                        |
| `X^n`                      | source block, bloc de source, 信源块                           | A length-`n` sequence emitted by a source.                                                             | EN: Compression algorithms encode blocks `X^n` into shorter bit strings.<br>FR: Les algorithmes de compression encodent les blocs `X^n` en chaînes binaires plus courtes.<br>中：压缩算法把信源块 `X^n` 编码成更短的比特串。     |
| `\ell(x)`                  | codeword length, longueur du mot de code, 码字长度              | The number of symbols or bits used to encode a source symbol or message.                               | EN: Frequent symbols should receive shorter codewords.<br>FR: Les symboles fréquents devraient recevoir des mots de code plus courts.<br>中：高频符号应获得更短码字。                                                      |
| —                          | lossless compression, compression sans perte, 无损压缩          | Compression allowing exact reconstruction of the original message.                                     | EN: ZIP compression is designed to be lossless.<br>FR: La compression ZIP est conçue pour être sans perte.<br>中：ZIP 压缩旨在无损恢复原始数据。                                                                            |
| —                          | prefix code, code préfixe, 前缀码                              | A code in which no codeword is a prefix of another codeword.                                           | EN: Prefix codes allow instantaneous decoding.<br>FR: Les codes préfixes permettent un décodage instantané.<br>中：前缀码允许即时解码。                                                                                  |
| `\sum_x 2^{-\ell(x)}\leq1` | Kraft inequality, inégalité de Kraft, Kraft 不等式             | A necessary and sufficient condition for the existence of a binary prefix code with lengths `\ell(x)`. | EN: Kraft’s inequality constrains possible prefix-code lengths.<br>FR: L’inégalité de Kraft contraint les longueurs possibles des codes préfixes.<br>中：Kraft 不等式约束前缀码长度的可能形式。                                |
| —                          | Huffman coding, codage de Huffman, Huffman 编码               | An optimal prefix coding algorithm for known symbol probabilities.                                     | EN: Huffman coding assigns shorter codes to more probable symbols.<br>FR: Le codage de Huffman attribue des codes plus courts aux symboles plus probables.<br>中：Huffman 编码给高概率符号分配更短码字。                      |
| —                          | arithmetic coding, codage arithmétique, 算术编码                | A compression method encoding an entire message as a subinterval of the unit interval.                 | EN: Arithmetic coding can approach entropy limits closely.<br>FR: Le codage arithmétique peut approcher de près les limites entropiques.<br>中：算术编码可非常接近熵极限。                                                  |
| —                          | source coding theorem, théorème du codage de source, 信源编码定理 | A theorem stating that entropy gives the fundamental limit of lossless compression.                    | EN: Average code length cannot be pushed below entropy in the asymptotic ideal.<br>FR: La longueur moyenne du code ne peut pas descendre sous l’entropie dans l’idéal asymptotique.<br>中：在渐近理想情形中，平均码长不能低于熵。 |
| `A_\epsilon^{(n)}`         | typical set, ensemble typique, 典型集                          | The set of length-`n` sequences whose probabilities are close to the entropy rate prediction.          | EN: Most long iid sequences lie in the typical set.<br>FR: La plupart des longues suites iid appartiennent à l’ensemble typique.<br>中：大多数长 iid 序列落在典型集中。                                                     |

## 36.5 Channels, noise, and channel capacity

| Symbol (LaTeX)                      | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                                                                    | Definition                                                                               | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| ----------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `P_{Y\mid X}`                       | communication channel, canal de communication, 通信信道                                                                  | A conditional distribution describing how input symbols produce output symbols.          | EN: A noisy channel randomly corrupts transmitted symbols.<br>FR: Un canal bruité corrompt aléatoirement les symboles transmis.<br>中：有噪信道会随机破坏传输符号。                                                        |
| `X`                                 | channel input, entrée du canal, 信道输入                                                                                 | The symbol or message sent into a communication channel.                                 | EN: A transmitter chooses the channel input `X`.<br>FR: Un émetteur choisit l’entrée du canal `X`.<br>中：发送端选择信道输入 `X`。                                                                                     |
| `Y`                                 | channel output, sortie du canal, 信道输出                                                                                | The symbol received after transmission through a channel.                                | EN: The receiver observes `Y`, not necessarily the original `X`.<br>FR: Le récepteur observe `Y`, pas nécessairement le `X` original.<br>中：接收端观测到 `Y`，不一定是原始 `X`。                                          |
| `C`                                 | channel capacity, capacité du canal, 信道容量                                                                            | The maximum reliable communication rate over a channel.                                  | EN: Capacity is measured in bits per channel use when logarithms are base two.<br>FR: La capacité se mesure en bits par utilisation du canal avec logarithmes en base deux.<br>中：当对数以二为底时，容量单位是每次信道使用的比特数。 |
| `C=\max_{P_X} I(X;Y)`               | capacity formula for a discrete memoryless channel, formule de capacité d’un canal discret sans mémoire, 离散无记忆信道容量公式 | The channel capacity as the maximum mutual information over input distributions.         | EN: Capacity optimizes the input distribution for reliable transmission.<br>FR: La capacité optimise la distribution d’entrée pour une transmission fiable.<br>中：容量通过优化输入分布实现可靠传输极限。                       |
| —                                   | discrete memoryless channel, canal discret sans mémoire, 离散无记忆信道                                                     | A channel whose outputs depend only on current inputs and not on previous transmissions. | EN: The binary symmetric channel is a discrete memoryless channel.<br>FR: Le canal binaire symétrique est un canal discret sans mémoire.<br>中：二元对称信道是离散无记忆信道。                                              |
| `BSC(p)`                            | binary symmetric channel, canal binaire symétrique, 二元对称信道                                                           | A binary channel flipping each bit independently with probability `p`.                   | EN: `BSC(p)` models random bit flips.<br>FR: `BSC(p)` modélise des inversions aléatoires de bits.<br>中：`BSC(p)` 建模随机比特翻转。                                                                                  |
| `BEC(\epsilon)`                     | binary erasure channel, canal binaire à effacement, 二元擦除信道                                                           | A binary channel where each input is either received correctly or erased.                | EN: Erasures are easier to correct than unknown bit flips.<br>FR: Les effacements sont plus faciles à corriger que les inversions inconnues.<br>中：擦除错误比未知比特翻转更容易纠正。                                        |
| —                                   | additive white Gaussian noise channel, canal gaussien à bruit blanc additif, 加性白 Gaussian 噪声信道                       | A continuous channel where Gaussian noise is added to the input signal.                  | EN: AWGN channels are basic models in wireless communication.<br>FR: Les canaux AWGN sont des modèles fondamentaux en communication sans fil.<br>中：AWGN 信道是无线通信中的基本模型。                                     |
| `C=\frac{1}{2}\log(1+\mathrm{SNR})` | Gaussian channel capacity, capacité du canal gaussien, Gaussian 信道容量                                                 | The capacity formula for a real Gaussian channel under a power constraint.               | EN: Higher signal-to-noise ratio increases capacity logarithmically.<br>FR: Un rapport signal-bruit plus élevé augmente la capacité logarithmiquement.<br>中：更高信噪比会以对数方式增加容量。                               |

## 36.6 Channel coding and error correction

| Symbol (LaTeX)       | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                           | Definition                                                                                                   | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| -------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `M`                  | message set size, taille de l’ensemble des messages, 消息集合大小 | The number of possible messages to be transmitted.                                                           | EN: A code with `M` messages can transmit `\log_2 M` bits of information.<br>FR: Un code avec `M` messages peut transmettre `\log_2 M` bits d’information.<br>中：含 `M` 个消息的编码可传输 `\log_2 M` 比特信息。                              |
| `n`                  | block length, longueur de bloc, 块长                          | The number of channel uses assigned to one codeword.                                                         | EN: Longer block length can reduce error probability.<br>FR: Une longueur de bloc plus grande peut réduire la probabilité d’erreur.<br>中：更长块长可降低错误概率。                                                                         |
| `R=\frac{\log M}{n}` | coding rate, taux de codage, 编码率                            | The amount of information transmitted per channel use.                                                       | EN: Reliable communication is possible below channel capacity.<br>FR: Une communication fiable est possible sous la capacité du canal.<br>中：低于信道容量时可实现可靠通信。                                                                   |
| `\mathcal{C}`        | codebook, livre de code, 码本                                 | The set of codewords used for transmission.                                                                  | EN: The encoder maps each message to a codeword in `\mathcal{C}`.<br>FR: L’encodeur associe chaque message à un mot de code dans `\mathcal{C}`.<br>中：编码器把每个消息映射到码本 `\mathcal{C}` 中的码字。                                        |
| `d_H(x,y)`           | Hamming distance, distance de Hamming, Hamming 距离           | The number of positions where two equal-length strings differ.                                               | EN: Larger Hamming distance improves error-correction capability.<br>FR: Une plus grande distance de Hamming améliore la capacité de correction d’erreurs.<br>中：更大的 Hamming 距离提高纠错能力。                                         |
| `d_{\min}`           | minimum distance, distance minimale, 最小距离                   | The smallest Hamming distance between distinct codewords in a code.                                          | EN: A code with minimum distance `d_{\min}` can detect up to `d_{\min}-1` errors.<br>FR: Un code de distance minimale `d_{\min}` peut détecter jusqu’à `d_{\min}-1` erreurs.<br>中：最小距离为 `d_{\min}` 的编码可检测至多 `d_{\min}-1` 个错误。 |
| —                    | error-correcting code, code correcteur d’erreurs, 纠错码       | A code designed to recover original messages despite transmission errors.                                    | EN: Error-correcting codes are used in storage and communication systems.<br>FR: Les codes correcteurs d’erreurs sont utilisés dans le stockage et les communications.<br>中：纠错码用于存储和通信系统。                                     |
| —                    | linear code, code linéaire, 线性码                             | A code forming a linear subspace over a finite field.                                                        | EN: Linear codes are compactly described by generator and parity-check matrices.<br>FR: Les codes linéaires se décrivent par matrices génératrices et de contrôle.<br>中：线性码可由生成矩阵和校验矩阵紧凑描述。                                   |
| `G`                  | generator matrix, matrice génératrice, 生成矩阵                 | A matrix whose rows span a linear code.                                                                      | EN: Multiplying a message vector by `G` produces a codeword.<br>FR: Multiplier un vecteur message par `G` produit un mot de code.<br>中：消息向量乘以 `G` 得到码字。                                                                       |
| `H`                  | parity-check matrix, matrice de contrôle, 校验矩阵              | A matrix used to check whether a received vector belongs to a linear code.                                   | EN: A valid codeword satisfies `Hx=0` over the finite field.<br>FR: Un mot de code valide satisfait `Hx=0` sur le corps fini.<br>中：合法码字在有限域上满足 `Hx=0`。                                                                        |
| —                    | syndrome decoding, décodage par syndrome, 伴随式译码             | A decoding method using the syndrome to infer likely error patterns.                                         | EN: Syndrome decoding converts received-word errors into algebraic checks.<br>FR: Le décodage par syndrome transforme les erreurs reçues en contrôles algébriques.<br>中：伴随式译码把接收错误转化为代数校验。                                    |
| —                    | channel coding theorem, théorème du codage de canal, 信道编码定理 | A theorem stating that rates below capacity allow arbitrarily reliable communication with long enough codes. | EN: Shannon’s theorem separates possible rates from impossible rates.<br>FR: Le théorème de Shannon sépare les taux possibles des taux impossibles.<br>中：Shannon 定理区分可达传输率与不可达传输率。                                            |

## 36.7 Rate-distortion and lossy compression

| Symbol (LaTeX) | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                         | Definition                                                                  | Examples                                                                                                                                                                               |
| -------------- | --------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `d(x,\hat{x})` | distortion measure, mesure de distorsion, 失真度量            | A function measuring loss when reconstructing `x` as `\hat{x}`.             | EN: Squared error is a common distortion measure for signals.<br>FR: L’erreur quadratique est une mesure de distorsion courante pour les signaux.<br>中：平方误差是信号处理中常见的失真度量。              |
| `D`            | distortion level, niveau de distorsion, 失真水平              | The allowed average distortion in lossy compression.                        | EN: Lower distortion requires higher coding rate.<br>FR: Une distorsion plus faible exige un taux de codage plus élevé.<br>中：更低失真需要更高编码率。                                              |
| `R(D)`         | rate-distortion function, fonction taux-distorsion, 率失真函数 | The minimum coding rate needed to represent a source within distortion `D`. | EN: Rate-distortion theory gives limits of lossy compression.<br>FR: La théorie taux-distorsion donne les limites de la compression avec perte.<br>中：率失真理论给出有损压缩的极限。                   |
| `\hat{X}`      | reconstruction, reconstruction, 重构                        | The decoded approximation of the source variable.                           | EN: In image compression, `\hat{X}` is the reconstructed image.<br>FR: En compression d’image, `\hat{X}` est l’image reconstruite.<br>中：在图像压缩中，`\hat{X}` 是重构图像。                        |
| —              | lossy compression, compression avec perte, 有损压缩           | Compression allowing approximate reconstruction with controlled distortion. | EN: JPEG uses lossy compression.<br>FR: JPEG utilise une compression avec perte.<br>中：JPEG 使用有损压缩。                                                                                     |
| —              | quantization, quantification, 量化                          | Mapping a large or continuous set of values to a smaller discrete set.      | EN: Audio compression quantizes signal amplitudes.<br>FR: La compression audio quantifie les amplitudes du signal.<br>中：音频压缩会量化信号幅度。                                                   |
| —              | vector quantization, quantification vectorielle, 向量量化     | Quantization of blocks or vectors rather than individual scalar values.     | EN: Vector quantization exploits structure across components.<br>FR: La quantification vectorielle exploite la structure entre composantes.<br>中：向量量化利用分量之间的结构。                        |
| —              | codebook quantizer, quantificateur à livre de code, 码本量化器 | A quantizer selecting reconstruction values from a finite codebook.         | EN: Each input vector is mapped to its nearest codeword.<br>FR: Chaque vecteur d’entrée est associé au mot de code le plus proche.<br>中：每个输入向量被映射到最近码字。                                |
| —              | transform coding, codage par transformée, 变换编码            | A compression method applying a transform before quantization and encoding. | EN: Image codecs often use transform coding.<br>FR: Les codecs d’image utilisent souvent le codage par transformée.<br>中：图像编码器常使用变换编码。                                                 |
| —              | perceptual coding, codage perceptuel, 感知编码                | Lossy coding designed around human perceptual limitations.                  | EN: Audio compression removes details that listeners are unlikely to notice.<br>FR: La compression audio supprime des détails que les auditeurs remarquent peu.<br>中：音频压缩会去除人耳不易察觉的细节。 |

## 36.8 Information theory in machine learning

| Symbol (LaTeX)                    | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                      | Definition                                                                                     | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                               |
| --------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `\mathcal{L}_{CE}`                | cross-entropy loss, perte d’entropie croisée, 交叉熵损失                    | A loss function comparing predicted probabilities with true labels.                            | EN: Neural classifiers often minimize cross-entropy loss.<br>FR: Les classifieurs neuronaux minimisent souvent la perte d’entropie croisée.<br>中：神经网络分类器常最小化交叉熵损失。                                     |
| `D_{\mathrm{KL}}(q(z)\Vert p(z))` | KL regularization, régularisation KL, KL 正则化                           | A penalty encouraging an approximate distribution to remain close to a reference distribution. | EN: Variational autoencoders use KL regularization in their objective.<br>FR: Les autoencodeurs variationnels utilisent une régularisation KL dans leur objectif.<br>中：变分自编码器在目标函数中使用 KL 正则化。          |
| `ELBO`                            | evidence lower bound, borne inférieure de l’évidence, 证据下界             | A lower bound on log marginal likelihood used in variational inference.                        | EN: Maximizing ELBO balances data fit and posterior regularization.<br>FR: Maximiser l’ELBO équilibre ajustement aux données et régularisation postérieure.<br>中：最大化 ELBO 平衡数据拟合与后验正则化。                |
| `I(X;Z)`                          | representation information, information de représentation, 表征信息        | Mutual information between input `X` and representation `Z`.                                   | EN: A representation with high `I(X;Z)` preserves much input information.<br>FR: Une représentation avec grand `I(X;Z)` conserve beaucoup d’information d’entrée.<br>中：具有高 `I(X;Z)` 的表征保留较多输入信息。       |
| —                                 | information bottleneck, goulot d’information, 信息瓶颈                     | A principle seeking compressed representations that preserve information about a target.       | EN: Information bottleneck trades compression of `X` against prediction of `Y`.<br>FR: Le goulot d’information échange compression de `X` contre prédiction de `Y`.<br>中：信息瓶颈在压缩 `X` 与预测 `Y` 之间权衡。     |
| `I(Z;Y)`                          | predictive information, information prédictive, 预测信息                   | Mutual information between a representation and the target variable.                           | EN: Useful representations preserve information about labels.<br>FR: Les représentations utiles conservent l’information sur les étiquettes.<br>中：有用表征保留关于标签的信息。                                       |
| —                                 | minimum description length, longueur minimale de description, 最小描述长度   | A model selection principle preferring the shortest total description of model and data.       | EN: MDL links compression with statistical learning.<br>FR: MDL relie compression et apprentissage statistique.<br>中：MDL 把压缩与统计学习联系起来。                                                                 |
| —                                 | variational inference, inférence variationnelle, 变分推断                  | Approximate Bayesian inference by optimizing over a tractable family of distributions.         | EN: Variational inference minimizes a KL divergence indirectly through ELBO.<br>FR: L’inférence variationnelle minimise indirectement une divergence KL via l’ELBO.<br>中：变分推断通过 ELBO 间接最小化 KL 散度。      |
| —                                 | contrastive learning, apprentissage contrastif, 对比学习                   | A learning method using positive and negative pairs to learn representations.                  | EN: Contrastive objectives often relate to mutual information lower bounds.<br>FR: Les objectifs contrastifs se relient souvent à des bornes inférieures d’information mutuelle.<br>中：对比学习目标常与互信息下界相关。 |
| `NCE`                             | noise-contrastive estimation, estimation contrastive par bruit, 噪声对比估计 | A method estimating models by distinguishing data from noise samples.                          | EN: NCE turns density estimation into classification.<br>FR: La NCE transforme l’estimation de densité en classification.<br>中：NCE 把密度估计转化为分类问题。                                                       |

## 36.9 Statistical mechanics, microstates, and ensembles

| Symbol (LaTeX) | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                         | Definition                                                                         | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                         |
| -------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `\Omega`       | state space of microstates, espace des micro-états, 微观态空间                 | The set of microscopic configurations available to a physical system.              | EN: Spin configurations form a microstate space in the Ising model.<br>FR: Les configurations de spins forment un espace de micro-états dans le modèle d’Ising.<br>中：在 Ising 模型中，自旋构型构成微观态空间。                    |
| `x\in\Omega`   | microstate, micro-état, 微观态                                               | A complete microscopic configuration of a system.                                  | EN: One assignment of all spins is a microstate.<br>FR: Une attribution de tous les spins est un micro-état.<br>中：所有自旋的一种赋值就是一个微观态。                                                                              |
| —              | macrostate, macro-état, 宏观态                                               | A coarse description of a system by macroscopic quantities.                        | EN: Temperature and pressure describe a macrostate.<br>FR: Température et pression décrivent un macro-état.<br>中：温度和压强描述宏观态。                                                                                     |
| `E(x)`         | energy function, fonction d’énergie, 能量函数                                 | A function assigning energy to each microstate.                                    | EN: Low-energy microstates are favored at low temperature.<br>FR: Les micro-états de basse énergie sont favorisés à basse température.<br>中：低温下低能微观态更受偏好。                                                        |
| `S`            | thermodynamic entropy, entropie thermodynamique, 热力学熵                     | A macroscopic measure of disorder or multiplicity of compatible microstates.       | EN: Entropy increases when more microstates are compatible with the same macrostate.<br>FR: L’entropie augmente lorsque davantage de micro-états correspondent au même macro-état.<br>中：当更多微观态对应同一宏观态时，熵增加。      |
| `S=k_B\log W`  | Boltzmann entropy formula, formule d’entropie de Boltzmann, Boltzmann 熵公式 | A formula relating entropy to the number `W` of compatible microstates.            | EN: Boltzmann entropy connects counting with thermodynamics.<br>FR: L’entropie de Boltzmann relie comptage et thermodynamique.<br>中：Boltzmann 熵把计数与热力学联系起来。                                                      |
| `k_B`          | Boltzmann constant, constante de Boltzmann, Boltzmann 常数                  | A physical constant relating temperature to energy.                                | EN: `k_B T` sets a thermal energy scale.<br>FR: `k_B T` fixe une échelle d’énergie thermique.<br>中：`k_B T` 给出热能尺度。                                                                                               |
| —              | ensemble, ensemble statistique, 系综                                        | A probability distribution over microstates representing thermodynamic conditions. | EN: The canonical ensemble fixes temperature rather than energy exactly.<br>FR: L’ensemble canonique fixe la température plutôt que l’énergie exactement.<br>中：正则系综固定温度，而不是精确固定能量。                               |
| —              | microcanonical ensemble, ensemble microcanonique, 微正则系综                   | An ensemble assigning equal probability to microstates with fixed energy.          | EN: The microcanonical ensemble models isolated systems.<br>FR: L’ensemble microcanonique modélise les systèmes isolés.<br>中：微正则系综建模孤立系统。                                                                        |
| —              | canonical ensemble, ensemble canonique, 正则系综                              | An ensemble for systems in thermal contact with a heat bath at fixed temperature.  | EN: The canonical ensemble gives probabilities proportional to `e^{-\beta E(x)}`.<br>FR: L’ensemble canonique donne des probabilités proportionnelles à `e^{-\beta E(x)}`.<br>中：正则系综给出正比于 `e^{-\beta E(x)}` 的概率。 |

## 36.10 Gibbs measures, partition functions, and free energy

| Symbol (LaTeX)                   | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                   | Definition                                                                                    | Examples                                                                                                                                                                       |
| -------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `\beta`                          | inverse temperature, température inverse, 逆温度                       | A parameter usually equal to `1/(k_BT)`, controlling concentration on low-energy states.      | EN: Large `\beta` corresponds to low temperature.<br>FR: Un grand `\beta` correspond à une basse température.<br>中：较大的 `\beta` 对应较低温度。                                         |
| `p(x)=\frac{e^{-\beta E(x)}}{Z}` | Gibbs distribution, distribution de Gibbs, Gibbs 分布                 | A probability distribution assigning weights to states according to energy and temperature.   | EN: Gibbs distributions favor low-energy states.<br>FR: Les distributions de Gibbs favorisent les états de basse énergie.<br>中：Gibbs 分布偏好低能态。                                  |
| `Z`                              | partition function, fonction de partition, 配分函数                     | The normalizing constant of a Gibbs distribution and a generator of thermodynamic quantities. | EN: The partition function normalizes Boltzmann weights.<br>FR: La fonction de partition normalise les poids de Boltzmann.<br>中：配分函数归一化 Boltzmann 权重。                          |
| `Z=\sum_x e^{-\beta E(x)}`       | discrete partition function, fonction de partition discrète, 离散配分函数 | The partition function for a finite or countable state space.                                 | EN: Computing `Z` is often the hard part in probabilistic models.<br>FR: Calculer `Z` est souvent la partie difficile des modèles probabilistes.<br>中：计算 `Z` 往往是概率模型中的困难部分。    |
| `F=-\beta^{-1}\log Z`            | Helmholtz free energy, énergie libre de Helmholtz, Helmholtz 自由能    | A thermodynamic potential derived from the partition function.                                | EN: Free energy combines energy and entropy effects.<br>FR: L’énergie libre combine effets d’énergie et d’entropie.<br>中：自由能结合能量效应与熵效应。                                        |
| `\langle E\rangle`               | expected energy, énergie moyenne, 平均能量                              | The average energy under a Gibbs distribution.                                                | EN: Expected energy decreases as temperature decreases.<br>FR: L’énergie moyenne diminue lorsque la température baisse.<br>中：温度降低时，平均能量下降。                                     |
| —                                | Boltzmann weight, poids de Boltzmann, Boltzmann 权重                  | The unnormalized factor `e^{-\beta E(x)}` assigned to a microstate.                           | EN: Boltzmann weights compare relative probabilities of states.<br>FR: Les poids de Boltzmann comparent les probabilités relatives des états.<br>中：Boltzmann 权重比较不同状态的相对概率。    |
| —                                | Gibbs measure, mesure de Gibbs, Gibbs 测度                            | A probability measure on configurations defined by energy, temperature, and interactions.     | EN: Gibbs measures describe equilibrium states of lattice models.<br>FR: Les mesures de Gibbs décrivent les états d’équilibre des modèles sur réseau.<br>中：Gibbs 测度描述格点模型的平衡态。 |
| `\log Z`                         | log-partition function, log-partition, 对数配分函数                       | The logarithm of the partition function, often convex and central in exponential families.    | EN: Gradients of `\log Z` give expected sufficient statistics.<br>FR: Les gradients de `\log Z` donnent les statistiques suffisantes moyennes.<br>中：`\log Z` 的梯度给出充分统计量的期望。    |
| —                                | variational free energy, énergie libre variationnelle, 变分自由能        | A variational objective combining expected energy and entropy.                                | EN: Variational inference minimizes a free-energy-like objective.<br>FR: L’inférence variationnelle minimise un objectif analogue à l’énergie libre.<br>中：变分推断最小化类似自由能的目标。     |

## 36.11 Maximum entropy, exponential families, and variational principles

| Symbol (LaTeX)     | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                | Definition                                                                                                             | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| ------------------ | ---------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| —                  | maximum entropy principle, principe du maximum d’entropie, 最大熵原理 | The principle of choosing the highest-entropy distribution subject to known constraints.                               | EN: With fixed mean and variance, the maximum entropy distribution on the real line is Gaussian.<br>FR: Avec moyenne et variance fixées, la loi d’entropie maximale sur la droite réelle est gaussienne.<br>中：在均值和方差固定时，实线上的最大熵分布是 Gaussian 分布。 |
| `p_\theta(x)`      | exponential family, famille exponentielle, 指数族                   | A family of distributions expressible through sufficient statistics, natural parameters, and a log-partition function. | EN: Bernoulli, Gaussian, and Poisson models fit exponential-family forms.<br>FR: Les modèles de Bernoulli, gaussien et Poisson entrent dans les familles exponentielles.<br>中：Bernoulli、Gaussian 和 Poisson 模型都可写成指数族形式。                         |
| `T(x)`             | sufficient statistic, statistique suffisante, 充分统计量              | A statistic capturing all sample information about a parameter within a model.                                         | EN: In many exponential families, likelihood depends on data through `T(x)`.<br>FR: Dans beaucoup de familles exponentielles, la vraisemblance dépend des données via `T(x)`.<br>中：在许多指数族中，似然通过 `T(x)` 依赖数据。                                    |
| `\theta`           | natural parameter, paramètre naturel, 自然参数                       | The canonical parameter multiplying sufficient statistics in an exponential family.                                    | EN: Natural parameters make convex duality especially clean.<br>FR: Les paramètres naturels rendent la dualité convexe particulièrement claire.<br>中：自然参数使凸对偶结构特别清晰。                                                                            |
| `A(\theta)`        | log-partition function, fonction log-partition, 对数配分函数           | The normalizer ensuring an exponential-family density integrates or sums to one.                                       | EN: `A(\theta)` is convex in the natural parameter.<br>FR: `A(\theta)` est convexe en le paramètre naturel.<br>中：`A(\theta)` 关于自然参数是凸的。                                                                                                         |
| `\nabla A(\theta)` | mean parameter map, application vers le paramètre moyen, 均值参数映射  | The map from natural parameters to expected sufficient statistics.                                                     | EN: In exponential families, `\nabla A(\theta)=\mathbb{E}_\theta[T(X)]`.<br>FR: Dans les familles exponentielles, `\nabla A(\theta)=\mathbb{E}_\theta[T(X)]`.<br>中：在指数族中，`\nabla A(\theta)=\mathbb{E}_\theta[T(X)]`。                            |
| —                  | Legendre duality, dualité de Legendre, Legendre 对偶               | A convex duality relating natural parameters and mean parameters.                                                      | EN: Free energy and entropy are linked by Legendre transforms.<br>FR: Énergie libre et entropie sont reliées par transformées de Legendre.<br>中：自由能与熵通过 Legendre 变换相联系。                                                                         |
| —                  | variational principle, principe variationnel, 变分原理               | A principle characterizing a quantity as an optimum over distributions or functions.                                   | EN: Free energy admits a variational characterization.<br>FR: L’énergie libre admet une caractérisation variationnelle.<br>中：自由能具有变分刻画。                                                                                                         |
| —                  | mean-field approximation, approximation de champ moyen, 平均场近似    | An approximation replacing interacting variables by simpler independent or averaged effects.                           | EN: Mean-field methods approximate complex Gibbs measures.<br>FR: Les méthodes de champ moyen approximent les mesures de Gibbs complexes.<br>中：平均场方法近似复杂 Gibbs 测度。                                                                              |
| —                  | belief propagation, propagation de croyances, 信念传播               | A message-passing algorithm for approximate inference on graphical models.                                             | EN: Belief propagation is exact on trees under standard assumptions.<br>FR: La propagation de croyances est exacte sur les arbres sous hypothèses standards.<br>中：在标准假设下，信念传播在树上是精确的。                                                           |

## 36.12 Phase transitions, critical phenomena, and information flow

| Symbol (LaTeX) | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                         | Definition                                                                             | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| -------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| —              | phase transition, transition de phase, 相变                                 | A qualitative change in macroscopic behavior as a parameter changes.                   | EN: Water freezing is a physical phase transition.<br>FR: La congélation de l’eau est une transition de phase physique.<br>中：水结冰是物理相变。                                                                                          |
| `T_c`          | critical temperature, température critique, 临界温度                          | The temperature at which a phase transition occurs.                                    | EN: Below `T_c`, an Ising model may develop spontaneous magnetization.<br>FR: Sous `T_c`, un modèle d’Ising peut développer une aimantation spontanée.<br>中：低于 `T_c` 时，Ising 模型可能出现自发磁化。                                        |
| —              | order parameter, paramètre d’ordre, 序参量                                   | A macroscopic quantity distinguishing phases.                                          | EN: Magnetization is an order parameter for ferromagnetic transitions.<br>FR: L’aimantation est un paramètre d’ordre pour les transitions ferromagnétiques.<br>中：磁化强度是铁磁相变的序参量。                                                 |
| —              | critical point, point critique, 临界点                                       | A parameter value where scale-invariant behavior and singular fluctuations may appear. | EN: Near a critical point, correlations can extend over long distances.<br>FR: Près d’un point critique, les corrélations peuvent s’étendre sur de longues distances.<br>中：在临界点附近，相关可延伸到很长距离。                                   |
| `\xi`          | correlation length, longueur de corrélation, 相关长度                         | A length scale over which correlations decay significantly.                            | EN: Correlation length often diverges near a continuous phase transition.<br>FR: La longueur de corrélation diverge souvent près d’une transition continue.<br>中：在连续相变附近，相关长度常发散。                                               |
| —              | critical exponent, exposant critique, 临界指数                                | An exponent describing power-law behavior near a critical point.                       | EN: Critical exponents classify universality classes.<br>FR: Les exposants critiques classifient les classes d’universalité.<br>中：临界指数用于分类普适类。                                                                                  |
| —              | universality class, classe d’universalité, 普适类                            | A class of systems sharing the same large-scale critical behavior.                     | EN: Different microscopic systems can share one universality class.<br>FR: Des systèmes microscopiques différents peuvent partager une même classe d’universalité.<br>中：不同微观系统可共享同一个普适类。                                        |
| —              | renormalization group, groupe de renormalisation, 重整化群                    | A framework studying how system descriptions change across scales.                     | EN: Renormalization explains why microscopic details may disappear at large scales.<br>FR: La renormalisation explique pourquoi les détails microscopiques peuvent disparaître aux grandes échelles.<br>中：重整化解释为何微观细节在大尺度下可能消失。 |
| —              | coarse-graining, grossissement, 粗粒化                                       | Replacing fine-scale details by aggregate variables or effective descriptions.         | EN: Coarse-graining reduces microscopic complexity.<br>FR: Le grossissement réduit la complexité microscopique.<br>中：粗粒化降低微观复杂性。                                                                                                |
| —              | information flow across scales, flux d’information entre échelles, 跨尺度信息流 | The movement or loss of relevant information when changing resolution.                 | EN: Renormalization tracks which information survives coarse-graining.<br>FR: La renormalisation suit l’information qui survit au grossissement.<br>中：重整化追踪哪些信息在粗粒化后仍然保留。                                                       |
| —              | Ising model, modèle d’Ising, Ising 模型                                     | A lattice spin model with local interactions, central in phase-transition theory.      | EN: The Ising model is a canonical model of magnetism and collective behavior.<br>FR: Le modèle d’Ising est un modèle canonique du magnétisme et du comportement collectif.<br>中：Ising 模型是磁性与集体行为的典型模型。                         |
| —              | spin glass, verre de spin, 自旋玻璃                                           | A disordered system with frustration and many competing low-energy states.             | EN: Spin glasses connect statistical physics with optimization and learning theory.<br>FR: Les verres de spin relient physique statistique, optimisation et apprentissage.<br>中：自旋玻璃连接统计物理、优化与学习理论。                             |

# 37. Category Theory and Higher Structures

## 37.1 Categories, objects, morphisms, and diagrams

| Symbol (LaTeX)                          | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                              | Definition                                                                                                                  | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
| --------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `\mathcal{C}`                           | category, catégorie, 范畴                        | A structure consisting of objects, morphisms between objects, identity morphisms, and associative composition.              | EN: The category of sets has sets as objects and functions as morphisms.<br>FR: La catégorie des ensembles a pour objets les ensembles et pour morphismes les fonctions.<br>中：集合范畴以集合为对象，以函数为态射。                                                                    |
| `X\in\mathcal{C}`                       | object, objet, 对象                              | An entity inside a category, studied through its morphisms rather than internal elements alone.                             | EN: A vector space is an object of the category of vector spaces.<br>FR: Un espace vectoriel est un objet de la catégorie des espaces vectoriels.<br>中：向量空间是向量空间范畴中的对象。                                                                                             |
| `f:X\to Y`                              | morphism, morphisme, 态射                        | An arrow from one object to another, generalizing functions, maps, homomorphisms, and structure-preserving transformations. | EN: A group homomorphism is a morphism in the category of groups.<br>FR: Un homomorphisme de groupes est un morphisme dans la catégorie des groupes.<br>中：群同态是群范畴中的态射。                                                                                              |
| `\operatorname{Hom}_{\mathcal{C}}(X,Y)` | Hom-set, ensemble Hom, Hom 集                   | The collection of morphisms from object `X` to object `Y` in a category.                                                    | EN: In `\mathbf{Set}`, `\operatorname{Hom}(X,Y)` is the set of functions from `X` to `Y`.<br>FR: Dans `\mathbf{Set}`, `\operatorname{Hom}(X,Y)` est l’ensemble des fonctions de `X` vers `Y`.<br>中：在 `\mathbf{Set}` 中，`\operatorname{Hom}(X,Y)` 是从 `X` 到 `Y` 的函数集合。 |
| `\operatorname{id}_X`                   | identity morphism, morphisme identité, 恒等态射    | The morphism from an object to itself that acts as a neutral element under composition.                                     | EN: For every object `X`, `\operatorname{id}_X` leaves all morphisms unchanged under composition.<br>FR: Pour tout objet `X`, `\operatorname{id}_X` laisse les morphismes inchangés par composition.<br>中：对每个对象 `X`，`\operatorname{id}_X` 在复合下不改变态射。                |
| `g\circ f`                              | composition, composition, 复合                   | The operation combining `f:X\to Y` and `g:Y\to Z` into `g\circ f:X\to Z`.                                                   | EN: Function composition is the model example of categorical composition.<br>FR: La composition des fonctions est l’exemple modèle de composition catégorique.<br>中：函数复合是范畴复合的典型例子。                                                                                 |
| `(h\circ g)\circ f=h\circ(g\circ f)`    | associativity, associativité, 结合律              | The axiom requiring composition to be independent of parenthesization.                                                      | EN: Associativity lets diagrams of composable arrows be read unambiguously.<br>FR: L’associativité permet de lire sans ambiguïté les diagrammes de flèches composables.<br>中：结合律使可复合箭头图式的读取不依赖括号。                                                                   |
| `\mathcal{D}`                           | subcategory, sous-catégorie, 子范畴               | A category formed from selected objects and morphisms of a larger category, closed under identities and composition.        | EN: Finite-dimensional vector spaces form a subcategory of vector spaces.<br>FR: Les espaces vectoriels de dimension finie forment une sous-catégorie des espaces vectoriels.<br>中：有限维向量空间构成向量空间范畴的子范畴。                                                             |
| —                                       | small category, petite catégorie, 小范畴          | A category whose objects and morphisms form sets rather than proper classes.                                                | EN: A finite poset can be regarded as a small category.<br>FR: Un ensemble ordonné fini peut être vu comme une petite catégorie.<br>中：有限偏序集可视为小范畴。                                                                                                                  |
| —                                       | large category, grande catégorie, 大范畴          | A category whose objects may form a proper class.                                                                           | EN: The category of all sets is usually treated as large.<br>FR: La catégorie de tous les ensembles est généralement traitée comme grande.<br>中：所有集合构成的范畴通常视为大范畴。                                                                                                   |
| —                                       | commutative diagram, diagramme commutatif, 交换图 | A diagram of objects and morphisms in which all directed paths with the same endpoints give the same composite morphism.    | EN: Commutative diagrams express equations between morphisms visually.<br>FR: Les diagrammes commutatifs expriment visuellement des équations entre morphismes.<br>中：交换图用图形方式表达态射之间的等式。                                                                             |
| `\mathcal{C}^{op}`                      | opposite category, catégorie opposée, 对偶范畴     | The category obtained by reversing all morphisms of `\mathcal{C}`.                                                          | EN: Many categorical notions have dual versions in `\mathcal{C}^{op}`.<br>FR: Beaucoup de notions catégoriques ont des versions duales dans `\mathcal{C}^{op}`.<br>中：许多范畴概念在 `\mathcal{C}^{op}` 中有对偶版本。                                                             |

## 37.2 Functors and natural transformations

| Symbol (LaTeX)                     | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                                        | Definition                                                                                                                 | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| ---------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `F:\mathcal{C}\to\mathcal{D}`      | functor, foncteur, 函子                                                                    | A structure-preserving map between categories, sending objects to objects and morphisms to morphisms.                      | EN: The forgetful functor from groups to sets sends a group to its underlying set.<br>FR: Le foncteur d’oubli des groupes vers les ensembles envoie un groupe vers son ensemble sous-jacent.<br>中：从群到集合的遗忘函子把群送到其底层集合。                   |
| `F(f):F(X)\to F(Y)`                | image of a morphism, image d’un morphisme, 态射的像                                          | The morphism assigned by a functor to a morphism `f:X\to Y`.                                                               | EN: A linear-map functor sends a linear map to another induced linear map.<br>FR: Un foncteur linéaire envoie une application linéaire vers une application induite.<br>中：线性映射函子把一个线性映射送到相应的诱导线性映射。                                      |
| `F(g\circ f)=F(g)\circ F(f)`       | functoriality, fonctorialité, 函子性                                                        | The property that a functor preserves composition and identities.                                                          | EN: Functoriality ensures that structural operations commute with mapping by `F`.<br>FR: La fonctorialité assure que les opérations structurelles commutent avec l’application par `F`.<br>中：函子性保证结构操作与 `F` 的映射相容。                       |
| `F:\mathcal{C}^{op}\to\mathcal{D}` | contravariant functor, foncteur contravariant, 反变函子                                      | A functor that reverses morphism direction, equivalently a functor from the opposite category.                             | EN: The dual-space construction is contravariant on vector spaces.<br>FR: La construction de l’espace dual est contravariante sur les espaces vectoriels.<br>中：对偶空间构造在向量空间上是反变的。                                                         |
| `F:\mathcal{C}\to\mathbf{Set}`     | set-valued functor, foncteur à valeurs dans les ensembles, 集合值函子                         | A functor assigning a set to each object of a category.                                                                    | EN: The Hom functor `\operatorname{Hom}(A,-)` is set-valued.<br>FR: Le foncteur Hom `\operatorname{Hom}(A,-)` est à valeurs dans les ensembles.<br>中：Hom 函子 `\operatorname{Hom}(A,-)` 是集合值函子。                                            |
| `\eta:F\Rightarrow G`              | natural transformation, transformation naturelle, 自然变换                                   | A family of morphisms `\eta_X:F(X)\to G(X)` compatible with every morphism in the source category.                         | EN: Natural transformations are morphisms between functors.<br>FR: Les transformations naturelles sont des morphismes entre foncteurs.<br>中：自然变换是函子之间的态射。                                                                                |
| `\eta_X`                           | component of a natural transformation, composante d’une transformation naturelle, 自然变换分量 | The morphism at object `X` belonging to a natural transformation.                                                          | EN: Naturality says the components `\eta_X` fit together coherently.<br>FR: La naturalité dit que les composantes `\eta_X` s’assemblent de façon cohérente.<br>中：自然性表示各个分量 `\eta_X` 以相容方式组合。                                             |
| `G(f)\circ\eta_X=\eta_Y\circ F(f)` | naturality square, carré de naturalité, 自然性方块                                            | The commutative square expressing compatibility of a natural transformation with morphisms.                                | EN: Naturality squares make “canonical construction” precise.<br>FR: Les carrés de naturalité rendent précise la notion de construction canonique.<br>中：自然性方块精确表达“典范构造”。                                                                 |
| `F\simeq G`                        | natural isomorphism, isomorphisme naturel, 自然同构                                          | A natural transformation whose every component is an isomorphism.                                                          | EN: Naturally isomorphic functors represent the same construction up to canonical equivalence.<br>FR: Des foncteurs naturellement isomorphes représentent la même construction à équivalence canonique près.<br>中：自然同构的函子表示同一个构造，只差典范等价。 |
| —                                  | endofunctor, endofoncteur, 自函子                                                           | A functor from a category to itself.                                                                                       | EN: The power-set construction is an endofunctor on `\mathbf{Set}`.<br>FR: La construction de l’ensemble des parties est un endofoncteur sur `\mathbf{Set}`.<br>中：幂集构造是 `\mathbf{Set}` 上的自函子。                                            |
| `\operatorname{Nat}(F,G)`          | set of natural transformations, ensemble des transformations naturelles, 自然变换集           | The collection of natural transformations from functor `F` to functor `G`.                                                 | EN: Functor categories use natural transformations as morphisms.<br>FR: Les catégories de foncteurs utilisent les transformations naturelles comme morphismes.<br>中：函子范畴以自然变换作为态射。                                                       |
| `[\mathcal{C},\mathcal{D}]`        | functor category, catégorie de foncteurs, 函子范畴                                           | A category whose objects are functors from `\mathcal{C}` to `\mathcal{D}` and whose morphisms are natural transformations. | EN: Functor categories organize all diagrams of a fixed shape.<br>FR: Les catégories de foncteurs organisent tous les diagrammes d’une forme fixée.<br>中：函子范畴组织固定形状的所有图式。                                                                |

## 37.3 Isomorphisms, equivalences, and universal viewpoints

| Symbol (LaTeX)                               | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                         | Definition                                                                                                            | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
| -------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `f:X\overset{\sim}{\to}Y`                    | isomorphism, isomorphisme, 同构                                             | A morphism with a two-sided inverse.                                                                                  | EN: Isomorphic groups have the same group structure.<br>FR: Des groupes isomorphes ont la même structure de groupe.<br>中：同构的群具有相同群结构。                                                                                                 |
| `f^{-1}`                                     | inverse morphism, morphisme inverse, 逆态射                                  | A morphism satisfying `f^{-1}\circ f=\operatorname{id}` and `f\circ f^{-1}=\operatorname{id}`.                        | EN: A bijective linear map has an inverse linear map.<br>FR: Une application linéaire bijective possède une application linéaire inverse.<br>中：双射线性映射有逆线性映射。                                                                          |
| `X\simeq Y`                                  | isomorphic objects, objets isomorphes, 同构对象                               | Objects connected by an isomorphism.                                                                                  | EN: Category theory often treats isomorphic objects as structurally the same.<br>FR: La théorie des catégories traite souvent les objets isomorphes comme structurellement identiques.<br>中：范畴论通常把同构对象视为结构上相同。                        |
| —                                            | equivalence of categories, équivalence de catégories, 范畴等价                | A relation between categories weaker than isomorphism but preserving categorical structure up to natural isomorphism. | EN: Equivalent categories may have different objects but the same mathematical content.<br>FR: Des catégories équivalentes peuvent avoir des objets différents mais le même contenu mathématique.<br>中：等价范畴可以有不同对象，却表达相同数学内容。         |
| `F:\mathcal{C}\rightleftarrows\mathcal{D}:G` | quasi-inverse functors, foncteurs quasi-inverses, 准逆函子                    | Functors realizing an equivalence of categories up to natural isomorphism.                                            | EN: A quasi-inverse reverses a categorical equivalence up to coherent isomorphism.<br>FR: Un quasi-inverse renverse une équivalence catégorique à isomorphisme cohérent près.<br>中：准逆函子在相干同构意义下逆转范畴等价。                                |
| —                                            | full functor, foncteur plein, 满函子                                         | A functor surjective on every Hom-set map.                                                                            | EN: A full functor captures all morphisms between images.<br>FR: Un foncteur plein capture tous les morphismes entre les images.<br>中：满函子捕捉像对象之间的所有态射。                                                                                |
| —                                            | faithful functor, foncteur fidèle, 忠实函子                                   | A functor injective on every Hom-set map.                                                                             | EN: A faithful functor does not identify distinct morphisms.<br>FR: Un foncteur fidèle n’identifie pas des morphismes distincts.<br>中：忠实函子不会把不同态射混同。                                                                                  |
| —                                            | fully faithful functor, foncteur pleinement fidèle, 充分忠实函子                | A functor inducing bijections on all Hom-sets.                                                                        | EN: Fully faithful embeddings preserve all morphism-level information.<br>FR: Les plongements pleinement fidèles préservent toute l’information des morphismes.<br>中：充分忠实嵌入保留所有态射层面的信息。                                               |
| —                                            | essentially surjective functor, foncteur essentiellement surjectif, 本质满函子 | A functor whose target objects are all isomorphic to objects in its image.                                            | EN: A fully faithful and essentially surjective functor is an equivalence.<br>FR: Un foncteur pleinement fidèle et essentiellement surjectif est une équivalence.<br>中：充分忠实且本质满的函子给出范畴等价。                                             |
| —                                            | skeleton, squelette, 骨架                                                   | A full subcategory containing exactly one representative from each isomorphism class of objects.                      | EN: A skeleton removes duplicate isomorphic objects.<br>FR: Un squelette élimine les doublons isomorphes.<br>中：骨架去除同构意义下重复的对象。                                                                                                        |
| —                                            | universal property, propriété universelle, 泛性质                            | A characterization of an object by the unique existence of morphisms to or from it.                                   | EN: Products, quotients, tensor products, and free objects are defined by universal properties.<br>FR: Produits, quotients, produits tensoriels et objets libres sont définis par propriétés universelles.<br>中：积、商、张量积和自由对象都可由泛性质定义。 |
| —                                            | canonical construction, construction canonique, 典范构造                      | A construction independent of arbitrary choices, usually expressed by a universal property or naturality.             | EN: Universal properties explain why certain constructions are canonical.<br>FR: Les propriétés universelles expliquent pourquoi certaines constructions sont canoniques.<br>中：泛性质解释某些构造为何是典范的。                                       |

## 37.4 Products, coproducts, and universal constructions

| Symbol (LaTeX)                       | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                            | Definition                                                                                          | Examples                                                                                                                                                                      |
| ------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------ | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `X\times Y`                          | categorical product, produit catégorique, 范畴积                | An object equipped with projections to `X` and `Y` satisfying a universal mapping property.         | EN: In sets, the categorical product is the Cartesian product.<br>FR: Dans les ensembles, le produit catégorique est le produit cartésien.<br>中：在集合中，范畴积就是笛卡尔积。               |
| `\pi_X:X\times Y\to X`               | projection morphism, projection, 投影态射                        | A canonical morphism from a product to one factor.                                                  | EN: Product projections recover each component of a pair.<br>FR: Les projections du produit récupèrent chaque composante d’une paire.<br>中：积的投影取出有序对的各个分量。                    |
| `X\sqcup Y`                          | coproduct, coproduit, 余积                                     | The dual of product; an object receiving canonical morphisms from `X` and `Y`.                      | EN: In sets, coproduct is disjoint union.<br>FR: Dans les ensembles, le coproduit est l’union disjointe.<br>中：在集合中，余积是不交并。                                                    |
| `i_X:X\to X\sqcup Y`                 | coproduct injection, injection canonique, 余积嵌入               | A canonical morphism from a summand into a coproduct.                                               | EN: Coproduct injections include each piece into the combined object.<br>FR: Les injections du coproduit incluent chaque morceau dans l’objet combiné.<br>中：余积嵌入把每一部分放入合并对象中。 |
| `1`                                  | terminal object, objet terminal, 终对象                         | An object receiving a unique morphism from every object.                                            | EN: A one-point set is terminal in `\mathbf{Set}`.<br>FR: Un singleton est terminal dans `\mathbf{Set}`.<br>中：单点集是 `\mathbf{Set}` 中的终对象。                                      |
| `0`                                  | initial object, objet initial, 初对象                           | An object admitting a unique morphism to every object.                                              | EN: The empty set is initial in `\mathbf{Set}`.<br>FR: L’ensemble vide est initial dans `\mathbf{Set}`.<br>中：空集是 `\mathbf{Set}` 中的初对象。                                        |
| —                                    | zero object, objet zéro, 零对象                                 | An object that is both initial and terminal.                                                        | EN: The zero vector space is a zero object in vector spaces.<br>FR: L’espace vectoriel nul est un objet zéro dans les espaces vectoriels.<br>中：零向量空间是向量空间范畴中的零对象。             |
| `X^Y`                                | exponential object, objet exponentiel, 指数对象                  | An object representing morphisms from `Y` to `X` in categories with suitable products.              | EN: In sets, `X^Y` is the set of functions from `Y` to `X`.<br>FR: Dans les ensembles, `X^Y` est l’ensemble des fonctions de `Y` vers `X`.<br>中：在集合中，`X^Y` 是从 `Y` 到 `X` 的函数集。 |
| `\operatorname{ev}:X^Y\times Y\to X` | evaluation morphism, morphisme d’évaluation, 求值态射            | The morphism evaluating a generalized function object at an argument.                               | EN: Evaluation sends a function and an input to the function value.<br>FR: L’évaluation envoie une fonction et une entrée vers la valeur de la fonction.<br>中：求值把函数和输入送到函数值。  |
| —                                    | free object, objet libre, 自由对象                               | An object generated by data with no relations except those forced by the structure.                 | EN: The free group on a set contains formal words in that set.<br>FR: Le groupe libre sur un ensemble contient les mots formels en cet ensemble.<br>中：集合上的自由群包含该集合生成的形式词。     |
| —                                    | quotient object, objet quotient, 商对象                         | An object obtained by imposing identifications or relations, often defined by a universal property. | EN: Quotient groups identify elements modulo a normal subgroup.<br>FR: Les groupes quotients identifient les éléments modulo un sous-groupe normal.<br>中：商群按正规子群模掉元素。         |
| —                                    | representable construction, construction représentable, 可表构造 | A construction expressible as a representing object for a functor.                                  | EN: Universal objects often represent functors of solutions.<br>FR: Les objets universels représentent souvent des foncteurs de solutions.<br>中：泛对象常表示解空间函子。                  |

## 37.5 Limits, colimits, pullbacks, and pushouts

| Symbol (LaTeX)             | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                | Definition                                                                                             | Examples                                                                                                                                                                               |
| -------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `D:J\to\mathcal{C}`        | diagram, diagramme, 图式                           | A functor from an index category `J` into a category `\mathcal{C}`.                                    | EN: A diagram packages objects and arrows of a fixed shape.<br>FR: Un diagramme regroupe objets et flèches d’une forme fixée.<br>中：图式把固定形状的对象和箭头组织起来。                                  |
| `\lim D`                   | limit, limite, 极限                                | A universal object mapping compatibly to all objects of a diagram.                                     | EN: Products and pullbacks are special cases of limits.<br>FR: Les produits et produits fibrés sont des cas particuliers de limites.<br>中：积和拉回都是极限的特殊情形。                               |
| `\operatorname{colim}D`    | colimit, colimite, 余极限                           | A universal object receiving compatible morphisms from all objects of a diagram.                       | EN: Coproducts and pushouts are special cases of colimits.<br>FR: Les coproduits et sommes amalgamées sont des cas particuliers de colimites.<br>中：余积和推出都是余极限的特殊情形。                    |
| `X\times_Z Y`              | pullback, produit fibré, 拉回                      | The universal object representing pairs in `X` and `Y` with matching images in `Z`.                    | EN: Fiber products in algebraic geometry are categorical pullbacks.<br>FR: Les produits fibrés en géométrie algébrique sont des produits fibrés catégoriques.<br>中：代数几何中的纤维积就是范畴拉回。    |
| `X\sqcup_Z Y`              | pushout, somme amalgamée, 推出                     | The dual of pullback; a universal object gluing `X` and `Y` along `Z`.                                 | EN: Pushouts formalize gluing spaces along a common subspace.<br>FR: Les sommes amalgamées formalisent le recollement d’espaces le long d’un sous-espace commun.<br>中：推出形式化沿公共子空间粘合空间。 |
| `\ker f`                   | kernel, noyau, 核                                 | A universal morphism measuring elements or subobjects mapped to zero, in categories with zero objects. | EN: In groups or modules, kernels capture solutions to `f(x)=0`.<br>FR: Dans les groupes ou modules, les noyaux capturent les solutions de `f(x)=0`.<br>中：在群或模中，核捕捉满足 `f(x)=0` 的元素。    |
| `\operatorname{coker}f`    | cokernel, conoyau, 余核                            | The dual of kernel; a universal quotient killing the image of a morphism.                              | EN: Cokernels measure what remains after quotienting by the image.<br>FR: Les conoyaux mesurent ce qui reste après quotient par l’image.<br>中：余核度量除掉像之后剩下的部分。                          |
| `\operatorname{eq}(f,g)`   | equalizer, égalisateur, 等化子                      | The universal object on which two parallel morphisms become equal.                                     | EN: Equalizers generalize solution sets of equations `f(x)=g(x)`.<br>FR: Les égalisateurs généralisent les ensembles de solutions de `f(x)=g(x)`.<br>中：等化子推广方程 `f(x)=g(x)` 的解集。        |
| `\operatorname{coeq}(f,g)` | coequalizer, coégalisateur, 余等化子                 | The universal quotient forcing two parallel morphisms to become equal.                                 | EN: Coequalizers impose relations generated by `f(x)\sim g(x)`.<br>FR: Les coégalisateurs imposent les relations engendrées par `f(x)\sim g(x)`.<br>中：余等化子施加由 `f(x)\sim g(x)` 生成的关系。   |
| —                          | complete category, catégorie complète, 完备范畴      | A category having all small limits.                                                                    | EN: `\mathbf{Set}` is complete.<br>FR: `\mathbf{Set}` est complète.<br>中：`\mathbf{Set}` 是完备范畴。                                                                                         |
| —                          | cocomplete category, catégorie cocomplète, 余完备范畴 | A category having all small colimits.                                                                  | EN: Module categories are usually complete and cocomplete.<br>FR: Les catégories de modules sont généralement complètes et cocomplètes.<br>中：模范畴通常既完备又余完备。                             |
| —                          | filtered colimit, colimite filtrante, 滤过余极限      | A colimit indexed by a filtered category, often preserving finite algebraic structure.                 | EN: Direct limits of modules are filtered colimits in many cases.<br>FR: Les limites inductives de modules sont souvent des colimites filtrantes.<br>中：模的直极限在许多情形下是滤过余极限。              |

## 37.6 Adjunctions, monads, and comonads

| Symbol (LaTeX)                                                                       | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                         | Definition                                                                                      | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |
| ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `F\dashv G`                                                                          | adjunction, adjonction, 伴随                                                | A pair of functors related by a natural bijection between Hom-sets.                             | EN: Free and forgetful functors often form adjunctions.<br>FR: Les foncteurs libres et d’oubli forment souvent des adjonctions.<br>中：自由函子和遗忘函子常形成伴随。                                                                                           |
| `F`                                                                                  | left adjoint, adjoint à gauche, 左伴随                                       | The functor on the left side of an adjunction, often freely generating structure.               | EN: The free-group functor is left adjoint to the forgetful functor.<br>FR: Le foncteur groupe libre est adjoint à gauche du foncteur d’oubli.<br>中：自由群函子是遗忘函子的左伴随。                                                                            |
