| `G`                                                                                  | right adjoint, adjoint à droite, 右伴随                                      | The functor on the right side of an adjunction, often forgetting or extracting structure.       | EN: Right adjoints often preserve limits.<br>FR: Les adjoints à droite préservent souvent les limites.<br>中：右伴随通常保持极限。                                                                                                                         |
| `\operatorname{Hom}_{\mathcal{D}}(F X,Y)\cong\operatorname{Hom}_{\mathcal{C}}(X,GY)` | Hom adjunction formula, formule Hom d’adjonction, Hom 伴随公式                | The natural bijection defining an adjunction.                                                   | EN: The formula says maps out of a free object correspond to maps from generators.<br>FR: La formule dit que les applications depuis un objet libre correspondent aux applications depuis les générateurs.<br>中：该公式表示从自由对象出发的映射对应于从生成元出发的映射。   |
| `\eta:\operatorname{Id}_{\mathcal{C}}\Rightarrow GF`                                 | unit of an adjunction, unité d’une adjonction, 伴随单位                       | A natural transformation from the identity functor to the composite of right and left adjoints. | EN: The unit embeds generators into the underlying object of a free construction.<br>FR: L’unité insère les générateurs dans l’objet sous-jacent d’une construction libre.<br>中：伴随单位把生成元嵌入自由构造的底层对象。                                           |
| `\varepsilon:FG\Rightarrow\operatorname{Id}_{\mathcal{D}}`                           | counit of an adjunction, counité d’une adjonction, 伴随余单位                  | A natural transformation from the composite of left and right adjoints to the identity functor. | EN: The counit evaluates the freely generated structure back into a structured object.<br>FR: La counité évalue la structure librement engendrée dans un objet structuré.<br>中：伴随余单位把自由生成结构求值回结构对象。                                            |
| —                                                                                    | triangle identities, identités triangulaires, 三角恒等式                       | Coherence identities ensuring that unit and counit define a genuine adjunction.                 | EN: Triangle identities express that adjunction maps invert each other coherently.<br>FR: Les identités triangulaires expriment l’inversion cohérente des applications d’adjonction.<br>中：三角恒等式表达伴随映射之间的相干互逆。                                  |
| `T=GF`                                                                               | monad, monade, 单子                                                         | An endofunctor with unit and multiplication, often arising from an adjunction.                  | EN: Monads organize algebraic structures and computational effects.<br>FR: Les monades organisent structures algébriques et effets computationnels.<br>中：单子组织代数结构和计算效应。                                                                        |
| `\mu:T^2\Rightarrow T`                                                               | monad multiplication, multiplication de monade, 单子乘法                      | A natural transformation flattening two applications of a monad into one.                       | EN: List monad multiplication flattens a list of lists into a list.<br>FR: La multiplication de la monade des listes aplatit une liste de listes en liste.<br>中：列表单子的乘法把列表的列表展平成列表。                                                            |
| —                                                                                    | algebra for a monad, algèbre pour une monade, 单子代数                        | An object equipped with a compatible action of a monad.                                         | EN: Groups can be viewed as algebras for a suitable monad on sets.<br>FR: Les groupes peuvent être vus comme des algèbres pour une monade appropriée sur les ensembles.<br>中：群可看作集合上某个适当单子的代数。                                                 |
| —                                                                                    | comonad, comonade, 余单子                                                    | The categorical dual of a monad, with counit and comultiplication.                              | EN: Comonads model context-dependent or coalgebraic structures.<br>FR: Les comonades modélisent des structures contextuelles ou coalgébriques.<br>中：余单子建模依赖语境或余代数式结构。                                                                          |
| —                                                                                    | Eilenberg-Moore category, catégorie d’Eilenberg-Moore, Eilenberg-Moore 范畴 | The category of algebras over a monad.                                                          | EN: Eilenberg-Moore categories turn monadic structure into ordinary categorical objects.<br>FR: Les catégories d’Eilenberg-Moore transforment la structure monadique en objets catégoriques ordinaires.<br>中：Eilenberg-Moore 范畴把单子结构转化为普通范畴对象。 |

## 37.7 Monoidal, closed, and enriched categories

| Symbol (LaTeX)                     | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                           | Definition                                                                                      | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| ---------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `(\mathcal{C},\otimes,\mathbf{1})` | monoidal category, catégorie monoïdale, 幺半范畴                                | A category equipped with a tensor product, unit object, and associativity and unit constraints. | EN: Vector spaces form a monoidal category under tensor product.<br>FR: Les espaces vectoriels forment une catégorie monoïdale par produit tensoriel.<br>中：向量空间在张量积下构成幺半范畴。                                                |
| `\otimes`                          | tensor product in a monoidal category, produit tensoriel catégorique, 范畴张量积 | A bifunctor combining two objects into one object.                                              | EN: Tensor product abstracts parallel composition or product-like combination.<br>FR: Le produit tensoriel abstrait la composition parallèle ou une combinaison de type produit.<br>中：张量积抽象平行组合或乘积式组合。                     |
| `\mathbf{1}`                       | monoidal unit, unité monoïdale, 幺半单位                                        | The object acting as the identity for the tensor product.                                       | EN: The base field is the monoidal unit in vector spaces.<br>FR: Le corps de base est l’unité monoïdale dans les espaces vectoriels.<br>中：基域是向量空间范畴中的幺半单位。                                                                 |
| —                                  | symmetric monoidal category, catégorie monoïdale symétrique, 对称幺半范畴         | A monoidal category where tensor factors can be swapped coherently.                             | EN: Sets with Cartesian product form a symmetric monoidal category.<br>FR: Les ensembles avec produit cartésien forment une catégorie monoïdale symétrique.<br>中：集合在笛卡尔积下构成对称幺半范畴。                                         |
| —                                  | braided monoidal category, catégorie monoïdale tressée, 辫状幺半范畴              | A monoidal category with coherent but not necessarily symmetric swapping of tensor factors.     | EN: Quantum group representations often form braided monoidal categories.<br>FR: Les représentations de groupes quantiques forment souvent des catégories monoïdales tressées.<br>中：量子群表示常构成辫状幺半范畴。                        |
| `[X,Y]`                            | internal Hom, Hom interne, 内 Hom                                            | An object representing morphisms from `X` to `Y` inside the category.                           | EN: In a closed monoidal category, morphisms can themselves be internal objects.<br>FR: Dans une catégorie monoïdale fermée, les morphismes peuvent eux-mêmes être des objets internes.<br>中：在闭幺半范畴中，态射本身可由内部对象表示。         |
| —                                  | closed monoidal category, catégorie monoïdale fermée, 闭幺半范畴                 | A monoidal category where tensoring has a right adjoint given by internal Hom.                  | EN: Function spaces arise naturally in closed monoidal categories.<br>FR: Les espaces de fonctions apparaissent naturellement dans les catégories monoïdales fermées.<br>中：函数空间自然出现在闭幺半范畴中。                                |
| —                                  | Cartesian monoidal category, catégorie monoïdale cartésienne, 笛卡尔幺半范畴       | A monoidal category whose tensor product is given by categorical product.                       | EN: `\mathbf{Set}` is Cartesian monoidal under product.<br>FR: `\mathbf{Set}` est monoïdale cartésienne par le produit.<br>中：`\mathbf{Set}` 在积下是笛卡尔幺半范畴。                                                                   |
| —                                  | enriched category, catégorie enrichie, 充实范畴                                 | A category whose Hom-objects live in a monoidal category rather than just in sets.              | EN: Metric spaces can be viewed as enriched categories over ordered distances.<br>FR: Les espaces métriques peuvent être vus comme catégories enrichies sur les distances ordonnées.<br>中：度量空间可视为在有序距离上充实的范畴。              |
| `\mathcal{V}`                      | base of enrichment, base d’enrichissement, 充实基范畴                            | The monoidal category in which Hom-objects of an enriched category live.                        | EN: Categories enriched over vector spaces have Hom-vector spaces.<br>FR: Les catégories enrichies sur les espaces vectoriels ont des Hom-espaces vectoriels.<br>中：在向量空间上充实的范畴具有 Hom 向量空间。                                 |
| —                                  | tensored category, catégorie tensoriée, 张量化范畴                               | An enriched category admitting an external tensoring by objects of the enrichment base.         | EN: Tensoring allows objects to be scaled or parameterized by enrichment objects.<br>FR: La tensorisation permet de paramétrer des objets par les objets d’enrichissement.<br>中：张量化允许用充实基范畴中的对象参数化对象。                      |
| —                                  | compact closed category, catégorie compacte fermée, 紧闭范畴                    | A symmetric monoidal category where objects have duals compatible with tensor structure.        | EN: Compact closed categories model duality in linear algebra and quantum processes.<br>FR: Les catégories compactes fermées modélisent la dualité en algèbre linéaire et processus quantiques.<br>中：紧闭范畴建模线性代数和量子过程中的对偶性。 |

## 37.8 Abelian categories, exactness, and homological algebra

| Symbol (LaTeX)              | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                   | Definition                                                                                                                 | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                     |
| --------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| —                           | additive category, catégorie additive, 加性范畴         | A category with finite biproducts and abelian-group structures on Hom-sets compatible with composition.                    | EN: Categories of modules are additive.<br>FR: Les catégories de modules sont additives.<br>中：模范畴是加性范畴。                                                                                      |
| `X\oplus Y`                 | biproduct, biproduit, 双积                            | An object serving simultaneously as product and coproduct in an additive category.                                         | EN: Direct sums of vector spaces are biproducts.<br>FR: Les sommes directes d’espaces vectoriels sont des biproduits.<br>中：向量空间直和是双积。                                                        |
| —                           | abelian category, catégorie abélienne, Abel 范畴      | An additive category with kernels and cokernels where monomorphisms and epimorphisms behave like subobjects and quotients. | EN: The category of abelian groups is an abelian category.<br>FR: La catégorie des groupes abéliens est une catégorie abélienne.<br>中：Abel 群范畴是 Abel 范畴。                                     |
| `0\to A\to B\to C\to0`      | short exact sequence, suite exacte courte, 短正合列     | An exact sequence expressing `B` as an extension of `C` by `A`.                                                            | EN: Short exact sequences encode subobject and quotient data.<br>FR: Les suites exactes courtes encodent sous-objet et quotient.<br>中：短正合列编码子对象与商对象数据。                                       |
| —                           | exact functor, foncteur exact, 正合函子                 | A functor preserving exact sequences.                                                                                      | EN: Exact functors preserve kernels, images, and cokernels in the required way.<br>FR: Les foncteurs exacts préservent noyaux, images et conoyaux de la façon requise.<br>中：正合函子按要求保持核、像与余核。 |
| —                           | left exact functor, foncteur exact à gauche, 左正合函子  | A functor preserving exactness at the left part of short exact sequences.                                                  | EN: Hom functors are often left exact.<br>FR: Les foncteurs Hom sont souvent exacts à gauche.<br>中：Hom 函子通常是左正合的。                                                                            |
| —                           | right exact functor, foncteur exact à droite, 右正合函子 | A functor preserving exactness at the right part of short exact sequences.                                                 | EN: Tensor product is often right exact.<br>FR: Le produit tensoriel est souvent exact à droite.<br>中：张量积通常是右正合的。                                                                            |
| `R^iF`                      | right derived functor, foncteur dérivé droit, 右导出函子 | A functor measuring failure of left exact functors to be exact.                                                            | EN: Sheaf cohomology is a right derived functor of global sections.<br>FR: La cohomologie des faisceaux est un foncteur dérivé droit des sections globales.<br>中：层上同调是全局截面函子的右导出函子。          |
| `L_iF`                      | left derived functor, foncteur dérivé gauche, 左导出函子 | A functor measuring failure of right exact functors to be exact.                                                           | EN: Tor is a left derived functor of tensor product.<br>FR: Tor est un foncteur dérivé gauche du produit tensoriel.<br>中：Tor 是张量积的左导出函子。                                                     |
| `\operatorname{Ext}^i(A,B)` | Ext group, groupe Ext, Ext 群                        | A derived Hom group measuring extensions and higher morphism data.                                                         | EN: `\operatorname{Ext}^1` classifies many short exact extensions.<br>FR: `\operatorname{Ext}^1` classifie beaucoup d’extensions exactes courtes.<br>中：`\operatorname{Ext}^1` 分类许多短正合扩张。     |
| `\operatorname{Tor}_i(A,B)` | Tor group, groupe Tor, Tor 群                        | A derived tensor group measuring non-flatness and hidden relations.                                                        | EN: Tor detects when tensoring destroys exactness.<br>FR: Tor détecte quand le produit tensoriel détruit l’exactitude.<br>中：Tor 检测张量积何时破坏正合性。                                                |
| —                           | injective object, objet injectif, 内射对象              | An object used to build resolutions for right derived functors.                                                            | EN: Injective resolutions compute sheaf cohomology.<br>FR: Les résolutions injectives calculent la cohomologie des faisceaux.<br>中：内射分解用于计算层上同调。                                             |
| —                           | projective object, objet projectif, 投射对象            | An object used to build resolutions for left derived functors.                                                             | EN: Projective resolutions compute Tor and Ext in module categories.<br>FR: Les résolutions projectives calculent Tor et Ext dans les catégories de modules.<br>中：投射分解在模范畴中计算 Tor 和 Ext。     |

## 37.9 Derived, triangulated, and stable categories

| Symbol (LaTeX)        | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                           | Definition                                                                              | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| --------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `C^\bullet`           | chain complex, complexe de chaînes, 链复形                                     | A sequence of objects and differentials whose consecutive composition is zero.          | EN: Complexes organize algebraic data across degrees.<br>FR: Les complexes organisent les données algébriques par degrés.<br>中：复形按次数组织代数数据。                                                                             |
| `d^2=0`               | differential condition, condition différentielle, 微分条件                      | The requirement that applying two consecutive differentials gives zero.                 | EN: The condition `d^2=0` makes homology well-defined.<br>FR: La condition `d^2=0` rend l’homologie bien définie.<br>中：条件 `d^2=0` 使同调良定义。                                                                               |
| `H^n(C^\bullet)`      | cohomology of a complex, cohomologie d’un complexe, 复形上同调                   | The quotient of closed degree-`n` elements by exact degree-`n` elements.                | EN: Cohomology measures failure of exactness.<br>FR: La cohomologie mesure l’échec de l’exactitude.<br>中：上同调度量正合性的失败。                                                                                                   |
| `K(\mathcal{A})`      | homotopy category of complexes, catégorie homotopique des complexes, 复形同伦范畴 | The category of complexes modulo chain homotopy.                                        | EN: Chain-homotopic maps become equal in `K(\mathcal{A})`.<br>FR: Les applications homotopes de complexes deviennent égales dans `K(\mathcal{A})`.<br>中：链同伦映射在 `K(\mathcal{A})` 中变为相等。                                  |
| `D(\mathcal{A})`      | derived category, catégorie dérivée, 导出范畴                                   | A category obtained from complexes by formally inverting quasi-isomorphisms.            | EN: Derived categories retain homological information invisible to ordinary categories.<br>FR: Les catégories dérivées gardent l’information homologique invisible aux catégories ordinaires.<br>中：导出范畴保留普通范畴中不可见的同调信息。 |
| —                     | quasi-isomorphism, quasi-isomorphisme, 拟同构                                  | A morphism of complexes inducing isomorphisms on all cohomology objects.                | EN: Derived categories treat quasi-isomorphic complexes as equivalent.<br>FR: Les catégories dérivées traitent les complexes quasi-isomorphes comme équivalents.<br>中：导出范畴把拟同构的复形视为等价。                                  |
| `D^b(\mathcal{A})`    | bounded derived category, catégorie dérivée bornée, 有界导出范畴                  | The derived category of complexes with nonzero terms only in finitely many degrees.     | EN: Coherent sheaves often appear inside bounded derived categories.<br>FR: Les faisceaux cohérents apparaissent souvent dans les catégories dérivées bornées.<br>中：凝聚层常出现在有界导出范畴中。                                     |
| `X[1]`                | shift functor, foncteur de décalage, 平移函子                                   | A functor shifting degrees in a complex.                                                | EN: Triangulated categories use shifts to encode homological suspension.<br>FR: Les catégories triangulées utilisent les décalages pour encoder la suspension homologique.<br>中：三角范畴用平移函子编码同调悬挂。                        |
| `X\to Y\to Z\to X[1]` | distinguished triangle, triangle distingué, 区分三角                            | The basic exactness-like structure in a triangulated category.                          | EN: Distinguished triangles replace short exact sequences in derived settings.<br>FR: Les triangles distingués remplacent les suites exactes courtes dans les cadres dérivés.<br>中：在导出语境中，区分三角替代短正合列。                   |
| —                     | triangulated category, catégorie triangulée, 三角范畴                           | A category equipped with a shift functor and distinguished triangles satisfying axioms. | EN: Derived categories are standard examples of triangulated categories.<br>FR: Les catégories dérivées sont des exemples standards de catégories triangulées.<br>中：导出范畴是三角范畴的标准例子。                                     |
| —                     | stable category, catégorie stable, 稳定范畴                                     | A category where suspension is invertible and fiber and cofiber sequences align.        | EN: Stable homotopy theory is organized by stable categories.<br>FR: La théorie homotopique stable est organisée par des catégories stables.<br>中：稳定同伦论由稳定范畴组织。                                                         |
| —                     | t-structure, t-structure, t-结构                                              | A structure on a triangulated or stable category allowing recovery of an abelian heart. | EN: t-structures connect derived categories back to abelian categories.<br>FR: Les t-structures relient les catégories dérivées aux catégories abéliennes.<br>中：t-结构把导出范畴连接回 Abel 范畴。                                   |

## 37.10 Toposes, sheaves, and categorical logic

| Symbol (LaTeX)   | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                 | Definition                                                                                     | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| ---------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `\mathbf{Sh}(X)` | category of sheaves, catégorie des faisceaux, 层范畴                 | The category of sheaves on a topological space or site.                                        | EN: `\mathbf{Sh}(X)` encodes local-to-global structure on `X`.<br>FR: `\mathbf{Sh}(X)` encode la structure locale-globale sur `X`.<br>中：`\mathbf{Sh}(X)` 编码 `X` 上的局部到整体结构。                                |
| —                | Grothendieck topology, topologie de Grothendieck, Grothendieck 拓扑 | A categorical notion of covering families generalizing open covers.                            | EN: Étale covers define a Grothendieck topology on schemes.<br>FR: Les recouvrements étales définissent une topologie de Grothendieck sur les schémas.<br>中：étale 覆盖在概形上定义 Grothendieck 拓扑。               |
| —                | site, site, 景                                                     | A category equipped with a Grothendieck topology.                                              | EN: Sheaves can be defined on sites, not only topological spaces.<br>FR: Les faisceaux peuvent être définis sur des sites, pas seulement sur des espaces topologiques.<br>中：层可以定义在景上，而不只定义在拓扑空间上。         |
| —                | sheaf, faisceau, 层                                                | A functor-like object satisfying locality and gluing conditions with respect to covers.        | EN: Sheaves formalize data that can be checked and glued locally.<br>FR: Les faisceaux formalisent des données vérifiables et recollables localement.<br>中：层形式化可局部检查并粘合的数据。                               |
| —                | stack, champ, 叠                                                   | A higher sheaf-like object keeping track of objects, isomorphisms, and descent data.           | EN: Moduli problems with automorphisms are often represented by stacks.<br>FR: Les problèmes de modules avec automorphismes sont souvent représentés par des champs.<br>中：带自同构的模问题常由叠表示。                  |
| —                | descent, descente, 下降                                             | The process of reconstructing global objects from local objects and compatibility data.        | EN: Descent says when locally defined objects glue globally.<br>FR: La descente dit quand des objets définis localement se recollent globalement.<br>中：下降说明局部定义对象何时能粘合成整体对象。                              |
| —                | topos, topos, Topos                                               | A category behaving like a generalized universe of sets, often supporting internal logic.      | EN: A sheaf topos generalizes both topology and logic.<br>FR: Un topos de faisceaux généralise à la fois topologie et logique.<br>中：层 Topos 同时推广拓扑与逻辑。                                                    |
| `\Omega`         | subobject classifier, classificateur de sous-objets, 子对象分类器       | An object classifying subobjects in an elementary topos.                                       | EN: In sets, the subobject classifier is the two-element truth-value set.<br>FR: Dans les ensembles, le classificateur de sous-objets est l’ensemble des deux valeurs de vérité.<br>中：在集合中，子对象分类器是二元真值集合。 |
| —                | internal logic, logique interne, 内部逻辑                             | The logic naturally interpreted inside a category or topos.                                    | EN: The internal logic of a topos need not be classical.<br>FR: La logique interne d’un topos n’est pas nécessairement classique.<br>中：Topos 的内部逻辑不一定是经典逻辑。                                               |
| —                | geometric morphism, morphisme géométrique, 几何态射                   | A structure-preserving morphism between toposes, usually given by an adjoint pair of functors. | EN: Continuous maps induce geometric morphisms between sheaf toposes.<br>FR: Les applications continues induisent des morphismes géométriques entre topos de faisceaux.<br>中：连续映射诱导层 Topos 之间的几何态射。       |
| —                | classifying topos, topos classifiant, 分类 Topos                    | A topos representing models of a logical theory.                                               | EN: Classifying toposes connect logic, geometry, and category theory.<br>FR: Les topos classifiants relient logique, géométrie et théorie des catégories.<br>中：分类 Topos 连接逻辑、几何与范畴论。                      |
| —                | elementary topos, topos élémentaire, 初等 Topos                     | A category with finite limits, exponentials, and a subobject classifier.                       | EN: Elementary toposes abstract many set-like operations.<br>FR: Les topos élémentaires abstraient beaucoup d’opérations de type ensemble.<br>中：初等 Topos 抽象许多类似集合的操作。                                     |

## 37.11 Higher categories, ∞-categories, and homotopical structures

| Symbol (LaTeX)          | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                     | Definition                                                                                                 | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
| ----------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| —                       | higher category, catégorie supérieure, 高阶范畴                           | A categorical structure with morphisms between morphisms, and possibly higher morphisms continuing upward. | EN: Higher categories record transformations between transformations.<br>FR: Les catégories supérieures enregistrent des transformations entre transformations.<br>中：高阶范畴记录变换之间的变换。                                   |
| `2\text{-}\mathcal{C}`  | 2-category, 2-catégorie, 2-范畴                                         | A category-like structure with objects, 1-morphisms, and 2-morphisms.                                      | EN: Categories, functors, and natural transformations form a 2-category.<br>FR: Les catégories, foncteurs et transformations naturelles forment une 2-catégorie.<br>中：范畴、函子和自然变换构成一个 2-范畴。                            |
| `\alpha:f\Rightarrow g` | 2-morphism, 2-morphisme, 2-态射                                         | A morphism between morphisms in a higher category.                                                         | EN: A natural transformation is a 2-morphism between functors.<br>FR: Une transformation naturelle est un 2-morphisme entre foncteurs.<br>中：自然变换是函子之间的 2-态射。                                                          |
| —                       | bicategory, bicatégorie, 双范畴                                          | A weak 2-category where associativity and unit laws hold up to coherent isomorphism.                       | EN: Bicategories allow composition to be associative only up to specified equivalence.<br>FR: Les bicatégories permettent une associativité seulement à équivalence spécifiée près.<br>中：双范畴允许复合只在指定等价意义下结合。          |
| —                       | ∞-category, ∞-catégorie, ∞-范畴                                         | A higher category with morphisms at all levels, usually weakly associative in a homotopical sense.         | EN: ∞-categories organize spaces, spectra, and derived geometry.<br>FR: Les ∞-catégories organisent espaces, spectres et géométrie dérivée.<br>中：∞-范畴组织空间、谱和导出几何。                                                     |
| —                       | quasi-category, quasi-catégorie, 拟范畴                                  | A model of ∞-categories using simplicial sets satisfying inner horn-filling conditions.                    | EN: Quasi-categories are widely used in modern higher category theory.<br>FR: Les quasi-catégories sont largement utilisées en théorie moderne des catégories supérieures.<br>中：拟范畴广泛用于现代高阶范畴论。                       |
| `\Delta^n`              | n-simplex, n-simplexe, n-单形                                           | A basic combinatorial simplex used in simplicial sets and higher category models.                          | EN: Simplices encode composable chains and higher coherence data.<br>FR: Les simplexes encodent chaînes composables et données de cohérence supérieure.<br>中：单形编码可复合链和高阶相干数据。                                         |
| —                       | simplicial set, ensemble simplicial, 单纯集                              | A combinatorial object made of simplices in all dimensions with face and degeneracy maps.                  | EN: Simplicial sets model spaces and ∞-categories.<br>FR: Les ensembles simpliciaux modélisent espaces et ∞-catégories.<br>中：单纯集可建模空间和 ∞-范畴。                                                                          |
| —                       | homotopy coherent diagram, diagramme homotopiquement cohérent, 同伦相干图式 | A diagram commuting up to specified higher homotopies rather than strictly.                                | EN: Homotopy coherence replaces strict equality by structured higher compatibility.<br>FR: La cohérence homotopique remplace l’égalité stricte par une compatibilité supérieure structurée.<br>中：同伦相干用结构化高阶相容性替代严格相等。 |
| —                       | Kan complex, complexe de Kan, Kan 复形                                  | A simplicial set modeling an ∞-groupoid or homotopy type.                                                  | EN: Spaces are often represented by Kan complexes in homotopy theory.<br>FR: Les espaces sont souvent représentés par des complexes de Kan en théorie de l’homotopie.<br>中：同伦论中常用 Kan 复形表示空间。                         |
| —                       | ∞-groupoid, ∞-groupoïde, ∞-群胚                                         | An ∞-category in which all morphisms are invertible up to higher equivalence.                              | EN: Homotopy types can be viewed as ∞-groupoids.<br>FR: Les types d’homotopie peuvent être vus comme des ∞-groupoïdes.<br>中：同伦型可视为 ∞-群胚。                                                                              |
| —                       | model category, catégorie de modèles, 模型范畴                            | A category equipped with weak equivalences, fibrations, and cofibrations for doing homotopy theory.        | EN: Model categories provide a formal setting for homotopical algebra.<br>FR: Les catégories de modèles fournissent un cadre formel pour l’algèbre homotopique.<br>中：模型范畴为同伦代数提供形式框架。                                 |

## 37.12 Operads, higher algebra, and categorical foundations of modern mathematics

| Symbol (LaTeX)   | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                           | Definition                                                                                                                                | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| ---------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| —                | operad, opérade, 操作子                                                        | A structure encoding operations with multiple inputs and one output, together with rules for composing them.                              | EN: Operads organize algebraic operations with many inputs.<br>FR: Les opérades organisent des opérations algébriques à plusieurs entrées.<br>中：操作子组织多输入的代数操作。                                                          |
| `\mathcal{O}(n)` | n-ary operations of an operad, opérations n-aires d’une opérade, 操作子的 n 元操作 | The component of an operad parameterizing operations with `n` inputs.                                                                     | EN: `\mathcal{O}(2)` contains binary operations.<br>FR: `\mathcal{O}(2)` contient les opérations binaires.<br>中：`\mathcal{O}(2)` 包含二元操作。                                                                                |
| —                | algebra over an operad, algèbre sur une opérade, 操作子上的代数                    | An object equipped with operations governed by a given operad.                                                                            | EN: Associative algebras are algebras over the associative operad.<br>FR: Les algèbres associatives sont des algèbres sur l’opérade associative.<br>中：结合代数是结合操作子上的代数。                                                   |
| `E_n`            | En-operad, opérade En, En 操作子                                               | An operad encoding multiplication commutative up to controlled higher homotopies depending on `n`.                                        | EN: `E_1` models associative multiplication up to homotopy.<br>FR: `E_1` modélise la multiplication associative à homotopie près.<br>中：`E_1` 建模同伦意义下的结合乘法。                                                              |
| `E_\infty`       | E-infinity operad, opérade E-infini, E∞ 操作子                                 | An operad encoding homotopy-coherent commutative multiplication.                                                                          | EN: `E_\infty`-algebras are commutative up to all higher coherences.<br>FR: Les algèbres `E_\infty` sont commutatives à toutes cohérences supérieures près.<br>中：`E_\infty` 代数在所有高阶相干意义下交换。                             |
| —                | higher algebra, algèbre supérieure, 高阶代数                                    | Algebra carried out in higher categorical or homotopical settings.                                                                        | EN: Higher algebra studies rings, modules, and categories with homotopical structure.<br>FR: L’algèbre supérieure étudie anneaux, modules et catégories avec structure homotopique.<br>中：高阶代数研究带同伦结构的环、模和范畴。            |
| —                | ∞-operad, ∞-opérade, ∞-操作子                                                  | A higher-categorical version of an operad suitable for homotopy-coherent operations.                                                      | EN: ∞-operads are used to define monoidal ∞-categories.<br>FR: Les ∞-opérades servent à définir les ∞-catégories monoïdales.<br>中：∞-操作子用于定义幺半 ∞-范畴。                                                                     |
| —                | factorization algebra, algèbre de factorisation, 因子化代数                      | A structure assigning algebraic data to open sets with multiplication controlled by disjointness and locality.                            | EN: Factorization algebras model observables in quantum field theory.<br>FR: Les algèbres de factorisation modélisent les observables en théorie quantique des champs.<br>中：因子化代数建模量子场论中的可观测量。                          |
| —                | higher topos, topos supérieur, 高阶 Topos                                     | An ∞-categorical generalization of a topos.                                                                                               | EN: Higher toposes combine sheaf theory, homotopy theory, and logic.<br>FR: Les topos supérieurs combinent théorie des faisceaux, homotopie et logique.<br>中：高阶 Topos 结合层论、同伦论和逻辑。                                      |
| —                | univalent foundations, fondements univalents, 单价基础                          | A foundation of mathematics based on type theory and the principle that equivalent objects may be identified.                             | EN: Univalent foundations connect logic with homotopy theory.<br>FR: Les fondements univalents relient logique et théorie de l’homotopie.<br>中：单价基础把逻辑与同伦论联系起来。                                                         |
| —                | homotopy type theory, théorie des types homotopique, 同伦类型论                  | A type-theoretic foundation interpreting types as spaces and equalities as paths.                                                         | EN: Homotopy type theory gives computational language for homotopical mathematics.<br>FR: La théorie des types homotopique donne un langage computationnel pour les mathématiques homotopiques.<br>中：同伦类型论为同伦数学提供计算性语言。 |
| —                | categorification, catégorification, 范畴化                                     | The process of replacing set-level or numerical structures by categorical structures whose decategorification recovers the original data. | EN: Categorification turns equations into isomorphisms and numbers into objects.<br>FR: La catégorification transforme les équations en isomorphismes et les nombres en objets.<br>中：范畴化把等式提升为同构，把数提升为对象。               |
| —                | decategorification, décatégorification, 去范畴化                                | The process of extracting ordinary invariants from categorical structures.                                                                | EN: Taking a Grothendieck group is a common decategorification.<br>FR: Prendre un groupe de Grothendieck est une décatégorification courante.<br>中：取 Grothendieck 群是常见的去范畴化操作。                                          |
# 38. Logic, Model Theory, Set Theory, and Foundations

## 38.1 Formal languages, formulas, and logical syntax

| Symbol (LaTeX)      | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                           | Definition                                                                               | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
| ------------------- | ------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `\mathcal{L}`       | formal language, langage formel, 形式语言       | A specified collection of symbols and formation rules used to build formulas.            | EN: First-order arithmetic is written in a formal language with symbols for `0`, successor, addition, and multiplication.<br>FR: L’arithmétique du premier ordre s’écrit dans un langage formel avec `0`, successeur, addition et multiplication.<br>中：一阶算术使用含 `0`、后继、加法和乘法符号的形式语言。 |
| `\varphi`           | formula, formule, 公式                        | A well-formed expression of a formal language.                                           | EN: `\varphi(x)` may express that `x` is prime.<br>FR: `\varphi(x)` peut exprimer que `x` est premier.<br>中：`\varphi(x)` 可表达 `x` 是素数。                                                                                                                                               |
| `P(x)`              | predicate, prédicat, 谓词                     | A symbol or formula expressing a property of objects.                                    | EN: `P(x)` can mean “`x` is even.”<br>FR: `P(x)` peut signifier « `x` est pair ».<br>中：`P(x)` 可表示“`x` 是偶数”。                                                                                                                                                                         |
| `R(x,y)`            | relation symbol, symbole de relation, 关系符号  | A symbol expressing a relation among several variables or objects.                       | EN: `<` is a binary relation symbol in ordered structures.<br>FR: `<` est un symbole de relation binaire dans les structures ordonnées.<br>中：`<` 是有序结构中的二元关系符号。                                                                                                                     |
| `f(x)`              | function symbol, symbole de fonction, 函数符号  | A symbol representing an operation in a formal language.                                 | EN: Addition is a binary function symbol in arithmetic.<br>FR: L’addition est un symbole de fonction binaire en arithmétique.<br>中：加法是算术中的二元函数符号。                                                                                                                                   |
| `c`                 | constant symbol, symbole de constante, 常元符号 | A symbol naming a distinguished object in a structure.                                   | EN: The symbol `0` is a constant symbol in arithmetic.<br>FR: Le symbole `0` est une constante en arithmétique.<br>中：符号 `0` 是算术中的常元符号。                                                                                                                                              |
| `x,y,z`             | variable, variable, 变元                      | A symbol that may stand for an arbitrary object in a domain.                             | EN: In `x+y=y+x`, `x` and `y` are variables.<br>FR: Dans `x+y=y+x`, `x` et `y` sont des variables.<br>中：在 `x+y=y+x` 中，`x` 与 `y` 是变元。                                                                                                                                                |
| `t`                 | term, terme, 项                              | An expression denoting an object, built from variables, constants, and function symbols. | EN: `x+1` is a term in the language of arithmetic.<br>FR: `x+1` est un terme dans le langage de l’arithmétique.<br>中：`x+1` 是算术语言中的项。                                                                                                                                                |
| `\neg\varphi`       | negation, négation, 否定                      | A logical operation expressing that a formula is not true.                               | EN: `\neg P(x)` means that `x` does not have property `P`.<br>FR: `\neg P(x)` signifie que `x` n’a pas la propriété `P`.<br>中：`\neg P(x)` 表示 `x` 不具有性质 `P`。                                                                                                                         |
| `\varphi\wedge\psi` | conjunction, conjonction, 合取                | A formula true when both component formulas are true.                                    | EN: `P(x)\wedge Q(x)` asserts both properties.<br>FR: `P(x)\wedge Q(x)` affirme les deux propriétés.<br>中：`P(x)\wedge Q(x)` 同时断言两个性质。                                                                                                                                               |
| `\varphi\vee\psi`   | disjunction, disjonction, 析取                | A formula true when at least one component formula is true.                              | EN: `P(x)\vee Q(x)` allows either condition.<br>FR: `P(x)\vee Q(x)` permet l’une ou l’autre condition.<br>中：`P(x)\vee Q(x)` 允许两个条件中至少一个成立。                                                                                                                                          |
| `\varphi\to\psi`    | implication, implication, 蕴含                | A formula expressing that `\psi` follows if `\varphi` holds.                             | EN: `P(x)\to Q(x)` says every `P`-case is a `Q`-case.<br>FR: `P(x)\to Q(x)` dit que tout cas `P` est un cas `Q`.<br>中：`P(x)\to Q(x)` 表示每个满足 `P` 的情形也满足 `Q`。                                                                                                                         |

## 38.2 Quantifiers, semantics, and satisfaction

| Symbol (LaTeX)              | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                      | Definition                                                                         | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| --------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `\forall x\,\varphi(x)`     | universal quantifier, quantificateur universel, 全称量词                   | A quantifier asserting that a formula holds for every object in the domain.        | EN: `\forall x\, x=x` states reflexivity of equality.<br>FR: `\forall x\, x=x` affirme la réflexivité de l’égalité.<br>中：`\forall x\, x=x` 断言等号的自反性。                                                                    |
| `\exists x\,\varphi(x)`     | existential quantifier, quantificateur existentiel, 存在量词               | A quantifier asserting that a formula holds for at least one object in the domain. | EN: `\exists x\, P(x)` says that something has property `P`.<br>FR: `\exists x\, P(x)` dit qu’un objet possède la propriété `P`.<br>中：`\exists x\, P(x)` 表示存在某个对象具有性质 `P`。                                              |
| `\exists!x\,\varphi(x)`     | unique existence quantifier, quantificateur d’existence unique, 唯一存在量词 | A quantifier asserting that exactly one object satisfies a formula.                | EN: `\exists!x\,\varphi(x)` means there is one and only one such `x`.<br>FR: `\exists!x\,\varphi(x)` signifie qu’il existe un et un seul tel `x`.<br>中：`\exists!x\,\varphi(x)` 表示存在且仅存在一个这样的 `x`。                       |
| `x\in Fv(\varphi)`          | free variable, variable libre, 自由变元                                    | A variable occurring in a formula without being bound by a quantifier.             | EN: In `x>0`, the variable `x` is free.<br>FR: Dans `x>0`, la variable `x` est libre.<br>中：在 `x>0` 中，变元 `x` 是自由变元。                                                                                                      |
| `\forall x` binds `x`       | bound variable, variable liée, 约束变元                                    | A variable whose occurrence is governed by a quantifier.                           | EN: In `\forall x\,P(x)`, the variable `x` is bound.<br>FR: Dans `\forall x\,P(x)`, la variable `x` est liée.<br>中：在 `\forall x\,P(x)` 中，变元 `x` 被约束。                                                                    |
| `\mathcal{M}`               | structure, structure, 结构                                               | A domain together with interpretations of the symbols of a formal language.        | EN: The natural numbers with addition and multiplication form an arithmetic structure.<br>FR: Les entiers naturels avec addition et multiplication forment une structure arithmétique.<br>中：自然数连同加法和乘法构成一个算术结构。         |
| `M`                         | domain of a structure, domaine d’une structure, 结构的论域                  | The underlying set of objects over which quantifiers range.                        | EN: In the usual arithmetic structure, the domain is `\mathbb{N}`.<br>FR: Dans la structure arithmétique usuelle, le domaine est `\mathbb{N}`.<br>中：在通常算术结构中，论域是 `\mathbb{N}`。                                          |
| `\mathcal{M}\models\varphi` | satisfaction, satisfaction, 满足                                         | The relation saying that a formula is true in a structure.                         | EN: `\mathbb{N}\models 0<1` says that `0<1` is true in the natural numbers.<br>FR: `\mathbb{N}\models 0<1` dit que `0<1` est vrai dans les entiers naturels.<br>中：`\mathbb{N}\models 0<1` 表示 `0<1` 在自然数结构中为真。           |
| `\mathcal{M}\models T`      | model of a theory, modèle d’une théorie, 理论的模型                         | A structure satisfying every sentence in a theory.                                 | EN: A group is a model of the group axioms.<br>FR: Un groupe est un modèle des axiomes de groupe.<br>中：群是群公理的一个模型。                                                                                                      |
| `T\models\varphi`           | semantic consequence, conséquence sémantique, 语义后承                     | Every model of theory `T` satisfies formula or sentence `\varphi`.                 | EN: If all fields satisfy a sentence, it is a semantic consequence of field axioms.<br>FR: Si tous les corps satisfont une phrase, elle est conséquence sémantique des axiomes de corps.<br>中：若所有域都满足某句子，则该句子是域公理的语义后承。 |
| `\models\varphi`            | validity, validité, 有效性                                                | A formula or sentence true in every relevant structure.                            | EN: Logical tautologies are valid in every structure.<br>FR: Les tautologies logiques sont valides dans toute structure.<br>中：逻辑重言式在所有结构中有效。                                                                            |
| `\varphi[a]`                | assignment, affectation, 赋值                                            | A choice of objects from a domain for the free variables of a formula.             | EN: Satisfaction of `x<3` depends on the assignment of `x`.<br>FR: La satisfaction de `x<3` dépend de l’affectation de `x`.<br>中：`x<3` 是否满足取决于对 `x` 的赋值。                                                                |

## 38.3 Proof systems, derivability, and metatheorems

| Symbol (LaTeX)            | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                       | Definition                                                                                              | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
| ------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `\Gamma`                  | set of assumptions, ensemble d’hypothèses, 假设集          | A collection of premises used in a derivation.                                                          | EN: From assumptions `\Gamma`, one tries to derive a conclusion.<br>FR: À partir des hypothèses `\Gamma`, on cherche à dériver une conclusion.<br>中：从假设集 `\Gamma` 出发，目标是推出结论。                                        |
| `\Gamma\vdash\varphi`     | syntactic derivability, dérivabilité syntaxique, 语法可推导性 | The relation saying that `\varphi` can be derived from assumptions `\Gamma` in a proof system.          | EN: `\Gamma\vdash\varphi` is about formal proof, not direct semantic truth.<br>FR: `\Gamma\vdash\varphi` concerne la preuve formelle, non la vérité sémantique directe.<br>中：`\Gamma\vdash\varphi` 涉及形式证明，而不是直接语义真值。 |
| —                         | axiom, axiome, 公理                                       | A formula accepted as a starting point in a formal system.                                              | EN: Group theory begins from the group axioms.<br>FR: La théorie des groupes commence par les axiomes de groupe.<br>中：群论从群公理出发。                                                                                      |
| —                         | inference rule, règle d’inférence, 推理规则                 | A rule allowing new formulas to be derived from old formulas.                                           | EN: Modus ponens derives `\psi` from `\varphi` and `\varphi\to\psi`.<br>FR: Le modus ponens dérive `\psi` de `\varphi` et `\varphi\to\psi`.<br>中：分离规则由 `\varphi` 和 `\varphi\to\psi` 推出 `\psi`。                       |
| —                         | formal proof, preuve formelle, 形式证明                     | A finite sequence or tree of formulas justified by axioms and inference rules.                          | EN: A formal proof can be checked mechanically.<br>FR: Une preuve formelle peut être vérifiée mécaniquement.<br>中：形式证明可以被机械检查。                                                                                       |
| `\Gamma\Rightarrow\Delta` | sequent, séquent, 相继式                                   | A formal expression representing that conclusions `\Delta` follow from assumptions `\Gamma`.            | EN: Sequent calculus manipulates sequents by structural and logical rules.<br>FR: Le calcul des séquents manipule des séquents par règles structurelles et logiques.<br>中：相继式演算通过结构规则和逻辑规则操作相继式。                     |
| —                         | natural deduction, déduction naturelle, 自然演绎            | A proof system organized around introduction and elimination rules for logical connectives.             | EN: Natural deduction mirrors ordinary mathematical reasoning.<br>FR: La déduction naturelle reflète le raisonnement mathématique ordinaire.<br>中：自然演绎贴近普通数学推理。                                                      |
| —                         | sequent calculus, calcul des séquents, 相继式演算            | A proof system based on sequents and rule-based transformations.                                        | EN: Sequent calculus is useful for proving cut elimination.<br>FR: Le calcul des séquents est utile pour prouver l’élimination des coupures.<br>中：相继式演算常用于证明切消定理。                                                    |
| —                         | soundness theorem, théorème de correction, 可靠性定理        | A theorem stating that syntactic derivability implies semantic consequence.                             | EN: Soundness says formal proofs do not prove semantically false conclusions.<br>FR: La correction dit que les preuves formelles ne prouvent pas de conclusions sémantiquement fausses.<br>中：可靠性表示形式证明不会推出语义上假的结论。   |
| —                         | completeness theorem, théorème de complétude, 完备性定理     | A theorem stating that semantic consequence implies syntactic derivability for a proof system.          | EN: First-order logic is complete in Gödel’s semantic sense.<br>FR: La logique du premier ordre est complète au sens sémantique de Gödel.<br>中：一阶逻辑在 Gödel 语义意义下是完备的。                                                |
| —                         | consistency, cohérence, 一致性                             | A theory is consistent if it does not derive a contradiction.                                           | EN: A consistent theory cannot prove both `\varphi` and `\neg\varphi`.<br>FR: Une théorie cohérente ne peut pas prouver à la fois `\varphi` et `\neg\varphi`.<br>中：一致理论不能同时证明 `\varphi` 与 `\neg\varphi`。             |
| —                         | cut elimination, élimination des coupures, 切消           | A proof-theoretic result showing that certain intermediate lemmas can be eliminated from formal proofs. | EN: Cut elimination yields structural information about proofs.<br>FR: L’élimination des coupures donne des informations structurelles sur les preuves.<br>中：切消给出关于证明结构的信息。                                          |

## 38.4 First-order theories, compactness, and Löwenheim-Skolem phenomena

| Symbol (LaTeX)                       | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                                                  | Definition                                                                                            | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |
| ------------------------------------ | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `T`                                  | theory, théorie, 理论                                                                                | A set of sentences in a formal language, usually taken as axioms and consequences.                    | EN: The theory of groups consists of sentences true in all groups.<br>FR: La théorie des groupes contient les phrases vraies dans tous les groupes.<br>中：群理论由所有群中为真的句子组成。                                                                                                                      |
| —                                    | sentence, phrase, 句子                                                                               | A formula with no free variables.                                                                     | EN: `\forall x\,x=x` is a sentence.<br>FR: `\forall x\,x=x` est une phrase.<br>中：`\forall x\,x=x` 是一个句子。                                                                                                                                                                                       |
| —                                    | complete theory, théorie complète, 完全理论                                                            | A consistent theory deciding every sentence in its language.                                          | EN: A complete theory proves or refutes each sentence.<br>FR: Une théorie complète prouve ou réfute chaque phrase.<br>中：完全理论对每个句子都能证明或否定。                                                                                                                                                      |
| —                                    | incomplete theory, théorie incomplète, 不完全理论                                                       | A theory leaving at least one sentence undecided.                                                     | EN: Peano arithmetic is incomplete if consistent.<br>FR: L’arithmétique de Peano est incomplète si elle est cohérente.<br>中：若 Peano 算术一致，则它是不完全的。                                                                                                                                              |
| —                                    | axiomatization, axiomatisation, 公理化                                                                | A presentation of a class of structures or theory by axioms.                                          | EN: Fields are axiomatized by ring axioms plus inverse axioms for nonzero elements.<br>FR: Les corps sont axiomatisés par les axiomes d’anneau plus les axiomes d’inverse pour les éléments non nuls.<br>中：域由环公理加非零元素可逆公理来公理化。                                                                 |
| —                                    | finite axiomatizability, axiomatisabilité finie, 有限公理化                                             | The property that a theory or class can be described by finitely many axioms.                         | EN: Some algebraic structures admit finite axiomatizations.<br>FR: Certaines structures algébriques admettent des axiomatisations finies.<br>中：某些代数结构可以被有限公理化。                                                                                                                                 |
| —                                    | compactness theorem, théorème de compacité, 紧致性定理                                                  | A theorem stating that a first-order theory has a model if every finite subset has a model.           | EN: Compactness produces nonstandard models of arithmetic.<br>FR: La compacité produit des modèles non standards de l’arithmétique.<br>中：紧致性可产生算术的非标准模型。                                                                                                                                       |
| —                                    | upward Löwenheim-Skolem theorem, théorème de Löwenheim-Skolem ascendant, 向上 Löwenheim-Skolem 定理    | A theorem giving larger models for theories with infinite models under suitable cardinal assumptions. | EN: If a first-order theory has an infinite model, it often has models of larger cardinalities.<br>FR: Si une théorie du premier ordre a un modèle infini, elle a souvent des modèles de cardinalités plus grandes.<br>中：若一阶理论有无限模型，通常也有更大基数的模型。                                               |
| —                                    | downward Löwenheim-Skolem theorem, théorème de Löwenheim-Skolem descendant, 向下 Löwenheim-Skolem 定理 | A theorem giving smaller elementary submodels of infinite structures.                                 | EN: Large structures in countable languages may have countable elementary submodels.<br>FR: De grandes structures en langage dénombrable peuvent avoir des sous-modèles élémentaires dénombrables.<br>中：可数语言中的大结构可能有可数初等等价子模型。                                                                 |
| —                                    | nonstandard model, modèle non standard, 非标准模型                                                      | A model of a theory not isomorphic to the intended or standard model.                                 | EN: Nonstandard models of arithmetic contain infinite integers.<br>FR: Les modèles non standards de l’arithmétique contiennent des entiers infinis.<br>中：算术的非标准模型包含无限整数。                                                                                                                       |
| —                                    | elementary submodel, sous-modèle élémentaire, 初等子模型                                                | A substructure satisfying the same first-order formulas with parameters from the substructure.        | EN: Elementary submodels preserve first-order truth about their elements.<br>FR: Les sous-modèles élémentaires préservent la vérité du premier ordre sur leurs éléments.<br>中：初等子模型保持关于其元素的一阶真值。                                                                                               |
| `\mathcal{M}\preccurlyeq\mathcal{N}` | elementary embedding or substructure notation, notation de sous-structure élémentaire, 初等子结构记号     | Notation indicating that `\mathcal{M}` is an elementary substructure of `\mathcal{N}`.                | EN: `\mathcal{M}\preccurlyeq\mathcal{N}` means first-order formulas agree on parameters from `M`.<br>FR: `\mathcal{M}\preccurlyeq\mathcal{N}` signifie que les formules du premier ordre coïncident sur les paramètres de `M`.<br>中：`\mathcal{M}\preccurlyeq\mathcal{N}` 表示带 `M` 中参数的一阶公式真值一致。 |

## 38.5 Model theory, types, ultraproducts, and stability

| Symbol (LaTeX)                   | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                             | Definition                                                                                                                | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
| -------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `\mathcal{M}\equiv\mathcal{N}`   | elementary equivalence, équivalence élémentaire, 初等等价         | Two structures satisfy the same first-order sentences.                                                                    | EN: Elementarily equivalent structures need not be isomorphic.<br>FR: Des structures élémentairement équivalentes ne sont pas nécessairement isomorphes.<br>中：初等等价的结构不一定同构。                                                                           |
| `\operatorname{Th}(\mathcal{M})` | theory of a structure, théorie d’une structure, 结构的理论         | The set of all sentences true in a structure.                                                                             | EN: `\operatorname{Th}(\mathbb{Q},<)` is the theory of dense linear order without endpoints.<br>FR: `\operatorname{Th}(\mathbb{Q},<)` est la théorie des ordres linéaires denses sans extrémités.<br>中：`\operatorname{Th}(\mathbb{Q},<)` 是无端点稠密线性序理论。 |
| `S_n(A)`                         | type space, espace des types, 型空间                             | The space of complete `n`-types over a parameter set `A`.                                                                 | EN: Type spaces organize possible first-order behaviors of tuples.<br>FR: Les espaces de types organisent les comportements du premier ordre possibles des tuples.<br>中：型空间组织元组可能具有的一阶行为。                                                             |
| `p(x)`                           | type, type, 型                                                 | A consistent set of formulas describing a possible element or tuple.                                                      | EN: A type records all formulas that an element could satisfy.<br>FR: Un type enregistre toutes les formules qu’un élément pourrait satisfaire.<br>中：型记录一个元素可能满足的全部公式。                                                                                |
| `a\models p`                     | realization of a type, réalisation d’un type, 型的实现            | An element or tuple satisfying every formula in a type.                                                                   | EN: A saturated model realizes many types over small parameter sets.<br>FR: Un modèle saturé réalise beaucoup de types sur de petits ensembles de paramètres.<br>中：饱和模型实现许多小参数集上的型。                                                                   |
| —                                | saturated model, modèle saturé, 饱和模型                          | A model realizing all types over sufficiently small parameter sets, according to a cardinal bound.                        | EN: Saturated models behave like large universal domains.<br>FR: Les modèles saturés se comportent comme de grands domaines universels.<br>中：饱和模型类似大型万有论域。                                                                                            |
| `\prod_i M_i/\mathcal{U}`        | ultraproduct, ultraproduit, 超积                                | A quotient of a product of structures by an ultrafilter, preserving first-order properties through Łoś’s theorem.         | EN: Ultraproducts construct nonstandard models from ordinary ones.<br>FR: Les ultraproduits construisent des modèles non standards à partir de modèles ordinaires.<br>中：超积可由普通模型构造非标准模型。                                                              |
| `\mathcal{U}`                    | ultrafilter, ultrafiltre, 超滤子                                 | A maximal filter on a set, used to decide which index sets count as large.                                                | EN: Nonprincipal ultrafilters are used in nonstandard analysis.<br>FR: Les ultrafiltres non principaux sont utilisés en analyse non standard.<br>中：非主超滤子用于非标准分析。                                                                                      |
| —                                | Łoś’s theorem, théorème de Łoś, Łoś 定理                        | A theorem stating that first-order truth in an ultraproduct is determined by truth on a large set of indices.             | EN: Łoś’s theorem is the fundamental theorem of ultraproducts.<br>FR: Le théorème de Łoś est le théorème fondamental des ultraproduits.<br>中：Łoś 定理是超积的基本定理。                                                                                          |
| —                                | quantifier elimination, élimination des quantificateurs, 量词消去 | A property that every formula is equivalent to a quantifier-free formula in a theory.                                     | EN: Algebraically closed fields admit quantifier elimination in the language of rings.<br>FR: Les corps algébriquement clos admettent l’élimination des quantificateurs dans le langage des anneaux.<br>中：代数闭域在环语言中有量词消去。                             |
| —                                | stable theory, théorie stable, 稳定理论                           | A theory with controlled behavior of types, avoiding certain order-like complexity.                                       | EN: Algebraically closed fields are central examples in stability theory.<br>FR: Les corps algébriquement clos sont des exemples centraux en théorie de la stabilité.<br>中：代数闭域是稳定理论中的核心例子。                                                           |
| —                                | o-minimality, o-minimalité, o-极小性                             | A tameness property for ordered structures where definable subsets of the line are finite unions of points and intervals. | EN: O-minimal structures support tame geometry over ordered fields.<br>FR: Les structures o-minimales soutiennent une géométrie modérée sur les corps ordonnés.<br>中：o-极小结构支持有序域上的温和几何。                                                               |

## 38.6 Set theory, membership, ordinals, and cardinals

| Symbol (LaTeX)             | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                             | Definition                                                                        | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |
| -------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `x\in A`                   | membership, appartenance, 属于                                  | The relation saying that `x` is an element of set `A`.                            | EN: `3\in\mathbb{N}` says that `3` is a natural number.<br>FR: `3\in\mathbb{N}` dit que `3` est un entier naturel.<br>中：`3\in\mathbb{N}` 表示 `3` 是自然数。                                                                                        |
| `A\subseteq B`             | subset, sous-ensemble, 子集                                     | Every element of `A` is also an element of `B`.                                   | EN: `\mathbb{N}\subseteq\mathbb{Z}`.<br>FR: `\mathbb{N}\subseteq\mathbb{Z}`.<br>中：`\mathbb{N}\subseteq\mathbb{Z}`。                                                                                                                           |
| `\varnothing`              | empty set, ensemble vide, 空集                                  | The set with no elements.                                                         | EN: The empty set is a subset of every set.<br>FR: L’ensemble vide est un sous-ensemble de tout ensemble.<br>中：空集是任意集合的子集。                                                                                                                   |
| `\{x\in A\mid\varphi(x)\}` | set comprehension notation, notation de compréhension, 集合描述记号 | Notation for the subset of `A` consisting of elements satisfying `\varphi`.       | EN: `\{n\in\mathbb{N}\mid n\text{ is even}\}` is the set of even natural numbers.<br>FR: `\{n\in\mathbb{N}\mid n\text{ est pair}\}` est l’ensemble des entiers naturels pairs.<br>中：`\{n\in\mathbb{N}\mid n\text{ 是偶数}\}` 是偶自然数集。            |
| `\mathcal{P}(A)`           | power set, ensemble des parties, 幂集                           | The set of all subsets of `A`.                                                    | EN: If `A=\{1,2\}`, then `\mathcal{P}(A)=\{\varnothing,\{1\},\{2\},\{1,2\}\}`.<br>FR: Si `A=\{1,2\}`, alors `\mathcal{P}(A)=\{\varnothing,\{1\},\{2\},\{1,2\}\}`.<br>中：若 `A=\{1,2\}`，则 `\mathcal{P}(A)=\{\varnothing,\{1\},\{2\},\{1,2\}\}`。 |
| `A\times B`                | Cartesian product, produit cartésien, 笛卡尔积                    | The set of ordered pairs with first component in `A` and second component in `B`. | EN: Coordinate planes are modeled by Cartesian products.<br>FR: Les plans de coordonnées sont modélisés par des produits cartésiens.<br>中：坐标平面可由笛卡尔积建模。                                                                                      |
| `\alpha,\beta`             | ordinal, ordinal, 序数                                          | A set-theoretic object representing order type of well-ordered sets.              | EN: Ordinals extend natural numbers to transfinite order types.<br>FR: Les ordinaux étendent les entiers naturels aux types d’ordre transfinis.<br>中：序数把自然数推广到超限序型。                                                                          |
| `\omega`                   | first infinite ordinal, premier ordinal infini, 第一个无限序数       | The ordinal corresponding to the order type of natural numbers.                   | EN: `\omega` represents the order type `0,1,2,\ldots`.<br>FR: `\omega` représente le type d’ordre `0,1,2,\ldots`.<br>中：`\omega` 表示 `0,1,2,\ldots` 的序型。                                                                                       |
| `\kappa`                   | cardinal, cardinal, 基数                                        | A set-theoretic object measuring size up to bijection.                            | EN: Countable infinite sets have cardinality `\aleph_0`.<br>FR: Les ensembles infinis dénombrables ont cardinalité `\aleph_0`.<br>中：可数无限集的基数是 `\aleph_0`。                                                                                    |
| `\lvert A\rvert`           | cardinality, cardinalité, 势 / 基数                              | The size of a set, measured by bijection class.                                   | EN: `\lvert\mathbb{N}\rvert=\lvert\mathbb{Z}\rvert`.<br>FR: `\lvert\mathbb{N}\rvert=\lvert\mathbb{Z}\rvert`.<br>中：`\lvert\mathbb{N}\rvert=\lvert\mathbb{Z}\rvert`。                                                                           |
| `\aleph_0`                 | countable infinity, infini dénombrable, 可数无穷                  | The cardinality of the natural numbers.                                           | EN: The integers and rationals are countably infinite.<br>FR: Les entiers et les rationnels sont dénombrablement infinis.<br>中：整数和有理数都是可数无穷的。                                                                                                |
| `2^{\aleph_0}`             | continuum cardinality, cardinal du continu, 连续统基数             | The cardinality of the real numbers.                                              | EN: The real line has cardinality `2^{\aleph_0}`.<br>FR: La droite réelle a cardinalité `2^{\aleph_0}`.<br>中：实数直线的基数是 `2^{\aleph_0}`。                                                                                                        |

## 38.7 ZF, ZFC, choice, and independence
