| Symbol (LaTeX) | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                                     | Definition                                                                                                           | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| -------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `ZF`           | Zermelo-Fraenkel set theory, théorie des ensembles de Zermelo-Fraenkel, ZF 集合论        | A standard axiomatic foundation for set theory without the axiom of choice.                                          | EN: `ZF` formalizes most ordinary set constructions.<br>FR: `ZF` formalise la plupart des constructions ordinaires d’ensembles.<br>中：`ZF` 形式化大多数普通集合构造。                                                   |
| `ZFC`          | Zermelo-Fraenkel set theory with choice, théorie Zermelo-Fraenkel avec choix, ZFC 集合论 | `ZF` set theory plus the axiom of choice.                                                                            | EN: Most mainstream mathematics is formalizable in `ZFC`.<br>FR: La plupart des mathématiques courantes sont formalisables dans `ZFC`.<br>中：主流数学大多可在 `ZFC` 中形式化。                                          |
| `AC`           | axiom of choice, axiome du choix, 选择公理                                                | An axiom stating that every family of nonempty sets has a choice function.                                           | EN: The axiom of choice implies that every vector space has a basis.<br>FR: L’axiome du choix implique que tout espace vectoriel possède une base.<br>中：选择公理推出每个向量空间都有基。                                  |
| —              | choice function, fonction de choix, 选择函数                                              | A function selecting one element from each set in a family of nonempty sets.                                         | EN: A choice function picks one representative from each nonempty set.<br>FR: Une fonction de choix prend un représentant dans chaque ensemble non vide.<br>中：选择函数从每个非空集合中选出一个代表元素。                       |
| `WO`           | well-ordering theorem, théorème du bon ordre, 良序定理                                    | The statement that every set can be well-ordered, equivalent to the axiom of choice over `ZF`.                       | EN: The well-ordering theorem implies that the real numbers can be well-ordered.<br>FR: Le théorème du bon ordre implique que les réels peuvent être bien ordonnés.<br>中：良序定理推出实数集可以被良序化。                 |
| `ZL`           | Zorn’s lemma, lemme de Zorn, Zorn 引理                                                  | A maximal principle equivalent to the axiom of choice over `ZF`.                                                     | EN: Zorn’s lemma is often used to prove existence of maximal ideals.<br>FR: Le lemme de Zorn sert souvent à prouver l’existence d’idéaux maximaux.<br>中：Zorn 引理常用于证明极大理想存在。                               |
| `CH`           | continuum hypothesis, hypothèse du continu, 连续统假设                                     | The statement that there is no cardinal strictly between `\aleph_0` and `2^{\aleph_0}`.                              | EN: `CH` is independent of `ZFC`, assuming `ZFC` is consistent.<br>FR: `CH` est indépendante de `ZFC`, si `ZFC` est cohérente.<br>中：若 `ZFC` 一致，则 `CH` 独立于 `ZFC`。                                          |
| `GCH`          | generalized continuum hypothesis, hypothèse généralisée du continu, 广义连续统假设           | The statement that `2^\kappa` is the next cardinal after `\kappa` for every infinite cardinal `\kappa`.              | EN: `GCH` extends `CH` to all infinite cardinalities.<br>FR: `GCH` étend `CH` à toutes les cardinalités infinies.<br>中：`GCH` 把 `CH` 推广到所有无限基数。                                                            |
| —              | independence result, résultat d’indépendance, 独立性结果                                   | A result showing that a statement can neither be proved nor refuted from a given axiom system, assuming consistency. | EN: The independence of `CH` changed the understanding of set-theoretic foundations.<br>FR: L’indépendance de `CH` a transformé la compréhension des fondements ensemblistes.<br>中：`CH` 的独立性改变了对集合论基础的理解。 |
| —              | forcing, forcing, 力迫                                                                  | A method for constructing models of set theory with controlled new sets.                                             | EN: Forcing is used to prove independence results.<br>FR: Le forcing sert à prouver des résultats d’indépendance.<br>中：力迫用于证明独立性结果。                                                                       |
| —              | constructible universe, univers constructible, 可构造宇宙                                  | Gödel’s inner model `L`, built by iterated definability.                                                             | EN: In `L`, the generalized continuum hypothesis holds.<br>FR: Dans `L`, l’hypothèse généralisée du continu est vraie.<br>中：在 `L` 中，广义连续统假设成立。                                                            |
| —              | large cardinal, grand cardinal, 大基数                                                   | A strong infinity axiom asserting the existence of cardinals with powerful closure or reflection properties.         | EN: Large cardinal axioms measure strength beyond ordinary `ZFC`.<br>FR: Les axiomes de grands cardinaux mesurent une force au-delà de `ZFC` ordinaire.<br>中：大基数公理度量超出普通 `ZFC` 的强度。                       |

## 38.8 Recursion, computability, and decidability

| Symbol (LaTeX) | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                       | Definition                                                                                  | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| -------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| —              | algorithm, algorithme, 算法                                               | A finite effective procedure for solving a class of problems.                               | EN: Euclid’s algorithm computes greatest common divisors.<br>FR: L’algorithme d’Euclide calcule les plus grands communs diviseurs.<br>中：Euclid 算法计算最大公约数。                                                                                     |
| `\varphi_e`    | partial computable function, fonction partiellement calculable, 部分可计算函数 | A function computed by a program or Turing machine that may not halt on every input.        | EN: Partial computable functions model algorithms that may diverge.<br>FR: Les fonctions partiellement calculables modélisent les algorithmes pouvant diverger.<br>中：部分可计算函数建模可能不停止的算法。                                                       |
| —              | total computable function, fonction totale calculable, 全可计算函数           | A computable function defined on every input.                                               | EN: Addition on natural numbers is total computable.<br>FR: L’addition sur les entiers naturels est totalement calculable.<br>中：自然数加法是全可计算函数。                                                                                                 |
| —              | recursive function, fonction récursive, 递归函数                            | A computable function in classical recursion theory.                                        | EN: Primitive recursive functions form a basic subclass of recursive functions.<br>FR: Les fonctions récursives primitives forment une sous-classe fondamentale des fonctions récursives.<br>中：原始递归函数是递归函数的基本子类。                              |
| `A\leq_T B`    | Turing reducibility, réductibilité de Turing, Turing 可归约性               | A relation saying that problem `A` can be solved using an oracle for problem `B`.           | EN: Turing reducibility compares computational difficulty.<br>FR: La réductibilité de Turing compare la difficulté computationnelle.<br>中：Turing 可归约性比较计算困难度。                                                                                 |
| `A\equiv_T B`  | Turing equivalence, équivalence de Turing, Turing 等价                    | Two problems are mutually Turing reducible.                                                 | EN: Turing-equivalent problems have the same oracle-computational power.<br>FR: Les problèmes Turing-équivalents ont la même puissance computationnelle avec oracle.<br>中：Turing 等价问题具有相同的 oracle 计算能力。                                       |
| —              | decidable set, ensemble décidable, 可判定集                                 | A set whose membership can be determined by an algorithm that always halts.                 | EN: The set of even natural numbers is decidable.<br>FR: L’ensemble des entiers naturels pairs est décidable.<br>中：偶自然数集合是可判定集。                                                                                                               |
| —              | recursively enumerable set, ensemble récursivement énumérable, 递归可枚举集   | A set whose members can be listed by an algorithm, possibly without deciding nonmembership. | EN: Theorems of a formal system form a recursively enumerable set under effective proof rules.<br>FR: Les théorèmes d’un système formel forment un ensemble récursivement énumérable sous règles effectives.<br>中：在有效证明规则下，一个形式系统的定理构成递归可枚举集。 |
| —              | halting problem, problème de l’arrêt, 停机问题                              | The problem of deciding whether an arbitrary program halts on a given input.                | EN: The halting problem is undecidable.<br>FR: Le problème de l’arrêt est indécidable.<br>中：停机问题不可判定。                                                                                                                                         |
| —              | decidability, décidabilité, 可判定性                                        | The property that a problem has an algorithmic decision procedure.                          | EN: Propositional validity is decidable.<br>FR: La validité propositionnelle est décidable.<br>中：命题逻辑有效性是可判定的。                                                                                                                                |
| —              | undecidability, indécidabilité, 不可判定性                                   | The property that no algorithm can decide all instances of a problem.                       | EN: First-order validity is undecidable in sufficiently expressive languages.<br>FR: La validité du premier ordre est indécidable dans des langages suffisamment expressifs.<br>中：在足够表达性的语言中，一阶有效性不可判定。                                       |
| —              | Church-Turing thesis, thèse de Church-Turing, Church-Turing 论题          | The thesis that effectively computable functions are exactly Turing-computable functions.   | EN: The Church-Turing thesis is a conceptual thesis, not a formal theorem.<br>FR: La thèse de Church-Turing est une thèse conceptuelle, non un théorème formel.<br>中：Church-Turing 论题是概念性论题，不是形式定理。                                           |

## 38.9 Gödel incompleteness, arithmetization, and formal limits

| Symbol (LaTeX)              | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                       | Definition                                                                                             | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |
| --------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `PA`                        | Peano arithmetic, arithmétique de Peano, Peano 算术                       | A first-order formal theory of natural numbers with induction.                                         | EN: `PA` formalizes much elementary number theory.<br>FR: `PA` formalise une grande partie de la théorie élémentaire des nombres.<br>中：`PA` 形式化了大量初等数论。                                                                                        |
| `Q`                         | Robinson arithmetic, arithmétique de Robinson, Robinson 算术              | A weak finitely axiomatized arithmetic theory sufficient for many incompleteness arguments.            | EN: Robinson arithmetic is much weaker than `PA` but still expressive.<br>FR: L’arithmétique de Robinson est beaucoup plus faible que `PA` mais reste expressive.<br>中：Robinson 算术远弱于 `PA`，但仍有相当表达力。                                           |
| `\ulcorner\varphi\urcorner` | Gödel number, nombre de Gödel, Gödel 编码数                                | A natural number encoding a formula, proof, or syntactic object.                                       | EN: Gödel numbering lets arithmetic talk about syntax.<br>FR: Le codage de Gödel permet à l’arithmétique de parler de syntaxe.<br>中：Gödel 编码使算术能够谈论语法对象。                                                                                       |
| —                           | arithmetization of syntax, arithmétisation de la syntaxe, 语法算术化         | Encoding formal syntax and proof relations inside arithmetic.                                          | EN: Arithmetization turns statements about proofs into statements about numbers.<br>FR: L’arithmétisation transforme les énoncés sur les preuves en énoncés sur les nombres.<br>中：语法算术化把关于证明的陈述转化为关于数的陈述。                                      |
| `\operatorname{Prov}_T(x)`  | provability predicate, prédicat de prouvabilité, 可证性谓词                  | A formula expressing that `x` codes a proof or provable sentence in theory `T`.                        | EN: Provability predicates formalize “there exists a proof in `T`.”<br>FR: Les prédicats de prouvabilité formalisent « il existe une preuve dans `T` ».<br>中：可证性谓词形式化“在 `T` 中存在一个证明”。                                                          |
| `\operatorname{Con}(T)`     | consistency statement, énoncé de cohérence, 一致性陈述                       | A formal sentence expressing that theory `T` does not prove a contradiction.                           | EN: Gödel’s second incompleteness theorem concerns `\operatorname{Con}(T)`.<br>FR: Le second théorème d’incomplétude de Gödel concerne `\operatorname{Con}(T)`.<br>中：Gödel 第二不完全性定理涉及 `\operatorname{Con}(T)`。                                 |
| —                           | first incompleteness theorem, premier théorème d’incomplétude, 第一不完全性定理 | A theorem stating that sufficiently strong consistent effective theories of arithmetic are incomplete. | EN: No such theory proves every true arithmetic sentence.<br>FR: Aucune telle théorie ne prouve toute phrase arithmétique vraie.<br>中：这样的理论不能证明所有真实算术句子。                                                                                       |
| —                           | second incompleteness theorem, second théorème d’incomplétude, 第二不完全性定理 | A theorem stating that sufficiently strong consistent theories cannot prove their own consistency.     | EN: A suitable theory `T` cannot prove `\operatorname{Con}(T)` if `T` is consistent.<br>FR: Une théorie appropriée `T` ne peut pas prouver `\operatorname{Con}(T)` si `T` est cohérente.<br>中：若适当理论 `T` 一致，则 `T` 不能证明 `\operatorname{Con}(T)`。 |
| —                           | diagonal lemma, lemme diagonal, 对角引理                                    | A lemma enabling formulas to refer to their own Gödel codes.                                           | EN: The diagonal lemma constructs self-referential arithmetic sentences.<br>FR: Le lemme diagonal construit des phrases arithmétiques autoréférentielles.<br>中：对角引理构造自指的算术句子。                                                                  |
| —                           | self-reference, autoréférence, 自指                                       | A phenomenon where a statement refers to itself, often through coding.                                 | EN: Gödel sentences use controlled formal self-reference.<br>FR: Les phrases de Gödel utilisent une autoréférence formelle contrôlée.<br>中：Gödel 句子使用受控的形式自指。                                                                                  |
| —                           | ω-consistency, ω-cohérence, ω-一致性                                       | A stronger consistency condition concerning infinitely many natural number instances.                  | EN: Early incompleteness proofs used ω-consistency assumptions.<br>FR: Les premières preuves d’incomplétude utilisaient des hypothèses d’ω-cohérence.<br>中：早期不完全性证明使用了 ω-一致性假设。                                                                |
| —                           | formal limitation, limitation formelle, 形式限制                            | A boundary on what can be proved, decided, or expressed in a formal system.                            | EN: Incompleteness theorems reveal limits of formal axiomatic systems.<br>FR: Les théorèmes d’incomplétude révèlent les limites des systèmes axiomatiques formels.<br>中：不完全性定理揭示形式公理系统的边界。                                                     |

## 38.10 Type theory, constructive logic, and proof assistants

| Symbol (LaTeX)     | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                            | Definition                                                                                        | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
| ------------------ | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `A:\mathsf{Type}`  | type, type, 类型                                                               | A collection-like object whose inhabitants are terms.                                             | EN: Natural numbers form a type in type theory.<br>FR: Les entiers naturels forment un type en théorie des types.<br>中：自然数在类型论中构成一个类型。                                                                                                |
| `a:A`              | term inhabiting a type, terme habitant un type, 类型中的项                        | A term `a` belonging to type `A`.                                                                 | EN: `0:\mathbb{N}` says that `0` is a term of natural-number type.<br>FR: `0:\mathbb{N}` dit que `0` est un terme du type des entiers naturels.<br>中：`0:\mathbb{N}` 表示 `0` 是自然数类型中的项。                                                 |
| `A\to B`           | function type, type fonction, 函数类型                                           | The type of functions from type `A` to type `B`.                                                  | EN: A proof of `A\to B` transforms proofs of `A` into proofs of `B`.<br>FR: Une preuve de `A\to B` transforme les preuves de `A` en preuves de `B`.<br>中：`A\to B` 的证明把 `A` 的证明转化为 `B` 的证明。                                            |
| `\Pi_{x:A}B(x)`    | dependent function type, type produit dépendant, 依赖函数类型                      | A function type whose output type depends on the input.                                           | EN: Universal quantification is represented by dependent function types.<br>FR: La quantification universelle est représentée par les types fonctionnels dépendants.<br>中：全称量词可由依赖函数类型表示。                                             |
| `\Sigma_{x:A}B(x)` | dependent pair type, type somme dépendante, 依赖对类型                            | A type of pairs where the second component’s type depends on the first component.                 | EN: Existential quantification is represented by dependent pair types.<br>FR: La quantification existentielle est représentée par les types de paires dépendantes.<br>中：存在量词可由依赖对类型表示。                                                |
| `a=_A b`           | identity type, type d’identité, 恒等类型                                         | A type representing equality or identification between two terms of the same type.                | EN: In homotopy type theory, identity proofs behave like paths.<br>FR: En théorie des types homotopique, les preuves d’identité se comportent comme des chemins.<br>中：在同伦类型论中，恒等证明类似路径。                                               |
| —                  | Curry-Howard correspondence, correspondance de Curry-Howard, Curry-Howard 对应 | The correspondence between propositions and types, proofs and programs.                           | EN: A constructive proof can correspond to a program.<br>FR: Une preuve constructive peut correspondre à un programme.<br>中：构造性证明可以对应一个程序。                                                                                            |
| —                  | constructive logic, logique constructive, 构造性逻辑                              | A logic requiring explicit constructions for existence and avoiding unrestricted excluded middle. | EN: To prove `\exists x\,P(x)` constructively, one gives a witness.<br>FR: Pour prouver constructivement `\exists x\,P(x)`, on donne un témoin.<br>中：构造性地证明 `\exists x\,P(x)` 需要给出见证。                                                 |
| `A\vee\neg A`      | law of excluded middle, principe du tiers exclu, 排中律                         | The principle that every proposition is either true or false.                                     | EN: Classical logic accepts excluded middle generally; constructive logic does not always do so.<br>FR: La logique classique accepte généralement le tiers exclu ; la logique constructive pas toujours.<br>中：经典逻辑一般接受排中律；构造性逻辑不总是接受。 |
| —                  | proof assistant, assistant de preuve, 证明助手                                   | Software for writing and checking formal proofs.                                                  | EN: Lean, Coq, Isabelle, and Agda are proof assistants.<br>FR: Lean, Coq, Isabelle et Agda sont des assistants de preuve.<br>中：Lean、Coq、Isabelle 和 Agda 是证明助手。                                                                        |
| —                  | formalization, formalisation, 形式化                                            | The process of expressing mathematical definitions and proofs in a formal system.                 | EN: Formalization makes proof checking precise and mechanical.<br>FR: La formalisation rend la vérification des preuves précise et mécanique.<br>中：形式化使证明检查精确且机械化。                                                                    |
| —                  | dependent type theory, théorie des types dépendants, 依赖类型论                   | A type theory allowing types to depend on terms.                                                  | EN: Dependent type theory is a foundation for many proof assistants.<br>FR: La théorie des types dépendants fonde beaucoup d’assistants de preuve.<br>中：依赖类型论是许多证明助手的基础。                                                              |

## 38.11 Philosophical and structural foundations of mathematics

| Symbol (LaTeX) | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                            | Definition                                                                                         | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
| -------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------ | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| —              | foundation of mathematics, fondement des mathématiques, 数学基础 | A framework explaining the basic objects, principles, and justification of mathematics.            | EN: Set theory and type theory are two major foundational frameworks.<br>FR: La théorie des ensembles et la théorie des types sont deux grands cadres fondationnels.<br>中：集合论和类型论是两种主要数学基础框架。                       |
| —              | formalism, formalisme, 形式主义                                  | A view emphasizing formal symbol manipulation and axiomatic systems.                               | EN: Hilbert’s program is historically associated with formalist ambitions.<br>FR: Le programme de Hilbert est historiquement associé aux ambitions formalistes.<br>中：Hilbert 纲领在历史上与形式主义目标相关。                       |
| —              | logicism, logicisme, 逻辑主义                                    | The view that mathematics can be reduced to logic.                                                 | EN: Frege and Russell are central figures in logicist history.<br>FR: Frege et Russell sont des figures centrales de l’histoire logiciste.<br>中：Frege 与 Russell 是逻辑主义史中的核心人物。                                       |
| —              | intuitionism, intuitionnisme, 直觉主义                           | A philosophy requiring mathematical objects and proofs to be grounded in constructive mental acts. | EN: Intuitionism rejects unrestricted use of excluded middle.<br>FR: L’intuitionnisme rejette l’usage illimité du tiers exclu.<br>中：直觉主义拒绝无限制使用排中律。                                                                 |
| —              | platonism, platonisme, 柏拉图主义                                 | The view that mathematical objects exist independently of human construction.                      | EN: A platonist may regard the natural numbers as objectively existing.<br>FR: Un platonicien peut considérer les entiers naturels comme existant objectivement.<br>中：柏拉图主义者可能认为自然数客观存在。                            |
| —              | structuralism, structuralisme, 结构主义                          | The view that mathematics studies positions in structures rather than isolated intrinsic objects.  | EN: Natural numbers can be understood through their role in the successor structure.<br>FR: Les entiers naturels peuvent être compris par leur rôle dans la structure du successeur.<br>中：自然数可通过其在后继结构中的角色来理解。      |
| —              | finitism, finitisme, 有限主义                                    | A view accepting only finite mathematical objects or finitely checkable reasoning as basic.        | EN: Finitist reasoning avoids completed infinite totalities.<br>FR: Le raisonnement finitiste évite les totalités infinies achevées.<br>中：有限主义推理避免完成的无限总体。                                                          |
| —              | predicativism, prédicativisme, 谓述主义                          | A view restricting definitions that quantify over totalities containing the object being defined.  | EN: Predicative foundations avoid certain circular definitions.<br>FR: Les fondements prédicatifs évitent certaines définitions circulaires.<br>中：谓述主义基础避免某些循环定义。                                                   |
| —              | impredicativity, imprédicativité, 非谓述性                       | A definition or principle quantifying over a totality that includes the object being defined.      | EN: Some set-theoretic and type-theoretic systems allow impredicative definitions.<br>FR: Certains systèmes ensemblistes et typés permettent des définitions imprédicatives.<br>中：某些集合论和类型论系统允许非谓述定义。               |
| —              | consistency strength, force de cohérence, 一致性强度              | A measure comparing theories by the strength of consistency statements they can prove or imply.    | EN: Large cardinal axioms increase consistency strength.<br>FR: Les axiomes de grands cardinaux augmentent la force de cohérence.<br>中：大基数公理提高一致性强度。                                                                |
| —              | interpretability, interprétabilité, 可解释性                     | A relation where one theory can encode or simulate another theory.                                 | EN: Interpretability compares formal theories beyond simple inclusion of axioms.<br>FR: L’interprétabilité compare les théories formelles au-delà de la simple inclusion d’axiomes.<br>中：可解释性用于比较形式理论，而不只是比较公理包含关系。 |
| —              | reverse mathematics, mathématiques à rebours, 反向数学           | A program classifying the axioms needed to prove ordinary mathematical theorems.                   | EN: Reverse mathematics asks which set-existence principles a theorem requires.<br>FR: Les mathématiques à rebours demandent quels principes d’existence d’ensembles un théorème exige.<br>中：反向数学考察一个定理需要哪些集合存在原则。  |

## 38.12 Modern foundations, univalence, and formal mathematics

| Symbol (LaTeX) | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                           | Definition                                                                                                              | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| -------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| —              | univalent foundations, fondements univalents, 单价基础                          | A foundation based on type theory where equivalence of structures can be identified with equality.                      | EN: Univalent foundations make “isomorphic objects are equal” precise in a controlled way.<br>FR: Les fondements univalents rendent précise l’idée que les objets isomorphes sont égaux.<br>中：单价基础以受控方式精确表达“同构对象相等”。                     |
| —              | univalence axiom, axiome d’univalence, 单价公理                                 | An axiom stating that equivalences between types correspond to identities between types.                                | EN: The univalence axiom links equality with equivalence.<br>FR: L’axiome d’univalence relie égalité et équivalence.<br>中：单价公理把相等与等价联系起来。                                                                                                |
| —              | homotopy type theory, théorie des types homotopique, 同伦类型论                  | A foundation interpreting types as spaces and equalities as paths.                                                      | EN: Homotopy type theory connects logic, topology, and computation.<br>FR: La théorie des types homotopique relie logique, topologie et calcul.<br>中：同伦类型论连接逻辑、拓扑与计算。                                                                    |
| —              | higher inductive type, type inductif supérieur, 高阶归纳类型                      | A type specified by constructors for points and paths, and sometimes higher paths.                                      | EN: The circle can be represented as a higher inductive type.<br>FR: Le cercle peut être représenté comme un type inductif supérieur.<br>中：圆可以表示为一个高阶归纳类型。                                                                               |
| —              | synthetic mathematics, mathématiques synthétiques, 综合式数学                    | Mathematics developed internally in a foundational language without reducing everything to external encodings.          | EN: Synthetic differential geometry treats infinitesimals directly inside a suitable logic.<br>FR: La géométrie différentielle synthétique traite directement les infinitésimaux dans une logique appropriée.<br>中：综合微分几何在适当逻辑内部直接处理无穷小。 |
| —              | formal proof library, bibliothèque de preuves formelles, 形式证明库              | A curated collection of machine-checked definitions, theorems, and proofs.                                              | EN: Mathlib is a major formal proof library for Lean.<br>FR: Mathlib est une grande bibliothèque de preuves formelles pour Lean.<br>中：Mathlib 是 Lean 的大型形式证明库。                                                                           |
| —              | computer-verified proof, preuve vérifiée par ordinateur, 计算机验证证明            | A proof checked by a proof assistant or formal verifier.                                                                | EN: Computer-verified proofs reduce reliance on informal checking.<br>FR: Les preuves vérifiées par ordinateur réduisent la dépendance à la vérification informelle.<br>中：计算机验证证明降低对非形式检查的依赖。                                            |
| —              | proof term, terme de preuve, 证明项                                            | A term representing a proof in a type-theoretic system.                                                                 | EN: Under Curry-Howard, a proof of a proposition is a term of its corresponding type.<br>FR: Selon Curry-Howard, une preuve d’une proposition est un terme du type correspondant.<br>中：在 Curry-Howard 对应下，一个命题的证明是其对应类型的项。               |
| —              | tactic, tactique, 策略                                                        | A command or procedure guiding a proof assistant to construct proof terms.                                              | EN: Tactics automate routine proof steps.<br>FR: Les tactiques automatisent les étapes routinières de preuve.<br>中：策略自动化常规证明步骤。                                                                                                          |
| —              | kernel, noyau, 内核                                                           | The small trusted core of a proof assistant that checks proof correctness.                                              | EN: A small kernel limits the trusted code base.<br>FR: Un petit noyau limite la base de code de confiance.<br>中：小内核限制需要信任的代码基础。                                                                                                         |
| —              | formal verification, vérification formelle, 形式验证                            | The use of formal methods to prove correctness of programs, systems, or mathematical statements.                        | EN: Formal verification is used for critical software and theorem proving.<br>FR: La vérification formelle est utilisée pour les logiciels critiques et la preuve de théorèmes.<br>中：形式验证用于关键软件和定理证明。                                    |
| —              | foundations-as-infrastructure, fondements comme infrastructure, 作为基础设施的数学基础 | A modern view treating foundations as practical infrastructure for organizing, verifying, and transferring mathematics. | EN: Formal libraries make foundations operational for large-scale mathematics.<br>FR: Les bibliothèques formelles rendent les fondements opérationnels pour les mathématiques à grande échelle.<br>中：形式库使数学基础成为大规模数学中的可操作基础设施。           |
# 39. Combinatorics, Graph Theory, and Discrete Structures

## 39.1 Counting, permutations, combinations, and basic principles

| Symbol (LaTeX)                         | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                                | Definition                                                                                         | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| -------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `n!`                                   | factorial, factorielle, 阶乘                                                       | The product of all positive integers from `1` to `n`.                                              | EN: `n!` counts the number of ways to arrange `n` distinct objects.<br>FR: `n!` compte le nombre de façons d’ordonner `n` objets distincts.<br>中：`n!` 计算 `n` 个不同对象的排列数。                                                                 |
| `\binom{n}{k}`                         | binomial coefficient, coefficient binomial, 二项式系数                                | The number of ways to choose `k` elements from an `n`-element set.                                 | EN: `\binom{n}{k}` counts unordered selections.<br>FR: `\binom{n}{k}` compte les choix non ordonnés.<br>中：`\binom{n}{k}` 计算无序选取方式。                                                                                                      |
| `P(n,k)`                               | permutation count, nombre d’arrangements, 排列数                                    | The number of ordered selections of `k` distinct elements from `n` elements.                       | EN: Seating `k` people in ordered chairs uses permutations.<br>FR: Placer `k` personnes sur des chaises ordonnées utilise des arrangements.<br>中：把 `k` 个人安排到有序座位上使用排列数。                                                                 |
| `C(n,k)`                               | combination count, nombre de combinaisons, 组合数                                   | The number of unordered selections of `k` elements from `n` elements.                              | EN: Choosing a committee is a combination problem.<br>FR: Choisir un comité est un problème de combinaisons.<br>中：选择委员会是组合问题。                                                                                                           |
| `\sum_i a_i`                           | sum rule, principe de somme, 加法原理                                                | If choices are disjoint, the total number is the sum of the numbers of choices.                    | EN: If cases do not overlap, count them separately and add.<br>FR: Si les cas ne se recouvrent pas, on les compte séparément puis on additionne.<br>中：若情形不重叠，可分别计数后相加。                                                                  |
| `\prod_i a_i`                          | product rule, principe du produit, 乘法原理                                          | If choices occur in independent stages, the total number is the product of the numbers of choices. | EN: Choosing a shirt and then a hat uses the product rule.<br>FR: Choisir une chemise puis un chapeau utilise le principe du produit.<br>中：先选衬衫再选帽子使用乘法原理。                                                                              |
| —                                      | pigeonhole principle, principe des tiroirs, 鸽巢原理                                 | If more objects than boxes are distributed into boxes, some box contains at least two objects.     | EN: Among `13` people, at least two share a birth month.<br>FR: Parmi `13` personnes, au moins deux partagent un mois de naissance.<br>中：`13` 个人中至少两人生日月份相同。                                                                            |
| —                                      | generalized pigeonhole principle, principe généralisé des tiroirs, 广义鸽巢原理        | If `N` objects are placed into `k` boxes, some box contains at least `\lceil N/k\rceil` objects.   | EN: `101` objects in `10` boxes force one box to contain at least `11` objects.<br>FR: `101` objets dans `10` tiroirs forcent un tiroir à contenir au moins `11` objets.<br>中：`101` 个对象放入 `10` 个盒子，至少一个盒子含有 `11` 个对象。                   |
| `\binom{n}{k}=\binom{n}{n-k}`          | symmetry of binomial coefficients, symétrie des coefficients binomiaux, 二项式系数对称性 | Choosing `k` elements is equivalent to choosing the `n-k` elements left out.                       | EN: Choosing a team is equivalent to choosing who is excluded.<br>FR: Choisir une équipe équivaut à choisir ceux qui sont exclus.<br>中：选择队伍等价于选择未入选者。                                                                                   |
| `(x+y)^n=\sum_k\binom{n}{k}x^ky^{n-k}` | binomial theorem, théorème binomial, 二项式定理                                       | The expansion formula for a power of a sum.                                                        | EN: The binomial theorem explains why `\binom{n}{k}` appears in polynomial expansion.<br>FR: Le théorème binomial explique pourquoi `\binom{n}{k}` apparaît dans le développement polynomial.<br>中：二项式定理解释了为何 `\binom{n}{k}` 出现在多项式展开中。 |
| —                                      | double counting, double comptage, 双重计数                                           | A proof technique counting the same set in two different ways.                                     | EN: Double counting often proves combinatorial identities.<br>FR: Le double comptage prouve souvent des identités combinatoires.<br>中：双重计数常用于证明组合恒等式。                                                                                   |
| —                                      | bijective proof, preuve bijective, 双射证明                                          | A proof establishing equality by constructing a bijection between two counted sets.                | EN: A bijective proof explains why two formulas count the same objects.<br>FR: Une preuve bijective explique pourquoi deux formules comptent les mêmes objets.<br>中：双射证明解释两个公式为何计数同一类对象。                                                |

## 39.2 Inclusion-exclusion, recurrence, and generating functions

| Symbol (LaTeX)                                                            | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                                        | Definition                                                                                     | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                         |
| ------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `\lvert A\cup B\rvert=\lvert A\rvert+\lvert B\rvert-\lvert A\cap B\rvert` | inclusion-exclusion principle, principe d’inclusion-exclusion, 容斥原理                      | A counting principle correcting overcounting by alternating additions and subtractions.        | EN: Inclusion-exclusion counts objects satisfying at least one condition.<br>FR: L’inclusion-exclusion compte les objets satisfaisant au moins une condition.<br>中：容斥原理计算满足至少一个条件的对象数。           |
| `a_n`                                                                     | sequence term, terme d’une suite, 数列项                                                    | The `n`th element of a sequence.                                                               | EN: A combinatorial sequence may count structures of size `n`.<br>FR: Une suite combinatoire peut compter les structures de taille `n`.<br>中：组合数列可计数规模为 `n` 的结构。                                 |
| `a_n=a_{n-1}+a_{n-2}`                                                     | recurrence relation, relation de récurrence, 递推关系                                        | An equation defining terms of a sequence from previous terms.                                  | EN: Fibonacci numbers satisfy a recurrence relation.<br>FR: Les nombres de Fibonacci satisfont une relation de récurrence.<br>中：Fibonacci 数满足递推关系。                                               |
| `F_n`                                                                     | Fibonacci number, nombre de Fibonacci, Fibonacci 数                                       | A sequence defined by `F_n=F_{n-1}+F_{n-2}` with standard initial values.                      | EN: Fibonacci numbers count tilings by squares and dominoes.<br>FR: Les nombres de Fibonacci comptent des pavages par carrés et dominos.<br>中：Fibonacci 数可计数由方块和多米诺骨牌组成的铺法。                      |
| `A(x)=\sum_{n\geq0}a_nx^n`                                                | ordinary generating function, fonction génératrice ordinaire, 普通生成函数                     | A formal power series encoding a sequence by its coefficients.                                 | EN: Generating functions turn recurrence relations into algebraic equations.<br>FR: Les fonctions génératrices transforment les récurrences en équations algébriques.<br>中：生成函数把递推关系转化为代数方程。     |
| `E(x)=\sum_{n\geq0}a_n\frac{x^n}{n!}`                                     | exponential generating function, fonction génératrice exponentielle, 指数生成函数              | A generating function often suited to labeled combinatorial structures.                        | EN: Exponential generating functions are natural for labeled objects.<br>FR: Les fonctions génératrices exponentielles sont naturelles pour les objets étiquetés.<br>中：指数生成函数适合处理带标号对象。          |
| `[x^n]A(x)`                                                               | coefficient extraction, extraction de coefficient, 系数提取                                  | Notation for the coefficient of `x^n` in a power series.                                       | EN: `[x^n]A(x)` recovers the number counted by `a_n`.<br>FR: `[x^n]A(x)` récupère le nombre compté par `a_n`.<br>中：`[x^n]A(x)` 提取由 `a_n` 计数的数量。                                                  |
| —                                                                         | formal power series, série formelle, 形式幂级数                                               | A power series manipulated algebraically without requiring analytic convergence.               | EN: Combinatorics often uses formal power series purely algebraically.<br>FR: La combinatoire utilise souvent les séries formelles de façon purement algébrique.<br>中：组合学常以纯代数方式使用形式幂级数。         |
| —                                                                         | partition of an integer, partition d’un entier, 整数分拆                                     | A way of writing an integer as a sum of positive integers, disregarding order.                 | EN: `5=3+2` and `5=2+3` are the same integer partition.<br>FR: `5=3+2` et `5=2+3` sont la même partition d’un entier.<br>中：`5=3+2` 与 `5=2+3` 是同一个整数分拆。                                           |
| `p(n)`                                                                    | partition function, fonction de partition, 分拆函数                                          | The number of integer partitions of `n`.                                                       | EN: `p(n)` grows rapidly with `n`.<br>FR: `p(n)` croît rapidement avec `n`.<br>中：`p(n)` 随 `n` 快速增长。                                                                                              |
| `S(n,k)`                                                                  | Stirling number of the second kind, nombre de Stirling de seconde espèce, 第二类 Stirling 数 | The number of ways to partition an `n`-element set into `k` nonempty unlabeled blocks.         | EN: `S(n,k)` counts set partitions.<br>FR: `S(n,k)` compte les partitions d’un ensemble.<br>中：`S(n,k)` 计算集合划分数。                                                                                  |
| `c(n,k)`                                                                  | Stirling number of the first kind, nombre de Stirling de première espèce, 第一类 Stirling 数 | A number counting permutations of `n` elements by number of cycles, under a common convention. | EN: Stirling numbers of the first kind connect permutations and cycle structure.<br>FR: Les nombres de Stirling de première espèce relient permutations et cycles.<br>中：第一类 Stirling 数连接排列与循环结构。 |

## 39.3 Graphs, vertices, edges, and connectivity

| Symbol (LaTeX) | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                 | Definition                                                                 | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| -------------- | ------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `G=(V,E)`      | graph, graphe, 图                                  | A structure consisting of vertices and edges connecting pairs of vertices. | EN: A road network can be modeled as a graph.<br>FR: Un réseau routier peut être modélisé par un graphe.<br>中：道路网络可建模为图。                                                                                 |
| `V(G)`         | vertex set, ensemble des sommets, 顶点集             | The set of vertices of a graph.                                            | EN: People in a social network are vertices.<br>FR: Les personnes dans un réseau social sont des sommets.<br>中：社交网络中的人是顶点。                                                                               |
| `E(G)`         | edge set, ensemble des arêtes, 边集                 | The set of edges of a graph.                                               | EN: Friendships in a social network are edges.<br>FR: Les amitiés dans un réseau social sont des arêtes.<br>中：社交网络中的好友关系是边。                                                                              |
| `uv`           | edge between vertices, arête entre sommets, 顶点间的边 | An edge connecting vertices `u` and `v`.                                   | EN: The notation `uv\in E(G)` means `u` and `v` are adjacent.<br>FR: La notation `uv\in E(G)` signifie que `u` et `v` sont adjacents.<br>中：记号 `uv\in E(G)` 表示 `u` 与 `v` 相邻。                              |
| `u\sim v`      | adjacency, adjacence, 相邻                          | A relation meaning two vertices are joined by an edge.                     | EN: Adjacent vertices are directly connected.<br>FR: Les sommets adjacents sont directement connectés.<br>中：相邻顶点之间有直接连接。                                                                                 |
| `\deg(v)`      | degree of a vertex, degré d’un sommet, 顶点度数       | The number of edges incident to a vertex.                                  | EN: A high-degree vertex is connected to many neighbors.<br>FR: Un sommet de grand degré est connecté à beaucoup de voisins.<br>中：高度数顶点连接许多邻居。                                                           |
| `N(v)`         | neighborhood, voisinage, 邻域                       | The set of vertices adjacent to `v`.                                       | EN: `N(v)` lists the immediate neighbors of `v`.<br>FR: `N(v)` liste les voisins immédiats de `v`.<br>中：`N(v)` 列出 `v` 的直接邻居。                                                                             |
| `K_n`          | complete graph, graphe complet, 完全图               | A graph on `n` vertices where every pair of distinct vertices is adjacent. | EN: `K_n` has the maximum possible number of edges among simple graphs on `n` vertices.<br>FR: `K_n` a le nombre maximal d’arêtes parmi les graphes simples à `n` sommets.<br>中：`K_n` 在 `n` 顶点简单图中有最多边数。 |
| `P_n`          | path graph, graphe chemin, 路图                     | A graph shaped as a single path with `n` vertices.                         | EN: `P_n` models a linear chain.<br>FR: `P_n` modélise une chaîne linéaire.<br>中：`P_n` 建模线性链。                                                                                                            |
| `C_n`          | cycle graph, graphe cycle, 圈图                     | A graph forming a cycle on `n` vertices.                                   | EN: `C_n` connects the last vertex back to the first.<br>FR: `C_n` relie le dernier sommet au premier.<br>中：`C_n` 把最后一个顶点连回第一个顶点。                                                                        |
| —              | connected graph, graphe connexe, 连通图              | A graph in which every pair of vertices is joined by a path.               | EN: A connected communication network has a route between any two nodes.<br>FR: Un réseau de communication connexe a un chemin entre deux nœuds quelconques.<br>中：连通通信网络中任意两个节点之间都有路径。                   |
| —              | connected component, composante connexe, 连通分量     | A maximal connected subgraph.                                              | EN: Disconnected graphs split into connected components.<br>FR: Les graphes non connexes se décomposent en composantes connexes.<br>中：非连通图分解为若干连通分量。                                                     |

## 39.4 Trees, paths, cycles, and graph traversal

| Symbol (LaTeX) | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                    | Definition                                                                                                     | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| -------------- | ---------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `T`            | tree, arbre, 树                                       | A connected graph with no cycles.                                                                              | EN: A tree with `n` vertices has `n-1` edges.<br>FR: Un arbre à `n` sommets a `n-1` arêtes.<br>中：含 `n` 个顶点的树有 `n-1` 条边。                                                                  |
| —              | forest, forêt, 森林                                    | A graph with no cycles, equivalently a disjoint union of trees.                                                | EN: Removing edges from a tree creates a forest.<br>FR: Supprimer des arêtes d’un arbre crée une forêt.<br>中：从树中删除若干边会得到森林。                                                              |
| —              | leaf, feuille, 叶子                                    | A vertex of degree one in a tree.                                                                              | EN: Every finite tree with at least two vertices has at least two leaves.<br>FR: Tout arbre fini avec au moins deux sommets possède au moins deux feuilles.<br>中：至少含两个顶点的有限树至少有两个叶子。     |
| —              | root, racine, 根                                      | A distinguished vertex of a rooted tree.                                                                       | EN: Rooted trees organize hierarchical data.<br>FR: Les arbres enracinés organisent les données hiérarchiques.<br>中：有根树组织层级数据。                                                           |
| —              | spanning tree, arbre couvrant, 生成树                   | A subgraph that is a tree containing all vertices of a connected graph.                                        | EN: A connected graph always has a spanning tree.<br>FR: Un graphe connexe possède toujours un arbre couvrant.<br>中：连通图总有生成树。                                                            |
| —              | minimum spanning tree, arbre couvrant minimal, 最小生成树 | A spanning tree of minimum total edge weight in a weighted graph.                                              | EN: Minimum spanning trees are used in network design.<br>FR: Les arbres couvrants minimaux sont utilisés dans la conception de réseaux.<br>中：最小生成树用于网络设计。                               |
| —              | path, chemin, 路径                                     | A sequence of vertices connected by edges, usually with no repeated vertices under the simple-path convention. | EN: Paths describe possible routes through a graph.<br>FR: Les chemins décrivent les trajets possibles dans un graphe.<br>中：路径描述图中的可行路线。                                                 |
| —              | walk, marche, 游走                                     | A sequence of adjacent vertices, allowing repeated vertices and edges.                                         | EN: Random walks move through a graph by choosing neighboring vertices.<br>FR: Les marches aléatoires se déplacent dans un graphe en choisissant des voisins.<br>中：随机游走通过选择邻居在图上移动。      |
| —              | cycle, cycle, 圈                                      | A closed path returning to its starting vertex without repeating internal vertices.                            | EN: Cycles indicate redundant routes in a network.<br>FR: Les cycles indiquent des trajets redondants dans un réseau.<br>中：圈表示网络中的冗余路径。                                                  |
| —              | depth-first search, parcours en profondeur, 深度优先搜索   | A graph traversal exploring as far as possible before backtracking.                                            | EN: Depth-first search detects cycles and connected components.<br>FR: Le parcours en profondeur détecte cycles et composantes connexes.<br>中：深度优先搜索可检测圈和连通分量。                           |
| —              | breadth-first search, parcours en largeur, 广度优先搜索    | A graph traversal exploring vertices by increasing distance from the start.                                    | EN: Breadth-first search finds shortest paths in unweighted graphs.<br>FR: Le parcours en largeur trouve les plus courts chemins dans les graphes non pondérés.<br>中：广度优先搜索可在无权图中寻找最短路径。 |
| `d(u,v)`       | graph distance, distance dans un graphe, 图距离         | The length of a shortest path between vertices `u` and `v`.                                                    | EN: Graph distance measures separation in a network.<br>FR: La distance dans un graphe mesure la séparation dans un réseau.<br>中：图距离度量网络中的间隔。                                            |

## 39.5 Directed graphs, weighted graphs, and network structures

| Symbol (LaTeX) | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                   | Definition                                                                            | Examples                                                                                                                                                                               |
| -------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `D=(V,A)`      | directed graph, graphe orienté, 有向图                                 | A graph whose edges have directions, often called arcs.                               | EN: Web pages and hyperlinks form a directed graph.<br>FR: Les pages web et les hyperliens forment un graphe orienté.<br>中：网页和超链接构成有向图。                                                |
| `A(D)`         | arc set, ensemble des arcs, 弧集                                      | The set of directed edges in a directed graph.                                        | EN: An arc from `u` to `v` is not the same as an arc from `v` to `u`.<br>FR: Un arc de `u` vers `v` n’est pas le même qu’un arc de `v` vers `u`.<br>中：从 `u` 到 `v` 的弧不同于从 `v` 到 `u` 的弧。 |
| `u\to v`       | directed edge, arête orientée, 有向边                                  | An edge directed from vertex `u` to vertex `v`.                                       | EN: `u\to v` can represent influence or dependency.<br>FR: `u\to v` peut représenter une influence ou une dépendance.<br>中：`u\to v` 可表示影响或依赖。                                          |
| `\deg^+(v)`    | outdegree, degré sortant, 出度                                        | The number of arcs leaving a vertex.                                                  | EN: A web page with many outgoing links has high outdegree.<br>FR: Une page web avec beaucoup de liens sortants a un grand degré sortant.<br>中：有许多外链的网页具有高出度。                          |
| `\deg^-(v)`    | indegree, degré entrant, 入度                                         | The number of arcs entering a vertex.                                                 | EN: A highly cited paper has high indegree in a citation graph.<br>FR: Un article très cité a un grand degré entrant dans un graphe de citations.<br>中：被大量引用的论文在引用图中具有高入度。             |
| —              | strongly connected directed graph, graphe fortement connexe, 强连通有向图 | A directed graph where every vertex can reach every other vertex by directed paths.   | EN: Strong connectivity means two-way reachability among all vertices.<br>FR: La forte connexité signifie l’accessibilité mutuelle entre tous les sommets.<br>中：强连通表示所有顶点之间都有双向可达性。    |
| —              | strongly connected component, composante fortement connexe, 强连通分量   | A maximal subgraph that is strongly connected.                                        | EN: Directed graphs decompose into strongly connected components.<br>FR: Les graphes orientés se décomposent en composantes fortement connexes.<br>中：有向图可分解为强连通分量。                     |
| `w(e)`         | edge weight, poids d’une arête, 边权                                  | A numerical value assigned to an edge.                                                | EN: Edge weights may represent distance, cost, or capacity.<br>FR: Les poids d’arêtes peuvent représenter distance, coût ou capacité.<br>中：边权可表示距离、成本或容量。                              |
| —              | weighted graph, graphe pondéré, 加权图                                 | A graph whose edges or vertices carry numerical weights.                              | EN: Transportation networks often use weighted graphs.<br>FR: Les réseaux de transport utilisent souvent des graphes pondérés.<br>中：交通网络常使用加权图。                                        |
| —              | shortest path, plus court chemin, 最短路径                              | A path with minimum total length or weight between vertices.                          | EN: Navigation systems compute shortest paths.<br>FR: Les systèmes de navigation calculent des plus courts chemins.<br>中：导航系统计算最短路径。                                                   |
| —              | topological ordering, ordre topologique, 拓扑排序                       | A linear ordering of vertices in a directed acyclic graph respecting edge directions. | EN: Task dependencies can be scheduled by topological ordering.<br>FR: Les dépendances de tâches peuvent être planifiées par ordre topologique.<br>中：任务依赖可通过拓扑排序安排。                    |
| `DAG`          | directed acyclic graph, graphe orienté acyclique, 有向无环图             | A directed graph containing no directed cycles.                                       | EN: DAGs represent dependency structures and causal graphs.<br>FR: Les DAG représentent des dépendances et des graphes causaux.<br>中：有向无环图表示依赖结构和因果图。                                  |

## 39.6 Matchings, coverings, flows, and cuts

| Symbol (LaTeX) | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                        | Definition                                                                                       | Examples                                                                                                                                                                             |
| -------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `M`            | matching, couplage, 匹配                                                   | A set of edges with no shared endpoints.                                                         | EN: Matching models pairing students with projects.<br>FR: Un couplage modélise l’appariement d’étudiants avec des projets.<br>中：匹配可建模学生与项目的配对。                                      |
| —              | perfect matching, couplage parfait, 完美匹配                                 | A matching covering every vertex of a graph.                                                     | EN: A perfect matching pairs all vertices with no leftovers.<br>FR: Un couplage parfait apparie tous les sommets sans reste.<br>中：完美匹配使所有顶点都被配对且无剩余。                                 |
| —              | maximum matching, couplage maximum, 最大匹配                                 | A matching with the largest possible number of edges.                                            | EN: Maximum matching maximizes the number of paired agents.<br>FR: Un couplage maximum maximise le nombre d’agents appariés.<br>中：最大匹配最大化被配对对象数量。                                    |
| —              | bipartite graph, graphe biparti, 二分图                                     | A graph whose vertices can be split into two parts so that edges go only between the parts.      | EN: Job assignment problems are naturally bipartite graphs.<br>FR: Les problèmes d’affectation sont naturellement des graphes bipartis.<br>中：岗位分配问题天然形成二分图。                          |
| `K_{m,n}`      | complete bipartite graph, graphe biparti complet, 完全二分图                  | A bipartite graph where every vertex in one part is connected to every vertex in the other part. | EN: `K_{m,n}` has `mn` edges.<br>FR: `K_{m,n}` a `mn` arêtes.<br>中：`K_{m,n}` 有 `mn` 条边。                                                                                              |
| —              | vertex cover, couverture de sommets, 点覆盖                                 | A set of vertices incident to every edge.                                                        | EN: Vertex cover asks for vertices that touch all edges.<br>FR: La couverture de sommets cherche des sommets touchant toutes les arêtes.<br>中：点覆盖寻找能覆盖所有边的顶点集合。                      |
| —              | edge cover, couverture d’arêtes, 边覆盖                                     | A set of edges incident to every vertex.                                                         | EN: Edge cover chooses edges that touch all vertices.<br>FR: La couverture d’arêtes choisit des arêtes touchant tous les sommets.<br>中：边覆盖选择能覆盖所有顶点的边。                               |
| —              | independent set, ensemble indépendant, 独立集                               | A set of vertices with no edges between any two of them.                                         | EN: Independent sets model mutually nonconflicting choices.<br>FR: Les ensembles indépendants modélisent des choix mutuellement non conflictuels.<br>中：独立集建模相互不冲突的选择。                |
| —              | Hall’s marriage theorem, théorème du mariage de Hall, Hall 婚配定理          | A criterion for the existence of a matching covering one side of a bipartite graph.              | EN: Hall’s theorem gives exact conditions for feasible matching.<br>FR: Le théorème de Hall donne les conditions exactes d’un couplage faisable.<br>中：Hall 定理给出可行匹配的精确条件。            |
| `s-t` cut      | cut, coupe, 割                                                            | A partition separating source `s` from sink `t` in a flow network.                               | EN: Cuts limit possible flow from source to sink.<br>FR: Les coupes limitent le flot possible de la source au puits.<br>中：割限制从源点到汇点的可能流量。                                            |
| —              | network flow, flot de réseau, 网络流                                        | An assignment of flows to directed edges satisfying capacity and conservation constraints.       | EN: Network flow models traffic, logistics, and communication capacity.<br>FR: Les flots de réseau modélisent trafic, logistique et capacité de communication.<br>中：网络流建模交通、物流和通信容量。 |
| —              | max-flow min-cut theorem, théorème flot maximal coupe minimale, 最大流最小割定理 | A theorem stating that the maximum flow value equals the minimum cut capacity.                   | EN: Max-flow min-cut connects optimization with graph separation.<br>FR: Flot maximal coupe minimale relie optimisation et séparation de graphes.<br>中：最大流最小割定理连接优化与图分割。             |

## 39.7 Coloring, planarity, and graph structure

| Symbol (LaTeX) | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                                     | Definition                                                                                                  | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| -------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| —              | graph coloring, coloriage de graphe, 图着色                                              | Assigning colors to graph elements under constraints, usually adjacent vertices receiving different colors. | EN: Timetable scheduling can be modeled as graph coloring.<br>FR: La planification d’emplois du temps peut être modélisée par coloriage de graphe.<br>中：课程表安排可建模为图着色。                     |
| `\chi(G)`      | chromatic number, nombre chromatique, 色数                                              | The minimum number of colors needed for a proper vertex coloring of a graph.                                | EN: Bipartite graphs have chromatic number at most `2`.<br>FR: Les graphes bipartis ont un nombre chromatique au plus `2`.<br>中：二分图的色数至多为 `2`。                                            |
| —              | proper coloring, coloriage propre, 正常着色                                               | A coloring in which adjacent vertices receive different colors.                                             | EN: Proper coloring avoids conflicts on adjacent vertices.<br>FR: Un coloriage propre évite les conflits entre sommets adjacents.<br>中：正常着色避免相邻顶点颜色冲突。                                    |
| —              | edge coloring, coloriage des arêtes, 边着色                                              | Assigning colors to edges so that adjacent edges satisfy a constraint, often different colors.              | EN: Edge coloring models scheduling of pairwise interactions.<br>FR: Le coloriage des arêtes modélise la planification d’interactions par paires.<br>中：边着色可建模成对互动的排程。                     |
| `\chi'(G)`     | chromatic index, indice chromatique, 边色数                                              | The minimum number of colors needed for a proper edge coloring.                                             | EN: The chromatic index measures edge-coloring complexity.<br>FR: L’indice chromatique mesure la complexité du coloriage des arêtes.<br>中：边色数度量边着色复杂度。                                    |
| —              | planar graph, graphe planaire, 平面图                                                    | A graph drawable in the plane without crossing edges.                                                       | EN: Road maps are often approximately planar graphs.<br>FR: Les cartes routières sont souvent approximativement des graphes planaires.<br>中：道路地图通常近似为平面图。                                 |
| —              | plane graph, graphe plongé dans le plan, 平面嵌入图                                        | A planar graph with a fixed crossing-free embedding in the plane.                                           | EN: A plane graph has well-defined faces.<br>FR: Un graphe plongé dans le plan a des faces bien définies.<br>中：平面嵌入图具有明确的面。                                                               |
| `F`            | face of a plane graph, face d’un graphe planaire, 平面图的面                               | A connected region of the plane left after drawing a plane graph.                                           | EN: Euler’s formula counts vertices, edges, and faces.<br>FR: La formule d’Euler compte sommets, arêtes et faces.<br>中：Euler 公式计数顶点、边和面。                                                  |
| `V-E+F=2`      | Euler formula for planar graphs, formule d’Euler pour graphes planaires, 平面图 Euler 公式 | A relation among vertices, edges, and faces in a connected plane graph.                                     | EN: Euler’s formula is fundamental in planar graph theory.<br>FR: La formule d’Euler est fondamentale dans la théorie des graphes planaires.<br>中：Euler 公式是平面图理论的基础。                      |
| —              | graph minor, mineur de graphe, 图 minor                                                | A graph obtained by deleting vertices, deleting edges, and contracting edges.                               | EN: Planarity can be characterized by forbidden minors.<br>FR: La planarité peut être caractérisée par des mineurs interdits.<br>中：平面性可由禁用 minor 刻画。                                      |
| —              | Kuratowski theorem, théorème de Kuratowski, Kuratowski 定理                             | A theorem characterizing planar graphs by absence of subdivisions of `K_5` and `K_{3,3}`.                   | EN: Kuratowski’s theorem gives a structural obstruction to planarity.<br>FR: Le théorème de Kuratowski donne une obstruction structurelle à la planarité.<br>中：Kuratowski 定理给出平面性的结构性障碍。  |
| —              | four color theorem, théorème des quatre couleurs, 四色定理                                | A theorem stating that every planar graph can be properly vertex-colored with at most four colors.          | EN: Map coloring is the classical motivation for the four color theorem.<br>FR: Le coloriage des cartes est la motivation classique du théorème des quatre couleurs.<br>中：地图着色是四色定理的经典动机。 |

## 39.8 Extremal and probabilistic combinatorics

| Symbol (LaTeX)           | Term (EN, FR, ZH)                                                         | Definition                                                                                                          | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| ------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| —                        | extremal combinatorics, combinatoire extrémale, 极值组合学                     | The study of maximum or minimum sizes of structures under forbidden configurations.                                 | EN: Extremal graph theory asks how many edges force a triangle.<br>FR: La théorie extrémale des graphes demande combien d’arêtes forcent un triangle.<br>中：极值图论研究多少条边会迫使三角形出现。                                           |
| `\operatorname{ex}(n,H)` | Turán number, nombre de Turán, Turán 数                                    | The maximum number of edges in an `n`-vertex graph containing no copy of `H`.                                       | EN: `\operatorname{ex}(n,K_3)` concerns triangle-free graphs.<br>FR: `\operatorname{ex}(n,K_3)` concerne les graphes sans triangle.<br>中：`\operatorname{ex}(n,K_3)` 研究无三角形图的最大边数。                                        |
| —                        | Turán theorem, théorème de Turán, Turán 定理                                | A foundational theorem giving the maximum number of edges in a graph with no complete subgraph of a specified size. | EN: Turán’s theorem generalizes Mantel’s theorem.<br>FR: Le théorème de Turán généralise le théorème de Mantel.<br>中：Turán 定理推广了 Mantel 定理。                                                                              |
| —                        | Ramsey theory, théorie de Ramsey, Ramsey 理论                               | The study of unavoidable patterns in large enough structures.                                                       | EN: Ramsey theory says complete disorder is impossible at large scale.<br>FR: La théorie de Ramsey dit que le désordre complet est impossible à grande échelle.<br>中：Ramsey 理论表明大尺度下完全无序不可能存在。                           |
| `R(s,t)`                 | Ramsey number, nombre de Ramsey, Ramsey 数                                 | The smallest number of vertices forcing either a clique of size `s` or an independent set of size `t`.              | EN: `R(3,3)=6` is the classic party theorem case.<br>FR: `R(3,3)=6` est le cas classique du théorème des amis et étrangers.<br>中：`R(3,3)=6` 是经典“聚会定理”情形。                                                                 |
| —                        | probabilistic method, méthode probabiliste, 概率方法                          | A proof method showing existence by proving that a random object has positive probability of satisfying a property. | EN: The probabilistic method proves existence without constructing an explicit object.<br>FR: La méthode probabiliste prouve l’existence sans construire explicitement l’objet.<br>中：概率方法在不显式构造对象的情况下证明存在性。              |
